// ============================================================================
// SECTION 1 — TYPE DEFINITIONS
// All interfaces and types used throughout the script.
// UIPart is a built-in NAI API type — no redefinition needed.
// ============================================================================

// ── Character Dynamics ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Fields that update during play, representing moment-to-moment character state.

interface ArcMilestone { id: string; description: string; achieved: boolean; achievedAt?: number; }

// [v1.7.0 JOURNAL] Player Journal — a player-authored running record ("The story so far").
// Bubbles are the source of truth (storyStorage); the lorebook entries are a projection
// of them: non-archived bubbles feed one Always-On entry, archived bubbles a keyless,
// disabled (never-injected) entry that unarchiving restores from.
interface JournalBubble { id: string; text: string; createdAt: number; archived: boolean; }
interface JournalState { bubbles: JournalBubble[]; activeLoreId?: string; archiveLoreId?: string; }

// [v2.12.0 CHARJOURNAL] Per-character journal — same bubble shape as the main
// journal, but no activeLoreId/archiveLoreId: this one has no dedicated lorebook
// entries of its own. Active bubbles fold straight into buildCharDynamicBlock
// (the character's OWN lorebook entry) as one more toggleable field, same
// treatment as equipment/conditions/relationship.
interface CharJournalBubble { id: string; text: string; createdAt: number; archived: boolean; }
interface CharJournalState { bubbles: CharJournalBubble[]; }

interface EphemeralSnapshot {
    // A snapshot of a character's immediate internal state at one moment in the story.
    // eventAnchor: the specific thing they just perceived or reacted to.
    // emotionalResponse: how it landed, in their own voice.
    // immediateDesire: what they intend to do right now.
    // compressedLine: a fallback single-line summary (composed from the three fields above).
    eventAnchor: string;
    emotionalResponse: string;
    immediateDesire: string;
    compressedLine: string;
    generatedAtGen: number; // [v0.9.24 E1] was generatedAtTokens
}

// [v2.14.0 GENESIS] Point-in-time copy of the mid-range layer, logged ONLY when a
// field actually changed vs. the live value (see generateMidRange's change gate).
// A field stable for ten scenes leaves one entry, not ten — this is a trend trail
// for the generator to read ("goal has held for N updates"), not a churn log like
// ephemeralHistory (which exists for lexical variety and appends unconditionally).
interface MidRangeSnapshot {
    currentDesire: string;
    socialMask:    string;
    shiftedView:   string;
    atGen: number;
}

// [v2.14.0 GENESIS] Same change-gated trail for the relationship dynamic fields.
interface RelationshipSnapshot {
    currentStatus: string;
    trajectory:    string;
    summary:       string;
    atGen: number;
}

interface MidRangeState {
    // Slower-moving layer between ephemeral (moment) and arc (story).
    baselineView:    string;  // FROZEN initial worldview — genesis only, never overwritten
    currentDesire:   string;  // sustained goal shaping decisions across scenes
    socialMask:      string;  // what they are performing vs. what is actually happening inside
    shiftedView:     string;  // something they now believe that they did not before
    updatedAtGen: number; // [v0.9.24 E1] was updatedAtTokens
}

// ── Character Relationships ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

interface RelationshipArchetypeDef {
    // A named relationship type with an optional ordered progression of stages.
    // stages[0] = coldest/earliest, stages[max] = warmest/latest. Empty stages
    // means the archetype is structural-only (label, no track) — legacy behaviour.
    id: string;
    name: string;
    stages: string[];
}

// [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] Same shape as RelationshipArchetypeDef, deliberately — a named
// character condition with an optional ordered severity progression. stages[0] =
// mildest, stages[max] = most severe. Empty stages = pure on/off toggle (no severity
// track; the narrative line omits the state sentence entirely). Unlike a relationship,
// a character can have MULTIPLE active conditions at once — see
// CharacterRecord.activeConditions below.
interface ConditionDef {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    stages: string[];
}

// One active condition on a character. defId refs cfg.conditions; stageIndex is
// ignored (treated as 0/no state text) if that def has no stages. NOTE: this is a
// deliberately different name from the old, already-deprecated `conditions` /
// `activeConditionId` fields migrateCharacter() strips off old saves — reusing
// those names would collide with that cleanup.
interface CharacterConditionState {
    defId: string;
    stageIndex: number;
}

interface PrimaryRelationship {
    targetName: string;
    enabled?: boolean;
    archetype: string;            // resolved display name (kept in sync with archetypeId; AI-context + legacy)
    archetypeId?: string;         // ID ref into cfg.relationshipArchetypes
    stageIndex?: number;          // current stage position within the archetype's stages[]
    howItStarted: string;
    threads: string[];
    currentStatus: string;
    trajectory: string;
    summary: string;
    milestones: ArcMilestone[];   // RETIRED from UI; field kept so old saves don't corrupt
}

interface UnresolvedHook {
    // An active narrative tension between two characters. pressureLevel 0–100.
    // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] At >=85 it injects directly into the Author's Note
    // alongside that character's thoughts. Below 85, currently tracked but not
    // injected anywhere (this comment was stale re: "background drama" — that
    // destination was removed, see CE-INDEX PASS 9).
    id: string;
    targetName: string;
    description: string;
    pressureLevel: number;
    developmentNotes: string[];
    resolved: boolean;
    surfacedCount?: number;
}

// ── Character Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

interface CharacterRecord {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    isProtagonist?: boolean;
    conceptDump?: string;
    nicknames: string[];

    // Biographical
    species?: string;
    age?: string;
    gender?: string;
    occupationRole?: string;
    briefDescription?: string;

    // Flags
    scenePresent: boolean;
    active: boolean;
    lorebookEntryId?: string;
    featuredForEphemeral: boolean;
    dynamicPriorityRank?: number;  // [vNEXT] 1-based manual priority; unset = unranked. Under a dynamic cap, lower rank = processed first. One character per rank (assignment swaps).

    // Static narrative fields (AI-generated or author-written, infrequently updated)
    contextTemplateOverride?: string;
    physicalDescription?: string;
    voiceAndMannerisms?: string;
    personalityCore?: string;
    background?: string;
    customFieldValues: Record<string, string>;
    coreValues: string[];

    // Image generation — visual identity fields (edited via NAI sidebar; read by bridge)
    // [CHANGE] Added to replace the old bridge roster's visual fields.
    // Bridge portrait keys are always derived as: char.id + '_portrait' and char.id + '_em_' + emotionId
    // [CHANGE] portraitKey removed — portrait lookup uses char.id directly
    visuals?: string;       // base character visual tags — applied in ALL generation modes
    uc?: string;            // base unwanted content — applied in ALL generation modes
    sceneVisuals?: string;  // additional tags for scene illustrations only (auto-snap, director's cut)
    avatarVisuals?: string; // additional tags for avatar portraits only (emotion images)
    avatarWidth?: number;   // avatar portrait width in pixels
    avatarHeight?: number;  // avatar portrait height in pixels
    avatarSeed?: number;    // avatar generation seed — 0 = random

    // [v2.24.0 CHARCHAT-PERSONA] Per-character texting register — STRUCTURED, not
    // generated. Every axis is a key into one of the CHARCHAT_*_OPTS tables, and the
    // whole thing compiles to ONE short clause that rides the next-message instruction
    // (buildCharChatPersonaClause). That is the reason it is structured rather than
    // free prose: the instruction block is terse and sits at the true bottom of
    // context, so a generated paragraph would both drown it and be re-paid every turn.
    // `notes` is the escape hatch for anything the axes can't say.
    // Still optional and absent by default — an absent/disabled persona means the
    // global CHARCHAT_DEFAULT_RUBRIC in the system prompt is the whole register, so
    // old saves need no migration fill.
    // [LEGACY v2.21.0] register/voice/content are the OLD freeform shape. Nothing
    // writes them anymore; buildCharChatPersonaClause still READS them so a character
    // authored against v2.21.0-v2.23.0 keeps working (they fold in as trailing notes).
    // Delete them once no story in the wild has one — flagged, not silently kept.
    chatPersona?: {
        enabled?: boolean;
        style?: string;      // key into CHARCHAT_STYLE_OPTS
        verbosity?: string;  // key into CHARCHAT_VERBOSITY_OPTS — also drives max_tokens
        emoji?: string;      // key into CHARCHAT_EMOJI_OPTS
        notes?: string;      // free-text extra clause, appended verbatim
        register?: string; voice?: string; content?: string;   // [LEGACY] read-only
    };

    // Dynamic state
    primaryRelationship: PrimaryRelationship;
    hooks: UnresolvedHook[];
    activeConditions: CharacterConditionState[];  // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] multiple can be active at once
    ephemeral: EphemeralSnapshot | null;
    ephemeralHistory: EphemeralSnapshot[];
    lastEphemeralGen: number;  // [v0.9.24 E1] was lastEphemeralTokens
    midRange: MidRangeState | null;
    midRangeHistory: MidRangeSnapshot[];         // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] change-gated trend trail
    relationshipHistory: RelationshipSnapshot[]; // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] change-gated trend trail
    lastMidrangeGen: number;   // was lastMidrangeTokens
    lastArcGen: number;        // was lastArcTokens
    lastHookGen: number;       // was lastHookTokens
    // [v2.54.0] Token-mode stamp companions to the *Gen fields above. Written alongside
    // the gen stamps on every update (each generator reads the current token counter at
    // persist time), so both units stay fresh and switching dynamicIntervalMode never
    // fires a spurious cycle from a stamp left in the wrong unit.
    lastEphemeralTokens?: number;
    lastMidrangeTokens?: number;
    lastArcTokens?: number;
    lastHookTokens?: number;

    // Equipment and resources
    equippedItemIds: string[];
    charFactionRelationships: Record<string, number>;

    // Engine flags
    charContextEnabled: boolean;
    autoUpdateEnabled: boolean;
    autoThoughtsEnabled: boolean;
    hooksEnabled: boolean;
    lastTokenEstimate: number;

    createdAt: number;
    updatedAt: number;
}

interface CharacterMeta {
    // Lightweight index entry for each character — used for list rendering and fast lookups.
    // [CHANGE] portraitKey removed — bridge derives portrait key from id directly.
    id: string;
    name: string;
    isProtagonist?: boolean;
    entityId?: string;
    hasCriticalHook?: boolean;
    scenePresent: boolean;
    active: boolean;
    briefDescription?: string;
    species?: string;
    age?: string;
    occupationRole?: string;
    currentStance?: string;       // mirrors midRange.socialMask
    lastThought?: string;         // mirrors ephemeral.compressedLine
    lastTokenEstimate?: number;
    currentLocationId?: string;
    equippedItemNames?: string[];
    isStatic?: boolean;            // [v0.9.31 DYN1] mirrors CharacterRecord.isStatic for card render
    dynamicPriorityRank?: number;  // [vNEXT] mirrors CharacterRecord.dynamicPriorityRank so the cap sorts without a storage read
}

// ── World-Building Support ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

interface CustomFieldDef { id: string; label: string; placeholder?: string; instructions: string; }

interface SpeciesDef {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    description: string;
    lorebookEntryId?: string;
    lorebookTags?: string[];
}

interface FactionDef {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    description: string;
    lorebookEntryId?: string;
    relationshipStates: string[];  // e.g. ['Hostile', 'Wary', 'Neutral', 'Friendly', 'Allied']
    neutralIndex?: number;         // the "unaffiliated"/baseline index — a character AT this index is
                                    // treated as having no meaningful standing (filtered from rosters/
                                    // display). Defaults to the middle state if undefined — see factionNeutralIndex().
    lorebookTags?: string[];
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] NpcArchetypeDef / ItemArchetypeDef interfaces removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.


// ── Entity World Graph ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] EntityConnection trimmed to awareness/adjacency only —
// requiresResourceTag/resourceCost/isSilent/requiresVehicle/travelActionId were
// all Actions/Travel-cost fields with no meaning once those systems are gone.
interface EntityConnection {
    targetId: string;
    label: string;
    oneWay?: boolean;
}

interface EntityRecord {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    type: 'location' | 'item' | 'narrative' | 'character';
    parentId: string | null;
    narrativeParentId?: string | null;
    storyState?: 'locked' | 'active' | 'completed';
    description: string;
    atmosphere: string;
    state: string;
    stateDescriptions: Record<string, string>;
    connectedEntityIds: EntityConnection[];
    presentCharacterIds: string[];
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] actions / actionInstances / rules / skillBonuses / showActionWidget
    // removed — Actions system cut.
    tagSubscriptions?: string[];           // [v0.9.86] library tag names whose templates are live-resolved at runtime
    isEquipped?: boolean;                  // [WP-5] item equip state
    equippedToCharId?: string;             // [WP-5] which character an equipped item is on
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] isVehicle/gated/entryCostTag/entryCostAmount/entryActionId removed — dead Actions/Travel remnants, no enforcement guard reads them anymore.
    inventoryIds?: string[];
    lorebookEntryId?: string;   // [WP-1] linked NAI lorebook entry id (entity profile)
    lorebookTags?: string[];    // [WP-1] extra trigger keys beyond the entity name
    lorebookText?: string;      // [WP-1] dedicated extended-lore body (NOT description/state)
    locationVisuals?: string;   // [WP-img] image tags for location-specific renders (gated; not the general scene prompt)
    questTarget?: string;
    eventDayTarget?: number;   // [v0.9.9.2] absolute day this narrative is due; drives the AN countdown when tracked
    icon?: string;           // emoji or short glyph shown in bridge entity pickers and map nodes
    createdAt: number;
    updatedAt: number;
}

interface EntityMeta {
    // Lightweight index entry for each entity node in the world graph.
    id: string;
    name: string;
    type: string;
    parentId: string | null;
    narrativeParentId?: string | null;
    storyState?: string;
    childCount: number;
    presentCharacterCount: number;
    state: string;
    icon?: string;           // propagated from EntityRecord for bridge display
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] gated/isVehicle removed from EntityMeta — dead Actions/Travel remnants.
}

// ── NPC Web and Knowledge ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

interface WebLink {
    // A bilateral dynamic relationship between two characters in the NPC web.
    id: string;
    charA_id: string;
    charB_id: string;
    dynamic: string;  // e.g. "Bitter rivals competing for the captain's approval."
    priority: number; // Default: 30. Higher pushes it deeper into the prompt.
}

// ── Global Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

interface ContextTemplates {
    // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] trackedNarrative/location/player/npcWeb/activeNarrative
    // removed — those injections (and the Drama tab editor UI for all of them) are
    // gone. Only the two AN survivors remain, still macro-parsed via
    // parseMacroTemplate() but with no UI exposing them for editing anymore.
    npc: string;           // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] per-character thoughts line — now ONLY the "Thinking:" line (relationship/mid-range moved to the character lorebook)
    criticalHook: string;  // 85%+ unresolved hook line, merged into the same AN block
    npcWeb: string;        // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] one bilateral NPC-web dynamic, bundled into the AN when both parties are scene-present
}

// [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Narrative-prose templates for the per-character dynamic block
// folded into that character's lorebook entry (buildCharDynamicBlock /
// buildSceneLorebookActivation): equipment, conditions, relationship, and mid-range.
// The NPC web (bilateral, two-character shared data) is deliberately NOT here — it
// stays in the AN (cfg.contextTemplates.npcWeb), injected once when both parties are
// present, to avoid the per-character double injection folding it here would cause.
// Deliberately a SEPARATE bucket from ContextTemplates: this feeds the lorebook, not
// the AN, and is authored in World Settings. Still macro-parsed via parseMacroTemplate().
interface LorebookTemplates {
    // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Narrative-prose lines for the per-character dynamic block
    // folded into that character's lorebook entry (buildCharDynamicBlock). Each is
    // macro-parsed via parseMacroTemplate(); empty [ ] blocks self-suppress.
    condition: string;      // one active condition -> one line, e.g. "X is currently Y. Z."
    equipment: string;      // worn/carried line; macros |name| |equipList|
    relationship: string;   // relationship archetype + target
    relTrajectory: string;  // where the relationship is heading
    relStatus: string;      // current relationship status
    goal: string;           // mid-range current desire
    socialMask: string;     // mid-range social mask (outward presentation)
    shiftedView: string;    // mid-range shifted view (changed belief)
    journal: string;        // [v2.12.0 CHARJOURNAL] this character's own journal entries; macro |journalBody|
}

// [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Per-field include switches for buildCharDynamicBlock. Each
// gates ONE narrative line; unchecking drops that field from the lorebook entry
// without touching the underlying character data. Authored in World Settings
// alongside the templates above.
interface LorebookFieldToggles {
    equipment: boolean;
    conditions: boolean;
    relationship: boolean;
    relTrajectory: boolean;
    relStatus: boolean;
    goal: boolean;
    socialMask: boolean;
    shiftedView: boolean;
    journal: boolean;   // [v2.12.0 CHARJOURNAL]
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: Memory Sections ──────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] The wizard builds the static Memory block from an ordered list of named
// sections (Setting Name, Style, Themes, Conflict, ...). Each section is generated
// in its own scoped pass (so they never bleed into each other), reviewed/edited by
// the user, then assembled and committed ONCE to NAI memory (or, for Style, to the
// Author's Note). Memory is treated as static world canon — committed at build time,
// not rewritten during play (cache stability). The user may re-commit at will; the
// engine does not block it.
//   target 'memory' -> assembled into the Memory block as "[ Label ]\n<content>"
//   target 'an'     -> committed to the Author's Note instead (e.g. Style)
interface WizardMemorySection {
    id: string;
    label: string;          // section heading, e.g. "Themes"
    enabled: boolean;       // unchecked sections are omitted from the assembled block
    content: string;        // the editable generated body (blank -> omitted even if enabled)
    instructions: string;   // hard scope guidance for the per-section generator
    target: 'memory' | 'an';
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: Chats + Topics ───────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] The wizard is a set of topic-scoped chats (modeled on Story Engine's
// multi-chat records). Each chat is a transcript on one WizardTopic. The user can
// keep several chats, bounce between them, choose which OTHER chats / lorebook
// entries / the story each chat pulls in as context, and "commit" a chat to its
// topic (which routes by the topic's commitKind — e.g. memory-section fan-out,
// the Author's Note, or the projectConcept field).
//
// This is the DATA layer only: records, the topic registry, storage, and CRUD.
// Generation (chat turns + the summarize/commit passes) is layered on separately.
interface WizardChatMessage {
    role: 'user' | 'assistant';
    content: string;
}

// commitKind routes what "commit this chat" does:
//   'concept'      -> writes/refreshes cfg.projectConcept (the general context chat)
//   'memoryFanout' -> runs per-section scoped generation over cfg.wizardSections,
//                     then assembles + commits Memory (and AN for Style sections)
//   'note'         -> reserved: a single free section committed straight to memory/AN
// Entity-producing topics (characters, factions, locations, ...) get their own
// commitKinds in later phases — kept open as a string so the registry is extensible.
type WizardCommitKind = 'concept' | 'memoryFanout' | 'note' | string;

interface WizardTopic {
    id: string;
    label: string;
    instructions: string;     // the chat persona / scope — parallels field-gen instructions
    commitKind: WizardCommitKind;
    seedMessage?: string;     // [vNEXT] optional first assistant turn, seeded into new chats on this topic
}

// [v1.23.0 DISTILL] Cached distillation of a chat's transcript into declarative world
// facts. Regenerated only when the chat has grown past sourceMsgCount, so a linked chat
// costs one extra generation per growth spurt rather than one per batch generation.
interface WizardNotesCache {
    text: string;
    sourceMsgCount: number;   // chat.messages.length when this was distilled
    updatedAt: number;
}
interface WizardChat {
    id: string;
    topicId: string;
    title: string;
    messages: WizardChatMessage[];
    contextChatIds: string[];      // other wizard chats fed in as read-only context
    includeStory: boolean;         // pull recent story text as context
    lorebookEntryIds: string[];    // lorebook entries fed in as context
    characterIds?: string[];       // [v0.9.99] characters fed in as context (chat-owned, independent of global charContextEnabled)
    notesCache?: WizardNotesCache; // [v1.23.0] see WizardNotesCache
    createdAt: number;
    updatedAt: number;
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] TierTravelDefault removed — travel-tier cost/narration cut with Travel.

interface GlobalConfig {
    // Engine timing intervals (in tokens)
    contextTemplates: ContextTemplates;
    lorebookTemplates: LorebookTemplates;  // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS]
    lorebookFields: LorebookFieldToggles;  // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] per-field include switches
    interfaceMode?: string;        // [v0.9.9.0] 'bridge' | 'native' | 'both' — which HUD renderer(s) mount
    thoughtIntervalGens: number;   // [v0.9.24 E1] generations between ephemeral updates
    dynamicIntervalGens: number;   // generations between mid-range updates
    arcIntervalGens: number;       // generations between arc/relationship updates
    hookIntervalGens: number;      // generations between hook updates
    // [v2.54.0] Optional token-based cadence. When dynamicIntervalMode === 'tokens', the
    // dynamic engine measures the *IntervalTokens fields against a persistent story token
    // counter instead of the *IntervalGens fields against the generation counter. Default
    // 'gens' — the token fields are only consulted in token mode.
    dynamicIntervalMode?: 'gens' | 'tokens';
    thoughtIntervalTokens?: number; // context tokens between ephemeral updates (token mode)
    dynamicIntervalTokens?: number; // tokens between mid-range updates
    arcIntervalTokens?: number;     // tokens between arc/relationship updates
    hookIntervalTokens?: number;    // tokens between hook updates
    totalGenerations: number;      // persistent story-level generation counter
    ephemeralCap: number;
    maxDynamicCharacters?: number; // [vNEXT] cap on present characters processed as dynamic per turn (0/undefined = no cap). Ranked chars fill slots first (by rank), then unranked present-dynamic chars.

    // Character sheet
    sheetCategories: SheetCategory[];

    // Defaults for new characters
    autoUpdateDefault: boolean;
    autoThoughtsDefault: boolean;
    showFieldHeaders: boolean;

    // Style
    generalStyle: string;

    // Field customisation
    fieldLabels: Record<string, string>;
    fieldInstructions: Record<string, string>;
    fieldBudgets: Record<string, number>;
    customStaticFields: CustomFieldDef[];

    // Presets
    relationshipArchetypes: RelationshipArchetypeDef[];
    conditions: ConditionDef[];  // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS]

    // Global resources and factions
    species: SpeciesDef[];
    factions: FactionDef[];
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] factionResourceDefs / factionResourceSections removed — Faction
    // Resources cut. tierTravelDefaults removed — Travel cut. stakeDefs / actionTemplates
    // removed — Actions cut.
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] npcArchetypes/itemArchetypes removed from GlobalConfig — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.
    defaultBackgroundUrl?: string;  // [v0.9.40] module-specific scene default background URL

    // [vNEXT] World-Build Wizard — concept fields the user seeds up front (title/genre/
    // initial idea) plus the ordered Memory-block section list the wizard assembles.
    projectName?: string;
    projectGenre?: string;
    projectConcept?: string;        // the user's initial idea; chat starts from this
    wizardSections?: WizardMemorySection[];
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] holdingDef removed — Holding cut.

    // [v2.27.0 CHARCHAT-FRESH-THOUGHTS, per user, REPLACES v2.24.0 CHARCHAT-THINK
    // outright — see the CE-INDEX entry for why] Character Chat: refresh the contact's
    // ephemeral snapshot via generateEphemeral() — the SAME generator the story's
    // dynamic engine uses, not a second bespoke implementation — right after each
    // reply, so buildCharChatInstruction's existing "right now" line is never more
    // than one turn stale for the FOLLOWING reply. [v2.29.0] Originally fired before
    // the reply instead; moved to onGenerationEnd after testing showed the pre-reply
    // timing fails outright (NAI rejects a nested api.v1.generate() call while the
    // reply's own generation is still in progress) — see onGenerationEnd's CHARCHAT
    // branch for the full writeup. Unlike the old inline "[Thinking: ...]" line this
    // IS persisted (char.ephemeral / char.ephemeralHistory) and viewable, and it costs
    // a separate GLM call rather than sharing the reply's own token budget. Optional;
    // undefined reads as off (an extra call per turn is opt-in), so no migration fill
    // needed. old charChatThinking is a dead orphan field on any pre-v2.27.0 save.
    charChatFreshThoughts?: boolean;

    // [FEATURE v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-IMG] Chat image attachments. When on, an
    // "[Image: <description>]" tag written by EITHER side of a chat is caught after the
    // turn lands, run through a chat-scoped architect pass, and dispatched to the bridge
    // for generation. Optional; undefined reads as off — per user, auto-with-a-toggle,
    // and off by default because each image costs a REAL architect generation
    // (api.v1.generate, unlike the chat turn itself which is free — empirically
    // confirmed via the probe: script output budget does not move across a native
    // editor.generate() reply) plus an image generation in the bridge.
    charChatImages?: boolean;
}

// ── Image Settings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// [v2.13.0 IMGFIELDS] Replaces the old 5-slot ImagePreset system entirely. Click-
// to-add fields: each has its own base-prompt/UC fragment and an optional weight,
// toggled active/inactive independently (any number can be active at once — not
// a single-select "active preset" anymore). Active fields compile into the final
// prompt/UC at generation time (see compileActiveImageFields). Global storage
// (user's call — reusable across every story, matching where presets already
// lived). Starts EMPTY on a fresh install — no migration from old preset tags.

interface ImagePromptField {
    id: string;
    name: string;        // display name, e.g. "Cinematic Quality"
    basePrompt: string;  // positive prompt fragment
    uc: string;           // negative/UC fragment
    weight: number;      // 0.1-10; 1 = unweighted (compiled plain, no wrapping)
    active: boolean;     // click-to-activate/deactivate; compiled in when true
}

interface GlobalImageSettings {
    autoDownload: boolean;
    contextSize: number;         // number of story paragraphs fed to the architect prompt
    architectPrompt: string;     // GLM system prompt for image prompt generation
    architectAutoSnap: boolean;  // whether to run architect pass on auto-snap
    defaultWidth: number;        // [v2.13.0] ratio now lives on the Image Studio widget; this is
    defaultHeight: number;       //           the persisted last-used/default value it reads from
    defaultScale: number;        // [v2.13.0] prompt guidance scale; widget field, default 5 if blank
    defaultSteps: number;        // [v2.13.0] no per-generation field (user: "Steps are set to 28") —
                                  //           this is the editable default, kept in the field-manager
                                  //           settings area rather than removed outright
}

// ── Bridge Payload Shapes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// [CHANGE] New types documenting the data shapes sent from engine to bridge.
// These are broadcast via broadcastToTM and received by handleEngineMessage.

interface SceneCharacterInfo {
    // Compact character snapshot included in SCENE_STATE_UPDATE.
    id: string;
    name: string;
    entityId: string;
    briefDescription: string;
    socialMask: string;       // midRange.socialMask — what they are performing
    compressedLine: string;   // ephemeral.compressedLine — immediate state line
    isProtagonist: boolean;
    isFollowing?: boolean;         // [v0.9.31 FOLLOW1] entity body is under protagonist body
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] SceneActionInfo / SceneInstanceInfo removed — Actions system cut.
interface SceneStatePayload {
    // Full payload sent as EVT_SCENE_STATE after any state-changing operation.
    // Bridge renders this into the Scene HUD.
    locationId: string;
    locationName: string;
    locationDescription: string;
    presentCharacters: SceneCharacterInfo[];
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] quickInstances field removed — QuickInstance type no longer exists.
    locationGraph?: any;          // graph of current location: parent, children, connections — awareness only, no travel-cost annotations
    factionRelationships?: { id: string; name: string; states: string[]; stateIdx: number; stateLabel: string; }[];   // the player's OWN faction standings (charFactionRelationships), not a separate party-level ladder
    presentItems?: { id: string; name: string; icon?: string; }[];  // item entities at current location
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.x] presentMinorNpcs removed — minor-NPC tier retired, everyone is a normal character now.
    // NOTE: bridge still reads state.presentMinorNpcs at three sites (Section 6b "Present NPCs"
    // render, two target-selector populators) — degrades gracefully to empty array via its own
    // `|| []` guards, but won't show these characters until the bridge pass picks this up.
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] npcArchetypes/itemArchetypes removed from SceneStatePayload — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.
    defaultBgUrl?: string;          // [v0.9.40] cfg.defaultBackgroundUrl forwarded to bridge for the location banner
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] clock field removed — Clock/Calendar cut.
}

interface CharacterVisualRecord {
    // Per-character visual data payload sent as EVT_CHAR_DATA_SYNC.
    // Bridge uses this as the source of truth for image generation character matching.
    id: string;
    name: string;
    nicknames: string[];
    // Visual identity fields
    visuals: string;
    uc: string;
    sceneVisuals: string;
    avatarVisuals: string;
    avatarWidth: number;
    avatarHeight: number;
    avatarSeed: number;
    // Narrative identity fields (used to build chat persona — replaces separate roster persona)
    species: string;
    age: string;
    gender: string;
    briefDescription: string;
    physicalDescription: string;
    personalityCore: string;
    voiceAndMannerisms: string;
    background: string;
}

// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

interface ImportMergeCounts { added: number; updated: number; }

interface ImportResult {
    charCount: number;
    entityCount: number;
    configImported: boolean;
    configCategoryCounts?: { [category: string]: ImportMergeCounts };  // [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] per-array upsert tallies, e.g. actionTemplates: {added:1,updated:2}
    npcWebImported: boolean;
    npcWebCounts?: ImportMergeCounts;   // [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE]
    partyStateImported: boolean;
}

// ── Bridge Event Name Constants ───────────────────────────────────────────────
// [CHANGE] New. Every engine→bridge and bridge→engine event name is defined here.
// Use these constants everywhere — never raw strings — so typos fail loudly at
// author time rather than silently at runtime.

// Engine → Bridge (outbound from engine, received by bridge handleEngineMessage)
const EVT_SCENE_STATE    = 'SCENE_STATE_UPDATE';      // SceneStatePayload — HUD rerender
const EVT_CHAR_DATA_SYNC = 'CHARACTER_DATA_SYNC';     // CharacterVisualRecord[] — roster update
const EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE = 'GENERATE_IMAGE';           // image payload — bridge generates and displays
const EVT_CLICK_GEN      = 'CLICK_GENERATE_NOW';      // tells bridge to click the NAI Generate button
const EVT_STORY_ID_SYNC  = 'STORY_ID_SYNC';           // current story ID after handshake
const EVT_OPEN_ROSTER    = 'OPEN_ROSTER';             // tells bridge to open the character roster
const EVT_SHOW_GALLERY   = 'SHOW_GALLERY';            // tells bridge to open the image gallery
const EVT_HANDSHAKE_REQ  = 'HANDSHAKE_REQUEST';       // engine announces its script ID to bridge
const EVT_PONG           = 'PONG';                    // response to bridge PING

// Bridge → Engine (inbound from bridge, received by handleBridgeMessage)
const EVT_HANDSHAKE_ACK   = 'HANDSHAKE_ACK';           // bridge confirms connection
const EVT_PING            = 'PING';                    // bridge keepalive check
const EVT_CHAR_SYNC_PUSH  = 'CHAR_CONFIGS_SYNC';       // bridge pushes character configs (legacy path)
const EVT_TTS_SPEAK       = 'TTS_SPEAK';               // bridge requests NAI TTS for chat response
const EVT_CHAT_SUM_REQ    = 'CHAT_SUMMARIZE_REQUEST';  // bridge requests GLM summary of chat history
const EVT_RUN_ACTION_INSTANCE = 'RUN_ACTION_INSTANCE'; // [WP-5] run a new-system ActionInstance (bridge/sidebar)
const EVT_AUTOSNAP        = 'AUTO_SNAP';               // [WP-img] bridge ⚙️ button → engine runs executeAutoSnap
const EVT_AUTOSNAP_PROMPT = 'AUTO_SNAP_PROMPT';        // [v0.9.8.0] Image Studio: architect builds a prompt, returned (not generated)
const EVT_DIRECTOR_SUBMIT = 'DIRECTOR_CUT_SUBMIT';     // bridge submits a manual director's cut snap
const EVT_REQ_SCENE_STATE = 'REQUEST_SCENE_STATE';     // bridge requests an immediate scene state push
const EVT_REQ_CHAR_DATA   = 'REQUEST_CHAR_DATA';       // bridge requests an immediate character data push
const EVT_IMPORT_WORLD    = 'AE_IMPORT_WORLD';         // bridge→engine: deliver a parsed module to importPayload()
const EVT_DEMO_FAILED     = 'AE_DEMO_FAILED';          // bridge→engine: demo fetch failed, seed a fresh story

// [v0.9.93 PROFILE] Combined character profile — the cohesive HUD review/tune surface.
// Separate from the cheap scene-detail path (buildEntityDetailPayload) so the heavy
// editable record is only built when a profile is actually open. Native editor is NOT
// replaced; this is additive.
const EVT_CHAR_PROFILE        = 'AE_CHAR_PROFILE_RESPONSE'; // engine→bridge: full editable character record
const EVT_FETCH_CHAR_PROFILE  = 'AE_FETCH_CHAR_PROFILE';    // bridge→engine: request a character's profile
const EVT_SAVE_CHAR_FIELD     = 'AE_SAVE_CHAR_FIELD';       // bridge→engine: write one field back to the record
const EVT_REGEN_CHAR_FIELD    = 'AE_REGEN_CHAR_FIELD';      // bridge→engine: regen one narrative field from the concept dump

// [v2.72.0 SLOT-OPTIONS] Pick-from-N generation for a single field. Distinct from
// EVT_REGEN_CHAR_FIELD, which writes one value straight onto the record: this one
// GENERATES NOTHING PERSISTENT — it returns candidates and the bridge decides. The
// write still goes through AE_SAVE_CHAR_FIELD, so there is exactly one write path.
const EVT_SLOT_OPTIONS        = 'AE_SLOT_OPTIONS';          // engine→bridge: N candidate values for one field
const EVT_GEN_SLOT_OPTIONS    = 'AE_GEN_SLOT_OPTIONS';      // bridge→engine: request N candidates for one field

// [v0.9.97 WIZHUD] World-Build Wizard surfaced into the HUD (chat -> review -> commit).
// Engine owns wizard state + generation; bridge renders the chat and context picker.
const EVT_WIZ_STATE           = 'AE_WIZ_STATE';            // engine→bridge: full wizard snapshot (topics, chats, active chat)
const EVT_WIZ_REPLY           = 'AE_WIZ_REPLY';            // engine→bridge: a chat turn finished (ok/err); fresh state follows
const EVT_WIZ_CONTEXT_SOURCES = 'AE_WIZ_CONTEXT_SOURCES';  // engine→bridge: selectable context sources for a chat
const EVT_WIZ_FETCH_STATE     = 'AE_WIZ_FETCH_STATE';      // bridge→engine: request the snapshot
const EVT_WIZ_NEW_CHAT        = 'AE_WIZ_NEW_CHAT';         // bridge→engine: create a chat under a topic

// [v2.66.0 DIAG-CMD] Surface-independent diagnostic console — reaches the engine
// directly from the bridge's window.CE_ENGINE.cmd() console helper, regardless of
// what's open in the story (no NAI Send, no budget cost, no chat dependency).
// See aeHandleDiagCommand (SECTION 3) for the actual /log and /diag command set.

const EVT_WIZ_EDIT_MSG        = 'AE_WIZ_EDIT_MSG';         // bridge→engine: edit a message in place
const EVT_WIZ_DELETE_MSG      = 'AE_WIZ_DELETE_MSG';       // bridge→engine: delete a message

const EVT_WIZ_GEN_RESULT      = 'AE_WIZ_GEN_RESULT';       // engine→bridge: generate/commit finished {kind,count,ok}
// [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] Hard mode switch: while on, story generation is blocked and yoink
// output is unconditionally swallowed regardless of continuityId match (defense in
// depth on top of the existing hijack). Driven by the wizard tab/modal being open.
const EVT_WIZ_MODE            = 'AE_WIZ_MODE';             // bridge→engine: { on: boolean } — wizard tab opened/closed
const EVT_WIZ_MODE_STATE      = 'AE_WIZ_MODE_STATE';       // engine→bridge: { active, draining } — for a status toast/banner
const EVT_WIZLIVE_START       = 'AE_WIZLIVE_START';        // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] bridge→engine: { topicId? } — open an in-document wizard chat
const EVT_WIZLIVE_END         = 'AE_WIZLIVE_END';          // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] bridge→engine: { action: 'save'|'discard' }  ([v2.5.0] commit/summarize retired — save upserts to the CE Chats lorebook)

const EVT_WIZLIVE_STATE       = 'AE_WIZLIVE_STATE';        // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] engine→bridge: { active, topicId, startedAt }
// [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] In-story character chat. Deliberately a SEPARATE family from
// AE_WIZLIVE_* (same shape, different namespace — the two will diverge). Engine-side
// only as of v2.21.0: the bridge has no handler for any of these yet (that is P6).
const EVT_CHARCHAT_START      = 'AE_CHARCHAT_START';       // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] bridge→engine: { contactId, povId? } — open an in-story chat with a character
const EVT_CHARCHAT_END        = 'AE_CHARCHAT_END';         // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT; comment corrected v2.55.0] bridge→engine: { action: 'save'|'discard' }. BOTH ship: 'save' (Close) writes the thread to the CE Character Chats lorebook and strips the paragraphs; 'discard' (Clear) deletes the saved thread and strips the paragraphs. (The old "P1 discard-only / save arrives in P4" note was stale — endCharChat has handled both since v2.22.0.)
const EVT_CHARCHAT_SUMMARIZE  = 'AE_CHARCHAT_SUMMARIZE';   // [v2.55.0 CHARCHAT-MANAGER, per user: bridge chat-manager "summarize" button] bridge→engine: {} — run previewSummarizeCharChat() and open the engine's own editable summary modal (_charchatShowSummaryModal). Deliberately NOT an EVT_CHARCHAT_END action: summarize is a preview that KEEPS the chat open. Fire-and-forget — nothing crosses back to the bridge; the preview lives in the engine-side modal, same as the native Chat-button path already does.
const EVT_CHARCHAT_FETCH      = 'AE_CHARCHAT_FETCH';       // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] bridge→engine: request a state broadcast
const EVT_CHARCHAT_STATE      = 'AE_CHARCHAT_STATE';       // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT, payload extended v2.32.0, lastThought added v2.35.0] engine→bridge: { active, contactId, contactName, povId, povName, startedAt, lastEmotion, lastThought, turns[], images, freshThoughts }. turns[] = [{ ids[], isPov, speaker, body, emotion, images[{uuid,desc,pending}], raw }] — see charChatTurnsPayload. lastThought = the contact's char.ephemeral.compressedLine ('' when none) — read-only, the broadcast never generates one.
// [v2.51.5 CHARCHAT-THREADS, per user: bridge HUD Chat launcher] Contact-list message
// counts for the bridge's new in-flow Chat view (the phone-contacts picker). A thin
// wire wrapper over the EXISTING listCharChatThreads() — no new business logic, no new
// storage; the native picker already reads the same function. Fetched once per Chat-view
// open (bridge caches it; POV switching filters in memory, exactly as the native picker
// does), so this is not a hot path.
const EVT_CHARCHAT_THREADS_REQ  = 'AE_CHARCHAT_THREADS_REQ';  // [v2.51.5 CHARCHAT-THREADS] bridge→engine: {} — request the saved-thread roster
const EVT_CHARCHAT_THREADS_RESP = 'AE_CHARCHAT_THREADS_RESP'; // [v2.51.5 CHARCHAT-THREADS] engine→bridge: { threads: [{ contactId, povId, turnCount }] } — one record per (contactId, povId) pair, straight from listCharChatThreads(); entryId is intentionally NOT sent (the bridge never resolves an entry id itself — it sends AE_CHARCHAT_START { contactId, povId } and the engine re-derives the deterministic id)
const EVT_ENGINE_THINKING     = 'AE_ENGINE_THINKING';       // [FEATURE v2.51.4 ENGINE-THINKING-SIGNAL, per user: "an indicator for any of our thinking and image generation pauses"] engine→bridge: { on: boolean }. Reference-counted engine-side (see _aeThinkingPulse) so overlapping generations (a main reply + a nested ephemeral/architect probe) don't have one's end turn the indicator off under the other. Drives bridge's _aeSetEngineThinking (the GHOST-SIGNATURE toggle-button glyph) — but that function is ALSO called directly by the bridge's own image-generation code, independent of this event, since image gen there is not always engine-initiated.

// (the old set assumed a second-person text-adventure with a player-protagonist).
// Memory-target sections assemble into the static Memory block IN LIST ORDER; the two
// AN-target sections (ATTG, Style) sit last and assemble into the Author's Note
// (visually lower in context). This is the default output order — users reorder freely
// in the Memory tab. Sections past Central Conflict start disabled.
var DEFAULT_WIZARD_SECTIONS: WizardMemorySection[] = [
    { id: 'wsec_premise', label: 'Premise', enabled: true, content: '', target: 'memory',
      instructions: 'Write ONE sentence \u2014 the story\'s logline: who the central figure is, the situation they are in, and what is at stake. Specific to this story, not a theme and not a question. Do not assume a point of view or address the reader. Register to aim for: "A disgraced navigator hired to chart waters her old commander declared unmappable." "A woman who stayed in the house after the divorce because leaving would mean admitting it mattered."' },
    { id: 'wsec_setting', label: 'Setting', enabled: true, content: '', target: 'memory',
      instructions: 'State the world, place, and era the story sits in, and the scope it occupies within that world. The facts of the stage \u2014 not plot, not characters, not theme. One or two sentences.' },
    { id: 'wsec_themes', label: 'Themes', enabled: true, content: '', target: 'memory',
      instructions: 'Name the emotional register and the throughlines the story keeps returning to. Abstractions, not events. Do not restate the setting or describe specific characters.' },
    { id: 'wsec_conflict', label: 'Central Conflict', enabled: true, content: '', target: 'memory',
      instructions: 'Describe the opposing forces and what is contested between them \u2014 the shape of the pressure, not its resolution and not the individual beats.' },
    { id: 'wsec_worldrules', label: 'World Rules', enabled: false, content: '', target: 'memory',
      instructions: 'Describe how this world works where it departs from the ordinary: the powers, technologies, or social orders in play, what they make possible, and what they cost. Rules, not instances.' },

      instructions: 'Describe how attraction, desire, and intimacy operate in this world and what tends to follow from them \u2014 the tone and the stakes of closeness, not a specific pairing.' },
    { id: 'wsec_danger', label: 'Danger and Consequences', enabled: false, content: '', target: 'memory',
      instructions: 'Describe how harm, violence, and danger operate in this world and what their aftermath looks like \u2014 how lethal, how lasting, how the narrative treats it. Not a specific confrontation.' },

Pick a well-known author whose sensibility fits this story. Output only the bracketed line \u2014 no heading, no commentary, no markdown.' },

Then describe the resulting voice concretely: sentence rhythm, level of description, use of interiority, how the prose handles emotion. Under 100 words. No markdown.' }
];

// ── World-Build Wizard: chat storage + CRUD (storyStorage) ───────────────────
async function loadWizardChats(): Promise<WizardChat[]> {
    let raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZ_CHATS, '[]') as string;
    try {
        let arr = JSON.parse(raw);
        if (Array.isArray(arr)) { for (let mc of arr) { if (!mc.characterIds) mc.characterIds = []; } return arr; }
        return [];
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] loadWizardChats parse error:', e);
        return [];
    }
}

async function saveWizardChats(chats: WizardChat[]): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZ_CHATS, JSON.stringify(chats || []));
}

async function loadWizardChat(id: string): Promise<WizardChat | null> {
    let chats = await loadWizardChats();
    return chats.find(function(c) { return c.id === id; }) || null;
}

async function saveWizardChat(chat: WizardChat): Promise<void> {
    if (!chat || !chat.id) return;
    let chats = await loadWizardChats();
    let idx = chats.findIndex(function(c) { return c.id === chat.id; });
    chat.updatedAt = Date.now();
    if (idx >= 0) chats[idx] = chat;
    else chats.push(chat);
    await saveWizardChats(chats);
}

async function createWizardChat(topicId: string, title: string): Promise<WizardChat> {
    // [vNEXT] Seed the topic's opening message (if any) so a new chat greets the user
    // and explains what this topic produces.
    let seedMsgs: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    try {
        let seedCfg = await loadConfig();
        let seedTopic = getWizardTopic(seedCfg, topicId);
        if (seedTopic && (seedTopic as any).seedMessage && String((seedTopic as any).seedMessage).trim()) {
            seedMsgs.push({ role: 'assistant', content: String((seedTopic as any).seedMessage).trim() });
        }
    } catch (eSeed) { /* non-fatal: start empty */ }
    let chat: WizardChat = {
        id: 'wchat_' + generateId(),
        topicId: topicId,
        title: title || 'New Chat',
        messages: seedMsgs,
        contextChatIds: [],
        includeStory: false,
        lorebookEntryIds: [],
        characterIds: [],
        createdAt: Date.now(),
        updatedAt: Date.now()
    };
    await saveWizardChat(chat);
    return chat;
}

async function deleteWizardChat(id: string): Promise<void> {
    let chats = await loadWizardChats();
    chats = chats.filter(function(c) { return c.id !== id; });
    await saveWizardChats(chats);
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: staging store (storyStorage) ─────────────────────────
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Generalizes the generate -> review -> commit pattern that
// previously existed only for the intro draft (generateIntroDraft/commitIntroDraft).
// Batch generators now stage parsed output as WizardDraft records; nothing touches
// cfg.* or the lorebook until commitStagedDrafts() runs. This means a bad generation
// (CJK drift, dot-point mush, half-parsed entries) can be discarded without leaving
// orphan records and orphan lorebook entries behind.
//
// kind is one of: 'species' | 'faction' | 'archetype' | 'item' | 'location'
// fields is a flat string map so one store and one editor serve every kind; the
// per-kind shape lives in WIZ_DRAFT_FIELDS below and commit routes on kind.
interface WizardDraft {
    id: string;
    kind: string;
    fields: { [k: string]: string };
    sourceChatIds: string[];
    parentId: string | null;   // locations only; null for every other kind
    createdAt: number;
}

// [v1.22.2] WizardTombstone REMOVED — see the note above discardWizardDraft.

// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Per-kind field schema. Drives the staging editor's inputs and the
// commit routing. `multiline` selects textInput vs multilineTextInput in the native UI.
var WIZ_DRAFT_FIELDS: { [kind: string]: { key: string; label: string; multiline: boolean }[] } = {
    species:   [{ key: 'name', label: 'Name', multiline: false }, { key: 'description', label: 'Lore', multiline: true }],
    faction:   [{ key: 'name', label: 'Name', multiline: false }, { key: 'description', label: 'Lore', multiline: true }],
    archetype: [{ key: 'name', label: 'Name', multiline: false }, { key: 'brief', label: 'Brief', multiline: true }],
    item:      [{ key: 'name', label: 'Name', multiline: false }, { key: 'description', label: 'Description', multiline: true }],
    location:  [{ key: 'name', label: 'Name', multiline: false }, { key: 'description', label: 'On arrival', multiline: true }, { key: 'lore', label: 'Lorebook', multiline: true }],
    // [v1.22.2 CHAR-STAGE] Characters previously went chat -> names -> dump -> fields ->
    // saveCharacter in one unbroken run, so a failed dump/fields call silently produced a
    // character with nothing but a name. Staging the name + concept dump gives the user a
    // visible, editable checkpoint; the structured fields are derived at commit time from
    // whatever dump is on screen then.
    character: [{ key: 'name', label: 'Name', multiline: false }, { key: 'dump', label: 'Concept', multiline: true }]
};

// Human-readable plural per kind, for toasts and headers.
function wizKindLabel(kind: string): string {
    if (kind === 'species')   return 'species';
    if (kind === 'faction')   return 'factions';
    if (kind === 'archetype') return 'archetypes';
    if (kind === 'item')      return 'items';
    if (kind === 'location')  return 'locations';
    if (kind === 'character') return 'characters';
    return kind;
}

// [v1.22.3 TEXTAREA-SIZE] Native multilineTextInput has no auto-grow and no `rows` prop
// (only `style`), and the Worker sandbox has no DOM to measure with. Read-mode bubbles
// use type:'text', which grows to fit; swapping in a fixed-minHeight textarea therefore
// COLLAPSES a long message into a small scrolling box. Estimate the needed height from
// the content up front so the editor opens at roughly the size the text already occupied.
//
// ~46 chars per visual line at fontSize 11-12px in the sidebar's width; 16px per line.
// Clamped so a one-liner does not get a giant box and an essay does not fill the screen.
function wizTextareaHeight(text: string, minPx: number, maxPx: number): string {
    let t = String(text || '');
    let lines = 0;
    let paras = t.split('\n');
    for (let p of paras) {
        lines += Math.max(1, Math.ceil(p.length / 46));
    }
    let px = 20 + (lines * 16);
    if (px < minPx) px = minPx;
    if (px > maxPx) px = maxPx;
    return px + 'px';
}

async function loadWizardDrafts(): Promise<WizardDraft[]> {
    let raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZ_DRAFTS, '[]') as string;
    try {
        let arr = JSON.parse(raw);
        if (Array.isArray(arr)) return arr;
        return [];
    } catch (eWdLoad) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] loadWizardDrafts parse error:', eWdLoad);
        return [];
    }
}

async function saveWizardDrafts(drafts: WizardDraft[]): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZ_DRAFTS, JSON.stringify(drafts || []));
}

async function loadWizardDraftsOfKind(kind: string): Promise<WizardDraft[]> {
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    return all.filter(function(d) { return d.kind === kind; });
}

// Appends parsed generator output as drafts. Returns the ids created, so a caller that
// generated-and-wants-to-commit-immediately (the legacy run*Batch wrappers) can commit
// exactly its own drafts without sweeping up anything the user already had staged.
async function addWizardDrafts(kind: string, items: { [k: string]: string }[], sourceChatIds: string[], parentId: string | null): Promise<string[]> {
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    let newIds: string[] = [];
    for (let it of (items || [])) {
        let did = 'wdraft_' + generateId();
        newIds.push(did);
        all.push({
            id: did,
            kind: kind,
            fields: it,
            sourceChatIds: (sourceChatIds || []).slice(),
            parentId: parentId || null,
            createdAt: Date.now()
        });
    }
    await saveWizardDrafts(all);
    return newIds;
}

async function updateWizardDraftField(draftId: string, fieldKey: string, value: string): Promise<void> {
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    let target = all.find(function(d) { return d.id === draftId; });
    if (!target) return;
    target.fields[fieldKey] = value;
    await saveWizardDrafts(all);
}

// [v1.22.2] Tombstones REMOVED. v1.22.0 recorded discarded draft names and fed them into
// each generator's "these ALREADY EXIST — do NOT recreate" list. That was wrong: a
// rejected draft is not an existing entity. If the user staged "Hogwarts", discarded it
// because the lore was bad, and regenerated, the prompt then explicitly forbade the one
// name the brainstorming chat was about — so the model invented unrelated originals
// instead. Discarding now simply deletes the draft. Only staged (still-pending) names
// feed the do-not-recreate list, which is all that was ever needed: it stops a second
// Generate press from duplicating the batch already sitting on screen.

// Discard one staged draft.
async function discardWizardDraft(draftId: string): Promise<void> {
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    await saveWizardDrafts(all.filter(function(d) { return d.id !== draftId; }));
}

// Discard every staged draft of a kind.
async function discardAllWizardDrafts(kind: string): Promise<number> {
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    let doomed = all.filter(function(d) { return d.kind === kind; });
    await saveWizardDrafts(all.filter(function(d) { return d.kind !== kind; }));
    return doomed.length;
}

// Remove drafts by id (used after a successful commit).
async function removeWizardDrafts(draftIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
    if (!draftIds || !draftIds.length) return;
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    await saveWizardDrafts(all.filter(function(d) { return draftIds.indexOf(d.id) < 0; }));
}

// [vNEXT] Export World-Build Wizard chat transcripts to a JSON file the user can
// save. Reuses the same api.v1.file.save pattern as the world export (fileDownload
// permission is requested at init). scope 'all' dumps every chat; scope 'one' dumps
// just the chat with the given id. Each chat carries its topic label (resolved from
// the registry) for readability. Returns the number of chats written (0 = nothing to
// export / failure), and surfaces its own toasts.
async function exportWizardChats(scope: 'all' | 'one', chatId?: string): Promise<number> {
    try {
        const cfg = await loadConfig();
        let chats = await loadWizardChats();
        if (scope === 'one') {
            chats = chats.filter(function(c) { return c.id === chatId; });
        }
        if (!chats.length) {
            api.v1.ui.toast('No wizard chats to export.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2500 });
            return 0;
        }

        // Attach a human-readable topic label to each exported chat without mutating
        // the stored records.
        const exportChats = chats.map(function(c) {
            const topic = getWizardTopic(cfg, c.topicId);
            return {
                id: c.id,
                title: c.title,
                topicId: c.topicId,
                topicLabel: topic ? topic.label : '',
                includeStory: !!c.includeStory,
                contextChatIds: c.contextChatIds || [],
                lorebookEntryIds: c.lorebookEntryIds || [],
                characterIds: (c as any).characterIds || [],
                createdAt: c.createdAt,
                updatedAt: c.updatedAt,
                messages: (c.messages || []).map(function(m) { return { role: m.role, content: m.content }; })
            };
        });

        const payload = {
            _aeWizardChats: true,
            aeVersion: '0.9.9.2',
            exportedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
            projectName: (cfg as any).projectName || '',
            chatCount: exportChats.length,
            chats: exportChats
        };

        const safeBase = (((cfg as any).projectName || 'ae_world').replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi, '_').toLowerCase()) || 'ae_world';
        const fname = safeBase + (scope === 'one' ? '_wizardchat' : '_wizardchats') + '.json';
        await api.v1.file.save(fname, JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
        api.v1.ui.toast('Exported ' + exportChats.length + ' chat' + (exportChats.length === 1 ? '' : 's') + '.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
        return exportChats.length;
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] exportWizardChats failed:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Chat export failed — see engine log.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 3000 });
        return 0;
    }
}

// [vNEXT] Resolve a topic by id from the config registry (falls back to the seeded
// defaults if config has none yet).
function getWizardTopic(cfg: GlobalConfig, topicId: string): WizardTopic | null {
    let list = ((cfg as any).wizardTopics && (cfg as any).wizardTopics.length)
        ? (cfg as any).wizardTopics
        : DEFAULT_WIZARD_TOPICS;
    return list.find(function(t: WizardTopic) { return t.id === topicId; }) || null;
}

// [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] The pre-LOREFOLD fat npc template (relationship + goal + conditions
// packed into the AN). loadConfig() migrates any saved config still carrying this exact

};

// [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] The bracket around |stageText| self-suppresses when a condition
// has no stages (pure on/off) — renders just "X is currently Y." with no second
// sentence. The |name|/|conditionName| bracket is its own block so the whole line
// self-suppresses if either is somehow empty, rather than emitting a broken fragment.
var DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_TEMPLATES: LorebookTemplates = {
    condition:     "[|name| is currently |conditionName|.][ |stageText|]",
    equipment:     "[|name| is wearing/carrying |equipList|.]",
    // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] relationship/relStatus/shiftedView reshaped so each renders
    // grammatically given the value shape its generator now emits (see generateMidRange
    // / generateRelationshipUpdate contracts). relStatus is a passthrough because the
    // generator emits a self-contained sentence naming both parties; goal/socialMask/
    // trajectory keep their copular frame and are fed bare, lowercase, period-less
    // complements; shiftedView gains "that" and takes a natural subordinate clause.
    relationship:  "[|name|'s relationship to |primaryRelationship.targetName|: |relArchetype|.]",
    relTrajectory: "[|name|'s bond with |primaryRelationship.targetName| is |primaryRelationship.trajectory|.]",
    relStatus:     "[|primaryRelationship.currentStatus|]",
    goal:          "[|name| currently wants |midRange.currentDesire|.]",
    socialMask:    "[Outwardly, |name| is presenting as |midRange.socialMask|.]",
    shiftedView:   "[|name| has come to believe that |midRange.shiftedView|.]",
    journal:       "[|name|'s own notes: |journalBody|]"
};

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 3 — SCRIPT-LEVEL STATE VARIABLES
// All mutable module-level state. Declared here so the full state surface is
// visible in one place. Never rely on implicit global declaration.
// ============================================================================

// ── [DIAG v2.65.0 LOG-CHANNELS] Channelled console logger ───────────────────
// Replaces the previously undifferentiated aeLog() flood (252 call sites,
// every one of them always-on) with a channel-gated wrapper. The channel is
// auto-derived at call time from the '[AE:xxx]' or '[AE:xxx:yyy]' tag already
// present at the front of nearly every existing log message, so call SITES did
// not need their message text rewritten — only the function name changed,
// api.v1.log() to aeLog(). A ':'-delimited tag prefix-matches its base channel
// (enabling 'charchat' also enables 'charchat:resp', 'charchat:tail', etc.) so
// one toggle covers a whole subsystem's diagnostics without listing each tag.
// A message with NO recognizable [AE:xxx] tag always passes through (rare —
// mostly the /budgetlog and /gendiag dump commands, which are already
// user-invoked and low frequency, not per-turn spam).
//
// api.v1.error() calls are UNCHANGED and always visible — errors are already
// the sparse, high-signal half of this file's logging (168 sites vs. 252 for
// api.v1.log), and gating them behind a channel that could default OFF risks
// silencing a real failure. Only the noisy log() flood needed a mute switch.
//
// Persisted via api.v1.storage (global scope, not story-scoped — these are
// developer diagnostics, not story state, same scoping choice AE_DIAG_STORAGE_KEY
// below makes). Control at runtime from any wizard chat: /log <channel> on|off,
// /log all, /log none, /log list — see runWizardChatTurn's diag-command block.
var AE_LOG_STORAGE_KEY = 'ae_log_channels_v1';
// Channels ON by default — everything else is silent until explicitly enabled.
// 'charchat' stays on by default because it is the live investigation; narrow
// this once the empty-reply bug is found and closed.
var AE_LOG_DEFAULT_ON = ['charchat'];
var _aeLogEnabled: {[k: string]: boolean} = {};
for (let _aeLogDfltCh of AE_LOG_DEFAULT_ON) { _aeLogEnabled[_aeLogDfltCh] = true; }

// Fires immediately (not gated behind onScriptsLoaded) so persisted channel
// choices apply as early as possible; the tiny window before this resolves
// falls back to AE_LOG_DEFAULT_ON above, which is itself a safe, quiet default.
(async function _aeLogInit() {
    try {
        let _aeLogRaw = await api.v1.storage.getOrDefault(AE_LOG_STORAGE_KEY, '');
        if (_aeLogRaw) {
            let _aeLogParsed = JSON.parse(_aeLogRaw);
            if (_aeLogParsed && typeof _aeLogParsed === 'object') _aeLogEnabled = _aeLogParsed;
        }
    } catch (_eAeLogInit) { /* keep the coded defaults */ }
})();

function _aeLogChannelOf(first: any): string {
    if (typeof first !== 'string') return '';
    let _aeLogM = first.match(/^\[AE:([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+(?::[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)*)\]/);
    return _aeLogM ? _aeLogM[1] : '';
}
function _aeLogChannelOn(channel: string): boolean {
    if (!channel) return true; // untagged lines always pass through — see header comment
    if (_aeLogEnabled['*']) return true; // escape hatch: /log all
    if (_aeLogEnabled[channel]) return true;
    let _aeLogBase = channel.split(':')[0];
    return !!_aeLogEnabled[_aeLogBase];
}
// Drop-in replacement for api.v1.log(...) — same variadic signature, channel-gated.
function aeLog(...args: any[]): void {
    try {
        if (!_aeLogChannelOn(_aeLogChannelOf(args[0]))) return;
        (api.v1.log as any).apply(null, args);
    } catch (_eAeLog) { /* logging must never break the caller */ }
}
async function aeLogSetChannel(channel: string, on: boolean): Promise<void> {
    _aeLogEnabled[channel] = on;
    try { await api.v1.storage.set(AE_LOG_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(_aeLogEnabled)); } catch (_eAeLogSet) {}
}
function aeLogList(): string {
    let _aeLogOn = Object.keys(_aeLogEnabled).filter(function(k) { return _aeLogEnabled[k]; });
    let _aeLogOff = Object.keys(_aeLogEnabled).filter(function(k) { return !_aeLogEnabled[k]; });
    return 'Log channels ON: ' + (_aeLogOn.length ? _aeLogOn.join(', ') : '(none)')
        + '\nKnown but OFF: ' + (_aeLogOff.length ? _aeLogOff.join(', ') : '(none)')
        + '\nAny [AE:xxx] tag never listed here is OFF by default. Prefix match applies '
        + '(e.g. "charchat" also covers "charchat:resp"). Commands: /log <channel> on|off, '
        + '/log all, /log none, /log list.';
}
// ── [DIAG LOG-CHANNELS] end ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── [DIAG v2.65.0 CE-DIAG-FLAGS] CharChat runtime kill-switches ─────────────
// Isolation switches for the CE-HANDOFF empty-reply/double-Send investigation
// (session 3). Every flag defaults to false = current, unmodified v2.64.0
// behaviour; flipping one at a time lets a failing turn be re-tested with a
// single suspect mechanism removed, without a ship cycle per hypothesis.
// Persisted via api.v1.storage (global, not story-scoped — dev switches, not
// story state). Control at runtime from any wizard chat: /diag <flag> on|off,
// /diag list — see runWizardChatTurn's diag-command block.
var AE_DIAG_STORAGE_KEY = 'ae_diag_flags_v1';
interface CEDiagFlags {
    ccRawContext: boolean;      // skip runCharChatContextOverride entirely — NAI gets the
                                 // raw story context for a chat turn. DIAGNOSTIC ONLY: this
                                 // WILL leak story scaffolding/system prompt into the chat.
    ccSkipWidgets: boolean;     // skip refreshCharChatImageWidgets' clear/rebuild pass
                                 // (called from inside refreshCharChatDecorations)
    ccSkipDecorations: boolean; // skip refreshCharChatDecorations entirely in onGenerationEnd
                                 // (this also skips widgets, since that call nests inside it —
                                 // use ccSkipWidgets instead to isolate just the widget pass)
    ccNoSeenDefer: boolean;     // onResponse: do not request re-delivery via seenData:false on
                                 // non-final chunks — accept cross-chunk misses, clean on final only
    ccNoPrime: boolean;         // disable v2.64.0's CHARCHAT-SLOT-PRIME call (Fresh Thoughts OFF path)
    ccNoThought: boolean;       // disable the post-reply ephemeral thought call (Fresh Thoughts ON path)
    ccNoImages: boolean;        // skip _charchatProcessImages() entirely for the turn
    ccNoPrefill: boolean;       // [v2.69.0 CHARCHAT-USERTOKEN] disable the dangling speaker cue at the
                                 // end of context. Set true to restore pre-v2.69.0 behaviour (context
                                 // ends on a closed '[ ... ]' block) and reproduce the empty-reply bug.
    wlNoPrefill: boolean;       // [v2.70.0 WIZLIVE-USERTOKEN] same fix, same flag pattern, for Live
                                 // Wizard Chat's onBeforeContextBuild branch.
}
var CE_DIAG: CEDiagFlags = {
    ccRawContext: false, ccSkipWidgets: false, ccSkipDecorations: false,
    ccNoSeenDefer: false, ccNoPrime: false, ccNoThought: false, ccNoImages: false,
    ccNoPrefill: false, wlNoPrefill: false
};
(async function _aeDiagInit() {
    try {
        let _aeDiagRaw = await api.v1.storage.getOrDefault(AE_DIAG_STORAGE_KEY, '');
        if (_aeDiagRaw) {
            let _aeDiagParsed = JSON.parse(_aeDiagRaw);
            if (_aeDiagParsed && typeof _aeDiagParsed === 'object') Object.assign(CE_DIAG, _aeDiagParsed);
        }
    } catch (_eAeDiagInit) { /* keep all-off defaults — unmodified behaviour */ }
})();
async function aeDiagSet(flag: string, on: boolean): Promise<string> {
    if (!(flag in CE_DIAG)) return 'Unknown diag flag: ' + flag + '. ' + aeDiagList();
    (CE_DIAG as any)[flag] = on;
    try { await api.v1.storage.set(AE_DIAG_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(CE_DIAG)); } catch (_eAeDiagSet) {}
    aeLog('[AE:diag] ' + flag + ' = ' + on);
    return flag + ' set to ' + on + '. ' + (on ? 'NON-DEFAULT behaviour is now active for this flag.' : 'Back to default behaviour for this flag.');
}
function aeDiagList(): string {
    return 'Diag flags (all default false/unmodified): '
        + Object.keys(CE_DIAG).map(function(k) { return k + '=' + (CE_DIAG as any)[k]; }).join(', ');
}
// ── [DIAG CE-DIAG-FLAGS] end ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── [DIAG v2.66.0 DIAG-CMD-SHARED] Surface-independent /log and /diag handler ──
// [CORRECTED v2.66.0] v2.65.0 put /log and /diag ONLY inside runWizardChatTurn,
// reachable exclusively through the MODAL World-Build Wizard's chat panel
// (AE_WIZ_SEND). Live Wizard Chat (_aeLiveWiz, the actively-used surface) types
// straight into the story document through NAI's own Send button and never
// calls this function at all — the commands were silently unreachable from
// there. Same lesson this file already learned once for CharChat activation:
// never gate a cross-cutting facility on a UI surface that isn't guaranteed to
// exist. Pulled out here so it can be called from BOTH runWizardChatTurn (kept,
// harmless) AND the new AE_DIAG_CMD bridge message (SECTION 18), which reaches
// the engine directly regardless of what's open in the story — see the bridge's
// window.CE_ENGINE.cmd() console helper.
//
// Returns the reply string if cmdLower matched a recognized diag command, or
// null if it did not (caller falls through to its own normal handling).
async function aeHandleDiagCommand(rawText: string, cmdLower: string): Promise<string | null> {
    if (cmdLower === '/log' || cmdLower.indexOf('/log ') === 0) {
        let _logParts = rawText.split(/\s+/);
        let _logSub = (_logParts[1] || 'list').toLowerCase();
        if (_logSub === 'list') return aeLogList();
        if (_logSub === 'all') { await aeLogSetChannel('*', true); return 'All log channels ON.'; }
        if (_logSub === 'none') {
            for (let _logK in _aeLogEnabled) _aeLogEnabled[_logK] = false;
            await aeLogSetChannel('charchat', false); // triggers the persist write
            return 'All log channels OFF (including "*"). \'charchat\' stays off until re-enabled \u2014 it is no longer the coded default once you\'ve explicitly touched channel state.';
        }
        let _logCh = _logParts[1];
        let _logState = (_logParts[2] || '').toLowerCase();
        if (!_logCh || (_logState !== 'on' && _logState !== 'off')) {
            return 'Usage: /log list | /log all | /log none | /log <channel> on|off\n' + aeLogList();
        }
        await aeLogSetChannel(_logCh, _logState === 'on');
        return 'Channel "' + _logCh + '" set ' + _logState.toUpperCase() + '.\n' + aeLogList();
    }
    if (cmdLower === '/diag' || cmdLower.indexOf('/diag ') === 0) {
        let _diagParts = rawText.split(/\s+/);
        let _diagSub = (_diagParts[1] || 'list').toLowerCase();
        if (_diagSub === 'list') return aeDiagList();
        let _diagFlag = _diagParts[1];
        let _diagState = (_diagParts[2] || '').toLowerCase();
        if (!_diagFlag || (_diagState !== 'on' && _diagState !== 'off')) {
            return 'Usage: /diag list | /diag <flag> on|off\n' + aeDiagList();
        }
        return await aeDiagSet(_diagFlag, _diagState === 'on');
    }
    return null;
}
// ── [DIAG DIAG-CMD-SHARED] end ───────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── Engine State ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// ae_lock: probability engine mutex — prevents double-rolling on a single player input.
var ae_lock = false;
var ae_genInFlight = false;  // [v0.9.36] an editor.generate() is active (cleared in onGenerationEnd)

// [v2.54.0] Per-story-turn token accumulator for the token-mode dynamic cadence. onResponse
// sums the generated tokenIds across a story turn's streamed chunks; onGenerationEnd's story
// path banks the total onto STORAGE_TOKEN_COUNT and resets. _aeStoryTurnCid tracks which
// generation the running total belongs to so it self-resets on a new continuityId without
// needing a separate start-of-turn reset hook. Never touched by chat/wizard/yoink turns —
// every one of those paths returns from onResponse before the accumulator runs.
var _aeStoryTurnTokens = 0;
var _aeStoryTurnCid: string | null = null;

// ── Editor Yoink generation (budget-free) ────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] The World-Build Wizard's chats and field generators must not draw on the
// script generation budget. Instead of api.v1.generate(), we hijack the normal
// editor generation path (the "yoink" method, per Yoink Editor Gen / Brainstorming
// Partner): set _yoinkState.active, swap our prompt in via onContextBuilt, capture
// + swallow the output via onResponse, and resolve the promise in onGenerationEnd.
// While active, the engine's normal context/response/end logic early-returns so a
// yoink gen never injects world-state, ticks the story counter, or fires triggers.
interface AEYoinkState {
    active: boolean;
    messages: any[];
    responseText: string;
    resolve: ((value: string) => void) | null;
    reject: ((reason: any) => void) | null;
    // [v1.22.1 YOINK-WATCHDOG] NAI's own per-generation id (present on onContextBuilt /
    // onResponse / onGenerationEnd params). Captured when a yoink's context is built;
    // checked by onResponse/onGenerationEnd before touching _aeYoink state, so a late
    // event from a generation the watchdog already gave up on (see executeYoinkGeneration)
    // cannot resolve, or corrupt the buffer of, a newer call that has since taken the slot.
    continuityId: string | null;
}
var _aeYoink: AEYoinkState = { active: false, messages: [], responseText: '', resolve: null, reject: null, continuityId: null };

// ── [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] Hard wizard mode switch ─────────────────────────────────
// [WIZMODE] The per-call _aeYoink.active bracket only isolates a SINGLE generation.
// This is a session-level switch, set true for as long as the wizard tab/modal is
// open (bridge sends AE_WIZ_MODE on tab enter/exit). While on:
//   - onGenerationRequested blocks any user/story-initiated generation outright.
//   - onResponse swallows ALL output unconditionally (not just continuityId matches),
//     so a stalled/abandoned yoink can never leak into the document.
//   - generationParameters are snapshotted and max_tokens is pinned for wizard replies,
//     restored on exit.
//   - The wizard's assembled context (buildWizardContext) is frozen per chat on first
//     use and reused for the rest of the session, so only the transcript grows per
//     turn — fewer context substitutions, whatever caching benefit that permits.
// A zombie continuityId set is the backstop: if the 90s yoink watchdog force-recovers
// a stuck generation, its id is remembered so any of its late output/end-event is still
// caught even after mode has since exited (bounded, does not block exit indefinitely).
var _aeWizardMode = false;
var _wizModeParamSnapshot: any = null;
var _wizModeExitPending = false;
var _wizFrozenCtx: { [chatId: string]: string } = {};
var _wizZombieIds: { [continuityId: string]: boolean } = {};
var AE_WIZARD_MAX_TOKENS = 300; // pinned output cap for wizard replies while mode is on

async function broadcastWizModeState(): Promise<void> {
    try { await broadcastToTM(EVT_WIZ_MODE_STATE, { active: _aeWizardMode, draining: _wizModeExitPending }); }
    catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] broadcastWizModeState failed:', e); }
}

async function aeSetWizardMode(on: boolean): Promise<void> {
    if (on) {
        if (_aeWizardMode) return;
        // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] The two wizard modes are mutually exclusive: classic mode
        // swallows ALL output unconditionally, which would eat live-chat replies.
        if (_aeLiveWiz.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('An in-story wizard chat is open \u2014 end it before opening the classic wizard.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
        if (_aeCharChat.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A character chat is open in the story \u2014 end it before opening the classic wizard.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
        _aeWizardMode = true;
        _wizModeExitPending = false;
        await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZ_MODE, 'true').catch(function() {});
        try {
            _wizModeParamSnapshot = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
            await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: AE_WIZARD_MAX_TOKENS });
        } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] mode-enter param pin failed, continuing without pin:', e); _wizModeParamSnapshot = null; }
        api.v1.ui.toast('Wizard mode ON \u2014 story generation paused', { type: 'info', autoClose: 3000 });
        await broadcastWizModeState();
        return;
    }
    // Exiting. If a yoink is genuinely still in flight, defer the actual restore until
    // it resolves (onGenerationEnd) or the watchdog gives up on it (both bounded by
    // AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS) — never leave the document unswallowed mid-generation.
    if (_aeYoink.active) {
        _wizModeExitPending = true;
        aeLog('[AE:wizard] mode-exit requested while a yoink is in flight \u2014 draining before restore.');
        await broadcastWizModeState();
        return;
    }
    await aeCompleteWizardModeExit();
}

async function aeCompleteWizardModeExit(): Promise<void> {
    _aeWizardMode = false;
    _wizModeExitPending = false;
    _wizFrozenCtx = {};
    await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(STORAGE_WIZ_MODE).catch(function() {});
    if (_wizModeParamSnapshot) {
        try {
            let r: any = { model: _wizModeParamSnapshot.model };
            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.max_tokens !== undefined) r.max_tokens = _wizModeParamSnapshot.max_tokens;
            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.temperature !== undefined) r.temperature = _wizModeParamSnapshot.temperature;
            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.top_p !== undefined) r.top_p = _wizModeParamSnapshot.top_p;
            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.top_k !== undefined) r.top_k = _wizModeParamSnapshot.top_k;
            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.frequency_penalty !== undefined) r.frequency_penalty = _wizModeParamSnapshot.frequency_penalty;
