            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.presence_penalty !== undefined) r.presence_penalty = _wizModeParamSnapshot.presence_penalty;
            if (_wizModeParamSnapshot.min_p !== undefined) r.min_p = _wizModeParamSnapshot.min_p;
            await api.v1.generationParameters.update(r);
        } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] failed to restore generation params after wizard mode exit:', e); }
        _wizModeParamSnapshot = null;
    }
    api.v1.ui.toast('Wizard mode OFF \u2014 story generation resumed', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
    await broadcastWizModeState();
}
// ── [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] end ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] In-document wizard chat ──────────────────────────────────
// The chat lives IN the story document instead of a hidden yoink conversation:
//   - startLiveWizardChat appends a boundary marker + an editable protocol
//     paragraph (both source:'instruction' — NAI's native instruct blocks) and
//     an opening greeting; the user then talks in { } instruct blocks and
//     presses NAI's own Send. Replies are ordinary generations landing in the
//     document — nothing is swallowed, no onResponse capture, no watchdog.
//   - CACHING CONTRACT: the story prefix (system prompt, memory, lorebook,
//     early story text) is NEVER touched while the chat is live. The only
//     context differences on a chat turn are the Author's Note being
//     deactivated (tail of context) and the chat paragraphs growing at the
//     document tail. Ending the chat removes those paragraphs, so story
//     generation resumes with a byte-identical top of context. Committing to
//     Memory is the single deliberate exception — that is the feature.
//   - endLiveWizardChat collects the transcript (instruction-source paragraphs
//     = user turns, everything else = wizard turns), archives it as a normal
//     WizardChat record unless discarding, optionally commits raw or distilled
//     notes to Memory, then removes every paragraph from the marker down.
//   - Batch generators are untouched — they still run through
//     executeYoinkGeneration. Classic WIZMODE and WIZLIVE are mutually
//     exclusive (guards in aeSetWizardMode and startLiveWizardChat).
interface LiveWizState {
    active: boolean;
    markerId: number | null;        // sectionId of the boundary marker paragraph
    setupIds: number[];             // sectionIds of engine-authored setup paragraphs (excluded from the transcript)
    topicId: string;                // wizard topic supplying the persona; '' = generic live persona
    paramSnapshot: any;             // generationParameters snapshot restored on end (persisted so a reload can still restore)
    startedAt: number;
    sourceEntryId: string | null;   // [v2.5.0] CE-Chats lorebook entry this chat resumes/overwrites; null = a fresh chat (Save creates a new entry)
    contextEntryIds: string[];      // [v2.5.0] CE-Chats entries switched forceActivation:true for THIS session; switched back off on end
    title: string;                  // [v2.6.0] user-facing chat name — defaults to the topic label at start/resume, renameable live; NEVER carries a timestamp
}
var _aeLiveWiz: LiveWizState = { active: false, markerId: null, setupIds: [], topicId: '', paramSnapshot: null, startedAt: 0, sourceEntryId: null, contextEntryIds: [], title: '' };

const AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_MARKER  = 'ae-wizlive-marker';
const AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_USER    = 'ae-wizlive-user';   // [v2.5.0] Director (instruction) turns — cool blue
const AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_WIZ     = 'ae-wizlive-wiz';    // [v2.5.0] Wizard replies + setup paragraphs — violet
const AE_WIZLIVE_MARKER_TEXT  = '\u2501\u2501 WIZARD CHAT \u2501\u2501 The story above is reference material. Everything below this line is an out-of-story planning chat and will be removed when the chat ends.';

// [v2.5.0 CE-CHATS] Live wizard chats persist to the lorebook instead of a bespoke
// Memory sentinel. A dedicated category holds one entry per saved chat; entries carry
// NO keys, so they never activate by keyword — they only enter context when their
// forceActivation ("Always On") flag is switched true, and go dormant again when
// switched false. That is how a chat (or a batch generator) "chooses" a saved chat as
// context: turn it on for the session, off when done. Resuming pulls the entry text
// back into the story to be continued, then Save overwrites the same entry.
var WIZLIVE_CAT_NAME  = 'CE Chats';
var _aeWizChatCatId: string | null = null;   // module cache of the category id (also mirrored to storyStorage)

async function saveLiveWizState(): Promise<void> {
    try { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZLIVE, JSON.stringify(_aeLiveWiz)); }
    catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] state save failed:', e); }
}

async function broadcastWizLiveState(): Promise<void> {
    try { await broadcastToTM(EVT_WIZLIVE_STATE, { active: _aeLiveWiz.active, topicId: _aeLiveWiz.topicId, startedAt: _aeLiveWiz.startedAt }); }
    catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] state broadcast failed:', e); }
}

// ── CE-Chats lorebook store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Find (or create) the "CE Chats" lorebook category. Cached in module scope and
// mirrored to storyStorage so we usually skip the categories() scan.
async function ensureWizChatCategory(): Promise<string> {
    if (_aeWizChatCatId) return _aeWizChatCatId;
    try {
        let cached = (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZLIVE_CAT, '')) as string;
        if (cached) {
            let c = await api.v1.lorebook.category(cached);
            if (c) { _aeWizChatCatId = cached; return cached; }
        }
    } catch (_e0) {}
    try {
        let cats = await api.v1.lorebook.categories();
        let found = (cats || []).filter(function(c: any) { return (c.name || '') === WIZLIVE_CAT_NAME; })[0];
        if (found) {
            _aeWizChatCatId = found.id;
            try { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZLIVE_CAT, found.id); } catch (_e1) {}
            return found.id;
        }
    } catch (_e2) {}
    let newId = await api.v1.lorebook.createCategory({ id: api.v1.uuid(), name: WIZLIVE_CAT_NAME, enabled: true });
    _aeWizChatCatId = newId;
    try { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZLIVE_CAT, newId); } catch (_e3) {}
    return newId;
}

// All CE-Chats entries (each is one saved wizard chat), newest lorebook order.
async function listWizChatEntries(): Promise<any[]> {
    try { let cat = await ensureWizChatCategory(); return (await api.v1.lorebook.entries(cat)) || []; }
    catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] listWizChatEntries:', e); return []; }
}

// Serialize a transcript into a round-trippable, human-readable block. The header
// line carries the title and (optionally) the source topic so a resume restores the
// right persona. Turns are "Director:"/"Wizard:" prefixed — the same shape the chat
// already reads naturally as context when switched Always-On.
// [v2.6.0] The timestamp is stamped into this HEADER line only (a [saved:...] tag) —
// title/displayName (the lorebook entry's actual NAME) never carries a date, per design.
function serializeChatTranscript(title: string, msgs: WizardChatMessage[], topicId: string): string {
    let head = '=== ' + (title || 'Wizard Chat') + (topicId ? ' [topic:' + topicId + ']' : '') + ' [saved:' + new Date().toLocaleString() + ']' + ' ===';
    let parts: string[] = [head, ''];
    for (let i = 0; i < msgs.length; i++) {
        let m = msgs[i];
        parts.push((m.role === 'user' ? 'Director: ' : 'Wizard: ') + m.content);
        parts.push('');
    }
    return parts.join('\n').replace(/\s+$/, '');
}

// Inverse of serializeChatTranscript. String-parsed (no bracket-bearing regex) so the
// engine brace-balance check stays meaningful. Lines before the first role header are
// ignored; a role line starts a turn, and following lines accrue to it until the next
// role header — so multi-line wizard replies survive the round trip.
function parseChatTranscript(text: string): { title: string; topicId: string; msgs: WizardChatMessage[] } {
    let title = ''; let topicId = '';
    let lines = String(text || '').split('\n');
    let msgs: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    let cur: WizardChatMessage | null = null;
    for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
        let line = lines[i];
        let t = line.trim();
        if (t.length >= 6 && t.indexOf('===') === 0 && t.lastIndexOf('===') === t.length - 3) {
            let inner = t.substring(3, t.length - 3).trim();
            let tp = inner.indexOf('[topic:');
            if (tp >= 0) {
                let close = inner.indexOf(']', tp);
                if (close > tp) {
                    topicId = inner.substring(tp + 7, close).trim();
                    inner = (inner.substring(0, tp) + inner.substring(close + 1)).trim();
                }
            }
            // [v2.6.0] Strip the [saved:...] stamp too — it is metadata, never part of the title.
            let sp = inner.indexOf('[saved:');
            if (sp >= 0) {
                let sclose = inner.indexOf(']', sp);
                if (sclose > sp) inner = (inner.substring(0, sp) + inner.substring(sclose + 1)).trim();
            }
            title = inner;
            continue;
        }
        let role: 'user' | 'assistant' | null = null;
        let rest = '';
        if (line.indexOf('Director:') === 0) { role = 'user'; rest = line.substring(9); }
        else if (line.indexOf('Wizard:') === 0) { role = 'assistant'; rest = line.substring(7); }
        if (role) {
            if (cur) msgs.push(cur);
            if (rest.charAt(0) === ' ') rest = rest.substring(1);
            cur = { role: role, content: rest };
        } else if (cur) {
            cur.content += '\n' + line;
        }
    }
    if (cur) msgs.push(cur);
    let outM: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    for (let k = 0; k < msgs.length; k++) {
        let c = msgs[k].content.replace(/^\s+/, '').replace(/\s+$/, '');
        if (c.length > 0) outM.push({ role: msgs[k].role, content: c });
    }
    return { title: title, topicId: topicId, msgs: outM };
}

// Create-or-overwrite a CE-Chats entry. sourceEntryId (when it still exists) is
// overwritten in place; otherwise a fresh keyless, forceActivation:false entry is made.
async function upsertChatEntry(sourceEntryId: string | null, title: string, msgs: WizardChatMessage[], topicId: string): Promise<string> {
    let cat = await ensureWizChatCategory();
    let text = serializeChatTranscript(title, msgs, topicId);
    if (sourceEntryId) {
        let existing: any = null;
        try { existing = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(sourceEntryId); } catch (_e) {}
        if (existing) {
            await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(sourceEntryId, { text: text, displayName: title, category: cat });
            return sourceEntryId;
        }
    }
    let id = api.v1.uuid();
    let newId = await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry({ id: id, category: cat, displayName: title, text: text, keys: [], enabled: true, forceActivation: false, hidden: false });
    return newId || id;
}

// Flip forceActivation ("Always On") for a set of entries. Turning entries on is a
// deliberate top-of-context change (like the old Memory commit was); it happens once
// at chat start and reverts once at chat end, so all turns WITHIN a chat stay
// byte-identical and the prefix cache survives the session.
async function setContextEntriesActivation(ids: string[], on: boolean): Promise<void> {
    for (let i = 0; i < ids.length; i++) {
        try { await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(ids[i], { forceActivation: on }); }
        catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] context toggle failed:', e); }
    }
}

// ── Decorations: split Director (blue) vs Wizard (violet) ───────────────────
// Registered once with empty user/wiz scopes; refreshLiveWizDecorations recomputes
// the two id sets from the live document (called on start, restore, and each turn end).
async function _wizliveRegisterDecorations(markerId: number): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.editor.decorations.registerRules([
        { id: AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_MARKER, type: 'node', match: '^', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(147, 112, 219, 0.22)', borderTop: '2px solid rgba(147, 112, 219, 0.85)', borderBottom: '1px solid rgba(147, 112, 219, 0.4)' }, scope: { sectionIds: [markerId] } },
        { id: AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_USER,   type: 'node', match: '^', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(74, 158, 217, 0.10)', borderLeft: '3px solid rgba(74, 158, 217, 0.60)' }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        { id: AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_WIZ,    type: 'node', match: '^', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(147, 112, 219, 0.07)', borderLeft: '3px solid rgba(147, 112, 219, 0.45)' }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } }
    ]);
}

async function refreshLiveWizDecorations(): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeLiveWiz.active || _aeLiveWiz.markerId === null) return;
    let all: any[] = [];
    try { all = await api.v1.document.scan(); } catch (_eScan) { return; }
    let userIds: number[] = [];
    let wizIds: number[] = [];
    let seen = false;
    for (let idx = 0; idx < all.length; idx++) {
        let row = all[idx];
        if (row.sectionId === _aeLiveWiz.markerId) { seen = true; continue; }
        if (!seen) continue;
        // Engine-authored setup (protocol/greeting) reads as the wizard side.
        if (_aeLiveWiz.setupIds.indexOf(row.sectionId) >= 0) { wizIds.push(row.sectionId); continue; }
        let isUser = row.section.source === 'instruction';
        if (!isUser) {
            let org = row.section.origin || [];
            if (org.length > 0) {
                let allTyped = true;
                let anyUser = false;
                for (let o of org) {
                    if (o.data === 'user') anyUser = true;
                    if (o.data !== 'user' && o.data !== 'edit') { allTyped = false; break; }
                }
                isUser = allTyped && anyUser;
            }
        }
        if (isUser) userIds.push(row.sectionId); else wizIds.push(row.sectionId);
    }
    try {
        await api.v1.editor.decorations.updateRules([
            { id: AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_USER, scope: { sectionIds: userIds } },
            { id: AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_WIZ,  scope: { sectionIds: wizIds } }
        ]);
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] decoration refresh failed (cosmetic only):', e); }
}

// Build the persona + protocol paragraphs shared by start and resume.
function _wizliveProtocolText(cfg: GlobalConfig, topicId: string): string {
    let topic = topicId ? getWizardTopic(cfg, topicId) : null;
    let persona = topic ? topic.instructions : 'You are a warm, collaborative worldbuilding assistant helping the user develop their story. Write in pure prose \u2014 no markdown, bolding, headers, or lists. Offer one or two concrete suggestions that push the idea forward and ask exactly ONE focused question per turn. Typically respond in 2-5 sentences.';
    let protoParts: string[] = [];
    protoParts.push('WIZARD CHAT MODE. ' + persona);
    protoParts.push('The user speaks in instruction blocks like this one. Reply to their newest instruction in plain conversational prose \u2014 do NOT continue the story itself. The story text above the chat marker is reference material you may draw on.');
    if (cfg.projectName || cfg.projectGenre || cfg.projectConcept) {
        let pj: string[] = [];
        if (cfg.projectName) pj.push('Project: ' + cfg.projectName);
        if (cfg.projectGenre) pj.push('Genre: ' + cfg.projectGenre);
        if (cfg.projectConcept) pj.push('Concept: ' + cfg.projectConcept);
        protoParts.push(pj.join('. ') + '.');
    }
    return protoParts.join(' ');
}

async function startLiveWizardChat(topicId: string, ctxEntryIds?: string[]): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeLiveWiz.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A wizard chat is already open in the story.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
    if (_aeWizardMode) { api.v1.ui.toast('Close the classic wizard first \u2014 the two modes are exclusive.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeCharChat.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A character chat is open in the story \u2014 end it first.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeYoink.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A background generation is running \u2014 try again in a moment.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }

    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let topic = topicId ? getWizardTopic(cfg, topicId) : null;
    let protoText = _wizliveProtocolText(cfg, topicId);
    let greeting = (topic && (topic as any).seedMessage && String((topic as any).seedMessage).trim())
        ? String((topic as any).seedMessage).trim()
        // [FEATURE v2.29.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-WRAP, per user] No longer tells the user to type
        // a curly brace themselves — _wizliveNormalizeDocument wraps a Director turn in
        // braces automatically after every reply, the same "you don't have to do it
        // yourself" wording change CharChat's own greeting got in v2.26.0/v2.27.0.
        : 'I\u2019m listening \u2014 write your message in a new paragraph, then press Send.';

    let before = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    await api.v1.document.appendParagraphs([
        { text: AE_WIZLIVE_MARKER_TEXT, source: 'instruction' },
        { text: protoText, source: 'instruction' },
        { text: greeting }
    ]);
    let after = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    let added = after.slice(before.length);
    if (added.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not start the chat \u2014 document append failed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let markerId = added[0];

    let snap: any = null;
    try {
        snap = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
        await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: AE_WIZARD_MAX_TOKENS });
    } catch (eP) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] param pin failed, continuing without pin:', eP); snap = null; }

    let ctx = Array.isArray(ctxEntryIds) ? ctxEntryIds.slice() : [];
    let startTitle = (topic && topic.label) ? topic.label : 'General Worldbuilding';
    _aeLiveWiz = { active: true, markerId: markerId, setupIds: added.slice(1), topicId: topicId || '', paramSnapshot: snap, startedAt: Date.now(), sourceEntryId: null, contextEntryIds: ctx, title: startTitle };
    await saveLiveWizState();

    if (ctx.length > 0) {
        try { await setContextEntriesActivation(ctx, true); }
        catch (eCtx) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] context activation failed:', eCtx); }
    }

    try { await _wizliveRegisterDecorations(markerId); await refreshLiveWizDecorations(); }
    catch (eD) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] decoration registration failed (cosmetic only):', eD); }

    api.v1.ui.toast('Wizard chat open \u2014 talk in { } instruct blocks below the marker, then press Send.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 6000 });
    await broadcastWizLiveState();
}

// Pull a saved chat back into the story and go live on it. The entry it came from is
// held as sourceEntryId so Save & Close overwrites that same entry.
async function resumeLiveWizardChatFromEntry(entryId: string): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeLiveWiz.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A wizard chat is already open in the story.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
    if (_aeWizardMode) { api.v1.ui.toast('Close the classic wizard first \u2014 the two modes are exclusive.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeCharChat.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A character chat is open in the story \u2014 end it first.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeYoink.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A background generation is running \u2014 try again in a moment.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }

    let entry: any = null;
    try { entry = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(entryId); } catch (_e) {}
    if (!entry || !entry.text) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not load that saved chat.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let parsed = parseChatTranscript(entry.text);
    if (parsed.msgs.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('That saved chat is empty.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }

    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let protoText = _wizliveProtocolText(cfg, parsed.topicId);

    // marker + protocol, then one paragraph per turn (user turns rebuilt as { } instruct blocks).
    let paras: any[] = [
        { text: AE_WIZLIVE_MARKER_TEXT, source: 'instruction' },
        { text: protoText, source: 'instruction' }
    ];
    for (let i = 0; i < parsed.msgs.length; i++) {
        let m = parsed.msgs[i];
        if (m.role === 'user') {
            // [v2.53.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-DOUBLE] Rebuild a Director turn in the SAME bare form the
            // write pass produces -- "Director: body", source:'instruction', no literal brace.
            // The old '{ body }' wrap re-introduced the double brace AND dropped the "Director:"
            // label, so a resumed chat read differently from a live-typed one. Header-strip
            // guards a saved body that somehow still carries the prefix (mirrors CharChat's
            // CHARCHAT-RESUME-IDEMPOTENT, v2.51.2).
            let wlRB = _wizliveStripDirectorHeader(_wizliveStripBraces(m.content.replace(/\n+/g, ' ').trim()));
            paras.push({ text: 'Director: ' + wlRB, source: 'instruction' });
        } else {
            let chunks = m.content.split('\n');
            for (let j = 0; j < chunks.length; j++) {
                let c = chunks[j].replace(/^\s+/, '').replace(/\s+$/, '');
                if (c.length > 0) paras.push({ text: c });
            }
        }
    }

    let before = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    await api.v1.document.appendParagraphs(paras);
    let after = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    let added = after.slice(before.length);
    if (added.length < 2) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not insert the chat \u2014 document append failed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let markerId = added[0];
    let protocolId = added[1];

    let snap: any = null;
    try {
        snap = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
        await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: AE_WIZARD_MAX_TOKENS });
    } catch (eP) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] param pin failed, continuing without pin:', eP); snap = null; }

    let resumeTitle = (parsed.title || '').trim() || ((getWizardTopic(cfg, parsed.topicId) || {} as any).label) || 'General Worldbuilding';
    _aeLiveWiz = { active: true, markerId: markerId, setupIds: [protocolId], topicId: parsed.topicId || '', paramSnapshot: snap, startedAt: Date.now(), sourceEntryId: entryId, contextEntryIds: [], title: resumeTitle };
    await saveLiveWizState();

    try { await _wizliveRegisterDecorations(markerId); await refreshLiveWizDecorations(); }
    catch (eD) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] decoration registration failed (cosmetic only):', eD); }

    api.v1.ui.toast('Chat resumed in the story \u2014 continue below the marker. Save & Close overwrites this saved chat.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 6000 });
    await broadcastWizLiveState();
}

function _wizliveStripBraces(t: string): string {
    let s = (t || '').trim();
    if (s.length >= 2 && s.charAt(0) === '{' && s.charAt(s.length - 1) === '}') s = s.substring(1, s.length - 1).trim();
    return s;
}

// [v2.53.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-DOUBLE] Strip a leading "Director:" label from a turn body, so the
// canon pass is idempotent once it stopped writing the literal brace (a braceless canon that
// left the label in the body grew "Director: Director: ..." on every reopen). Mirrors
// _charchatStripHeader's shape (fixed literal label, not a per-user name; tolerates **bold**,
// tabs, and a full-width colon; two passes for a "Director:\nDirector:" split). Role is never
// inferred from text here -- _wizliveRowIsUser already classified this row as a Director turn.
function _wizliveStripDirectorHeader(body: string): string {
    let wlT = (body || '').replace(/^\s+/, '');
    let wlRx = new RegExp('^\\*{0,2}Director\\*{0,2}[ \\t]*[:\uff1a]\\s*', 'i');
    for (let wlPass = 0; wlPass < 2; wlPass++) {
        if (wlRx.test(wlT)) wlT = wlT.replace(wlRx, '');
        else break;
    }
    return wlT;
}

// [v2.29.0] Factored out of collectLiveWizTranscript's own inline copy, per user's
// new brace-auto-wrap request needing the exact same classification — same
// "one shared function, not drifting duplicates" call already made for CharChat's
// _charchatRowIsPov (grep it for the precedent). Role is NEVER inferred from text:
// instruction-source paragraphs are Director turns (NAI's { } instruct blocks); a
// paragraph whose origin metadata is entirely user/edit-typed is also a Director
// turn (they didn't use braces this time).
function _wizliveRowIsUser(section: any): boolean {
    if (section.source === 'instruction') return true;
    let wlOrg = section.origin || [];
    if (wlOrg.length === 0) return false;
    let wlAllTyped = true;
    let wlAnyUser = false;
    for (let wlO of wlOrg) {
        if (wlO.data === 'user') wlAnyUser = true;
        if (wlO.data !== 'user' && wlO.data !== 'edit') { wlAllTyped = false; break; }
    }
    return wlAllTyped && wlAnyUser;
}

// [BUGFIX v2.54.0 WIZLIVE-MERGE, per user: "wizard user messages not automatically wrapped
// / not marked as instruct"] Ports CharChat's v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE fix that
// _wizliveNormalizeDocument's own header (below) flagged as DELIBERATELY NOT PORTED, with
// the caveat "the same NAI editor mechanism underlies both chat modes, so the same bug is
// plausible here too." It is: ROOT CAUSE of the report is that when the user types a
// Director turn with no trailing paragraph break, NAI's native Send continues the Wizard
// reply DIRECTLY INTO that same paragraph. The merged section's origin then spans a
// user-typed head + an ai-generated tail, so _wizliveRowIsUser sees non-user/edit
// ('ai'/'prompt') data and returns FALSE — the turn stops being recognised as a Director
// turn, and neither _wizliveNormalizeDocument nor _wizliveCanonicalStoryOverride ever wraps
// it or stamps source:'instruction'. This is exactly why CharChat "does not have the same
// problem": it splits the glued paragraph first. Returns the offset into section.text where
// the AI-authored portion begins, or -1 if the origin is homogeneous (nothing to split).
// Byte-for-byte the logic of _charchatMergeSplitIdx (grep it for the full reasoning).
function _wizliveMergeSplitIdx(section: any): number {
    let wlOrg = (section && section.origin) || [];
    if (wlOrg.length < 2) return -1;
    let wlSorted = wlOrg.slice().sort(function(a: any, b: any) { return a.position - b.position; });
    let wlSawUser = false;
    for (let wlO of wlSorted) {
        if (wlO.data === 'user' || wlO.data === 'edit') { wlSawUser = true; continue; }
        if (wlSawUser && (wlO.data === 'ai' || wlO.data === 'prompt')) return wlO.position;
    }
    return -1;
}

// WRITE-PASS ONLY (called from _wizliveNormalizeDocument at onGenerationEnd — never the
// read-only side, which must not mutate the document, and which also runs on dryRun).
// Physically splits any Director paragraph NAI glued a Wizard reply onto into two real
// paragraphs: the user-typed head keeps the original sectionId (a legitimate, just shorter,
// Director turn that _wizliveRowIsUser now classifies correctly), and the AI-authored tail
// becomes a fresh paragraph via insertParagraphAfter with no source override — api.v1.
// document's contract gives script-written text 'script' origin, which _wizliveRowIsUser
// reads as NOT a Director turn, same as any Wizard reply. Runs BEFORE the wrap loop so
// normalisation never has to see a merged section. Mirrors _charchatSplitMergedSections.
async function _wizliveSplitMergedSections(): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeLiveWiz.markerId === null) return;
    let wlAll: any[] = [];
    try { wlAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); }
    catch (eSc) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] merge-split scan failed:', eSc); return; }
    let wlSeen = false;
    for (let wlR of wlAll) {
        if (wlR.sectionId === _aeLiveWiz.markerId) { wlSeen = true; continue; }
        if (!wlSeen) continue;
        if (_aeLiveWiz.setupIds.indexOf(wlR.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
        let wlFull = wlR.section.text || '';
        let wlIdx = _wizliveMergeSplitIdx(wlR.section);
        if (wlIdx <= 0 || wlIdx >= wlFull.length) continue;
        let wlHead = wlFull.substring(0, wlIdx);
        let wlTail = wlFull.substring(wlIdx);
        try {
            await api.v1.document.insertParagraphAfter(wlR.sectionId, { text: wlTail });
            await api.v1.document.updateParagraph(wlR.sectionId, { text: wlHead });
        } catch (eSp) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] merge-split failed for section ' + wlR.sectionId + ':', eSp); }
    }
}

// [FEATURE v2.29.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-WRAP, per user: "just like character chats"] Rewrites
// every Director (user) turn in the live document to "Director: body" (bare, source:'instruction'; see [v2.53.0] note below) so the user
// never has to type braces themselves — CharChat's exact selling point, ported here.
// WRITE-PASS ONLY, called from onGenerationEnd, same timing rule CharChat's own
// _charchatNormalizeDocument follows and for the same reason: mutating the document
// mid-context-build would race the generation currently building against it.
// DELIBERATE DIFFERENCE FROM CHARCHAT, not an oversight: CharChat ALSO has a read-only
// twin (_charchatCanonicalChatText) that overrides storyText.content so the model's
// view is canonical on the SAME turn it's typed. WIZLIVE does not get one — WIZLIVE's
// onBeforeContextBuild deliberately leaves the story prefix (and the live document
// text within it) completely untouched, to protect prefix caching across the chat
// (see that hook's own WIZLIVE comment). Adding a same-turn override here would mean
// reversing THAT design decision too, which nothing in this request asked for. Net
// effect: a Director turn is typed raw, reaches the model raw for THAT one reply, and
// is rewritten with braces immediately after — visible correctly from that point on,
// and correctly formatted for every SUBSEQUENT generation's raw-document read. One
// turn of lag on the very first message of a session, same trade CharChat itself
// would have if its read-only twin were ever removed.
// NOT PORTED, flagged rather than guessed at: CharChat's v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE fix
// (_charchatSplitMergedSections) for NAI gluing a completion directly onto the same
// paragraph the user just typed into, with no trailing paragraph break. The same NAI
// editor mechanism underlies both chat modes, so the same bug is plausible here too —
// untested, not addressed this pass. Watch for a Director turn that looks like it
// swallowed the start of a Wizard reply, the exact symptom that bug produced there.
// ASSUMPTION, stated rather than silently made: the literal label is "Director" (no
// per-user customizable name exists for WIZLIVE, unlike CharChat's povName) — matches
// the fixed "Director:"/"Wizard:" prefixes serializeChatTranscript already uses for
// the SAVED transcript, so this is consistent with an existing convention, not a new
// one invented for this patch.
// [v2.53.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-DOUBLE, mirrors CHARCHAT-BRACE-DOUBLE v2.52.0] CORRECTION to the
// v2.29.0 design narrated above: a Director turn is now written BARE as "Director: body" with
// source:'instruction', NOT the literal "{Director: body}" the older text still describes. A
// Director turn is an instruction paragraph and NAI renders its OWN brace around one, so the
// literal wrap was a SECOND brace -- it re-baked "{ {Director: ...} }" on every reopen, the
// exact CharChat bug fixed in v2.52.0. The read-side twin (_wizliveCanonicalStoryOverride) and
// the resume path (resumeLiveWizardChatFromEntry) were moved to the same bare form in this
// patch; per CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE (v2.42.0) the model's view carries the "Director:" header, not
// a brace. Old stories self-migrate: an old "{Director: body}" turn strips to "body" and
// re-canons to bare on the next reply, idempotent thereafter.
async function _wizliveNormalizeDocument(): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeLiveWiz.active || _aeLiveWiz.markerId === null) return;
    // [BUGFIX v2.54.0 WIZLIVE-MERGE] Split any Director paragraph NAI glued a Wizard reply
    // onto BEFORE scanning, so the wrap loop below sees a clean user-only Director section
    // instead of a merged one _wizliveRowIsUser rejects (which is why the turn never got
    // wrapped or instruction-stamped — the reported bug). Same split-before-normalise
    // ordering _charchatNormalizeDocument uses.
    try { await _wizliveSplitMergedSections(); } catch (eMs) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] merge-split pass failed (non-fatal):', eMs); }
    let wlAll: any[] = [];
    try { wlAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); }
    catch (eWs) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] turn scan failed:', eWs); return; }
    let wlSeen = false;
    let wlUpdates: { sectionId: number; section: any }[] = [];
    for (let wlR of wlAll) {
        if (wlR.sectionId === _aeLiveWiz.markerId) { wlSeen = true; continue; }
        if (!wlSeen) continue;
        if (_aeLiveWiz.setupIds.indexOf(wlR.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
        if (!_wizliveRowIsUser(wlR.section)) continue;   // only Director turns get wrapped; Wizard replies pass through untouched
        let wlTxt = (wlR.section.text || '');
        if (!wlTxt.trim()) continue;
        let wlBody = _wizliveStripDirectorHeader(_wizliveStripBraces(wlTxt));
        if (!wlBody) continue;
        // [v2.53.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-DOUBLE] Bare "Director: body", source:'instruction' stamped as
        // we rewrite (NAI draws the single visible brace; the stamp carries turn identity now
        // that the literal brace is gone). Re-stamp even when the text already matches, to
        // migrate a Director turn an older build left un-instruction'd.
        let wlCanon = 'Director: ' + wlBody;
        if (wlCanon !== wlR.section.text || wlR.section.source !== 'instruction') {
            wlUpdates.push({ sectionId: wlR.sectionId, section: { text: wlCanon, source: 'instruction' } });
        }
    }
    if (wlUpdates.length > 0) {
        try { await api.v1.document.updateParagraphs(wlUpdates); }
        catch (eWu) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] brace-wrap rewrite failed (cosmetic only):', eWu); }
    }
}

// [v2.58.0 WIZLIVE-INSTRUCTION, mirrors CHARCHAT's buildCharChatInstruction] The true-bottom
// instruction for wizard chat. CharChat learned across v2.24.0-v2.51.0 that the only place
// an instruction is reliably obeyed is the TRUE BOTTOM of context — immediately before the
// generation point. WIZLIVE's protocol text (the setup paragraph) sits inside storyText at
// whatever depth NAI's budget trims it to, which means it drifts upward as the chat grows
// and the model progressively ignores it. This function replaces that reliance: the protocol
// paragraph is still useful as a greeting/framing for the USER, but the MODEL's steering now
// comes from here. Mirrors buildCharChatInstruction's four concerns: task, role constraint,
// format constraint, anti-impersonation.
function buildWizardChatInstruction(): string {
    let wlOut: string[] = [];
    // Task + anti-impersonation: tell the model exactly what to write and what NOT to write.
    // "Director" is named explicitly because the model can still see the instruction-brace
    // shape of Director turns even after v2.58.0's header strip, and naming the role it must
    // not produce is the cheapest anti-impersonation clause.
    wlOut.push('[ Instruction: Write the Wizard\u2019s next reply to the Director\u2019s latest message. '
             + 'Reply in conversational prose \u2014 do NOT continue the story text above the chat marker. '
             + 'Write ONLY the Wizard\u2019s response. Never write a "Director:" turn or any text on the Director\u2019s behalf. ]');
    // Format constraint: same "no markdown" rubric the protocol text carries, restated here
    // because it is far more effective at the bottom of context than in a setup paragraph.
    wlOut.push('[ Write in pure prose. No markdown, no bolding, no headers, no bullet lists. ]');
    return wlOut.join('\n');
}

// [FEATURE v2.51.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-WRAP-T1, per user: "the wizard chat benefits from the same
// auto-bracket wrapping from the first message"] Closes the one-turn lag v2.29.0 documented
// and accepted (see _wizliveNormalizeDocument's header: "One turn of lag on the very first
// message of a session"). Same read-only-twin idea as CharChat's _charchatCanonicalChatText,
// but DELIBERATELY SURGICAL rather than a full region rebuild — this is the difference that
// keeps WIZLIVE's core invariant intact.
// [v2.58.0 WIZLIVE-CTX-STRIP] Now ALSO strips the "Director:" header from the model's view,
// so Director turns appear as plain body text inside NAI's instruction brace. The document
// keeps "Director: body" (the write pass _wizliveNormalizeDocument is unchanged). This is
// the model-view-only anti-impersonation fix: same principle as CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE v2.42.0.
//
// WHY NOT CHARCHAT'S FULL REBUILD: CharChat rebuilds the entire chat region and drops the
// greeting, so its storyText diverges from the raw document at the TOP of the region. That is
// fine for CharChat (it owns its whole prefix and re-encodes once on enter/exit). WIZLIVE must
// NOT do that: its whole design is that system prompt/memory/lorebook AND the live document
// text reach the model byte-identical to a story turn, so the story prefix cache survives the
// whole chat and story generation resumes without a re-encode (see onBeforeContextBuild's
// WIZLIVE comment and CE-INDEX v2.4.0/v2.29.0). So instead of rebuilding, we take a COPY of
// params.storyText.content and rewrite ONLY the Director turns that are still unwrapped in the
// document (normally just the newest one — earlier turns were wrapped by the prior
// onGenerationEnd's _wizliveNormalizeDocument). Every other byte is untouched, so divergence
// from the cached prefix begins only at the newest turn, which is new text anyway.
//
// SAFETY OF THE IN-PLACE REPLACE: the search is anchored PAST the marker (chat region only,
// never the narrative prefix) and advances a forward cursor in document order, so a short
// Director body cannot collide with earlier prose. If the marker was trimmed out of the
// budgeted storyText, or a raw paragraph does not appear verbatim (whitespace shift), we skip
// that turn and leave it raw — degrading to exactly the pre-fix one-turn-lag behaviour, never
// worse. The DOCUMENT is not mutated here; that still happens post-generation in
// _wizliveNormalizeDocument, race-free. This only changes what the model READS this turn.
async function _wizliveCanonicalStoryOverride(params: any): Promise<string | null> {
    if (!_aeLiveWiz.active || _aeLiveWiz.markerId === null) return null;
    let wcStory = (params && params.storyText && params.storyText.content) ? params.storyText.content : '';
    if (!wcStory) return null;
    let wcMarkIdx = wcStory.indexOf(AE_WIZLIVE_MARKER_TEXT);
    if (wcMarkIdx < 0) return null;   // chat region trimmed out of budget — leave story text as NAI trimmed it
    let wcFrom = wcMarkIdx + AE_WIZLIVE_MARKER_TEXT.length;

    let wcAll: any[] = [];
    try { wcAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); }
    catch (eWc) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] canonical scan failed \u2014 story text left raw this turn:', eWc); return null; }

    let wcSeen = false;
    let wcChanged = false;
    for (let wcR of wcAll) {
        if (wcR.sectionId === _aeLiveWiz.markerId) { wcSeen = true; continue; }
        if (!wcSeen) continue;
        if (_aeLiveWiz.setupIds.indexOf(wcR.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
        if (!_wizliveRowIsUser(wcR.section)) continue;   // only Director turns get wrapped; Wizard replies pass through untouched
        let wcRaw = (wcR.section.text || '');
        if (!wcRaw.trim()) continue;
        // [v2.53.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-DOUBLE] Bare, header-led form matching the write pass.
        // [v2.58.0 WIZLIVE-CTX-STRIP, mirrors CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE v2.42.0] MODEL'S VIEW
        // strips the "Director:" header entirely. The DOCUMENT keeps "Director: body" (the
        // write pass is unchanged), but in context the model sees only the bare body text
        // inside NAI's own instruction brace. This is the structural anti-impersonation
        // fix: a model that never sees "Director:" in the transcript has no prefix to
        // continue when it wants to start the user's next turn. CharChat learned this at
        // v2.42.0 (removing literal braces from the model's view of POV turns); same
        // principle, same result.
        let wcBody = _wizliveStripDirectorHeader(_wizliveStripBraces(wcRaw));
        if (!wcBody) continue;
        if (wcBody === wcRaw) continue;                   // already bare in the document — model already sees it right
        let wcIdx = wcStory.indexOf(wcRaw, wcFrom);       // anchored past the marker, advancing in document order
        if (wcIdx < 0) continue;                          // trimmed / whitespace-shifted — degrade to raw (no worse than pre-fix)
        wcStory = wcStory.substring(0, wcIdx) + wcBody + wcStory.substring(wcIdx + wcRaw.length);
        wcFrom = wcIdx + wcBody.length;
        wcChanged = true;
    }
    return wcChanged ? wcStory : null;
}

// Walk the document from the marker down and rebuild the conversation. Role mapping:
// instruction-source paragraphs are user turns (NAI's { } instruct blocks); anything
// whose origin metadata is entirely user/edit-typed is also treated as a user turn
// (they forgot the braces); everything else is the wizard. Consecutive wizard
// paragraphs merge into one turn. Engine-authored setup paragraphs are excluded.
async function collectLiveWizTranscript(): Promise<{ msgs: WizardChatMessage[]; ids: number[] }> {
    let out: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    let ids: number[] = [];
    if (_aeLiveWiz.markerId === null) return { msgs: out, ids: ids };
    let all = await api.v1.document.scan();
    let seen = false;
    for (let row of all) {
        if (row.sectionId === _aeLiveWiz.markerId) { seen = true; ids.push(row.sectionId); continue; }
        if (!seen) continue;
        ids.push(row.sectionId);
        if (_aeLiveWiz.setupIds.indexOf(row.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
        let txt = (row.section.text || '').trim();
        if (!txt) continue;
        let isUser = _wizliveRowIsUser(row.section);
        if (isUser) {
            out.push({ role: 'user', content: _wizliveStripBraces(txt) });
        } else if (out.length > 0 && out[out.length - 1].role === 'assistant') {
            out[out.length - 1].content += '\n' + txt;
        } else {
            out.push({ role: 'assistant', content: txt });
        }
    }
    return { msgs: out, ids: ids };
}

// Run one distillation pass over the live transcript and RETURN the text without
// committing anything. The modal shows it as an editable preview the user can then
// save to the lorebook (or ignore). Replaces the old auto-commit "summarize" action.
async function previewSummarizeLiveChat(): Promise<string> {
    let collected = await collectLiveWizTranscript();
    if (collected.msgs.length === 0) return '';
    let sTranscript = collected.msgs.map(function(m) { return (m.role === 'user' ? 'USER: ' : 'WIZARD: ') + m.content; }).join('\n');
    let sOut = await executeYoinkGeneration([
        { role: 'system', content: 'Distill the following worldbuilding chat into dense, declarative canon statements suitable for a lorebook entry. Keep only established facts and decisions \u2014 drop questions, hedges, and conversational filler. Write in pure prose or short single-line statements. No markdown, no headers, no bullet lists. Be concise.' },
        { role: 'user', content: sTranscript }
    ], { tag: 'liveWizSummarize' });
    return (sOut || '').trim();
}

async function endLiveWizardChat(action: string): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeLiveWiz.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('No wizard chat is open.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 2500 }); return; }
    let act = (action === 'discard') ? 'discard' : 'save';

    let collected = await collectLiveWizTranscript();
    let msgs = collected.msgs;

    // Save FIRST, destroy LAST — a save failure returns early leaving the chat intact
    // in the document, so nothing is ever lost to a half-completed end.
    if (act === 'save' && msgs.length > 0) {
        try {
            let title = (_aeLiveWiz.title || '').trim() || 'Wizard Chat';
            await upsertChatEntry(_aeLiveWiz.sourceEntryId, title, msgs, _aeLiveWiz.topicId);
            api.v1.ui.toast('Chat saved to the CE Chats lorebook.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
        } catch (eS) {
            api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] lorebook save failed:', eS);
            api.v1.ui.toast('Save failed \u2014 the chat is still in your document, nothing was removed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 5000 });
            return;
        }
    }

    // Switch this session's context entries back off so story mode returns to its exact
    // pre-chat prefix (preserving the story-generation cache).
    if (_aeLiveWiz.contextEntryIds && _aeLiveWiz.contextEntryIds.length > 0) {
        try { await setContextEntriesActivation(_aeLiveWiz.contextEntryIds, false); }
        catch (eCtx) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] context deactivation failed:', eCtx); }
    }

    // Remove the chat region, marker included, in one call.
    try {
        if (collected.ids.length > 0) await api.v1.document.removeParagraphs(collected.ids);
    } catch (eR) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] paragraph removal failed \u2014 clean up manually from the marker down:', eR); }

    try { api.v1.editor.decorations.removeRules([AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_MARKER, AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_USER, AE_WIZLIVE_RULE_WIZ]); } catch (_eDr) {}

    // Restore pinned generation params (same field-by-field shape as the WIZMODE restore).
    if (_aeLiveWiz.paramSnapshot) {
        try {
            let ps: any = _aeLiveWiz.paramSnapshot;
            let r: any = { model: ps.model };
            if (ps.max_tokens !== undefined) r.max_tokens = ps.max_tokens;
            if (ps.temperature !== undefined) r.temperature = ps.temperature;
            if (ps.top_p !== undefined) r.top_p = ps.top_p;
            if (ps.top_k !== undefined) r.top_k = ps.top_k;
            if (ps.frequency_penalty !== undefined) r.frequency_penalty = ps.frequency_penalty;
            if (ps.presence_penalty !== undefined) r.presence_penalty = ps.presence_penalty;
            if (ps.min_p !== undefined) r.min_p = ps.min_p;
            await api.v1.generationParameters.update(r);
        } catch (eRe) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] param restore failed:', eRe); }
    }

    _aeLiveWiz = { active: false, markerId: null, setupIds: [], topicId: '', paramSnapshot: null, startedAt: 0, sourceEntryId: null, contextEntryIds: [], title: '' };
    try { await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_WIZLIVE); } catch (_eRm) {}
    api.v1.ui.toast('Wizard chat closed \u2014 story mode restored.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
    await broadcastWizLiveState();
}

// Startup rehydration: a chat left open in this story (script reload, tab close)
// comes back live IF its marker paragraph still exists; otherwise the stale state
// is cleared and any pinned params restored from the persisted snapshot. Context
// entries stay Always-On across a reload (lorebook state is story-scoped and
// persistent) and are only switched off when the chat is finally ended.
async function restoreLiveWizOnStartup(): Promise<void> {
    let raw = '';
    try { raw = (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZLIVE, '')) as string; } catch (_e0) {}
    if (!raw) return;
    let st: any = null;
    try { st = JSON.parse(raw); } catch (_e1) { st = null; }
    if (!st || !st.active || typeof st.markerId !== 'number') {
        try { await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_WIZLIVE); } catch (_e2) {}
        return;
    }
    let ids: number[] = [];
    try { ids = await api.v1.document.sectionIds(); } catch (_e3) {}
    if (ids.indexOf(st.markerId) < 0) {
        if (st.paramSnapshot) {
            try {
                let rp: any = { model: st.paramSnapshot.model };
                if (st.paramSnapshot.max_tokens !== undefined) rp.max_tokens = st.paramSnapshot.max_tokens;
                await api.v1.generationParameters.update(rp);
            } catch (_e4) {}
        }
        // Stale marker: make sure any leftover Always-On context entries are switched off.
        if (Array.isArray(st.contextEntryIds) && st.contextEntryIds.length > 0) {
            try { await setContextEntriesActivation(st.contextEntryIds, false); } catch (_e4b) {}
        }
        try { await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_WIZLIVE); } catch (_e5) {}
        aeLog('[AE:wizlive] stale chat state cleared (marker no longer in document).');
        return;
    }
    _aeLiveWiz = {
        active: true,
        markerId: st.markerId,
        setupIds: Array.isArray(st.setupIds) ? st.setupIds : [],
        topicId: st.topicId || '',
        paramSnapshot: st.paramSnapshot || null,
        startedAt: st.startedAt || Date.now(),
        sourceEntryId: (typeof st.sourceEntryId === 'string') ? st.sourceEntryId : null,
        contextEntryIds: Array.isArray(st.contextEntryIds) ? st.contextEntryIds : [],
        title: (typeof st.title === 'string' && st.title) ? st.title : 'Wizard Chat'
    };
    try { await _wizliveRegisterDecorations(st.markerId); await refreshLiveWizDecorations(); }
    catch (eD) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] decoration restore failed (cosmetic only):', eD); }
    api.v1.ui.toast('Wizard chat still open in this story \u2014 continue below the marker or end it from the World-Build Wizard.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 6000 });
    await broadcastWizLiveState();
}
// ── [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] end ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// ============================================================================
// [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] IN-STORY CHARACTER CHAT (P1 — engine core)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// A texting-style conversation with ONE character, held in the document tail,
// driven by NAI's own Send/generate path. Modeled on WIZLIVE (marker paragraph +
// instruct-block turns + param pin + strip-on-end) but with one decisive
// difference: WIZLIVE deliberately leaves the top of context untouched to
// preserve the story prefix cache, whereas CHARCHAT OWNS THE WHOLE PROMPT.
//
// While a chat is active, runCharChatContextOverride() replaces, per generation:
//   systemPrompt  chat framing + "you are X" + X's folded lorebook identity +
//                 X's dynamic block + X's persona register.  (top of context)
//   lorebook      EVERY character-linked entry deactivated — both participants'
//                 (already folded into the system prompt above, so leaving them
//                 on would duplicate) and everyone else's (the persona-bleed
//                 guard: only the two chat participants are scene-present, so
//                 the NPC web between them surfaces naturally). NON-character
//                 entries (locations, factions, world lore) are left alone.
//   authorsNote   REPURPOSED as the contact's ephemeral/emotional state. Not
//                 suppressed — the AN is a near-bottom injection slot and this is
//                 exactly the sort of recency-weighted content it is for. Note it
//                 lands at the user's configured depth (~3 paragraphs from the
//                 end), i.e. INSIDE recent chat history, not after it; that is why
//                 the next-message instruction uses storyText instead.
//   storyText     "[ NARRATIVE CONTEXT ]" header prepended; the next-message
//                 instruction APPENDED (true bottom, invisible in the document).
//
// COST: overriding the system prompt changes the top of context, so the story
// prefix cache is invalidated on entering and again on exiting. That cost is
// PER-SESSION, not per-turn — mid-chat the sys prompt/memory/lorebook are
// constant and only the tail grows, so the cache behaves normally from turn 2.
// Reasoned from how prefix caching works generally, NOT from an NAI-specific
// citation — if latency/cost looks wrong in testing, look here first.
//
// NO CONTEXT POISONING: story generation cannot run while a chat is open, so any
// generation firing while _aeCharChat.active IS a chat turn, which is what makes
// applying the override unconditionally safe. That guarantee rests on the
// mutual-exclusion guards (in startCharChat / startLiveWizardChat /
// resumeLiveWizardChatFromEntry / aeSetWizardMode) plus the fact that ending a
// chat strips every chat paragraph, returning the document byte-identical.
// The override is applied on the dryRun pass too, so NAI's context viewer shows
// the real chat context — that is the debugging window for this whole subsystem.
//
// NOT IN P1 (do not assume these exist): persona-register editor/generator (P3),
// Save/Summarize to a "CE Character Chats" lorebook category + its cached-id
// storage key (P4 — deliberately NOT declared here, a key with no reader is the
// dead code this project keeps cutting), activation modes (P4). Decoration bubble
// polish (was P5) and a bridge header presence (was planned as P6's HUD panel, built
// instead as bridge v2.14.0's smaller dropdown) are DONE as of v2.47.0/bridge v2.14.0.
// endCharChat therefore ships DISCARD ONLY.
// ============================================================================
interface CharChatState {
    active: boolean;
    markerId: number | null;        // sectionId of the boundary marker paragraph
    setupIds: number[];             // engine-authored setup paragraphs (excluded from the transcript)
    // [v2.46.0 CHARCHAT-SHELL-BASELINE] stagedPovIds DELETED. It recorded the ids of POV turns
    // the ENGINE or the BRIDGE authored, because origin metadata cannot tell an engine-written
    // POV turn from an engine-written contact reply (both are 'script'). With ops 'stage',
    // 'send' and 'povclaim' gone, nothing outside NAI writes a POV turn, so there is nothing to
    // record and the premise of the field is gone with its writers. This is the deletion
    // v2.34.0's own index entry called for in advance: "If bridge text is ever made genuinely
    // user-origin, stagedPovIds becomes redundant and should be deleted, not left lying."
    // Classification reverts to _charchatRowIsPov's origin rule — the v2.31.0 behaviour, which
    // is the last one the user confirmed formatted correctly and the one that ran for every
    // version before a bridge started writing paragraphs.
    // *** THE ONE THING TO WATCH ON FIRST LIVE TEST *** this field existed to prevent the
    // "{ {Nate: ...} }" / "{ {Nate: Emotion: neutral} }" corruption class. It is only safe to
    // remove because the user genuinely types now. If that corruption reappears, the origin
    // rule is not seeing 'user' on typed text and THIS is the first place to look — do not
    // reinstate the field without first logging what NAI actually stamps.
    // Saved state from before this version still carries the key; rehydration simply no longer
    // reads it, so there is no migration and no throw.
    // [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] What this subsystem is doing RIGHT NOW, for the
    // bridge's status line: '' | 'thinking' | 'replying' | 'image'. Deliberately NOT
    // persisted anywhere — it describes an in-flight call, so a copy that survived a
    // reload could only ever be a lie. Broadcast-only state; saveCharChatState never
    // reads it back in because there is nothing to restore.
    busyStage: string;
    // [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-POST] thoughtPass / thoughtAnchorIds DELETED with the
    // v2.39.0-v2.44.1 native-thought design. Nothing reads them; the thought no longer rides
    // NAI's own generation, so there is no pass to flag and nothing written to the document to
    // anchor against. Old saved state may still carry both keys \u2014 harmless: restoreCharChat
    // OnStartup rebuilds this object field by field and simply never looks at them.
    contactId: string;              // the character being texted \u2014 the model speaks AS this one
    contactName: string;            // denormalized so context assembly needs no character load on the hot path
    povId: string;                  // [v2.29.0, REVERSES the old LOCKED DECISION] whose phone this is, AND now the second half of the saved-thread partition key (see charChatEntryId) — no longer label-only. '' = unset ("No POV" bucket, back-compat with pre-v2.29.0 threads).
    povName: string;
    paramSnapshot: any;             // generationParameters snapshot restored on end (persisted so a reload can still restore)
    startedAt: number;
    lastEmotion: string;            // [v2.21.0] last parsed Emotion: tag \u2014 diagnostic/bridge only; the tag itself stays in the document
}
var _aeCharChat: CharChatState = { active: false, markerId: null, setupIds: [], busyStage: '', contactId: '', contactName: '', povId: '', povName: '', paramSnapshot: null, startedAt: 0, lastEmotion: '' };
// [v2.37.0 CHARCHAT-DOM-INJECT] _aeCharChatDraft REMOVED with v2.36.0's commit-on-Send.
// The draft never reaches the engine at all now: the bridge holds it in its own memory and
// injects it into NAI's editor at pointerdown on NAI's Send (see the CE-INDEX). The engine
// only learns about the turn afterwards, via op 'povclaim', by which point it is already
// real document text NAI itself committed.

// [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-ARM-CANCEL] The in-flight post-reply thought's cancellation signal, or
// null when none is running. A bare module var and deliberately NOT on CharChatState: it
// describes a call that is happening right now, so a copy that survived a reload could only
// be a lie (same reasoning as busyStage, which is also never persisted). Set by
// generateChatEphemeral for the life of its api.v1.generate() call and cleared in its finally;
// read ONLY by op 'arm', which cancels it because a Send is imminent. See the CE-INDEX
// v2.45.0 block for why a cancel and not a hope that the sequence is serial.
var _ccThoughtSignal: any = null;

// [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE-RACE / CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG, per user report: sometimes no
// reply to the first message in a chat, and a suspicion that the busy/thinking indicator
// clears before it is actually safe to Send again] One shared timestamp + logger used by
// every checkpoint in the post-reply lifecycle (reply landed -> thinking stage -> thought
// settled -> image stage -> images settled -> busy cleared -> ghost signal on/off -> next
// Send's 'arm' op), all under ONE tag so the console shows a single ordered timeline with
// the gap between each step.
// [CORRECTED v2.61.0, per user's live log] v2.60.0 wrongly declared CHARCHAT-MERGE-RACE
// ruled out. It is NOT — it is CONFIRMED, just with a longer settle window than the single
// 150ms retry tested. Live evidence: a turn's split-pass found "0 split" on BOTH the first
// scan AND the retry (150ms apart), yet a SEPARATE, LATER document.scan() (the empty-reply
// dump, itself running some hundreds of ms further out in the same handler) found the full
// reply text already present. NAI was still actively writing the reply's paragraphs when
// both split-pass scans ran; a single fixed retry is not patient enough. See
// _charchatSplitMergedSections' own v2.61.0 CHARCHAT-SETTLE-POLL rewrite below, which
// replaces the fixed retry with a bounded poll against the EXPECTED growth for a real turn,
// not mere pass-to-pass agreement (two scans can wrongly agree "nothing changed" if NAI
// simply hadn't started writing by either of them). CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG's OWN hypothesis
// (busy indicator clearing too early) still looks genuinely ruled out — arm events keep
// arriving many seconds after busyStage clears, nowhere near a slot-release race — so the
// race-diag marks stay purely as supporting context now, not the leading suspect.
var _ccRaceDiagLastAt: number = 0;
function _ccRaceDiagMark(label: string): void {
    let ccNow = Date.now();
    let ccElapsed = _ccRaceDiagLastAt ? (ccNow - _ccRaceDiagLastAt) : 0;
    aeLog('[AE:charchat:race-diag] ' + label + ' (+' + ccElapsed + 'ms since last mark)');
    _ccRaceDiagLastAt = ccNow;
}

// [DIAG v2.60.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG, per user: "nothing is showing in the story editor
// when a response never happens"] Tracks the non-setup section count left behind by the LAST
// successful turn. A real turn always adds AT LEAST 2 new sections by the time
// _charchatSplitMergedSectionsPass has run (the Director's own paragraph, plus the reply —
// merged-and-split counts as 2 same as separately-generated).
// [CORRECTED v2.61.0] This fired a CONFIRMED FALSE POSITIVE: the dump it produced showed the
// reply text fully present and correctly formed — the diagnostic's OWN comparison scan simply
// ran too early, before NAI had finished writing (see CHARCHAT-MERGE-RACE above). The
// v2.61.0 settle-poll rewrite feeds this check a section count that has already waited for
// the expected growth (or genuinely given up after a bounded number of attempts), which
// should make a real firing of this diagnostic much more trustworthy going forward — a firing
// now means the settle-poll itself gave up, not that a single short check happened to run
// early. -1 sentinel skips the check on the very first turn of a chat (no prior baseline
// exists yet). Bare module var, not on CharChatState, for the same reason _ccThoughtSignal
// isn't: it describes the state of THIS session's most recent turn, and a value that survived
// a reload would be a stale lie — reset to -1 on start/resume/end. TEMPORARY: remove once
// CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG is closed.
var _ccLastSeenSectionCount: number = -1;

// [DIAG v2.63.0 CHARCHAT-RESP-DIAG] The blind spot every previous diagnostic in this arc
// worked AROUND instead of through. CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG watches the turn lifecycle,
// CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG watches the document afterwards — but nothing has ever watched
// the one place the reply text physically passes through on its way from NAI to the page:
// the onResponse hook. Every conclusion of the form "NAI generated nothing" has been an
// INFERENCE from an absence downstream, and it cannot distinguish the two cases that need
// different fixes:
//     calls === 0                 -> the model really did return nothing. Prompt/transport.
//     calls > 0, finalSeen false  -> text WAS delivered and we deferred it with
//                                    seenData:false, and the final call that was supposed to
//                                    re-deliver it never arrived. WE lost the reply.
// The second case has been flagged UNVERIFIED in onResponse's own comment since v2.21.0 and
// has never been tested. These four vars answer it in one turn.
// Keyed by continuityId so a stale count from the previous turn can never be misread as this
// turn's: onGenerationEnd compares its own continuityId against _ccRespCid and reports
// "onResponse never fired" when they differ, rather than reprinting old numbers.
var _ccRespCid: string = '';
var _ccRespCalls: number = 0;
var _ccRespBest: string = '';       // longest text seen this turn — seenData:false re-delivery is cumulative (script-types: "the same data in addition to any new data"), so longest === most complete
var _ccRespFinalSeen: boolean = false;

const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_MARKER = 'ae-charchat-marker';
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_POV    = 'ae-charchat-pov';      // POV turns — right-justified bubble
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_CONTACT= 'ae-charchat-contact';  // the character's replies — left-justified bubble
// [v2.47.0 CHARCHAT-BUBBLES] INLINE, not node: the Emotion: line is the SECOND LINE of the
// SAME section as the reply body (see CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX's own comment — one section,
// two lines), so it cannot get its own node-level box. A node rule styles the whole
// paragraph; this inline rule styles only the substring its `match` pattern finds within
// that same paragraph, layered on top of the CONTACT node rule's bubble. Per user: "emotions
// become a subtle tagline after the bubble - not their own [bubble]."
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOTION= 'ae-charchat-emotion';
// [v2.48.0 CHARCHAT-IMGWIDGET] The one string both artifacts must agree on. The bridge
// greps for this exact prefix with a TreeWalker (grep AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_SENTINEL there too
// — it is duplicated as a literal, since the two files share no module). Changing it
// is a flag-day: change BOTH or chat photos silently stop binding.
// [v2.49.0 CHARCHAT-BUBBLES2] Three more rules, all forced by one live finding: NAI
// SPLITS a section whose text contains '\n' into separate sections. _charchatFormatReply
// writes "Name: body" + '\n' + "  Emotion: word" as ONE updateParagraph call, and v2.47.0
// assumed that stayed one section with two lines (CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX's own comment
// says as much). It does not. The emotion line lands as its OWN section, inherits the
// CONTACT node rule, and renders as its own separate bubble — which is exactly what the
// user reported ("I'd prefer the emotion tags included in the bubble above them instead of
// getting their own").
//   _HEAD    a contact bubble that IS followed by an emotion line: flat bottom, no bottom
//            border, no bottom margin — so it butts against the tail below it.
//   _EMOBOX  the emotion line's own section: flat top, no top border, no top margin, same
//            background. HEAD + EMOBOX together read as ONE bubble with a tagline in it.
//   _NAME    hides the "Name: " prefix, per user ("we won't need the name headers
//            displayed"). Inline + display:none: the text STAYS in the document, which is
//            non-negotiable — _charchatRowIsPov, the transcript walk, and the save format
//            all read it. This hides it; it does not remove it.
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_HEAD   = 'ae-charchat-head';
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOBOX = 'ae-charchat-emobox';
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_NAME   = 'ae-charchat-name';
// [v2.51.1 CHATIMG-HIDE-TAG, per user] Hides the resolved/unresolved "[Image#hex: desc]"
// tag text in a chat bubble. The tag STAYS in the document (it is the durable pointer —
// see _charchatImageRx's note) but is the raw image PROMPT, which the user should not see:
// the bridge paints the real photo from a separate widget anchor. display:none, same
// mechanism as AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_NAME hiding the "Name:" prefix.
const AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_IMGTAG = 'ae-charchat-imgtag';
const AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_SENTINEL = 'ce-chatimg:';
// [v2.50.0 CHARCHAT-AVATAR] Second sentinel, same channel. One anchor per CONTACT TURN
// (not per section — a three-paragraph reply gets ONE avatar, beside its first line).
// [v2.57.0 CHARCHAT-AVATAR-EMOTION] Payload is now 'contactId' OR 'contactId|emotion',
// where emotion is one of the canonical 24 (charChatParseEmotion -> resolveEmotion, read
// off the turn's own trailing "  Emotion: <word>" line). The bridge resolves that to a
// per-emotion portrait slot (charId+'_em_'+emotion) and falls back to the character's
// hardwired avatar slot (charId+'_em_Avatar', legacy charId+'_portrait') when that slot
// has no image — see _loadCharEmotionImage bridge-side. The bare no-suffix form is still
// emitted for a turn with no readable emotion and still means "just use the Avatar slot".
// Either way the bridge floats the image into the gutter the contact bubble's
// max-width:78% leaves open on the left. POV turns get NONE, per user ("avatars attached
// to character (not pov) message bubbles"). Same flag-day rule as the image sentinel: this
// literal is duplicated in the bridge, change BOTH or avatars silently stop binding — and
// the '|' suffix specifically needs bridge >= 2.47.0 (an older bridge treats the whole
// body as the id and matches no character).
const AE_CHARCHAT_AVA_SENTINEL = 'ce-chatava:';
const AE_CHARCHAT_MAX_TOKENS  = 120;                    // SMS-length DEFAULT pin — [v2.24.0] now only the fallback for a contact whose persona verbosity is 'auto'. See charChatMaxTokensFor().
// [v2.27.0] AE_CHARCHAT_THINK_TOKENS removed — CHARCHAT-THINK is gone (see the
// CE-INDEX v2.27.0 entry); its replacement, generateEphemeral(), is a fully separate
// call with its own budget and shares nothing with the reply's own max_tokens pin.
const AE_CHARCHAT_SUMMARY_MAX_TOKENS = 300;             // [v2.23.0] Matches AE_WIZARD_MAX_TOKENS's precedent for "a short prose passage" — used only transiently during previewSummarizeCharChat, never left pinned.
// [v2.45.0] AE_CHARCHAT_THINK_MS DELETED — zero callers, verified. It was v2.38.0's
// aeGenerateBounded timeout; that primitive went with v2.39.0 and the constant has sat
// orphaned ever since. The two context budgets below stay and are deliberately asymmetric:
// the transcript is what the contact is actually answering, the story is background, so the
// story gets the smaller share.
const AE_CHATEPH_STORY_TOKENS = 700;
const AE_CHATEPH_CHAT_CHARS = 2400;
const AE_CHARCHAT_MARKER_TEXT = '\u2501\u2501 CHARACTER CHAT \u2501\u2501 The story above is reference material. Everything below this line is a text-message conversation and will be removed when the chat ends.';

// [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] Global fallback texting rubric, used when a character has no
// chatPersona of their own (or has one that is disabled/empty). Adapted from the Chat
// Simulator's proven persona rubric. P3 adds the per-character editor + generator that
// can override this.
const CHARCHAT_DEFAULT_RUBRIC = [
    'Translate this character\u2019s personality into a realistic text-messaging style based on their age, traits, habits and tech familiarity. For example:',
    '* Digital native / casual: relaxed grammar, lowercase, slang.',
    '* Professional / adult: standard punctuation, clear syntax, concise phrasing.',
    '* Senior / tech-unsavvy: overly formal grammar, or excessive punctuation ("...").',
    'Never repeat the same emoticon consecutively. Avoid cliches. Only rarely include narrative prose or actions (e.g. *sighs*). Messages must be under 32 words \u2014 keep replies SMS-length.'
].join('\n');

async function saveCharChatState(): Promise<void> {
    try { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CHARCHAT, JSON.stringify(_aeCharChat)); }
    catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] state save failed:', e); }
}

// [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-HUD] Now carries the TURN LIST, not just chat metadata. This is the
// one real gap that stopped the bridge from being able to paint bubbles: everything else
// it needs already existed engine-side. Turns are assembled by charChatTurnsPayload()
// from _charchatScanTurns, so the bridge never has to infer role from the DOM or from
// text — it renders exactly what the document's own section metadata says.
async function broadcastCharChatState(): Promise<void> {
    try {
        let ccTurns: any[] = [];
        if (_aeCharChat.active) {
            try { ccTurns = await charChatTurnsPayload(); }
            catch (eT) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] turn payload build failed (sending metadata only):', eT); }
        }
        let ccCfgB = await loadConfig();
        // [v2.35.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT] The contact's current inner state, surfaced so the
        // header can show it on demand. Same field generateEphemeral() persists and the
        // char edit modal already displays (char.ephemeral.compressedLine) — read-only
        // here, this never generates one. Empty string when there is no thought yet or
        // the character can't be loaded; the bridge hides the control on falsy.
        let ccThought = '';
        try {
            if (_aeCharChat.active && _aeCharChat.contactId) {
                let ccThChar = await loadCharacter(_aeCharChat.contactId);
                if (ccThChar && ccThChar.ephemeral && ccThChar.ephemeral.compressedLine) {
                    ccThought = String(ccThChar.ephemeral.compressedLine).trim();
                }
            }
        } catch (_eTh) { ccThought = ''; }
        await broadcastToTM(EVT_CHARCHAT_STATE, {
            active: _aeCharChat.active, contactId: _aeCharChat.contactId, contactName: _aeCharChat.contactName,
            povId: _aeCharChat.povId, povName: _aeCharChat.povName, startedAt: _aeCharChat.startedAt,
            lastEmotion: _aeCharChat.lastEmotion,
            lastThought: ccThought,
            turns: ccTurns,
            images: !!(ccCfgB && ccCfgB.charChatImages),
            freshThoughts: !!(ccCfgB && ccCfgB.charChatFreshThoughts),
            busyStage: _aeCharChat.busyStage || ''   // [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST]
        });
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] state broadcast failed:', e); }
}

// [v2.51.5 CHARCHAT-THREADS] Push the saved-thread roster to the bridge's Chat launcher.
// Deliberately thin: listCharChatThreads() already returns exactly the per-(contactId,
// povId) records the picker wants, with turnCount; we drop entryId on the wire (the bridge
// never resolves an entry id — it sends AE_CHARCHAT_START { contactId, povId } and the
// engine re-derives the deterministic id via charChatEntryId). One bulk lorebook.entries()
// call, same discipline the native picker follows — never N lookups.
async function broadcastCharChatThreads(): Promise<void> {
    try {
        let ccThreads = await listCharChatThreads();
        let ccOut = (ccThreads || []).map(function(t: any) {
            return { contactId: t.contactId, povId: t.povId, turnCount: t.turnCount };
        });
        await broadcastToTM(EVT_CHARCHAT_THREADS_RESP, { threads: ccOut });
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] threads broadcast failed:', e); }
}

// [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] Set the in-flight stage and push it. One line at every
// call site, and it always broadcasts — the whole complaint this answers is that work
// happens with no indication, so a setter that could silently not repaint would be
// pointless. Cheap: broadcastCharChatState is already fired after every chat action.
async function _charchatSetBusy(stage: string): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeCharChat.busyStage === stage) return;
    _aeCharChat.busyStage = stage || '';
    aeLog('[AE:charchat:busy] ' + (stage || '(idle)'));
    try { await broadcastCharChatState(); } catch (_eSb) {}
}

// [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-HUD] Contacts screen data. POV-first, mirroring the native picker's
// own flow (openCharChatPicker -> _charchatShowContactsForPov). ONE listCharChatThreads()
// call for every pair, filtered in memory — same "one bulk lorebook call, never N
// lookups" discipline the native picker already follows.
// [v2.46.0 CHARCHAT-SHELL-BASELINE] broadcastCharChatContacts() DELETED. Its only reader was
// bridge v2.12.0's contacts screen, deleted with the panel; its only sender was that screen's
// requestCharChatContacts(). AE_CHARCHAT_CONTACTS_REQ's dispatcher case goes with it (grep it
// — same pass, not left as a handler for a message nobody sends). Picking a POV and a contact
// is openCharChatPicker's job until the overlay gives it a home; that native path never went
// through this function. _charchatThreadsForPov and listCharChatThreads are UNTOUCHED and
// still have live callers (the native picker) — verified, not assumed.

// ── Decorations: POV (amber) vs contact (teal) ──────────────────────────
// Same shape as _wizliveRegisterDecorations: registered once with empty scopes, then
// refreshed from the live document on start, restore, and each turn end. Bubble polish
// is P5 — this is only enough to see the chat region and who said what.
async function _charchatRegisterDecorations(markerId: number): Promise<void> {
    // [v2.47.0 CHARCHAT-BUBBLES] Real bubbles, not tinted highlighting. Every value below is
    // an --ae-* theme token, the SAME custom properties the bridge's own CSS uses — not
    // hardcoded colours. This works with no new cross-artifact push because theme classes
    // apply to document.documentElement (verified this pass: the theme picker sets
    // document.documentElement.className), and NAI's own document renders as descendant
    // content of that same element, so var(--ae-accent-2-dim) etc. resolves via ordinary CSS
    // inheritance — the bridge never has to hand the engine resolved colour values.
    // UNVERIFIED, worth a live check: whether a node marker's `style` reaches the paragraph's
    // own box (so margin-left:auto / max-width actually justify it) as opposed to only an
    // inner text wrapper. script-types.txt's own wording ("node markers style entire
    // paragraphs") says box-level, and that is the assumption this rule is built on.
    await api.v1.editor.decorations.registerRules([
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_MARKER,  type: 'node', match: '^', style: { backgroundColor: 'color-mix(in srgb, var(--ae-chat-marker) 22%, transparent)', borderTop: '2px solid var(--ae-chat-marker)', borderBottom: '1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--ae-chat-marker) 40%, transparent)' }, scope: { sectionIds: [markerId] } }, // [v2.50.1 CHAT-TOKEN-PARITY] was 3 hardcoded rgba(232,160,74,*) literals
        // POV (yours): right-justified, accent-2 tinted — matches the bridge's own send-bubble language.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_POV,     type: 'node', match: '^', style: {
            display: 'block', marginLeft: 'auto', marginRight: '0', maxWidth: '78%',
            padding: '8px 12px', borderRadius: 'var(--ae-chat-radius) var(--ae-chat-radius) 5px var(--ae-chat-radius)', // [v2.50.1] was hardcoded 16px
            backgroundColor: 'var(--ae-chat-pov-bg)', border: '1px solid var(--ae-chat-pov-border)', // [v2.50.1] was --ae-accent-2-dim/-border directly
            color: 'var(--ae-text-main)', fontFamily: 'var(--ae-font-sans)'
        }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        // Contact (theirs): left-justified, raised-surface tinted.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_CONTACT, type: 'node', match: '^', style: {
            display: 'block', marginRight: 'auto', marginLeft: '0', maxWidth: '78%',
            padding: '8px 12px', borderRadius: 'var(--ae-chat-radius) var(--ae-chat-radius) var(--ae-chat-radius) 5px', // [v2.50.1] was hardcoded 16px
            backgroundColor: 'var(--ae-chat-them-bg)', border: '1px solid var(--ae-chat-them-border)', // [v2.50.1] was --ae-bg-raised/-border directly
            color: 'var(--ae-text-main)', fontFamily: 'var(--ae-font-sans)'
        }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        // [v2.47.0] The Emotion: tagline. INLINE type, matched by pattern (not a fixed
        // sectionId list) — CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX is exactly two leading spaces then
        // 'Emotion: ', so the pattern below is deliberately loose (\s* not two literal
        // spaces) to survive if that whitespace ever changes without this rule going stale.
        // Scoped to contact sectionIds only (see refreshCharChatDecorations) — POV turns
        // never carry an emotion line, so scoping the pattern search to contact ids only is
        // strictly cheaper, not a correctness requirement.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOTION, type: 'inline', match: '^\\s*Emotion:.*$', flags: 'm', style: {
            color: 'var(--ae-text-dim)', fontStyle: 'italic', fontSize: 'var(--ae-font-size-xs)',
            fontFamily: 'var(--ae-font-sans)'
        }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        // [v2.49.0] A contact bubble WITH an emotion tail below it. Same box as CONTACT but
        // open at the bottom, so the tail can close it.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_HEAD, type: 'node', match: '^', style: {
