            display: 'block', marginRight: 'auto', marginLeft: '0', marginBottom: '0', maxWidth: '78%',
            padding: '8px 12px 2px', borderRadius: 'var(--ae-chat-radius) var(--ae-chat-radius) 0 0', // [v2.50.1] was hardcoded 16px
            backgroundColor: 'var(--ae-chat-them-bg)', border: '1px solid var(--ae-chat-them-border)', borderBottom: 'none', // [v2.50.1] was --ae-bg-raised/-border directly
            color: 'var(--ae-text-main)', fontFamily: 'var(--ae-font-sans)'
        }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        // [v2.49.0] The emotion tail. Closes the bubble the HEAD opened — same background,
        // no top border, no top margin, so the seam disappears and the two sections read as
        // one bubble with a tagline inside it.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOBOX, type: 'node', match: '^', style: {
            display: 'block', marginRight: 'auto', marginLeft: '0', marginTop: '0', maxWidth: '78%',
            padding: '0 12px 7px', borderRadius: '0 0 var(--ae-chat-radius) 5px', // [v2.50.1] was hardcoded 16px
            backgroundColor: 'var(--ae-chat-them-bg)', border: '1px solid var(--ae-chat-them-border)', borderTop: 'none', // [v2.50.1] was --ae-bg-raised/-border directly
            color: 'var(--ae-text-dim)', fontFamily: 'var(--ae-font-sans)'
        }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        // [v2.49.0] Hide the "Name: " prefix. match is rebuilt per refresh from the live
        // names (see _charchatNameRuleMatch) — registered here with a deliberately
        // never-matching pattern so a stale rule cannot hide the wrong text before the
        // first refresh lands.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_NAME, type: 'inline', match: '(?!)', flags: 'm', style: { display: 'none' }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } },
        // [v2.51.1 CHATIMG-HIDE-TAG, per user] Hide the image PROMPT tag in the bubble. The
        // pattern is STATIC (both the resolved "[Image#hex: desc]" and the transient
        // unresolved "[Image: desc]" forms), so — like EMOTION above — it is registered once
        // with its real match here and only RESCOPED per refresh, never rebuilt. flags 'gi':
        // global so both tags in a two-image turn are hidden, case-insensitive so a
        // lower-case model-written "[image: ...]" is caught too (the documented flags form,
        // script-types.txt registerRules example). scope starts empty; refresh fills it.
        { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_IMGTAG, type: 'inline', match: '\\[[ \\t]*Image(?:#[0-9a-f]{8})?[ \\t]*:[ \\t]*[^\\]\\n\\r]+?[ \\t]*\\]', flags: 'gi', style: { display: 'none' }, scope: { sectionIds: [] } }
    ]);
}

// [v2.49.0] The "Name: " prefix pattern for the two live speakers. Returns a
// never-matching pattern when neither name is known, rather than something like '^:' that
// could match real prose.
function _charchatNameRuleMatch(): string {
    let ccNames: string[] = [];
    if (_aeCharChat.contactName) ccNames.push(_charchatEscapeRx(_aeCharChat.contactName));
    if (_aeCharChat.povName) ccNames.push(_charchatEscapeRx(_aeCharChat.povName));
    if (!ccNames.length) return '(?!)';
    return '^(?:' + ccNames.join('|') + '):[ \\t]*';
}

// [v2.48.0 CHARCHAT-IMGWIDGET] Image anchors for the chat.
//
// WHY A WIDGET AND NOT A UIPartImage: script-types.txt is explicit about
// UIPartImage.src — "Image data as a base64 data URI... URLs are not supported for
// security reasons." So the ONLY way an engine-authored image part could show a chat
// photo is by carrying the whole base64 payload through the Worker, against
// memoryLimit: 128. It is not a preference: the platform forbids the cheap path. What
// this function creates instead is an EMPTY ANCHOR carrying a 1px transparent SENTINEL
// STRING; the BRIDGE finds that string with a TreeWalker and paints the real <img> from
// its own IndexedDB, so the bytes never enter the Worker at all. This is exactly the
// mechanism Auto-Illustrator v3 runs in production (see its createWidgets + the bridge's
// findPlaceholder/injectImage), adopted rather than re-derived.
//
// WHY THIS SOLVES DRIFT, per user ("To keep them anchored, we may need a native widget
// to keep them in place without drift"): a node widget is positioned BY NAI relative to
// its sectionId and repositioned by NAI on every document edit, and disposed by NAI when
// its section is removed. The anchoring is the platform's job, not a poll of ours —
// which is what the deleted panel's _ccStartAnchor/_ccApplyAnchor poll was for.
//
// THE SENTINEL CARRIES THE 8-HEX SHORT ID, NOT THE FULL UUID, and that is forced, not
// sloppy: _charchatProcessImages mints a full api.v1.uuid(), dispatches it to the bridge
// (which dbSaves under the FULL uuid), but writes only the 8-hex prefix into the document
// tag. The document is this function's only source of truth on a restore/reload, so the
// short id is all it can know. The bridge prefix-resolves short -> full against its own
// gallery index. Do not "fix" this by widening the document tag: the tag is prose the
// user can see, and the short form is deliberate.
//
// WIDGETS DO NOT SURVIVE A RELOAD, and this function is the rebuild: it is called from
// refreshCharChatDecorations (deliberately — that is ALREADY called on start, restore,
// and every turn end, so piggybacking guarantees the widgets and the bubble decorations
// can never drift out of sync across those seven call sites). clearWidgets() first, then
// recreate from a fresh scan — same clear-and-rebuild shape Auto-Illustrator uses. No
// tombstone list is needed here, unlike Auto-Illustrator's: this rebuilds from the
// DOCUMENT TAGS, so an image whose tag the user deleted simply is not found and cannot
// be resurrected. The document is the state.
async function refreshCharChatImageWidgets(): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active || _aeCharChat.markerId === null) return;
    // clearWidgets() is scoped to widgets created BY THIS SCRIPT (per its doc comment),
    // and the chat is this script's only widget user, so a blanket clear is safe today.
    // If a second widget feature ever lands, this must become handle-tracked instead.
    try { api.v1.editor.decorations.clearWidgets(); } catch (_eccW0) {}

    let ccAll: any[] = [];
    try { ccAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); } catch (_eccWs) { return; }

    let ccOpts: any[] = [];
    // [v2.57.0 CHARCHAT-AVATAR-EMOTION] Avatar options are COLLECTED here and emitted
    // after the walk, rather than pushed inline like the image sentinels below. Reason,
    // and it is a real ordering constraint rather than a style choice: the emotion a turn
    // carries is written on its OWN section BELOW the body ("  Emotion: happy" — see
    // CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX), but the avatar has to anchor on the section ABOVE the
    // body (see the sectionId note below — the bridge's gutter positioning depends on it).
    // So at the moment the anchor is chosen the emotion is still unread. Deferring lets one
    // walk do both without a lookahead index.
    // NOT a second document scan — ccAll is already in memory; this is a second loop over
    // the same array, which is why anchoring after the Emotion: line (single-pass, but it
    // would land the avatar below the message instead of beside its first line) was not
    // worth the CSS rework.
    let ccAvaRuns: any[] = [];
    let ccPendingRun: any = null;   // the run awaiting its Emotion: line; null when none is open
    let ccSeen = false;
    let ccPrevId = _aeCharChat.markerId;   // [v2.50.0] section above the current one; seeds to the marker
    // [v2.50.0 CHARCHAT-AVATAR] Track contact RUNS so exactly one avatar anchors per turn.
    // ccInContactRun is true for the 2nd..Nth section of a multi-paragraph reply, which is
    // how a three-line reply gets ONE avatar (on its first section) rather than three.
    let ccInContactRun = false;
    for (let ccI = 0; ccI < ccAll.length; ccI++) {
        let ccRow = ccAll[ccI];
        if (ccRow.sectionId === _aeCharChat.markerId) { ccSeen = true; ccInContactRun = false; ccPendingRun = null; continue; }
        if (!ccSeen) continue;
        let ccTxt = (ccRow.section && ccRow.section.text) || '';

        // Avatar anchoring is independent of the image scan below and must run even for a
        // section with no image tag, so it comes first and does not `continue` on empty text.
        if (_aeCharChat.setupIds.indexOf(ccRow.sectionId) >= 0) {
            ccInContactRun = false;                       // setup paragraphs are not turns
            ccPendingRun = null;
        } else {
            let ccEmoLine = /^[ \t]*Emotion:/.test(ccTxt);
            let ccIsPov = _charchatRowIsPov(ccRow.section, ccRow.sectionId);
            if (ccEmoLine || ccIsPov) {
                // Emotion tail belongs to the contact turn above it (not a new one); POV
                // turns get no avatar. Either way, break the run without anchoring.
                // [v2.57.0] The tail is now also where the open run LEARNS its emotion.
                // charChatParseEmotion is the shared parser (not a fourth private copy of
                // the pattern) and it already runs resolveEmotion, so the sentinel can only
                // ever carry one of the canonical 24 — exactly the vocabulary the bridge's
                // per-emotion slot lookup keys off. A malformed tail returns '' and the
                // sentinel degrades to the bare contactId form, which the bridge still
                // paints as the plain Avatar slot.
                if (ccEmoLine && ccPendingRun) {
                    ccPendingRun.emotion = charChatParseEmotion(ccTxt);
                    ccPendingRun = null;
                }
                if (ccIsPov) { ccInContactRun = false; ccPendingRun = null; }
            } else {
                // A contact BODY section. Anchor an avatar only on the FIRST of a run.
                if (!ccInContactRun) {
                    // sectionId's doc comment: the widget inserts AFTER this section, so
                    // to place the avatar BESIDE the bubble (not below it) we anchor on
                    // the PREVIOUS section and let the bridge pull it down into the
                    // gutter. ccPrevId is the section immediately above this one.
                    ccPendingRun = { anchorId: ccPrevId, emotion: '' };
                    ccAvaRuns.push(ccPendingRun);
                }
                ccInContactRun = true;
            }
        }
        ccPrevId = ccRow.sectionId;

        if (!ccTxt) continue;
        // Fresh regex per section: _charchatImageResolvedRx() is /g and therefore
        // stateful via lastIndex. Reusing one instance across sections silently skips
        // matches. (This is the same reason the scanner elsewhere rebuilds it per use.)
        let ccRx = _charchatImageResolvedRx();
        let ccM: RegExpExecArray | null;
        while ((ccM = ccRx.exec(ccTxt)) !== null) {
            // [v2.49.0 CHARCHAT-IMGWIDGET-FIX] Stripped to NAI's own documented example
            // shape. v2.48.0 sent { type, sectionId, side, display, content:[box[text]] }
            // and the main thread rejected the whole option:
            //   "createWidgets: optionsList[0] does not match any of the allowed types"
            // The OUTER shape was verified correct against DecorationCreateNodeWidgetOptions
            // (type/sectionId/side/display/content are all declared there), so the rejection
            // was inside content — and rather than guess which field the runtime validator
            // dislikes, this is now literally the shape script-types.txt's own createWidgets
            // example uses: type + sectionId + a bare text part. Nothing else. Every dropped
            // field was unproven IN A WIDGET in this file: 'box' and styled 'text' are used
            // 39x/265x here but only ever inside MODALS, and noTemplate/userSelect had
            // essentially no prior use at all.
            // DROPPING noTemplate IS SAFE ONLY BECAUSE the sentinel no longer carries the
            // desc: it is now the 8-hex id and nothing else, so there is no model prose in it
            // that could contain {{...}} for UIPartText to resolve as a storage key. The two
            // changes are one change. The bridge reads the caption from
            // EVT_CHARCHAT_STATE.turns[].images[].desc instead, which it already receives.
            // side:'after' was redundant anyway — sectionId's own doc comment reads
            // "Section to insert the widget AFTER".
            ccOpts.push({
                type: 'node',
                sectionId: ccRow.sectionId,
                content: [{
                    type: 'text',
                    text: AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_SENTINEL + ccM[1],
                    style: { fontSize: '1px', color: 'transparent', lineHeight: '0' }
                }]
            });
        }
    }

    // [v2.57.0 CHARCHAT-AVATAR-EMOTION] Emit the avatar sentinels now that every run's
    // Emotion: tail has been read. Order within the options list does not matter — each
    // widget carries its own explicit sectionId — so appending after the image sentinels
    // is safe.
    // FLAG DAY: the '|emotion' suffix is parsed by the bridge's _ccavPaintOne (bridge
    // v2.47.0+). An OLDER bridge slices on the sentinel prefix and would read
    // "contactId|happy" as the whole id, matching no character and painting nothing —
    // so this engine wants bridge >= 2.47.0. The reverse pairing is fine: a 2.47.0
    // bridge with an older engine just sees no suffix and falls back to the Avatar slot.
    for (let ccA = 0; ccA < ccAvaRuns.length; ccA++) {
        let ccRun = ccAvaRuns[ccA];
        ccOpts.push({
            type: 'node',
            sectionId: ccRun.anchorId,
            content: [{
                type: 'text',
                text: AE_CHARCHAT_AVA_SENTINEL + (_aeCharChat.contactId || '')
                    + (ccRun.emotion ? ('|' + ccRun.emotion) : ''),
                style: { fontSize: '1px', color: 'transparent', lineHeight: '0' }
            }]
        });
    }

    if (!ccOpts.length) return;
    try { await api.v1.editor.decorations.createWidgets(ccOpts); }
    catch (eccW) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] chat widget create failed (photos/avatars will not bind):', eccW); }
}

async function refreshCharChatDecorations(): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active || _aeCharChat.markerId === null) return;
    let ccAll: any[] = [];
    try { ccAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); } catch (_eccScan) { return; }
    let povIds: number[] = [];
    let contactIds: number[] = [];
    // [v2.49.0 CHARCHAT-BUBBLES2] headIds/emoIds are DISJOINT from contactIds — a section
    // must land in exactly one node rule's scope or two boxes fight over the same paragraph.
    let headIds: number[] = [];
    let emoIds: number[] = [];
    let ccLastContact = -1;   // the contact section directly above; -1 when the run is broken
    let ccEmoLineRx = /^[ \t]*Emotion:/;
    let ccSeen = false;
    for (let ccIdx = 0; ccIdx < ccAll.length; ccIdx++) {
        let ccRow = ccAll[ccIdx];
        if (ccRow.sectionId === _aeCharChat.markerId) { ccSeen = true; continue; }
        if (!ccSeen) continue;
        if (_aeCharChat.setupIds.indexOf(ccRow.sectionId) >= 0) { contactIds.push(ccRow.sectionId); ccLastContact = -1; continue; }
        // An emotion line is its own section (NAI splits on the '\n' _charchatFormatReply
        // writes). Promote the contact bubble above it to HEAD and take this one as the tail.
        if (ccEmoLineRx.test((ccRow.section && ccRow.section.text) || '')) {
            emoIds.push(ccRow.sectionId);
            if (ccLastContact >= 0) {
                let ccK = contactIds.indexOf(ccLastContact);
                if (ccK >= 0) contactIds.splice(ccK, 1);
                headIds.push(ccLastContact);
            }
            ccLastContact = -1;
            continue;
        }
        // [v2.24.0] was an inline copy of the source/origin classifier — now shared with
        // the transcript walk and the normaliser, which is the only way all three can be
        // guaranteed to agree about who said what.
        if (_charchatRowIsPov(ccRow.section, ccRow.sectionId)) { povIds.push(ccRow.sectionId); ccLastContact = -1; }
        else {
            // [FEATURE v2.71.0 CHARCHAT-TRAILING-BLANK-COLOR, per user] The blank paragraph
            // CHARCHAT-TRAILING-BLANK (v2.70.0) leaves after a reply is genuinely
            // unclassifiable by _charchatRowIsPov — no source, no origin — so it fell
            // through to contactIds and was painted as part of the character's own bubble,
            // which is the opposite of what it's for (a place for the NEXT, user, message).
            // Detected here as "empty text AND the last section in the document" and routed
            // to povIds instead. VISUAL ONLY: this does not touch the section's actual
            // source/origin, so _charchatScanTurns and _charchatCanonicalChatText (which
            // call _charchatRowIsPov directly, not through this loop) are unaffected — the
            // model's context never sees this section as a POV turn while it's empty. The
            // moment the user types into it, its real content and classification take over
            // on the next refresh regardless of which color it briefly wore while blank.
            let ccIsTrailingBlank = (ccIdx === ccAll.length - 1) && !((ccRow.section && ccRow.section.text) || '').trim();
            if (ccIsTrailingBlank) { povIds.push(ccRow.sectionId); ccLastContact = -1; }
            else { contactIds.push(ccRow.sectionId); ccLastContact = ccRow.sectionId; }
        }
    }
    try {
        await api.v1.editor.decorations.updateRules([
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_POV,     scope: { sectionIds: povIds } },
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_CONTACT, scope: { sectionIds: contactIds } },
            // [v2.47.0] Same scope as CONTACT — the pattern match already restricts which
            // TEXT within those sections actually gets tagged, so reusing the list costs
            // nothing extra and stays trivially in sync with the contact classification.
            // [v2.49.0] The inline emotion text rule spans BOTH the tail sections and the
            // contact bubbles — belt and braces: if NAI ever stops splitting on '\n' and
            // the emotion line comes back inside the reply's own section, this rule still
            // styles it (and HEAD/EMOBOX simply find nothing to scope, which is harmless).
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOTION, scope: { sectionIds: contactIds.concat(headIds, emoIds) } },
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_HEAD,    scope: { sectionIds: headIds } },
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOBOX,  scope: { sectionIds: emoIds } },
            // Names are hidden across every speaking section, both sides.
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_NAME, match: _charchatNameRuleMatch(), flags: 'm',
              scope: { sectionIds: povIds.concat(contactIds, headIds) } },
            // [v2.51.1 CHATIMG-HIDE-TAG] Same scope as NAME — an image tag can appear in
            // either speaker's turn (the POV side attaches photos too), and HEAD is included
            // for the same belt-and-braces reason EMOTION includes it above.
            { id: AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_IMGTAG, scope: { sectionIds: povIds.concat(contactIds, headIds) } }
        ]);
    } catch (eccD) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] decoration refresh failed (cosmetic only):', eccD); }
    // [v2.48.0 CHARCHAT-IMGWIDGET] Deliberately called from HERE rather than from each of
    // this function's own seven call sites: the image anchors and the bubble decorations
    // describe the same document state, so binding them together is the only way they
    // cannot drift. Awaited but non-fatal — it has its own try/catch inside, and a
    // missing photo anchor must never take the bubbles down with it.
    // [DIAG CE-DIAG-FLAGS] ccSkipWidgets — skip the clear+rebuild widget pass but keep
    // bubble decorations (the updateRules call above still ran). Isolates the handoff's
    // untested next-step #4: the widget pass is the most document-invasive per-turn
    // operation and sits directly between "reply landed" and "next Send declared safe".
    if (CE_DIAG.ccSkipWidgets) { aeLog('[AE:diag] ccSkipWidgets ON \u2014 widget clear/rebuild skipped this turn.'); return; }
    try { await refreshCharChatImageWidgets(); } catch (_eccIw) {}
}

// ── Context assembly ────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── [v2.24.0 CHARCHAT-PERSONA] Structured texting persona ───────────────
// Three closed axes plus a free-text escape hatch, per user: STRUCTURED OPTIONS,
// NOT GENERATED. The reason is placement, not taste — the compiled persona rides the
// next-message instruction (buildCharChatInstruction), a terse block at the TRUE
// bottom of context. A generated paragraph would drown that block and be re-paid on
// every single turn; three enum lookups cost one sentence.
// 'auto' on every axis is the deliberate default and means "say nothing, let
// CHARCHAT_DEFAULT_RUBRIC in the system prompt do its job" — that rubric already tells
// the model to derive a texting style from the character's own age/traits/tech
// familiarity, which is the right behaviour until an author disagrees with it. So an
// empty compiled clause is a CORRECT output, not an unconfigured one.
// Each option's `clause` is what actually reaches the model: short, imperative, and
// written to be concatenated rather than read in isolation.
interface CharChatOpt { id: string; label: string; clause: string; }

const CHARCHAT_STYLE_OPTS: CharChatOpt[] = [
    { id: 'auto',     label: 'Auto (from their profile)', clause: '' },
    { id: 'casual',   label: 'Digital native / casual',   clause: 'relaxed grammar, lowercase, slang and abbreviations' },
    { id: 'standard', label: 'Standard / adult',          clause: 'standard punctuation, clear syntax, concise phrasing' },
    { id: 'formal',   label: 'Formal / tech-unsavvy',     clause: 'overly formal grammar, full sentences, heavy ellipses' },
    { id: 'clipped',  label: 'Clipped / guarded',         clause: 'short and flat, minimal punctuation, gives little away' },
    { id: 'warm',     label: 'Warm / effusive',           clause: 'affectionate, generous with words, leans into feeling' },
    { id: 'florid',   label: 'Florid / theatrical',       clause: 'ornate word choice, dramatic phrasing, deliberate flourish' }
];

// `tokens` is the whole reason verbosity is not just another clause: telling the model
// "be brief" while handing it a 240-token cap is two instructions that disagree, and
// the cap always wins. This axis sets BOTH. tokens:0 means "use AE_CHARCHAT_MAX_TOKENS".
interface CharChatVerbOpt extends CharChatOpt { tokens: number; }
const CHARCHAT_VERBOSITY_OPTS: CharChatVerbOpt[] = [
    { id: 'auto',   label: 'Auto (SMS length)',        clause: '',                                        tokens: 0   },
    { id: 'terse',  label: 'Terse (a few words)',      clause: 'a few words, under 10 words',             tokens: 60  },
    { id: 'short',  label: 'Short (one sentence)',     clause: 'one sentence, under 20 words',            tokens: 90  },
    { id: 'normal', label: 'Normal (SMS length)',      clause: 'SMS length, under 32 words',              tokens: 120 },
    { id: 'chatty', label: 'Chatty (two or three)',    clause: 'two or three sentences, under 50 words',  tokens: 170 },
    { id: 'long',   label: 'Long (a short paragraph)', clause: 'up to a short paragraph, under 80 words', tokens: 240 }
];

const CHARCHAT_EMOJI_OPTS: CharChatOpt[] = [
    { id: 'auto',  label: 'Auto (from their profile)', clause: '' },
    { id: 'none',  label: 'Never',                     clause: 'never use emoji or kaomoji' },
    { id: 'rare',  label: 'Rare',                      clause: 'emoji only rarely, and never the same one twice in a row' },
    { id: 'some',  label: 'Some',                      clause: 'occasional emoji, never the same one twice in a row' },
    { id: 'heavy', label: 'Heavy',                     clause: 'expressive emoji and kaomoji throughout, but never repeat one consecutively' }
];

function _charchatOpt(opts: CharChatOpt[], id: string): CharChatOpt {
    let ccWant = id || 'auto';
    for (let ccI = 0; ccI < opts.length; ccI++) { if (opts[ccI].id === ccWant) return opts[ccI]; }
    return opts[0];
}

// The compiled persona as ONE line for the instruction block. Empty when the persona is
// absent, disabled, or fully 'auto' — see the 'auto' note above; that is the intended
// resting state.
function buildCharChatPersonaClause(char: CharacterRecord): string {
    let cp = char.chatPersona;
    if (!cp || cp.enabled === false) return '';
    let ccBits: string[] = [];
    let ccSt = _charchatOpt(CHARCHAT_STYLE_OPTS, cp.style || 'auto').clause;
    if (ccSt) ccBits.push('Style: ' + ccSt + '.');
    let ccVb = _charchatOpt(CHARCHAT_VERBOSITY_OPTS, cp.verbosity || 'auto').clause;
    if (ccVb) ccBits.push('Length: ' + ccVb + '.');
    let ccEm = _charchatOpt(CHARCHAT_EMOJI_OPTS, cp.emoji || 'auto').clause;
    if (ccEm) ccBits.push('Emoji: ' + ccEm + '.');
    if (cp.notes && cp.notes.trim()) ccBits.push(cp.notes.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' '));
    // [LEGACY v2.21.0] the old freeform shape, folded in as trailing notes so a
    // character authored before v2.24.0 does not silently lose their register.
    let ccLeg: string[] = [];
    if (cp.register && cp.register.trim()) ccLeg.push(cp.register.trim());
    if (cp.voice && cp.voice.trim())       ccLeg.push(cp.voice.trim());
    if (cp.content && cp.content.trim())   ccLeg.push(cp.content.trim());
    if (ccLeg.length > 0) ccBits.push(ccLeg.join(' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' '));
    return ccBits.join(' ');
}

// The output cap for THIS contact. Verbosity owns it entirely (see
// CHARCHAT_VERBOSITY_OPTS's `tokens` note). [v2.27.0] No longer tops up for
// "thinking" — CHARCHAT-THINK's inline reasoning line shared this budget with the
// reply itself; its replacement, generateEphemeral(), is a fully separate call with
// its own budget, so there is nothing left for this function to add for it.
function charChatMaxTokensFor(char: CharacterRecord | null, cfg: GlobalConfig | null): number {
    let ccBase = AE_CHARCHAT_MAX_TOKENS;
    let ccCp = char ? char.chatPersona : null;
    if (ccCp && ccCp.enabled !== false) {
        let ccV = _charchatOpt(CHARCHAT_VERBOSITY_OPTS, ccCp.verbosity || 'auto') as CharChatVerbOpt;
        if (ccV && ccV.tokens > 0) ccBase = ccV.tokens;
    }
    return ccBase;
}

// THE steering surface. Everything the model needs to be this character, in one
// contiguous top-of-context block: framing, then "you are the following character",
// then that character's identity (their real lorebook text, folded in — which is why
// the entry itself is deactivated), their dynamic layer, and their texting register.
// Adjacency is guaranteed here in a way lorebook insertion order could never promise.
async function buildCharChatSystemPrompt(contact: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<string> {
    let ccOut: string[] = [];
    ccOut.push('SMARTPHONE MESSAGING APP. You are writing one side of a live text-message conversation.');
    ccOut.push('Below the narrative context you will find a chat transcript. Write ONLY the next single message, as the character defined below. Do not narrate the story, do not write the other person\u2019s messages, and do not continue the prose above the chat marker \u2014 that story text is reference material for what these two know and where they stand, nothing more.');
    // [v2.24.0 CHARCHAT-PERSONA] The GLOBAL rubric lives here and only here. The
    // character's OWN persona (style/verbosity/emoji) no longer rides the system
    // prompt — it compiles to one line on the next-message instruction instead, per
    // user, where it is most present. Two useful side effects of the split: the
    // system prompt stays identical across every contact in a story (one less thing
    // invalidating the prefix cache when switching chats), and a persona edit takes
    // effect on the very next turn rather than needing a chat restart.
    ccOut.push(CHARCHAT_DEFAULT_RUBRIC);
    ccOut.push('You are the following character:');

    let ccIdentity = '';
    try { ccIdentity = (await buildLorebookContent(contact)).trim(); }
    catch (eccI) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] identity build failed:', eccI); }
    if (ccIdentity) ccOut.push(ccIdentity);
    else ccOut.push(contact.name + (contact.briefDescription ? ('\n' + contact.briefDescription) : ''));

    let ccDyn = '';
    try { ccDyn = (await buildCharDynamicBlock(contact, cfg)).trim(); }
    catch (eccD2) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] dynamic block failed:', eccD2); }
    if (ccDyn) ccOut.push(ccDyn);

    return ccOut.join('\n\n');
}

// The next-message instruction. Appended to the END of storyText.content, which puts it
// at true bottom of context and keeps it invisible in the document.
// [OPEN — test this first] Whether appending to storyText.content disturbs where the
// model continues from is the single biggest unknown in this design. If it misbehaves,
// the fallback is to carry this in the Author's Note and accept AN's fixed depth.
// [v2.24.0] This block grew from one line into the chat's whole steering tail, in
// deliberate priority order — task, then register, then state, then output protocol.
// Everything that used to be scattered (persona in the system prompt, ephemeral in the
// Author's Note) now lands here, because "most present" is a position, not a volume:
// the bottom of context is the only place we control precisely.
function buildCharChatInstruction(contact: CharacterRecord, povName: string, cfg: GlobalConfig | null): string {
    let ccName = _aeCharChat.contactName || contact.name;
    let ccAddr = povName ? (', addressed to ' + povName) : '';
    let ccOut: string[] = [];

    // [v2.22.0/v2.24.0] The name header is never requested and never trusted — it is
    // applied programmatically (charChatCleanReply on the way in, then
    // _charchatNormalizeDocument as a standing pass). Saying so explicitly is cheap and
    // stops the model spending its 120 tokens re-deriving a header we throw away.
    ccOut.push('[ Instruction: Write the next single message from ' + ccName + ccAddr + '. '
             + 'If the previous message also belongs to ' + ccName + ' \u2014 complement it rather than repeating it. '
             + 'Write ONLY the message body: the name header is applied automatically, so never write one yourself. '
             // [v2.41.0 CHARCHAT-CONTACT-BRACE] Naming the specific failure, not just forbidding
             // headers in the abstract. The model was writing a header plus a lone brace,
             // imitating the convention it can see on the POV turns above. The prohibition was
             // already there and was already losing to the shape of the context; the cleanup in
             // _charchatCanonContact is the actual guarantee, this is the cheap prevention.
             + 'Never write curly braces around your message \u2014 those belong to ' + (povName || 'the other person') + '\u2019s messages only. ]');

    let ccPersona = buildCharChatPersonaClause(contact);
    if (ccPersona) ccOut.push('[ ' + ccName + '\u2019s texting style: ' + ccPersona + ' ]');

    // [v2.24.0, per user] The ephemeral line used to ride the Author's Note. The AN
    // lands at the user's configured depth INSIDE recent history and the hook exposes no
    // depth control, so a "right now" line was always some arbitrary distance from the
    // point of generation. Here it sits at the true bottom — most present, which is
    // exactly what a right-now line wants to be. The AN is deactivated during chat as a
    // result; see runCharChatContextOverride.
    // [v2.43.0 CHARCHAT-TAIL-ORDER, per user] Read here, PUSHED LAST (see the bottom of this
    // function). v2.24.0 moved this line off the Author's Note precisely so it could be
    // most-present at the true bottom of context — and then the photo block and the emotion
    // list piled up underneath it, so the one block whose entire job is to be closest to the
    // generation point was sitting THIRD of five, behind ~110 words of usually-irrelevant
    // instruction. Per the user's own context dump, that is where the thought was buried.
    let ccEph = (contact.ephemeral && contact.ephemeral.compressedLine) ? String(contact.ephemeral.compressedLine).trim() : '';

    // [v2.27.0] CHARCHAT-THINK's inline "[Thinking: ...]" request is GONE — the model
    // no longer generates or reasons through a private line as part of this same
    // completion. Freshness comes from the ccEph block above instead.
    // [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-POST] ccEph IS one turn stale again, and that is now the
    // accepted design rather than a defect to chase. With GlobalConfig.charChatFreshThoughts
    // on, generateChatEphemeral() runs from onGenerationEnd AFTER each reply, so the line
    // below describes the moment the LAST reply landed. v2.38.0-v2.44.1 spent four versions
    // trying to make it describe THIS moment and every one of them cost the reply itself;
    // read the CE-INDEX v2.45.0 block before trying a fifth. NOTE the read below is unchanged
    // and unconditional: if thinking failed, was cancelled, or is switched off, char.ephemeral
    // simply still holds
    // the previous snapshot and it is served exactly as before. That is the silent
    // fallback the user asked for, and it needs no code here because it needs none
    // anywhere — not writing is the fallback.
    // [v2.43.0 CHARCHAT-EMOTION-LIST, per user] REVERSES v2.25.0, which spelled the 24 words
    // out here via ALL_EMOTIONS_LIST. v2.25.0's own reasoning conceded the point: it called
    // the list "encouragement, not enforcement" because resolveEmotion() normalises the
    // answer regardless. Encouragement is not free at the bottom of context — it cost 24
    // words directly in front of the generation point, ahead of the thought. The list is gone
    // and the normaliser (which was always the actual enforcement) does the work alone.
    // ALL_EMOTIONS_LIST is left DECLARED: it is still the single source of truth for the 24
    // words, and this is now simply not one of its readers.
    // [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-IMG] Attachment clause, only when the feature is on — an
    // instruction to send photos the engine will not act on is worse than no
    // instruction, since the model would emit tags that render as literal text.
    // The model is asked for a DESCRIPTION, not for tags: the architect pass turns it
    // into a real prompt, and the character's own visual record is applied
    // programmatically (see buildCharChatImagePrompt), so asking the model to describe
    // its own appearance would just be a worse, contradictory copy of data we hold.
    // NOTE the deliberate divergence from DEFAULT_NOTE (the legacy auto-illustrator
    // story note, grep it): that one asks for comma-separated Danbooru tags including
    // the character's own name, because it had no architect in the loop. This does.
    if (cfg && cfg.charChatImages) {
        ccOut.push('[ You may attach a photo when it fits \u2014 a selfie, something you are looking at, '
                 + 'or anything ' + (povName || 'they') + ' asks you to show. Do it when it is natural, and always when asked. '
                 + 'To attach one, put this on its own line AFTER your message text: [Image: a clear description of what the photo shows] '
                 + 'Describe the shot in plain words (what you are doing, wearing, where you are, the framing). '
                 + 'Do not describe your own face or body \u2014 that is applied automatically. Never attach more than one photo per message. ]');
    }

    // [v2.43.0 CHARCHAT-EMOTION-LIST, per user] The 24-word list is GONE. It was 24 words of
    // the most expensive context in the file spent constraining an output that gets normalised
    // anyway: resolveEmotion()/EMOTION_MAP already maps 136 synonyms onto exactly those 24
    // canonical words (verified by count), every canonical word self-maps, and BOTH readers of
    // this tag — charChatParseEmotion on the way in and _charchatCanonContact on the document
    // — already run it through that map. So "annoyed" was always going to become "irritated"
    // whether or not the model was handed the list. A word that misses the map resolves to
    // 'neutral', which is the same graceful degradation the emotion-image widget already
    // depends on. Ask for one plain word; let the filter do the job it already does.
    // [v2.62.0 CHARCHAT-TAIL-TERMINAL] DELIBERATE PARTIAL REVERSAL of v2.43.0
    // CHARCHAT-TAIL-ORDER. v2.43.0 pushed the thought LAST, and it was right that the thought
    // wants to be most-present. What it did not account for is that the thought is the ONLY
    // block in this tail that is conditional, and it is a STATEMENT rather than a directive.
    // The consequence, only visible once the thought subsystem started producing reliably:
    //   turn 1 of a fresh chat  -> no ephemeral yet -> prompt ends '[ End your message ... ]'
    //   turn 2 onward           -> ephemeral exists -> prompt ends '[ Name right now: ... ]'
    // So the LAST thing the model reads changes shape permanently after the first reply, which
    // maps exactly onto the reported failure (first message always answers, every message after
    // it returns an empty completion, and switching thoughts off makes every turn behave like
    // turn 1). Whatever the mechanism, the prompt should not silently change its terminal block
    // halfway through a conversation. The thought keeps its position at the bottom of the
    // instruction stack; the Emotion directive is now unconditionally the final line, so turn 2's
    // tail is structurally identical to turn 1's. See CE-INDEX v2.62.0.
    if (ccEph) ccOut.push('[ ' + ccName + ' right now: ' + ccEph + ' ]');
    ccOut.push('[ End your message with a new line: Emotion: <a single word for how ' + ccName + ' feels right now> ]');
    return ccOut.join('\n');
}

// ── [v2.24.0 CHARCHAT-TURNS] Canonical turn formatting ──────────────────
// THE replacement for the v2.22.0 POV scaffold, per user: instead of appending a
// half-written "{user: " into the document in ADVANCE and depending on the user to
// finish it (including a closing brace they were never obliged to type), the user
// writes whatever they like and we rewrite it into canonical shape AFTERWARDS.
//   "{Hi, Sara}"  ->  "{user: Hi, Sara}"
//   "Hi, Sara"    ->  "{user: Hi, Sara}"
//   "{Hi, Sara"   ->  "{user: Hi, Sara}"     (unclosed brace closed for them)
//   "Hi, Paul"    ->  "Sara:\nHi, Paul"        (contact side)
// A header the MODEL wrote itself is stripped and re-applied rather than trusted, so a
// WRONG one (the contact's name on the POV's line, or vice versa) can never survive.
// As the format stabilises in the transcript the model will tend to produce the right
// one unprompted, per user — and this pass costs nothing on the turns where it does.
// This SUPERSEDES the v2.23.0 dangling-scaffold context strip, which fixed the same
// root cause (an unclosed "{user: " reaching the model, whose most natural
// continuation is to finish user's line rather than reply as the contact) by deleting
// the scaffold from context. That was a patch on a self-inflicted wound; there is no
// scaffold now, and every POV turn is re-emitted WITH its closing brace whether the
// user typed one or not, so a dangling "{" cannot reach the model from any source.

const CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX = '  Emotion: ';   // [v2.24.0, per user] two leading spaces. The one authored string in this subsystem whose exact whitespace is load-bearing — user's emotion-image widget matches on it.

// One shared regex builder rather than three drifting copies (the v2.23.0 file had the
// emotion pattern written out in three places, and _charchatBareBody's copy silently
// stopped matching the moment the prefix gained leading spaces).
function _charchatEmotionRx(): RegExp { return /[\n\r\s]*Emotion:[ \t]*([^\n\r]+?)[ \t]*$/i; }

// ── [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-IMG] Image attachment tags ────────────────────────────
// TWO regexes, and the distinction is the whole mechanism:
//   UNRESOLVED  "[Image: a description]"          — what the MODEL writes. Deliberately
//               tolerant of an inner leading space (bracket-space-Image-colon) and of
//               any case, per user (both spaced and unspaced forms are caught).
//   RESOLVED    "[Image#7f3a91c2: a description]" — what WE rewrite it to once the
//               image has been dispatched, where the hex is the image uuid's prefix.
// The '#' is what separates them, and it separates them for free: the unresolved
// pattern requires ':' directly after the word, so a resolved tag can never re-match
// it. That is what makes the pass idempotent — it can run on every generation end
// without re-architecting (and re-paying for) images it already handled.
//
// WHY THE TAG STAYS IN THE DOCUMENT (deliberate, same reasoning as the Emotion
// sidecar one floor up — see charChatCleanReply's own note): the document text is the
// durable record. It is what collectCharChatTranscript serialises into the lorebook on
// Save and what resumeCharChatFromEntry reads back. Strip the tag once rendered and a
// resumed thread has a hole where the image was — the bytes are still in the bridge's
// IndexedDB but nothing points at them. Embedding the uuid IN the text means the
// pointer survives the document -> lorebook -> document round trip as plain text, with
// no side-table to keep in sync. Rendering it AS an image instead of as text is the
// bridge's job, not the document's.
function _charchatImageRx(): RegExp { return /\[[ \t]*Image[ \t]*:[ \t]*([^\]\n\r]+?)[ \t]*\]/gi; }
function _charchatImageResolvedRx(): RegExp { return /\[[ \t]*Image#([0-9a-f]{8})[ \t]*:[ \t]*([^\]\n\r]+?)[ \t]*\]/gi; }

// Max attachments dispatched per generation-end pass. A model that decides every
// message deserves a photo would otherwise spend real budget without bound; this is a
// backstop, not a design feature.
const AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_MAX_PER_TURN = 2;
const AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_MAX_TOKENS = 300;   // matches buildArchitectPromptFromStory's own architect cap

// Role classification, in ONE place. Previously duplicated verbatim in
// refreshCharChatDecorations and collectCharChatTranscript. Role is NEVER inferred from
// text — same guarantee WIZLIVE gives, and the reason the whole POV-optional design
// works at all.
// [BUGFIX v2.32.1 CHARCHAT-BRIDGE-ORIGIN, per user report] 'script' origin ADDED
// alongside 'user'/'edit'. Root cause of the doubled-brace / bled-together report
// ("{ {Nate: ...}Sara: ...} }"): a bridge-sent turn is appended via
// api.v1.document.appendParagraph, which gives that text origin data:'script' — NOT
// 'user'. The (correct, narrower) origin check below used to require 'user'/'edit' to
// call a paragraph POV, so a script-authored turn fell through to FALSE... except
// handleCharChatAction's 'send' op ALSO stamped source:'instruction' on it (reusing the
// setup-paragraph shortcut above), which forced it POV regardless of what NAI appended
// afterward. That shortcut is exactly what _charchatMergeSplitIdx's own v2.25.0 comment
// already named as the confirmed source of this class of bug for typed turns — the
// bridge path walked into the identical trap via a different door. Fix is two-sided:
// this function now recognises 'script' as legitimately-typed origin in its own right
// (so the source:'instruction' shortcut is no longer needed for bridge turns), AND
// handleCharChatAction's 'send' op no longer sets source:'instruction' on a real turn
// (grep CHARCHAT-BRIDGE-ORIGIN there) — that field stays reserved for actual setup/
// marker/resumed-history paragraphs. See _charchatMergeSplitIdx just below for the
// other half: it must also recognise 'script' as a valid pre-AI origin, or the merge
// this fix now correctly EXPOSES (script text + AI reply glued onto the same section)
// would still never get physically split.
// [BUGFIX v2.34.0 CHARCHAT-STAGED-IDS, per user] REVERTS v2.32.1 CHARCHAT-BRIDGE-ORIGIN's
// 'script' widening, which was self-contradicting and is the confirmed root cause of the
// "{ {Nate: ...} }" / "{ {Nate: Emotion: neutral} }" corruption. The contradiction, stated
// plainly: _charchatSplitMergedSections inserts THE CONTACT'S REPLY TAIL via
// insertParagraphAfter, and its own comment gives the reason it is safe to do so \u2014
// "script-written text gets 'script' origin, which _charchatRowIsPov correctly reads as
// NOT a POV turn". v2.32.1 made 'script' read as POV to accommodate bridge-sent turns, so
// every engine-authored contact paragraph (a split tail, an Emotion: line) started
// classifying as the POV speaking, got wrapped by _charchatCanonPov, and then re-wrapped
// when grouping joined it to the adjacent real POV turn. Origin CANNOT answer this
// question: an engine-written POV turn and an engine-written contact reply are both
// 'script'. So the engine now RECORDS what it authored (stagedPovIds) instead of trying to
// infer it back out of metadata, and this function returns to the v2.31.0 rule \u2014 the
// last version that formatted correctly, per user.
// [v2.46.0 CHARCHAT-SHELL-BASELINE] The stagedPovIds consult is GONE and this is the v2.31.0
// rule again, unaided: a section is a POV turn if NAI stamped it entirely user/edit-typed.
// That rule was correct for four years of this subsystem and only ever failed for paragraphs
// the ENGINE or the BRIDGE wrote — which no longer happens for POV turns. The sectionId
// parameter is KEPT: every caller already passes it, removing it touches five call sites for
// no gain, and the next feature that needs per-section ground truth will want it back.
function _charchatRowIsPov(section: any, sectionId?: number): boolean {
    if (section.source === 'instruction') return true;
    let ccOrg = section.origin || [];
    if (ccOrg.length === 0) return false;
    let ccAllTyped = true;
    let ccAnyUser = false;
    for (let ccO of ccOrg) {
        if (ccO.data === 'user') ccAnyUser = true;
        if (ccO.data !== 'user' && ccO.data !== 'edit') { ccAllTyped = false; break; }
    }
    return ccAllTyped && ccAnyUser;
}

// [BUGFIX v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE] Detect a section whose origin metadata crosses from
// user-typed (user/edit) to AI-generated (ai/prompt) data. This is the signature of NAI's
// native Send continuing a completion DIRECTLY INTO the same paragraph the user just
// typed into, because the cursor had no trailing paragraph break — rather than starting a
// fresh section for the reply. _charchatRowIsPov trusts a 'instruction' source for the
// WHOLE section regardless of what got appended to it afterward, so a merged section like
// this was being canonicalised as one giant POV turn: the user's own text PLUS the start
// of the contact's reply, wrapped together in POV braces. That is the confirmed source of
// both the "double brace" and "contact's header bleeding into the POV body" reports.
// Returns the character offset into section.text where the AI-authored portion begins,
// or -1 if the origin is homogeneous (nothing to split). Sorted defensively; origin spans
// are not documented as guaranteed-ordered.
// [BUGFIX v2.32.1 CHARCHAT-BRIDGE-ORIGIN] 'script' recognised as pre-AI origin — see
// _charchatRowIsPov's note just above for the full story. Without this, a bridge-sent
// turn (origin: script) that NAI glued a completion onto (origin gains ai/prompt) was
// INVISIBLE to this detector: ccSawUser only ever flipped on 'user'/'edit', so a
// script→ai boundary was never found and the merged section was never physically
// split — it was scanned, canonicalised, and re-saved as one giant blob instead.
function _charchatMergeSplitIdx(section: any): number {
    let ccOrg = (section && section.origin) || [];
    if (ccOrg.length < 2) return -1;
    let ccSorted = ccOrg.slice().sort(function(a: any, b: any) { return a.position - b.position; });
    let ccSawUser = false;
    for (let ccO of ccSorted) {
        if (ccO.data === 'user' || ccO.data === 'edit') { ccSawUser = true; continue; }
        if (ccSawUser && (ccO.data === 'ai' || ccO.data === 'prompt')) return ccO.position;
    }
    return -1;
}

// Strip a leading "Name:" header — whoever wrote it. Every name we know about is tried,
// so a header the model got WRONG is removed too rather than kept and doubled up.
function _charchatStripHeader(body: string, names: string[]): string {
    let ccT = (body || '').replace(/^\s+/, '');
    for (let ccPass = 0; ccPass < 2; ccPass++) {   // 2 passes: "Sara:\nSara: hi" happens
        let ccHit = false;
        for (let ccI = 0; ccI < names.length; ccI++) {
            if (!names[ccI]) continue;
            // [\s*] not [\r?\n?] after the colon, and the difference is a REAL bug found
            // by test, not a style choice: _charchatScanTurns joins a grouped turn with
            // '\n', so a reply NAI split as ["Sara:", "", "Hi, Paul"] arrives here as
            // "Sara:\n\nHi, Paul". Consuming at most ONE newline left the blank line in
            // place — which is precisely the "the character response adds a second
            // [newline]" report this pass is meant to fix.
            let ccRx = new RegExp('^\\*{0,2}' + _charchatEscapeRx(names[ccI]) + '\\*{0,2}[ \\t]*[:\uff1a]\\s*', 'i');
            if (ccRx.test(ccT)) { ccT = ccT.replace(ccRx, ''); ccHit = true; break; }
        }
        if (!ccHit) break;
    }
    return ccT;
}

// Unwrap our own brace wrapper, tolerating BOTH the unclosed case and the never-opened
// case — per user, the user was never obliged to type either one.
function _charchatUnwrapPov(text: string): string {
    let ccS = (text || '').trim();
    // [BUGFIX v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-POV-IDEMPOTENT, per user] Was a single strip, so a re-sent
    // turn came back double-wrapped: the user deletes their message, retypes it, and the
    // canon of an already-canonical turn nested as a second layer. Reported directly, and
    // the double wrap changed the reply's behaviour enough to be worth removing as a
    // variable rather than explaining. Loop until there is no wrapper left, so
    // canon(canon(x)) === canon(x) and re-prompting is byte-identical to prompting once.
    // Bounded rather than while(true): a pathologically nested wrapper should cost a fixed
    // of passes, not hang the worker. 8 is far past anything a human or model produces.
    for (let ccPass = 0; ccPass < 8; ccPass++) {
        let ccBefore = ccS;
        if (ccS.charAt(0) === '{') ccS = ccS.substring(1);
        if (ccS.length > 0 && ccS.charAt(ccS.length - 1) === '}') ccS = ccS.substring(0, ccS.length - 1);
        ccS = ccS.trim();
        if (ccS === ccBefore) break;
    }
    return ccS.trim();
}

// CANONICAL POV TURN. '' means "there is no turn here" (an empty brace block, a stray
// blank paragraph) — callers drop it rather than emitting an empty one.
// [BUGFIX v2.27.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-REVERT, per user] REVERTS v2.26.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE.
// v2.26.0 theorised NAI's own editor draws the visible bracket and stopped this
// function from ALSO wrapping the text in literal '{'/'}'. User's direct test
// falsifies that: a typed "{Hi!}" still came back doubled as "{{POVname: Hi!}}", and
// a bare "Hi!" came back as bodyless "{POVname}" — not a render-only bracket at all.
// Removing our own wrap also broke turn recognition outright: with no literal brace,
// a POV paragraph had nothing marking it as a fresh instruction turn, so it read as a
// continuation (a bare newline) of the previous contact turn instead of a new one.
// Output is "{Name: body}" again, matching every version before v2.26.0.
// _charchatUnwrapPov still tolerantly STRIPS a leading/trailing brace the user or
// model wrote — that part was never the bug and stays. NOTE: the real double-brace
// root cause was already fixed in v2.25.0 (_charchatSplitMergedSections, physical
// section splitting) — this revert does not touch or undo that fix.
function _charchatCanonPov(text: string, povName: string, contactName: string): string {
    let ccBody = _charchatStripHeader(_charchatUnwrapPov(text), [povName, contactName]).replace(/\s+$/, '');
    if (!ccBody) return '';
    return '{' + (povName ? (povName + ': ' + ccBody) : ccBody) + '}';
}

// CANONICAL CONTACT TURN. The Emotion tag is split off first and re-attached last, so
// the header strip can never mistake it for body and a body strip can never eat it.
function _charchatCanonContact(text: string, contactName: string, povName: string): string {
    let ccT = (text || '');
    let ccEmotion = '';
    let ccCut = ccT.match(_charchatEmotionRx());
    if (ccCut && ccCut[1] && ccCut.index !== undefined) {
        let ccE = ccCut[1].trim();
        // [v2.25.0, per user] Canonicalise to the 24-state list rather than keeping
        // whatever synonym the model wrote — resolveEmotion() is the same normaliser the
        // image-generation pipeline already uses (EMOTION_MAP), so "annoyed" becomes
        // "irritated" etc. instead of silently missing user's emotion-image widget, which
        // keys off one of the 24 canonical words.
        if (ccE.length > 0 && ccE.length <= 40) { ccEmotion = resolveEmotion(ccE); ccT = ccT.substring(0, ccCut.index); }
    }
    let ccBody = _charchatStripHeader(ccT, [contactName, povName]).replace(/\s+$/, '');
    // [BUGFIX v2.41.0 CHARCHAT-CONTACT-BRACE, per user live test] The model writes a header
    // plus a lone opening brace. It sees Name-colon after a brace-wrapped POV turn,
    // generalises the convention, and opens a brace of its own. On one reported send it
    // emitted ONLY that and stopped, so the message never arrived at all.
    // _charchatStripHeader removes the header correctly and leaves the bare brace as the
    // body, which this function then faithfully re-emits — enshrining the model's abandoned
    // scaffold as a turn.
    // The POV path has unwrapped stray braces since v2.24.0 (_charchatUnwrapPov); the contact
    // path never needed it until the model started imitating the POV form. Unwrap here too,
    // then treat a body that is nothing but punctuation as NO body: returning '' makes
    // _charchatNormalizeDocument drop the turn (see its ccCanon check) instead of keeping it.
    if (ccBody.indexOf('{') >= 0 || ccBody.indexOf('}') >= 0) {
        // [BUGFIX v2.62.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-LINE, per user live transcript] The v2.41.0 unwrap only
        // ever looked at the FIRST and LAST character of the joined body, so it caught
        // "{...}" and "}" alone but not the shape actually reported:
        //     Nix: }
        //     Nix: Can't you figure it out? ...
        // That is one contact TURN whose grouped text is "}\nCan't you figure it out? ..." \u2014
        // the stray brace is a whole LINE, with real message text underneath it. charAt(0) is
        // '}' (not '{') so nothing was stripped, the scaffold-only guard below never fired
        // (the body has letters), and _charchatNormalizeDocument's grow pass then stamped a
        // header onto the brace line and enshrined it as its own visible message.
        // Strip leading and trailing lines that are pure brace/punctuation FIRST, then run the
        // existing boundary unwrap on what is left.
        let ccLinesB = ccBody.split('\n');
        while (ccLinesB.length > 1 && !/[\p{L}\p{N}]/u.test(ccLinesB[0])) ccLinesB.shift();
        while (ccLinesB.length > 1 && !/[\p{L}\p{N}]/u.test(ccLinesB[ccLinesB.length - 1])) ccLinesB.pop();
        let ccDeBraced = ccLinesB.join('\n');
        if (ccDeBraced !== ccBody) {
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:contact-brace] stripped a scaffold-only line from a contact turn '
                + '(the model opened or closed a brace on its own line before writing the message).');
            ccBody = ccDeBraced;
        }
        let ccUnwrapped = _charchatUnwrapPov(ccBody);
        ccUnwrapped = _charchatStripHeader(ccUnwrapped, [contactName, povName]).replace(/\s+$/, '');
        if (ccUnwrapped !== ccBody) ccBody = ccUnwrapped;
    }
    // Scaffold-only: no letter or digit anywhere. Not merely 'is it a brace' — the observed
    // failures are all header-plus-brace or brace-only, and a body of pure punctuation is
    // never a real text message in any of them.
    if (ccBody && !/[\p{L}\p{N}]/u.test(ccBody)) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:contact-brace] dropped a scaffold-only contact body '
            + JSON.stringify(ccBody.substring(0, 20)) + ' — the model wrote a header/brace and no message. '
            + 'The turn is dropped rather than enshrined; if this fires every turn, the reply prompt is losing to the context shape.');
        ccBody = '';
    }
    if (!ccBody && !ccEmotion) return '';
    // [v2.25.0, per user] Header and body share a line now ("Sara: message") \u2014 a
    // real text message doesn't put the sender's name on its own line. Emotion keeps its
    // own line below (unchanged, per user): CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX's leading newline is
    // still what user's emotion-image widget scans for.
    let ccOut = (contactName || 'They') + ': ' + (ccBody || '\u2026');
    if (ccEmotion) ccOut += '\n' + CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX + ccEmotion;
    return ccOut;
}

// A logical turn = a run of CONSECUTIVE same-role paragraphs. Grouping before
// normalising is not an optimisation, it is correctness: NAI splits a returned reply
// into sections however it likes, and normalising each section in isolation would stamp
// a "Sara:" header onto every line of a three-paragraph reply. Grouping also silently
// absorbs the stray blank paragraph a trailing newline leaves behind — the "the reply
// adds a second newline" report.
async function _charchatScanTurns(): Promise<{ turns: { isPov: boolean; ids: number[]; texts: string[] }[]; ids: number[] }> {
    let ccTurns: { isPov: boolean; ids: number[]; texts: string[] }[] = [];
    let ccAllIds: number[] = [];
    if (_aeCharChat.markerId === null) return { turns: ccTurns, ids: ccAllIds };
    let ccAll: any[] = [];
    try { ccAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); }
    catch (eTs) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] turn scan failed:', eTs); return { turns: ccTurns, ids: ccAllIds }; }
    let ccSeen = false;
    for (let ccR of ccAll) {
        if (ccR.sectionId === _aeCharChat.markerId) { ccSeen = true; ccAllIds.push(ccR.sectionId); continue; }
        if (!ccSeen) continue;
        ccAllIds.push(ccR.sectionId);
        if (_aeCharChat.setupIds.indexOf(ccR.sectionId) >= 0) continue;

        // [BUGFIX v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE] READ-ONLY safety net. The real fix physically
        // splits a merged section in _charchatSplitMergedSections (write-pass only, since
        // this function must never mutate the document \u2014 it also runs on dryRun). If
        // that split hasn't run yet (or failed), don't let a merged section inherit ONE
        // role from a stale 'instruction' source: cut it at the same origin boundary and
        // feed the two halves to their correct turns instead. NOTE: if this ever fires
        // during the WRITE pass (i.e. the physical split above failed), both halves still
        // carry the SAME sectionId into two different turns, and _charchatNormalizeDocument
        // schedules one updateParagraphs write per turn against that id \u2014 the second
        // write wins, so the OTHER half's text is silently lost for this cycle. Accepted:
        // strictly better than the pre-v2.25.0 behaviour (which corrupted BOTH halves
        // every time), and the physical split succeeding is the common case this guards.
        let ccFullText = ccR.section.text || '';
        let ccSplitIdx = _charchatMergeSplitIdx(ccR.section);
        if (ccSplitIdx > 0 && ccSplitIdx < ccFullText.length) {
            let ccHead = ccFullText.substring(0, ccSplitIdx);
            let ccTail = ccFullText.substring(ccSplitIdx);
            let ccLastH = (ccTurns.length > 0) ? ccTurns[ccTurns.length - 1] : null;
            if (ccLastH && ccLastH.isPov) { ccLastH.ids.push(ccR.sectionId); ccLastH.texts.push(ccHead); }
            else ccTurns.push({ isPov: true, ids: [ccR.sectionId], texts: [ccHead] });
            ccTurns.push({ isPov: false, ids: [ccR.sectionId], texts: [ccTail] });
            continue;
        }

        let ccIsPov = _charchatRowIsPov(ccR.section, ccR.sectionId);
        // [DIAG v2.51.3 CHARCHAT-BRACE-DIAG] CLOSED at v2.59.0. The user's own live log
        // confirmed the hypothesis this diagnostic existed to test: origin metadata reaching
        // _charchatRowIsPov here is genuinely native ('user'/'edit'/'script'), never an
        // injected/misclassified pattern from the old DOM-INJECT panel (which was already
        // confirmed deleted at bridge v2.13.0). Per-row logging removed as promised in the
        // original comment ("TEMPORARY: remove once CHARCHAT-BRACE-DIAG is closed").
        let ccLast = (ccTurns.length > 0) ? ccTurns[ccTurns.length - 1] : null;
        if (ccLast && ccLast.isPov === ccIsPov) { ccLast.ids.push(ccR.sectionId); ccLast.texts.push(ccR.section.text || ''); }
        else ccTurns.push({ isPov: ccIsPov, ids: [ccR.sectionId], texts: [ccR.section.text || ''] });
    }
    return { turns: ccTurns, ids: ccAllIds };
}

// [BUGFIX v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE] WRITE-PASS ONLY (called from _charchatNormalizeDocument,
// onGenerationEnd — never from the read-only side, which must not mutate the document).
// Splits any section _charchatMergeSplitIdx flags as merged (see _charchatSplitMergedSectionsPass
// just below for the full mechanism).
// [v2.61.0 CHARCHAT-SETTLE-POLL, per user's live log] REPLACES v2.59.0/v2.60.0's single fixed
// 150ms retry. That approach had a confirmed failure mode: two scans 150ms apart can BOTH
// report the same too-low section count if NAI simply had not started writing the reply yet
// by either scan — "did the count change between two scans" cannot distinguish "settled" from
// "not started." Live evidence: a turn's split-pass found "0 split" on the first scan AND a
// 150ms-later retry, both agreeing on the same section count — yet a THIRD, separate scan
// (the empty-reply diagnostic's own dump, running later in the same handler) found the full
// reply text already present. NAI was still actively writing between the second and third
// scans. The fix: poll against the EXPECTED growth for a real turn (the last known section
// count + 2, for the Director's own paragraph plus a reply) rather than mere pass-to-pass
// agreement, bounded so a genuinely-failed turn does not poll forever.
const CC_SETTLE_MAX_PASSES = 5;    // hard ceiling: 1 initial scan + up to 4 retries
const CC_SETTLE_SLEEP_MS = 200;    // gap between polls
async function _charchatSplitMergedSections(): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeCharChat.markerId === null) return;
    // No baseline yet (turn 1 of a chat) — nothing to compare growth against, so do exactly
    // the single scan+split pass and record the baseline for next turn. Matches pre-v2.61.0
    // behaviour for a chat's opening turn.
    let ccExpectMin = (_ccLastSeenSectionCount >= 0) ? (_ccLastSeenSectionCount + 2) : -1;
    let ccPass = await _charchatSplitMergedSectionsPass();
    let ccFinalSeen = ccPass.seen;
    let ccPassNum = 1;
    while (ccExpectMin >= 0 && ccFinalSeen < ccExpectMin && ccPassNum < CC_SETTLE_MAX_PASSES) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:merge-diag] pass ' + ccPassNum + ' \u2014 seen=' + ccFinalSeen
            + ', expected at least ' + ccExpectMin + ' for a settled turn. NAI may still be '
            + 'writing \u2014 retrying after a short delay.');
        try { await api.v1.timers.sleep(CC_SETTLE_SLEEP_MS); } catch (_eSl) {}
        let ccRetry = await _charchatSplitMergedSectionsPass();
        ccFinalSeen = ccRetry.seen;
        ccPassNum++;
    }
    if (ccExpectMin >= 0 && ccFinalSeen >= ccExpectMin && ccPassNum > 1) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:merge-diag] settled after ' + ccPassNum + ' passes \u2014 seen='
            + ccFinalSeen + ' reached the expected ' + ccExpectMin + '. THE RACE IS REAL; a '
            + 'single fixed retry was not patient enough for this turn.');
    } else if (ccExpectMin >= 0 && ccFinalSeen < ccExpectMin) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:merge-diag] gave up after ' + ccPassNum + ' passes (' + (CC_SETTLE_MAX_PASSES - 1)
            + ' retries, ' + (CC_SETTLE_SLEEP_MS * (CC_SETTLE_MAX_PASSES - 1)) + 'ms total) without reaching '
            + 'the expected ' + ccExpectMin + ' \u2014 seen=' + ccFinalSeen + '. See CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG below.');
    }

    // [DIAG v2.60.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG, per user: "nothing is showing in the story
    // editor when a response never happens"] Now runs AFTER the settle-poll above has already
    // waited for the expected growth (or genuinely given up), so a firing here is much more
    // trustworthy than the v2.60.0 version — that one produced a CONFIRMED false positive by
    // comparing a scan that simply ran too early. Dump whatever the newest section(s) actually
    // contain so we can see directly whether NAI wrote nothing, an empty/whitespace paragraph,
    // or something unexpected, on the turns where the poll above still could not reach the
    // expected count within its bound. -1 sentinel skips turn 1 (no baseline yet). TEMPORARY:
    // remove once CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG is closed.
    if (_ccLastSeenSectionCount >= 0 && ccFinalSeen <= _ccLastSeenSectionCount + 1) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:empty-reply-diag] section count still barely moved after settle-polling: was '
            + _ccLastSeenSectionCount + ', now ' + ccFinalSeen + ' \u2014 NAI likely generated NO '
            + 'reply text at all (only the Director\'s own turn landed, if that). Dumping the '
            + 'newest section(s) below.');
        try {
            let ccAllDump: any[] = await api.v1.document.scan();
            let ccSeenDump = false;
            let ccDumpRows: any[] = [];
            for (let ccD of ccAllDump) {
                if (ccD.sectionId === _aeCharChat.markerId) { ccSeenDump = true; continue; }
                if (!ccSeenDump) continue;
                if (_aeCharChat.setupIds.indexOf(ccD.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
                ccDumpRows.push(ccD);
            }
            let ccTail = ccDumpRows.slice(-3);
            if (ccTail.length === 0) {
                aeLog('[AE:charchat:empty-reply-diag] the chat region is completely empty past the marker/setup paragraphs.');
            }
            for (let ccDR of ccTail) {
                let ccDText = (ccDR.section && ccDR.section.text) || '';
                aeLog('[AE:charchat:empty-reply-diag] id=' + ccDR.sectionId
                    + ' source=' + JSON.stringify(ccDR.section.source)
                    + ' origin=' + JSON.stringify(ccDR.section.origin)
                    + ' textLen=' + ccDText.length
                    + ' text=' + JSON.stringify(ccDText));
            }
        } catch (eDump) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat:empty-reply-diag] dump scan failed:', eDump); }
    }
    // [BUGFIX v2.62.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-BASELINE] The baseline is NO LONGER recorded here.
    // This function runs BEFORE _charchatNormalizeDocument's grow/remove pass, so the count it
    // took was a PRE-grow count \u2014 while the next turn's comparison count is taken after
    // that grow has already added the 'Emotion:' line as its own section. A perfectly ordinary
    // turn therefore appeared to add +2 sections when it had only added +1, and the v2.60.0
    // empty-reply check false-passed on exactly the failure it was written to catch (see the
    // v2.61.0 addendum, which concluded the heuristic itself was unsound \u2014 it was not, its
    // baseline was measured one pass too early). _charchatNormalizeDocument now records it at
    // the end of its own writes, where the document is finally settled.
}

// Physically splits any section _charchatMergeSplitIdx flags into two real paragraphs: the
// user-typed head keeps the original sectionId and source (still a legitimate, just
// shorter, POV paragraph), and the AI-authored tail becomes a fresh paragraph via
// insertParagraphAfter with no source override — api.v1.document.append()'s own contract
// says script-written text gets 'script' origin, which _charchatRowIsPov correctly reads
// as NOT a POV turn (script data isn't 'user'/'edit'), same as any other contact reply.
// Runs BEFORE _charchatScanTurns in the write pass so grouping/canonicalisation never has
// to see a merged section at all — the in-memory fallback in _charchatScanTurns above is
// strictly a safety net for if this fails.
// [v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE-RACE] Factored out of _charchatSplitMergedSections so the outer
// function can run this pass more than once (bounded settle-poll as of v2.61.0) without
// duplicating the scan logic.
async function _charchatSplitMergedSectionsPass(): Promise<{ seen: number; split: number }> {
    let ccAll: any[] = [];
    try { ccAll = await api.v1.document.scan(); }
    catch (eSc) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] merge-split scan failed:', eSc); return { seen: 0, split: 0 }; }
    let ccSeen = false;
    // [DIAG v2.33.0 CHARCHAT-SPLIT-DIAG] The probe caught a section with a textbook
    // script(0-25)->ai(25-33) boundary that this pass should have split and didn't, so
    // every decision is now logged. Cheap (no generation, no extra scan) and it answers
    // the one question the probe left open: does this pass RUN, does it FIND the
    // boundary, and does the write land? Remove once CHARCHAT-MERGE is closed.
    let ccDiagSeen = 0, ccDiagSplit = 0;
    for (let ccR of ccAll) {
        if (ccR.sectionId === _aeCharChat.markerId) { ccSeen = true; continue; }
        if (!ccSeen) continue;
        if (_aeCharChat.setupIds.indexOf(ccR.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
        ccDiagSeen++;
        let ccFull = ccR.section.text || '';
        let ccIdx = _charchatMergeSplitIdx(ccR.section);
        if (ccIdx <= 0 || ccIdx >= ccFull.length) {
            if (ccIdx > 0) aeLog('[AE:charchat:split] section ' + ccR.sectionId + ' idx=' + ccIdx + ' REJECTED (>= len ' + ccFull.length + ')');
            continue;
        }
        let ccHead = ccFull.substring(0, ccIdx);
        let ccTail = ccFull.substring(ccIdx);
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:split] section ' + ccR.sectionId + ' SPLIT at ' + ccIdx
            + ' head=' + JSON.stringify(ccHead.substring(0, 40))
            + ' tail=' + JSON.stringify(ccTail.substring(0, 40)));
        try {
            await api.v1.document.insertParagraphAfter(ccR.sectionId, { text: ccTail });
            await api.v1.document.updateParagraph(ccR.sectionId, { text: ccHead });
            ccDiagSplit++;
        } catch (eSp) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] merge-split failed for section ' + ccR.sectionId + ':', eSp); }
    }
    aeLog('[AE:charchat:split] pass complete — ' + ccDiagSeen + ' chat sections scanned, ' + ccDiagSplit + ' split.');
    return { seen: ccDiagSeen, split: ccDiagSplit };
}

// [v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE] ccNoBrace renders the POV turn WITHOUT its brace wrapper,
// for the model's view only. See _charchatCanonicalChatText for why, and see
// _charchatNormalizeDocument (which never passes it) for why the DOCUMENT keeps its braces.
function _charchatCanonTurn(turn: { isPov: boolean; ids: number[]; texts: string[] }, povName: string, contactName: string, ccNoBrace?: boolean): string {
    let ccJoined = turn.texts.join('\n');
    if (!turn.isPov) return _charchatCanonContact(ccJoined, contactName, povName);
    let ccPov = _charchatCanonPov(ccJoined, povName, contactName);
    if (ccNoBrace && ccPov) ccPov = _charchatUnwrapPov(ccPov);
    return ccPov;
}

// WRITE PASS — onGenerationEnd ONLY, per user's "maybe we handle it after generation?".
// Deliberately NOT called from onBeforeContextBuild: mutating the document mid-context-
// build would race the very generation it is building for, and that hook also fires on
// dryRun, so a costing pass would rewrite the user's paragraphs. The read-only twin
// (_charchatCanonicalChatText) keeps the MODEL's view canonical every turn; this keeps
// the DOCUMENT's view canonical one beat later, which is all the document needs.
async function _charchatNormalizeDocument(): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active || _aeCharChat.markerId === null) return;
    // [BUGFIX v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-MERGE] Fix any section NAI glued a completion onto BEFORE
    // grouping turns \u2014 see _charchatSplitMergedSections. Failure here is non-fatal
    // (logged, falls through to _charchatScanTurns's own in-memory fallback).
    try { await _charchatSplitMergedSections(); }
    catch (eSpAll) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] merge-split pass failed (continuing with unsplit sections):', eSpAll); }
    let ccScan = await _charchatScanTurns();
    let ccUpdates: { sectionId: number; section: any }[] = [];
    let ccRemove: number[] = [];
    let ccGrow: { afterId: number; lines: string[] }[] = [];
    for (let ccT of ccScan.turns) {
        // [v2.52.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-DOUBLE] POV turns written WITHOUT the literal brace (the
        // 'true' below is ccNoBrace). They are source:'instruction' and NAI renders its OWN
        // brace around an instruction paragraph, so the literal '{...}' this used to write was
        // a SECOND layer. _charchatScanTurns reads RAW text (never NAI's render brace), so the
        // unwrap could only see/strip the literal layer while NAI's outer one persisted -- every
        // reopen re-baked "{ {Name: ...} }". Braceless here makes the document match the model
        // view (both "Name: body") and idempotent. Full repro in CE-INDEX v2.52.0.
        let ccCanon = _charchatCanonTurn(ccT, _aeCharChat.povName, _aeCharChat.contactName, true);
        if (!ccCanon) { for (let ccX of ccT.ids) ccRemove.push(ccX); continue; }
        let ccLines = ccCanon.split('\n');
        // Fit the canonical lines back onto the sections this turn already occupies:
        // rewrite in place where they line up, fold any overflow onto the last written
        // section, drop sections the canonical form no longer needs. This deliberately
        // does NOT force one-paragraph-per-turn — NAI owns how a returned reply is split
        // into sections and fighting that is a losing game; we only fix the text.
        let ccKeep = Math.min(ccLines.length, ccT.ids.length);
        for (let ccI = 0; ccI < ccKeep; ccI++) {
            // [v2.52.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-DOUBLE] Force source:'instruction' on POV sections as we
            // rewrite them: it is what carries turn identity now that the literal brace is gone
            // (_charchatRowIsPov already trusts source==='instruction'). updateParagraphs takes a
            // Partial<Section>, so source is settable here (verified in script-types). Makes the
            // brace-drop safe for a POV paragraph NAI somehow left un-instruction'd, and migrates
            // a turn saved by an older build in the same pass that fixes its text.
            if (ccLines[ccI] !== ccT.texts[ccI]) {
                let ccSec: any = { text: ccLines[ccI] };
                if (ccT.isPov) ccSec.source = 'instruction';
                ccUpdates.push({ sectionId: ccT.ids[ccI], section: ccSec });
            }
        }
        for (let ccJ = ccKeep; ccJ < ccT.ids.length; ccJ++) ccRemove.push(ccT.ids[ccJ]);
        // [BUGFIX v2.34.0 CHARCHAT-GROW, per user] This pass could only ever SHRINK a turn:
        // surplus canonical lines were folded onto the last section joined by '\n', which NAI
        // renders as ONE visual line \u2014 the confirmed "Sara: ...  Emotion: bored glued onto
        // the reply" report. A canonical turn is allowed to need MORE sections than the
        // document currently gives it (body + 'Emotion:' line = 2 lines in 1 section), so grow
        // it instead. Deferred, not inserted mid-loop, because inserting here would invalidate
        // the section ids ccScan already resolved for the turns after this one.
        if (ccLines.length > ccT.ids.length && ccT.ids.length > 0) {
            ccGrow.push({ afterId: ccT.ids[ccT.ids.length - 1], lines: ccLines.slice(ccT.ids.length) });
        }
    }
    if (ccUpdates.length > 0) {
        try { await api.v1.document.updateParagraphs(ccUpdates); }
        catch (eNu) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] turn normalisation rewrite failed (cosmetic only):', eNu); }
    }
    // [BUGFIX v2.34.0 CHARCHAT-GROW] Grow AFTER the in-place rewrites (the head line is
    // canonical by then) and BEFORE the removals (which only target surplus ids this loop
    // never wrote). insertParagraphsAfter, NOT insertParagraphAfter \u2014 the latter returns
    // Promise<void> (verified in script-types.txt) and so cannot be chained by returned id;
    // the plural form takes the whole ordered surplus in one call, which is the shape needed.
    for (let ccG of ccGrow) {
        let ccSecs: any[] = [];
        for (let ccL of ccG.lines) ccSecs.push({ text: ccL });
        try { await api.v1.document.insertParagraphsAfter(ccG.afterId, ccSecs); }
        catch (eNg) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] turn grow failed (cosmetic only):', eNg); }
    }
    if (ccRemove.length > 0) {
        try { await api.v1.document.removeParagraphs(ccRemove); }
        catch (eNr) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] turn normalisation cleanup failed (cosmetic only):', eNr); }
    }
    // [BUGFIX v2.62.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-BASELINE] Baseline for the next turn's empty-reply
    // check, moved here from _charchatSplitMergedSections (see the note at its old site). This is
    // the only point in the turn where the document is actually settled: after the in-place
    // rewrites, after the grow, after the removals. Counted with the same skip rules the split
    // pass uses so the two numbers are comparable.
    try {
        let ccBaseAll = await api.v1.document.scan();
        let ccBaseSeen = false, ccBaseN = 0;
        let ccLastId: number | null = null, ccLastText = '';
        for (let ccB of ccBaseAll) {
            if (ccB.sectionId === _aeCharChat.markerId) { ccBaseSeen = true; continue; }
            if (!ccBaseSeen) continue;
            if (_aeCharChat.setupIds.indexOf(ccB.sectionId) >= 0) continue;
            ccBaseN++;
            ccLastId = ccB.sectionId;
            ccLastText = ccB.section.text || '';
        }
        _ccLastSeenSectionCount = ccBaseN;
        // [FEATURE v2.70.0 CHARCHAT-TRAILING-BLANK, per user] The turn's last section is always
        // the reply's Emotion line (or the reply body, if Emotion parsing found nothing) --
        // never blank, since _charchatCanonContact drops an all-blank canon entirely. Without a
        // blank paragraph after it, the user's next keystroke lands mid-line and they have to
        // hit Enter themselves before typing their own message. One-shot per turn: only fires
        // when the last section is non-blank, so this can never stack up multiple empty
        // paragraphs turn over turn. Not currently configurable -- see the change note for how
        // to add a GlobalConfig toggle if this needs to be optional later.
        if (ccLastId !== null && ccLastText.trim()) {
            try { await api.v1.document.insertParagraphAfter(ccLastId, { text: '' }); }
            catch (eTb) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] trailing blank paragraph failed (cosmetic only):', eTb); }
        }
    } catch (eNb) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] baseline recount failed (empty-reply diag disabled this turn):', eNb); }
}
