// READ-ONLY twin of _charchatNormalizeDocument, for context assembly. This is what makes
// the model's view canonical on the SAME turn the user types it — the write pass only
// runs after a generation, so without this the newest turn would reach the model raw
// (unclosed brace, no header, or a header on the wrong side), which is precisely the
// class of bug v2.23.0 chased.
// [v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE, per user] Braces are stripped from the MODEL'S VIEW. The
// user's reasoning, and it is right: the headers are what tell the model who is speaking, and
// "Nate:" / "Sara:" already do that unaided — so in the context the braces carry no
// information the header does not, while actively teaching the model a convention it then
// imitates. That imitation is the confirmed source of the header-plus-brace turn (v2.41.0)
// and it
// contributed to the thought pass writing a chat reply instead of a snapshot (v2.42.0): a
// transcript is a pattern, and every pattern in it gets copied.
// THE DOCUMENT KEEPS ITS BRACES — this function is the READ-ONLY twin and only builds
// context. v2.27.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-REVERT established by direct test that an unbraced POV
// paragraph reads as a continuation of the previous contact turn, and _charchatRowIsPov and
// _charchatCanonPov both depend on the literal brace being there. _charchatNormalizeDocument
// (the write pass) calls _charchatCanonTurn WITHOUT the flag and so is unchanged.
async function _charchatCanonicalChatText(): Promise<string> {
    let ccScan = await _charchatScanTurns();
    let ccOut: string[] = [];
    for (let ccT of ccScan.turns) {
        let ccC = _charchatCanonTurn(ccT, _aeCharChat.povName, _aeCharChat.contactName, true);
        if (ccC) ccOut.push(ccC);
    }
    return ccOut.join('\n\n');
}

// The whole steering thesis, in one function. Called from onBeforeContextBuild while a
// chat is active — including on dryRun, so the context viewer shows the real thing.
async function runCharChatContextOverride(params: any): Promise<any> {
    let ccResult: any = {};
    try {
        let ccCfg = await loadConfig();
        let ccContact = await loadCharacter(_aeCharChat.contactId);
        if (!ccContact) {
            api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] contact ' + _aeCharChat.contactId + ' could not be loaded \u2014 leaving context untouched this turn.');
            return undefined;
        }

        // [v2.29.0 CHARCHAT-FRESH-THOUGHTS-TIMING, per user's gen log] The pre-reply
        // ephemeral refresh that lived HERE in v2.27.0/v2.28.0 is GONE, moved to the
        // CHARCHAT branch of onGenerationEnd. ROOT CAUSE, confirmed directly by the
        // error text in the user's gen log ("A generation is already in progress"),
        // not the v2.28.0 re-entrancy theory: onBeforeContextBuild fires WHILE this
        // turn's own reply generation is already in progress (NAI's own internal
        // state, separate from this file's ae_genInFlight), so ANY nested
        // api.v1.generate() call made from in here is rejected outright — every
        // single time, hence "static," not merely stale. v2.28.0's _diagProbeActive
        // guard was still correct and necessary (it stopped the recursion the nested
        // call's re-entrant hook-firing would otherwise have caused) — it just wasn't
        // sufficient, because the very first nested call already failed before
        // recursion was ever the active problem. See onGenerationEnd for where this
        // now lives and why that timing is safe.

        // [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-POST] THE FORK IS GONE. Every generation reaching this
        // function is a REPLY again, exactly as it was in v2.37.1. The thought no longer rides
        // NAI's own generation at all \u2014 it is a direct api.v1.generate() call fired from
        // onGenerationEnd AFTER the reply lands (grep generateChatEphemeral). See the CE-INDEX
        // v2.45.0 block for the four versions of evidence that killed the fork; the short
        // version is that NAI wraps this slot in its own turn scaffold no matter what we
        // substitute here, and a labelled-line snapshot cannot survive that wrapping.

        // 1. TOP OF CONTEXT — we own it.
        ccResult.systemPrompt = { active: true, content: await buildCharChatSystemPrompt(ccContact, ccCfg) };

        // 2. LOREBOOK — deactivate every character-linked entry. Participants' entries
        //    are already folded into the system prompt (duplication guard); everyone
        //    else's is the persona-bleed guard. Enumerated from the character index,
        //    NOT from params.lorebookEntries: that parameter carries current state for
        //    entries the framework already knows about this build, which is not a
        //    guaranteed enumeration of every entry. Non-character lorebook entries are
        //    deliberately untouched — world lore is still true during a text message.
        let ccSuppress: any[] = [];
        try {
            let ccIdx = await loadIndex();
            for (let ccMeta of ccIdx.filter(Boolean)) {
                let ccChar = await loadCharacter(ccMeta.id);
                if (ccChar && ccChar.lorebookEntryId) ccSuppress.push([ccChar.lorebookEntryId, { active: false }]);
            }
        } catch (eccL) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] lorebook suppression failed \u2014 persona bleed possible this turn:', eccL); }
        if (ccSuppress.length > 0) ccResult.lorebookEntries = ccSuppress;

        // 3. AUTHOR'S NOTE — DEACTIVATED. [v2.24.0, per user] It used to carry the
        //    contact's ephemeral line. That line now rides the next-message instruction
        //    at the true bottom of context instead (see buildCharChatInstruction): the
        //    AN lands at the user's configured depth INSIDE recent history and the hook
        //    exposes no depth control, so it could never be made "most present", which
        //    is the entire job of a right-now line. Deactivating rather than leaving it
        //    alone is deliberate — the story's own AN is about the STORY, and a text
        //    message is not a story turn (same reasoning as WIZLIVE's AN deactivation).

        // 4. STORY TEXT — narrative header at the top, the CANONICAL chat region in the
        // middle, next-message instruction at the true bottom.
        // [v2.24.0 CHARCHAT-TURNS, supersedes the v2.23.0 dangling-scaffold strip] The
        // chat region is rebuilt from the document rather than passed through: every POV
        // turn re-emitted with its closing brace, every reply with a correct header, no
        // blank paragraphs. That makes v2.23.0's targeted "strip an untouched scaffold"
        // hack unnecessary — it existed only because we ourselves put an unclosed
        // "{user: " in the document, and nothing does that anymore.
        // GUARD, and the reason this is safe: only rebuild when the marker actually
        // SURVIVES in params.storyText.content. That content is already trimmed to the
        // context budget; the document scan is not. If the marker is still present then
        // the whole chat region is present (it is the tail), so a rebuild is length-
        // neutral. If it has been trimmed away, the chat has outgrown the budget and
        // storyText is left exactly as NAI trimmed it.
        // Also deliberate: the setup/greeting paragraph is dropped on rebuild. It is
        // instructions to the USER ("start a paragraph with a curly brace"), which the
        // model has no business reading and previously did, every single turn.
        let ccStory = (params && params.storyText && params.storyText.content) ? params.storyText.content : '';
        let ccMarkIdx = ccStory.indexOf(AE_CHARCHAT_MARKER_TEXT);
        if (ccMarkIdx >= 0) {
            let ccCanonChat = '';
            try { ccCanonChat = await _charchatCanonicalChatText(); }
            catch (eCc) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] canonical chat rebuild failed \u2014 passing the raw region through:', eCc); ccCanonChat = ''; ccMarkIdx = -1; }
            if (ccMarkIdx >= 0) {
                ccStory = ccStory.substring(0, ccMarkIdx) + AE_CHARCHAT_MARKER_TEXT
                        + (ccCanonChat ? ('\n\n' + ccCanonChat) : '');
            }
        }
        ccStory = ccStory.replace(/\s+$/, '');
        let ccBody = '[ NARRATIVE CONTEXT ]\n' + ccStory;
        ccBody += '\n\n' + buildCharChatInstruction(ccContact, _aeCharChat.povName, ccCfg);
        // [BUGFIX v2.69.0 CHARCHAT-USERTOKEN, per user's token-probability capture] THE ROOT
        // CAUSE OF THE EMPTY REPLY, measured rather than inferred. NAI's own token-probability
        // viewer on a failed Send shows the single most likely next token is the chat-template
        // control token <|user|> at 42-56% raw / 53-92% after sampling settings \u2014 i.e. the
        // model is not producing a bad reply, it is ENDING ITS TURN. An immediate end-of-turn
        // token is a zero-length completion, which is exactly what onResponse reports
        // (call#1 final=true len=0) with a perfectly normal generation lifecycle around it.
        //
        // WHY it wants to end the turn, and the differential that proves it: NAI puts the TAIL
        // of storyText AFTER the <|assistant|> marker, so whatever this function ends with is
        // read by the model as its own in-progress output. In normal story mode that tail is
        // narrative prose which trails off mid-scene \u2014 nothing is finished, so continuing is
        // the only sensible move and <|user|> stays negligible. In chat mode the tail was the
        // instruction stack, whose last line is a CLOSED bracket block:
        //     [ End your message with a new line: Emotion: <...> ]
        // Nothing dangles. From the model's seat it has written a complete, well-formed thing
        // and the natural next move is to hand the turn back. Hence the coin-flip behaviour
        // (each Send re-samples: ~50% <|user|> = silence, ~90% 'N' = a reply), the "3+ presses"
        // reports, and why widgets/decorations/images/priming all made no difference \u2014 none
        // of them touch the token distribution.
        //
        // THE FIX: never end the assistant turn on a closed block. End it on a dangling speaker
        // cue, so the only coherent continuation is the message body. This is the same shape the
        // canonical transcript above already uses ('Nix: ...'), so it reads as the next line of a
        // conversation already in progress rather than as a new instruction. The user's own
        // capture of a WORKING turn shows the model's top token was 'N' at 87-93% \u2014 it was
        // already trying to write this exact header; we now write it for it and remove the gamble.
        //
        // NOT DONE VIA logit_bias / stop: both exist, but ONLY on GenerationParams, which is the
        // api.v1.generate() path. Native Send exposes neither (verified in script-types.txt, and
        // consistent with the standing note above _charchatEscapeRx). Banning <|user|> outright
        // was therefore never available for the surface that actually has the bug.
        //
        // The header is stripped-and-reapplied downstream regardless (charChatCleanReply always
        // prepends its own, _charchatStripHeader removes any it finds), so the completion arriving
        // as a bare body is exactly what the existing pipeline already expects.
        if (!CE_DIAG.ccNoPrefill) {
            ccBody += '\n\n' + (_aeCharChat.contactName || ccContact.name || 'They') + ':';
        } else {
            aeLog('[AE:diag] ccNoPrefill ON \u2014 context ends on a closed block (pre-v2.69.0 shape).');
        }
        // [DIAG v2.62.0 CHARCHAT-TAIL-DIAG] The one thing every previous diagnostic in this arc
        // inferred instead of observing: what the model literally reads immediately before the
        // generation point. Free (no scan, no generation), and it is the direct A/B for
        // CHARCHAT-TAIL-TERMINAL above \u2014 a failing turn and a working turn can now be
        // compared byte-for-byte at the only position that differs between them. dryRun is NOT
        // suppressed deliberately: NAI's own context viewer pass logs it too, which is the
        // cheapest way to inspect the tail without sending anything.
        try {
            let ccTailLog = ccBody.substring(Math.max(0, ccBody.length - 300));
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:tail] eph=' + ((ccContact.ephemeral && ccContact.ephemeral.compressedLine) ? 'yes' : 'no')
                + ' storyTextLen=' + ccBody.length + ' last300=' + JSON.stringify(ccTailLog));
        } catch (_eTail) {}
        ccResult.storyText = { active: true, content: ccBody };

    } catch (eccAll) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] context override failed \u2014 falling back to untouched context:', eccAll);
        return undefined;
    }
    return ccResult;
}

// ── Post-hoc reply cleanup ────────────────────────────────────────
// Native generation exposes no per-turn stop sequences (generationParameters.get()
// has no `stop` field — that is the api.v1.generate path only), so instruct/paragraph
// turn alternation plus this regex net is the whole anti-impersonation defence.
// Adapted from the Chat Simulator's truncation pass.
function _charchatEscapeRx(str: string): string {
    return (str || '').replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
}

// [DELIBERATE DEVIATION from the handoff — flagged, reverse if wrong] The handoff says
// the Emotion tag is "parsed + stripped". We parse it but DO NOT strip it. Stripping is
// what the Chat Simulator does because it renders its own bubbles and the raw text never
// reaches the document. Here the reply IS the document, and the entire stated reason for
// keeping the emotion sidecar is that user's separate emotion-image widget script reads
// `Emotion: <word>` off document text with zero changes. Strip it and that script has
// nothing left to read — the two requirements are mutually exclusive as written, and the
// synergy is the one worth keeping. Parsed value is mirrored to _aeCharChat.lastEmotion
// for diagnostics/bridge use; the tag stays in the text.
function charChatParseEmotion(raw: string): string {
    let ccM = (raw || '').match(_charchatEmotionRx());
    if (ccM && ccM[1]) {
        let ccE = ccM[1].trim();
        // [v2.25.0, per user] Canonicalise to the 24-state list via the same
        // resolveEmotion() the image pipeline already uses (EMOTION_MAP) — a synonym the
        // model writes instead (e.g. "annoyed") would otherwise reach the document
        // verbatim and silently miss user's emotion-image widget, which keys off one of
        // the 24 canonical words. resolveEmotion never returns '' (unrecognised words
        // fall back to 'neutral'), so the <=40 length guard below still governs whether
        // an emotion was found at all; normalisation only applies to guard-passing matches.
        if (ccE.length > 0 && ccE.length <= 40) return resolveEmotion(ccE);   // <=40 guard against false positives
    }
    return '';
}

// [v2.27.0] _charchatThoughtRx / charChatParseThought DELETED — CHARCHAT-THINK's
// inline "[Thinking: ...]" line is gone, so there is nothing left to strip or parse
// here (zero remaining callers verified before removal). Thoughts now come from
// generateEphemeral() instead — a real, persisted record (char.ephemeral /
// ephemeralHistory), not a line scraped out of the reply text. See the CE-INDEX
// v2.27.0 entry.

function charChatCleanReply(raw: string, contactName: string, povName: string): string {
    let ccT = (raw || '');
    if (!ccT.trim()) return ccT;

    // Split the Emotion tag off, clean the message body only, then re-attach it. Doing
    // it this way means an impersonation truncation can never eat the tag, and the tag
    // can never be mistaken for message content by the name-prefix strip.
    let ccEmotion = charChatParseEmotion(ccT);
    if (ccEmotion) {
        let ccCut = ccT.match(_charchatEmotionRx());
        if (ccCut && ccCut.index !== undefined) ccT = ccT.substring(0, ccCut.index);
    }

    // Drop a self-prefix the model added on its own — defensive only, since the
    // instruction now explicitly tells it not to write one. We prepend our OWN canonical
    // header below regardless, so a model-written one would otherwise double up.
    // [v2.24.0] Shared with the document normaliser (_charchatStripHeader) rather than
    // a second private copy of the same regex, and it now tries the POV's name too:
    // a header on the WRONG side ("user:" opening Sara's reply) was previously kept and
    // then had "Sara:" stamped on top of it.
    ccT = _charchatStripHeader(ccT, [contactName, povName]);

    // Truncate the moment the model starts speaking for the POV, or trails off with
    // their bare name (both are the model taking over the user's side of the chat).
    if (povName) {
        let ccRxList = [
            new RegExp('(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*?\\*?' + _charchatEscapeRx(povName) + '\\*?\\*?\\s*[:\uff1a]', 'i'),
            new RegExp('(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*?\\*?' + _charchatEscapeRx(povName) + '\\*?\\*?\\s*$', 'i')
        ];
        for (let ccRx of ccRxList) {
            let ccMt = ccT.match(ccRx);
            if (ccMt && ccMt.index !== undefined && ccMt.index > 0) ccT = ccT.substring(0, ccMt.index);
        }
    }

    ccT = ccT.replace(/\s+$/, '').replace(/^\s+/, '');

    // [v2.22.0] The name header is OURS, not the model's — same "bolted on around
    // generation" treatment Emotion already got, in the opposite direction: Emotion IS
    // requested from the model and kept; the header is NEVER requested and ALWAYS
    // applied here, so it is canonical regardless of what the model did or didn't write.
    // [v2.24.0] This is now the FIRST of two applications, not the only one:
    // _charchatNormalizeDocument re-canonicalises the same turn after generation. Both
    // exist because they cover different failure modes — this one owns the text on the
    // way in, that one owns anything the user or NAI did to it afterwards.
    let ccBody = ccT || '\u2026';   // an empty body after cleanup still gets a header + placeholder rather than vanishing silently
    // [v2.25.0, per user] Header and body share a line ("Sara: message"), matching
    // _charchatCanonContact's new format exactly — this and that function must stay in
    // sync (they cover the same shape at two different times, see the comment above).
    let ccOut = (contactName || 'They') + ': ' + ccBody;
    if (ccEmotion) ccOut += '\n' + CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX + ccEmotion;
    return ccOut;
}

// ── Session lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────

// [BUGFIX v2.34.0 CHARCHAT-RESUME, per user] This used to UNCONDITIONALLY append a fresh
// marker + greeting, so the bridge path (which calls straight in here) started a brand new
// thread every time \u2014 the chat menu would show "12 messages" and then hand back an empty
// chat, leaving the old thread orphaned in the lorebook. The native picker never showed this
// because IT resolves resume-vs-start in its own onSelect (openCharChatPicker \u2192
// listCharChatThreads \u2192 resumeCharChatFromEntry) before ever calling this function; the
// bridge has no such pre-step, and the bridge's contact-tap comment claiming "the engine
// resolves resume-vs-start itself" was simply false. It is true NOW: the entry id is
// deterministic (charChatEntryId), so this function does the same lookup the picker does and
// delegates. Placed ABOVE the mode guards deliberately \u2014 resumeCharChatFromEntry re-checks
// every one of them itself, so a guarded-out resume still reports the identical toast, and
// duplicating the list here would only risk the two copies drifting apart.
async function startCharChat(contactId: string, povId: string): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active) {
        let ccExistingId = charChatEntryId(contactId, povId);
        let ccExisting: any = null;
        try { ccExisting = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(ccExistingId); }
        catch (_eRes) { ccExisting = null; }
        if (ccExisting && ccExisting.text) { await resumeCharChatFromEntry(ccExistingId, contactId); return; }
    }
    if (_aeCharChat.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A character chat is already open in the story.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
    if (_aeLiveWiz.active)  { api.v1.ui.toast('A wizard chat is open in the story \u2014 end it before starting a character chat.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeWizardMode)      { api.v1.ui.toast('Close the classic wizard first \u2014 the modes are exclusive.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeYoink.active)    { api.v1.ui.toast('A background generation is running \u2014 try again in a moment.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }

    let ccContact = await loadCharacter(contactId);
    if (!ccContact) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not load that character.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let ccCfgStart = await loadConfig();
    let ccPov: CharacterRecord | null = null;
    if (povId) { ccPov = await loadCharacter(povId); }

    // [LOCKED DECISION] POV is a LABEL ONLY. It names who the contact is texting so the
    // instruction can address them; it grants no scene presence and injects no profile.
    let ccPovName = ccPov ? ccPov.name : '';

    // [v2.24.0] Both paths get the same instructions now: there is no scaffold to
    // "finish" anymore (see the removal note at _charchatAppendPovScaffold's old site),
    // so every user starts a turn the same way. [v2.27.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-REVERT, per
    // user] _charchatCanonPov wraps in literal braces again (see its own comment for
    // why), but the WORDING here is deliberately KEPT from v2.26.0, not reverted with
    // the code: the user never had to type a brace even before v2.26.0
    // (_charchatCanonPov re-wraps regardless of source — "Hi, Sara" and "{Hi, Sara}"
    // both come out the same), so telling them to type one was never accurate. This
    // greeting just no longer credits NAI with doing the wrapping, since that specific
    // claim is what testing disproved.
    let ccGreet = 'You are now texting ' + ccContact.name
        + '. Write your message in a new paragraph, then press Send.'
        + (ccPovName ? (' Your name is added for you automatically; just write what you want to say.') : '');

    let ccBefore = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    await api.v1.document.appendParagraphs([
        { text: AE_CHARCHAT_MARKER_TEXT, source: 'instruction' },
        { text: ccGreet, source: 'instruction' }
    ]);
    let ccAfter = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    let ccAdded = ccAfter.slice(ccBefore.length);
    if (ccAdded.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not start the chat \u2014 document append failed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let ccMarkerId = ccAdded[0];

    // Pin output short. Same snapshot->update->restore shape as WIZLIVE.
    // [v2.24.0] The cap is now this CONTACT's, not a global constant — verbosity is a
    // persona axis and it sets the token budget as well as the wording, because
    // instructing "be brief" under a 240-token cap is two instructions that disagree.
    let ccSnap: any = null;
    try {
        ccSnap = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
        await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: charChatMaxTokensFor(ccContact, ccCfgStart) });
    } catch (eccP) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] param pin failed, continuing without pin:', eccP); ccSnap = null; }

    _aeCharChat = {
        active: true, markerId: ccMarkerId, setupIds: ccAdded.slice(1),
        contactId: contactId, contactName: ccContact.name,
        povId: ccPov ? povId : '', povName: ccPovName,
        paramSnapshot: ccSnap, startedAt: Date.now(), lastEmotion: ''
    };
    _ccLastSeenSectionCount = -1;   // [DIAG v2.60.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG] fresh session, no baseline yet
    await saveCharChatState();

    try { await _charchatRegisterDecorations(ccMarkerId); await refreshCharChatDecorations(); }
    catch (eccDr) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] decoration registration failed (cosmetic only):', eccDr); }

    // [BUGFIX v2.51.3 CHARCHAT-TOAST-WRONG, per user: "the toast is wrong... user input is
    // automatically wrapped in braces for them"] Was telling the user to "write in { }
    // blocks" — wrong instruction for how this actually works: the user types a plain
    // message into NAI's own editor and presses NAI's own Send; onGenerationEnd's
    // _charchatNormalizeDocument pass (see CHARCHAT-BRACE-DIAG just above) adds the
    // braces/header afterward. The user never types a brace themselves.
    api.v1.ui.toast('Texting ' + ccContact.name + ' \u2014 type your message below the marker, then press Send.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 6000 });
    await broadcastCharChatState();
}

// Walk the LIVE document from the marker down (mirrors collectLiveWizTranscript).
// [v2.22.0] Now returns ids AND msgs from one document.scan() — used to be ids-only
// (P1 had nowhere to save a transcript to). Role comes from row.section.source, exactly
// like WIZLIVE — NEVER from name-prefix text, so this works identically whether or not
// a POV was set. Consecutive non-POV paragraphs merge into one turn (a reply split
// across several document sections — header, body, Emotion line — becomes one logical
// turn again, regardless of whether NAI kept onResponse's returned text as one section
// or split it along the embedded newlines; both cases produce the same result here).
async function collectCharChatTranscript(): Promise<{ ids: number[]; msgs: WizardChatMessage[] }> {
    let ccScan = await _charchatScanTurns();
    let ccMsgs: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    for (let ccT of ccScan.turns) {
        // Canonicalise on the way out too, not just on the way in: a saved thread should
        // round-trip in the same shape the live document is kept in, and a turn the user
        // edited by hand between generations has never been through the write pass.
        let ccC = _charchatCanonTurn(ccT, _aeCharChat.povName, _aeCharChat.contactName);
        if (!ccC) continue;   // empty turn — a blank paragraph, or a brace block never typed into
        ccMsgs.push({ role: ccT.isPov ? 'user' : 'assistant', content: ccT.isPov ? _charchatStripBraces(ccC) : ccC });
    }
    return { ids: ccScan.ids, msgs: ccMsgs };
}

function _charchatStripBraces(t: string): string {
    let s = (t || '').trim();
    if (s.length >= 2 && s.charAt(0) === '{' && s.charAt(s.length - 1) === '}') s = s.substring(1, s.length - 1).trim();
    return s;
}

// ── Saved threads (one per character PAIR) ──────────────────────────────
// [v2.29.0, REVERSES the v2.22.0 LOCKED DECISION below, per user] Was: one lorebook
// entry per CONTACT, full stop — POV was "label only" and starting a second
// conversation with someone you'd already texted just found/overwrote the same
// entry. Per user: "each character pair can have one chat between them" — POV is
// now a real partition key. charChatEntryId takes povId as a second argument;
// entries stay backward compatible with every thread saved before this pass with
// ZERO migration needed (see charChatEntryId's own comment for how).
// [v2.22.0 CHARCHAT-SAVE, HISTORICAL — no longer true, kept for context] One
// lorebook entry per CONTACT, not per session — matching how a phone actually
// works. The entry id was DETERMINISTIC (charChatEntryId), so there was no
// per-session sourceEntryId to track the way WIZLIVE needs one: Save always
// overwrote the one thread for that contact, and Clear deleted it outright.
var CHARCHAT_CAT_NAME = 'CE Character Chats';
var _aeCharChatCatId: string | null = null;

async function ensureCharChatCategory(): Promise<string> {
    if (_aeCharChatCatId) return _aeCharChatCatId;
    try {
        let cached = (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_CHARCHAT_CAT, '')) as string;
        if (cached) {
            let c = await api.v1.lorebook.category(cached);
            if (c) { _aeCharChatCatId = cached; return cached; }
        }
    } catch (_e0) {}
    try {
        let cats = await api.v1.lorebook.categories();
        let found = (cats || []).filter(function(c: any) { return (c.name || '') === CHARCHAT_CAT_NAME; })[0];
        if (found) {
            _aeCharChatCatId = found.id;
            try { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CHARCHAT_CAT, found.id); } catch (_e1) {}
            return found.id;
        }
    } catch (_e2) {}
    let newId = await api.v1.lorebook.createCategory({ id: api.v1.uuid(), name: CHARCHAT_CAT_NAME, enabled: true });
    _aeCharChatCatId = newId;
    try { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CHARCHAT_CAT, newId); } catch (_e3) {}
    return newId;
}

// [v2.29.0] Now a function of BOTH ids. povId='' reuses the EXACT pre-v2.29.0 id
// shape ('ae_charchat_<contactId>', no suffix) — every thread saved before this
// pass is automatically and correctly the "no POV" bucket under the new scheme,
// with no rekey/migration pass needed. A real povId appends '_pov_<povId>'; UUIDs
// never contain the literal substring '_pov_', so this splits unambiguously (see
// _charchatParseEntryId, the inverse).
function charChatEntryId(contactId: string, povId: string): string {
    return 'ae_charchat_' + contactId + (povId ? ('_pov_' + povId) : '');
}

// Inverse of charChatEntryId. Returns null for anything outside our own prefix
// (defensive — the category is hidden:true but nothing stops something else from
// living in it).
function _charchatParseEntryId(eId: string): { contactId: string; povId: string } | null {
    let prefix = 'ae_charchat_';
    if (!eId || eId.indexOf(prefix) !== 0) return null;
    let rest = eId.substring(prefix.length);
    if (!rest) return null;
    let povIdx = rest.indexOf('_pov_');
    if (povIdx === -1) return { contactId: rest, povId: '' };
    return { contactId: rest.substring(0, povIdx), povId: rest.substring(povIdx + 5) };
}

// [FEATURE v2.26.0 CHARCHAT-SUMMARY-SAVE, per user] Save an edited summary preview to
// the character's own Journal, not a lorebook entry — SUPERSEDES the v2.25.0 design
// (saveCharChatSummaryToLorebook / the "CE Character Chats" lorebook category), which
// is REMOVED. Reuses charJournalAddBubble as-is: same storage, same broadcast
// (AE_CHAR_JOURNAL_STATE), same surface user already reviews bubbles from — no new
// lorebook category, no new id scheme, no new UI to build. A summary becomes one more
// journal bubble for the contact, exactly like any other journal entry.
async function saveCharChatSummaryToJournal(summaryText: string): Promise<boolean> {
    let ccBody = (summaryText || '').trim();
    if (!ccBody || !_aeCharChat.contactId) return false;
    await charJournalAddBubble(_aeCharChat.contactId, ccBody);
    return true;
}

// [FEATURE v2.25.0/v2.26.0 CHARCHAT-SUMMARY-SAVE] Replaces the old toast+log stub
// (previously flagged in the CE-INDEX as "a real review modal is P6 territory") with an
// actual editable-preview modal, mirroring buildLiveChatTab's _wizModalPreview pattern: a
// multilineTextInput bound to the pending text, Save (writes via
// saveCharChatSummaryToJournal and closes) or Discard (drops the text and closes). The
// live chat itself is untouched either way \u2014 Summarize was always a preview action
// that keeps the chat open, and still is.
var _charchatSummaryPreview: string = '';   // '' = no summary modal pending

async function _charchatShowSummaryModal(): Promise<void> {
    let handle: any = null;
    async function render(): Promise<void> {
        let parts: UIPart[] = [
            { type: 'text', text: 'A narrative-prose recap of the conversation so far. Edit as needed, then save it to ' + (_aeCharChat.contactName || 'their') + '\u2019s Journal \u2014 or discard it. The live chat stays open either way.', style: { fontSize: '12px', opacity: '0.8', marginBottom: '10px' } },
            { type: 'multilineTextInput', initialValue: _charchatSummaryPreview, onChange: function(v: string) { _charchatSummaryPreview = v; }, style: { minHeight: '140px' } },
            { type: 'row', style: { gap: '8px', marginTop: '10px' }, content: [
                { type: 'button', text: 'Save to Journal', style: { flex: '1 1 0', backgroundColor: 'rgba(126,200,160,0.18)', border: '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.4)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                    try {
                        let saved = await saveCharChatSummaryToJournal(_charchatSummaryPreview);
                        if (!saved) { api.v1.ui.toast('Nothing to save.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
                        api.v1.ui.toast('Summary saved to ' + (_aeCharChat.contactName || 'their') + '\u2019s Journal.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
                        _charchatSummaryPreview = '';
                        try { if (handle && !handle.isClosed()) await handle.close(); } catch (_eCl) {}
                    } catch (eSv) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] summary save:', eSv); api.v1.ui.toast('Could not save the summary.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); }
                } } as UIPart,
                { type: 'button', text: 'Discard', style: { flex: '1 1 0' }, callback: async function() {
                    _charchatSummaryPreview = '';
                    try { if (handle && !handle.isClosed()) await handle.close(); } catch (_eCl2) {}
                } } as UIPart
            ] }
        ];
        try { if (handle && !handle.isClosed()) { await handle.update({ content: parts }); return; } } catch (_eUp) {}
        handle = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-charchat-summary', title: 'Summary \u2014 Texting ' + _aeCharChat.contactName, size: 'medium', content: parts });
    }
    await render();
}

// Sigil-delimited, NOT name-prefix-delimited — deliberately, so role reconstruction
// never depends on whether a POV was set. @POV/@CONTACT never appear in the live
// document; they exist only in the persisted lorebook text. Turn content is stored
// EXACTLY as it appeared live (including the contact's own "Name:" header and the
// trailing Emotion line) so a resumed thread is a lossless round trip.
// [BUGFIX v2.23.0, per user] Header simplified — plain names, no ids, no timestamp.
// contactId is NEVER stored in text at all: it's recovered from the entry's own
// deterministic id (charChatEntryId) wherever it's needed, so there is nothing to keep
// in sync here. povName is the only thing this header needs to carry for a resume —
// POV was always "label only" (see P1), so a name string is the whole of what a resume
// needs; there is no CharacterRecord field of theirs anything here ever reads.
function serializeCharChatTranscript(contactName: string, povName: string, msgs: WizardChatMessage[]): string {
    let head = '=== Texting ' + contactName + (povName ? (' (as ' + povName + ')') : '') + ' ===';
    let parts: string[] = [head, ''];
    for (let i = 0; i < msgs.length; i++) {
        parts.push(msgs[i].role === 'user' ? '@POV' : '@CONTACT');
        parts.push(msgs[i].content);
        parts.push('');
    }
    return parts.join('\n').replace(/\s+$/, '');
}

// Cheap, header-only read — no sigil walk needed. contactId is deliberately NOT parsed
// here: callers already know it (it's the deterministic entry id minus the prefix), so
// there's nothing to recover from text.
function parseCharChatEntryHeader(text: string): { povName: string } {
    let first = String(text || '').split('\n')[0] || '';
    let m = first.match(/\(as (.+?)\)/);
    return { povName: m ? m[1].trim() : '' };
}

// Inverse of serializeCharChatTranscript. Sigil lines are the ONLY thing that starts a
// new turn; everything else (including blank lines) accrues to whichever turn is
// currently open, so a multi-line contact reply (name header + body + Emotion line)
// survives the round trip as one turn — same guarantee parseChatTranscript gives WIZLIVE,
// just keyed on an unambiguous sigil instead of a fixed Director:/Wizard: prefix.
function parseCharChatTranscript(text: string): { msgs: WizardChatMessage[] } {
    let lines = String(text || '').split('\n');
    let msgs: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    let cur: WizardChatMessage | null = null;
    for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
        let t = lines[i].trim();
        if (t === '@POV' || t === '@CONTACT') {
            if (cur) msgs.push(cur);
            cur = { role: (t === '@POV' ? 'user' : 'assistant'), content: '' };
            continue;
        }
        if (!cur) continue; // header line / pre-first-sigil spacer
        cur.content += (cur.content ? '\n' : '') + lines[i];
    }
    if (cur) msgs.push(cur);
    let out: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
    for (let k = 0; k < msgs.length; k++) {
        let c = msgs[k].content.replace(/^\s+/, '').replace(/\s+$/, '');
        if (c.length > 0) out.push({ role: msgs[k].role, content: c });
    }
    return { msgs: out };
}

function countCharChatTurns(text: string): number {
    let m = String(text || '').match(/^@(?:POV|CONTACT)\s*$/gm);
    return m ? m.length : 0;
}

// ONE bulk lorebook.entries(cat) call, same shape as WIZLIVE's listWizChatEntries —
// never N individual entry() lookups for a roster-sized picker.
// [v2.29.0] Returns one record per (contactId, povId) PAIR now, not per contact —
// see the CE-INDEX v2.29.0 entry. contactId/povId both come straight off the
// entry's own deterministic id (_charchatParseEntryId), same "nothing to parse out
// of the header, nothing to drift out of sync" guarantee the old per-contact
// version had.
async function listCharChatThreads(): Promise<{ contactId: string; povId: string; entryId: string; turnCount: number }[]> {
    let out: { contactId: string; povId: string; entryId: string; turnCount: number }[] = [];
    try {
        let cat = await ensureCharChatCategory();
        let entries = await api.v1.lorebook.entries(cat);
        for (let e of (entries || [])) {
            let parsed = _charchatParseEntryId(e.id || '');
            if (!parsed || !parsed.contactId) continue;
            out.push({ contactId: parsed.contactId, povId: parsed.povId, entryId: e.id, turnCount: countCharChatTurns(e.text || '') });
        }
    } catch (eList) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] listCharChatThreads:', eList); }
    return out;
}

// Scoped view for one POV, built from a single listCharChatThreads() call — no
// extra lorebook round trip per POV selection. contactId -> {entryId, turnCount}.
function _charchatThreadsForPov(all: { contactId: string; povId: string; entryId: string; turnCount: number }[], povId: string): Map<string, { entryId: string; turnCount: number }> {
    let m = new Map<string, { entryId: string; turnCount: number }>();
    for (let t of all) { if (t.povId === povId) m.set(t.contactId, { entryId: t.entryId, turnCount: t.turnCount }); }
    return m;
}

// Strip a self-header/Emotion line back OFF a turn's content for the distiller — the
// live document formatting is exactly what we do NOT want the summary prompt to see.
function _charchatBareBody(content: string, ownName: string): string {
    let c = _charchatStripHeader(content, [ownName]);
    // [BUGFIX v2.24.0] This used to be /\n?Emotion:.../ — a private copy of the emotion
    // pattern that assumed the tag was preceded by at most one newline and no spaces.
    // CHARCHAT_EMOTION_PREFIX now carries two leading spaces (per user), which that
    // copy would have silently stopped matching, leaking "Emotion: wistful" into every
    // summarizer prompt. Shared builder now, so the prefix can change again safely.
    c = c.replace(_charchatEmotionRx(), '');
    return c.trim();
}

// [REMOVED v2.24.0] _charchatAppendPovScaffold is GONE, along with its three
// call sites (startCharChat, resumeCharChatFromEntry, and the onGenerationEnd CHARCHAT
// branch). Flagged loudly rather than deleted quietly because it was load-bearing for
// two versions and its absence is the point of this pass.
// It appended a pre-filled "{user: " opener the user was meant to finish. Three
// separate reported bugs were all it:
//   - it assumed the user would type the closing "}" — they were never obliged to, and
//     an unclosed "{user: " reaching context is a dangling instruction block whose most
//     natural continuation is to finish user's line, not reply as the contact (the
//     "needs two clicks / wrong speaker" report; v2.23.0 patched the symptom by stripping
//     it back out of context, which is what a self-inflicted wound looks like);
//   - it appended unconditionally after every reply, so a user who typed their message
//     into a NEW paragraph instead of the scaffold accumulated a second POV header;
//   - it fought the user's own newlines rather than absorbing them.
// The replacement is the canonical turn engine above: write whatever you like, we make it
// canonical afterwards. That is strictly more general — it also fixes headers the MODEL
// invents, which no amount of scaffolding could reach.

// Create-or-overwrite THE thread for this (contact, pov) PAIR — deterministic id,
// no lookup needed to know where to write. [v2.29.0] povId param added back (was
// dropped in v2.23.0 when POV was "label only" — see the CE-INDEX v2.29.0 entry for
// why it's back and why this is a compatible change, not a breaking one).
async function upsertCharChatEntry(contact: CharacterRecord, povId: string, povName: string, msgs: WizardChatMessage[]): Promise<string> {
    let cat = await ensureCharChatCategory();
    let id = charChatEntryId(contact.id, povId);
    let text = serializeCharChatTranscript(contact.name, povName, msgs);
    let existing: any = null;
    try { existing = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(id); } catch (_e) {}
    if (existing) {
        await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(id, { text: text, displayName: 'Texting ' + contact.name, category: cat });
        return id;
    }
    // [DEVIATION from the CE-Chats pattern, deliberate] hidden:true — these entries are
    // pure archival storage for the Chat picker, never meant for manual lorebook
    // browsing/editing the way a CE Chats reference entry is.
    let newId = await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry({ id: id, category: cat, displayName: 'Texting ' + contact.name, text: text, keys: [], enabled: true, forceActivation: false, hidden: true });
    return newId || id;
}

// Pull a saved thread back into the story. POV is read from the entry itself, not
// re-asked — an established thread already has a POV, and re-prompting would break the
// "just click and continue" flow the badge exists to enable.
async function resumeCharChatFromEntry(entryId: string, contactId: string): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeCharChat.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('A character chat is already open in the story.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
    if (_aeLiveWiz.active)  { api.v1.ui.toast('A wizard chat is open in the story \u2014 end it before starting a character chat.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeWizardMode)      { api.v1.ui.toast('Close the classic wizard first \u2014 the modes are exclusive.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    if (_aeYoink.active)    { api.v1.ui.toast('A background generation is running \u2014 try again in a moment.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }

    let ccContact = await loadCharacter(contactId);
    if (!ccContact) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not load that character.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let entry: any = null;
    try { entry = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(entryId); } catch (_e) {}
    if (!entry || !entry.text) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not load that saved chat.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }

    // [v2.23.0] POV NAME is read straight off the header, not re-derived from a
    // loadCharacter round trip — the header never stores anything else of theirs.
    // [v2.29.0] POV ID is now ALSO needed (it's half of endCharChat's save/clear
    // target once this chat is active again) — parsed off entryId itself, the same
    // source listCharChatThreads already used to build the picker's sublabel/onSelect
    // for this exact entry, so it is guaranteed consistent with what the user picked.
    let hdr = parseCharChatEntryHeader(entry.text);
    let ccPovName = hdr.povName;
    let ccEntryParsed = _charchatParseEntryId(entryId);
    let ccPovId = ccEntryParsed ? ccEntryParsed.povId : '';
    let parsed = parseCharChatTranscript(entry.text);
    if (parsed.msgs.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('That saved chat is empty.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }

    let paras: any[] = [ { text: AE_CHARCHAT_MARKER_TEXT, source: 'instruction' } ];
    for (let i = 0; i < parsed.msgs.length; i++) {
        let m = parsed.msgs[i];
        if (m.role === 'user') {
            // [v2.24.0] No inner padding — this is the canonical POV shape now
            // (_charchatCanonPov), and content already carries its own "Pov: " header
            // from collectCharChatTranscript. Rebuilding it any other way would just get
            // normalised back to this on the first turn anyway.
            // [BUGFIX v2.51.2 CHARCHAT-RESUME-IDEMPOTENT, per user: "reloading chats
            // sometimes leads to old POV messages being re-wrapped in curly braces"]
            // This unconditionally wrapped m.content, trusting that collectCharChatTranscript
            // (the save side) always hands back brace-free content — true for anything saved
            // by the current build, but nothing here ENFORCES it for an OLDER saved entry
            // whose content might still carry a literal wrapper from before that guarantee
            // existed. Unwrapping first makes this tolerant of both, the same fix
            // v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-POV-IDEMPOTENT already proved for the live-typing path:
            // canon(canon(x)) === canon(x) regardless of which shape x arrives in.
            // [v2.52.0 CHARCHAT-BRACE-DOUBLE] No literal brace -- the paragraph is
            // source:'instruction' and NAI draws its own. Adding one here too was a confirmed
            // cause of a resumed POV turn returning as "{ {Name: ...} }". Unwrap still runs so a
            // thread saved by an older build (which may carry a literal wrapper) normalises clean.
            paras.push({ text: _charchatUnwrapPov(m.content), source: 'instruction' });
        } else {
            let chunks = m.content.split('\n');
            for (let j = 0; j < chunks.length; j++) {
                let c = chunks[j].replace(/^\s+/, '').replace(/\s+$/, '');
                if (c.length > 0) paras.push({ text: c });
            }
        }
    }

    let before = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    await api.v1.document.appendParagraphs(paras);
    let after = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
    let added = after.slice(before.length);
    if (added.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not resume the chat \u2014 document append failed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 4000 }); return; }
    let markerId = added[0];

    let snap: any = null;
    try {
        snap = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
        await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: charChatMaxTokensFor(ccContact, await loadConfig()) });
    } catch (eP) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] param pin failed, continuing without pin:', eP); snap = null; }

    _aeCharChat = {
        active: true, markerId: markerId, setupIds: [],
        contactId: contactId, contactName: ccContact.name,
        povId: ccPovId, povName: ccPovName,   // [v2.29.0] povId now IS persisted/functional again — see the field's own comment
        paramSnapshot: snap, startedAt: Date.now(), lastEmotion: ''
    };
    _ccLastSeenSectionCount = -1;   // [DIAG v2.60.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG] resumed session, no baseline yet
    await saveCharChatState();

    try { await _charchatRegisterDecorations(markerId); await refreshCharChatDecorations(); }
    catch (eD) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] decoration registration failed (cosmetic only):', eD); }

    api.v1.ui.toast('Chat with ' + ccContact.name + ' resumed \u2014 continue below the marker.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 6000 });
    await broadcastCharChatState();
}

// Non-ending preview, mirrors previewSummarizeLiveChat. [LOCKED PURPOSE, per user] this
// is a NARRATIVE recap matching the story's own prose — no chat-transcript formatting,
// no turn labels, no Emotion lines (that's what Close/save already preserves, separately,
// for resuming). Feeds the distiller a bare role:body transcript via _charchatBareBody
// (stripping the live "Name:"/Emotion formatting back out first). DESTINATION UNDECIDED:
// this only returns text, same as WIZLIVE's own preview — whether/where it auto-lands
// (Memory? the story itself? nothing?) is deferred, not guessed at.
async function previewSummarizeCharChat(): Promise<string> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active) return '';
    let collected = await collectCharChatTranscript();
    if (collected.msgs.length === 0) return '';
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let sTranscript = collected.msgs.map(function(m) {
        let who = (m.role === 'user') ? (_aeCharChat.povName || 'They') : _aeCharChat.contactName;
        return who + ': ' + _charchatBareBody(m.content, who);
    }).join('\n');
    let styleHint = [cfg.projectGenre, cfg.projectConcept].filter(Boolean).join(' \u2014 ');

    // [BUGFIX v2.23.0] ROOT CAUSE of summaries reading like truncated fragments instead
    // of prose: executeYoinkGeneration rides api.v1.editor.generate(), which uses
    // whatever generationParameters are AMBIENT right now — including AE_CHARCHAT_MAX_TOKENS
    // (120, SMS-length), still pinned because Summarize is a preview that does not end
    // the chat. Bump the cap for just this one call, then restore the chat's own pin in a
    // finally — the NEXT real chat turn still needs the SMS-length cap back in place.
    let ccSumSnap: any = null;
    try {
        ccSumSnap = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
        await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: AE_CHARCHAT_SUMMARY_MAX_TOKENS });
    } catch (ePin) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] summarize token bump failed, using whatever is ambient:', ePin); }

    let sOut = '';
    try {
        sOut = await executeYoinkGeneration([
            { role: 'system', content: 'Distill the following text-message conversation into a short third-person narrative recap, written in the story\u2019s own prose style' + (styleHint ? (' (' + styleHint + ')') : '') + '. Do NOT preserve chat formatting, turn labels, or timestamps \u2014 write it as prose summarizing what was exchanged, the way a narrator would recap it. Keep only what was actually established or decided. Be concise.' },
            { role: 'user', content: sTranscript }
        ], { tag: 'charChatSummarize' });
    } finally {
        if (ccSumSnap) {
            try { await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ max_tokens: ccSumSnap.max_tokens }); }
            catch (eUnpin) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] summarize token restore failed \u2014 chat may stay at the wider cap until closed:', eUnpin); }
        }
    }
    return (sOut || '').trim();
}

// [v2.22.0] 'save' (Close) now writes the one thread for this contact for real;
// 'discard' (Clear) now also DELETES that saved thread — one thread per contact means
// starting over with someone means clearing what's there, not accumulating saves.
async function endCharChat(action: string): Promise<void> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active) { api.v1.ui.toast('No character chat is open.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 2500 }); return; }
    let act = (action === 'save') ? 'save' : 'discard';
    let ccContactId = _aeCharChat.contactId;

    let collected: { ids: number[]; msgs: WizardChatMessage[] } = { ids: [], msgs: [] };
    try { collected = await collectCharChatTranscript(); }
    catch (eccC) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] transcript scan failed:', eccC); }

    // Save/delete FIRST, destroy the document LAST — a failure here returns early,
    // leaving the chat intact rather than losing it to a half-completed end.
    if (act === 'save') {
        if (collected.msgs.length > 0) {
            try {
                let ccContact = await loadCharacter(ccContactId);
                if (ccContact) {
                    await upsertCharChatEntry(ccContact, _aeCharChat.povId, _aeCharChat.povName, collected.msgs);
                    api.v1.ui.toast('Chat with ' + ccContact.name + ' saved.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
                }
            } catch (eS) {
                api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] lorebook save failed:', eS);
                api.v1.ui.toast('Save failed \u2014 the chat is still in your document, nothing was removed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 5000 });
                return;
            }
        }
    } else {
        // [v2.29.0] One thread per PAIR now, not per contact — clearing deletes only
        // THIS pov's thread with this contact; a different pov's thread with the same
        // contact is untouched. Non-fatal if nothing was ever saved for this pair.
        try { await api.v1.lorebook.removeEntry(charChatEntryId(ccContactId, _aeCharChat.povId)); } catch (_eDel) {}
    }

    try {
        if (collected.ids.length > 0) await api.v1.document.removeParagraphs(collected.ids);
    } catch (eccR) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] paragraph removal failed \u2014 clean up manually from the marker down:', eccR); }

    try { api.v1.editor.decorations.removeRules([AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_MARKER, AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_POV, AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_CONTACT, AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOTION, AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_HEAD, AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_EMOBOX, AE_CHARCHAT_RULE_NAME]); } catch (_eccDr2) {}
    // [v2.48.0 CHARCHAT-IMGWIDGET] The photo anchors go with the rules. Ending a chat
    // strips every chat paragraph, so NAI would dispose these itself on sectionRemoved —
    // this is belt-and-braces for the 'save' path and for any ordering where the clear
    // lands before the strip.
    try { api.v1.editor.decorations.clearWidgets(); } catch (_eccW1) {}

    // Restore pinned generation params (same field-by-field shape as the WIZLIVE restore).
    if (_aeCharChat.paramSnapshot) {
        try {
            let ccPs: any = _aeCharChat.paramSnapshot;
            let ccR: any = { model: ccPs.model };
            if (ccPs.max_tokens !== undefined) ccR.max_tokens = ccPs.max_tokens;
            if (ccPs.temperature !== undefined) ccR.temperature = ccPs.temperature;
            if (ccPs.top_p !== undefined) ccR.top_p = ccPs.top_p;
            if (ccPs.top_k !== undefined) ccR.top_k = ccPs.top_k;
            if (ccPs.frequency_penalty !== undefined) ccR.frequency_penalty = ccPs.frequency_penalty;
            if (ccPs.presence_penalty !== undefined) ccR.presence_penalty = ccPs.presence_penalty;
            if (ccPs.min_p !== undefined) ccR.min_p = ccPs.min_p;
            await api.v1.generationParameters.update(ccR);
        } catch (eccRe) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] param restore failed:', eccRe); }
    }

    _aeCharChat = { active: false, markerId: null, setupIds: [], contactId: '', contactName: '', povId: '', povName: '', paramSnapshot: null, startedAt: 0, lastEmotion: '' };
    _ccLastSeenSectionCount = -1;   // [DIAG v2.60.0 CHARCHAT-EMPTY-REPLY-DIAG] chat closed, clear the stale baseline
    try { await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_CHARCHAT); } catch (_eccRm) {}
    api.v1.ui.toast(act === 'save' ? 'Story mode restored.' : 'Chat cleared \u2014 story mode restored.', { type: 'success', autoClose: act === 'save' ? 2000 : 3000 });
    await broadcastCharChatState();
}

// Startup rehydration — identical contract to restoreLiveWizOnStartup: a chat left open
// in this story comes back live IF its marker paragraph still exists, otherwise the stale
// state is cleared and any pinned params restored from the persisted snapshot. Restoring
// the pin matters more here than for WIZLIVE: a stranded 120-token cap would silently
// truncate every story generation until someone worked out why.
async function restoreCharChatOnStartup(): Promise<void> {
    let ccRaw = '';
    try { ccRaw = (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_CHARCHAT, '')) as string; } catch (_ecc0) {}
    if (!ccRaw) return;
    let ccSt: any = null;
    try { ccSt = JSON.parse(ccRaw); } catch (_ecc1) { ccSt = null; }
    if (!ccSt || !ccSt.active || typeof ccSt.markerId !== 'number') {
        try { await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_CHARCHAT); } catch (_ecc2) {}
        return;
    }
    let ccSecIds: number[] = [];
    try { ccSecIds = await api.v1.document.sectionIds(); } catch (_ecc3) {}
    if (ccSecIds.indexOf(ccSt.markerId) < 0) {
        if (ccSt.paramSnapshot) {
            try {
                let ccRp: any = { model: ccSt.paramSnapshot.model };
                if (ccSt.paramSnapshot.max_tokens !== undefined) ccRp.max_tokens = ccSt.paramSnapshot.max_tokens;
                await api.v1.generationParameters.update(ccRp);
            } catch (_ecc4) {}
        }
        try { await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_CHARCHAT); } catch (_ecc5) {}
        aeLog('[AE:charchat] stale chat state cleared (marker no longer in document).');
        return;
    }
    _aeCharChat = {
        active: true,
        markerId: ccSt.markerId,
        setupIds: Array.isArray(ccSt.setupIds) ? ccSt.setupIds : [],
        contactId: (typeof ccSt.contactId === 'string') ? ccSt.contactId : '',
        contactName: (typeof ccSt.contactName === 'string') ? ccSt.contactName : '',
        povId: (typeof ccSt.povId === 'string') ? ccSt.povId : '',
        povName: (typeof ccSt.povName === 'string') ? ccSt.povName : '',
        paramSnapshot: ccSt.paramSnapshot || null,
        startedAt: ccSt.startedAt || Date.now(),
        lastEmotion: (typeof ccSt.lastEmotion === 'string') ? ccSt.lastEmotion : ''
    };
    // A chat whose contact was deleted between sessions would steer every generation at
    // a character that no longer exists. Fail loudly and close rather than limp.
    let ccChk = _aeCharChat.contactId ? await loadCharacter(_aeCharChat.contactId) : null;
    if (!ccChk) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat] restored chat points at a missing character \u2014 closing it.');
        await endCharChat('discard');
        return;
    }
    try { await _charchatRegisterDecorations(ccSt.markerId); await refreshCharChatDecorations(); }
    catch (eccDr3) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] decoration restore failed (cosmetic only):', eccDr3); }
    api.v1.ui.toast('Still texting ' + _aeCharChat.contactName + ' \u2014 continue below the marker, or end it from the Chat button.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 6000 });
    await broadcastCharChatState();
}

// ── Native entry point ─────────────────────────────────────────
// [v2.22.0] Not-in-chat: characters with a saved thread show a message-count badge and
// resume directly (POV comes from the save, never re-asked) — characters without one
// go through the existing pick-a-POV-then-start flow. In-chat: Close (save + end),
// Clear (discard + end, deletes the saved thread), Summarize (preview, chat stays open).
async function openCharChatPicker(): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeCharChat.active) {
        // [v2.27.0 CHARCHAT-FRESH-THOUGHTS, was CHARCHAT-THINK] The switch lives here for now because this is
        // the only Character Chat surface a user is ever looking at. It is a GlobalConfig
        // value, so toggling it once sticks for every chat and every story. FLAGGED: the
        // tidier home is the Config modal (Section 24, Engine Tweaks) alongside the other
        // global engine switches — put it there when that section is next opened up,
        // rather than leaving it discoverable only from inside a chat.
        let ccCfgPk = await loadConfig();
        await openPickerModal({
            title: 'Texting ' + _aeCharChat.contactName,
            items: [
                { id: 'close', label: 'Close (save and end)' },
                { id: 'summarize', label: 'Summarize (preview, keeps the chat open)' },
                { id: 'thinking', label: 'Fresh thoughts: ' + (ccCfgPk.charChatFreshThoughts ? 'ON' : 'off') + ' \u2014 refresh ' + _aeCharChat.contactName + '\u2019s inner state after each reply' },
                { id: 'images', label: 'Photo attachments: ' + (ccCfgPk.charChatImages ? 'ON' : 'off') + ' \u2014 let either side send pictures (costs a generation each)' },
                { id: 'clear', label: 'Clear (discard and delete the saved thread)' }
            ],
            getId: function(i: any) { return i.id; },
            getLabel: function(i: any) { return i.label; },
            searchable: false,
            onSelect: async function(i: any) {
                if (i.id === 'close') { await endCharChat('save'); return; }
                if (i.id === 'clear') { await endCharChat('discard'); return; }
                if (i.id === 'thinking') {
                    // [v2.27.0] No re-pin needed anymore: generateEphemeral() is a fully
                    // separate call with its own budget, so toggling this never changes
                    // the reply's own max_tokens (unlike the old CHARCHAT-THINK, which
                    // shared the reply's budget and had to re-pin on toggle).
                    let ccC2 = await loadConfig();
                    ccC2.charChatFreshThoughts = !ccC2.charChatFreshThoughts;
                    await saveConfig(ccC2);
                    api.v1.ui.toast('Fresh thoughts ' + (ccC2.charChatFreshThoughts ? 'on \u2014 ' + _aeCharChat.contactName + '\u2019s state refreshes after each reply.' : 'off.'), { type: 'info', autoClose: 4000 });
                    return;
                }
                if (i.id === 'images') {
                    // [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-IMG] Same home as the fresh-thoughts switch, and
                    // the same flag applies: the tidier place for both is the Config
                    // modal's Engine Tweaks section, whenever that is next opened up.
                    let ccC3 = await loadConfig();
                    ccC3.charChatImages = !ccC3.charChatImages;
                    await saveConfig(ccC3);
                    api.v1.ui.toast('Photo attachments ' + (ccC3.charChatImages ? 'on \u2014 either side can send pictures.' : 'off.'), { type: 'info', autoClose: 4000 });
                    try { await broadcastCharChatState(); } catch (_eccTg) {}
                    return;
                }
                if (i.id === 'summarize') {
                    let ccPreview = await previewSummarizeCharChat();
                    if (!ccPreview) { api.v1.ui.toast('Nothing to summarize yet.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
                    // [v2.25.0 CHARCHAT-SUMMARY-SAVE] Real editable-preview modal, replacing
                    // the old toast+log stub — see _charchatShowSummaryModal.
                    _charchatSummaryPreview = ccPreview;
                    await _charchatShowSummaryModal();
                }
            }
        });
        return;
    }
    // [v2.29.0, per user] POV-first now, not contact-first: "Text as who?" then "who to
    // text?" — see the CE-INDEX v2.29.0 entry. Replaces the old contact-first flow
    // (_charchatPickPov, contact chosen then pov asked only for a brand-new thread)
    // entirely; it's gone; this is its only remaining caller's replacement.
    let ccIdx1 = await loadIndex();
    let ccPovCands = ccIdx1.filter(function(m: any) { return m && m.active; });
    if (ccPovCands.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('No characters to text yet.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
    await openPickerModal({
        title: 'Text as who?',
        items: ccPovCands,
        getId: function(m: any) { return m.id; },
        getLabel: function(m: any) { return m.name; },
        searchable: true,
        pinnedOptions: [ { id: '__nopov__', label: 'No POV \u2014 just me', icon: '\ud83d\udcf1', onSelect: async function() { await _charchatShowContactsForPov('', ''); } } ],
        onSelect: async function(m: any) { await _charchatShowContactsForPov(m.id, m.name); }
    });
}

// [v2.29.0] Second step of the POV-first flow: given a chosen pov (or '' for none),
// list the contacts they could text, sublabelled with the message count for THIS
// SPECIFIC PAIR — a contact with a thread under a different pov shows as fresh here,
// exactly the "each character pair can have one chat between them" behavior per
// user. One listCharChatThreads() call (all pairs), filtered in memory by pov —
// same "one bulk call, never N lookups" discipline the old version already had.
async function _charchatShowContactsForPov(povId: string, povName: string): Promise<void> {
    let ccIdx2 = await loadIndex();
    let ccCands = ccIdx2.filter(function(m: any) { return m && m.active && m.id !== povId; });
    if (ccCands.length === 0) { api.v1.ui.toast('No characters to text yet.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 }); return; }
    let ccAllThreads = await listCharChatThreads();
    let ccThreads = _charchatThreadsForPov(ccAllThreads, povId);
    await openPickerModal({
        title: povName ? ('Texting as ' + povName) : 'Text which character?',
        items: ccCands,
        getId: function(m: any) { return m.id; },
        getLabel: function(m: any) { return m.name; },
        getSublabel: function(m: any) {
            let th = ccThreads.get(m.id);
            if (th) return th.turnCount + (th.turnCount === 1 ? ' message' : ' messages');
            return m.briefDescription || '';
        },
        searchable: true,
        onSelect: async function(m: any) {
            let th = ccThreads.get(m.id);
            if (th) { await resumeCharChatFromEntry(th.entryId, m.id); return; }
            await startCharChat(m.id, povId);
        }
    });
}
// ── [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-HUD] Bridge-driven chat verbs ─────────────
// Fire a chat turn the way the Send button does. api.v1.editor.generate() is documented
// as "Trigger a generation at the end of the document, as if the user clicked the Send
// button" — no DOM, no click simulation, and CONFIRMED BY MEASUREMENT to cost zero
// script output budget (the probe watched getAllowedOutput across a full append ->
// generate -> reply cycle and it never moved). That is what makes an unlimited-turn HUD
// chat viable at all.
//
// isBlocked() is checked but NOT TRUSTED as sufficient: the probe caught
// editor.generate() throwing immediately after isBlocked() returned false, which matches
// the concurrency gotcha v2.29.0 already documented for nested generates ("A generation
// is already in progress" is NAI's own internal state, separate from this file's
// ae_genInFlight and evidently not fully surfaced by isBlocked). Hence the retry.
// [BUGFIX v2.34.4 CHARCHAT-GEN-BREAK, per user test] Guarantee the cursor has a trailing
// paragraph break before firing a generation that appends NOTHING of its own.
//
// This is RC1b's root cause, at the two sites v2.34.1 did not touch. 'stage' was fixed by
// appending POV turn + a trailing empty paragraph in one call, and the user confirms
// stage -> native Send now works. 'nudge' and 'regen' both fire _charchatFireGenerate()
// with no append at all, so the cursor sits at the end of whatever paragraph is currently
// last — which after a staged/typed POV turn (nudge) or after removeParagraphs drops the
// old reply (regen) is the POV turn itself. NAI's continuation logic has no break to start
// a fresh section from, so the contact's reply lands INSIDE the POV paragraph: the exact
// reported "adds character response to pov message".
//
// Prevention, not detection — same reasoning v2.34.1 recorded for 'stage': it needs no
// origin inference on a glued section, and _charchatMergeSplitIdx cannot even be consulted
// for a stagedPovIds-marked section, so a post-hoc rescue is not available here anyway.
//
// The blank is NOT added to stagedPovIds (it carries no typed span). If the reply lands in
// it, it is genuinely a contact turn and classifies as one; if nothing lands in it,
// _charchatCanonTurn returns '' and _charchatNormalizeDocument drops it as an empty turn.
// Self-cleaning either way, which is why this is safe to fire unconditionally.
//
// Skips the append when the document already ends in an empty section — re-nudging twice
// in a row should not stack blanks the normalise pass then has to sweep.
async function _charchatEnsureTrailingBreak(): Promise<void> {
    try {
        let ccBrIds = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
        if (ccBrIds.length) {
            // scan() with a from/to range pinned to the same id reads exactly one section.
            // (There is no api.v1.document.section(id) accessor — sectionIds() + a ranged
            // scan() is the documented way to read a single section's text.)
            let ccBrLastId = ccBrIds[ccBrIds.length - 1];
            let ccBrRows = await api.v1.document.scan(undefined, { from: ccBrLastId, to: ccBrLastId });
            let ccBrLast = (ccBrRows && ccBrRows.length) ? ccBrRows[ccBrRows.length - 1].section : null;
            if (ccBrLast && String((ccBrLast.text || '')).trim() === '') return;   // already have one
        }
        await api.v1.document.appendParagraph({ text: '' });
    } catch (eBr) {
        // Non-fatal and deliberately not a return-false: the generation should still run.
        // Worst case is the pre-v2.34.4 behaviour (a glued reply), which the normalise
        // pass's split machinery can still attempt to rescue.
        api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] trailing-break ensure failed (reply may glue):', eBr);
    }
}

async function _charchatFireGenerate(): Promise<boolean> {
    for (let ccAttempt = 0; ccAttempt < 3; ccAttempt++) {
        let ccBlocked = false;
        try { ccBlocked = await api.v1.editor.isBlocked(); } catch (_eIb) { ccBlocked = false; }
        if (!ccBlocked) {
            try { await api.v1.editor.generate(); return true; }
            catch (eG) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] editor.generate attempt ' + (ccAttempt + 1) + ' failed:', eG); }
        }
        await api.v1.timers.sleep(600);
    }
    api.v1.ui.toast('Could not start a reply — a generation may still be running.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3500 });
    return false;
}

// The bridge's whole write surface, in one dispatcher. Every op re-broadcasts state at
// the end so the panel repaints from the DOCUMENT rather than from an optimistic local
// copy — the document is the source of truth for this subsystem and the bridge should
// never hold a second opinion about what the transcript says.
async function handleCharChatAction(payload: any): Promise<void> {
    let ccOp = String((payload && payload.op) || '');

    // [v2.51.6 CHARCHAT-LAUNCHER-SETTINGS, per user] 'thinking' and 'images' are config-only
    // (they write GlobalConfig and re-broadcast; they touch no document), so they run BEFORE
    // the active-chat guard below — the bridge's Chat launcher now surfaces both PRE-chat,
    // matching what the native picker's own items always did by writing config directly. The
    // toast wording is contact-name-safe: '…their inner state…' when no chat is open, the
    // named form when one is.
    if (ccOp === 'thinking') {
        let ccThCfg = await loadConfig();
        let ccThWant = (payload && typeof payload.on === 'boolean') ? !!payload.on : !ccThCfg.charChatFreshThoughts;
        ccThCfg.charChatFreshThoughts = ccThWant;
        await saveConfig(ccThCfg);
        let ccThWho = _aeCharChat.active && _aeCharChat.contactName ? _aeCharChat.contactName + '’s' : 'their';
        api.v1.ui.toast('Fresh thoughts ' + (ccThWant ? 'on — ' + ccThWho + ' state refreshes after each reply.' : 'off.'), { type: 'info', autoClose: 3000 });
        await broadcastCharChatState();
        return;
    }
    if (ccOp === 'images') {
        let ccImgCfg = await loadConfig();
        let ccImgWant = (payload && typeof payload.on === 'boolean') ? !!payload.on : !ccImgCfg.charChatImages;
        ccImgCfg.charChatImages = ccImgWant;
        await saveConfig(ccImgCfg);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Photo attachments ' + (ccImgWant ? 'on — either side can send pictures.' : 'off.'), { type: 'info', autoClose: 3000 });
        await broadcastCharChatState();
        return;
    }

    // Every remaining op acts on the live document, so they require an open chat.
    if (!_aeCharChat.active) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat] action ignored — no chat is open.');
        return;
    }

    // [v2.46.0 CHARCHAT-SHELL-BASELINE] Ops 'stage' and 'send' are DELETED, with bridge
    // v2.13.0's panel. Both existed to turn BRIDGE-HELD TEXT into a POV paragraph — 'send'
    // appended and fired the reply (the pre-v2.33.0 HUD), 'stage' appended and stopped so the
    // user's own tap on NAI's Send could fire it (v2.33.0's budget pivot). Neither has had a
    // sender since bridge v2.8.0 moved to DOM injection, and injection is gone too: under the
    // shell design the user types in NAI's own editor, so NOTHING outside NAI writes a POV
    // turn. The engine has no draft to receive. ('send' itself is deleted further down, next
    // to 'images' — it does not sit adjacent to 'stage' in this file.)
    // The '/ccprobe' origin-dump diag went with 'send' — it was typed into the panel composer
    // that no longer exists, and the question it existed to answer (does origin stay in sync
    // with text after updateParagraph?) is settled: yes — CHARCHAT-STAGED-IDS is retired on
    // the strength of it (see stagedPovIds' own deleted-comment below).
    // WHAT REMAINS is the op vocabulary the NEW UI calls: 'arm' (cancel the in-flight
    // thought), 'nudge', 'regen', 'edit', 'remove', 'images'. Every one of them acts on the
    // document or on config — none of them carries a message body. That asymmetry IS the
    // design: NAI owns composing, we own everything around it.

    if (ccOp === 'arm') {
        if (payload && payload.on === false) return;
        // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG] This mark, read against the timeline the
        // onGenerationEnd marks above lay down, answers the user's question directly: did
        // this Send arrive while a thought was still genuinely in flight (see the branch
        // below), or after the busy indicator had already cleared — and if the latter, how
        // long after. A short gap here on a turn that still failed to reply would point at
        // NAI's own internal slot release lagging behind our JS-side "done" signal, not at
        // the cancellation logic itself.
        _ccRaceDiagMark('arm op received (a Send is imminent)');
        if (_ccThoughtSignal) {
            let ccArmSig = _ccThoughtSignal;
            _ccThoughtSignal = null;   // FIRST: one cancel per signal, and a second arm must not re-cancel a dead one
            try {
                ccArmSig.cancel().catch(function(eArmC1: any) {
                    api.v1.error('[AE:charchat:arm] thought cancel rejected \u2014 it may still be running. If a reply goes missing right after this line, this window is the first suspect:', eArmC1);
                });
                aeLog('[AE:charchat:arm] a Send is coming \u2014 CANCELLED the in-flight post-reply thought. '
                    + 'The previous thought stands, by design. If replies still go missing with Fresh Thoughts ON '
                    + 'and never with it OFF, cancellation is not freeing the slot in time (see the CE-INDEX v2.45.0 block).');
            } catch (eArmC2) {
                api.v1.error('[AE:charchat:arm] thought cancel threw (the send is unaffected):', eArmC2);
            }
        } else {
            // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG] Nothing to cancel — either Fresh Thoughts is
            // off, or the thought had already settled before this Send's pointerdown. Logged
            // explicitly (previously silent) so the race-diag timeline shows every arm event,
            // not just the ones that found something to cancel.
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:arm] a Send is coming \u2014 no thought was in flight to cancel.');
        }
        return;
    }

    // [v2.46.0 CHARCHAT-SHELL-BASELINE] Op 'povclaim' is DELETED. It existed for exactly one
    // reason: the bridge injected the user's turn into NAI's editor with execCommand, so the
    // engine had not authored that paragraph and could not classify it from its own records —
    // povclaim scanned the whole document for the injected string and recorded the id it found
    // in stagedPovIds. No injection, no claim to make. Its sender (_ccInjectDraft) is gone.
    // It also answered, for free, the question v2.37.0 flagged as load-bearing: what origin
    // does NAI stamp on execCommand-inserted text? That question is now moot — the text is
    // typed by a human into NAI's own editor, which is the case the origin rule was written
    // for in the first place (see _charchatRowIsPov).

    // [v2.46.0 CHARCHAT-SHELL-BASELINE] Op 'send' DELETED here with 'stage' (see the note
    // above the 'arm' op): the pre-v2.33.0 HUD's "take bridge text, append it as a POV turn,
    // fire the reply" path. It had no sender after v2.33.0's budget pivot and no possible
    // sender now that nothing outside NAI composes a turn. The '/ccprobe' origin dump lived
    // inside it and goes with it — typed into a composer that no longer exists, answering
    // a question that is now settled.

    if (ccOp === 'nudge') {
        // Generate with no new message — "say something else". The instruction block at
        // the bottom of context always asks for the contact's NEXT message, so this needs
        // no special wording; it just runs a turn with nothing added on the POV side.
        // [BUGFIX v2.34.4 CHARCHAT-GEN-BREAK, per user test] ...but "nothing added on the
        // POV side" is exactly what made the reply glue onto the POV turn: no append means
        // no trailing break for NAI to start a fresh section from. See
        // _charchatEnsureTrailingBreak. This is also the path the bridge's own native-Send
        // nudge lands on (empty draft -> no stage -> user taps NAI Send), which is why the
        // user reports the Send-button nudge "does not work properly".
        await _charchatEnsureTrailingBreak();
        await _charchatFireGenerate();
        return;
    }

    if (ccOp === 'regen') {
        let ccScan = await _charchatScanTurns();
        let ccLast: { isPov: boolean; ids: number[]; texts: string[] } | null = null;
        for (let ccI = ccScan.turns.length - 1; ccI >= 0; ccI--) {
            if (!ccScan.turns[ccI].isPov) { ccLast = ccScan.turns[ccI]; break; }
        }
        if (!ccLast) { api.v1.ui.toast('Nothing to regenerate yet.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 2500 }); return; }

        // Keep a copy in memory, drop the turn, fire. editor.generate() resolves when
        // generation STARTS, not when it finishes, so there is no await to roll back
        // against — the restore can only cover a failure to START. Accepted (per user):
        // worst case is a visible flicker, and the old reply comes back.
        let ccBackup = ccLast.texts.slice();
        try { await api.v1.document.removeParagraphs(ccLast.ids); }
        catch (eR) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] regen remove failed:', eR); return; }
        await broadcastCharChatState();

        // [BUGFIX v2.34.4 CHARCHAT-GEN-BREAK, per user test] Dropping the old reply leaves
        // the cursor at the end of the POV turn that preceded it — no trailing break, so
        // the regenerated reply glues onto the POV paragraph. Must run AFTER the remove
