        // (the break has to be the last section) and before firing.
        await _charchatEnsureTrailingBreak();

        let ccOk = await _charchatFireGenerate();
        if (!ccOk) {
            for (let ccB of ccBackup) {
                try { await api.v1.document.appendParagraph({ text: ccB }); }
                catch (_eRb) {}
            }
            await broadcastCharChatState();
        }
        return;
    }

    if (ccOp === 'edit') {
        let ccIds: number[] = (payload && Array.isArray(payload.ids)) ? payload.ids.map(Number) : [];
        let ccText = String((payload && payload.text) || '');
        if (!ccIds.length) return;
        // Fold the edit onto the FIRST section of the turn and drop the rest: the user
        // edited one logical message, and a turn may span several sections purely because
        // of how NAI split the original reply. Re-canonicalisation below re-applies the
        // header/braces, so the bridge sends BODY text and never has to know the format.
        try {
            await api.v1.document.updateParagraph(ccIds[0], { text: ccText });
            if (ccIds.length > 1) await api.v1.document.removeParagraphs(ccIds.slice(1));
        } catch (eE) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] edit failed:', eE); return; }
        // Safe to normalise here: no generation is in flight (this is a user action, not
        // a hook), which is the one condition the write pass requires.
        try { await _charchatNormalizeDocument(); } catch (_eEn) {}
        try { await refreshCharChatDecorations(); } catch (_eEd) {}
        await broadcastCharChatState();
        return;
    }

    if (ccOp === 'remove') {
        let ccIds: number[] = (payload && Array.isArray(payload.ids)) ? payload.ids.map(Number) : [];
        if (!ccIds.length) return;
        try { await api.v1.document.removeParagraphs(ccIds); }
        catch (eD) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] remove failed:', eD); return; }
        try { await refreshCharChatDecorations(); } catch (_eDd) {}
        await broadcastCharChatState();
        return;
    }

    aeLog('[AE:charchat] unknown action op: ' + ccOp);
}

// ── [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-IMG] Image attachments ───────────────────
// A chat-SCOPED architect pass. Deliberately NOT buildArchitectPromptFromStory, which
// reads the last N sections of the document: during a chat those sections ARE the
// texting transcript, so that function would earnestly compose an image prompt out of
// SMS banter and emotion tags. This one is given the model's own description of the
// shot and nothing else from the document.
//
// CHARACTER RESOLUTION, per user ("fills in character names too"): the speaker's visual
// record is seeded UNCONDITIONALLY rather than being left to getCharacterData's
// whole-word name match over the description. The description for a selfie is "me
// grinning, holding up the coffee" — the speaker's name appears nowhere in it, so the
// match would find nobody and the character would generate as a stranger. Anyone ELSE
// the description does name is matched normally and appended (capped at 4 to match the
// bridge picker's own cap), so "me and Nate at the bar" resolves both.
async function buildCharChatImagePrompt(description: string, speakerName: string): Promise<{ prompt: string; characters: { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string }[] } | null> {
    let ccDesc = (description || '').trim();
    if (!ccDesc) return null;
    await loadImageSettings();

    // Seed: the speaker, always. getCharacterData matches on its argument's text, so
    // passing the bare name is how we ask for "this specific character's record".
    let ccChars: { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string }[] = [];
    let ccSelf = speakerName ? getCharacterData(speakerName) : [];
    if (ccSelf.length) ccChars.push({ name: ccSelf[0].name, visuals: ccSelf[0].visuals, uc: ccSelf[0].uc });

    // Anyone else the description explicitly names.
    let ccOthers = getCharacterData(ccDesc);
    for (let ccO of ccOthers) {
        if (ccChars.some(function(c) { return c.name === ccO.name; })) continue;
        ccChars.push({ name: ccO.name, visuals: ccO.visuals, uc: ccO.uc });
        if (ccChars.length >= 4) break;
    }

    let ccNames = ccChars.map(function(c) { return c.name; }).join(', ');
    let ccUser = 'Photo description:\n' + ccDesc
        + '\n\nThis is a photo taken by ' + (speakerName || 'someone') + ' and sent in a text message \u2014 '
        + 'compose it as a casual phone snapshot (handheld framing, natural/available light), not a cinematic still, '
        + 'unless the description clearly calls for something else.'
        + (ccNames ? '\n\nCharacters identified in scene: ' + ccNames + '. Apply their specific visual tags.' : '')
        + '\n\nGenerate the image prompt:';

    try {
        let ccResp = await aeDiagGenerate('imgArchitect:charchat',
            [
                { role: 'system', content: globalSettings.architectPrompt || DEFAULT_ARCH },
                { role: 'user',   content: ccUser }
            ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_MAX_TOKENS }
        );
        let ccPrompt = ((ccResp.choices && ccResp.choices[0] &&
            (ccResp.choices[0].message && ccResp.choices[0].message.content || ccResp.choices[0].text)) || ccResp.text || '').trim();
        if (!ccPrompt) return null;
        return { prompt: ccPrompt, characters: ccChars };
    } catch (eCcImg) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat] image architect failed:', eCcImg);
        return null;
    }
}

// Dispatch ONE resolved attachment to the bridge. Mirrors assembleSnapshotImage's
// EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE payload shape exactly, with kind:'charchat' so the bridge can route
// it to the chat panel instead of the gallery strip. uuid is generated HERE, not in the
// bridge, because it is also what gets written into the document tag — the two must
// agree or the bubble can never find its own image.
async function dispatchCharChatImage(uuid: string, prompt: string, chars: { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string }[], speakerName: string, isPov: boolean): Promise<void> {
    let ccCompiled = compileActiveImageFields();
    await broadcastToTM(EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE, {
        uuid:             uuid,
        prompt:           prompt,
        characters:       chars,
        extraBaseVisuals: '',
        extraBaseUC:      '',
        kind:             'charchat',
        contactId:        _aeCharChat.contactId,
        povId:            _aeCharChat.povId,
        senderName:       speakerName,
        senderIsPov:      isPov,
        settings: {
            width:    globalSettings.defaultWidth,
            height:   globalSettings.defaultHeight,
            steps:    Math.min(globalSettings.defaultSteps || 28, 28),
            scale:    globalSettings.defaultScale || 5.0,
            quality:  ccCompiled.quality,
            negative: ccCompiled.negative
        },
        autoDownload: false
    });
}

// THE PASS. Called from onGenerationEnd's CHARCHAT branch, AFTER
// _charchatNormalizeDocument — ordering matters twice over: normalisation has already
// split any section NAI glued a completion onto (so a tag can't be half in one paragraph
// and half in another) and has settled the text we are about to rewrite.
//
// WHY NOT onBeforeContextBuild: this calls api.v1.generate() (via the architect). That
// hook fires WHILE the turn's own generation is in progress and NAI rejects nested
// generate calls outright — the exact "A generation is already in progress" failure
// v2.29.0 root-caused for CHARCHAT-FRESH-THOUGHTS. onGenerationEnd is the one place in
// the lifecycle where a nested generate is proven safe, which is why the fresh-thoughts
// refresh lives there too. _diagProbeActive is required for the same reason it is
// required there: the nested call re-fires the whole hook lifecycle including THIS
// branch, and would recurse into itself without it.
//
// Per user, EITHER SIDE's tags are caught — "any [Image: is grabbed so no matter who
// sends it". So this walks every turn, not just the newest contact reply, which also
// means a tag the user typed themselves into their own message works identically.
async function _charchatProcessImages(): Promise<number> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active || _aeCharChat.markerId === null) return 0;

    let ccScan = await _charchatScanTurns();
    let ccJobs: { sectionId: number; text: string; desc: string; isPov: boolean; speaker: string }[] = [];

    for (let ccT of ccScan.turns) {
        for (let ccI = 0; ccI < ccT.ids.length; ccI++) {
            let ccText = ccT.texts[ccI] || '';
            let ccRx = _charchatImageRx();
            let ccM = ccRx.exec(ccText);
            if (!ccM) continue;
            ccJobs.push({
                sectionId: ccT.ids[ccI],
                text: ccText,
                desc: ccM[1],
                isPov: ccT.isPov,
                speaker: ccT.isPov ? (_aeCharChat.povName || '') : (_aeCharChat.contactName || '')
            });
            if (ccJobs.length >= AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_MAX_PER_TURN) break;
        }
        if (ccJobs.length >= AE_CHARCHAT_IMG_MAX_PER_TURN) break;
    }
    if (!ccJobs.length) return 0;

    // [BUGFIX v2.61.0 CHARCHAT-IMG-BUSY-FLASH, per user: "I don't know why it always shows
    // _charchatProcessImages when no images showed up"] Moved HERE from the caller in
    // onGenerationEnd, which flipped busyStage='image' unconditionally whenever the GLOBAL
    // charChatImages feature toggle was on — regardless of whether THIS reply actually
    // requested a photo. That produced a misleading "sending photo…" flash on every single
    // turn even when ccJobs came back empty a moment later. Flipping it here, now that a real
    // job is confirmed, means the badge only ever appears when there is genuinely a photo
    // being dispatched.
    try { await _charchatSetBusy('image'); } catch (_eIB) {}

    if (!isBridgeReady) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('\ud83d\udcf7 Photo skipped \u2014 image generation needs the bridge connected.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3500 });
        return 0;
    }

    let ccDone = 0;
    for (let ccJ of ccJobs) {
        let ccUuid = api.v1.uuid();
        let ccShort = String(ccUuid).replace(/-/g, '').substring(0, 8).toLowerCase();

        // [v2.34.3] Manual _diagProbeActive pair REMOVED \u2014 aeDiagGenerate (the sole
        // api.v1.generate() call site) now sets and RESTORES the flag itself, so this pair
        // was both redundant and a footgun: it cleared to false unconditionally, which
        // would stomp an outer guard if this ever ran nested. See CHARCHAT-OVERRIDE-LEAK.
        let ccBuilt: { prompt: string; characters: { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string }[] } | null = null;
        try { ccBuilt = await buildCharChatImagePrompt(ccJ.desc, ccJ.speaker); }
        catch (eB) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] image prompt build failed:', eB); }

        if (!ccBuilt) {
            // Leave the tag UNRESOLVED rather than stamping a uuid onto an image that
            // was never dispatched — a resolved tag pointing at nothing would render
            // as a permanently-spinning bubble the next pass would refuse to retry.
            aeLog('[AE:charchat] image architect returned nothing for: ' + ccJ.desc);
            continue;
        }

        try { await dispatchCharChatImage(ccUuid, ccBuilt.prompt, ccBuilt.characters, ccJ.speaker, ccJ.isPov); }
        catch (eD) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] image dispatch failed:', eD); continue; }

        // Rewrite ONLY the first unresolved tag in this section, and only after a
        // successful dispatch. String.replace with a non-global regex hits the first
        // match only, which is what the cap above already assumes.
        let ccNew = ccJ.text.replace(/\[[ \t]*Image[ \t]*:[ \t]*([^\]\n\r]+?)[ \t]*\]/i,
            '[Image#' + ccShort + ': ' + ccJ.desc + ']');
        try {
            await api.v1.document.updateParagraph(ccJ.sectionId, { text: ccNew });
            ccDone++;
        } catch (eU) {
            api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] image tag rewrite failed (image still generating, bubble will not bind):', eU);
        }
    }
    return ccDone;
}

// Turn list for the bridge HUD. The bridge paints bubbles from THIS — it does not read
// the DOM for role. Role comes from _charchatScanTurns (section source/origin metadata),
// which is the same guarantee the rest of this subsystem relies on: role is NEVER
// inferred from text.
async function charChatTurnsPayload(): Promise<any[]> {
    if (!_aeCharChat.active || _aeCharChat.markerId === null) return [];
    let ccScan = await _charchatScanTurns();
    let ccOut: any[] = [];
    for (let ccT of ccScan.turns) {
        let ccCanon = _charchatCanonTurn(ccT, _aeCharChat.povName, _aeCharChat.contactName);
        if (!ccCanon) continue;

        // Split the display text into the parts the bridge renders separately. The
        // emotion tag and the image tags stay in the DOCUMENT (see _charchatImageRx's
        // note) but the bridge wants them as fields, not as literal text in a bubble.
        let ccEmotion = '';
        let ccCut = ccCanon.match(_charchatEmotionRx());
        if (ccCut && ccCut[1] && ccCut.index !== undefined) {
            ccEmotion = resolveEmotion(ccCut[1].trim());
            ccCanon = ccCanon.substring(0, ccCut.index);
        }

        let ccImgs: { uuid: string; desc: string; pending: boolean }[] = [];
        let ccRxR = _charchatImageResolvedRx();
        let ccMr: any;
        while ((ccMr = ccRxR.exec(ccCanon)) !== null) ccImgs.push({ uuid: ccMr[1], desc: ccMr[2], pending: false });
        let ccRxU = _charchatImageRx();
        let ccMu: any;
        while ((ccMu = ccRxU.exec(ccCanon)) !== null) ccImgs.push({ uuid: '', desc: ccMu[1], pending: true });

        // Body = the text with both tag forms and the leading name header removed.
        let ccBody = ccCanon
            .replace(_charchatImageResolvedRx(), '')
            .replace(_charchatImageRx(), '');
        ccBody = _charchatStripHeader(_charchatUnwrapPov(ccBody), [_aeCharChat.povName, _aeCharChat.contactName]);
        ccBody = ccBody.replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n').trim();

        ccOut.push({
            ids:      ccT.ids,
            isPov:    ccT.isPov,
            speaker:  ccT.isPov ? (_aeCharChat.povName || 'You') : (_aeCharChat.contactName || 'They'),
            body:     ccBody,
            emotion:  ccEmotion,
            images:   ccImgs,
            raw:      ccCanon
        });
    }
    return ccOut;
}

// ── [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] end ─────────────────────────────────────


// [vNEXT] Run a budget-free generation against a custom message array. Resolves with
// the captured response text. Guards against overlapping with any in-flight gen.
// [v0.9.7.12] editor.generate() shares the same script-level rate limit as
// api.v1.generate(). Long wizard sessions can exhaust this budget. Added:
//   - api.v1.editor.isBlocked() check (editor already mid-generation)
//   - api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() floor (< 80 tokens = budget near empty)
//   - friendly error surfaced as toast when the rate limit is hit
// [v1.21.0 YOINK-HARDEN] Renamed from executeYoinkGeneration — this is now the single-
// attempt primitive. executeYoinkGeneration (below) wraps this with model pinning,
// a language guard, and one corrective retry. Do not call this directly from wizard
// code; call executeYoinkGeneration.

// ── [DIAG BUDGET] Per-generation token-budget instrument ─────────────────────
// [DIAG] Additive, engine-only. Flip AE_DIAG_BUDGET=false to silence, or delete this
// block plus its call sites (all tagged [DIAG]) to remove entirely. Measures the
// api.v1.script output/input allowance deltas across each generation so we can SEE
// whether the yoink (editor.generate hijack) draws on the script budget vs a plain
// api.v1.generate(). Type /budgetprobe or /budgetlog into any wizard chat to run the
// A/B probe or dump the recent log; every real generation also logs a line + a toast.
var AE_DIAG_BUDGET = true;
var _diagProbeActive = false;      // true while ANY of our own nested api.v1.generate() calls is in flight — api.v1.generate() fires the full hook lifecycle, so every hook checks this first and no-ops rather than treating the nested call as a story/chat/wizard turn. [v2.34.2/v2.34.3] Set and RESTORED by aeDiagGenerate alone (the sole api.v1.generate() call site); the old hand-rolled true/false pairs at each caller are gone — they were redundant once the wrapper owned it, and they cleared unconditionally, which stomps an outer guard when nested.
interface DiagBudgetPre { out: number; inp: number; kind: string; continuityId: string | null; }
var _diagBudgetPre: DiagBudgetPre | null = null;
var _diagBudgetLog: string[] = [];
function diagBudO(): number { try { return api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput ? api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() : -1; } catch (e) { return -1; } }
function diagBudI(): number { try { return api.v1.script.getAllowedInput ? api.v1.script.getAllowedInput() : -1; } catch (e) { return -1; } }
function diagReplenishSec(): number { try { let t = api.v1.script.getTimeUntilAllowedOutput ? api.v1.script.getTimeUntilAllowedOutput(1000) : -1; return t >= 0 ? Math.round(t / 1000) : -1; } catch (e) { return -1; } }
// [v2.74.0 GEN-BUDGET] Public budget snapshot for the HUD. diagBudO/diagBudI above are
// diagnostic-only and channel-gated; this one is a first-class payload the bridge shows
// to the user, so it is deliberately separate and always available.
//
// waitSec uses getTimeUntilAllowedOutput, which per script-types "does not take into
// account whether the user interaction flag is set on those tokens" — so it is a FLOOR,
// not a promise. Output replenishes only while the user has interacted with the script
// recently, which is exactly why the bridge copy tells them to advance the story rather
// than just wait. Do not present waitSec as a countdown to guaranteed availability.
//
// NOTE: this never calls waitForAllowedOutput. generateFieldContent documents why —
// these paths run from modal/bridge callbacks where that signal never fires, so awaiting
// it deadlocks. Check and report; never block.
function aeBudgetReport(needOut?: number): { out: number; inp: number; waitSec: number; need: number } {
    const bOut = diagBudO();
    const bInp = diagBudI();
    const bNeed = (typeof needOut === 'number' && needOut > 0) ? needOut : 0;
    let bWait = -1;
    try {
        if (bNeed > 0 && api.v1.script.getTimeUntilAllowedOutput) {
            const ms = api.v1.script.getTimeUntilAllowedOutput(bNeed);
            bWait = ms >= 0 ? Math.ceil(ms / 1000) : -1;
        } else {
            bWait = diagReplenishSec();
        }
    } catch (eBud) { bWait = -1; }
    return { out: bOut, inp: bInp, waitSec: bWait, need: bNeed };
}

function diagBudgetSnapshot(kind: string, continuityId: string | null): void {
    if (!AE_DIAG_BUDGET) return;
    _diagBudgetPre = { out: diagBudO(), inp: diagBudI(), kind: kind, continuityId: continuityId };
}
function diagBudgetClose(continuityId: string | null): void {
    if (!AE_DIAG_BUDGET || !_diagBudgetPre) return;
    if (continuityId && _diagBudgetPre.continuityId && continuityId !== _diagBudgetPre.continuityId) return;
    let pre = _diagBudgetPre; _diagBudgetPre = null;
    let pO = diagBudO(), pI = diagBudI();
    let dO = pO - pre.out, dI = pI - pre.inp;
    let line = '[AE:budget] ' + pre.kind
        + '  out ' + pre.out + '\u2192' + pO + ' (\u0394' + (dO >= 0 ? '+' : '') + dO + ')'
        + '  in ' + pre.inp + '\u2192' + pI + ' (\u0394' + (dI >= 0 ? '+' : '') + dI + ')'
        + '  replenish~' + diagReplenishSec() + 's';
    aeLog(line);
    _diagBudgetLog.push(line);
    if (_diagBudgetLog.length > 30) _diagBudgetLog.shift();
}
function dumpBudgetLog(): string {
    if (!_diagBudgetLog.length) return 'No budget readings yet. Run a wizard turn or /budgetprobe first.\nCurrent: out=' + diagBudO() + ' in=' + diagBudI() + ' replenish~' + diagReplenishSec() + 's';
    return 'Recent generation budget readings (newest last):\n' + _diagBudgetLog.join('\n') + '\nCurrent: out=' + diagBudO() + ' in=' + diagBudI();
}
// A/B probe: measure a tiny api.v1.generate() vs a tiny yoink, back to back, so their
// real budget cost can be compared directly. api.v1.generate is glm-4-6-only for chat
// (per docs), which is why the probe pins that model. _diagProbeActive keeps a probe
// generation from advancing story state if api.v1.generate turns out to fire the hooks.
async function runBudgetProbe(): Promise<string> {
    let L: string[] = ['Budget A/B probe (out=output allowance, in=input allowance):'];
    L.push('start:            out=' + diagBudO() + ' in=' + diagBudI());
    let a0o = diagBudO(), a0i = diagBudI(), aOk = false, aErr = '';
    // [v2.34.3] Manual _diagProbeActive pair removed \u2014 aeDiagGenerate sets/restores it.
    try {
        let r: any = await aeDiagGenerate('diag:budgetprobe:direct', [{ role: 'user', content: 'Reply with exactly one word: ping' }], { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 8, temperature: 0 });
        aOk = !!(r && r.choices && r.choices[0]);
    } catch (e) { aErr = String((e && (e as any).message) || e); }
    let a1o = diagBudO(), a1i = diagBudI();
    L.push('api.v1.generate:  ' + (aOk ? 'ok ' : ('ERR ' + aErr + ' ')) + ' out \u0394' + (a1o - a0o) + '  in \u0394' + (a1i - a0i));
    let b0o = diagBudO(), b0i = diagBudI(), bOk = false, bErr = '';
    try {
        let ry = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: 'Reply with exactly one word: ping' }, { role: 'user', content: 'ping' }], { tag: 'diag:budgetprobe' });
        bOk = !!(ry && ry.trim());
    } catch (e) { bErr = String((e && (e as any).message) || e); }
    let b1o = diagBudO(), b1i = diagBudI();
    L.push('yoink:            ' + (bOk ? 'ok ' : ('ERR ' + bErr + ' ')) + ' out \u0394' + (b1o - b0o) + '  in \u0394' + (b1i - b0i));
    L.push('end:              out=' + diagBudO() + ' in=' + diagBudI() + '  replenish~' + diagReplenishSec() + 's');
    let out = L.join('\n');
    aeLog(out);
    return out;
}
// ── [DIAG BUDGET] end ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── [DIAG GEN v1.0.0] Generation call diagnostics — engine-wide ──────────────
// [v2.20.0 GENDIAG] Root-caused report: "Generate All From Concept Dump" (and,
// less visibly, every other wizard/field generator) could die on a single
// transient failure — most often an AbortError surfacing from NAI's own
// generation pipeline on api.v1.editor.generate() — with nothing surfaced but a
// generic "Failed." toast and a bare console.error. There was also no shared
// visibility across the two call families: the budget-free YOINK path
// (executeYoinkGeneration, used by the World-Build Wizard + "Generate All") and
// the budgeted DIRECT path (api.v1.generate(), used by per-field regen,
// ephemeral/mid-range/relationship/hook updates, and image-prompt generation)
// each swallowed their own errors independently with no common record.
//
// This block gives every engine generation call — both paths — one shared,
// tagged ring buffer, so a failure of any kind can be reviewed after the fact
// via console (visible in eruda) or the in-UI viewer (Config modal's
// "🩺 Generation Diagnostics" section, and a matching entry point next to
// "Generate All From Concept Dump" in the character editor). See
// buildGenDiagContent() (Section 24 area) for the UI, and diagGenRecord() /
// aeDiagGenerate() below for the two recording points. Deliberately NOT wired
// into api.v1.storage/storyStorage — this is in-memory, session-only, cheap-by-
// design diagnostic scaffolding, not user data; it resets on script reload same
// as _diagBudgetLog above. No new storage key.
interface AEGenDiagEntry {
    id: number;
    tag: string;                 // which generator/feature (and often character) this call belongs to
    method: 'yoink' | 'direct';  // executeYoinkGeneration vs api.v1.generate
    startedAt: number;           // Date.now() when the call began
    ms: number;                  // wall-clock latency for this entry's outcome
    outcome: 'ok' | 'empty' | 'error';
    errorName: string;           // e.g. 'AbortError', 'Error' — '' when outcome !== 'error'
    errorMessage: string;
    retried: boolean;            // a same-messages retry was attempted after a thrown error (yoink only)
    driftDetected: boolean;      // non-Latin/CJK drift heuristic tripped (yoink only)
    validatorFailed: boolean;    // caller-supplied validator rejected the raw output (yoink only)
    outLen: number;              // length of the returned text, 0 on error
    budgetOut: number;
    budgetIn: number;
}
var AE_DIAG_GEN = true;          // flip-in-code kill switch, mirrors AE_DIAG_BUDGET above
var AE_DIAG_GEN_MAX = 60;        // ring buffer cap — oldest entries drop first
var _diagGenLog: AEGenDiagEntry[] = [];
var _diagGenSeq = 0;

function diagGenRecord(entry: {
    tag: string; method: 'yoink' | 'direct'; startedAt: number; ms: number;
    outcome: 'ok' | 'empty' | 'error'; errorName: string; errorMessage: string;
    retried: boolean; driftDetected: boolean; validatorFailed: boolean; outLen: number;
    budgetOut: number; budgetIn: number;
}): void {
    if (!AE_DIAG_GEN) return;
    _diagGenSeq++;
    let full: AEGenDiagEntry = { id: _diagGenSeq, tag: entry.tag, method: entry.method, startedAt: entry.startedAt,
        ms: entry.ms, outcome: entry.outcome, errorName: entry.errorName, errorMessage: entry.errorMessage,
        retried: entry.retried, driftDetected: entry.driftDetected, validatorFailed: entry.validatorFailed,
        outLen: entry.outLen, budgetOut: entry.budgetOut, budgetIn: entry.budgetIn };
    _diagGenLog.push(full);
    if (_diagGenLog.length > AE_DIAG_GEN_MAX) _diagGenLog.shift();
    let line = '[AE:gendiag] #' + full.id + ' ' + full.method + '  tag=' + full.tag
        + '  outcome=' + full.outcome
        + (full.errorName ? ('  ' + full.errorName + ': ' + full.errorMessage) : '')
        + '  ms=' + full.ms
        + (full.retried ? '  retried' : '')
        + (full.driftDetected ? '  drift' : '')
        + (full.validatorFailed ? '  validatorFailed' : '')
        + '  outLen=' + full.outLen
        + '  budget(out=' + full.budgetOut + ',in=' + full.budgetIn + ')';
    if (full.outcome === 'error') api.v1.error(line); else aeLog(line);
}

function dumpGenDiagLog(): string {
    if (!_diagGenLog.length) return 'No generation calls logged yet this session.';
    let lines = _diagGenLog.slice().reverse().map(function(e) {
        return '#' + e.id + ' [' + new Date(e.startedAt).toLocaleTimeString() + ']  ' + e.method + '  ' + e.tag
            + '\n    outcome=' + e.outcome
            + (e.errorName ? ('  ' + e.errorName + ': ' + e.errorMessage) : '')
            + '  ms=' + e.ms
            + (e.retried ? '  retried' : '')
            + (e.driftDetected ? '  drift' : '')
            + (e.validatorFailed ? '  validatorFailed' : '')
            + '  outLen=' + e.outLen
            + '  budget(out=' + e.budgetOut + ',in=' + e.budgetIn + ')';
    });
    let errCount = _diagGenLog.filter(function(e) { return e.outcome === 'error'; }).length;
    return 'Recent generation calls, newest first (' + _diagGenLog.length + ' logged, ' + errCount + ' error' + (errCount === 1 ? '' : 's') + '):\n' + lines.join('\n');
}

function clearGenDiagLog(): void { _diagGenLog = []; _diagGenSeq = 0; }

// [v2.39.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-NATIVE] aeGenerateBounded() removed — its only caller was
// generateChatEphemeral. [v2.45.0] generateChatEphemeral is BACK (post-reply, direct
// api.v1.generate()) and deliberately did NOT bring this with it: it takes a cancellation
// signal instead, cancelled by op 'arm' when a Send is imminent, which is the bound that
// actually matters here. A wall-clock timeout would only abandon a call that is still
// running. It was a good primitive:
// real cancellation via api.v1.createCancellationSignal(), always awaited to settlement,
// never a Promise.race. If a future pass needs a bounded nested generation, take it from
// v2.38.0 rather than rewriting it — the invariant it protects is subtle and the naive
// version (race, abandon, proceed) reintroduces the exact collision it existed to remove.
// Deleted rather than parked: an unused generation path is how this file grew a hidden HUD.

// Thin instrumented wrapper around api.v1.generate() — the DIRECT (budgeted)
// generation path. Every existing caller's messages/params pass through
// unchanged and the raw response is returned unchanged (including on success
// with empty text) so none of the callers' own parsing/fallback logic needs to
// change — this only adds a diagGenRecord() entry on the way through. Callers
// keep their own try/catch; a thrown error is recorded here AND rethrown so
// existing fallback behavior (return null/false, toast, etc.) is unaffected.
// [BUGFIX v2.34.2 CHARCHAT-OVERRIDE-LEAK, per user report] ROOT CAUSE of the instruction
// text appearing IN the document ("...not the true feeling. ]Sara: whatever u want"), and
// of nudge/regen misbehaving. onBeforeContextBuild's CHARCHAT branch claimed in its own
// comment that "story generation cannot run while a chat is open, so every generation
// reaching this line IS a chat turn — which is what makes applying the override
// unconditionally safe." That is FALSE, and this file's own budget probe already recorded
// the counter-evidence in the user's log: "onBeforeContextBuild fired (api.v1.generate
// DOES build story context)". generateEphemeral() and the imgArchitect pass are
// api.v1.generate() calls that run WHILE a chat is open, so they reached that line and had
// the full chat override applied to them — system prompt replaced, character lorebook
// suppressed, and a storyText whose tail is buildCharChatInstruction's bracket block. The
// user's leaked fragment is exactly that tail, closing ' ]' included. It also explains the
// repeated 'extractJSON failed' on the ephemeral/architect tags: those calls ask for JSON
// but were handed a context ending in "Write ONLY the message body: ... never write a name
// header", so the model answered the wrong question.
// _diagProbeActive is ALREADY the documented mechanism for exactly this ("true while ANY of
// our own nested api.v1.generate() calls is in flight ... every hook checks this first and
// no-ops rather than treating the nested call as a story/chat/wizard turn" — see its own
// declaration comment). It was simply never set for every nested call: runBudgetProbe and
// the imgArchitect loop set it by hand, generateEphemeral's other paths did not. Setting it
// HERE covers all of them at once, because this wrapper is the SOLE caller of
// api.v1.generate() in the file (verified: grep 'api.v1.generate(' — this is the only
// call site outside comments).
// SAVE/RESTORE, not a blind reset to false: two call sites (runBudgetProbe, the
// imgArchitect loop) already set the flag around a region that CONTAINS an aeDiagGenerate
// call, so clearing unconditionally on the way out would drop their guard early and
// re-expose the very window they set it for. Restoring the prior value makes nesting safe
// in either order.
// [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] Optional 5th-arg cancellation signal, threaded through
// to api.v1.generate(). Verified against script-types.txt: generate(messages, params,
// callback?, behaviour?, signal?) — callback and behaviour are passed as undefined so the
// existing call sites' semantics are byte-identical to before. Every current caller omits
// the argument and is unaffected; only aeGenerateBounded() supplies one.
// [v2.45.0] behaviour param KEPT (v2.44.0's collision probe added it and is otherwise
// deleted). Every other caller passes undefined and is unaffected — api.v1.generate()'s own
// default applies exactly as before. Its one live caller is now generateChatEphemeral, which
// asks for 'background': it runs after the reply has landed, while the user is reading and
// typing, and must never block the editor. Signature per script-types.txt:
// generate(messages, params, callback?, behaviour?, signal?).
async function aeDiagGenerate(tag: string, messages: any[], params: any, signal?: any, behaviour?: 'background' | 'blocking'): Promise<any> {
    let t0 = Date.now();
    let b0o = diagBudO(), b0i = diagBudI();
    let ccProbePrev = _diagProbeActive;
    _diagProbeActive = true;
    try {
        let resp: any = signal
            ? await api.v1.generate(messages, params, undefined, behaviour, signal)
            : await api.v1.generate(messages, params, undefined, behaviour);
        let outText = String((resp && resp.choices && resp.choices[0] &&
            (resp.choices[0].text || (resp.choices[0].message && resp.choices[0].message.content))) || (resp && resp.text) || '');
        diagGenRecord({ tag: tag, method: 'direct', startedAt: t0, ms: Date.now() - t0,
            outcome: outText.trim() ? 'ok' : 'empty', errorName: '', errorMessage: '',
            retried: false, driftDetected: false, validatorFailed: false,
            outLen: outText.length, budgetOut: b0o, budgetIn: b0i });
        return resp;
    } catch (e) {
        diagGenRecord({ tag: tag, method: 'direct', startedAt: t0, ms: Date.now() - t0,
            outcome: 'error', errorName: String((e && (e as any).name) || 'Error'), errorMessage: String((e && (e as any).message) || e),
            retried: false, driftDetected: false, validatorFailed: false,
            outLen: 0, budgetOut: b0o, budgetIn: b0i });
        throw e;
    } finally {
        _diagProbeActive = ccProbePrev;
    }
}
// ── [DIAG GEN] end ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function executeYoinkGenerationAttempt(messages: any[]): Promise<string> {
    return new Promise(function(resolve, reject) {
        if (_aeYoink.active) { reject(new Error('A yoink generation is already in progress')); return; }
        if (ae_genInFlight || ae_lock) { reject(new Error('Editor is busy; try again in a moment')); return; }
        // [v0.9.7.12] Budget floor: < 80 tokens remaining = rate limit imminent.
        // The runtime state generators already guard at 220; 80 is a softer floor for
        // interactive wizard calls so the user gets a clear message, not a raw API error.
        const ygBudget = api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput ? api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() : 9999;
        if (ygBudget < 80) {
            const ygMsg = 'Script generation budget is low. Wait a moment, then try again.';
            api.v1.ui.toast(ygMsg, { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 });
            reject(new Error(ygMsg)); return;
        }
        _aeYoink.active = true;
        _aeYoink.messages = messages;
        _aeYoink.responseText = '';
        _aeYoink.resolve = resolve;
        _aeYoink.reject = reject;
        // [v1.22.1] Cleared here and captured in onContextBuilt, so this attempt only ever
        // accepts hook events belonging to the generation it actually started.
        _aeYoink.continuityId = null;
        diagBudgetSnapshot('YOINK', null);   // [DIAG] pre-read for this yoink; closed in onGenerationEnd
        api.v1.editor.generate().catch(function(ygErr) {
            _aeYoink.active = false;
            _aeYoink.resolve = null;
            _aeYoink.reject = null;
            _aeYoink.continuityId = null;
            // [v0.9.7.12] Surface rate-limit errors as a human-readable toast rather
            // than letting the raw NAI error bubble silently to the log.
            const ygEMsg = String((ygErr && ygErr.message) || ygErr || '');
            if (ygEMsg.toLowerCase().indexOf('limit') >= 0 || ygEMsg.toLowerCase().indexOf('budget') >= 0) {
                api.v1.ui.toast('Generation limit reached. Wait a moment before retrying.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 5000 });
            }
            reject(ygErr);
        });
    });
}

// [v1.21.0 YOINK-HARDEN] Cheap non-Latin/CJK drift heuristic — not a language detector,
// just a signal that generation derailed into another script entirely. Users reported
// the World-Build Wizard occasionally outputting Chinese and/or bullet-pointed junk;
// root cause hypothesis is that executeYoinkGenerationAttempt rides whatever model the
// story's live generation settings currently point at, but NAI's api.v1 docs state
// role-tagged system/user/assistant messages are only currently supported by glm-4-6
// for scripted generation (see api.v1.generate / api.v1.generationParameters docs).
var AE_NONLATIN_DRIFT_RE = /[\u3040-\u30FF\u3400-\u4DBF\u4E00-\u9FFF\uAC00-\uD7AF\uF900-\uFAFF]/;

interface AEYoinkOptions {
    // Optional pass/fail check on the raw response (e.g. "contains NAME:"). A failing
    // check triggers the same one-shot corrective retry as language-drift detection.
    validator?: (raw: string) => boolean;
    // [v2.20.0 GENDIAG] Which generator/feature (and often character) this call belongs
    // to, for diagGenRecord() — see the [DIAG GEN] block above. Defaults to 'untagged'
    // when omitted; every call site in this file passes one.
    tag?: string;
}

// [v1.22.1 YOINK-WATCHDOG] Root cause of the July 2026 "wizard freezes all generation"
// report: NAI's own generation pipeline can wedge (observed as "Encoder Worker Timeout"
// in the browser console) WITHOUT ever calling onGenerationEnd. Every prior layer of
// this system — the _aeYoink.active guard, the v1.21.0 model-pin's try/finally restore,
// even the pre-existing onGenerationEnd catch-block failsafe — only runs in response to
// a NAI hook actually firing. None of them protect against NAI never calling back at
// all. A single stuck generation therefore left BOTH _aeYoink.active AND the pinned
// model (glm-4-6) stuck forever: every later normal story turn silently skipped AE's
// world-state injection (onBeforeContextBuild's `if (_aeYoink.active) return;` guard)
// AND ran on the wrong model, while every later wizard call was rejected outright.
//
// Fix has two independent parts:
//   1. A genuine client-side timeout (AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS), via Promise.race against
//      api.v1.timers.sleep — NOT dependent on any NAI hook ever firing. On timeout we
//      force-recover _aeYoink ourselves rather than waiting for cleanup that may never
//      come.
//   2. Force-recovery must not corrupt a NEWER call that starts after we give up on the
//      old one. onContextBuilt/onResponse/onGenerationEnd all carry NAI's own per-
//      generation `continuityId`; we capture it when a yoink's context is built and
//      every subsequent hook checks it before touching _aeYoink, so a late event from
//      an abandoned generation is recognized as orphaned and ignored instead of
//      resolving (or corrupting the response buffer of) whatever call is active by then.
//      See onContextBuilt / onResponse / onGenerationEnd below.
var AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS = 90000;

// [v1.22.1] The model pin is the prime suspect for triggering the encoder wedge in the
// first place — every yoink call was forcing a model switch (and switch-back) even
// when nothing needed it, and glm-4-6 uses a different tokenizer than most NAI story
// models, making repeated switching a plausible way to destabilize the encoder worker.
// Defaulting OFF until confirmed safe. The watchdog above is needed regardless of this
// setting — NAI's pipeline can wedge independent of any model-switching.
var AE_YOINK_MODEL_PIN_ENABLED = false;

function ygTimeoutRejection(ms: number): Promise<never> {
    return api.v1.timers.sleep(ms).then(function() {
        throw new Error('Yoink generation timed out after ' + Math.round(ms / 1000) + 's (encoder/generation pipeline appears stuck) \u2014 recovered automatically.');
    });
}

// Runs one attempt bounded by AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS. If the timeout wins, the underlying
// attempt is NOT cancelled (NAI's api.v1 exposes no cancellation for editor.generate())
// — it may still be physically running — so we force-recover _aeYoink ourselves here
// rather than leaving that to cleanup code that may never run. Any state the abandoned
// call's own late callbacks would have touched is now guarded by continuityId (see
// hooks below), so this recovery cannot corrupt whatever call runs next.
async function ygAttemptWithWatchdog(msgs: any[], tag?: string): Promise<string> {
    try {
        return await Promise.race([executeYoinkGenerationAttempt(msgs), ygTimeoutRejection(AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS)]);
    } catch (ygTimeoutErr) {
        if (_aeYoink.active) {
            api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] yoink watchdog fired (tag=' + (tag || 'untagged') + ') \u2014 force-recovering stuck generation state:', ygTimeoutErr);
            // [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] Remember this generation's id (if captured) so any late
            // output/end-event is still caught by the onResponse/onGenerationEnd zombie
            // check even after wizard mode has since exited.
            if (_aeYoink.continuityId) _wizZombieIds[_aeYoink.continuityId] = true;
            _aeYoink.active = false;
            _aeYoink.resolve = null;
            _aeYoink.reject = null;
            _aeYoink.continuityId = null;
            // [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] Don't leave a pending mode-exit blocked forever on a
            // generation that may never call back — resolve it now, bounded by this
            // same watchdog timeout.
            if (_wizModeExitPending) { aeCompleteWizardModeExit().catch(function(e) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] mode-exit completion after watchdog failed:', e); }); }
        }
        throw ygTimeoutErr;
    }
}

// [v2.20.0 GENDIAG] Classifies a thrown yoink error as worth one immediate same-messages
// retry. Root cause of the "Generate All From Concept Dump" report: a thrown error on the
// FIRST attempt (observed as AbortError, surfacing from NAI's own generation pipeline via
// api.v1.editor.generate() — see executeYoinkGenerationAttempt) used to skip straight past
// ALL retry logic below and fail the whole call on one transient hiccup. Excluded from
// retry: our OWN deterministic state guards (already-in-progress / editor-busy / budget-
// floor), which will reject identically on an immediate retry, and our own watchdog
// timeout, which already waited AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS once — retrying blind risks doubling
// the wait for a pipeline that may be genuinely wedged. Everything else (AbortError and
// any other error NAI's pipeline can throw) is treated as transient and worth one retry.
function ygIsRetryableError(e: any): boolean {
    let msg = String((e && (e as any).message) || e || '');
    if (msg.indexOf('already in progress') >= 0) return false;
    if (msg.indexOf('Editor is busy') >= 0) return false;
    if (msg.indexOf('budget is low') >= 0) return false;
    if (msg.indexOf('timed out after') >= 0) return false;
    return true;
}

// [v1.21.0 YOINK-HARDEN, v1.22.1 YOINK-WATCHDOG, v2.20.0 GENDIAG] Orchestrates a yoink
// generation with defensive layers on top of executeYoinkGenerationAttempt, added after
// reports of the World-Build Wizard drifting into non-English output and dot-point
// formatting, batch generators silently returning zero parsed items, (v1.22.1) NAI's
// generation pipeline wedging and freezing all further generation, wizard or normal, and
// (v2.20.0) a single transient AbortError failing the whole call outright:
//
//   1. MODEL PIN (best-effort, off by default — see AE_YOINK_MODEL_PIN_ENABLED): when
//      enabled, snapshots api.v1.generationParameters, skips the update entirely if
//      already on glm-4-6 (avoids needless switching churn), pins for the duration of
//      this call, restores the snapshot in finally. The finally is now reliable even on
//      a stuck generation, because ygAttemptWithWatchdog guarantees the await it wraps
//      resolves or rejects within AE_YOINK_TIMEOUT_MS no matter what NAI's backend does.
//   2. LANGUAGE GUARD: appends an English-only instruction to the first system message,
//      once, on top of each call's existing format rules.
//   3. ERROR RETRY (new in v2.20.0): if the FIRST attempt throws (rather than returning
//      bad output), ygIsRetryableError() decides whether it's worth one same-messages
//      retry — see that function's comment. A non-retryable or twice-failing error still
//      propagates to the caller exactly as before; callers' existing catch blocks are
//      unaffected.
//   4. VALIDATE + ONE CORRECTIVE RETRY: once a response comes back (first attempt or the
//      error retry), checks the raw response against AE_NONLATIN_DRIFT_RE and the optional
//      caller-supplied validator. On failure (or an empty response), retries the SAME
//      messages once more (also watchdog-bounded) with a corrective out-of-band line
//      appended, then returns whatever that produced. Callers' existing empty/malformed-
//      output handling is unchanged.
//   5. DIAGNOSTICS (new in v2.20.0): every outcome — success, empty, or a propagated error
//      — is recorded via diagGenRecord() (tag from opts.tag, defaulting to 'untagged') so
//      it shows up in the shared ring buffer alongside the DIRECT path (aeDiagGenerate).
//      See the [DIAG GEN] block above and buildGenDiagContent() (Section 24 area) for the
//      viewer.
async function executeYoinkGeneration(messages: any[], opts?: AEYoinkOptions): Promise<string> {
    let ygTag = (opts && opts.tag) || 'untagged';
    let ygT0 = Date.now();
    let ygB0o = diagBudO(), ygB0i = diagBudI();
    let ygMessages = messages.slice();
    if (ygMessages.length > 0 && ygMessages[0] && ygMessages[0].role === 'system') {
        ygMessages[0] = { role: 'system', content: String(ygMessages[0].content || '') + '\n\nRespond in English only, regardless of any other language present in the reference context.' };
    }

    let ygPrior: any = null;
    if (AE_YOINK_MODEL_PIN_ENABLED) {
        try { ygPrior = await api.v1.generationParameters.get(); }
        catch (ygGetErr) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] generationParameters.get failed, skipping model pin:', ygGetErr); }

        if (ygPrior && ygPrior.model !== 'glm-4-6') {
            try { await api.v1.generationParameters.update({ model: 'glm-4-6' }); }
            catch (ygPinErr) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] glm-4-6 pin failed, continuing on current model:', ygPinErr); ygPrior = null; }
        } else if (ygPrior) {
            ygPrior = null;   // already on glm-4-6 — nothing to restore, nothing to skip past.
        }
    }

    try {
        let ygRaw = '';
        let ygErrorRetried = false;
        try {
            ygRaw = await ygAttemptWithWatchdog(ygMessages, ygTag);
        } catch (ygFirstErr) {
            if (!ygIsRetryableError(ygFirstErr)) {
                diagGenRecord({ tag: ygTag, method: 'yoink', startedAt: ygT0, ms: Date.now() - ygT0,
                    outcome: 'error', errorName: String((ygFirstErr && (ygFirstErr as any).name) || 'Error'),
                    errorMessage: String((ygFirstErr && (ygFirstErr as any).message) || ygFirstErr),
                    retried: false, driftDetected: false, validatorFailed: false, outLen: 0,
                    budgetOut: ygB0o, budgetIn: ygB0i });
                throw ygFirstErr;
            }
            aeLog('[AE:wizard] yoink attempt failed (' + String((ygFirstErr && (ygFirstErr as any).name) || 'Error') + ': ' + String((ygFirstErr && (ygFirstErr as any).message) || ygFirstErr) + ') \u2014 retrying once (tag=' + ygTag + ')');
            ygErrorRetried = true;
            try {
                ygRaw = await ygAttemptWithWatchdog(ygMessages, ygTag);
            } catch (ygSecondErr) {
                diagGenRecord({ tag: ygTag, method: 'yoink', startedAt: ygT0, ms: Date.now() - ygT0,
                    outcome: 'error', errorName: String((ygSecondErr && (ygSecondErr as any).name) || 'Error'),
                    errorMessage: String((ygSecondErr && (ygSecondErr as any).message) || ygSecondErr),
                    retried: true, driftDetected: false, validatorFailed: false, outLen: 0,
                    budgetOut: ygB0o, budgetIn: ygB0i });
                throw ygSecondErr;
            }
        }

        let ygEmpty = !ygRaw || !ygRaw.trim();
        let ygDrift = !ygEmpty && AE_NONLATIN_DRIFT_RE.test(ygRaw);
        let ygInvalid = !ygEmpty && !!(opts && opts.validator) && !opts.validator(ygRaw);
        let ygCorrectiveRetried = false;
        if (ygEmpty || ygDrift || ygInvalid) {
            ygCorrectiveRetried = true;
            let ygRetryMessages = ygMessages.slice();
            ygRetryMessages.push({ role: 'user', content: '[Your previous response broke format or used non-English text. Respond in English only, in plain prose, with no bullet points, lists, or markdown. Follow the FORMAT above exactly.]' });
            try {
                let ygRetryRaw = await ygAttemptWithWatchdog(ygRetryMessages, ygTag);
                if (ygRetryRaw && ygRetryRaw.trim()) ygRaw = ygRetryRaw;
            } catch (ygRetryErr) {
                aeLog('[AE:wizard] corrective retry failed, keeping first-attempt output:', ygRetryErr);
            }
        }
        diagGenRecord({ tag: ygTag, method: 'yoink', startedAt: ygT0, ms: Date.now() - ygT0,
            outcome: (ygRaw && ygRaw.trim()) ? 'ok' : 'empty', errorName: '', errorMessage: '',
            retried: ygErrorRetried || ygCorrectiveRetried, driftDetected: ygDrift, validatorFailed: ygInvalid,
            outLen: (ygRaw || '').length, budgetOut: ygB0o, budgetIn: ygB0i });
        return ygRaw;
    } finally {
        if (ygPrior) {
            let ygRestore: any = { model: ygPrior.model };
            if (ygPrior.max_tokens !== undefined) ygRestore.max_tokens = ygPrior.max_tokens;
            if (ygPrior.temperature !== undefined) ygRestore.temperature = ygPrior.temperature;
            if (ygPrior.top_p !== undefined) ygRestore.top_p = ygPrior.top_p;
            if (ygPrior.top_k !== undefined) ygRestore.top_k = ygPrior.top_k;
            if (ygPrior.frequency_penalty !== undefined) ygRestore.frequency_penalty = ygPrior.frequency_penalty;
            if (ygPrior.presence_penalty !== undefined) ygRestore.presence_penalty = ygPrior.presence_penalty;
            if (ygPrior.min_p !== undefined) ygRestore.min_p = ygPrior.min_p;
            try { await api.v1.generationParameters.update(ygRestore); }
            catch (ygRestoreErr) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] failed to restore generation params after yoink pin:', ygRestoreErr); }
        }
    }
}
var ae_sbcInFlight = false;  // [v0.9.36] a scene-state broadcast is running
var ae_sbcPending  = false;  // [v0.9.36] another scene-state broadcast was requested while one ran
var ae_cdbInFlight = false;  // [v0.9.36] a character-data broadcast is running
var ae_cdbPending  = false;  // [v0.9.36] another character-data broadcast was requested while one ran
var ae_pendingGenerate = false; // [v0.9.37] a move effect requested generation; the top-level handler fires it once after ALL effects

// ── Bridge Communication State ────────────────────────────────────────────────
// isBridgeReady: set true after first successful EVT_HANDSHAKE_ACK.
// engineScriptId is not stored here (this IS the engine); the bridge stores it.
var isBridgeReady = false;

// Debounce timers for broadcast functions (Section 18).
// Both functions are called frequently (on every save); debouncing prevents storms.
var _sceneBroadcastTimeout: any = null;
var _charDataBroadcastTimeout: any = null;

// ── Character Visual Cache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// [CHANGE] Renamed from cachedCharConfigs (bridge roster data) to cachedCharVisuals
// (engine character visual fields). Now populated TWO ways:
//   1. On init: seeded from storyStorage so the HUD has data before the bridge connects.
//   2. On EVT_CHAR_SYNC_PUSH: bridge can push overrides (kept for legacy compat).
// getCharacterData() reads from this cache. Bridge reads it via EVT_CHAR_DATA_SYNC.
var cachedCharVisuals: CharacterVisualRecord[] = [];

// ── Image Settings State ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Loaded from api.v1.storage (global, not story-bound) by loadImageSettings().
var imagePromptFields: ImagePromptField[] = [];
var globalSettings: GlobalImageSettings = {
    autoDownload: false,
    contextSize: DEFAULT_CTX,
    architectPrompt: DEFAULT_ARCH,
    architectAutoSnap: true
};

// ── NAI Sidebar Modal Handles ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Handles for the NAI-side modal windows (GM authoring tools).
// Each is set when a modal opens and nulled when it closes.
var charEditModalHandle: any   = null;   // character editor modal
var configModalHandle: any     = null;   // global config/settings modal
var entityEditModalHandle: any = null;   // entity/location editor modal
var editingCharId: string | null   = null;  // ID of the character currently open in charEditModal
var editingEntityId: string | null = null;  // ID of the entity currently open in entityEditModal

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 4 — UTILITY FUNCTIONS
// Pure helpers and factory functions with no side effects.
// These are called throughout the script and must be defined before first use.
// ============================================================================


// ── ID Generation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function generateId(): string {
    // Generates a collision-resistant ID from timestamp + random suffix.
    // Used for all new records (characters, entities, rules, milestones, etc.).
    return Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).substring(2);
}

// [v1.6.3 ICON-LIB] The entity `icon` field is free text shared by the engine and the bridge.
// The bridge can resolve a bare library name (e.g. "crown") to an SVG glyph; the engine's
// native labels can't, and would render the literal word ("crown Cave"). aeIconPfx returns a
// label prefix ONLY for glyph icons (emoji / non-ASCII symbols), suppressing bare ASCII
// library-name icons so native labels stay clean while the bridge shows the SVG. Returns the
// icon plus a trailing space, or '' when there is no glyph to show.
function aeIconPfx(icon?: string): string {
    if (!icon) return '';
    let s = String(icon).trim();
    if (!s) return '';
    // A library name is plain ASCII word characters (letters/digits/underscore/hyphen). Any
    // codepoint outside ASCII (emoji, arrows, symbols) marks a real glyph and is kept.
    let isBareName = /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/.test(s);
    return isBareName ? '' : (s + ' ');
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: assemble + commit the Memory block ───────────────────
// [vNEXT] Assembles enabled, non-blank, memory-targeted sections into the static
// Memory block ("[ Label ]\n<content>", blank-separated) and returns it. Style and
// any other 'an'-targeted sections are NOT included here — they commit separately.
function assembleWizardMemory(sections: WizardMemorySection[]): string {
    let blocks: string[] = [];
    for (let s of (sections || [])) {
        if (!s || s.target !== 'memory') continue;
        if (!s.enabled) continue;
        let body = (s.content || '').trim();
        if (!body) continue;
        blocks.push('[ ' + s.label + ' ]\n' + body);
    }
    return blocks.join('\n\n');
}

// [vNEXT] Assembles 'an'-targeted sections (e.g. Style) into an Author's Note body.
function assembleWizardAuthorNote(sections: WizardMemorySection[]): string {
    let blocks: string[] = [];
    for (let s of (sections || [])) {
        if (!s || s.target !== 'an') continue;
        if (!s.enabled) continue;
        let body = (s.content || '').trim();
        if (!body) continue;
        // [v2.19.0 WIZARD-2.0] If the body is already a complete bracketed AN block
        // (e.g. a generated ATTG line "[ Author: ...; Genre: ... ]"), emit it verbatim;
        // otherwise wrap it as a labelled "[ Label: body ]" block (the Style default).
        if (body.charAt(0) === '[' && body.charAt(body.length - 1) === ']') {
            blocks.push(body);
        } else {
            blocks.push('[ ' + s.label + ': ' + body + ' ]');
        }
    }
    return blocks.join('\n\n');
}

// [v0.9.7.3 AN-FIX] Commits the assembled wizard sections to the real NAI surfaces.
// Memory sections OVERWRITE the Memory field (static world canon — stable, written once).
// AN sections (Style, etc.) are written into a NAMED REGION of the Author's Note using
// AE_WIZ_AN_SENTINEL so they coexist with the scene-state block injected at generation
// time by onBeforeContextBuild. The scene block is NEVER stored in the persisted AN —
// it is appended ephemerally per-generation. The wizard region always sits FIRST in the
// persisted AN; the scene block is appended AFTER it at generation time so it has the
// highest recency weight in the AN slot.
async function commitWizardMemory(cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<boolean> {
    try {
        let mem = assembleWizardMemory(cfg.wizardSections || []);
        await api.v1.memory.set(mem);

        let wizAN = assembleWizardAuthorNote(cfg.wizardSections || []);

        // Read the current persisted AN and strip both managed regions before rewriting.
        // Safe to call repeatedly (idempotent). Strip scene sentinel first (defensive),
        // then strip any prior wizard region.
        let existingAN = '';
        try { existingAN = (await api.v1.an.get()) || ''; } catch (_eRead) {}

        // Strip stale scene block (defensive — should never be persisted).
        let sceneMarker = '\n\n' + AE_AN_SENTINEL;
        let sceneIdx = existingAN.lastIndexOf(sceneMarker);
        if (sceneIdx >= 0) {
            existingAN = existingAN.substring(0, sceneIdx);
        } else if (existingAN.indexOf(AE_AN_SENTINEL) === 0) {
            existingAN = '';
        }
        existingAN = existingAN.replace(/\s+$/, '');

        // Strip any prior wizard region.
        let wizMarker = '\n\n' + AE_WIZ_AN_SENTINEL;
        let wizIdx = existingAN.lastIndexOf(wizMarker);
        if (wizIdx >= 0) {
            existingAN = existingAN.substring(0, wizIdx);
        } else if (existingAN.indexOf(AE_WIZ_AN_SENTINEL) === 0) {
            existingAN = '';
        }
        existingAN = existingAN.replace(/\s+$/, '');

        // Re-assemble: any non-AE base content + wizard region (if any AN sections enabled).
        let finalAN: string;
        if (wizAN.trim()) {
            let wizRegion = AE_WIZ_AN_SENTINEL + '\n' + wizAN.trim();
            finalAN = existingAN ? (existingAN + '\n\n' + wizRegion) : wizRegion;
        } else {
            finalAN = existingAN;
        }

        await api.v1.an.set(finalAN);
        return true;
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] commitWizardMemory error:', e);
        return false;
    }
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: context assembly ─────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] Builds the read-only context preamble for a wizard chat from its selected
// sources: project concept fields, other wizard chats, lorebook entries, and recent
// story text. Each source is clearly delimited so the model treats it as reference.
// ── World-Build Wizard: token budget + transcript distillation (v1.23.0) ─────
// Until now buildWizardContext concatenated every linked chat's FULL raw transcript with
// no cap of any kind. That is invisible on a 28k-context account and fatal on a 12k one:
// a few long brainstorm chats linked as context sources can exceed the whole window, and
// NAI's handling of an oversized messages[] override is undocumented. Since almost every
// yoink call is [system, user] with ALL formatting rules living in the system message,
// losing the front of that array would strip the rules and produce exactly the
// ungoverned, malformed output this subsystem has been chasing.
//
// Two defences, applied together:
//   1. DISTILL — a linked chat contributes settled facts, not its deliberation. Cached on
//      the chat record; regenerated only when the chat grows.
//   2. CAP — measure the assembled blocks with the real tokenizer against the account's
//      real ceiling (api.v1.maxTokens is subscription-tier aware, so no tier is hardcoded)
//      and drop whole low-priority blocks until it fits.

// Reserve for the persona + format rules that ride in the system message alongside ctx.
var AE_CTX_RESERVE_INSTRUCTIONS = 2000;
// Reserve for the model's own output.
var AE_CTX_RESERVE_OUTPUT = 1200;
// Never hand back a budget smaller than this; below it, context is useless anyway.
var AE_CTX_MIN_BUDGET = 800;
// Chats shorter than this stay raw: distilling 3 messages costs a generation and saves
// nothing, and a short exchange is already close to "just the facts".
var AE_DISTILL_MIN_MESSAGES = 6;

// Which model's tokenizer/ceiling applies. editor.generate() runs on the story's live
// model, so ask generationParameters rather than assuming.
async function wizActiveModel(): Promise<string> {
    try {
        let gp = await api.v1.generationParameters.get();
        if (gp && gp.model) return String(gp.model);
    } catch (eModel) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] could not read active model, assuming glm-4-6:', eModel); }
    return 'glm-4-6';
}

// Real token count, with a coarse chars/4 fallback if the tokenizer is unavailable.
async function wizCountTokens(text: string, model: string): Promise<number> {
    let t = String(text || '');
    if (!t) return 0;
    try {
        let ids = await api.v1.tokenizer.encode(t, model);
        if (ids && typeof ids.length === 'number') return ids.length;
    } catch (eTok) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] tokenizer.encode failed, estimating:', eTok); }
    return Math.ceil(t.length / 4);
}

// Context tokens available for reference material, after instructions, output, and (for a
// live chat turn) the transcript that will be replayed alongside it.
async function wizContextBudget(model: string, ownMessagesTokens: number): Promise<number> {
    let ceiling = 0;
    try { ceiling = await api.v1.maxTokens(model); }
    catch (eMax) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] maxTokens unavailable, skipping context cap:', eMax); return 0; }
    if (!ceiling || ceiling <= 0) return 0;
    let budget = ceiling - AE_CTX_RESERVE_INSTRUCTIONS - AE_CTX_RESERVE_OUTPUT - (ownMessagesTokens || 0);
    if (budget < AE_CTX_MIN_BUDGET) budget = AE_CTX_MIN_BUDGET;
    return budget;
}

// A context block with a drop priority. Higher survives longer.
interface WizCtxBlock {
    text: string;
    priority: number;
    label: string;
}

// [v1.23.0] Trim whole blocks (never mid-block) until the total fits the budget. Drops
// the LOWEST priority first, and among equal priorities the LAST added first, so the
// project header and the earliest-linked chats survive longest. A budget of 0 means the
// ceiling could not be determined — in that case nothing is trimmed, matching pre-v1.23.0
// behaviour rather than guessing.
async function wizTrimBlocksToBudget(blocks: WizCtxBlock[], budget: number, model: string): Promise<{ kept: WizCtxBlock[]; dropped: string[]; tokens: number }> {
    let kept = blocks.slice();
    let dropped: string[] = [];
    if (!budget || budget <= 0) {
        let rawTotal = 0;
        for (let b of kept) rawTotal += await wizCountTokens(b.text, model);
        return { kept: kept, dropped: dropped, tokens: rawTotal };
    }

    let counts: number[] = [];
    for (let b of kept) counts.push(await wizCountTokens(b.text, model));
    let total = 0;
    for (let n of counts) total += n;

    while (total > budget && kept.length > 0) {
        // Find the drop candidate: lowest priority, latest index among ties.
        let victim = -1;
        for (let i = 0; i < kept.length; i++) {
            if (victim < 0) { victim = i; continue; }
            if (kept[i].priority < kept[victim].priority) victim = i;
            else if (kept[i].priority === kept[victim].priority) victim = i;
        }
        if (victim < 0) break;
        // The project header is priority 100 and must never be the last thing standing
        // that gets dropped; if only it remains, stop and accept the overage.
        if (kept.length === 1 || kept[victim].priority >= 100) break;
        total -= counts[victim];
        dropped.push(kept[victim].label);
        kept.splice(victim, 1);
        counts.splice(victim, 1);
    }

    return { kept: kept, dropped: dropped, tokens: total };
}

// [v1.23.0 DISTILL] Turn a chat transcript into declarative world facts. Reframed rather
// than merely constrained: the model is recording current world state, not summarizing a
// conversation. The contrastive pair targets SURFACE FORM (how a sentence is phrased),
// which is safe to name explicitly — unlike naming unwanted story CONCEPTS, which risks
// priming them. Abandoned ideas are simply absent from the output.
var AE_DISTILL_SYSTEM = [
    'You are recording the current state of a story world as established so far. You are not summarizing a conversation.',
    'Write only settled facts, in the present tense, as plain declarative prose. State each fact as though it has always been true of this world.',
    'Where a point was revised, record only the version that stands now.',
    'WRONG: "After some back-and-forth, they settled on a near-future dystopia."',
    'RIGHT: "The setting is a near-future dystopia where the water supply is privately owned."',
    'WRONG: "One idea raised was a rival guild, though it was set aside."',
    'RIGHT: (omit it entirely)',
    'Preserve every proper name, number, and concrete detail that still stands. Write in English. Use plain prose with no markdown, bullet points, headers, or numbering.',
    'Output only the world facts. No preamble, no commentary.'
].join('\n\n');

// Distill one chat, caching on the record. Returns notes text, or '' if distillation is
// unavailable — callers fall back to the raw transcript rather than losing the context.
// NOTE: this itself runs a yoink generation, so it must only ever be called BEFORE the
// caller starts its own yoink (buildWizardContext always runs first — see callers).
// allowGenerate=false returns only an existing cache and never generates, which is what
// the read-only context preview needs: pressing Preview must not spend a generation.
async function getWizardChatNotes(chat: WizardChat, allowGenerate: boolean): Promise<string> {
    if (!chat || !chat.messages || !chat.messages.length) return '';
    let msgCount = chat.messages.length;
    if (msgCount < AE_DISTILL_MIN_MESSAGES) return '';   // short chat: caller uses raw

    let cache = chat.notesCache;
    if (cache && cache.sourceMsgCount === msgCount && (cache.text || '').trim()) return cache.text;
    if (!allowGenerate) return '';   // stale or absent cache, and we may not generate

    let transcript = chat.messages.map(function(m) { return '[' + m.role.toUpperCase() + '] ' + m.content; }).join('\n');
    let notes = '';
    try {
        notes = await executeYoinkGeneration([
            { role: 'system', content: AE_DISTILL_SYSTEM },
            { role: 'user', content: transcript + '\n\nRecord the world facts established above.' }
        ], { tag: 'wizChatDistill:' + (chat.title || chat.id) });
    } catch (eDistill) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] distillation failed for chat "' + chat.title + '", falling back to raw transcript:', eDistill);
        return '';
    }
    notes = (notes || '').trim();
    if (!notes) return '';

    let fresh = await loadWizardChat(chat.id);
    if (fresh) {
        fresh.notesCache = { text: notes, sourceMsgCount: msgCount, updatedAt: Date.now() };
        await saveWizardChat(fresh);
    }
    return notes;
}

// [v1.23.0] allowDistill=false makes this a pure read (no generation), for the preview.
// The returned string is the same one the generator will receive.
async function buildWizardContext(chat: WizardChat, cfg: GlobalConfig, allowDistill?: boolean): Promise<string> {
    let res = await buildWizardContextDetailed(chat, cfg, allowDistill !== false);
    return res.text;
}

// [v1.23.0] Assembles the context as priority-tagged blocks, distills linked chats, then
// trims to the account's real token ceiling. Returns the joined text plus what was cut,
// so the preview can show it and the user can see WHY something is missing.
async function buildWizardContextDetailed(chat: WizardChat, cfg: GlobalConfig, allowDistill: boolean): Promise<{ text: string; dropped: string[]; tokens: number; budget: number; model: string }> {
    let blocks: WizCtxBlock[] = [];

    let pName = (cfg.projectName || '').trim();
    let pGenre = (cfg.projectGenre || '').trim();
    let pConcept = (cfg.projectConcept || '').trim();
    if (pName || pGenre || pConcept) {
        let head: string[] = [];
        if (pName)    head.push('Project: ' + pName);
        if (pGenre)   head.push('Genre: ' + pGenre);
        if (pConcept) head.push('Concept: ' + pConcept);
        blocks.push({ text: '--- PROJECT ---\n' + head.join('\n'), priority: 100, label: 'Project header' });
    }

    // [v1.23.0] Linked chats contribute their SETTLED FACTS, not their deliberation.
    // Falling back to the raw transcript when distillation is unavailable keeps the old
    // behaviour rather than silently dropping the chat entirely.
    let ctxIds = chat.contextChatIds || [];
    if (ctxIds.length > 0) {
        let allChats = await loadWizardChats();
        for (let cid of ctxIds) {
            let oc = allChats.find(function(c) { return c.id === cid; });
            if (!oc || !oc.messages || oc.messages.length === 0) continue;
            let notes = await getWizardChatNotes(oc, allowDistill);
            if (notes) {
                blocks.push({ text: '--- WORLD FACTS FROM: ' + oc.title + ' ---\n' + notes, priority: 80, label: 'Chat notes: ' + oc.title });
            } else {
                let transcript = oc.messages.map(function(m) { return '[' + m.role.toUpperCase() + '] ' + m.content; }).join('\n');
                blocks.push({ text: '--- CHAT: ' + oc.title + ' ---\n' + transcript, priority: 80, label: 'Chat transcript: ' + oc.title });
            }
        }
    }

    let loreIds = chat.lorebookEntryIds || [];
    for (let lid of loreIds) {
        try {
            let entry = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(lid);
            if (entry && entry.text && entry.text.trim()) {
                let lname = (entry as any).displayName || (entry as any).name || 'Lorebook Entry';
                blocks.push({ text: '--- LOREBOOK: ' + lname + ' ---\n' + entry.text.trim(), priority: 50, label: 'Lorebook: ' + lname });
            }
        } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] lorebook context read failed for ' + lid); }
    }

    // [v0.9.99] Per-chat character context — the chat decides, independent of each
    // character's global charContextEnabled flag. Formatted straight from the record.
    let charCtxIds = chat.characterIds || [];
    for (let chCid of charCtxIds) {
        try {
            let cc = await loadCharacter(chCid);
            if (!cc) continue;
            let ccLines: string[] = [];
            if (cc.briefDescription) ccLines.push(cc.briefDescription);
            if (cc.species || cc.age || cc.gender || cc.occupationRole) ccLines.push([cc.species, cc.age, cc.gender, cc.occupationRole].filter(Boolean).join(', '));
            if (cc.personalityCore) ccLines.push('Personality: ' + cc.personalityCore);
            if (cc.background) ccLines.push('Background: ' + cc.background);
            if (ccLines.length) blocks.push({ text: '--- CHARACTER: ' + cc.name + ' ---\n' + ccLines.join('\n'), priority: 60, label: 'Character: ' + cc.name });
        } catch (eCc) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] character context read failed for ' + chCid); }
    }

    if (chat.includeStory) {
        let story = await getRecentStoryText(2000);
        if (story.trim()) blocks.push({ text: '--- RECENT STORY ---\n' + story.trim(), priority: 10, label: 'Recent story' });
    }

    // Cap against the account's REAL ceiling. maxTokens() is subscription-tier aware, so a
    // 12k Scroll account trims where a 28k account does not, with no tier hardcoded here.
    let model = await wizActiveModel();
    // One encode call, not one per message: this is only a reserve, and the transcript is
    // trimmed properly (per-message) by runWizardChatTurn.
    let ownJoined = (chat.messages || []).map(function(m) { return m.content; }).join('\n');
    let ownTokens = ownJoined ? await wizCountTokens(ownJoined, model) : 0;
    let budget = await wizContextBudget(model, ownTokens);
    let trimmed = await wizTrimBlocksToBudget(blocks, budget, model);

    if (trimmed.dropped.length) {
        aeLog('[AE:wizard] context over budget (' + budget + ' tok); dropped: ' + trimmed.dropped.join(', '));
    }

    return {
        text: trimmed.kept.map(function(b) { return b.text; }).join('\n\n'),
        dropped: trimmed.dropped,
        tokens: trimmed.tokens,
        budget: budget,
        model: model
    };
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: chat turn (yoink) ────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] Appends the user's message, runs a budget-free yoink generation against
// the topic persona + assembled context + transcript, appends the assistant reply,
// and persists the chat. Returns the assistant text (or null on failure).
async function runWizardChatTurn(chatId: string, userText: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let chat = await loadWizardChat(chatId);
    if (!chat) return null;
    // [DIAG] Budget diagnostics via typed commands — engine-only. /budgetlog spends no budget.
    let _diagCmd = (userText || '').trim().toLowerCase();
    if (AE_DIAG_BUDGET && (_diagCmd === '/budgetprobe' || _diagCmd === '/budgetlog')) {
        let _diagOut = _diagCmd === '/budgetprobe' ? await runBudgetProbe() : dumpBudgetLog();
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'user', content: userText.trim() });
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'assistant', content: _diagOut });
        await saveWizardChat(chat);
        return _diagOut;
    }
    // [v2.20.0 GENDIAG] /gendiag dumps the shared yoink+direct generation-call log (see
    // the [DIAG GEN] block near the top of the file); /gendiagclear resets it. Spends no
    // budget — pure in-memory ring buffer read/reset, same convention as /budgetlog.
    if (AE_DIAG_GEN && (_diagCmd === '/gendiag' || _diagCmd === '/gendiagclear')) {
        let _genDiagOut = _diagCmd === '/gendiagclear' ? (clearGenDiagLog(), 'Generation diagnostics log cleared.') : dumpGenDiagLog();
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'user', content: userText.trim() });
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'assistant', content: _genDiagOut });
        await saveWizardChat(chat);
        return _genDiagOut;
    }
    // [DIAG v2.66.0 DIAG-CMD-SHARED] /log and /diag now live in aeHandleDiagCommand
    // (SECTION 3) so they're reachable from BOTH this modal wizard chat AND the new
    // AE_DIAG_CMD bridge message (SECTION 18) — see that function's header comment
    // for why a chat-turn-only command surface was the wrong call.
    let _sharedDiagOut = await aeHandleDiagCommand(userText.trim(), _diagCmd);
    if (_sharedDiagOut !== null) {
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'user', content: userText.trim() });
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'assistant', content: _sharedDiagOut });
        await saveWizardChat(chat);
        return _sharedDiagOut;
    }
    let topic = getWizardTopic(cfg, chat.topicId);
    let persona = topic ? topic.instructions : 'You are a helpful worldbuilding assistant. Keep replies short and conversational.';

    if (userText && userText.trim()) {
        chat.messages.push({ role: 'user', content: userText.trim() });
    }

    // [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] While mode is on, assemble the source context ONCE per chat and
    // reuse it — only the transcript (appended below) grows per turn. Recomputed fresh
    // the first time a chat is used after mode turns on; cleared entirely on mode exit
    // and whenever this chat's context selection changes (AE_WIZ_SET_CHAT_CONTEXT).
    let ctx: string;
    if (_aeWizardMode && _wizFrozenCtx[chatId] !== undefined) {
        ctx = _wizFrozenCtx[chatId];
    } else {
        ctx = await buildWizardContext(chat, cfg);
        if (_aeWizardMode) _wizFrozenCtx[chatId] = ctx;
    }
    let sysParts: string[] = [persona];
    if (ctx.trim()) {
        sysParts.push('The following is READ-ONLY reference context. Use it to inform your replies, but do not quote it back verbatim.\n\n' + ctx);
    }

    let msgs: any[] = [{ role: 'system', content: sysParts.join('\n\n') }];

    // [v1.23.0] The transcript is replayed in full on every turn and grows without bound.
    // On a 12k-context account a long chat can push the system message (which carries the
    // topic persona and all formatting rules) out of the window. Keep the seed message —
    // it frames the whole topic — plus as many of the MOST RECENT turns as fit, dropping
    // from the middle. buildWizardContext has already trimmed ctx against the same ceiling.
    let turnModel = await wizActiveModel();
    let ctxTokens = await wizCountTokens(sysParts.join('\n\n'), turnModel);
    let turnCeiling = 0;
    try { turnCeiling = await api.v1.maxTokens(turnModel); }
    catch (eTurnMax) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] maxTokens unavailable, replaying full transcript:', eTurnMax); }

    let history = chat.messages.slice();
    if (turnCeiling > 0) {
        let transcriptBudget = turnCeiling - ctxTokens - AE_CTX_RESERVE_OUTPUT;
        if (transcriptBudget < AE_CTX_MIN_BUDGET) transcriptBudget = AE_CTX_MIN_BUDGET;

        // Cheap chars/4 estimate first. Only pay for per-message tokenization when we are
        // plausibly near the ceiling, so a normal-length chat costs zero extra encode calls.
        let roughChars = 0;
        for (let rm of history) roughChars += (rm.content || '').length;
        let roughTokens = Math.ceil(roughChars / 4);

        if (roughTokens > transcriptBudget * 0.8) {
            let seed = (history.length > 0 && history[0].role === 'assistant') ? history[0] : null;
            let rest = seed ? history.slice(1) : history.slice();
            let seedTokens = seed ? await wizCountTokens(seed.content, turnModel) : 0;

            let keptRev: WizardChatMessage[] = [];
            let used = seedTokens;
            for (let i = rest.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
                let mt = await wizCountTokens(rest[i].content, turnModel);
                if (used + mt > transcriptBudget && keptRev.length > 0) break;
                used += mt;
                keptRev.push(rest[i]);
            }
            keptRev.reverse();
            let trimmedCount = rest.length - keptRev.length;
            history = seed ? [seed].concat(keptRev) : keptRev;
            if (trimmedCount > 0) aeLog('[AE:wizard] transcript over budget; dropped ' + trimmedCount + ' older message(s) from the middle.');
        }
    }

    for (let m of history) {
        msgs.push({ role: m.role, content: m.content });
    }

    let reply = '';
    try {
        reply = await executeYoinkGeneration(msgs, { tag: 'wizChatTurn:' + (chat.title || chatId) });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] chat turn generation failed:', e);
        return null;
    }
    reply = (reply || '').trim();
    if (!reply) return null;

    chat.messages.push({ role: 'assistant', content: reply });
    await saveWizardChat(chat);
    return reply;
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: per-section generation (yoink) ───────────────────────
// [vNEXT] Generates ONE memory section's content from a chat transcript, scoped hard
// to that section's instructions so sections never bleed into each other. Already-
// written sections are passed as read-only context to avoid overlap. Returns the
