// generated text (or null on failure).
async function generateWizardSection(section: WizardMemorySection, ctx: string, cfg: GlobalConfig, priorSections: WizardMemorySection[], hint?: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let sys = [
        'You are writing the "' + section.label + '" section of a story\'s static world canon.',
        section.instructions,
        'CONSTRAINT: Write ONLY the content of this section. Do not restate this section\'s name as a heading before the content. Do not write any other section\'s content. Be concise and concrete.',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown, bolding, italics, or bullet points. Output plain text only.'
    ].join('\n\n');

    let priorLines: string[] = [];
    for (let ps of (priorSections || [])) {
        if (ps.id === section.id) continue;
        let body = (ps.content || '').trim();
        if (body) priorLines.push('[' + ps.label + '] ' + body);
    }

    let usrParts: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) usrParts.push(ctx);
    if (priorLines.length > 0) usrParts.push('--- ALREADY-WRITTEN SECTIONS (do not repeat these; stay in your own scope) ---\n' + priorLines.join('\n'));
    if (hint && hint.trim()) usrParts.push('--- EXTRA GUIDANCE FOR THIS REGENERATION ---\n' + hint.trim());
    usrParts.push('Now write the "' + section.label + '" section.');

    let msgs: any[] = [
        { role: 'system', content: sys },
        { role: 'user', content: usrParts.join('\n\n') }
    ];

    let result = '';
    try {
        result = await executeYoinkGeneration(msgs, { tag: 'wizSection:' + section.label });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] section generation failed (' + section.label + '):', e);
        return null;
    }
    return (result || '').trim() || null;
}

// [v2.11.0] Regenerate ONE section in place (does not commit memory). sourceChatIds
// mirrors the multi-source context model every other generator tab uses (Species/
// Factions/etc via buildWizardMultiContext) — was single-chat-only before, which also
// meant the bridge's AE_WIZ_REGEN_ENTITY handler was silently truncating its own
// already-array-shaped sourceChatIds payload down to just the first id. Writes the
// result into cfg.wizardSections.
async function regenerateWizardSection(sectionId: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let section = (cfg.wizardSections || []).find(function(s) { return s.id === sectionId; });
    if (!section) return null;
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds || [], cfg);
    let prior = cfg.wizardSections || [];
    let gen = await generateWizardSection(section, ctx, cfg, prior, hint);
    if (!gen) return null;
    await mutConfig(function(c) {
        let target = (c.wizardSections || []).find(function(s) { return s.id === sectionId; });
        if (target) target.content = gen;
    });
    return gen;
}

// [v0.9.81] Build wizard context from MULTIPLE source chats (multi-select). Constructs a
// synthetic chat whose contextChatIds are the selected chats, so buildWizardContext pulls
// each selected chat's transcript (as a "--- CHAT: title ---" block) plus the project header.
// includeStory is on if ANY selected chat has it; selected chats' lorebook links are unioned.
// [v1.23.0] allowDistill=false makes this a pure read (used by the context preview).
async function buildWizardMultiContext(chatIds: string[], cfg: GlobalConfig, allowDistill?: boolean): Promise<string> {
    let res = await buildWizardMultiContextDetailed(chatIds, cfg, allowDistill !== false);
    return res.text;
}

async function buildWizardMultiContextDetailed(chatIds: string[], cfg: GlobalConfig, allowDistill: boolean): Promise<{ text: string; dropped: string[]; tokens: number; budget: number; model: string }> {
    let selIds = (chatIds || []).filter(Boolean);
    let allMultiChats = await loadWizardChats();
    let anyMultiStory = false;
    let unionLore: string[] = [];
    // [v2.6.0] Ids that resolve to NEITHER a classic chat NOR (checked below) fall through
    // to a direct lorebook-entry read — this is how a CE Chats entry (or any other saved
    // lorebook source a generator tab lets the user check) becomes generator context with
    // no owning classic-chat record required.
    let directLoreBlocks: WizCtxBlock[] = [];
    for (let sid of selIds) {
        let sc = allMultiChats.find(function(c) { return c.id === sid; });
        if (sc) {
            if (sc.includeStory) anyMultiStory = true;
            for (let lid of (sc.lorebookEntryIds || [])) { if (unionLore.indexOf(lid) < 0) unionLore.push(lid); }
            continue;
        }
        try {
            let le = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(sid);
            if (le && le.text && le.text.trim()) {
                let leName = (le as any).displayName || 'Saved Chat';
                directLoreBlocks.push({ text: '--- SAVED CHAT: ' + leName + ' ---\n' + le.text.trim(), priority: 80, label: 'Saved chat: ' + leName });
            }
        } catch (eLoreDirect) { aeLog('[AE:wizard] direct lorebook source read failed for ' + sid); }
    }
    let classicIds = selIds.filter(function(id) { return allMultiChats.some(function(c) { return c.id === id; }); });
    let synthChat: WizardChat = { id: 'transient', topicId: '', title: '', messages: [], contextChatIds: classicIds, includeStory: anyMultiStory, lorebookEntryIds: unionLore, createdAt: 0, updatedAt: 0 };
    let base = await buildWizardContextDetailed(synthChat, cfg, allowDistill);
    if (directLoreBlocks.length === 0) return base;
    // Splice the directly-selected lorebook sources in alongside the already-assembled
    // (already-budgeted) base text, then re-trim the combination to the same ceiling.
    let combined: WizCtxBlock[] = directLoreBlocks.concat([{ text: base.text, priority: 70, label: 'Chat/project context' }]);
    let trimmed2 = await wizTrimBlocksToBudget(combined, base.budget, base.model);
    return {
        text: trimmed2.kept.map(function(b) { return b.text; }).join('\n\n'),
        dropped: base.dropped.concat(trimmed2.dropped),
        tokens: trimmed2.tokens,
        budget: base.budget,
        model: base.model
    };
}

// [v1.22.0 PREVIEW] Return the exact reference context a batch generation for these
// source chats would receive, plus a short header describing what is (and is not)
// present. Read-only: no generation, no storage writes, no side effects.
//
// This exists because buildWizardContext's output was previously invisible. When a
// generation comes back empty or malformed, the first question is "was any context
// actually attached?" — and neither the user nor the author could answer it. An empty
// preview is now self-evident.
async function buildWizardContextPreview(sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let res: { text: string; dropped: string[]; tokens: number; budget: number; model: string };
    try {
        // allowDistill=false: pressing Preview must never spend a generation.
        res = await buildWizardMultiContextDetailed(sourceChatIds || [], cfg, false);
    } catch (ePrev) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] context preview build failed:', ePrev);
        return 'Context preview failed to build. See the script log for details.';
    }
    let body = (res.text || '').trim();
    if (!body) {
        return 'NO CONTEXT ATTACHED.\n\nGeneration will run on the topic instructions alone, with nothing about your world.\n\nTo fix: set a project name/genre/concept in the Setup tab, or check one or more context-source chats above.';
    }

    let headLines: string[] = [];
    headLines.push('--- CONTEXT PREVIEW ---');
    headLines.push('This is the reference context the generator will receive, verbatim.');
    headLines.push('Model: ' + res.model);
    if (res.budget > 0) {
        let pct = Math.round((res.tokens / res.budget) * 100);
        headLines.push('Size: ' + res.tokens + ' of ' + res.budget + ' available context tokens (' + pct + '%).');
    } else {
        headLines.push('Size: ~' + res.tokens + ' tokens (context ceiling unavailable, so no cap is applied).');
    }
    if (res.dropped.length) {
        headLines.push('');
        headLines.push('TRIMMED TO FIT \u2014 these blocks were dropped, lowest priority first:');
        for (let dl of res.dropped) headLines.push('  - ' + dl);
    }
    // A stale/absent notes cache shows as a raw transcript here but will be distilled on
    // the real run, so the live context is usually SMALLER than this preview. Say so
    // rather than letting the number look alarming.
    if (body.indexOf('--- CHAT: ') >= 0) {
        headLines.push('');
        headLines.push('Note: chats shown as raw transcripts have not been distilled yet. They will be condensed into world facts on the next generation, which usually shrinks this considerably.');
    }
    headLines.push('');
    return headLines.join('\n') + body;
}

// [v1.22.0 PREVIEW] A "Preview context" button UIPart for any wizard generator tab.
// Opens a read-only modal showing exactly what buildWizardMultiContext will hand the
// generator for the given source chats. Pure read; safe to press at any time.
function wizContextPreviewPart(effSrcIds: string[]): UIPart {
    return { type: 'button', text: '\ud83d\udd0d Preview context', style: { width: '100%', marginBottom: '8px', fontSize: '10px', opacity: '0.85' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
        let previewText = await buildWizardContextPreview(effSrcIds);
        await openEngineModal({ title: 'Context Preview', size: 'large', content: [
            { type: 'multilineTextInput', initialValue: previewText, disabled: true, style: { minHeight: '380px', fontSize: '11px', lineHeight: '1.45' } } as UIPart
        ]});
    } } as UIPart;
}

// [v2.6.0] Extra checkbox rows letting a generator tab's context-source picker ALSO
// select a saved CE Chats lorebook entry directly (no owning classic chat record needed
// — buildWizardMultiContextDetailed resolves these ids straight against the lorebook).
// Shares the same srcKey/redraw plumbing as the classic-chat checkboxes above it.
function _wizCeSourceCheckboxParts(ceEntries: any[], storedSrcIds: string[], srcKey: string, redraw: () => Promise<void>): UIPart[] {
    if (!ceEntries || ceEntries.length === 0) return [];
    let out: UIPart[] = [];
    out.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Saved CE Chats (lorebook)', style: { fontSize: '9px', opacity: '0.5', marginTop: '4px', marginBottom: '2px' } });
    ceEntries.forEach(function(e: any) {
        let onSrc = storedSrcIds.indexOf(e.id) >= 0;
        out.push({ type: 'checkboxInput', label: (e.displayName || 'Untitled chat'), initialValue: onSrc, onChange: async function(val: boolean) {
            let curRaw = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(srcKey, '') as string;
            let curIds: string[] = [];
            try { let pj = JSON.parse(curRaw || '[]'); if (Array.isArray(pj)) curIds = pj.filter(Boolean); } catch (eCe) { curIds = []; }
            if (val && curIds.indexOf(e.id) < 0) curIds.push(e.id);
            else if (!val) curIds = curIds.filter(function(x) { return x !== e.id; });
            await api.v1.tempStorage.set(srcKey, JSON.stringify(curIds));
            await redraw();
        } } as UIPart);
    });
    return out;
}

// [v1.22.0 STAGING] The staging review block for a generator tab. Renders every staged
// draft of `kind` as an editable box (fields driven by WIZ_DRAFT_FIELDS), with a
// per-draft Discard, and a Commit all / Discard all footer.
//
// Returns an empty array when nothing is staged, so a tab can unconditionally spread it.
// Nothing here writes records: edits land on the draft, and only "Commit all" calls
// commitStagedDrafts().
async function wizStagingParts(kind: string, redrawFn: () => Promise<void>): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    let stagedDrafts = await loadWizardDraftsOfKind(kind);
    if (!stagedDrafts.length) return [];

    let schema = WIZ_DRAFT_FIELDS[kind] || [];
    let stageParts: UIPart[] = [];

    stageParts.push({ type: 'text', text: '\ud83e\uddea Staged ' + wizKindLabel(kind) + ' (' + stagedDrafts.length + ') \u2014 not saved yet', style: { fontSize: '11px', fontWeight: 'bold', marginTop: '10px', marginBottom: '2px', color: '#e0c068' } });
    stageParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Review and edit below. Nothing is written to your world (or the lorebook) until you commit.', style: { fontSize: '9px', opacity: '0.5', marginBottom: '6px' } });

    stagedDrafts.forEach(function(sd) {
        let sdId = sd.id;
        let sdBox: UIPart[] = [];

        schema.forEach(function(fld) {
            let fKey = fld.key;
            let curVal = sd.fields[fKey] || '';
            if (fld.multiline) {
                // [v1.22.3] Size to content. Lore/concept fields routinely run 5-8 sentences;
                // a fixed 44px box turned every staging card into a cramped scroll surface.
                sdBox.push({ type: 'multilineTextInput', label: fld.label, initialValue: curVal, placeholder: '(' + fld.label.toLowerCase() + ')', style: { minHeight: wizTextareaHeight(curVal, 56, 300), marginBottom: '3px', fontSize: '11px', lineHeight: '1.45' }, onChange: async function(v: string) {
                    await updateWizardDraftField(sdId, fKey, v);
                } } as UIPart);
            } else {
                sdBox.push({ type: 'textInput', initialValue: curVal, placeholder: fld.label, style: { margin: '0 0 3px 0', fontSize: '12px', fontWeight: 'bold' }, onChange: async function(v: string) {
                    await updateWizardDraftField(sdId, fKey, v);
                } } as UIPart);
            }
        });

        // [v1.22.2] Per-draft actions. Regenerate reuses this draft's own source chats and
        // excludes only its own name from the do-not-recreate list, so it can legitimately
        // produce the same subject again with better prose. A failed regen leaves the draft
        // untouched rather than blanking it.
        sdBox.push({ type: 'row', style: { gap: '5px', marginTop: '2px' }, content: [
            { type: 'button', text: '\u267b Regenerate', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '10px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.12)', color: '#88ccff' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                api.v1.ui.toast('Regenerating\u2026', { id: 'wiz-regen', autoClose: false, type: 'info' });
                let rgOk = await regenerateWizardDraft(sdId);
                api.v1.ui.toast(rgOk ? 'Regenerated.' : 'Regeneration failed \u2014 draft left unchanged.', { id: 'wiz-regen', type: rgOk ? 'success' : 'error', autoClose: 2200 });
                await redrawFn();
            } },
            { type: 'button', text: '\ud83d\uddd1 Discard', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '10px', color: '#ff6b6b', backgroundColor: 'transparent', border: '1px solid rgba(255,107,107,0.3)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                await discardWizardDraft(sdId);
                await redrawFn();
            } }
        ] } as UIPart);

        stageParts.push({ type: 'box', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(224,192,104,0.06)', border: '1px solid rgba(224,192,104,0.25)', padding: '7px', borderRadius: '5px', marginBottom: '6px' }, content: sdBox } as UIPart);
    });

    let stagedCount = stagedDrafts.length;
    stageParts.push({ type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '10px' }, content: [
        { type: 'button', text: '\u2705 Commit all (' + stagedCount + ')', style: { flex: '2 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', fontWeight: 'bold', backgroundColor: 'rgba(126,200,160,0.22)', color: '#7ec8a0', border: '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.5)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
            api.v1.ui.toast('Committing ' + wizKindLabel(kind) + '\u2026', { id: 'wiz-stage-commit', autoClose: false, type: 'info' });
            let madeN = await commitStagedDrafts(kind);
            let leftN = (await loadWizardDraftsOfKind(kind)).length;
            let commitMsg = madeN > 0 ? ('Committed ' + madeN + ' ' + wizKindLabel(kind) + '.') : 'Nothing committed.';
            if (leftN > 0) commitMsg += ' ' + leftN + ' left staged (needs a name).';
            api.v1.ui.toast(commitMsg, { id: 'wiz-stage-commit', type: madeN > 0 ? 'success' : 'error', autoClose: 3000 });
            await redrawFn();
        } },
        { type: 'button', text: '\ud83d\uddd1 Discard all', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '10px', color: '#ff6b6b', backgroundColor: 'transparent', border: '1px solid rgba(255,107,107,0.3)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
            let goneN = await discardAllWizardDrafts(kind);
            api.v1.ui.toast('Discarded ' + goneN + ' staged ' + wizKindLabel(kind) + '.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            await redrawFn();
        } }
    ] } as UIPart);

    return stageParts;
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: species / faction batch generation (yoink) ───────────
// [vNEXT] Generate a batch of named items (species or factions), each with a short
// lore description, in one budget-free pass. Output is parsed from a simple
// NAME:/LORE: block format. Returns an array of {name, description} (may be < count
// if the model returns fewer). kind is 'species' or 'faction' for prompt phrasing.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] avoidNames carries names discarded during this staging session
// (tombstones) plus any staged-but-uncommitted names, so a regenerate after deleting a
// bad entry does not hand the same entry straight back.
async function generateEntityBatch(kind: string, count: number, sourceChatIds: string[], avoidNames?: string[]): Promise<{ name: string; description: string }[]> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let noun = (kind === 'faction') ? 'factions (organizations, powers, or groups)' : 'species (peoples or creature-types)';

    // [v0.9.80] Feed existing same-kind names in so the batch never recreates them.
    let batchExistingNames = ((kind === 'faction') ? (cfg.factions || []) : (cfg.species || []))
        .map(function(x: any) { return (x && x.name) ? String(x.name).trim() : ''; })
        .filter(Boolean);
    for (let avName of (avoidNames || [])) {
        if (avName && batchExistingNames.indexOf(avName) < 0) batchExistingNames.push(avName);
    }

    let batchSysLines: string[] = [
        'You are generating ' + noun + ' for a story world.',
        'Produce EXACTLY ' + count + ' distinct entries that fit the world and feel like they belong together without being repetitive.',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown. DO NOT bold headers or names. Output plain text only.'
    ];
    if (batchExistingNames.length) {
        batchSysLines.push('These ' + (kind === 'faction' ? 'factions' : 'species') + ' ALREADY EXIST — do NOT recreate, rename, or duplicate any of them. Generate entirely new, distinct entries:\n' + batchExistingNames.map(function(bn) { return '- ' + bn; }).join('\n'));
    }
    batchSysLines.push('FORMAT: For each entry output exactly:\nNAME: <short proper name>\nLORE: <a full, substantial description (roughly 5-8 sentences, one or two solid paragraphs) covering what it is, what matters about it, and the specific details that distinguish it>\nSeparate entries with a line containing only ---');
    batchSysLines.push('Draw on the specific names, ideas, and details raised in the brainstorming chat below — do not stay generic.');
    batchSysLines.push('Output ONLY the entries in that format. No preamble, no numbering, no extra commentary.');
    let sys = batchSysLines.join('\n\n');

    let usrParts: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) usrParts.push(ctx);
    usrParts.push('Generate ' + count + ' ' + (kind === 'faction' ? 'factions' : 'species') + ' now.');

    let raw = '';
    try {
        raw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usrParts.join('\n\n') }], { validator: function(r) { return /NAME:/i.test(r); }, tag: 'entityBatch:' + kind });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] batch generation failed (' + kind + '):', e);
        return [];
    }

    // Parse NAME:/LORE: blocks split on --- lines.
    let out: { name: string; description: string }[] = [];
    let chunks = (raw || '').split(/\n-{3,}\n/);
    for (let ch of chunks) {
        let nameMatch = ch.match(/NAME:\s*(.+)/i);
        let loreMatch = ch.match(/LORE:\s*([\s\S]+)/i);
        if (!nameMatch) continue;
        let nm = nameMatch[1].trim();
        let lore = loreMatch ? loreMatch[1].trim() : '';
        // Trim a trailing stray --- or NAME: bleed if the split missed.
        lore = lore.replace(/\n-{3,}[\s\S]*$/, '').replace(/\nNAME:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim();
        if (nm) out.push({ name: nm, description: lore });
    }
    return out;
}

// [vNEXT] Generate (or regenerate) the lore description for ONE named species/faction,
// scoped to that single item, with optional hint. Returns description text or null.
async function generateEntityLore(kind: string, name: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let kindNoun = (kind === 'faction') ? 'faction' : 'species';
    let sys = [
        'You are writing the lore description for a single ' + kindNoun + ' named "' + name + '" in a story world.',
        'Write a full, substantial description (roughly 5-8 sentences, one or two solid paragraphs) covering what it is, what matters about it, and the specific details that distinguish it. Stay focused on THIS ' + kindNoun + ' only, and draw on the specific ideas and details in the reference context rather than staying generic.',
        'Output ONLY the description prose. Do not begin with the name or a label.'
    ].join('\n\n');
    let usrParts: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) usrParts.push(ctx);
    if (hint && hint.trim()) usrParts.push('--- EXTRA GUIDANCE ---\n' + hint.trim());
    usrParts.push('Write the description for "' + name + '" now.');
    let result = '';
    try {
        result = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usrParts.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'entityLore:' + kind + ':' + name });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] entity lore generation failed (' + name + '):', e);
        return null;
    }
    return (result || '').trim() || null;
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: stage / commit (v1.22.0 STAGING) ─────────────────────
// [v1.22.0] Every batch kind now follows: stage*Batch (generate -> park as drafts) ->
// user reviews/edits/discards -> commitStagedDrafts (write records + lorebook, clear
// drafts + tombstones). The legacy run*Batch wrappers preserve the old one-shot
// behavior for existing callers (notably the bridge's AE_WIZ_GENERATE handler) by
// staging and then committing ONLY the ids they just created — so a one-shot call never
// sweeps up drafts the user is still reviewing in the native wizard.

// Names currently staged-but-uncommitted for a kind. Combined with tombstones to form
// the do-not-recreate list, so a second Generate pass extends the batch rather than
// duplicating it.
// [v1.22.2] Do-not-recreate list for a kind = names of drafts STILL STAGED, so a second
// Generate press extends the batch rather than duplicating what is already on screen.
// Discarded names are deliberately NOT included (see discardWizardDraft).
// excludeDraftId omits one draft's own name — used by per-draft Regenerate, which must
// be free to produce the same place/person again with better prose.
async function avoidNamesForKind(kind: string, excludeDraftId?: string): Promise<string[]> {
    let staged = await loadWizardDraftsOfKind(kind);
    let merged: string[] = [];
    for (let sd of staged) {
        if (excludeDraftId && sd.id === excludeDraftId) continue;
        let mn = (sd.fields.name || '').trim();
        if (mn && merged.indexOf(mn) < 0) merged.push(mn);
    }
    return merged;
}

// Stage N species/factions as drafts. Returns the created draft ids (empty on failure).
async function stageEntityBatch(kind: string, count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string[]> {
    let avoid = await avoidNamesForKind(kind);
    let items = await generateEntityBatch(kind, count, sourceChatIds, avoid);
    if (!items.length) return [];
    let rows = items.map(function(it) { return { name: it.name, description: it.description || '' }; });
    return await addWizardDrafts(kind, rows, sourceChatIds, null);
}

// Stage N minor-NPC archetypes as drafts.
// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] stageArchetypeBatch() removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// Stage N item templates as drafts.
// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] stageItemBatch() removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// Stage N locations as drafts under parentId. parentId rides on the draft record so a
// commit long after staging still files them in the right place in the tree.
async function stageLocationBatch(count: number, parentId: string | null, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string[]> {
    let avoid = await avoidNamesForKind('location');
    let items = await generateLocationBatch(count, parentId, sourceChatIds, avoid);
    if (!items.length) return [];
    let rows = items.map(function(it) { return { name: it.name, description: it.description || '', lore: it.lore || '' }; });
    return await addWizardDrafts('location', rows, sourceChatIds, parentId || null);
}

// [v1.22.3 REPROMPT] Re-roll the assistant's most recent reply without the user having to
// type anything. Drops the trailing assistant message, then re-runs the turn against the
// unchanged transcript — runWizardChatTurn already skips pushing a user message when
// userText is empty, so the same last user message is answered again.
//
// Guards: a chat with no user message yet has nothing to answer (the seed message is an
// assistant turn and must never be popped, or the topic's framing is lost). If generation
// fails, the popped reply is restored so a failed re-roll never destroys the old one.
async function regenerateWizardChatReply(chatId: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let chat = await loadWizardChat(chatId);
    if (!chat || !chat.messages || !chat.messages.length) return null;

    let hasUser = chat.messages.some(function(m) { return m.role === 'user'; });
    if (!hasUser) return null;

    let popped: any = null;
    let last = chat.messages[chat.messages.length - 1];
    if (last && last.role === 'assistant') {
        popped = chat.messages.pop();
        await saveWizardChat(chat);
    }

    let reply = await runWizardChatTurn(chatId, '');
    if (!reply && popped) {
        // Restore: re-load (runWizardChatTurn may have saved) and push the old reply back.
        let restored = await loadWizardChat(chatId);
        if (restored) {
            restored.messages.push(popped);
            await saveWizardChat(restored);
        }
    }
    return reply;
}

// [v1.22.2 CHAR-STAGE] Stage N characters as name + concept-dump drafts. The expensive
// per-character field extraction is deferred to commit, so a user who dislikes a name or
// concept can fix or discard it before any generation is spent structuring it.
async function stageCharacterBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string[]> {
    let names = await generateCharacterNames(count, sourceChatIds);
    if (!names.length) return [];
    let rows: { [k: string]: string }[] = [];
    for (let nm of names) {
        let dump = '';
        try { dump = await generateCharacterDump(nm, sourceChatIds); }
        catch (eCharDump) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] character dump failed for "' + nm + '":', eCharDump); }
        rows.push({ name: nm, dump: dump || '' });
    }
    return await addWizardDrafts('character', rows, sourceChatIds, null);
}

// [v1.22.2] Regenerate a single staged draft in place, reusing the chat sources it was
// generated from. Its own name is excluded from the do-not-recreate list so the model may
// legitimately produce the same subject again with better prose. Returns false on failure
// and leaves the existing draft untouched, so a bad regen never destroys good content.
async function regenerateWizardDraft(draftId: string): Promise<boolean> {
    let all = await loadWizardDrafts();
    let target = all.find(function(d) { return d.id === draftId; });
    if (!target) return false;

    let rgKind = target.kind;
    let rgSrc = target.sourceChatIds || [];
    let rgAvoid = await avoidNamesForKind(rgKind, draftId);
    let rgFields: { [k: string]: string } | null = null;

    try {
        if (rgKind === 'species' || rgKind === 'faction') {
            let rows = await generateEntityBatch(rgKind, 1, rgSrc, rgAvoid);
            if (rows.length) rgFields = { name: rows[0].name, description: rows[0].description || '' };
        // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] 'archetype'/'item' draft-regen branches removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.
        } else if (rgKind === 'location') {
            let rows = await generateLocationBatch(1, target.parentId || null, rgSrc, rgAvoid);
            if (rows.length) rgFields = { name: rows[0].name, description: rows[0].description || '', lore: rows[0].lore || '' };
        } else if (rgKind === 'character') {
            // Keep the name the user is already looking at; regenerate only the concept.
            let rgName = (target.fields.name || '').trim();
            if (!rgName) return false;
            let rgDump = await generateCharacterDump(rgName, rgSrc);
            if (rgDump && rgDump.trim()) rgFields = { name: rgName, dump: rgDump.trim() };
        }
    } catch (eRegen) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] regenerate failed for draft ' + draftId + ':', eRegen);
        return false;
    }

    if (!rgFields) return false;
    let fresh = await loadWizardDrafts();
    let live = fresh.find(function(d) { return d.id === draftId; });
    if (!live) return false;
    live.fields = rgFields;
    await saveWizardDrafts(fresh);
    return true;
}

// [v1.22.0 STAGING] The single irreversible write path. Commits staged drafts of a kind
// to real records (+ lorebook entries where that kind has them), then removes exactly
// the committed drafts and clears the kind's tombstones. Drafts with a blank name are
// skipped and LEFT STAGED rather than silently dropped, so the user can see and fix them.
//
// onlyIds restricts the commit to a specific set (used by the legacy run*Batch wrappers).
// Returns the number of records actually created.
async function commitStagedDrafts(kind: string, onlyIds?: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let drafts = await loadWizardDraftsOfKind(kind);
    if (onlyIds && onlyIds.length) {
        drafts = drafts.filter(function(d) { return onlyIds.indexOf(d.id) >= 0; });
    }
    if (!drafts.length) return 0;

    let committedIds: string[] = [];
    let made = 0;

    for (let d of drafts) {
        let dName = (d.fields.name || '').trim();
        if (!dName) continue;   // leave nameless drafts staged for the user to fix

        try {
            if (kind === 'species') {
                let spId = 'sp_' + generateId();
                await mutConfig(function(c) {
                    if (!c.species) c.species = [];
                    c.species.push({ id: spId, name: dName, description: d.fields.description || '', lorebookTags: [] });
                });
                await createLinkedSpeciesLorebookEntry(spId).catch(function() {});
            } else if (kind === 'faction') {
                let facId = 'fac_' + generateId();
                await mutConfig(function(c) {
                    if (!c.factions) c.factions = [];
                    c.factions.push({ id: facId, name: dName, description: d.fields.description || '', relationshipStates: ['Hostile', 'Wary', 'Neutral', 'Friendly', 'Allied'], lorebookTags: [] });
                });
                await createLinkedFactionLorebookEntry(facId).catch(function() {});
            } else if (kind === 'archetype') {
                let archId = 'arch_' + generateId();
                await mutConfig(function(c) {
                    if (!(c as any).npcArchetypes) (c as any).npcArchetypes = [];
                    (c as any).npcArchetypes.push({ id: archId, name: dName, briefTemplate: d.fields.brief || '', charResourceDefs: [], actionInstances: [], tagSubscriptions: [] });
                });
            } else if (kind === 'item') {
                let itmId = 'item_' + generateId();
                await mutConfig(function(c) {
                    if (!(c as any).itemArchetypes) (c as any).itemArchetypes = [];
                    (c as any).itemArchetypes.push({ id: itmId, name: dName, descriptionTemplate: d.fields.description || '', tagSubscriptions: [], spawnTarget: 'inventory' });
                });
            } else if (kind === 'location') {
                await createLocationEntity(dName, d.fields.description || '', d.fields.lore || '', d.parentId || null);
            } else if (kind === 'character') {
                // [v1.22.2 CHAR-STAGE] Structured fields are derived HERE, from the concept
                // dump as the user left it. If field extraction fails, the character is still
                // created with its name + dump rather than being lost, and the dump remains
                // visible on the record for a manual per-field regenerate.
                let chDump = d.fields.dump || '';
                let chFields: WizardCharFields | null = null;
                try { chFields = await generateCharacterFields(dName, chDump); }
                catch (eChFields) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] field extraction failed for "' + dName + '":', eChFields); }
                await createWizardCharacter(dName, chDump, chFields);
            } else {
                aeLog('[AE:wizard] commitStagedDrafts unknown kind: ' + kind);
                continue;
            }
            committedIds.push(d.id);
            made++;
        } catch (eCommit) {
            // Leave this draft staged so the user can retry; keep going with the rest.
            api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] commit failed for staged ' + kind + ' "' + dName + '":', eCommit);
        }
    }

    await removeWizardDrafts(committedIds);
    return made;
}

// [vNEXT] Batch-create species records from a chat: generate, push records, and
// create+link a lorebook entry (with tags = name) for each. Returns count created.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Now a thin stage -> commit wrapper. Commits only its own new drafts.
async function runWizardSpeciesBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let newIds = await stageEntityBatch('species', count, sourceChatIds);
    if (!newIds.length) return 0;
    return await commitStagedDrafts('species', newIds);
}

// [vNEXT] Batch-create faction records from a chat. Factions also get the default
// standing track (relationshipStates) so the existing index-based standing works.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Now a thin stage -> commit wrapper. Commits only its own new drafts.
async function runWizardFactionsBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let newIds = await stageEntityBatch('faction', count, sourceChatIds);
    if (!newIds.length) return 0;
    return await commitStagedDrafts('faction', newIds);
}

// [vNEXT] Batch-create minor-NPC archetypes from a wizard chat: generate NAME +
// one-line BRIEF pairs and push NpcArchetypeDef records to cfg.npcArchetypes.
// Resources/tags start empty (edit in the Bestiary: "+ Default Resources", tags);
// no lorebook entry — archetypes are spawn templates, not world lore. Returns count.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Pure generator: parses and RETURNS archetypes, writing nothing.
// Committing to cfg.npcArchetypes is commitStagedDrafts()'s job.
// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] generateArchetypeBatch() removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Legacy one-shot: stage then commit only the drafts just created.
// Retained for the bridge's AE_WIZ_GENERATE handler and any direct callers.
// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] runWizardArchetypesBatch() removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// [v1.14.0 ITEMS] Generate item templates from a wizard chat: NAME + one-line DESC
// pairs. Mechanics (skill bonuses, actions, tags) start EMPTY by design and are filled
// in the Item Template editor \u2014 the model only authors the name + description, the
// same shallow-generation split the Bestiary uses.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Pure generator: parses and RETURNS item templates, writing nothing.
// Committing to cfg.itemArchetypes is commitStagedDrafts()'s job.
// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] generateItemBatch() removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Legacy one-shot: stage then commit only the drafts just created.
// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] runWizardItemsBatch() removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// [v0.9.80] Manually add a single blank species/faction record (default name, empty
// lore) and link a lorebook entry, so the user can rename it and then ⚡ Regen its
// fields — the regen path re-syncs the existing entry. Returns the new record id.
async function addBlankWizardEntity(kind: string): Promise<string> {
    let blankId = ((kind === 'faction') ? 'fac_' : 'sp_') + generateId();
    let blankName = (kind === 'faction') ? 'New Faction' : 'New Species';
    await mutConfig(function(c) {
        if (kind === 'faction') {
            if (!c.factions) c.factions = [];
            c.factions.push({ id: blankId, name: blankName, description: '', relationshipStates: ['Hostile', 'Wary', 'Neutral', 'Friendly', 'Allied'], lorebookTags: [] });
        } else {
            if (!c.species) c.species = [];
            c.species.push({ id: blankId, name: blankName, description: '', lorebookTags: [] });
        }
    });
    if (kind === 'faction') await createLinkedFactionLorebookEntry(blankId).catch(function() {});
    else await createLinkedSpeciesLorebookEntry(blankId).catch(function() {});
    return blankId;
}

// [vNEXT] Regenerate one species/faction's description in place and re-sync its
// lorebook entry. kind is 'species' or 'faction'. Returns the new text or null.
async function regenerateWizardEntity(kind: string, itemId: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let list: any[] = (kind === 'faction') ? (cfg.factions || []) : (cfg.species || []);
    let item = list.find(function(x) { return x.id === itemId; });
    if (!item) return null;
    let gen = await generateEntityLore(kind, item.name, sourceChatIds, hint);
    if (!gen) return null;
    await mutConfig(function(c) {
        let arr: any[] = (kind === 'faction') ? (c.factions || []) : (c.species || []);
        let t = arr.find(function(x) { return x.id === itemId; });
        if (t) t.description = gen;
    });
    if (kind === 'faction') await syncFactionLorebookEntry(itemId).catch(function() {});
    else await syncSpeciesLorebookEntry(itemId).catch(function() {});
    return gen;
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: Locations (tier model + generators) ───────────
// [v0.9.82] Depth of an entity from the world root (top-level/parentId-null = 0).
async function computeEntityTier(entityId: string): Promise<number> {
    let tierIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
    let depth = 0; let cur: string | null = entityId; let guard = 0;
    while (cur && guard < 64) {
        let tm = tierIdx.find(function(m) { return m.id === cur; });
        if (!tm || !tm.parentId) break;
        depth++; cur = tm.parentId; guard++;
    }
    return depth;
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] tierBandIndexForDepth / entryActionIdForDepth / syncTierTemplate
// removed — tier-travel cost templates cut with Actions + Travel. Locations no longer
// stamp an entryActionId.

// [v0.9.82] Yoink-generate a batch of locations as {name, description}. parentId null =
// top-level. Existing sibling names are fed in to avoid duplicates; the parent's own
// description anchors children. Multi-chat context via buildWizardMultiContext.
//
// [v1.18.0 LOCGEN] >>> PAIRED PROMPT: generateSceneLocation() (Items block, near
// >>> generateSceneItem) emits the SAME NAME/DESC/LORE contract from story context
// >>> instead of chat context, and parses it with the same regexes. If you change the
// >>> format instructions or the parse here, change them there too.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] avoidNames carries tombstoned + staged-uncommitted names.
async function generateLocationBatch(count: number, parentId: string | null, sourceChatIds: string[], avoidNames?: string[]): Promise<{ name: string; description: string; lore: string }[]> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let parentEnt = parentId ? await loadEntity(parentId) : null;
    let locIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
    let existingLoc = locIdx.filter(function(m) { return m.type === 'location' && m.parentId === (parentId || null); }).map(function(m) { return m.name; }).filter(Boolean);
    for (let avLoc of (avoidNames || [])) {
        if (avLoc && existingLoc.indexOf(avLoc) < 0) existingLoc.push(avLoc);
    }
    let scopeNoun = parentEnt ? ('sub-locations inside "' + parentEnt.name + '"') : 'top-level locations (regions / major places) of the world';

    let locSysLines: string[] = [
        'You are generating ' + scopeNoun + ' for a story world.',
        'Produce EXACTLY ' + count + ' distinct locations that fit the world and belong together without being repetitive.',
        // [v1.22.2] The brainstorming chat is the AUTHORITY on which places to build, not
        // mere background flavour. Users reported discussing Hogwarts / Diagon Alley /
        // Hogsmeade and receiving unrelated inventions instead. State this first and
        // positively: name the places the chat names, and invent only to fill a shortfall.
        'The brainstorming chat below is your source of truth. If it names specific places, use those exact names and build those locations first, preserving the chat\'s own details about each. Invent additional locations only if the chat names fewer than ' + count + '.',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown. DO NOT bold headers or names. Output plain text only.'
    ];
    if (parentEnt && (parentEnt.description || '').trim()) {
        locSysLines.push('They are all inside this parent location:\n' + parentEnt.name + ': ' + parentEnt.description.trim());
    }
    if (existingLoc.length) {
        // [v1.22.2] Scoped wording: these are records that already exist in the world, so
        // recreating one would make a duplicate. This list no longer contains names the
        // user merely discarded (v1.22.0 tombstones), which is what previously caused the
        // generator to be forbidden from producing the very places the chat was about.
        locSysLines.push('Locations with these names already exist in the world, so choose different ones \u2014 build any OTHER places the chat names:\n' + existingLoc.map(function(ln) { return '- ' + ln; }).join('\n'));
    }
    locSysLines.push('FORMAT: For each location output exactly:\nNAME: <short place name>\nDESC: <1-2 sentences capturing ONLY what a camera physically senses on arrival (light, texture, scale). No history.>\nLORE: <a full, comprehensive entry (roughly 5-8 sentences) covering history, significance, culture, and tensions>\nSeparate entries with a line containing only ---');
    locSysLines.push('Draw every name and detail from the brainstorming chat. Output ONLY the entries in the exact format requested. No preamble, no numbering, no commentary.');
    let locSys = locSysLines.join('\n\n');

    let locUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) locUsr.push(ctx);
    // [v1.22.2] Restate the priority immediately before generation, where it carries the
    // most recency weight, rather than relying on the system message alone.
    locUsr.push('Generate ' + count + ' locations now, starting with the places named in the chat above.');

    let locRaw = '';
    try {
        locRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: locSys }, { role: 'user', content: locUsr.join('\n\n') }], { validator: function(r) { return /NAME:/i.test(r); }, tag: 'locationBatch' + (parentEnt ? (':' + parentEnt.name) : '') });
    } catch (eLocGen) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] location batch generation failed:', eLocGen);
        return [];
    }

    let locOut: { name: string; description: string; lore: string }[] = [];
    let locChunks = (locRaw || '').split(/\n-{3,}\n/);
    for (let lch of locChunks) {
        let lnm = lch.match(/NAME:\s*(.+)/i);
        let lde = lch.match(/DESC:\s*([\s\S]+?)(?:\nLORE:|$)/i);
        let llo = lch.match(/LORE:\s*([\s\S]+)/i);
        if (!lnm) continue;
        let lName = lnm[1].trim();
        let lDesc = lde ? lde[1].trim() : '';
        lDesc = lDesc.replace(/\nLORE:[\s\S]*$/i, '').replace(/\n-{3,}[\s\S]*$/, '').replace(/\nNAME:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim();
        let lLore = llo ? llo[1].trim() : '';
        lLore = lLore.replace(/\n-{3,}[\s\S]*$/, '').replace(/\nNAME:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim();
        if (lName) locOut.push({ name: lName, description: lDesc, lore: lLore });
    }
    return locOut;
}

// [v1.18.0 LOCGEN] The ONE place a generated {name, description, lore} triple becomes a
// persisted `location` entity plus its linked lorebook entry. Two callers, deliberately:
//
//   runWizardLocationsBatch()  (directly below)  — authoring-time, wizard chat context,
//                                                  Yoink generation, N at a time.
//   spawnLocation()            (Items block, next to spawnItem/generateSceneItem) —
//                                                  in-play, story context, one at a time.
//
// >>> PAIRED CODE: if you add a field stamped onto new locations here, make sure BOTH
// >>> callers can supply it. If you change what a location needs at birth, this helper
// >>> is the only place to change it — do not re-inline entity assembly in a caller.
//
// Siblings need no connectedEntityIds edge: locationGraph derives lateral adjacency from
// a shared parentId (see "Lateral siblings" in buildSceneStatePayload).
async function createLocationEntity(name: string, description: string, lore: string, parentId: string | null): Promise<string> {
    let cle = makeDefaultEntity(name || 'New Location');
    cle.type = 'location';
    cle.parentId = parentId || null;
    cle.description = description || '';
    (cle as any).lorebookText = lore || '';
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] entryActionId param/assignment removed — always dead, no consumer.
    await saveEntity(cle);
    if ((lore || '').trim()) { await createLinkedEntityLorebookEntry(cle.id).catch(function() {}); }
    return cle.id;
}

// [v0.9.82] Batch-create location entities under parentId, stamping each with the tier
// template for its depth. Returns the count created.
// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Legacy one-shot: stage then commit only the drafts just created.
// Tier/entry-action resolution now happens inside commitStagedDrafts, from the parentId
// carried on each draft record.
async function runWizardLocationsBatch(count: number, parentId: string | null, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let newIds = await stageLocationBatch(count, parentId || null, sourceChatIds);
    if (!newIds.length) return 0;
    return await commitStagedDrafts('location', newIds);
}

// [v0.9.82] Create one blank location under parentId. Returns id.
async function addBlankWizardLocation(parentId: string | null): Promise<string> {
    let le = makeDefaultEntity('New Location');
    le.type = 'location';
    le.parentId = parentId || null;
    await saveEntity(le);
    return le.id;
}

// [v0.9.83] Regenerate one location's short description AND comprehensive lorebook in a
// single pass; updates both fields and re-syncs (or creates) its lorebook entry.
async function regenerateWizardLocation(locId: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let ent = await loadEntity(locId);
    if (!ent) return null;
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let parentEnt = ent.parentId ? await loadEntity(ent.parentId) : null;
    let locSys = [
        'You are writing the entry for a single location named "' + ent.name + '"' + (parentEnt ? (' inside "' + parentEnt.name + '"') : '') + ' in a story world.',
        'Output exactly two labelled blocks and nothing else:\nDESC: <1-2 sentences of scene-present description — what a person directly sees, hears, and senses on arrival. Keep it short.>\nLORE: <a full, comprehensive entry (roughly 5-8 sentences) covering history, significance, who or what is here, how it connects to the wider world, and any secrets or tensions>',
        'Stay focused on THIS location, drawing on the specific ideas in the reference context. Do not add any other labels, preamble, or commentary.'
    ].join('\n\n');
    let locUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) locUsr.push(ctx);
    if (hint && hint.trim()) locUsr.push('--- EXTRA GUIDANCE ---\n' + hint.trim());
    locUsr.push('Write the DESC and LORE for "' + ent.name + '" now.');
    let locRes = '';
    try {
        locRes = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: locSys }, { role: 'user', content: locUsr.join('\n\n') }], { validator: function(r) { return /DESC:|LORE:/i.test(r); }, tag: 'locationRegen:' + ent.name });
    } catch (eLocReg) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] location regen failed:', eLocReg);
        return null;
    }
    let locOutRaw = (locRes || '').trim();
    if (!locOutRaw) return null;
    let rde = locOutRaw.match(/DESC:\s*([\s\S]+?)(?:\nLORE:|$)/i);
    let rlo = locOutRaw.match(/LORE:\s*([\s\S]+)/i);
    let newDesc = rde ? rde[1].trim() : '';
    newDesc = newDesc.replace(/\nLORE:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim();
    let newLore = rlo ? rlo[1].trim() : '';
    if (!newDesc && !newLore) { newLore = locOutRaw; }
    let freshEnt = await loadEntity(locId);
    if (!freshEnt) return null;
    if (newDesc) freshEnt.description = newDesc;
    if (newLore) (freshEnt as any).lorebookText = newLore;
    await saveEntity(freshEnt);
    if (newLore) {
        if ((freshEnt as any).lorebookEntryId) await syncEntityLorebookEntry(locId, true).catch(function() {});
        else await createLinkedEntityLorebookEntry(locId).catch(function() {});
    }
    return newLore || newDesc;
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: Characters (seed-and-go via comprehensive concept dump) ──
// [v0.9.84] Flow: generate names from chat (dedup) -> per character, generate a
// comprehensive concept dump from chat -> derive all dramatis-personae + static fields
// from THAT dump (not chat) -> create the character (entity body + record + lorebook).
// The dump is the persistent source; later per-field regens in the editor read it.

interface WizardCharFields {
    species: string; age: string; gender: string; occupationRole: string; briefDescription: string;
    physicalDescription: string; personalityCore: string; voiceAndMannerisms: string; background: string;
    coreValues: string[];
}

async function generateCharacterNames(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string[]> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let charIdx = await loadIndex();
    let existingNames = charIdx.map(function(m) { return m.name; }).filter(Boolean);
    let nameSys: string[] = [
        'You are identifying distinct CHARACTERS (individual people) for a story world.',
        'Produce EXACTLY ' + count + ' character names that the brainstorming context establishes or clearly implies. Real individuals, not groups, roles, or species.'
    ];
    if (existingNames.length) nameSys.push('These characters ALREADY EXIST \u2014 do NOT repeat them:\n' + existingNames.map(function(n) { return '- ' + n; }).join('\n'));
    nameSys.push('FORMAT: one name per line, each prefixed with "NAME: ". Nothing else \u2014 no descriptions, numbering, or commentary.');
    let nameUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) nameUsr.push(ctx);
    nameUsr.push('List ' + count + ' character names now.');
    let nameRaw = '';
    try { nameRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: nameSys.join('\n\n') }, { role: 'user', content: nameUsr.join('\n\n') }], { validator: function(r) { return /NAME:/i.test(r); }, tag: 'charNames' }); }
    catch (eCharNames) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] character names failed:', eCharNames); return []; }
    let names: string[] = [];
    let nameLines = (nameRaw || '').split('\n');
    for (let nl of nameLines) {
        let mm = nl.match(/NAME:\s*(.+)/i);
        if (!mm) continue;
        let nm = mm[1].trim().replace(/^[-*0-9.\s]+/, '').trim();
        if (nm && names.indexOf(nm) < 0) names.push(nm);
    }
    return names.slice(0, count);
}

async function generateCharacterDump(name: string, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let dumpSys = [
        'You are assembling a COMPREHENSIVE concept profile (a "concept dump") for a single character named "' + name + '" based entirely on the brainstorming context.',
        'Organize EVERYTHING the context establishes or implies about ' + name + ' into one thorough, well-structured profile: who they are, anatomical appearance, psychological contradictions, vocal timbre, formative history, relationships, and goals.',
        'CRITICAL RULE: Write entirely in plain prose. DO NOT use markdown, bolding, italics, or bullet points. DO NOT invent tragic backstories or cliché tropes unless explicitly stated in the context.',
        'Output ONLY the plain prose profile for ' + name + '. No preamble, no headers, no commentary.'
    ].join('\n\n');
    let dumpUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) dumpUsr.push(ctx);
    dumpUsr.push('Write the comprehensive concept profile for "' + name + '" now.');
    let dumpRaw = '';
    try { dumpRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: dumpSys }, { role: 'user', content: dumpUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'charDump:' + name }); }
    catch (eCharDump) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] character dump failed:', eCharDump); return ''; }
    return (dumpRaw || '').trim();
}

async function generateCharacterFields(name: string, dump: string): Promise<WizardCharFields | null> {
    if (!(dump || '').trim()) return null;
    let fieldSys = [
        'You are filling structured fields for the character "' + name + '", extracting data ONLY from the concept profile provided. Do not contradict it; where the profile is silent, infer the most fitting objective value.',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown, bolding, or italics. Output EXACTLY these labelled lines, in this exact order, and nothing else:\n' +
        'SPECIES: <one or two words>\n' +
        'AGE: <a number or short phrase>\n' +
        'GENDER: <short>\n' +
        'ROLE: <occupation or role, a few words>\n' +
        'BRIEF: <one tight sentence identifying who they are at a glance>\n' +
        'PHYSICAL: <Camera-view physical anatomy, skin, hair, eyes, and build. Sensual or striking details if applicable. No clothing. No mannerisms.>\n' +
        'PERSONALITY: <Psychological baseline and a core contradiction/hidden facet. No tragic tropes. Declarative.>\n' +
        'VOICE: <Vocal timbre, speech rhythm, and one signature physical mannerism.>\n' +
        'BACKGROUND: <One or two formative past events that shaped their current worldview. Past tense.>\n' +
        'VALUES: <3-5 deeply held values, semicolon-separated>'
    ].join('\n\n');
    let fieldUsr = '--- CONCEPT PROFILE ---\n' + dump.trim() + '\n\nFill the fields for "' + name + '" now.';
    let fieldRaw = '';
    try { fieldRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: fieldSys }, { role: 'user', content: fieldUsr }], { validator: function(r) { return /SPECIES:|BRIEF:/i.test(r); }, tag: 'charFields:' + name }); }
    catch (eCharFields) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] character fields failed:', eCharFields); return null; }

    function parseCharFields(txt: string): Record<string, string> {
        let labels = ['SPECIES', 'AGE', 'GENDER', 'ROLE', 'BRIEF', 'PHYSICAL', 'PERSONALITY', 'VOICE', 'BACKGROUND', 'VALUES'];
        let result: Record<string, string> = {};
        let fLines = (txt || '').split('\n');
        let cur = '';
        for (let fl of fLines) {
            let trimmed = fl.trim();
            let up = trimmed.toUpperCase();
            let hit = '';
            for (let lb of labels) { if (up.indexOf(lb + ':') === 0) { hit = lb; break; } }
            if (hit) { result[hit] = trimmed.slice(hit.length + 1).trim(); cur = hit; }
            else if (cur && trimmed) { result[cur] = (result[cur] ? result[cur] + ' ' : '') + trimmed; }
        }
        return result;
    }

    let parsed = parseCharFields(fieldRaw);
    let valuesRaw = parsed['VALUES'] || '';
    return {
        species: parsed['SPECIES'] || '', age: parsed['AGE'] || '', gender: parsed['GENDER'] || '',
        occupationRole: parsed['ROLE'] || '', briefDescription: parsed['BRIEF'] || '',
        physicalDescription: parsed['PHYSICAL'] || '', personalityCore: parsed['PERSONALITY'] || '',
        voiceAndMannerisms: parsed['VOICE'] || '', background: parsed['BACKGROUND'] || '',
        coreValues: valuesRaw.split(';').map(function(s) { return s.trim(); }).filter(Boolean)
    };
}

async function createWizardCharacter(name: string, dump: string, fields: WizardCharFields | null): Promise<string> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let body = makeDefaultEntity(name);
    body.type = 'character';
    body.parentId = null;
    await saveEntity(body);
    let ch = makeDefaultCharacter(name, cfg);
    ch.entityId = body.id;
    ch.conceptDump = dump || '';
    if (fields) {
        ch.species = fields.species || '';
        ch.age = fields.age || '';
        ch.gender = fields.gender || '';
        ch.occupationRole = fields.occupationRole || '';
        ch.briefDescription = fields.briefDescription || '';
        ch.physicalDescription = fields.physicalDescription || '';
        ch.personalityCore = fields.personalityCore || '';
        ch.voiceAndMannerisms = fields.voiceAndMannerisms || '';
        ch.background = fields.background || '';
        ch.coreValues = fields.coreValues || [];
    }
    await saveCharacter(ch);
    try { await createLinkedLorebookEntry(ch.id); } catch (eCharLb) { /* non-fatal */ }
    return ch.id;
}

// [v0.9.84] Generate ONE character end-to-end (dump from chat -> fields from dump -> create).
async function runWizardCharacterSingle(name: string, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<boolean> {
    let nm = (name || '').trim();
    if (!nm) return false;
    let dump = await generateCharacterDump(nm, sourceChatIds);
    let fields = await generateCharacterFields(nm, dump);
    await createWizardCharacter(nm, dump, fields);
    return true;
}

// [v0.9.84] Batch: names from chat (dedup) -> each character end-to-end. Returns count created.
// [v1.22.2 CHAR-STAGE] Now a thin stage -> commit wrapper, committing only its own drafts.
// The per-character dump/field calls still happen, just split across the two halves.
async function runWizardCharactersBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let newIds = await stageCharacterBatch(count, sourceChatIds);
    if (!newIds.length) return 0;
    return await commitStagedDrafts('character', newIds);
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: Intro / Opening scene ────────────────────────────────
// [v0.9.85] Pick where the story opens and who is present; generate the opening
// prose (yoink, budget-free), place the player + cast there, and insert into the editor.

async function generateIntroProse(locationId: string, charIds: string[], sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<string> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let loc = locationId ? await loadEntity(locationId) : null;
    let allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
    let protag = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.isProtagonist; });
    let castLines: string[] = [];
    for (let cid of (charIds || [])) {
        let ch = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.id === cid; });
        if (!ch) continue;
        castLines.push('- ' + ch.name + (ch.briefDescription ? (': ' + ch.briefDescription) : ''));
    }
    let introSys: string[] = [
        'You are writing the OPENING of an interactive story \u2014 the very first scene, in immersive second-person present tense ("You ...") from the protagonist\'s point of view.',
        'Establish the setting, mood, and immediate situation, and bring any present characters to life through concrete action and detail. Start *in media res* (already in the location, mid-action). End on a beat that invites the player to act; do not resolve the situation.',
        'CRITICAL RULE: Avoid all backstory, lore-dumping, and exposition. DO NOT use any markdown, bolding, italics, or headings.'
    ];
    if (loc) {
        let locLore = ((loc as any).lorebookText || '').trim();
        introSys.push('SETTING (where the scene opens):\n' + loc.name + ((loc.description || '').trim() ? (' \u2014 ' + loc.description.trim()) : '') + (locLore ? ('\nBackground: ' + locLore) : ''));
    }
    if (protag) {
        introSys.push('THE PROTAGONIST ("you"):\n' + protag.name + ((protag.briefDescription || '').trim() ? (': ' + protag.briefDescription.trim()) : ''));
    }
    if (castLines.length) introSys.push('PRESENT IN THE SCENE:\n' + castLines.join('\n'));
    introSys.push('Write 2-4 vivid paragraphs. Output ONLY the opening prose \u2014 no title, headings, labels, or commentary.');
    let introUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) introUsr.push(ctx);
    introUsr.push('Write the opening scene now.');
    let introRaw = '';
    try { introRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: introSys.join('\n\n') }, { role: 'user', content: introUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'introProse' }); }
    catch (eIntroGen) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] intro generation failed:', eIntroGen); return ''; }
    return (introRaw || '').trim();
}

async function placeIntroCast(locationId: string, charIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
    // Move each chosen character's body into the location and make them active/present.
    for (let cid of (charIds || [])) {
        let ch = await loadCharacter(cid);
        if (!ch || !ch.entityId) continue;
        let body = await loadEntity(ch.entityId);
        if (body) { body.parentId = locationId; await saveEntity(body); }
        let fc = await loadCharacter(cid);
        if (fc) { fc.active = true; fc.scenePresent = true; await saveCharacter(fc); }
    }
    // Move the protagonist (silent); this also recalculates scene presence so the placed
    // cast registers as present in the new room.
    await commitTravel(locationId, true);
}

// [v0.9.85] Generate prose first (so a failure leaves world state untouched), then place
// the cast + player, then insert the prose into the story editor as paragraphs.
// [v1.6.3 INTRO-STAGE] The intro generator now stages its output like the other wizard
// tabs: generate -> review/edit/regenerate -> commit. generateIntroDraft yoinks the opening
// prose and stores it as an editable draft WITHOUT touching the story; commitIntroDraft
// travels the player in and writes the (possibly edited) draft, then clears it.
async function generateIntroDraft(locationId: string, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<boolean> {
    if (!locationId) { api.v1.ui.toast('Pick an intro location first.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 2500 }); return false; }
    let loc = await loadEntity(locationId);
    if (!loc) { api.v1.ui.toast('That location no longer exists.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 2500 }); return false; }
    // [v0.9.86] Cast placement removed — characters get intro locations set in their character cards.
    let prose = await generateIntroProse(locationId, [], sourceChatIds);
    if (!(prose || '').trim()) return false;
    // [v1.22.0 STAGING] storyStorage, not tempStorage — a staged draft the user is asked
    // to review must survive a script reload / story switch.
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_WIZ_INTRO_DRAFT, JSON.stringify({ locationId: locationId, prose: prose.trim() })).catch(function() {});
    return true;
}

// [v1.22.0 STAGING] Read the staged intro draft, tolerating both the new storyStorage
// JSON string and any legacy tempStorage object left over from before the migration.
async function loadIntroDraft(): Promise<any> {
    let rawIntro = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZ_INTRO_DRAFT, '') as string;
    if (rawIntro && String(rawIntro).trim()) {
        try { return JSON.parse(rawIntro as string); }
        catch (eIntroParse) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] intro draft parse error:', eIntroParse); }
    }
    let legacyIntro = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZ_INTRO_DRAFT, null) as any;
    if (legacyIntro && legacyIntro.locationId) return legacyIntro;
    return null;
}

async function commitIntroDraft(): Promise<boolean> {
    let draft = await loadIntroDraft();
    if (!draft || !draft.locationId || !((draft.prose || '') as string).trim()) { api.v1.ui.toast('No intro draft to commit.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 2500 }); return false; }
    let loc = await loadEntity(draft.locationId);
    if (!loc) { api.v1.ui.toast('That location no longer exists.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 2500 }); return false; }
    await commitTravel(draft.locationId, true);
    let introParas = String(draft.prose).split(/\n\n+/);
    for (let para of introParas) {
        if (para.trim()) { await api.v1.document.appendParagraph({ text: para.trim() }).catch(function() {}); }
    }
    await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_WIZ_INTRO_DRAFT).catch(function() {});
    await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(STORAGE_WIZ_INTRO_DRAFT).catch(function() {});
    return true;
}

// [legacy] one-shot generate+insert, retained for any direct/scripted callers. The wizard
// UI now uses the staged path (generateIntroDraft then commitIntroDraft) instead.
async function runWizardIntro(locationId: string, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<boolean> {
    let okGen = await generateIntroDraft(locationId, sourceChatIds);
    if (!okGen) return false;
    return await commitIntroDraft();
}

// ── World-Build Wizard: Drama (hooks, NPC web, narrative threads) ─────────────
// [vNEXT] The Drama wizard seeds the three primitives the live Drama tab and the
// dynamic engine maintain: per-character UnresolvedHooks, bilateral WebLinks, and
// narrative-thread entities. Hooks and web links are CHARACTER-AWARE — the generators
// are fed the established roster and must resolve to real records; nothing is written
// against a person who does not exist. Seeded hooks deliberately mirror the dynamic
// engine's spawn shape (pressure 20-50, one concrete directed sentence) so they read
// identically to tension the engine raises later during play.

// Compact cast roster for drama generators: protagonist marked, each active character
// on one identifying line. Shared context so generated tensions reference real people.
function buildWizardCastContext(allChars: CharacterRecord[]): string {
    let castLines: string[] = [];
    let castProtag = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.isProtagonist; });
    if (castProtag) {
        let pBrief = (castProtag.briefDescription || '').trim();
        castLines.push('PROTAGONIST (the player, "you"): ' + castProtag.name + (pBrief ? (' — ' + pBrief) : ''));
    }
    for (let cc of allChars) {
        if (cc.isProtagonist) continue;
        if (!cc.active) continue;
        let ccBrief = (cc.briefDescription || '').trim();
        let ccRole = (cc.occupationRole || '').trim();
        let ccTail = ccBrief ? (' — ' + ccBrief) : (ccRole ? (' — ' + ccRole) : '');
        castLines.push('- ' + cc.name + ccTail);
    }
    if (castLines.length === 0) return '';
    return '--- CAST ---\n' + castLines.join('\n');
}

// Resolve a generated target name to the canonical hook target: the protagonist maps
// to the '__player__' token (the Drama tab and injection logic expect it); an exact
// cast name/nickname maps to that name; anything else is kept verbatim (the engine
// tolerates free-text targets so a near-miss is still editable rather than dropped).
function resolveHookTarget(raw: string, allChars: CharacterRecord[]): string {
    let rt = (raw || '').trim();
    if (!rt) return '';
    let rtLow = rt.toLowerCase();
    if (rtLow === 'none' || rtLow === 'n/a') return '';
    let rtProtag = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.isProtagonist; });
    if (rtProtag && (rtLow === rtProtag.name.toLowerCase() || rtLow === 'the protagonist' || rtLow === 'protagonist' || rtLow === 'you' || rtLow === 'the player' || rtLow === 'player')) return '__player__';
    for (let rc of allChars) {
        if (rc.isProtagonist) continue;
        if (rc.name.toLowerCase() === rtLow) return rc.name;
        if ((rc.nicknames || []).some(function(n) { return (n || '').toLowerCase() === rtLow; })) return rc.name;
    }
    return rt;
}

// Resolve a generated name to an existing character id (protagonist included), or ''.
function findWizardCharIdByName(allChars: CharacterRecord[], raw: string): string {
    let fn = (raw || '').trim().toLowerCase();
    if (!fn) return '';
    let fProtag = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.isProtagonist; });
    if (fProtag && (fn === 'the protagonist' || fn === 'protagonist' || fn === 'you' || fn === 'the player' || fn === 'player')) return fProtag.id;
    for (let fc of allChars) {
        if (fc.name.toLowerCase() === fn) return fc.id;
        if ((fc.nicknames || []).some(function(n) { return (n || '').toLowerCase() === fn; })) return fc.id;
    }
    return '';
}

// [vNEXT] Generate ONE starting hook for a character: a concrete unresolved tension
// directed at another existing cast member or the protagonist. Mirrors the dynamic
// engine's hook-spawn prompt for tonal parity. Returns {targetName, description,
// pressureLevel} (targetName already resolved) or null when no clean tension exists.
async function generateCharacterHook(char: CharacterRecord, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<{ targetName: string; description: string; pressureLevel: number } | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let cast = buildWizardCastContext(allChars);
    let dump = (char.conceptDump || '').trim();

    let hookSys = [
        'You are a dramaturg seeding the OPENING dramatic tension for the character "' + char.name + '" at the very start of a story.',
        'Identify ONE specific, unresolved tension this character carries toward another person: a conflict, a threat they pose or face, a debt, a secret they guard, a betrayal, or an urgent unfulfilled want. Passive moods and general feelings do NOT qualify. It must be directed at a named person and demand eventual action.',
        'The target MUST be one of the established cast, named EXACTLY as written, OR the protagonist. Do not invent a new person.',
        'Write the hook as a single, punchy sentence naming the core tension in concrete terms: what happened, what is owed, what is at stake.',
        '  Wrong: "feels uneasy about her place in the household"',
        '  Right: "Owes the magister a debt she cannot pay, and the collector arrives within the week"',
        'Set the pressure between 20 and 50 — this is a fresh hook that has not yet escalated.',
        'If this character has no plausible directed tension, set the target to "None".',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown, bolding, italics, or bullet points. Output EXACTLY these three labelled lines and nothing else:\nTARGET: <exact cast name, the protagonist, or None>\nPRESSURE: <a number from 20 to 50>\nHOOK: <one concrete sentence>'
    ].join('\n\n');

    let hookUsr: string[] = [];
    if (cast) hookUsr.push(cast);
    if (dump) hookUsr.push('--- ' + char.name.toUpperCase() + ' (concept profile) ---\n' + dump);
    if (ctx.trim()) hookUsr.push(ctx);
    if (hint && hint.trim()) hookUsr.push('--- EXTRA GUIDANCE FOR THIS REGENERATION ---\n' + hint.trim());
    hookUsr.push('Write the opening hook for "' + char.name + '" now.');

    let hookRaw = '';
    try { hookRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: hookSys }, { role: 'user', content: hookUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'wizHookSeed:' + char.name }); }
    catch (eHookGen) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] hook generation failed (' + char.name + '):', eHookGen); return null; }

    function parseHookFields(txt: string): { target: string; pressure: number; hook: string } {
        let pTarget = ''; let pPressure = 30; let pHookLines: string[] = []; let pCur = '';
        let pLines = (txt || '').split('\n');
        for (let pl of pLines) {
            let pt = pl.trim();
            let pu = pt.toUpperCase();
            if (pu.indexOf('TARGET:') === 0) { pTarget = pt.slice(7).trim(); pCur = 'target'; }
            else if (pu.indexOf('PRESSURE:') === 0) { let pm = pt.slice(9).match(/-?\d+/); if (pm) pPressure = parseInt(pm[0], 10); pCur = 'pressure'; }
            else if (pu.indexOf('HOOK:') === 0) { let hb = pt.slice(5).trim(); if (hb) pHookLines.push(hb); pCur = 'hook'; }
            else if (pCur === 'hook' && pt) { pHookLines.push(pt); }
        }
        return { target: pTarget, pressure: pPressure, hook: pHookLines.join(' ').trim() };
    }

    let parsedHook = parseHookFields(hookRaw);
    let resolvedTarget = resolveHookTarget(parsedHook.target, allChars);
    if (!resolvedTarget || !parsedHook.hook) return null;
    let clampedPressure = Math.min(50, Math.max(20, parsedHook.pressure || 30));
    return { targetName: resolvedTarget, description: parsedHook.hook, pressureLevel: clampedPressure };
}

// [vNEXT] Seed (or reseed) the single starting hook on one character. Replaces hooks[0]
// — the model is one-active-hook-per-character. Stamps lastHookGen with the current
// story gen count so the dynamic engine's escalator picks it up on schedule.
async function runWizardHookSingle(charId: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<boolean> {
    let baseChar = await loadCharacter(charId);
    if (!baseChar) return false;
    let gen = await generateCharacterHook(baseChar, sourceChatIds, hint);
    if (!gen) return false;
    let freshHookChar = await loadCharacter(charId);
    if (!freshHookChar) return false;
    let gc = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_GEN_COUNT, 0) as number;
    freshHookChar.hooks = [{
        id: api.v1.uuid(),
        targetName: gen.targetName,
        description: (gen.description || '').substring(0, 300),
        pressureLevel: Math.min(100, Math.max(0, gen.pressureLevel || 30)),
        developmentNotes: [],
        resolved: false
    }];
    freshHookChar.lastHookGen = Number(gc) || 0;
    await saveCharacter(freshHookChar);
    return true;
}

// [vNEXT] Seed one hook on each selected character. Returns the count successfully seeded.
async function runWizardHooksBatch(charIds: string[], sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let hookMade = 0;
    for (let hcid of (charIds || [])) {
        let hookOk = await runWizardHookSingle(hcid, sourceChatIds, '');
        if (hookOk) hookMade++;
    }
    return hookMade;
}

// [vNEXT] Generate a batch of bilateral cast dynamics (NPC web). Each names two distinct
// existing people and one concrete charged sentence between them. Parsed from labelled
// A:/B:/DYNAMIC:/PRIORITY: blocks. Mapping to ids + dedup happens in runWizardWebBatch.
async function generateWebBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<{ aName: string; bName: string; dynamic: string; priority: number }[]> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let cast = buildWizardCastContext(allChars);
    if (!cast) return [];

    let webSys: string[] = [
        'You are a dramaturg defining the web of bilateral dynamics between the established cast of a story.',
        'Produce EXACTLY ' + count + ' distinct PAIR dynamics. Each names two DIFFERENT people from the cast below (the protagonist may be one of them) and states the charged relationship between them in one concrete sentence.',
        'Focus on friction, history, and asymmetry: rivalries, old debts, unspoken attraction, divided loyalty, mutual dependence. Avoid bland "they are friends" pairings, and do not repeat the same pair twice.',
        'Assign each a priority from 0 to 100: how strongly this dynamic should colour scenes where both are present (default 30; use 70+ only for defining relationships).',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown, bolding, or bullet points. Output plain text only.',
        'FORMAT: for each pair output exactly:\nA: <exact cast name>\nB: <exact cast name>\nDYNAMIC: <one concrete sentence>\nPRIORITY: <a number from 0 to 100>\nSeparate pairs with a line containing only ---',
        'Output ONLY the pairs in that format. No preamble, no numbering, no commentary.'
    ];

    let webUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) webUsr.push(ctx);
    webUsr.push('Generate ' + count + ' bilateral dynamics now.');

    let webRaw = '';
    try { webRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: webSys.join('\n\n') }, { role: 'user', content: webUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'webBatch' }); }
    catch (eWebGen) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] web batch generation failed:', eWebGen); return []; }

    let webOut: { aName: string; bName: string; dynamic: string; priority: number }[] = [];
    let webChunks = (webRaw || '').split(/\n-{3,}\n/);
    for (let wch of webChunks) {
        let aMatch = wch.match(/(?:^|\n)\s*A:\s*(.+)/i);
        let bMatch = wch.match(/(?:^|\n)\s*B:\s*(.+)/i);
        let dMatch = wch.match(/DYNAMIC:\s*([\s\S]+?)(?:\nPRIORITY:|$)/i);
        let pMatch = wch.match(/PRIORITY:\s*(-?\d+)/i);
        if (!aMatch || !bMatch) continue;
        let aNm = aMatch[1].trim();
        let bNm = bMatch[1].trim();
        let dyn = dMatch ? dMatch[1].trim().replace(/\n-{3,}[\s\S]*$/, '').trim() : '';
        let pri = pMatch ? parseInt(pMatch[1], 10) : 30;
        if (aNm && bNm) webOut.push({ aName: aNm, bName: bNm, dynamic: dyn, priority: pri });
    }
    return webOut;
}

// [vNEXT] Generate web dynamics, map names to existing character ids, drop self-pairs,
// unresolved names, and pairs already present (unordered). Returns the count added.
async function runWizardWebBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
    let pairs = await generateWebBatch(count, sourceChatIds);
    if (pairs.length === 0) return 0;
    let web = await loadNpcWeb();
    let webMade = 0;
    for (let wp of pairs) {
        let aId = findWizardCharIdByName(allChars, wp.aName);
        let bId = findWizardCharIdByName(allChars, wp.bName);
        if (!aId || !bId || aId === bId) continue;
        let already = web.some(function(l) { return (l.charA_id === aId && l.charB_id === bId) || (l.charA_id === bId && l.charB_id === aId); });
        if (already) continue;
        web.push({ id: api.v1.uuid(), charA_id: aId, charB_id: bId, dynamic: (wp.dynamic || '').substring(0, 300), priority: Math.min(100, Math.max(0, wp.priority || 30)) });
        webMade++;
