    }
    if (webMade > 0) await saveNpcWeb(web);
    return webMade;
}

// [vNEXT] Regenerate the dynamic sentence on ONE existing web link in place (no new link).
async function regenerateWebLink(linkId: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
    let web = await loadNpcWeb();
    let link = web.find(function(l) { return l.id === linkId; });
    if (!link) return null;
    let wa = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.id === link.charA_id; });
    let wb = allChars.find(function(c) { return c.id === link.charB_id; });
    if (!wa || !wb) return null;
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let regSys = [
        'You are writing the charged bilateral dynamic between "' + wa.name + '" and "' + wb.name + '" in a story.',
        'State their relationship in one concrete sentence — friction, history, or asymmetry. Avoid bland pairings.',
        'Output ONLY the single sentence. No names prefix, no label, no markdown.'
    ].join('\n\n');
    let regUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) regUsr.push(ctx);
    if (hint && hint.trim()) regUsr.push('--- EXTRA GUIDANCE ---\n' + hint.trim());
    regUsr.push('Write the dynamic between "' + wa.name + '" and "' + wb.name + '" now.');
    let regRaw = '';
    try { regRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: regSys }, { role: 'user', content: regUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'webLinkRegen:' + wa.name + '-' + wb.name }); }
    catch (eWebReg) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] web regen failed:', eWebReg); return null; }
    let regTxt = (regRaw || '').trim();
    if (!regTxt) return null;
    let web2 = await loadNpcWeb();
    let t2 = web2.find(function(l) { return l.id === linkId; });
    if (t2) { t2.dynamic = regTxt.substring(0, 300); await saveNpcWeb(web2); }
    return regTxt;
}

// [vNEXT] Generate a batch of narrative-thread descriptors. Each is a short name, a
// story state (locked/active/completed), and a 1-2 sentence current-situation hook.
// Parsed from labelled NAME:/STATE:/DESC: blocks. Records are created in runWizard...Batch.
async function generateNarrativeBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<{ name: string; state: string; description: string }[]> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let narrIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
    let narrExisting = narrIdx.filter(function(n) { return n.type === 'narrative'; }).map(function(n) { return n.name; }).filter(Boolean);

    let narrSys: string[] = [
        'You are a dramaturg outlining the plot threads of a story — the quests, mysteries, conflicts, and obligations that will drive play.',
        'Produce EXACTLY ' + count + ' distinct narrative threads that fit the world and connect to the established cast and tensions.',
        'For each thread give a short evocative NAME, a STATE, and a DESC of one to two sentences stating the thread\'s current situation and what it pushes the player toward — concrete and unresolved, never a summary of how it ends.',
        'STATE must be exactly one of: locked (set up but not yet in play), active (currently driving the story), completed (already resolved). Most opening threads are "locked" or "active".',
        'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown, bolding, or bullet points. Output plain text only.'
    ];
    if (narrExisting.length) narrSys.push('These threads ALREADY EXIST — do NOT recreate, rename, or duplicate them:\n' + narrExisting.map(function(n) { return '- ' + n; }).join('\n'));
    narrSys.push('FORMAT: for each thread output exactly:\nNAME: <short thread name>\nSTATE: <locked|active|completed>\nDESC: <1-2 concrete sentences>\nSeparate threads with a line containing only ---');
    narrSys.push('Output ONLY the threads in that format. No preamble, no numbering, no commentary.');

    let narrUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) narrUsr.push(ctx);
    narrUsr.push('Generate ' + count + ' narrative threads now.');

    let narrRaw = '';
    try { narrRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: narrSys.join('\n\n') }, { role: 'user', content: narrUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'narrativeBatch' }); }
    catch (eNarrGen) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] narrative batch generation failed:', eNarrGen); return []; }

    let narrOut: { name: string; state: string; description: string }[] = [];
    let narrChunks = (narrRaw || '').split(/\n-{3,}\n/);
    for (let nch of narrChunks) {
        let nNameMatch = nch.match(/NAME:\s*(.+)/i);
        let nStateMatch = nch.match(/STATE:\s*(\w+)/i);
        let nDescMatch = nch.match(/DESC:\s*([\s\S]+)/i);
        if (!nNameMatch) continue;
        let nName = nNameMatch[1].trim();
        let nState = nStateMatch ? nStateMatch[1].trim().toLowerCase() : 'locked';
        let nDesc = nDescMatch ? nDescMatch[1].trim() : '';
        nDesc = nDesc.replace(/\n-{3,}[\s\S]*$/, '').replace(/\nNAME:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim();
        if (nName) narrOut.push({ name: nName, state: nState, description: nDesc });
    }
    return narrOut;
}

// [vNEXT] Create top-level narrative-thread entities from a generated batch. Mirrors the
// Drama tab's manual "+ New Thread" path exactly (entity only, no lorebook entry). The
// existing narrative tree editor handles nesting and state changes afterward.
async function runWizardNarrativesBatch(count: number, sourceChatIds: string[]): Promise<number> {
    let items = await generateNarrativeBatch(count, sourceChatIds);
    let narrCreated = 0;
    for (let nit of items) {
        if (!nit.name) continue;
        let nEnt = makeDefaultEntity(nit.name);
        nEnt.type = 'narrative';
        let nSt = (nit.state || 'locked');
        nEnt.storyState = (nSt === 'active' || nSt === 'completed') ? nSt as any : 'locked';
        nEnt.description = nit.description || '';
        nEnt.narrativeParentId = null;
        await saveEntity(nEnt);
        narrCreated++;
    }
    return narrCreated;
}

// [vNEXT] Add a single blank top-level narrative thread (rename + ⚡ Regen its situation).
async function addBlankWizardNarrative(): Promise<string> {
    let blankEnt = makeDefaultEntity('New Thread');
    blankEnt.type = 'narrative';
    blankEnt.storyState = 'locked';
    blankEnt.narrativeParentId = null;
    await saveEntity(blankEnt);
    return blankEnt.id;
}

// [vNEXT] Regenerate ONE narrative thread's situation description in place.
async function regenerateNarrativeThread(entId: string, sourceChatIds: string[], hint: string): Promise<string | null> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let nEntity = await loadEntity(entId);
    if (!nEntity || nEntity.type !== 'narrative') return null;
    let ctx = await buildWizardMultiContext(sourceChatIds, cfg);
    let nSys = [
        'You are writing the current situation of the narrative thread "' + nEntity.name + '" in a story.',
        'State in one to two concrete sentences what this thread is and what it pushes the player toward right now — unresolved, never a summary of how it ends.',
        'Output ONLY the description prose. No name prefix, no label, no markdown.'
    ].join('\n\n');
    let nUsr: string[] = [];
    if (ctx.trim()) nUsr.push(ctx);
    if (hint && hint.trim()) nUsr.push('--- EXTRA GUIDANCE ---\n' + hint.trim());
    nUsr.push('Write the situation for "' + nEntity.name + '" now.');
    let nRaw = '';
    try { nRaw = await executeYoinkGeneration([{ role: 'system', content: nSys }, { role: 'user', content: nUsr.join('\n\n') }], { tag: 'narrativeRegen:' + nEntity.name }); }
    catch (eNarrReg) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizard] narrative regen failed:', eNarrReg); return null; }
    let nTxt = (nRaw || '').trim();
    if (!nTxt) return null;
    let nFresh = await loadEntity(entId);
    if (nFresh) { nFresh.description = nTxt; await saveEntity(nFresh); }
    return nTxt;
}


// ── World-Build Wizard: memory fan-out commit ────────────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] For a 'memoryFanout' topic chat: regenerate each ENABLED section in its own
// scoped pass (sequential — NAI serializes generation; never parallel), write results
// back into cfg.wizardSections, persist config, then commit to Memory + Author's Note.
// Generation is sequential and order-stable so the "already-written" context grows as
// it goes. Returns the number of sections generated, or -1 on a hard failure.
async function runWizardMemoryFanout(chatId: string): Promise<number> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let chat = await loadWizardChat(chatId);
    if (!chat) return -1;

    // [v2.11.0] generateWizardSection now takes a pre-built ctx string rather than a
    // chat object — build it once here the same way it used to build it internally:
    // buildWizardContext (referenced chats/lorebook/story) plus this chat's OWN
    // transcript (buildWizardContext only resolves REFERENCED chats, not the given
    // chat's own messages, so that still needs its own block).
    let fanoutTranscript = (chat.messages || []).map(function(m) { return '[' + m.role.toUpperCase() + '] ' + m.content; }).join('\n');
    let fanoutExtraCtx = await buildWizardContext(chat, cfg);
    let fanoutCtxParts: string[] = [];
    if (fanoutExtraCtx.trim()) fanoutCtxParts.push(fanoutExtraCtx);
    if (fanoutTranscript.trim()) fanoutCtxParts.push('--- BRAINSTORMING CHAT ---\n' + fanoutTranscript);
    let fanoutCtx = fanoutCtxParts.join('\n\n');

    let sections = cfg.wizardSections || [];
    let enabled = sections.filter(function(s) { return s.enabled; });
    let written = 0;

    for (let i = 0; i < enabled.length; i++) {
        let sec = enabled[i];
        // Pass the live (progressively updated) section list as prior context.
        let priorNow = (await loadConfig()).wizardSections || [];
        let gen = await generateWizardSection(sec, fanoutCtx, cfg, priorNow);
        if (gen) {
            await mutConfig(function(c) {
                let target = (c.wizardSections || []).find(function(s) { return s.id === sec.id; });
                if (target) target.content = gen;
            });
            written++;
        }
    }

    let freshCfg = await loadConfig();
    await commitWizardMemory(freshCfg);
    return written;
}

// ── Emotion Resolution ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// [CHANGE] Added from AI engine. Normalises freeform model output to the
// canonical 24-state list used by bridge portrait selection and direct chat.

function resolveEmotion(raw: any): string {
    if (!raw) return 'neutral';
    const key = String(raw).toLowerCase().trim().replace(/[^a-z_]/g, '');
    return EMOTION_MAP[key] || 'neutral';
}

// ── Entity Factory ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function makeDefaultEntity(name: string): EntityRecord {
    // Returns a new EntityRecord with all required fields initialised to safe defaults.
    // callers typically override type, parentId, and description immediately after.
    return {
        id: generateId(),
        name: name,
        type: 'location',
        parentId: null,
        narrativeParentId: null,
        storyState: 'locked',
        description: '',
        atmosphere: '',
        state: 'default',
        stateDescriptions: { 'default': '' },
        connectedEntityIds: [],
        presentCharacterIds: [],
        // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] actions/rules/skillBonuses/showActionWidget/isVehicle/gated removed — dead Actions remnants.
        // encounters: removed v0.9.25 E4
        inventoryIds: [],
        notes: '',
        knowledgeString: '',
        lorebookTags: [],
        lorebookText: '',
        locationVisuals: '',
        questTarget: '',
        createdAt: Date.now(),
        updatedAt: Date.now()
    };
}

// ── Character Factory ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function makeDefaultCharacter(name: string, cfg: GlobalConfig): CharacterRecord {
    // Returns a new CharacterRecord with all required fields initialised.
    // [CHANGE] New visual fields initialised with safe empty/numeric defaults.
    // avatarWidth/Height default to 1024×1024 (square) so new records never have
    // undefined dimensions when the bridge constructs image generation requests.
    const char: CharacterRecord = {
        id: api.v1.uuid(),
        name: name,
        conceptDump: '',
        nicknames: [],
        species: '',
        age: '',
        gender: '',
        occupationRole: '',
        briefDescription: '',
        // [CHANGE] portraitKey omitted — no longer part of CharacterRecord
        // Visual identity (image generation)
        visuals: '',
        uc: '',
        sceneVisuals: '',
        avatarVisuals: '',
        avatarWidth: 1024,
        avatarHeight: 1024,
        avatarSeed: 0,
        // Flags
        scenePresent: false,
        active: true,
        lorebookEntryId: undefined,
        featuredForEphemeral: false,
        // Static narrative
        physicalDescription: '',
        voiceAndMannerisms: '',
        personalityCore: '',
        background: '',
        customFieldValues: {},
        coreValues: [],
        // Relationships and hooks
        primaryRelationship: {
            targetName: '', enabled: false, archetype: '', howItStarted: '',
            threads: [], currentStatus: '', trajectory: '', summary: '', milestones: []
        },
        hooks: [],
        activeConditions: [],  // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS]
        // Dynamic state
        ephemeral: null,
        ephemeralHistory: [],
        lastEphemeralGen: 0,  // [v0.9.24 E1]
        lastEphemeralTokens: 0, // [v2.54.0]
        midRange: null,
        midRangeHistory: [],       // [v2.14.0 GENESIS]
        relationshipHistory: [],   // [v2.14.0 GENESIS]
        lastMidrangeGen: 0,
        lastMidrangeTokens: 0, // [v2.54.0]
        lastArcGen: 0,
        lastArcTokens: 0, // [v2.54.0]
        lastHookGen: 0,
        lastHookTokens: 0, // [v2.54.0]
        // Equipment and resources
        equippedItemIds: [],
        charFactionRelationships: {},
        // Sheet
        sheetValues: {},
        // Engine flags
        charContextEnabled: true,
        autoUpdateEnabled: cfg.autoUpdateDefault,
        autoThoughtsEnabled: cfg.autoThoughtsDefault,
        hooksEnabled: true,
        lastTokenEstimate: 0,
        // World graph
        entityId: undefined,
        createdAt: Date.now(),
        updatedAt: Date.now()
    };
    return char;
}
// ============================================================================
// SECTION 5 — STORAGE: CHARACTER DATA
// All reads and writes for CharacterRecord and the character index.
// Every function here operates on api.v1.storyStorage.
// ============================================================================

// ── Index (CharacterMeta[]) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
// The index is the lightweight list used for sidebar rendering and fast lookups.
// Full CharacterRecord data lives in individual keys (STORAGE_CHAR_PFX + id).

async function loadIndex(): Promise<CharacterMeta[]> {
    return await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_INDEX, []) as CharacterMeta[];
}

async function saveIndex(idx: CharacterMeta[]): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_INDEX, idx.filter(Boolean));
}

// ── Migration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// migrateCharacter runs every time a CharacterRecord is loaded from storage.
// It forward-migrates old save formats and ensures all required fields exist.
// Safe to call multiple times on the same record.

function migrateCharacter(raw: any, cfg: GlobalConfig): CharacterRecord {
    const d = makeDefaultCharacter('Unnamed', cfg);
    const c = { ...d, ...raw };

    // ── Deprecated field removal ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    delete (c as any).storyRole;
    delete (c as any).conditions;
    delete (c as any).activeConditionId;
    delete (c as any).tier;
    delete (c as any).budgetCap;
    // [CHANGE] portraitKey removed — bridge derives portrait key from char.id directly.
    delete (c as any).portraitKey;
    // [CHANGE] homeLocationId was an unimplemented idea with no read path. Removed.
    delete (c as any).homeLocationId;

    // ── Equipment migration (equippedItemId → equippedItemIds) ────────────────
    if (!c.equippedItemIds) {
        c.equippedItemIds = (c as any).equippedItemId ? [(c as any).equippedItemId] : [];
    }
    delete (c as any).equippedItemId;
    if ('equippedItems' in c) delete (c as any).equippedItems;
    if ('wardrobe' in c) delete (c as any).wardrobe;

    // ── v0.9.3 field guards ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

    if (!c.charFactionRelationships) c.charFactionRelationships = {};

    // [v0.9.89] Migrate #gold → #currency on existing character resource defs.
    if (Array.isArray(c.charResourceDefs)) {
        c.charResourceDefs = c.charResourceDefs.map(function(r: any) {
            if (r.tag === '#gold') {
                return { ...r, id: r.id === 'cres_gold' ? 'cres_currency' : r.id, name: r.name === 'Wealth' ? 'Currency' : r.name, tag: '#currency' };
            }
            return r;
        });
    }

    // ── Structural guards ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if (!c.hooks) c.hooks = [];
    if (!c.ephemeralHistory) c.ephemeralHistory = [];
    // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] change-gated trend trails + baseline backfill
    if (!c.midRangeHistory) c.midRangeHistory = [];
    if (!c.relationshipHistory) c.relationshipHistory = [];
    if (c.midRange && (c.midRange as any).baselineView === undefined) (c.midRange as any).baselineView = '';
    
    // Purge old character arc properties from database memory
    delete (c as any).arcEnabled;
    delete (c as any).arcType;
    delete (c as any).developingAspects;
    delete (c as any).arcCurrentState;
    delete (c as any).arcTrajectory;
    delete (c as any).arcEndState;
    delete (c as any).arcMilestones;

    if (!c.primaryRelationship) c.primaryRelationship = {
        targetName: '', enabled: false, archetype: '', howItStarted: '',
        threads: [], currentStatus: '', trajectory: '', summary: '', milestones: []
    };
    if (c.primaryRelationship.enabled === undefined) c.primaryRelationship.enabled = false;
    if (!c.customFieldValues) c.customFieldValues = {};
    if (!c.sheetValues) c.sheetValues = {};
    if (!c.conceptDump) c.conceptDump = '';
    // [v2.54.0] The *Tokens stamps below were DELETED here from v0.9.24 through v2.53.x
    // (per-generation cadence had fully replaced per-token). They are reintroduced as an
    // opt-in alternative unit (dynamicIntervalMode === 'tokens') — deleting them would wipe
    // token progress on every character load. Default-to-0 instead, same as the gen stamps.
    if ((c as any).lastEphemeralTokens === undefined) (c as any).lastEphemeralTokens = 0;
    if ((c as any).lastMidrangeTokens  === undefined) (c as any).lastMidrangeTokens  = 0;
    if ((c as any).lastArcTokens       === undefined) (c as any).lastArcTokens       = 0;
    if ((c as any).lastHookTokens      === undefined) (c as any).lastHookTokens      = 0;
    if (c.lastEphemeralGen === undefined) c.lastEphemeralGen = 0;
    if (c.lastMidrangeGen  === undefined) c.lastMidrangeGen  = 0;
    if (c.lastArcGen       === undefined) c.lastArcGen       = 0;
    if (c.lastHookGen      === undefined) c.lastHookGen      = 0;
    if (c.charContextEnabled === undefined) c.charContextEnabled = true;
    if (c.hooksEnabled === undefined) c.hooksEnabled = true;

    // ── Visual field guards (v1.0.0) ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // [CHANGE] New fields. Old records won't have these; default to empty strings
    // and safe numeric values so bridge image generation never receives undefined.
    if (!c.visuals)       c.visuals       = '';
    if (!c.uc)            c.uc            = '';
    if (!c.sceneVisuals)  c.sceneVisuals  = '';
    if (!c.avatarVisuals) c.avatarVisuals = '';
    if (c.avatarWidth  === undefined || c.avatarWidth  === null) c.avatarWidth  = 1024;
    if (c.avatarHeight === undefined || c.avatarHeight === null) c.avatarHeight = 1024;
    if (c.avatarSeed   === undefined || c.avatarSeed   === null) c.avatarSeed   = 0;

    return c as CharacterRecord;
}

// ── Load ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function loadCharacter(id: string): Promise<CharacterRecord | null> {
    const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.get(STORAGE_CHAR_PFX + id);
    if (!raw) return null;
    const cfg = await loadConfig();
    return migrateCharacter(raw, cfg);
}

async function loadCharacterListForPicker(excludeId?: string): Promise<CharacterMeta[]> {
    // Returns active characters suitable for UI pickers (relationship target, entity link, etc.).
    // Validates that each index entry still has a backing storage record (handles stale entries).
    const idx = await loadIndex();
    const validated: CharacterMeta[] = [];
    for (const m of idx) {
        if (m.id === excludeId) continue;
        if (!m.active && !m.isProtagonist) continue;
        const exists = await api.v1.storyStorage.get(STORAGE_CHAR_PFX + m.id);
        if (exists) validated.push(m);
    }
    return validated;
}

async function loadAllCharacters(): Promise<CharacterRecord[]> {
    // [CHANGE] Performance fix: original called loadCharacter() per character,
    // which called loadConfig() per character — N+1 storage reads for N characters.
    // Now loads config once and passes it into migrateCharacter directly.
    const idx = await loadIndex();
    const cfg = await loadConfig();
    const chars = await Promise.all(
        idx.filter(Boolean).map(async function(m) {
            try {
                const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.get(STORAGE_CHAR_PFX + m.id);
                return raw ? migrateCharacter(raw, cfg) : null;
            } catch (e) {
                api.v1.error('[AE] loadAllCharacters: failed to load char ' + m.id, e);
                return null;
            }
        })
    );
    return chars.filter(Boolean) as CharacterRecord[];
}

// ── Save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function saveCharacter(char: CharacterRecord): Promise<void> {
    const cfg = await loadConfig();

    // Derive equipped item names from equippedItemIds for the index
    const equippedItemNames: string[] = [];
    for (const itemId of (char.equippedItemIds || [])) {
        try {
            const itemEnt = await loadEntity(itemId);
            if (itemEnt) equippedItemNames.push(itemEnt.name);
        } catch (ignoredErr) { /* non-fatal — item may not exist yet */ }
    }

    // Derive the character's current location from their entity body's parentId.
    // This is the single source of truth for character location — not a stored field.
    let currentLocationId: string | undefined = undefined;
    if (char.entityId) {
        try {
            const charEntBody = await loadEntity(char.entityId);
            if (charEntBody) currentLocationId = charEntBody.parentId !== null ? charEntBody.parentId : undefined;
        } catch (ignoredErr) { /* non-fatal — entity may not exist yet */ }
    }

    // Build the lightweight index entry
    // [CHANGE] portraitKey removed from CharacterMeta — bridge derives from char.id
    const meta: CharacterMeta = {
        id: char.id,
        name: char.name,
        isProtagonist: char.isProtagonist,
        entityId: char.entityId,
        hasCriticalHook: !!(char.hooks && char.hooks.some(function(h) { return !h.resolved && h.pressureLevel >= 85; })),
        scenePresent: char.scenePresent,
        active: char.active,
        briefDescription: char.briefDescription,
        species: char.species,
        age: char.age,
        occupationRole: char.occupationRole,
        currentStance: char.midRange ? char.midRange.socialMask : undefined,
        lastThought: char.ephemeral ? char.ephemeral.compressedLine : undefined,
        lastTokenEstimate: char.lastTokenEstimate,
        equippedItemNames: equippedItemNames.length > 0 ? equippedItemNames : undefined,
        currentLocationId: currentLocationId,
        isStatic: !!((char as any).isStatic),   // [v0.9.31 DYN1] propagate so card reads correct state
        dynamicPriorityRank: (typeof (char as any).dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && (char as any).dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? (char as any).dynamicPriorityRank : undefined  // [vNEXT] propagate rank so the cap sorts off the index
    };

    const idx = await loadIndex();
    const existingIdx = idx.findIndex(function(m) { return m.id === char.id; });
    if (existingIdx >= 0) { idx[existingIdx] = meta; } else { idx.push(meta); }

    await saveIndex(idx);
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CHAR_PFX + char.id, char);

    // Keep cfg.protagonistName in sync so __player__ resolves correctly in injections
    if (char.isProtagonist && char.name) {
        try {
            const cfgSync = await api.v1.storyStorage.get(STORAGE_CONFIG) as any;
            if (cfgSync && cfgSync.protagonistName !== char.name) {
                cfgSync.protagonistName = char.name;
                await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CONFIG, cfgSync);
            }
        } catch (e) { /* non-fatal */ }
    }

    // [CHANGE] Queue a debounced broadcast to keep the bridge's character visual
    // cache in sync. Defined in Section 18. Batches rapid sequential saves.
    queueCharacterDataBroadcast();
}

// ── Delete ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function deleteCharacter(id: string): Promise<void> {
    const char = await loadCharacter(id);
    if (char && char.entityId) {
        await deleteEntity(char.entityId); // Scrub the physical body from the world
    }
    await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_CHAR_PFX + id);
    const idx = await loadIndex();
    await saveIndex(idx.filter(function(m) { return m.id !== id; }));
    
    // Dispatch cascade event to bridge to clean up IndexedDB assets
    await broadcastToTM('AE_DELETE_PORTRAIT_CASCADE', { charId: id });
}


// ============================================================================
// SECTION 6 — STORAGE: ENTITY DATA
// All reads and writes for EntityRecord and the entity index.
// Entities are the world graph: locations, items, narrative nodes, character bodies.
// ============================================================================

// ── Index (EntityMeta[]) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function loadEntityIndex(): Promise<EntityMeta[]> {
    return await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_ENTITY_INDEX, []) as EntityMeta[];
}

async function saveEntityIndex(idx: EntityMeta[]): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_ENTITY_INDEX, idx.filter(Boolean));
}

// ── Migration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// migrateEntity runs every time an EntityRecord is loaded from storage.
// Handles all legacy field shapes from prior engine versions.

function migrateEntity(raw: any): EntityRecord {
    const ent = raw as any;
    if (!ent.id) ent.id = generateId();
    if (!ent.name) ent.name = 'Unnamed Entity';

    // Force legacy spatial sub-types into the current functional type system
    if (!ent.type || ['interior', 'exterior', 'transit', 'abstract'].includes(ent.type)) {
        ent.type = 'location';
    }
    // Migrate legacy shop type → narrative
    if (ent.type === 'shop') ent.type = 'narrative';

    if (ent.narrativeParentId === undefined) ent.narrativeParentId = null;
    if (ent.parentId === undefined) ent.parentId = null;
    if (!ent.storyState) ent.storyState = 'locked';

    // [v0.9.25 E4] Encounter migration removed; delete stale field from old saves
    delete (ent as any).encounters;

    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] rules/skillBonuses/isVehicle field guards and the
    // migrateEntityAction() flat-action migration removed — dead Actions remnants.

    if (!ent.description) ent.description = '';
    if (!ent.atmosphere) ent.atmosphere = '';
    if (!ent.state) ent.state = 'default';
    if (!ent.stateDescriptions) ent.stateDescriptions = {};

    // Migrate old connection array key to current key
    ent.connectedEntityIds = ent.connectedEntityIds || ent.connectedLocationIds || [];
    delete ent.connectedLocationIds;

    if (!ent.presentCharacterIds) ent.presentCharacterIds = [];

    // Remove legacy commerce fields
    delete ent.price;
    if (!ent.inventoryIds) ent.inventoryIds = [];

    if (!ent.notes) ent.notes = '';
    if (!ent.lorebookTags) ent.lorebookTags = [];
    if (ent.lorebookText === undefined) ent.lorebookText = '';
    if (ent.locationVisuals === undefined) ent.locationVisuals = '';
    if (!ent.tagSubscriptions) ent.tagSubscriptions = [];
    if (!ent.createdAt) ent.createdAt = Date.now();
    if (!ent.updatedAt) ent.updatedAt = Date.now();

    // Filter null entries in all arrays for safety
    ent.connectedEntityIds  = ent.connectedEntityIds.filter(Boolean);
    ent.presentCharacterIds = ent.presentCharacterIds.filter(Boolean);

    return ent as EntityRecord;
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] migrateEntityAction() removed via brace-matched deletion.

// ── Load ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function loadEntity(id: string): Promise<EntityRecord | null> {
    const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_ENTITY_PFX + id, null);
    return raw ? migrateEntity(raw) : null;
}

// ── Save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function saveEntity(ent: EntityRecord): Promise<void> {
    ent.updatedAt = Date.now();
    // [v0.9.24 E3] Auto-clear tracking when a narrative is completed.
    if (ent.type === 'narrative' && ent.storyState === 'completed') {
        try {
            let _trackedId = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_TRACKED_NARRATIVE, '') as string;
            if (_trackedId === ent.id) {
                await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_TRACKED_NARRATIVE, '');
                api.v1.ui.toast('📍 Tracking cleared — narrative completed.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            }
        } catch (_e3) { /* non-fatal */ }
    }
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_ENTITY_PFX + ent.id, ent);

    const idx = await loadEntityIndex();
    const meta: EntityMeta = {
        id: ent.id,
        name: ent.name,
        type: ent.type,
        parentId: ent.parentId,
        narrativeParentId: ent.narrativeParentId,
        storyState: ent.storyState,
        childCount: idx.filter(function(m) { return m.parentId === ent.id; }).length,
        presentCharacterCount: ent.presentCharacterIds.filter(Boolean).length,
        state: ent.state,
        icon: ent.icon || undefined
        // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] gated/isVehicle/hasGlobalAutoTrigger removed — dead Actions/Travel remnants.
    };

    const i = idx.findIndex(function(m) { return m.id === ent.id; });
    if (i >= 0) { idx[i] = meta; } else { idx.push(meta); }
    await saveEntityIndex(idx);

    // [CHANGE] Queue a debounced scene state broadcast. Any entity change — location
    // state, available actions, connection changes — may affect the HUD. Debouncing
    // at 200ms coalesces the storm of saves during bulk operations like imports.
    // Defined in Section 18. No-ops if bridge is not yet connected.
    queueSceneStateBroadcast();
}

// [UNIFY] migrateFactionArgsOnEntities removed - Faction standing actions are obsolete.

// ── Delete ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function deleteEntity(id: string): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.remove(STORAGE_ENTITY_PFX + id);
    const idx = await loadEntityIndex();

    // Collect orphaned children BEFORE removing the entry from the index,
    // since we need their parentId reference while it still points to the deleted entity.
    const orphaned = idx.filter(function(m) { return m.parentId === id; });

    await saveEntityIndex(idx.filter(function(m) { return m.id !== id; }));

    // Re-parent any child entities to the world root (null parent).
    // [CHANGE] Fixes a pre-existing dead-code bug: the original code checked
    // CharacterRecord.currentLocationId which is never stored on that type
    // (it is derived and only stored in CharacterMeta). Now correctly iterates
    // child entities via their parentId relationship in the entity index.
    for (const m of orphaned) {
        try {
            const child = await loadEntity(m.id);
            if (child) { child.parentId = null; await saveEntity(child); }
        } catch (ignoredErr) { /* non-fatal */ }
    }
}

// ── Copy ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function copyEntity(id: string, deep: boolean = false): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const src = await loadEntity(id);
        if (!src) return;

        const newId = generateId();
        const copy: EntityRecord = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(src));

        copy.id = newId;
        copy.name = 'Copy of ' + src.name;
        copy.createdAt = Date.now();
        copy.updatedAt = Date.now();

        // Clear runtime state — copy starts fresh, not carrying over scene presence
        copy.connectedEntityIds = [];
        copy.presentCharacterIds = [];

        // [v0.9.25 E4] Encounter range copy removed
        if (false && (copy as any).encounters) {
            (copy as any).encounters.ranges = ((copy as any).encounters.ranges || []).map(function(r) {
                return Object.assign({}, r, { id: generateId(), triggered: false });
            });
        }

        await saveEntity(copy);

        if (deep) {
            const allMeta = await loadEntityIndex();
            const children = allMeta.filter(function(m) { return m.parentId === src.id; });

            for (const childMeta of children) {
                const childSrc = await loadEntity(childMeta.id);
                if (!childSrc) continue;

                const childCopy: EntityRecord = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(childSrc));
                childCopy.id = generateId();
                childCopy.name = childSrc.name;
                childCopy.parentId = newId;
                childCopy.createdAt = Date.now();
                childCopy.updatedAt = Date.now();
                childCopy.connectedEntityIds = [];
                childCopy.presentCharacterIds = [];
                if (false && (childCopy as any).encounters) {
                    (childCopy as any).encounters.ranges = ((childCopy as any).encounters.ranges || []).map(function(r) {
                        return Object.assign({}, r, { id: generateId(), triggered: false });
                    });
                }
                await saveEntity(childCopy);
            }

            api.v1.ui.toast('Copied "' + src.name + '" with ' + children.length + ' child(ren).', { type: 'success' });
        } else {
            api.v1.ui.toast('Copied "' + src.name + '".', { type: 'success' });
        }

        await refreshSidebar();
        await openEntityEditModal(newId);
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] copyEntity:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Copy failed.', { type: 'error' });
    }
}

// ── History (recent location breadcrumb) ─────────────────────────────────────

async function loadEntityHistory(): Promise<string[]> {
    return await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_ENTITY_HISTORY, []) as string[];
}

async function saveEntityHistory(ids: string[]): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_ENTITY_HISTORY, ids.slice(0, 10));
}


// ============================================================================
// SECTION 7 — STORAGE: GLOBAL CONFIG
// Single GlobalConfig record per story. Governs engine intervals, field labels,
// resources, factions, skill modifiers, and generation style.
// ============================================================================

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] DEFAULT_TIME_BANDS removed — Clock/Calendar cut.

function makeDefaultConfig(): GlobalConfig {
    return {
        contextTemplates: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_TEMPLATES)),
        lorebookTemplates: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_TEMPLATES)),  // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS]
        lorebookFields: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_FIELDS)),  // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD]
        interfaceMode: 'native',   // [v0.9.9.6] default to the native HUD; bridge is opt-in
        thoughtIntervalGens: 2,    // [v0.9.24 E1] ephemeral every 2 generations
        dynamicIntervalGens: 4,    // mid-range every 4 generations
        arcIntervalGens: 10,       // arc/relationship every 10 generations
        hookIntervalGens: 6,       // hooks every 6 generations
        dynamicIntervalMode: 'gens', // [v2.54.0] default cadence unit (per-generation)
        thoughtIntervalTokens: 500,  // [v2.54.0] token-mode intervals — scaled ~250 tok/gen from the *Gens above
        dynamicIntervalTokens: 1000, // mid-range default per user ("Defaults = 1000")
        arcIntervalTokens: 2500,
        hookIntervalTokens: 1500,
        totalGenerations: 0,
        ephemeralCap: 3,
        maxDynamicCharacters: 0,   // [vNEXT] 0 = no cap (all present dynamic chars process)
        sheetCategories: [], // [PATCH] DEFAULT_SHEET_CATEGORIES removed; full Sheet Categories cut deferred to its own pass.
        autoUpdateDefault: true,
        autoThoughtsDefault: true,
        showFieldHeaders: true,
        generalStyle: DEFAULT_GENERAL_STYLE,
        fieldLabels: {},
        fieldInstructions: {},
        fieldBudgets: {},
        customStaticFields: [],
        relationshipArchetypes: [...DEFAULT_REL_ARCHETYPES],
        conditions: [...DEFAULT_CONDITIONS],  // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS]
        species: [],
        factions: [],
        projectName: '',
        projectGenre: '',
        projectConcept: '',
        wizardSections: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_WIZARD_SECTIONS)),
        defaultBackgroundUrl: '',
        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] time* defaults removed — Clock/Calendar cut.
    };
}

// [UNIFY] Action/Rules system removed - Faction standing and rule-arg repair helpers removed.


function migrateConfig(raw: any): GlobalConfig {
    // Merges raw saved data over defaults so new fields always have values.
    // Strips deprecated keys so old saves don't carry stale data.
    const d = makeDefaultConfig();
    const c = { ...d, ...raw };

    // Deprecated field removal
    delete c.storyRolePresets;
    delete c.conditionPresets;
    delete (c as any).defaultBudgetCap;
    delete (c as any).globalRules;
    delete (c as any).arcTypePresets; // Purge old config properties

    // Structural guards
    // [REMOVED v2.30.0, per user] Was: [HOLDING] Seed the baked-in default player
    // faction (idempotent by id). This existed to give the Holding system's roster a
    // default source — but Holding Document authoring + the player-faction picker
    // were already CE-STRIPped out (see buildFactionsWorkspace's own removal note,
    // "[CE-STRIP v2.0.x] Holding Document authoring + player-faction picker removed").
    // Nothing has read DEFAULT_PLAYER_FACTION_ID except this seed since that strip —
    // verified zero remaining callers before deleting both the seed and the constant
    // (grep DEFAULT_PLAYER_FACTION_ID / 'fac_player' turns up nothing else). A fresh
    // config no longer gets an uneditable "The Player" faction it never asked for;
    // existing saves that already have one are UNTOUCHED (this only ever ran on
    // migration, never as a delete pass) — remove it by hand via the Factions Manager
    // if you don't want it.
    if (!c.factions) c.factions = [];
    if (!c.relationshipArchetypes) c.relationshipArchetypes = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_REL_ARCHETYPES));
    // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] Old saves get the default set seeded once; existing saves
    // that already have SOME conditions (even a custom, edited list) are left alone —
    // same non-destructive pattern as relationshipArchetypes above.
    if (!c.conditions) c.conditions = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_CONDITIONS));
    if (!c.lorebookTemplates) c.lorebookTemplates = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_TEMPLATES));
    else {
        // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] backfill every lorebook template key so old saves gain the
        // new equipment/relationship/mid-range lines without clobbering customised ones.
        for (const _ltk of Object.keys(DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_TEMPLATES)) {
            if (!(c.lorebookTemplates as any)[_ltk]) (c.lorebookTemplates as any)[_ltk] = (DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_TEMPLATES as any)[_ltk];
        }
        // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] Reshape prior-default templates whose wording changed this
        // pass — only when still byte-identical to the old default (never clobber edits).
        for (const _mk of Object.keys(LOREBOOK_TEMPLATE_MIGRATIONS_V214)) {
            const _mig = LOREBOOK_TEMPLATE_MIGRATIONS_V214[_mk];
            if ((c.lorebookTemplates as any)[_mk] === _mig.old) (c.lorebookTemplates as any)[_mk] = _mig.neu;
        }
    }
    // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Per-field include switches — default any missing key to on.
    if (!c.lorebookFields) c.lorebookFields = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_FIELDS));
    else {
        for (const _lfk of Object.keys(DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_FIELDS)) {
            if ((c.lorebookFields as any)[_lfk] === undefined) (c.lorebookFields as any)[_lfk] = (DEFAULT_LOREBOOK_FIELDS as any)[_lfk];
        }
    }
    // Migrate legacy string[] archetypes → RelationshipArchetypeDef[]
    if (Array.isArray(c.relationshipArchetypes) && c.relationshipArchetypes.length > 0 && typeof c.relationshipArchetypes[0] === 'string') {
        c.relationshipArchetypes = (c.relationshipArchetypes as any[]).map(function(s: any) {
            return { id: 'arch_' + String(s).toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '_').slice(0, 24) + '_' + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 6), name: String(s), stages: [] };
        });
    }
    if (!c.customStaticFields)     c.customStaticFields = [];
    if (!c.fieldLabels)            c.fieldLabels = {};
    if (!c.fieldInstructions)      c.fieldInstructions = {};
    if (!c.fieldBudgets)           c.fieldBudgets = {};
    if (!c.sheetCategories)        c.sheetCategories = [];
    if (c.ephemeralCap === undefined) c.ephemeralCap = 3;

    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] Global/party resources migration removed — Resources cut.

    // v0.9.3 fields
    if (!c.species)  c.species  = [];
    if (!c.factions) c.factions = [];

    // [v2.54.0] Dynamic-update cadence unit + intervals. From v0.9.24 through v2.53.x the
    // token-interval fields were DELETED here on every load (per-generation cadence had
    // replaced per-token). They are now reintroduced as an OPT-IN alternative unit, so the
    // deletes are gone — stripping them would wipe the user's token settings every load. A
    // stale token value on a pre-v0.9.24 save is harmless: it is only consulted when
    // dynamicIntervalMode === 'tokens', which those old saves never set, and the defaults
    // below only fill a MISSING field, never overwrite a real one.
    if ((c as any).dynamicIntervalMode !== 'tokens' && (c as any).dynamicIntervalMode !== 'gens') (c as any).dynamicIntervalMode = 'gens';
    if (!(c as any).thoughtIntervalTokens) (c as any).thoughtIntervalTokens = 500;
    if (!(c as any).dynamicIntervalTokens) (c as any).dynamicIntervalTokens = 1000;
    if (!(c as any).arcIntervalTokens)     (c as any).arcIntervalTokens     = 2500;
    if (!(c as any).hookIntervalTokens)    (c as any).hookIntervalTokens    = 1500;
    // Ensure gen-interval fields exist (new saves via makeDefaultConfig will have them)
    if (!c.thoughtIntervalGens) c.thoughtIntervalGens = 2;
    if (!c.dynamicIntervalGens) c.dynamicIntervalGens = 4;
    if (!c.arcIntervalGens)     c.arcIntervalGens     = 10;
    if (!c.hookIntervalGens)    c.hookIntervalGens    = 6;
    if (c.maxDynamicCharacters === undefined) c.maxDynamicCharacters = 0;  // [vNEXT] no cap by default
    if (c.totalGenerations === undefined) c.totalGenerations = 0;
    if (c.defaultBackgroundUrl === undefined) c.defaultBackgroundUrl = ''; // [v0.9.40]
    if (!c.contextTemplates) c.contextTemplates = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_TEMPLATES));
    if (typeof (c as any).interfaceMode !== 'string') (c as any).interfaceMode = 'native';  // [v0.9.9.6]
    // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] The old v0.9.8.4 template-upgrade loop (location/
    // trackedNarrative/player/npc) is gone along with those keys. A save from before
    // this pass still has a contextTemplates object, just missing the new
    // criticalHook key — backfill it so old saves don't render a blank hook line.
    // Any orphaned location/player/npcWeb/trackedNarrative/activeNarrative string
    // values left on old saves are harmless — unread by anything now.
    if (c.contextTemplates && !(c.contextTemplates as any).criticalHook) {
        (c.contextTemplates as any).criticalHook = DEFAULT_TEMPLATES.criticalHook;
    }
    // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Backfill the re-added NPC-web AN template, and migrate the
    // pre-LOREFOLD fat npc template to thoughts-only so relationship/goal/conditions
    // are not injected in BOTH the AN and the lorebook. Hand-authored npc strings
    // (anything other than the known old default) are preserved untouched.
    if (c.contextTemplates && !(c.contextTemplates as any).npcWeb) {
        (c.contextTemplates as any).npcWeb = DEFAULT_TEMPLATES.npcWeb;
    }
    if (c.contextTemplates && (c.contextTemplates as any).npc === OLD_NPC_TEMPLATE_V28) {
        (c.contextTemplates as any).npc = DEFAULT_TEMPLATES.npc;
    }
    
    // [vNEXT] Seed the World-Build Wizard's Memory section list and concept fields.
    if (!c.wizardSections) {
        c.wizardSections = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_WIZARD_SECTIONS));
    }
    if (c.projectName === undefined) c.projectName = '';
    if (c.projectGenre === undefined) c.projectGenre = '';
    if (c.projectConcept === undefined) c.projectConcept = '';

    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] time* migration-fill removed — Clock/Calendar cut.

    return c as GlobalConfig;
}

async function loadConfig(): Promise<GlobalConfig> {
    const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_CONFIG, null);
    // [v0.9.37 SEED1] Run migrateConfig on the DEFAULT too, so a brand-new story is
    // seeded with sys_default_travel / sys_return_travel. Previously makeDefaultConfig
    // bypassed migrateConfig, leaving fresh stories with no travel templates until the
    // action editor happened to save a config -> travel silently did nothing.
    return migrateConfig(raw ? raw : makeDefaultConfig());
}

async function saveConfig(cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CONFIG, cfg);
}

// ── Relationship archetype helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────
function findArchetypeDef(cfg: GlobalConfig, rel: PrimaryRelationship | undefined): RelationshipArchetypeDef | null {
    if (!rel) return null;
    let defs = (cfg.relationshipArchetypes || []) as RelationshipArchetypeDef[];
    // Prefer explicit ID; fall back to name match (lazy migration for old saves).
    if (rel.archetypeId) {
        let byId = defs.find(function(d) { return d.id === rel.archetypeId; });
        if (byId) return byId;
    }
    if (rel.archetype) {
        let byName = defs.find(function(d) { return d.name.toLowerCase() === String(rel.archetype).toLowerCase(); });
        if (byName) return byName;
    }
    return null;
}

// Returns the current stage label for a relationship, or '' if no stages defined.
function resolveArchetypeStage(cfg: GlobalConfig, rel: PrimaryRelationship | undefined): string {
    let def = findArchetypeDef(cfg, rel);
    if (!def || !def.stages || !def.stages.length) return '';
    let idx = (rel && typeof rel.stageIndex === 'number') ? rel.stageIndex : 0;
    idx = Math.max(0, Math.min(def.stages.length - 1, idx));
    return def.stages[idx] || '';
}

// ── Unified search picker ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Any picker with potentially >5 options should use this. Renders a live search
// box that re-filters as the user types. items: { id, label }[]. onSelect gets the
// chosen id. Auto-omits the search box when there are 5 or fewer items.
interface PickItem { id: string; label: string; selected?: boolean; }
async function openSearchPicker(title: string, items: PickItem[], onSelect: (id: string) => Promise<void>, extraTop?: UIPart[]): Promise<void> {
    let searchKey = 'temp:ce_uipick_' + title.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '_').slice(0, 24);
    await api.v1.tempStorage.set(searchKey, '');
    let showSearch = items.length > 5;
    let pmP: any = null;
    async function draw() {
        let term = ((await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(searchKey, '')) as string).toLowerCase();
        let filtered = (showSearch && term) ? items.filter(function(it) { return it.label.toLowerCase().includes(term); }) : items;
        let content: UIPart[] = [];
        if (extraTop && extraTop.length) { for (const e of extraTop) content.push(e); }
        if (showSearch) {
            content.push({ type: 'textInput', placeholder: '\ud83d\udd0d Search\u2026', initialValue: term, onChange: async function(v: string) { await api.v1.tempStorage.set(searchKey, v); await draw(); } } as UIPart);
        }
        for (const it of filtered) {
            content.push({ type: 'button', text: it.label, style: { width: '100%', marginBottom: '4px', textAlign: 'left', backgroundColor: it.selected ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.2)' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.02)', color: it.selected ? '#7ec8a0' : undefined }, callback: async function() { if (pmP) pmP.close(); await onSelect(it.id); } } as UIPart);
        }
        if (!filtered.length) content.push({ type: 'text', text: 'No matches.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.5' } } as UIPart);
        if (pmP && !pmP.isClosed()) { await pmP.update({ content: content }); }
        else { pmP = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ce-search-picker', title: title, size: 'small', content: content }); }
    }
    await draw();
}


// ============================================================================
// SECTION 8 — STORAGE: PARTY STATE, NPC WEB, QUICK ACTION PRESETS
// Three independent storyStorage records that don't fit in the character or
// entity systems but are story-persistent.
// ============================================================================


// ── NPC Web ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// The bilateral dynamic relationship graph between NPCs (WebLink[]).
// Stored as a JSON string because it can grow large and benefits from direct
// string serialization rather than NAI's object storage path.

async function loadNpcWeb(): Promise<WebLink[]> {
    const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.get(STORAGE_NPC_WEB) as string;
    try { return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []; } catch { return []; }
}

async function saveNpcWeb(web: WebLink[]): Promise<void> {
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_NPC_WEB, JSON.stringify(web));
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 9 — STORAGE: IMAGE SETTINGS
// Image generation presets and global image settings.
// Stored in api.v1.storage (global, not story-bound) so they persist across
// stories without being part of the adventure module export.
// ============================================================================

// NOTE FOR ASSEMBLY: EVT_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC = 'AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC' was missed from
// Section 2 and should be added to the Bridge Event Name Constants block there.
// Used by saveImageSettings() below. [v2.13.0] Replaces the old PRESET_SYNC /
// AE_ALL_PRESETS_SYNC pair — one message carries the whole field library plus the
// default dimensions/scale/steps, since there's no more single "active preset".

async function loadImageSettings(): Promise<void> {
    // [CHANGE] Renamed from loadSettings() to clarify scope.
    // [CHANGE] Removed tempStorage mirroring (the 20 img_p*_steps/scale/etc. writes).
    //   Those keys only fed the now-removed AI engine NAI modal. Settings live
    //   in-memory (imagePromptFields[], globalSettings) for the engine and are
    //   broadcast to the bridge via saveImageSettings() → AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC.
    // [CHANGE] Removed cachedCharConfigs load from old STORAGE_KEY_CHAR_CONFIGS key.
    //   Character visual data is now seeded by buildCharacterDataPayload() in
    //   onScriptsLoaded (Section 27) and kept current by saveCharacter() calls.
    // [v2.13.0 IMGFIELDS] No preset migration — starts empty on a fresh install
    // (user's explicit call), replacing the old 5-slot generateDefaultPresets().

    const sf = await api.v1.storage.get(STORAGE_KEY_PROMPT_FIELDS);
    imagePromptFields = Array.isArray(sf) ? sf : [];

    const sg = await api.v1.storage.get(STORAGE_KEY_GLOBAL);
    if (sg) globalSettings = Object.assign({}, globalSettings, sg);

    // Guard every globalSettings field so partial saves from older versions work
    if (globalSettings.contextSize        === undefined) globalSettings.contextSize        = DEFAULT_CTX;
    if (!globalSettings.architectPrompt)                  globalSettings.architectPrompt   = DEFAULT_ARCH;
    if (globalSettings.architectAutoSnap   === undefined) globalSettings.architectAutoSnap   = true;
    if (globalSettings.autoDownload        === undefined) globalSettings.autoDownload        = false;
    if (globalSettings.defaultWidth        === undefined) globalSettings.defaultWidth        = 832;
    if (globalSettings.defaultHeight       === undefined) globalSettings.defaultHeight       = 1216;
    if (globalSettings.defaultScale        === undefined) globalSettings.defaultScale        = 5.0;
    if (globalSettings.defaultSteps        === undefined) globalSettings.defaultSteps        = 28;
}

async function saveImageSettings(): Promise<void> {
    // [CHANGE] Renamed from saveSettings() to clarify scope.
    await api.v1.storage.set(STORAGE_KEY_PROMPT_FIELDS, imagePromptFields);
    await api.v1.storage.set(STORAGE_KEY_GLOBAL,        globalSettings);

    // Keep bridge in sync with the field library + defaults so the Image Studio
    // widget and avatar/snapshot generation pick up changes immediately.
    if (isBridgeReady) {
        try {
            await broadcastToTM('AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC', {
                fields: imagePromptFields,
                defaults: { width: globalSettings.defaultWidth, height: globalSettings.defaultHeight, scale: globalSettings.defaultScale, steps: globalSettings.defaultSteps }
            });
        } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] saveImageSettings: AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC failed:', e); }
    }
}

// [v2.13.0 IMGFIELDS] Compiles every currently-ACTIVE field into the final
// quality/negative prompt strings. A field's weight only wraps its contribution
// in NAI's N::text:: syntax when weight != 1 (unweighted fields stay plain text,
// keeping the compiled result readable). Same compiled pair feeds every
// generation path (Auto-Snap, manual Director's Cut, Snapshot) — there's no
// per-generation selection step; "active" is a standing toggle per field.
function compileActiveImageFields(): { quality: string; negative: string } {
    const qualityParts: string[] = [];
    const negativeParts: string[] = [];
    for (const f of (imagePromptFields || [])) {
        if (!f.active) continue;
        const w = (typeof f.weight === 'number' && f.weight > 0) ? Math.max(0.1, Math.min(10, f.weight)) : 1;
        const bp = (f.basePrompt || '').trim();
        const uc = (f.uc || '').trim();
        if (bp) qualityParts.push(w !== 1 ? (w + '::' + bp + '::') : bp);
        if (uc) negativeParts.push(w !== 1 ? (w + '::' + uc + '::') : uc);
    }
    return { quality: qualityParts.join(', '), negative: negativeParts.join(', ') };
}

async function restoreImageDefaults(): Promise<void> {
    // [CHANGE] Renamed from restoreDefaults() to clarify scope.
    // [CHANGE] Simplified: removed modal.close() and renderModal() calls since
    //   the Director's Cut NAI modal no longer exists. Settings live in the
    //   AE config modal (Section 22) which uses its own refresh pattern.
    globalSettings.contextSize     = DEFAULT_CTX;
    globalSettings.architectPrompt = DEFAULT_ARCH;
    await saveImageSettings();
    api.v1.ui.toast('🔄 Image defaults restored.');
}
// ============================================================================
// SECTION 10 — LOREBOOK HELPERS
// Functions that build, create, and sync NAI lorebook entries for characters,
// factions, and species. Lorebook entries are how static character profiles
// get injected into context via NAI's native keyword-matching system.
//
// PATTERN NOTE: All three record types (character, faction, species) share an
// identical four-function family: getKeys / buildContent / createLinked / sync.
// This is a future candidate for a generalized syncLorebookRecord<T>(spec) helper
// once the refactor stabilizes. Not changing now — the calls are simple enough
// that the abstraction would add indirection without immediate benefit.
// ============================================================================

// ── Character Lorebook ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function getLorebookKeys(char: CharacterRecord): string[] {
    // Returns the name + all nicknames as lorebook trigger keys.
    return [char.name, ...char.nicknames.filter(Boolean)].filter(Boolean);
}

async function buildLorebookContent(char: CharacterRecord): Promise<string> {
    // Assembles the lorebook entry text from a character's static fields.
    // showFieldHeaders controls whether field labels are prepended.
    // This is the single authoritative builder — used by both create and sync.
    const cfg = await loadConfig();
    const showHeaders = cfg.showFieldHeaders;
    const lines: string[] = [];

    function addField(label: string, value: string | undefined | null) {
        if (!value || value.trim() === '') return;
        lines.push(showHeaders ? `${label}: ${value.trim()}` : value.trim());
    }

    // [v0.9.8.5] Identity line — collapsed from five separate Name/Age/Species/
    // Gender/Role fields into a single dossier header, e.g. "Gareth — human male,
    // 34, blacksmith". Header-agnostic by design (it reads as a header itself).
    let idLeader = [char.species, char.gender].map(function(s) { return (s || '').trim(); }).filter(Boolean).join(' ');
    let idMid = [idLeader, (char.age || '').trim(), (char.occupationRole || '').trim()].filter(Boolean).join(', ');
    let identityLine = (char.name || '').trim();
    if (identityLine && idMid) identityLine += ' \u2014 ' + idMid;
    else if (!identityLine && idMid) identityLine = idMid;
    if (identityLine) lines.push(identityLine);

    // Core static fields — label keys are customisable via cfg.fieldLabels
    addField(cfg.fieldLabels['physicalDescription'] || 'Physical Description', char.physicalDescription);
    addField(cfg.fieldLabels['personalityCore']     || 'Personality Core',     char.personalityCore);
    addField(cfg.fieldLabels['voiceAndMannerisms']  || 'Voice & Mannerisms',   char.voiceAndMannerisms);
    addField(cfg.fieldLabels['background']          || 'Background',           char.background);

    // Core values
    if (char.coreValues && char.coreValues.length > 0) {
        addField('Core Values', char.coreValues.filter(Boolean).join(', '));
    }

    // Author-defined custom static fields
    if (cfg.customStaticFields && char.customFieldValues) {
        for (const cf of cfg.customStaticFields) {
            addField(cf.label, char.customFieldValues[cf.id]);
        }
    }

    // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Equipment (and the rest of the dynamic layer) is NOT in this
    // static builder. buildCharDynamicBlock() produces it fresh each generation and
    // buildSceneLorebookActivation() appends it onto this entry's content per-build, so
    // the STORED entry stays static identity (equip/unequip triggers no lorebook re-sync).
    return lines.join('\n');
}

// [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] ── Dynamic Lorebook (narrative prose, NOT the wiki-style
// dossier above) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Deliberately a separate builder from buildLorebookContent: that one is static
// identity (labeled fields, content-stable, cache-friendly); this one is dynamic
// state (conditions today), rebuilt fresh every generation like equipment, and
// reads as prose rather than a field list per user's explicit call.

// Resolves one active condition to its narrative line, or '' if the def was
// deleted out from under an active state (orphaned reference — skip gracefully,
// don't throw) or the line template itself resolves empty.
function buildConditionLine(char: CharacterRecord, def: ConditionDef, stageIndex: number, cfg: GlobalConfig): string {
    let stageText = '';
    if (def.stages && def.stages.length > 0) {
        let idx = Math.max(0, Math.min(def.stages.length - 1, stageIndex || 0));
        stageText = def.stages[idx] || '';
    }
    const data = { name: char.name, conditionName: def.name, stageText: stageText };
    return parseMacroTemplate(cfg.lorebookTemplates.condition, data).trim();
}

// One line per active condition (user's call — not combined into one sentence).
// Returns '' if the character has no active conditions (or none resolve to text),
// so callers can skip emitting an empty temp lorebook entry.
async function buildDynamicLorebookContent(char: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<string> {
    const lines: string[] = [];
    const defs = cfg.conditions || [];
    for (const state of (char.activeConditions || [])) {
        const def = defs.find(function(d) { return d.id === state.defId; });
        if (!def) continue;
        const line = buildConditionLine(char, def, state.stageIndex, cfg);
        if (line) lines.push(line);
    }
    return lines.join('\n');
}

// [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Composite dynamic block folded into a character's lorebook entry:
// equipment + conditions + relationship + mid-range. Each line is gated by
// cfg.lorebookFields and rendered as prose via cfg.lorebookTemplates. Returns '' when
// nothing is enabled/populated so callers can skip an empty append. Rebuilt fresh every
// generation (state, not identity) — same cadence the old ae_equip_/ae_dynamic_ temp
// entries had, just consolidated into one entry so ordering is deterministic.
async function buildCharDynamicBlock(char: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<string> {
    const lines: string[] = [];
    const tpl = cfg.lorebookTemplates || ({} as LorebookTemplates);
    const inc = cfg.lorebookFields || ({} as LorebookFieldToggles);
    const data = Object.assign({}, char, buildCharMacroExtras(char, cfg));

    // Equipment — resolve each worn item to "name (detail)".
    if (inc.equipment && char.equippedItemIds && char.equippedItemIds.length > 0) {
        const worn: string[] = [];
        for (const itemId of char.equippedItemIds) {
            try {
                const item = await loadEntity(itemId);
                if (!item) continue;
                const detail = (item.description || '').trim() || ((item as any).atmosphere || '').trim();
                worn.push(detail ? (item.name + ' (' + detail + ')') : item.name);
            } catch (eItem) { /* non-fatal: skip unreadable item */ }
        }
        if (worn.length > 0) {
            const line = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.equipment, Object.assign({}, data, { equipList: worn.join(', ') })).trim();
            if (line) lines.push(line);
        }
    }

    // Conditions — one line per active condition (reuses the existing builder).
    if (inc.conditions) {
        const condBlock = await buildDynamicLorebookContent(char, cfg);
        if (condBlock.trim()) lines.push(condBlock.trim());
    }

    // Relationship — only when the primary relationship is enabled and has a target.
    if (char.primaryRelationship && char.primaryRelationship.enabled && char.primaryRelationship.targetName) {
        if (inc.relationship)  { const l = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.relationship,  data).trim(); if (l) lines.push(l); }
        if (inc.relTrajectory) { const l = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.relTrajectory, data).trim(); if (l) lines.push(l); }
        if (inc.relStatus)     { const l = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.relStatus,     data).trim(); if (l) lines.push(l); }
    }

    // Mid-range psychological state.
    if (inc.goal)        { const l = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.goal,        data).trim(); if (l) lines.push(l); }
    if (inc.socialMask)  { const l = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.socialMask,  data).trim(); if (l) lines.push(l); }
    if (inc.shiftedView) { const l = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.shiftedView, data).trim(); if (l) lines.push(l); }

    // Journal — [v2.12.0 CHARJOURNAL] this character's own running notes, folded in
    // exactly like conditions: active (non-archived) bubbles only, joined as prose.
    // Unbounded on purpose (user's call) — archiving/merge-down are the user's own
    // tools for keeping this from growing unbounded, same as the main journal.
    if (inc.journal) {
        const jState = await loadCharJournal(char.id);
        const activeBubbles = (jState.bubbles || []).filter(function(b) { return !b.archived; });
        const journalBody = activeBubbles.map(function(b) { return (b.text || '').trim(); }).filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');
        if (journalBody) {
            const line = parseMacroTemplate(tpl.journal, Object.assign({}, data, { journalBody: journalBody })).trim();
            if (line) lines.push(line);
        }
    }

    return lines.join('\n');
}

// [v2.55.0 CHAR-CAT] Character lorebook entries were the one entry family created with no
// category (faction/species/entity families already file under a named one — see
// ensureLorebookCategory below, the shared find-or-create helper this reuses as-is). Own
// name, distinct from the entity system's "AE Characters" (entityLorebookCategoryName) —
// that family covers EntityRecord-typed characters (a different record shape from
// CharacterRecord), so the two are deliberately not merged into one category.
var CHAR_CAT_NAME = 'CE Characters';

async function createLinkedLorebookEntry(charId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    const char = await loadCharacter(charId);
    if (!char) return;
    try {
        const content  = await buildLorebookContent(char);
        const keys     = getLorebookKeys(char);
        const catId    = await ensureLorebookCategory(CHAR_CAT_NAME);
        const entrySpec: any = { displayName: char.name, text: content, keys, enabled: true };
        if (catId) entrySpec.category = catId;
        const entryId  = await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry(entrySpec);
        char.lorebookEntryId = entryId;
        await saveCharacter(char);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast(`Lorebook entry created for ${char.name}.`, { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) {
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to create lorebook entry.', { type: 'error' });
        if (silent) throw e;
    }
}

async function syncLorebookEntry(charId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    const char = await loadCharacter(charId);
    if (!char?.lorebookEntryId) return;
    try {
        const content = await buildLorebookContent(char);
        // [v2.55.0 CHAR-CAT] Re-stamp category on every sync too, not just at creation —
        // entries made before this patch have none, and this is the cheapest place to
        // backfill them without a one-off migration pass.
        const catId = await ensureLorebookCategory(CHAR_CAT_NAME);
        const updateSpec: any = {
            text: content,
            keys: getLorebookKeys(char),
            displayName: char.name
        };
        if (catId) updateSpec.category = catId;
        await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(char.lorebookEntryId, updateSpec);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Lorebook entry synced.', { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] syncLorebookEntry:', e);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to sync lorebook entry.', { type: 'error' });
    }
}

// [v0.9.8.5] Builds the per-generation lorebook activation package for the current
// scene. For every active, scene-present character (and the protagonist):
//   - permanent entry present  -> force-activate it ([id, {active:true}])
//   - permanent entry absent    -> emit its static identity as an ephemeral temp
//                                  entry (no permanent lorebook needed, no regression)
//   - equipped items present    -> emit a separate ephemeral equipment temp entry
// Returned from onBeforeContextBuild so static identity lives in the lorebook block
// while the Author's Note carries only the dynamic layer.
// [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] paramLoreEntries is onBeforeContextBuild's params.lorebookEntries
// ([id, {active, content}][]) — used to APPEND the dynamic block onto a linked entry's
// CURRENT content (preserving any hand-edits) rather than rebuilding identity. Folding
// equipment + conditions + relationship + mid-range into the one character entry (per
// user's call) makes ordering deterministic: the dynamic layer can no longer land as a
// stray temp entry before its own character (the old ae_equip_/ae_dynamic_ ordering bug).
async function buildSceneLorebookActivation(paramLoreEntries?: any[]): Promise<{ lorebookEntries: any[]; temporaryLorebookEntries: any[] }> {
    const lorebookEntries: any[] = [];
    const temporaryLorebookEntries: any[] = [];
    try {
        const slaCfg = await loadConfig();
        const slaIdx = await loadIndex();
        const slaLoreMap = new Map<string, string>();
        for (const _pt of (paramLoreEntries || [])) {
            if (Array.isArray(_pt) && _pt.length >= 2 && _pt[0]) slaLoreMap.set(_pt[0], (_pt[1] && _pt[1].content) || '');
        }
        for (const meta of slaIdx.filter(Boolean)) {
            if (!meta.active) continue;
            if (!meta.scenePresent && !meta.isProtagonist) continue;
            const slaChar = await loadCharacter(meta.id);
            if (!slaChar) continue;

            // The full dynamic layer for this character, as one prose block.
            const slaDyn = (await buildCharDynamicBlock(slaChar, slaCfg)).trim();

            if (slaChar.lorebookEntryId) {
                // Linked: force-activate the permanent identity entry. If there's a
                // dynamic layer, override its content = current entry text + dynamic
                // block (append, don't clobber). Otherwise activate it untouched.
                if (slaDyn) {
                    let slaBase = slaLoreMap.has(slaChar.lorebookEntryId)
                        ? slaLoreMap.get(slaChar.lorebookEntryId)!
                        : await buildLorebookContent(slaChar);
                    slaBase = (slaBase || '').replace(/\s+$/, '');
                    lorebookEntries.push([slaChar.lorebookEntryId, { active: true, content: slaBase ? (slaBase + '\n\n' + slaDyn) : slaDyn }]);
                } else {
                    lorebookEntries.push([slaChar.lorebookEntryId, { active: true }]);
                }
            } else {
                // Unlinked: emit ONE ephemeral temp entry = identity + dynamic block.
                let slaStatic = (await buildLorebookContent(slaChar)).trim();
                let slaCombined = [slaStatic, slaDyn].filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');
                if (slaCombined) {
                    temporaryLorebookEntries.push({
                        id: 'ae_static_' + slaChar.id,
                        name: slaChar.name || 'Character',
                        content: slaCombined,
                        active: true,
                        appearWithPermanent: true
                    });
                }
            }
        }

        // [v1.6.3 LOC-LORE] Force-activate the player's CURRENT room's own dossier entry
        // the same way a scene-present character's entry is activated above. If the player
        // is in the Haunted House, the Haunted House's own lorebook entry activates. This
        // is immediate/single-location only — no ancestor walking up the location tree.
        try {
            const slaRoomId = await aeGetPlayerRoomId();
            if (slaRoomId) {
                const slaRoom = await loadEntity(slaRoomId);
                if (slaRoom && (slaRoom as any).lorebookEntryId) {
                    lorebookEntries.push([(slaRoom as any).lorebookEntryId, { active: true }]);
                }
            }
        } catch (eSlaLoc) { api.v1.error('[AE:context] current-location activation:', eSlaLoc); }
    } catch (eSla) { api.v1.error('[AE:context] buildSceneLorebookActivation:', eSla); }
    return { lorebookEntries: lorebookEntries, temporaryLorebookEntries: temporaryLorebookEntries };
}

// ── Faction Lorebook ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function getFactionLorebookKeys(faction: FactionDef): string[] {
    return [faction.name].concat(faction.lorebookTags || []).filter(Boolean);
}

function buildFactionLorebookContent(faction: FactionDef): string {
    const lines: string[] = [];
    if (faction.name) lines.push(faction.name);
    if (faction.description?.trim()) lines.push(faction.description.trim());
    if (faction.relationshipStates && faction.relationshipStates.length > 0) {
        lines.push(`Relationship standings: ${faction.relationshipStates.join(' → ')}`);
    }
    return lines.join('\n');
}

async function createLinkedFactionLorebookEntry(factionId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    const cfg  = await loadConfig();
    const fIdx = (cfg.factions || []).findIndex(function(f) { return f.id === factionId; });
    if (fIdx < 0) return;
    const faction = cfg.factions[fIdx];
    try {
        let catId = await ensureLorebookCategory('AE Factions');
        let entry: any = {
            displayName: faction.name,
            text: buildFactionLorebookContent(faction),
            keys: getFactionLorebookKeys(faction),
            enabled: true
        };
        if (catId) entry.category = catId;
        const entryId = await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry(entry);
        cfg.factions[fIdx].lorebookEntryId = entryId;
        await saveConfig(cfg);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast(`Lorebook entry created for ${faction.name}.`, { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) { if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to create faction lorebook entry.', { type: 'error' }); if (silent) throw e; }
}

// [v2.53.1 FACTION-NEUTRAL-INDEX] BUGFIX: called from the Factions wizard tab
// (boxContent's neutral/"unaffiliated" state row) but never defined anywhere in this
// file — a ReferenceError on every open once a single faction exists, thrown inside a
// UI-part forEach callback, which is why it also locked the user out of World-Build
// Wizard afterward (the callback that builds the tab content never completed). Matches
// the FactionRecord.neutralIndex doc comment: "Defaults to the middle state if
// undefined." Middle of an N-state track, rounded down.
function factionNeutralIndex(faction: { relationshipStates?: string[] }): number {
    const states = (faction && faction.relationshipStates) || [];
    if (states.length === 0) return 0;
    return Math.floor((states.length - 1) / 2);
}

async function syncFactionLorebookEntry(factionId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    const cfg     = await loadConfig();
    const faction = (cfg.factions || []).find(function(f) { return f.id === factionId; });
    if (!faction?.lorebookEntryId) return;
    try {
        await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(faction.lorebookEntryId, {
            text: buildFactionLorebookContent(faction),
            keys: getFactionLorebookKeys(faction),
            displayName: faction.name
        });
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Faction lorebook synced.', { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) { if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to sync faction lorebook.', { type: 'error' }); if (silent) throw e; }
}

async function unlinkFactionLorebookEntry(factionId: string): Promise<void> {
    const cfg = await loadConfig();
    const fIdx = (cfg.factions || []).findIndex(function(f) { return f.id === factionId; });
    if (fIdx < 0) return;
    if (cfg.factions[fIdx].lorebookEntryId) {
        try { await api.v1.lorebook.removeEntry(cfg.factions[fIdx].lorebookEntryId!); } catch(e) {}
        cfg.factions[fIdx].lorebookEntryId = undefined;
        await saveConfig(cfg);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Faction lorebook unlinked.', { type: 'info' });
    }
}

// ── Species Lorebook ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function getSpeciesLorebookKeys(sp: SpeciesDef): string[] {
    return [sp.name].concat(sp.lorebookTags || []).filter(Boolean);
}

function buildSpeciesLorebookContent(sp: SpeciesDef): string {
    const lines: string[] = [];
    if (sp.name) lines.push(sp.name);
    if (sp.description?.trim()) lines.push(sp.description.trim());
    return lines.join('\n');
}

async function createLinkedSpeciesLorebookEntry(speciesId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    const cfg  = await loadConfig();
    const sIdx = (cfg.species || []).findIndex(function(s) { return s.id === speciesId; });
    if (sIdx < 0) return;
    const sp = cfg.species[sIdx];
    try {
        let catId = await ensureLorebookCategory('AE Species');
        let entry: any = {
            displayName: sp.name,
            text: buildSpeciesLorebookContent(sp),
            keys: getSpeciesLorebookKeys(sp),
            enabled: true
        };
        if (catId) entry.category = catId;
        const entryId = await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry(entry);
        cfg.species[sIdx].lorebookEntryId = entryId;
        await saveConfig(cfg);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast(`Lorebook entry created for ${sp.name}.`, { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) { if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to create species lorebook entry.', { type: 'error' }); if (silent) throw e; }
}

async function syncSpeciesLorebookEntry(speciesId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    const cfg = await loadConfig();
    const sp  = (cfg.species || []).find(function(s) { return s.id === speciesId; });
    if (!sp?.lorebookEntryId) return;
    try {
        await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(sp.lorebookEntryId, {
            text: buildSpeciesLorebookContent(sp),
            keys: getSpeciesLorebookKeys(sp),
            displayName: sp.name
        });
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Species lorebook synced.', { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) { if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to sync species lorebook.', { type: 'error' }); if (silent) throw e; }
}

async function unlinkSpeciesLorebookEntry(speciesId: string): Promise<void> {
    const cfg = await loadConfig();
    const sIdx = (cfg.species || []).findIndex(function(s) { return s.id === speciesId; });
    if (sIdx < 0) return;
    if (cfg.species[sIdx].lorebookEntryId) {
        try { await api.v1.lorebook.removeEntry(cfg.species[sIdx].lorebookEntryId!); } catch(e) {}
        cfg.species[sIdx].lorebookEntryId = undefined;
        await saveConfig(cfg);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Species lorebook unlinked.', { type: 'info' });
    }
}
// ── Entity Lorebook (locations / items / narratives) ────────────────────────
// New in [WP-1]. Same getKeys/buildContent/create/sync family as the character
// lorebook above, but for world entities, filed under an "AE <Type>s" category.
// ensureLorebookCategory() is the shared find-or-create helper (reusable by the
// character/faction/species families on a later unification pass).
