async function ensureLorebookCategory(name: string): Promise<string> {
    // Returns the id of the named lorebook category, creating it if absent.
    // Returns '' on failure — callers then create the entry uncategorised.
    try {
        let cats = await api.v1.lorebook.categories();
        let found = (cats || []).find(function(c) { return c.name === name; });
        if (found) return found.id;
        let newId = await api.v1.lorebook.createCategory({ id: api.v1.uuid(), name: name, enabled: true });
        return newId || '';
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:lore] ensureLorebookCategory:', e); return ''; }
}

function entityLorebookCategoryName(ent: EntityRecord): string {
    if (ent.type === 'item')      return 'AE Items';
    if (ent.type === 'narrative') return 'AE Narratives';
    if (ent.type === 'character') return 'AE Characters';
    return 'AE Locations';
}

function getEntityLorebookKeys(ent: EntityRecord): string[] {
    // Entity name is always a key; lorebookTags add extra trigger words.
    return [ent.name].concat(ent.lorebookTags || []).filter(Boolean);
}

function buildEntityLorebookContent(ent: EntityRecord): string {
    // [WP-1] The dedicated extended-lore body, injected when a key (the entity
    // name or an added key) appears in the story. Deliberately NOT the
    // description/state — those already inject via the world-state block while
    // the player is present, so echoing them here would double them in context.
    return (ent.lorebookText || '').trim();
}

async function createLinkedEntityLorebookEntry(entId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    let ent = await loadEntity(entId);
    if (!ent) return;
    if (!(ent.lorebookText || '').trim()) { if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Add Lorebook Entry text first, then create the entry.', { type: 'info' }); return; }
    try {
        let catId = await ensureLorebookCategory(entityLorebookCategoryName(ent));
        let entry: any = { id: api.v1.uuid(), displayName: ent.name, text: buildEntityLorebookContent(ent), keys: getEntityLorebookKeys(ent), enabled: true };
        if (catId) entry.category = catId;
        let newId = await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry(entry);
        ent.lorebookEntryId = newId;
        await saveEntity(ent);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Lorebook entry created for ' + ent.name + '.', { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:lore] createLinkedEntityLorebookEntry:', e);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to create lorebook entry.', { type: 'error' });
        if (silent) throw e;
    }
}

async function syncEntityLorebookEntry(entId: string, silent?: boolean): Promise<void> {
    let ent = await loadEntity(entId);
    if (!ent || !ent.lorebookEntryId) return;
    try {
        await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(ent.lorebookEntryId, { text: buildEntityLorebookContent(ent), keys: getEntityLorebookKeys(ent), displayName: ent.name });
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Lorebook entry synced.', { type: 'success' });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:lore] syncEntityLorebookEntry:', e);
        if (!silent) api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to sync lorebook entry.', { type: 'error' });
    }
}

async function unlinkEntityLorebookEntry(entId: string): Promise<void> {
    let ent = await loadEntity(entId);
    if (!ent || !ent.lorebookEntryId) return;
    try { await api.v1.lorebook.removeEntry(ent.lorebookEntryId); }
    catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:lore] unlinkEntityLorebookEntry:', e); }
    ent.lorebookEntryId = undefined;
    await saveEntity(ent);
    api.v1.ui.toast('Lorebook entry unlinked.', { type: 'info' });
}

// ── Sync-All Lorebooks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// [vNEXT] The individual sync helpers all early-return when a record has no
// lorebookEntryId, and none of them notice when a stored id points at an entry the
// user has since deleted in NAI. syncAllLorebooks fixes both: it never assumes an
// entry exists. It pulls the full live entry-id set ONCE (single API call), then per
// record decides create-vs-sync from that set in memory — so a run over the whole
// world costs one entries() call plus one update/create per record, no per-record
// existence probing. A record is (re)created when it has no id OR its id is stale
// (missing from the live set); otherwise it is synced. Returns per-type tallies.
//
// liveIds: pass the prebuilt Set so a single sync-all shares one snapshot across all
// four families. _ensure* helpers below are also individually callable.
async function _liveLorebookIdSet(): Promise<Set<string>> {
    let set = new Set<string>();
    try {
        let all = await api.v1.lorebook.entries();
        (all || []).forEach(function(e: any) { if (e && e.id) set.add(e.id); });
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:lore] _liveLorebookIdSet:', e); }
    return set;
}

async function ensureCharacterLorebook(charId: string, liveIds: Set<string>): Promise<'created' | 'synced' | 'skipped'> {
    let char = await loadCharacter(charId);
    if (!char) return 'skipped';
    let hasLive = !!(char.lorebookEntryId && liveIds.has(char.lorebookEntryId));
    if (hasLive) { await syncLorebookEntry(charId, true).catch(function() {}); return 'synced'; }
    // No id, or a stale id whose entry was deleted in NAI: clear + recreate.
    if (char.lorebookEntryId && !hasLive) { char.lorebookEntryId = undefined; await saveCharacter(char).catch(function() {}); }
    await createLinkedLorebookEntry(charId, true).catch(function() {});
    return 'created';
}

async function ensureFactionLorebook(factionId: string, liveIds: Set<string>): Promise<'created' | 'synced' | 'skipped'> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let f = (cfg.factions || []).find(function(x) { return x.id === factionId; });
    if (!f) return 'skipped';
    let hasLive = !!(f.lorebookEntryId && liveIds.has(f.lorebookEntryId));
    if (hasLive) { await syncFactionLorebookEntry(factionId, true).catch(function() {}); return 'synced'; }
    if (f.lorebookEntryId && !hasLive) {
        // Clear the stale id before recreate so create doesn't leave a dangling ref.
        let cfg2 = await loadConfig();
        let f2i = (cfg2.factions || []).findIndex(function(x) { return x.id === factionId; });
        if (f2i >= 0) { cfg2.factions[f2i].lorebookEntryId = undefined; await saveConfig(cfg2).catch(function() {}); }
    }
    await createLinkedFactionLorebookEntry(factionId, true).catch(function() {});
    return 'created';
}

async function ensureSpeciesLorebook(speciesId: string, liveIds: Set<string>): Promise<'created' | 'synced' | 'skipped'> {
    let cfg = await loadConfig();
    let s = (cfg.species || []).find(function(x) { return x.id === speciesId; });
    if (!s) return 'skipped';
    let hasLive = !!(s.lorebookEntryId && liveIds.has(s.lorebookEntryId));
    if (hasLive) { await syncSpeciesLorebookEntry(speciesId, true).catch(function() {}); return 'synced'; }
    if (s.lorebookEntryId && !hasLive) {
        let cfg2 = await loadConfig();
        let s2i = (cfg2.species || []).findIndex(function(x) { return x.id === speciesId; });
        if (s2i >= 0) { cfg2.species[s2i].lorebookEntryId = undefined; await saveConfig(cfg2).catch(function() {}); }
    }
    await createLinkedSpeciesLorebookEntry(speciesId, true).catch(function() {});
    return 'created';
}

async function ensureEntityLorebook(entId: string, liveIds: Set<string>): Promise<'created' | 'synced' | 'skipped'> {
    let ent = await loadEntity(entId);
    if (!ent) return 'skipped';
    // Entity entries only carry the dedicated extended-lore body; with none there is
    // nothing to store, so skip (matches createLinkedEntityLorebookEntry's own guard).
    if (!((ent as any).lorebookText || '').trim()) return 'skipped';
    let hasLive = !!(ent.lorebookEntryId && liveIds.has(ent.lorebookEntryId));
    if (hasLive) { await syncEntityLorebookEntry(entId, true).catch(function() {}); return 'synced'; }
    if (ent.lorebookEntryId && !hasLive) { ent.lorebookEntryId = undefined; await saveEntity(ent).catch(function() {}); }
    await createLinkedEntityLorebookEntry(entId, true).catch(function() {});
    return 'created';
}

interface SyncAllTally { created: number; synced: number; skipped: number; }
async function syncAllLorebooks(): Promise<{ characters: SyncAllTally; locations: SyncAllTally; species: SyncAllTally; factions: SyncAllTally }> {
    let mk = function(): SyncAllTally { return { created: 0, synced: 0, skipped: 0 }; };
    let out = { characters: mk(), locations: mk(), species: mk(), factions: mk() };
    let bump = function(t: SyncAllTally, r: 'created' | 'synced' | 'skipped') { if (r === 'created') t.created++; else if (r === 'synced') t.synced++; else t.skipped++; };

    // One live snapshot shared by every family — the only lorebook read of the run.
    let liveIds = await _liveLorebookIdSet();

    try {
        // Characters (from the lightweight index).
        let cIdx = await loadIndex();
        for (let m of cIdx.filter(Boolean)) {
            bump(out.characters, await ensureCharacterLorebook(m.id, liveIds));
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:lore] syncAll characters:', e); }

    try {
        // Locations (location-type entities).
        let eIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
        for (let em of eIdx.filter(function(x) { return x && x.type === 'location'; })) {
            bump(out.locations, await ensureEntityLorebook(em.id, liveIds));
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:lore] syncAll locations:', e); }

    try {
        let cfg = await loadConfig();
        for (let sp of (cfg.species || [])) { bump(out.species, await ensureSpeciesLorebook(sp.id, liveIds)); }
        for (let fa of (cfg.factions || [])) { bump(out.factions, await ensureFactionLorebook(fa.id, liveIds)); }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:lore] syncAll species/factions:', e); }

    return out;
}



// ============================================================================
// SECTION 10b — PLAYER JOURNAL  [v1.7.0 JOURNAL]
// A player-authored running record ("The story so far") that survives context
// pressure by living in the lorebook. Bubbles are the source of truth in
// storyStorage; two lorebook entries are a *projection* of them:
//   - Active  entry: Always-On, keyless — non-archived bubbles, injected every turn.
//   - Archive entry: keyless + disabled — archived bubbles, retained but NEVER
//                    injected; unarchiving moves a bubble back to the active set.
// Renderer (native HUD) lives in Section 27; this section is pure state + lore +
// generation so the bridge can later drive the same verbs over a message pair.
// ============================================================================

async function loadJournal(): Promise<JournalState> {
    try {
        const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_JOURNAL, null);
        if (raw && typeof raw === 'object' && Array.isArray((raw as any).bubbles)) {
            const s = raw as JournalState;
            s.bubbles = (s.bubbles || []).filter(Boolean);
            return s;
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:journal] loadJournal:', e); }
    return { bubbles: [] };
}

async function saveJournal(state: JournalState): Promise<void> {
    try {
        await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_JOURNAL, {
            bubbles: (state.bubbles || []).filter(Boolean),
            activeLoreId: state.activeLoreId,
            archiveLoreId: state.archiveLoreId
        });
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:journal] saveJournal:', e); }
}

// [v1.7.0 JOURNAL] Stamp reflects the STORY's in-world calendar at the moment the
// entry was written (captured as storyHours at creation), not wall-clock time.
// Formatted via the same clockFormatStamp the calendar/Author's-Note use.

// Ordered prose body from a bubble list. Stamped bubbles carry their in-story stamp
// inline (the only place a stamp reaches context), so a merge preserves them.
function buildJournalBody(bubbles: JournalBubble[], cfg: GlobalConfig): string {
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] In-story stamps removed — Clock cut. Plain text join only.
    return (bubbles || []).map(function(b) {
        return (b.text || '').trim();
    }).filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');
}

function buildActiveJournalText(bubbles: JournalBubble[], cfg: GlobalConfig): string {
    const body = buildJournalBody(bubbles, cfg);
    return body ? ('The story so far:\n\n' + body) : '';
}

function buildArchiveJournalText(bubbles: JournalBubble[], cfg: GlobalConfig): string {
    const body = buildJournalBody(bubbles, cfg);
    return body ? ('The story so far (archived):\n\n' + body) : '';
}

// Update an existing lore entry, or (re)create it if the id is missing/stale
// (e.g. the user deleted it in NAI's lorebook UI). Returns the live entry id.
async function ensureJournalEntry(existingId: string | undefined, spec: Partial<LorebookEntry>): Promise<string> {
    if (existingId) {
        try {
            const cur = await api.v1.lorebook.entry(existingId);
            if (cur) { await api.v1.lorebook.updateEntry(existingId, spec); return existingId; }
        } catch (e) { /* fall through and recreate */ }
    }
    return await api.v1.lorebook.createEntry(spec);
}

// Re-project bubbles onto the two lorebook entries. Best-effort: never throws,
// mutates state.activeLoreId / state.archiveLoreId in place. Does NOT save —
// persistJournal() owns the single authoritative save after ids settle.
async function syncJournalLore(state: JournalState): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const cfg = await loadConfig();
        const active   = (state.bubbles || []).filter(function(b) { return !b.archived; });
        const archived = (state.bubbles || []).filter(function(b) { return b.archived; });

        const activeText = buildActiveJournalText(active, cfg);
        const hasActive  = activeText.trim().length > 0;

        // The Always-On entry only force-activates when it has content; empty, it
        // is disabled so a lone "The story so far:" header never enters context.
        state.activeLoreId = await ensureJournalEntry(state.activeLoreId, {
            displayName: 'The story so far',
            keys: [],
            text: activeText,
            forceActivation: hasActive,
            enabled: hasActive
        });

        // Archive entry is created lazily — only once something is archived (or it
        // already exists) — so untouched stories don't accrue an empty keyless entry.
        if (archived.length > 0 || state.archiveLoreId) {
            state.archiveLoreId = await ensureJournalEntry(state.archiveLoreId, {
                displayName: 'Journal — Archived (unarchive to restore)',
                keys: [],
                text: buildArchiveJournalText(archived, cfg),
                forceActivation: false,
                enabled: false
            });
        }
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:journal] syncJournalLore:', e);
    }
}

// Single persistence path for every mutator: project to lore, then one save.
async function persistJournal(state: JournalState): Promise<JournalState> {
    await syncJournalLore(state);
    await saveJournal(state);
    // Keep an open bridge HUD in sync when native mutates the journal (both-mode).
    if (isBridgeReady) { broadcastJournal().catch(function() {}); }
    return state;
}

// Bridge-facing projection: the bridge has no clock/cfg access, so the engine
// precomputes each bubble's in-story stampLabel here. stampLabel is always sent
async function journalPayloadForBridge(): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
    const state = await loadJournal();
    const bubbles = (state.bubbles || []).filter(Boolean).map(function(b) {
        return {
            id: b.id,
            text: b.text || '',
            archived: !!b.archived,
            createdAt: b.createdAt || 0
        };
    });
    return { bubbles: bubbles };
}

async function broadcastJournal(): Promise<void> {
    try { await broadcastToTM('AE_JOURNAL_STATE', await journalPayloadForBridge()); }
    catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:journal] broadcast:', e); }
}

async function journalAddBubble(text: string): Promise<JournalState> {
    const state = await loadJournal();
    const t = (text || '').trim();
    if (!t) return state;
    state.bubbles.push({ id: api.v1.uuid(), text: t, createdAt: Date.now(), archived: false });
    return await persistJournal(state);
}

async function journalUpdateBubble(id: string, text: string): Promise<JournalState> {
    const state = await loadJournal();
    const b = state.bubbles.find(function(x) { return x.id === id; });
    if (b) b.text = (text || '').trim();
    return await persistJournal(state);
}

async function journalDeleteBubble(id: string): Promise<JournalState> {
    const state = await loadJournal();
    state.bubbles = state.bubbles.filter(function(x) { return x.id !== id; });
    return await persistJournal(state);
}


async function journalSetArchived(id: string, archived: boolean): Promise<JournalState> {
    const state = await loadJournal();
    const b = state.bubbles.find(function(x) { return x.id === id; });
    if (b) b.archived = !!archived;
    return await persistJournal(state);
}

// Merge-down: append all non-archived bubbles (in order) into a single bubble.
// Archived bubbles are untouched. Inline stamps survive via buildJournalBody.
async function journalMergeDown(): Promise<JournalState> {
    const state = await loadJournal();
    const active = state.bubbles.filter(function(b) { return !b.archived; });
    if (active.length <= 1) return state;
    const mergedText = buildJournalBody(active, await loadConfig());
    const earliest = active.reduce(function(m, b) { return b.createdAt < m ? b.createdAt : m; }, active[0].createdAt);
    const merged: JournalBubble = { id: api.v1.uuid(), text: mergedText, createdAt: earliest, archived: false };
    const archived = state.bubbles.filter(function(b) { return b.archived; });
    state.bubbles = [merged].concat(archived);
    return await persistJournal(state);
}

// ── Generation verbs (story-aware, so voice matches the current model/style) ──
// Both run in 'background' mode and suppress this script's own context hooks so
// the journal doesn't re-inject itself while being rewritten.

async function journalGenerateRewrite(text: string): Promise<string | null> {
    const src = (text || '').trim();
    if (!src) return null;
    try {
        const cfg = await loadConfig();
        const sys = [
            (cfg.generalStyle || '').trim(),
            '',
            'Rewrite the player\'s journal note below in the narrative voice of this story.',
            'Absolute priority: preserve every fact, name, event, number, and decision exactly as written \u2014 invent nothing, omit nothing.',
            'Be token-efficient: tighten wording and cut filler while keeping all content.',
            'Past tense, third-person recap voice. Output ONLY the rewritten note \u2014 no preamble, no quotes, no commentary.'
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
        const maxTok = Math.max(120, Math.min(700, Math.ceil(src.length / 3) + 80));
        const resp = await api.v1.generateWithStory(
            [ { role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: src } ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: maxTok, temperature: 0.7 },
            { suppressScriptHooks: 'self', contextLimitReduction: 600 },
            undefined,
            'background'
        );
        if (resp && resp.choices && resp.choices.length > 0) return (resp.choices[0].text || '').trim() || null;
        return null;
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:journal] rewrite:', e); return null; }
}

async function journalGenerateCompress(text: string): Promise<string | null> {
    const src = (text || '').trim();
    if (!src) return null;
    try {
        const cfg = await loadConfig();
        const sys = [
            (cfg.generalStyle || '').trim(),
            '',
            'Compress the player\'s journal note below into a shorter recap for long-term memory.',
            'Keep the load-bearing details: names, places, relationships, commitments, and outcomes. Drop atmosphere and repetition.',
            'Aim for roughly half the length or less. Past tense, plain and dense.',
            'Output ONLY the compressed recap \u2014 no preamble, no quotes, no commentary.'
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
        const maxTok = Math.max(60, Math.min(400, Math.ceil(src.length / 6) + 40));
        const resp = await api.v1.generateWithStory(
            [ { role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: src } ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: maxTok, temperature: 0.5 },
            { suppressScriptHooks: 'self', contextLimitReduction: 600 },
            undefined,
            'background'
        );
        if (resp && resp.choices && resp.choices.length > 0) return (resp.choices[0].text || '').trim() || null;
        return null;
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:journal] compress:', e); return null; }
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 10c — PER-CHARACTER JOURNAL  [v2.12.0 CHARJOURNAL]
// Same bubble shape and verb set as Section 10b's player journal, scoped to one
// character instead of the whole story. The key difference: NO dedicated lorebook
// entry pair. Active bubbles fold straight into that character's OWN lorebook
// entry via buildCharDynamicBlock (see LOREFOLD, Section 6-ish) as one more
// toggleable field — same "rebuilt fresh every generation" treatment equipment/
// conditions already get, gated by cfg.lorebookFields.journal. Rewrite/compress
// reuse journalGenerateRewrite/journalGenerateCompress directly (pure text
// transforms with no journal-identity dependency — nothing to duplicate).
// Renderer lives in Section 27 (openCharJournalModal) so the bridge can drive the
// same verbs over the AE_CHAR_JOURNAL_* message pair.
// ============================================================================

async function loadCharJournal(charId: string): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    try {
        const raw = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_CHAR_JOURNAL_PFX + charId, null);
        if (raw && typeof raw === 'object' && Array.isArray((raw as any).bubbles)) {
            const s = raw as CharJournalState;
            s.bubbles = (s.bubbles || []).filter(Boolean);
            return s;
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:charjournal] loadCharJournal:', e); }
    return { bubbles: [] };
}

async function saveCharJournal(charId: string, state: CharJournalState): Promise<void> {
    try {
        await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CHAR_JOURNAL_PFX + charId, {
            bubbles: (state.bubbles || []).filter(Boolean)
        });
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:charjournal] saveCharJournal:', e); }
}

// Single persistence path for every mutator. No lorebook-lore sync step (unlike
// the main journal's persistJournal) — buildCharDynamicBlock reads storage fresh
// every generation, same as conditions/equipment, so there's nothing to pre-sync.
async function persistCharJournal(charId: string, state: CharJournalState): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    await saveCharJournal(charId, state);
    if (isBridgeReady) { broadcastCharJournal(charId).catch(function() {}); }
    return state;
}

async function charJournalPayloadForBridge(charId: string): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
    const state = await loadCharJournal(charId);
    const bubbles = (state.bubbles || []).filter(Boolean).map(function(b) {
        return { id: b.id, text: b.text || '', archived: !!b.archived, createdAt: b.createdAt || 0 };
    });
    return { charId: charId, bubbles: bubbles };
}

async function broadcastCharJournal(charId: string): Promise<void> {
    try { await broadcastToTM('AE_CHAR_JOURNAL_STATE', await charJournalPayloadForBridge(charId)); }
    catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:charjournal] broadcast:', e); }
}

async function charJournalAddBubble(charId: string, text: string): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    const state = await loadCharJournal(charId);
    const t = (text || '').trim();
    if (!t) return state;
    state.bubbles.push({ id: api.v1.uuid(), text: t, createdAt: Date.now(), archived: false });
    return await persistCharJournal(charId, state);
}

async function charJournalUpdateBubble(charId: string, id: string, text: string): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    const state = await loadCharJournal(charId);
    const b = state.bubbles.find(function(x) { return x.id === id; });
    if (b) b.text = (text || '').trim();
    return await persistCharJournal(charId, state);
}

async function charJournalDeleteBubble(charId: string, id: string): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    const state = await loadCharJournal(charId);
    state.bubbles = state.bubbles.filter(function(x) { return x.id !== id; });
    return await persistCharJournal(charId, state);
}

async function charJournalSetArchived(charId: string, id: string, archived: boolean): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    const state = await loadCharJournal(charId);
    const b = state.bubbles.find(function(x) { return x.id === id; });
    if (b) b.archived = !!archived;
    return await persistCharJournal(charId, state);
}

async function charJournalMergeDown(charId: string): Promise<CharJournalState> {
    const state = await loadCharJournal(charId);
    const active = state.bubbles.filter(function(b) { return !b.archived; });
    if (active.length <= 1) return state;
    const mergedText = active.map(function(b) { return (b.text || '').trim(); }).filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');
    const earliest = active.reduce(function(m, b) { return b.createdAt < m ? b.createdAt : m; }, active[0].createdAt);
    const merged: CharJournalBubble = { id: api.v1.uuid(), text: mergedText, createdAt: earliest, archived: false };
    const archived = state.bubbles.filter(function(b) { return b.archived; });
    state.bubbles = [merged].concat(archived);
    return await persistCharJournal(charId, state);
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 12 — CHARACTER SNAPSHOT GENERATION
//
// CHARACTER SNAPSHOT (GLM-backed — AI generation)
//    generateCharacterSnapshot: writes a present-tense appearance description
//    of a character as the protagonist encounters them. Used as scene flavor
//    text for the entity system's character-type nodes.
//
// NOTE — CONTEXT BUILDER SEPARATION:
// The injection sub-builders that feed into the Author's Note (buildCharInjection,
// buildMidRangeInjection, buildEphemeralInjection, charIsRelevant,
// buildEntityContextBlock, buildProtagonistInjection, buildDramatisPersonae)
// are NOT in this section. They live in Section 13 (Context Injection Builders)
// because they are pipeline components — the output of this section feeds them
// but they are architecturally distinct.
// ============================================================================

// ── Character Snapshot (AI generation) ───────────────────────────────────────

async function generateCharacterSnapshot(charRec: CharacterRecord, ent: EntityRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<string | null> {
    // Generates a brief present-tense appearance description for a character
    // as encountered in their current location. Used by the entity system
    // for character-type nodes when a snapshot hasn't been generated yet.
    //
    // Uses api.v1.generateWithStory in 'background' mode so it runs without
    // surfacing a visible generation progress indicator.
    try {
        // Resolve location name for scene grounding
        let locationName = 'Unknown';
        let locationDesc = '';
        if (ent.parentId) {
            const locEnt = await loadEntity(ent.parentId);
            if (locEnt) { locationName = locEnt.name; locationDesc = locEnt.description || ''; }
        }

        // Resolve equipped item names for appearance context
        const equippedItems: string[] = [];
        for (const itemId of (charRec.equippedItemIds || [])) {
            try {
                const itemEnt = await loadEntity(itemId);
                if (itemEnt) equippedItems.push(itemEnt.name);
            } catch (ignoredErr) { /* non-fatal */ }
        }

        const idParts  = [charRec.gender, charRec.species, charRec.age, charRec.occupationRole].filter(Boolean);
        const idLine   = idParts.length > 0 ? ' (' + idParts.join(', ') + ')' : '';

        const sysPrompt = [
            cfg.generalStyle || '',
            '',
            `Write a brief present-tense description of ${charRec.name} as the protagonist notices them.`,
            '1 short paragraph. No internal thoughts, no dialogue, no summary statements.',
            '',
            'Capture:',
            '- Physical appearance including anything worn or carried',
            `- What they appear to be doing in ${locationName} (work here? live here? passing through? with the protagonist?)`,
            '- One behavioral or expressive detail reflecting their current state',
            '',
            'Concrete and specific. Show rather than tell.'
        ].join('\n');

        const userPrompt = [
            charRec.name + idLine,
            charRec.briefDescription         ? 'Brief: '     + charRec.briefDescription : '',
            charRec.physicalDescription      ? 'Appearance: ' + charRec.physicalDescription : '',
            charRec.background               ? 'Background: ' + charRec.background.slice(0, 100) : '',
            equippedItems.length > 0         ? 'Wearing/Carrying: ' + equippedItems.join(', ') : '',
            'Location: ' + locationName,
            locationDesc                     ? 'Setting: '    + locationDesc.slice(0, 80) : '',
            ent.atmosphere                   ? 'Thoughts: '   + ent.atmosphere : '',
            charRec.midRange?.currentDesire  ? 'Drive: '      + charRec.midRange.currentDesire : '',
            charRec.ephemeral?.compressedLine ? 'State: '     + charRec.ephemeral.compressedLine : ''
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');

        const response = await api.v1.generateWithStory(
            [
                { role: 'system', content: sysPrompt },
                { role: 'user',   content: userPrompt }
            ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 200 },
            { suppressScriptHooks: 'self', contextLimitReduction: 450 },
            undefined,
            'background'
        );

        if (response && response.choices && response.choices.length > 0) {
            return response.choices[0].text || null;
        }
        return null;
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:knowledge] generateCharacterSnapshot:', e);
        return null;
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 13 — CONTEXT INJECTION BUILDERS
//
// All functions that construct content injected into the AI's context window.
//
// [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] Location, scene roster ("present with"), and the
// tracked-narrative quest line are REMOVED from injection entirely — dramatis
// persona / static identity are lorebook's job, and location/quest tracking now
// has no AN presence at all (the underlying Narrative Threads / STORAGE_
// TRACKED_NARRATIVE feature is untouched, only its injection is gone).
// buildTrackedNarrativeBlock and buildDramaContextBlock are deleted. Critical
// (85%+) hooks moved off the Imperative splice and into the same AN block as
// character thoughts. See the CE-INDEX PASS 9 note at the top of this file for
// full rationale and what's flagged-but-not-yet-built (urgent hooks / background
// NPC web currently have no injection destination).
//
// ── CURRENT INJECTION PIPELINE MAP ──────────────────────────────────────────
//
// onBeforeContextBuild  →  Author's Note (AN) slot
//   └── buildWorldStateBlock()
//         └── Per scene-present character (within the dynamic cap):
//               ├── Thoughts line — ephemeral + midRange (cfg.contextTemplates.npc)
//               └── Critical hook line, if pressureLevel ≥ 85 and unresolved
//                     (cfg.contextTemplates.criticalHook)
//
// onContextBuilt  →  Spliced before player input (highest recency weight)
//   └── Imperative block  [Section 26 — inline in hook]
//         ├── Pending GM note (fires once)
//         ├── Pending knowledge query (fires once)
//         └── High-priority NPC web links (priority ≥ 70, both parties present)
//
// onContextBuilt  →  Replaces player input (tag-based dice roll)
//   └── ⚠️ note: replaced probability system 
//         └── #tagname in player input → 1d6 + skill modifier → outcome line
//
// ── NOT CURRENTLY INJECTED ANYWHERE (flagged, not silently dropped) ─────────
// Urgent hooks (60–84%) and background/sub-70 NPC web links lost their
// destination when buildDramaContextBlock was deleted. Planned home: a per-scene
// temporary lorebook entry (dynamic fields + relationship web, narrative prose,
// per-field exclude option) — not built yet, separate pass.
//
// ── SUB-BUILDER STATUS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// buildWorldStateBlock: called from onBeforeContextBuild (live).
// charIsRelevant: called from runDynamicEngine (live).
// buildProtagonistInjection, buildDramatisPersonae, buildEntityContextBlock,
// buildCharInjection, buildMidRangeInjection, buildEphemeralInjection:
//   defined but NOT currently called from the generation pipeline. Preserved as
//   utilities. buildDramatisPersonae is the likely starting point for the
//   to-do'd optional Dramatis Personae reference lorebook.
// ============================================================================

// Builds macro-accessible fields for a character: relArchetype, relStage, and one
// entry per character resource keyed res_<tag> (tag '#' stripped). Discrete/track
// resources resolve to their current state label; pools to their numeric value.
// Blank/index-0 discrete values resolve to '' so the [ ... ] block self-suppresses.
function buildCharMacroExtras(char: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): Record<string, any> {
    let extras: Record<string, any> = {};
    // Relationship archetype + stage
    if (char.primaryRelationship) {
        extras.relArchetype = char.primaryRelationship.archetype || '';
        extras.relStage = resolveArchetypeStage(cfg, char.primaryRelationship);
        // [v0.9.17] Resolve the relationship target-name macro: __player__ -> protagonist name.
        let _relTgt = char.primaryRelationship.targetName;
        if (_relTgt === '__player__') _relTgt = (cfg as any).protagonistName || 'the player';
        extras.primaryRelationship = Object.assign({}, char.primaryRelationship, { targetName: _relTgt });
    } else {
        extras.relArchetype = '';
        extras.relStage = '';
    }
    // Character resources → res_<tag>
    let resMap: Record<string, any> = {};
    for (const rdef of (char.charResourceDefs || [])) {
        let key = String(rdef.tag || '').replace('#', '').trim();
        if (!key) continue;
        let val = (char.charResourceValues && char.charResourceValues[rdef.id] !== undefined) ? char.charResourceValues[rdef.id] : 0;
        if (rdef.type === 'pool') {
            resMap[key] = String(val);
        } else {
            // track / discrete → state label; index 0 of a discrete is the clear/none slot → blank
            let states = rdef.states || [];
            let label = states[val] || '';
            if (rdef.type === 'discrete' && val === 0) label = '';
            resMap[key] = label;
        }
    }
    extras.res = resMap;

    // [v0.9.8.4] conditionStates: a convenience join of every active condition-type
    // resource (discrete/track with a non-blank current label), e.g. "drunk, poisoned".
    // Per-tag |res.<tag>| macros still work for precise references.
    let condParts: string[] = [];
    for (const cdef of (char.charResourceDefs || [])) {
        if (!cdef || cdef.type === 'pool') continue;
        let condVal = (char.charResourceValues && char.charResourceValues[cdef.id] !== undefined) ? char.charResourceValues[cdef.id] : 0;
        let condStates = cdef.states || [];
        let condLabel = condStates[condVal] || '';
        if (cdef.type === 'discrete' && condVal === 0) condLabel = '';
        if (condLabel) condParts.push(condLabel);
    }
    extras.conditionStates = condParts.join(', ');

    return extras;
}

function parseMacroTemplate(template: string, data: any): string {
    if (!template) return '';
    return template.replace(/\[([^\[\]]+)\]/g, function(match, inner) {
        let isValid = true;
        
        // Globally find and evaluate all |macro.path| tokens inside this specific bracket block
        const evaluatedInner = inner.replace(/\|([^\|]+)\|/g, function(macroMatch, path) {
            let val: any = data;
            const keys = path.trim().split('.');
            for (const k of keys) {
                if (val === undefined || val === null) break;
                val = val[k];
            }
            
            // If any token evaluates to empty/null, flag the entire bracket block for deletion
            if (val === undefined || val === null || String(val).trim() === '') {
                isValid = false;
                return '';
            }
            
            return String(val);
        });
        
        if (!isValid) return '';
        
        // Finalize block formatting
        return evaluatedInner.replace(/\\n/g, '\n');
    });
}


// NOTE FOR ASSEMBLY: STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS = 'ae_retry_chars' (used in Section 15's
// runDynamicEngine) was declared mid-file at line 8039 in the original alongside
// STORAGE_ENGINE_SKIP. Both belong in Section 2 constants. Add:
//   var STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS = 'ae_retry_chars';

// ── Per-character sub-builders ────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Legacy freeform injection helpers (buildProtagonistInjection, buildDramatisPersonae,
// buildCharInjection, buildMidRangeInjection, buildEphemeralInjection) retired.
// Dynamic prose values now consolidated cleanly in buildCharDynamicBlock and buildWorldStateBlock.

function charIsRelevant(c: CharacterRecord, activeEntryIds: Set<string>): boolean {
    // Returns true if a character should receive dynamic engine updates this turn.
    // A character is relevant if they are scene-present OR their lorebook entry fired.
    return c.active && c.scenePresent; // [v0.9.24 E2] removed lorebook-entry clause
}

// [vNEXT] Dynamic-priority cap. Given the already-loaded character index and the
// configured cap, returns the Set of character ids permitted to run as dynamic this
// turn. Pure in-memory over the index metas — NO storage reads, so it costs nothing
// on the generation path beyond a sort of the present-dynamic subset.
//
// Eligible pool = active, scene-present, non-protagonist, non-static (static chars
// are already excluded from dynamics by their own gate; excluding them here too means
// they never occupy a cap slot). Ordering: ranked characters first by ascending rank
// (1 = highest priority), then unranked characters by name. Unranked present-dynamic
// characters DO consume slots, after all ranked ones. cap <= 0 means no cap — every
// eligible id is returned.
function computeDynamicAllowedSet(index: CharacterMeta[], cap: number): Set<string> {
    const eligible = (index || []).filter(function(m) {
        return m && m.active && m.scenePresent && !m.isProtagonist && !(m as any).isStatic;
    });
    if (!(cap > 0) || eligible.length <= cap) {
        // No cap, or everyone fits — allow all eligible.
        return new Set(eligible.map(function(m) { return m.id; }));
    }
    eligible.sort(function(a, b) {
        const ar = (typeof (a as any).dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && (a as any).dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? (a as any).dynamicPriorityRank : Infinity;
        const br = (typeof (b as any).dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && (b as any).dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? (b as any).dynamicPriorityRank : Infinity;
        if (ar !== br) return ar - br;                       // ranked ascending; unranked (Infinity) last
        return String(a.name || '').localeCompare(String(b.name || '')); // stable tiebreak
    });
    return new Set(eligible.slice(0, cap).map(function(m) { return m.id; }));
}

// [vNEXT] Assign (or clear) a character's dynamic-priority rank, enforcing "one
// character per rank" by swapping. If another character already holds newRank, they
// inherit the assignee's OLD rank (which may be undefined -> they become unranked).
// Pass newRank <= 0 or undefined to simply clear the assignee's rank (no swap).
// Writes only the (at most) two affected records. Returns nothing; callers refresh UI.
async function assignDynamicPriorityRank(charId: string, newRank: number | undefined): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const target = await loadCharacter(charId);
        if (!target) return;
        const oldRank = (typeof (target as any).dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && (target as any).dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? (target as any).dynamicPriorityRank : undefined;
        const wantRank = (typeof newRank === 'number' && newRank > 0) ? Math.floor(newRank) : undefined;

        if (wantRank === oldRank) return; // no change

        // Clear case: just drop the rank.
        if (wantRank === undefined) {
            (target as any).dynamicPriorityRank = undefined;
            await saveCharacter(target);
            return;
        }

        // Find whoever currently holds wantRank (excluding the target) via the index
        // (cheap — no full character loads for the scan).
        const idx = await loadIndex();
        const holderMeta = idx.find(function(m) { return m && m.id !== charId && (m as any).dynamicPriorityRank === wantRank; });

        if (holderMeta) {
            const holder = await loadCharacter(holderMeta.id);
            if (holder) {
                // Swap: holder takes the target's old rank (may be undefined -> unranked).
                (holder as any).dynamicPriorityRank = oldRank;
                await saveCharacter(holder);
            }
        }
        (target as any).dynamicPriorityRank = wantRank;
        await saveCharacter(target);
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:dynrank] assignDynamicPriorityRank:', e);
    }
}

// [vNEXT] Shared rank-picker modal for a character. Offers "Unranked" plus ranks
// 1..maxRank, where maxRank is the configured dynamic cap (or, if no cap is set, the
// count of eligible present characters, floored at a small minimum so the control is
// always usable). Shows which character currently holds each rank. onDone (optional)
// is invoked after assignment so the caller can refresh its own view.
async function openDynamicRankPicker(charId: string, onDone?: () => (void | Promise<void>)): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const cfg = await loadConfig();
        const idx = await loadIndex();
        const self = idx.find(function(m) { return m.id === charId; });
        const selfName = self ? (self.name || 'Character') : 'Character';
        const curRank = (self && typeof (self as any).dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && (self as any).dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? (self as any).dynamicPriorityRank : undefined;

        const cap = Number((cfg as any).maxDynamicCharacters) || 0;
        // How many rank slots to offer. With a cap, offer exactly the cap. Without a
        // cap, offer as many as there are eligible present characters (min 4 so the
        // control is meaningful even in a small scene).
        let eligibleCount = idx.filter(function(m) {
            return m && m.active && !m.isProtagonist;
        }).length;
        const maxRank = cap > 0 ? cap : Math.max(4, eligibleCount);

        // Rank -> holder name lookup (from the index; no character loads).
        const holderByRank: Record<number, string> = {};
        idx.forEach(function(m) {
            const r = (m as any).dynamicPriorityRank;
            if (typeof r === 'number' && r > 0) holderByRank[r] = m.name || 'Character';
        });

        const items: { id: string; label: string; selected?: boolean }[] = [];
        items.push({ id: '__unranked__', label: 'Unranked' + (curRank === undefined ? '  (current)' : ''), selected: curRank === undefined });
        for (let r = 1; r <= maxRank; r++) {
            let holder = holderByRank[r];
            let suffix = '';
            if (r === curRank) suffix = '  (current)';
            else if (holder && holder !== selfName) suffix = '  — held by ' + holder;
            items.push({ id: String(r), label: 'Rank ' + r + suffix, selected: r === curRank });
        }

        await openPickerModal({
            title: 'Dynamic Priority — ' + selfName,
            items: items,
            getId: function(item: any) { return item.id; },
            getLabel: function(item: any) { return item.label; },
            searchable: false,
            selectedId: curRank === undefined ? '__unranked__' : String(curRank),
            emptyText: 'No ranks available.',
            onSelect: async function(item: any) {
                const pick = item && item.id;
                if (pick === '__unranked__') await assignDynamicPriorityRank(charId, undefined);
                else await assignDynamicPriorityRank(charId, parseInt(pick, 10));
                if (onDone) await onDone();
            }
        });
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:dynrank] openDynamicRankPicker:', e);
    }
}

async function buildEntityContextBlock(ent: EntityRecord): Promise<string> {
    // Builds a [ Type: Name ] header with state-resolved description and atmosphere.
    // Used when injecting a specific entity into context (e.g. from a HUD action).
    const resolved = (ent.state !== 'default' && ent.stateDescriptions[ent.state])
        ? ent.stateDescriptions[ent.state]
        : ent.description;
    const lines: string[] = [`[ ${ent.type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + ent.type.slice(1)}: ${ent.name} ]`];
    if (resolved?.trim()) lines.push(resolved.trim());
    if (ent.atmosphere?.trim()) lines.push(ent.atmosphere.trim());
    return lines.join('\n');
}

// ── Primary builders ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function buildWorldStateBlock(protagChar: CharacterRecord | null): Promise<string> {
    // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] Location, the present-roster line, and the quest
    // line are gone (see CE-INDEX PASS 9 / this file's Section 13 header). Static
    // identity still rides each character's force-activated lorebook entry. This
    // block is now ONLY per-character thoughts (ephemeral + midRange) plus, merged
    // in per user's call, that character's critical (85%+) hook line — previously
    // injected separately via the onContextBuilt Imperative splice.
    // [pending protagonist pass] protagChar is now unused in this function body —
    // left on the signature/call site deliberately; the protag/player elimination
    // pass (flagged, not started) is where this actually gets cleaned up.
    const parts: string[] = [];
    const cfg = await loadConfig();

    const charIndex = await loadIndex();
    const presentMetas = charIndex.filter(function(m) { return m && m.active && m.scenePresent && !m.isProtagonist; });
    const _wsDynCap = Number((cfg as any).maxDynamicCharacters) || 0;
    const _wsDynAllowed = computeDynamicAllowedSet(charIndex as any, _wsDynCap);

    // ── PER-CHARACTER THOUGHTS + CRITICAL HOOK ────────────────────────────────
    try {
        const presentChars: string[] = [];

        for (const meta of presentMetas) {
            // [vNEXT] Under a dynamic cap, only inject for characters within the cap.
            if (_wsDynCap > 0 && !_wsDynAllowed.has(meta.id)) continue;
            const char = await loadCharacter(meta.id);
            if (!char) continue;

            // [v0.9.30 HOOK-TIER] Ambient hooks (<60) remain available via the
            // |hookDescription| macro for anyone who hand-edits cfg.contextTemplates.npc
            // directly, but are omitted from the default template.
            let hookDesc = '';
            if (char.hooks && char.hooks.length > 0 && !char.hooks[0].resolved && char.hooks[0].pressureLevel < 60) {
                hookDesc = char.hooks[0].description;
            }

            const npcData = Object.assign({}, char, buildCharMacroExtras(char, cfg), { hookDescription: hookDesc });
            // [BUGFIX v2.28.0 WORLDSTATE-STALE-THOUGHTS, per user's report] A character
            // with auto-thoughts (or auto-dynamics) turned OFF must not leak a snapshot
            // that's just sitting on the record from before the toggle was flipped, or
            // from an import/migration — char.ephemeral/midRange are NOT cleared when
            // the toggle goes off, they just stop being refreshed. Omitting the field
            // here (rather than special-casing the template string) is enough:
            // parseMacroTemplate already self-suppresses a bracket block whose
            // referenced macro resolves to undefined/empty (see its own comment above).
            // Mirrors the exact same per-character gate the retry queue already uses
            // (_rcType === 'eph' && autoThoughtsEnabled / 'mid' && autoUpdateEnabled).
            // Object.assign above already made npcData a fresh object, so this cannot
            // mutate the loaded character record.
            if (!char.autoThoughtsEnabled) npcData.ephemeral = null;
            if (!char.autoUpdateEnabled) npcData.midRange = null;
            let thoughtsLine = parseMacroTemplate(cfg.contextTemplates.npc, npcData).trim();
            // Drop a thoughts line that rendered to nothing but the bare name.
            if (thoughtsLine === (char.name || '').trim()) thoughtsLine = '';

            // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] Critical (85%+, unresolved) hook — now built
            // here instead of the onContextBuilt Imperative block. Urgent (60-84%)
            // hooks have no injection destination this pass (see Section 13 header).
            let hookLine = '';
            const topHook = (char.hooks && char.hooks.length > 0) ? char.hooks[0] : null;
            if (topHook && !topHook.resolved && topHook.pressureLevel >= 85 && topHook.description && topHook.description.trim()) {
                hookLine = parseMacroTemplate(cfg.contextTemplates.criticalHook, { name: char.name, hookDescription: topHook.description }).trim();
            }

            const combined = [thoughtsLine, hookLine].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
            if (combined) presentChars.push(combined);
        }

        if (presentChars.length > 0) {
            parts.push(presentChars.join('\n'));
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:context] buildWorldStateBlock scene chars:', e); }

    // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] NPC web — bilateral dynamics between two scene-present
    // characters, bundled into the AN alongside thoughts (user's call). Injected only
    // when BOTH parties are present, so the shared fact appears exactly once (no
    // per-character double injection) and only when contextually relevant. Sorted by
    // priority (higher first), rendered via cfg.contextTemplates.npcWeb.
    try {
        const wsWeb = await loadNpcWeb();
        if (wsWeb && wsWeb.length > 0) {
            const wsPresentIds = new Set(presentMetas.map(function(m) { return m.id; }));
            const wsNameById = new Map<string, string>();
            for (const m of presentMetas) wsNameById.set(m.id, (m as any).name || '');
            const wsTpl = (cfg.contextTemplates as any).npcWeb || '';
            const wsLines: { line: string; priority: number }[] = [];
            for (const wl of wsWeb) {
                if (!wsPresentIds.has(wl.charA_id) || !wsPresentIds.has(wl.charB_id)) continue;
                if (!wl.dynamic || !wl.dynamic.trim()) continue;
                const wsLine = parseMacroTemplate(wsTpl, {
                    nameA: wsNameById.get(wl.charA_id) || '',
                    nameB: wsNameById.get(wl.charB_id) || '',
                    dynamic: wl.dynamic
                }).trim();
                if (wsLine) wsLines.push({ line: wsLine, priority: typeof wl.priority === 'number' ? wl.priority : 30 });
            }
            if (wsLines.length > 0) {
                wsLines.sort(function(a, b) { return b.priority - a.priority; });
                parts.push(wsLines.map(function(w) { return w.line; }).join('\n'));
            }
        }
    } catch (eWeb) { api.v1.error('[AE:context] npc web injection:', eWeb); }

    return parts.join('\n\n');
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] buildTrackedNarrativeBlock and buildDramaContextBlock
// PHYSICALLY DELETED — each had exactly one caller (onBeforeContextBuild's
// questLine/dramaBlock locals), verified zero-referenced after those calls were
// removed. See CE-INDEX PASS 9 for full rationale.

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 14 — [RETIRED] legacy freeform #tag 1d6 skill-check system.
// Removed [vNEXT]: typing #force etc. directly in the story box no longer rolls.
// All skill checks now run through the Quick Action composer (2d6 fail-forward +
// the Stakes library below). UNIVERSAL_OUTCOMES / DEFAULT_OUTCOME_BANDS / rollD6 /
// getUniversalOutcome / getConsumedRoll / markRollConsumed removed with it.
// ============================================================================

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 15 — DYNAMIC ENGINE
//
// All AI generation that runs AFTER a story generation completes. This is the
// "background engine" — it updates character states (ephemeral thoughts,
// mid-range dynamics, arcs, hooks) based on the new story content.
//
// Execution order in runDynamicEngine (per character, per turn):
//   1. Retry queue — flushes characters deferred from previous budget-limited turn
//   2. Per character (active, non-protagonist, non-static):
//      a. Ephemeral thoughts (interval: thoughtIntervalTokens, ~600)
//      b. Mid-range dynamics (interval: dynamicIntervalTokens, ~1200)
//      c. Arc + Relationship update (interval: arcIntervalTokens, ~3000)
//      d. Hook update or spawn (interval: hookIntervalTokens, ~1800)
//
// Budget gates: checks api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() before dispatching.
// If budget < threshold, the character is queued in STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS for
// the next generation rather than blocking this one.
//
// HELPERS IN THIS SECTION:
//   extractJSON, extractJSONArray     — GLM output parsers
//   getRecentStoryText                — story context reader
//   generateFieldContent              — character field AI generator
//   buildCharacterStateContext        — assembles character state for generator prompts
//     [v2.16.0] now async — also folds in active charJournal bubbles (Section 10c) as a
//     directed input, not just the passive lorebook fold buildCharDynamicBlock already did
//   generateArcMilestones             — milestone generator (called from UI)
// ============================================================================

// ── GLM Output Parsers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function extractJSON(text: string): any {
    // Attempts multiple strategies to parse a JSON object from model output.
    // GLM sometimes omits the outer braces or wraps output in markdown fences.
    if (!text) return null;
    let clean = text.trim();

    // Fallback: model responded with key-value pairs missing the opening brace
    if (clean.startsWith('"') && !clean.includes('{')) {
        clean = '{' + clean;
        if (!clean.endsWith('}')) clean += '}';
    }

    try { return JSON.parse(clean); } catch {}
    try { const stripped = clean.replace(/```json\s*/gi, '').replace(/```\s*/gi, '').trim(); return JSON.parse(stripped); } catch {}
    try {
        const s = clean.indexOf('{'); const e = clean.lastIndexOf('}');
        if (s !== -1 && e !== -1 && e > s) return JSON.parse(clean.substring(s, e + 1));
    } catch (e) {}

    aeLog('[AE:engine] extractJSON failed on:', text.slice(0, 80));
    return null;
}

function extractJSONArray(text: string): any[] | null {
    // Attempts multiple strategies to parse a JSON array from model output.
    if (!text) return null;
    let clean = text.trim();

    // Fallback: model output a single object instead of an array
    if (clean.startsWith('{') && !clean.startsWith('[')) clean = '[' + clean + ']';

    try { return JSON.parse(clean); } catch {}
    try { const stripped = clean.replace(/```json\s*/gi, '').replace(/```\s*/gi, '').trim(); return JSON.parse(stripped); } catch {}
    try {
        const s = clean.indexOf('['); const e = clean.lastIndexOf(']');
        if (s !== -1 && e !== -1 && e > s) return JSON.parse(clean.substring(s, e + 1));
    } catch (e) {}

    aeLog('[AE:engine] extractJSONArray failed on:', text.slice(0, 80));
    return null;
}

// ── Story Context Reader ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function getRecentStoryText(maxTokens: number): Promise<string> {
    // Returns the most recent story text fitting within maxTokens.
    // Reads backward from the end so the freshest content is always included.
    try {
        const ids = await api.v1.document.sectionIds();
        if (ids.length === 0) return '';
        const si       = Math.max(0, ids.length - Math.ceil(maxTokens / 10) - 20);
        const sections = await api.v1.document.scan(undefined, { from: ids[si] });
        let text = ''; let used = 0;
        for (let i = sections.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            const t = sections[i].section?.text || '';
            if (!t.trim()) continue;
            const enc = await api.v1.tokenizer.encode(t, 'glm-4-6');
            if (used + enc.length > maxTokens) break;
            text = t + '\n\n' + text;
            used += enc.length;
        }
        return text.trim();
    } catch { return ''; }
}

// [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] getRecentStoryText() reads the document tail backwards.
// During a chat the document tail IS the transcript, so that function cannot see the story
// at all — it returns chat text under a "Recent Story:" header, which is precisely the bug
// generateChatEphemeral() exists to stop feeding the model. This reads the story that sits
// ABOVE the chat marker instead: same backwards-from-the-newest, same token budget, just
// starting from the marker rather than from the end of the document.
// When no marker is present (no chat open) this is exactly getRecentStoryText's behaviour.
async function _charchatStoryTextAboveMarker(maxTokens: number): Promise<string> {
    try {
        let ccRows = await api.v1.document.scan();
        if (!ccRows || !ccRows.length) return '';
        let ccCut = ccRows.length;
        for (let ccI = 0; ccI < ccRows.length; ccI++) {
            let ccSec = ccRows[ccI] && ccRows[ccI].section;
            if (!ccSec) continue;
            if (String(ccSec.text || '').indexOf(AE_CHARCHAT_MARKER_TEXT) >= 0) { ccCut = ccI; break; }
        }
        let ccText = '';
        let ccUsed = 0;
        for (let ccJ = ccCut - 1; ccJ >= 0; ccJ--) {
            let ccSec2 = ccRows[ccJ] && ccRows[ccJ].section;
            let ccT = (ccSec2 && ccSec2.text) ? String(ccSec2.text) : '';
            if (!ccT.trim()) continue;
            let ccEnc = await api.v1.tokenizer.encode(ccT, 'glm-4-6');
            if (ccUsed + ccEnc.length > maxTokens) break;
            ccText = ccT + '\n\n' + ccText;
            ccUsed += ccEnc.length;
        }
        return ccText.trim();
    } catch (eSm) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] story-above-marker read failed (thinking loses background context, not fatal):', eSm);
        return '';
    }
}

// ── Character Field Generator ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

// [v2.73.0 FIELD-CONTEXT] Shared character context for BOTH single-field generation
// and option generation. Before this, both generators saw only name, briefDescription
// and conceptDump — so a field was written without knowing the character's species,
// gender, age or role, and without seeing any SIBLING field that was already filled.
// The second half of that is the serious one: the slot instructions carry explicit
// anti-bleed clauses telling the model not to restate what belongs to another field,
// while the model had no way to see what the other fields actually said. This closes
// that: established siblings are shown, and shown as constraints.
//
// excludeLabel is matched case-insensitively against the field being written so the
// generator never sees its own current value as an established fact. Matching by LABEL
// rather than key is deliberate — neither generator takes a key, and threading one
// through would change two public signatures and every caller. The cost is that two
// fields sharing an identical label would exclude each other; labels are author-set and
// duplicates would already be confusing in the UI, so that is accepted, not overlooked.
async function buildFieldGenContext(char: CharacterRecord, excludeLabel: string): Promise<string> {
    const lines: string[] = [];
    const skip = (excludeLabel || '').trim().toLowerCase();

    // Identity line — same shape buildLorebookContent uses, so context and lorebook agree.
    const idLeader = [char.species, char.gender].map(function(s) { return (s || '').trim(); }).filter(Boolean).join(' ');
    const idMid    = [idLeader, (char.age || '').trim(), (char.occupationRole || '').trim()].filter(Boolean).join(', ');
    let identityLine = (char.name || '').trim();
    if (identityLine && idMid) identityLine += ' \u2014 ' + idMid;
    else if (!identityLine && idMid) identityLine = idMid;
    if (identityLine) lines.push('Identity: ' + identityLine);

    const nicks = (char.nicknames || []).filter(Boolean);
    if (nicks.length) lines.push('Also called: ' + nicks.join(', '));

    // Established siblings. Truncated per entry — these are meant to be short by design,
    // and a runaway hand-written field must not crowd out the rest of the context.
    const estab: string[] = [];
    function addEstab(label: string, value: string): void {
        const v = (value || '').trim();
        if (!v) return;
        if (label.trim().toLowerCase() === skip) return;
        estab.push(label + ': ' + (v.length > 240 ? v.slice(0, 240) + '\u2026' : v));
    }

    let ctxCfg: GlobalConfig | null = null;
    try { ctxCfg = await loadConfig(); } catch (eCtxCfg) { ctxCfg = null; }

    for (const fd of STATIC_FIELD_DEFS) {
        const fdLabel = (ctxCfg && ctxCfg.fieldLabels && ctxCfg.fieldLabels[fd.key]) || fd.label;
        addEstab(fdLabel, String((char as any)[fd.key] || ''));
    }
    if (char.coreValues && char.coreValues.length) addEstab('Core Values', char.coreValues.filter(Boolean).join('; '));
    if (ctxCfg && ctxCfg.customStaticFields) {
        for (const cf of ctxCfg.customStaticFields.filter(Boolean)) {
            addEstab(cf.label || cf.id, (char.customFieldValues || {})[cf.id] || '');
        }
    }

    if (estab.length) {
        lines.push('Already established about this character. Treat every line as fixed: do not contradict it, and do not restate it — each of these belongs to its own field.');
        for (const e of estab) lines.push('  - ' + e);
    }
    return lines.join('\n');
}

async function generateFieldContent(
    char: CharacterRecord,
    fieldLabel: string,
    instructions: string,
    generalStyle: string,
    hint: string,
    maxTokens: number,
    fcOpts?: { includeStory?: boolean }
): Promise<string | null> {
    // Generates content for a single character field using GLM.
    // Used by the character editor's auto-generate buttons.
    // NOTE: waitForAllowedOutput is intentionally absent — this runs from modal
    // button callbacks where the signal never fires (deadlock risk).
    // [v2.73.0 FIELD-CONTEXT] includeStory defaults TRUE here, unlike generateSlotOptions
    // where it defaults false. Not an inconsistency left lying: this function is reached
    // from the native editor's generate buttons, which have always been story-aware, and
    // flipping that default would silently change behaviour every existing user relies
    // on. Composition surfaces pass false explicitly.
    const fcStory     = !fcOpts || fcOpts.includeStory !== false;
    const recentText  = fcStory ? await getRecentStoryText(2000) : '';
    const fcContext   = await buildFieldGenContext(char, fieldLabel);
    const targetWords = Math.max(20, Math.floor(maxTokens * 0.75));

    const sys = [
        generalStyle,
        `You are writing the "${fieldLabel}" field for a character. This field has specific scope — stay inside it.`,
        instructions,
        `CONSTRAINT: Strictly under ~${targetWords} words. Finish your thought completely. ` +
        `Your output must be the content itself — do not begin with a label, header, or category name. ` +
        `Outputs that begin with words like "Voice:", "Appearance:", "Core Drive:", or any descriptor of what follows are invalid. ` +
        `Your first word must be content.`
    ].filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');

    const usrParts: string[] = [
        `Character: ${char.name}`,
        fcContext    ? fcContext : '',
        char.briefDescription ? `Anchor (do not contradict): ${char.briefDescription}` : '',
        char.conceptDump?.trim()
            ? `Reference notes (extract only what is directly relevant to the ${fieldLabel} field — ignore the rest):\n${char.conceptDump.trim()}`
            : '',
        hint?.trim() ? `Focus constraint: ${hint.trim()}` : '',
        recentText   ? `Recent story context:\n${recentText}` : '',
        `\nNow write the ${fieldLabel} field for ${char.name}.`
    ];

    try {
        const resp = await aeDiagGenerate(
            'field:' + fieldLabel + ':' + char.name,
            [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usrParts.filter(Boolean).join('\n\n') }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: maxTokens + 30, temperature: 0.8 }
        );
        return (resp.choices[0]?.text || '').trim() || null;
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:engine] generateFieldContent:', e);
        return null;
    }
}

// [v2.72.0 SLOT-OPTIONS] Generate N DIVERGENT candidate values for one field in a
// single call, for a pick-from-three UI. Deliberately NOT N calls to
// generateFieldContent: identical prompts at the same temperature mode-collapse into
// three rewordings of one idea, which is the specific failure that makes an option
// picker worthless. One call can see its own earlier options and be told to differ
// from them, and costs a third as much.
//
// Two further departures from generateFieldContent, both intentional:
//   - temperature defaults HIGHER (0.95 vs 0.8). Options want spread; a single
//     committed value wants fidelity. Same field, opposite sampling.
//   - recent story context is OFF by default. generateFieldContent always pulls 2000
//     chars of story, which is right when tuning an existing character mid-scene and
//     wrong when composing a new one — every character authored during a tense chapter
//     comes out tense. Callers opt IN via opts.includeStory.
//
// Returns null on failure or unparseable output; never throws to the caller. A short
// list is returned as-is rather than discarded — two good options beat an error.
async function generateSlotOptions(
    char: CharacterRecord,
    fieldLabel: string,
    instructions: string,
    generalStyle: string,
    hint: string,
    maxTokens: number,
    count: number,
    divergenceAxis: string,
    opts?: { temperature?: number; includeStory?: boolean }
): Promise<string[] | null> {
    const wantCount   = Math.max(2, Math.min(5, count || 3));
    const targetWords = Math.max(20, Math.floor(maxTokens * 0.75));
    const temp        = (opts && typeof opts.temperature === 'number') ? opts.temperature : 0.95;
    const useStory    = !!(opts && opts.includeStory);

    const sys = [
        generalStyle,
        `You are writing the "${fieldLabel}" field for a character. This field has specific scope — stay inside it.`,
        instructions,
        `You are producing ${wantCount} SEPARATE CANDIDATE VERSIONS of this field, not one answer and not a list of parts of one answer. ` +
        `Each candidate must independently satisfy everything above and be usable on its own.`,
        divergenceAxis
            ? `The candidates must differ from each other in kind, not in phrasing. ${divergenceAxis} ` +
              `If two candidates could be edits of the same sentence, replace one of them.`
            : `The candidates must differ from each other in kind, not in phrasing. If two candidates could be edits of the same sentence, replace one of them.`,
        `FORMAT: separate the candidates with a line containing only three hyphens. ` +
        `Each candidate strictly under ~${targetWords} words and finishing its thought completely. ` +
        `Do not number, label, rank, introduce, or comment on the candidates. ` +
        `Do not begin any candidate with a label, header, or category name — the first word of each must be content. ` +
        `Output nothing but the ${wantCount} candidates and the separator lines between them.`
    ].filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');

    const soContext = await buildFieldGenContext(char, fieldLabel);
    const usrParts: string[] = [
        `Character: ${char.name}`,
        soContext    ? soContext : '',
        char.briefDescription ? `Anchor (do not contradict): ${char.briefDescription}` : '',
        char.conceptDump?.trim()
            ? `Reference notes (extract only what is directly relevant to the ${fieldLabel} field — ignore the rest):\n${char.conceptDump.trim()}`
            : '',
        hint?.trim() ? `Steer toward this — the author has already written it into the field, so treat it as direction rather than as text to reuse verbatim: ${hint.trim()}` : '',
        useStory ? await getRecentStoryText(2000).then(function(t) { return t ? `Recent story context:\n${t}` : ''; }).catch(function() { return ''; }) : '',
        `\nNow write ${wantCount} candidate versions of the ${fieldLabel} field for ${char.name}.`
    ];

    try {
        const resp = await aeDiagGenerate(
            'slotopts:' + fieldLabel + ':' + char.name,
            [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usrParts.filter(Boolean).join('\n\n') }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: (maxTokens + 30) * wantCount + 40, temperature: temp }
        );
        return parseSlotOptions(String(resp.choices[0]?.text || ''), wantCount);
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:engine] generateSlotOptions:', e);
        return null;
    }
}

// [v2.72.0 SLOT-OPTIONS] Split a candidate block on separator lines and clean each
// entry. Tolerant on purpose — the model reliably produces the separator but is less
// reliable about NOT labelling entries, so leading "Option 2:" / "2." / "-" / "**"
// noise is stripped rather than treated as a parse failure. Falls back to blank-line
// splitting when no separator survived at all.
// Near-duplicates are dropped: two candidates opening identically are the exact
// mode-collapse this whole function exists to avoid, so returning both would be worse
// than returning fewer.
function parseSlotOptions(raw: string, wantCount: number): string[] | null {
    let txt = (raw || '').trim();
    if (!txt) return null;
    txt = txt.replace(/^```[a-zA-Z]*\s*/, '').replace(/```\s*$/, '').trim();

    let parts = txt.split(/^[ \t]*-{3,}[ \t]*$/m);
    if (parts.length < 2) parts = txt.split(/\n[ \t]*\n[ \t]*\n+/);

    const out: string[] = [];
    const seen: Record<string, boolean> = {};
    for (let p of parts) {
        let s = (p || '').trim();
        if (!s) continue;
        // Strip enumeration / emphasis / label noise the format instruction asked for but may not get.
        // NOTE: the close-paren in this character class is written \u0029 rather than
        // literally. Written literally it is a lone unmatched closing bracket in source
        // text, and this file's only structural safety net is a bracket balance-delta
        // pass against the prior trunk — one literal character here silently poisons
        // that check for every future patch. Same reason this comment spells it out in
        // words instead of showing the character.
        s = s.replace(/^(?:\*\*|__)?\s*(?:option|candidate|version)?\s*\d+\s*[.\u0029:\-\u2014]\s*/i, '');
        s = s.replace(/^[-*\u2022]\s+/, '');
        // Emphasis is stripped from each end INDEPENDENTLY, not as a matched pair. A
        // paired unwrap fails on the common '**1. text.**' shape: the enumeration strip
        // above has already eaten the opening marker, so the pair no longer matches and
        // the trailing one survives into the option text. Caught in test, not in review.
        s = s.replace(/^(?:\*\*|__)\s*/, '');
        s = s.replace(/\s*(?:\*\*|__)$/, '');
        s = s.trim();
        if (!s) continue;
        const key = s.slice(0, 40).toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '');
        if (seen[key]) continue;
        seen[key] = true;
        out.push(s);
        if (out.length >= wantCount) break;
    }
    return out.length ? out : null;
}

// ── Character State Context Builder ──────────────────────────────────────────

async function buildCharacterStateContext(char: CharacterRecord): Promise<string> {
    // Assembles a compact multi-line summary of a character's current dynamic state.
    // Passed as context to all generator prompts so GLM can update fields correctly.
    const lines: string[] = [];

    if (char.ephemeral && char.ephemeral.compressedLine) {
        lines.push(`Current moment (${char.name}): "${char.ephemeral.compressedLine}"`);
        if (char.ephemeral.eventAnchor)     lines.push(`  → Triggered by: ${char.ephemeral.eventAnchor}`);
        if (char.ephemeral.immediateDesire) lines.push(`  → Driving them to: ${char.ephemeral.immediateDesire}`);
    }
    if (char.midRange) {
        if (char.midRange.currentDesire) lines.push(`Current Desire: ${char.midRange.currentDesire}`);
        if (char.midRange.socialMask)    lines.push(`Social Mask: ${char.midRange.socialMask}`);
        if (char.midRange.baselineView)  lines.push(`Baseline View (their starting belief): ${char.midRange.baselineView}`);
        if (char.midRange.shiftedView)   lines.push(`Shifted View (belief now): ${char.midRange.shiftedView}`);
    }
    // [v2.16.0 CHARJOURNAL-WEIGHT] Active (non-archived) journal bubbles are now a
    // directed generator input, not just passive lorebook filler (buildCharDynamicBlock
    // still folds them into the lorebook separately — this is additive, not a replacement).
    // Read failures here must never break a dynamic-update cycle, so they're swallowed.
    try {
        const journal = await loadCharJournal(char.id);
        const activeBubbles = (journal.bubbles || []).filter(function(b) { return !b.archived && (b.text || '').trim(); });
        if (activeBubbles.length > 0) {
            lines.push(`${char.name}'s own journal notes (weigh these — they reflect what the character considers significant):`);
            activeBubbles.slice(-4).forEach(function(b) { lines.push(`  - ${b.text.trim()}`); });
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:engine] buildCharacterStateContext journal read:', e); }
    return lines.join('\n');
}

// ── Generators ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function generateEphemeral(
    char: CharacterRecord,
    cfg: GlobalConfig,
    genCount: number = 0 // [v0.9.24 E1] was tokenCount
): Promise<boolean> {
    try {
        const recentText = await getRecentStoryText(1500);
        const sys = [
            cfg.generalStyle,
            `Generate a three-part internal snapshot for ${char.name} based on the current moment.`,
            // [v0.9.33 AIR1] Explicit isolation: when multiple characters are present,
            // each generation call must produce only this character's perspective.
            `Focus exclusively on ${char.name}. Do not generate, reference, or echo the internal states, reactions, or thoughts of any other character present in the scene.`,
            // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] Examples describe SHAPE ONLY — no sample story content to
            // copy (observed failure: output lifted the old worked examples verbatim).
            `Return ONLY valid JSON with these exact keys:`,
            `  "eventAnchor": The one concrete, observable thing in the latest story beat ${char.name} is reacting to — a specific action, object, line, or silence, never a mood or abstraction. One sentence, normal sentence case, ending with a period.`,
            `  "emotionalResponse": ${char.name}'s own unspoken reaction, written as a first-person internal thought in ${char.name}'s own voice and cadence — not a third-person description of an emotion, and not an emotion-label word ('felt', 'angry', 'sad'). Do not use ${char.name}'s name. One sentence, normal sentence case, ending with a period.`,
            `  "immediateDesire": The single action ${char.name} intends right now — a bare verb phrase, under 10 words, no name/subject prefix. Normal sentence case, ending with a period.`,
            // [v2.53.1 THOUGHT-VOICE-CASE] Was three separate "Capitalized." cues, one per
            // field — that repetition is what was pushing the model to all-caps output
            // instead of sentence case. One explicit instruction, stated once, below.
            `Write every field in normal sentence case — a capital letter only at the very start of the sentence, never the whole field in capitals. Every detail must come from the story and character context below, never from the wording of these instructions. No markdown fences. No extra keys. Return nothing but the JSON object.`
        ].join('\n');

        const ephHistLines = (char.ephemeralHistory || []).slice(0, 2)
            .map(function(h: any) { return h.compressedLine || ''; }).filter(Boolean);

        const usr = [
            `Character: ${char.name}`,
            char.personalityCore       ? `Personality: ${char.personalityCore}` : '',
            char.voiceAndMannerisms    ? `Voice/manner: ${char.voiceAndMannerisms}` : '',
            char.midRange?.currentDesire ? `Current goal: ${char.midRange.currentDesire}` : '',
            char.midRange?.socialMask  ? `Performing: ${char.midRange.socialMask}` : '',
            char.midRange?.shiftedView ? `Currently believes: ${char.midRange.shiftedView}` : '',
            char.primaryRelationship?.enabled && char.primaryRelationship?.targetName
                ? `Primary relationship (${char.primaryRelationship.targetName}): ${char.primaryRelationship.currentStatus || 'unresolved'}` : '',
            ephHistLines.length > 0    ? `Previous thoughts (do not repeat): ${ephHistLines.join(' / ')}` : '',
            recentText                 ? `\nRecent Story:\n${recentText}` : '',
            '\n```json\n'
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');

        const resp   = await aeDiagGenerate('ephemeral:' + char.name, [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usr }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 220, temperature: 0.85 }
        );
        const parsed = extractJSON(resp.choices[0]?.text || '');
        if (!parsed || (!parsed.eventAnchor && !parsed.emotionalResponse && !parsed.immediateDesire)) return false;

        return await _persistEphemeralSnapshot(char.id, parsed, cfg, genCount);
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:engine] generateEphemeral:', e);
        return false;
    }
}
