// [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] Extracted verbatim from generateEphemeral's tail so
// generateChatEphemeral can share it. This is the ONLY thing the two generators have in
// common and the only thing worth sharing: they differ entirely in what they ask for and
// what context they ask it against, but a snapshot is a snapshot once it comes back, and
// two copies of the persistence rules (compose, cap, history push, sidebar) is how the
// two drift apart. Behaviour is unchanged from v2.37.1 for the story path.
async function _persistEphemeralSnapshot(charId: string, parsed: any, cfg: GlobalConfig, genCount: number): Promise<boolean> {
    const fresh = await loadCharacter(charId);
    if (!fresh) return false;

    const _ea = String(parsed.eventAnchor       || '').trim();
    const _er = String(parsed.emotionalResponse || '').trim();
    const _id = String(parsed.immediateDesire   || '').trim();
    const _composed: string = (_ea || _er || _id)
        ? [_ea, _er, _id].filter(Boolean).join(' — ')
        : String(parsed.compressedLine || '').trim();

    const snapshot: EphemeralSnapshot = {
        eventAnchor: _ea, emotionalResponse: _er, immediateDesire: _id,
        compressedLine: _composed, generatedAtGen: genCount // [v0.9.24 E1]
    };

    fresh.ephemeral        = snapshot;
    fresh.ephemeralHistory = [snapshot, ...(fresh.ephemeralHistory || [])].slice(0, Number(cfg.ephemeralCap) || 3);
    fresh.lastEphemeralGen = genCount; // [v0.9.24 E1]
    fresh.lastEphemeralTokens = await _aeCurrentTokenCount(); // [v2.54.0] token-mode stamp companion
    await saveCharacter(fresh);
    await refreshSidebar();
    return true;
}

// [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-POST] The chat-aware thinking generator, restored to the
// transport it should never have left. Called from onGenerationEnd's CHARCHAT branch AFTER the
// reply lands; produces the "right now" line the NEXT reply will carry.
//
// THIS IS generateEphemeral WITH A DIFFERENT PROMPT AND TWO ADDITIONS. That is the whole
// point: a clean two-message array through api.v1.generate(), no story context, no
// onBeforeContextBuild, and therefore none of NAI's turn scaffold ('<|assistant|><think>
// </think>', 'Write./nothink') that the v2.44.x context dump caught contaminating the native
// route twice per build. The user's own A/B probe confirmed this transport is never blocked by
// an active chat: outcome=ok, full budget, every run, every version of the saga.
//
// WHY IT IS NOT LITERALLY generateEphemeral — the finding that forced the split, unchanged
// since v2.38.0: generateEphemeral feeds getRecentStoryText(1500), which reads the document
// tail BACKWARDS. During a chat the tail IS the transcript, so it was handing the model a text
// conversation under the header "Recent Story:" and asking for "the latest story beat" while
// the actual story above the marker fell out of the window. Here the two are separated and
// labelled honestly: story-above-marker as background, transcript as the thing being replied
// to (buildChatThoughtBody), with the transcript explicitly terminated so the model does not
// simply continue it (v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-SHAPE — that fix was always sound, it was just
// fighting the scaffold as well).
//
// THE TWO ADDITIONS over generateEphemeral, both about the one real risk (see the CE-INDEX
// v2.45.0 block):
//   behaviour:'background' — this runs while the user reads and types. It must not block the
//     editor. Verified param, script-types.txt: generate(messages, params, callback?,
//     behaviour?, signal?).
//   a cancellation signal — parked in _ccThoughtSignal for op 'arm' to cancel at pointerdown
//     on the next Send. api.v1.createCancellationSignal() is verified in script-types.txt
//     (cancel(): Promise<void>, dispose(), cancelled). A signal that cannot be created is not
//     fatal: the call simply becomes uncancellable, which is exactly v2.29.0's behaviour.
//
// SILENT FAILURE IS THE DESIGN, per user ("it can fail silently and keep the last thought").
// Returning false writes nothing, so char.ephemeral still holds the previous snapshot and
// buildCharChatInstruction's ccEph read serves it unchanged. A cancelled call is the same
// story. There is no fallback path to write because doing nothing IS the fallback.
//
// Style note: string concatenation, not template literals, matching the CHARCHAT subsystem's
// own idiom (buildCharChatInstruction etc.) rather than generateEphemeral's.
// [BUGFIX v2.64.0 CHARCHAT-SLOT-PRIME] The empty-first-reply fix, derived entirely from
// what v2.63.0's instrumentation ruled OUT rather than from a fresh theory. Established by
// live log:
//   * onResponse fires exactly once, final=true, len=0 — a genuine empty completion, not a
//     deferral loss (the RESCUE never fired), so nothing downstream of NAI is at fault.
//   * The prompt is byte-identical on the retry that succeeds ([AE:charchat:tail] proves it),
//     so the prompt is exonerated.
//   * The bridge no longer injects anything at Send (the DOM-inject panel was deleted at
//     bridge v2.13.0), and the arm op found "no thought in flight to cancel" on the failing
//     turns, so the bridge and the cancellation path are exonerated too.
// What remains is the one asymmetry the user identified directly: with Fresh Thoughts ON,
// an api.v1.generate() runs between turns (generateChatEphemeral) and replies mostly land;
// with it OFF, nothing runs between turns and the FIRST native Send returns empty every time,
// while a second Send — or ANY intervening generation — succeeds. The generation slot needs to
// be exercised once after our end-of-turn document mutations (normalise + clearWidgets/
// createWidgets) before NAI's native editor.generate will produce tokens against them.
//
// This is a MITIGATION of a NAI-side settling issue, honestly labelled, not a claim to have
// fixed NAI: with Fresh Thoughts on the same failure still "flashes then settles" occasionally,
// so the ceiling this buys is "off-mode as reliable as on-mode," which is the whole reported gap.
//
// It reuses generateChatEphemeral's exact proven transport — aeDiagGenerate (sets
// _diagProbeActive so onBeforeContextBuild early-returns and the chat context is untouched),
// behaviour:'background' (never blocks the editor), and a cancellation signal parked in
// _ccThoughtSignal so op 'arm' cancels it at the next Send's pointerdown just like a thought.
// max_tokens:1, output discarded: this exists for its SIDE EFFECT on the slot, not its text.
async function _charchatPrimeSlot(): Promise<void> {
    let ccpSig: any = null;
    try {
        try { ccpSig = await api.v1.createCancellationSignal(); }
        catch (_ecpSig) { ccpSig = null; }
        _ccThoughtSignal = ccpSig;
        await aeDiagGenerate('chatPrime',
            [{ role: 'user', content: 'ok' }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 1, temperature: 0 },
            ccpSig, 'background');
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:prime] slot primed (Fresh Thoughts off) \u2014 a 1-token throwaway '
            + 'generation to settle NAI\u2019s generation slot after this turn\u2019s document edits, so the '
            + 'NEXT native Send does not come back empty. See CHARCHAT-SLOT-PRIME.');
    } catch (e) {
        // Identical failure posture to the thought pass: doing nothing is a safe fallback. The
        // only cost of a failed prime is that this specific turn keeps the two-press behaviour.
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:prime] slot prime failed (this turn may still need a second Send):', e);
    } finally {
        if (_ccThoughtSignal === ccpSig) _ccThoughtSignal = null;
        if (ccpSig && ccpSig.dispose) { try { ccpSig.dispose(); } catch (_ecpD) {} }
    }
}

async function generateChatEphemeral(char: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig, genCount: number = 0): Promise<boolean> {
    let ccteSig: any = null;
    try {
        try { ccteSig = await api.v1.createCancellationSignal(); }
        catch (_ecteSig) { ccteSig = null; }
        _ccThoughtSignal = ccteSig;

        let ccteSys = buildChatThoughtSystemPrompt(char, cfg);
        let ccteUsr = await buildChatThoughtBody(char);
        let ccteT0 = Date.now();
        let ccteResp: any = await aeDiagGenerate('chatEph:' + char.name,
            [{ role: 'system', content: ccteSys }, { role: 'user', content: ccteUsr }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 220, temperature: 0.85 },
            ccteSig, 'background');

        if (ccteSig && ccteSig.cancelled) {
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] cancelled mid-flight (a Send arrived) \u2014 discarded, previous thought stands.');
            return false;
        }
        let ccteText = String((ccteResp && ccteResp.choices && ccteResp.choices[0]
            && (ccteResp.choices[0].text || (ccteResp.choices[0].message && ccteResp.choices[0].message.content))) || '');
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] direct call returned ' + ccteText.length + ' chars in '
            + (Date.now() - ccteT0) + 'ms. This is the post-reply thought: it informs the NEXT reply, '
            + 'not the one that just landed.');
        if (!ccteText) return false;

        let ccteParsed = _charchatParseThought(ccteText);
        if (!ccteParsed || (!ccteParsed.eventAnchor && !ccteParsed.emotionalResponse && !ccteParsed.immediateDesire)) {
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] no usable thought \u2014 keeping the previous one, silently, as designed.');
            return false;
        }
        let ccteOk = await _persistEphemeralSnapshot(char.id, ccteParsed, cfg, genCount);
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] ' + (ccteOk
            ? 'persisted \u2014 the next reply\'s tail will carry it.'
            : 'persist failed \u2014 previous thought stands.'));
        return ccteOk;
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:charchat:thought] generateChatEphemeral failed (previous thought stands):', e);
        return false;
    } finally {
        // Identity-checked, not a blind clear. A cancelled call can settle AFTER the next
        // reply has already started its own thought, and a blind `= null` here would wipe the
        // NEW signal, silently making that one uncancellable \u2014 the exact class of bug
        // v2.34.3 removed from the hand-rolled _diagProbeActive pairs. Only the owner clears.
        if (_ccThoughtSignal === ccteSig) _ccThoughtSignal = null;
        if (ccteSig && ccteSig.dispose) { try { ccteSig.dispose(); } catch (_ecteD) {} }
    }
}

// [BUGFIX v2.41.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-PARSE] The chat thought's OWN parser. Deliberately local:
// the shared extractJSON is load-bearing for the wizard, entity batch and imgArchitect, all
// of which currently work, and the shape this has to handle is created by THIS subsystem's
// own prompt (buildChatThoughtBody ends with an open json fence to prime the model, so
// the completion legitimately arrives with no opening brace). Fixing the shared parser for a
// shape only this caller produces would put five working subsystems at risk for no gain.
//
// Order matters. Fence-strip BEFORE any brace reasoning: the fence is the reason the brace is
// missing, so repairing braces first just slices from the wrong one (extractJSON's third
// strategy does exactly that and is why this failed on every single run).
// Falls through to the shared extractJSON last, so anything that parsed before still parses.
// [v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-SHAPE] THE parser for the thought pass. Labelled lines first,
// JSON second.
//
// WHY THE WIRE FORMAT CHANGED, and the answer to "why was it JSON at all": nothing downstream
// wants JSON. _persistEphemeralSnapshot wants three named strings and reads nothing else. JSON
// was one way to carry three labels and a fragile one in this position — a single unescaped
// apostrophe or a trailing comma loses the WHOLE object (both observed live: "eventAnchor":
// Nate's defensive question about being fun.), and, sitting under a chat transcript, a json
// fence reads to the model as "write a code block in the chat" rather than "stop chatting."
// Three labelled lines cannot be broken by the character's own punctuation, degrade one field
// at a time instead of all-or-nothing, and look nothing like a chat message.
//
// SCOPE: chat thoughts ONLY, per user. This has one call site (onGenerationEnd's thought
// branch). generateEphemeral and the shared extractJSON still speak JSON and are untouched.
function _charchatParseThought(raw: string): any {
    if (!raw) return null;
    let ccR = String(raw);

    // Labelled lines. Tolerant on purpose: optional markdown bold/bullets, any case, and a
    // value that may run to the end of its line. Anchored to line starts so a label MENTIONED
    // inside a value cannot hijack the parse.
    let ccPick = function(ccLabel: string): string {
        // The trailing \\**? matters and is not padding: a model that bolds the label writes
        // **EVENT:** ..., where the closing asterisks sit AFTER the colon. Consuming only the
        // leading pair leaks '**' into the value (caught by test, not by reading).
        let ccRx = new RegExp('^[ \\t>*-]*\\**[ \\t]*' + ccLabel + '[ \\t]*\\**[ \\t]*[:\\uff1a][ \\t]*\\**[ \\t]*(.+?)[ \\t]*\\**[ \\t]*$', 'im');
        let ccM = ccR.match(ccRx);
        if (!ccM || !ccM[1]) return '';
        // Strip wrapping quotes if the model quoted the value anyway, and a trailing comma if
        // it half-remembered JSON. Both are harmless to strip and common in practice.
        let ccV = ccM[1].trim().replace(/,\s*$/, '').trim();
        if (ccV.length > 1 && ccV.charAt(0) === '"' && ccV.charAt(ccV.length - 1) === '"') ccV = ccV.substring(1, ccV.length - 1);
        return ccV.trim();
    };

    let ccEa = ccPick('EVENT');
    let ccEr = ccPick('RESPONSE');
    let ccId = ccPick('DESIRE');

    if (ccEa || ccEr || ccId) {
        // [DIAG v2.67.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-CONTENT] Was y/n presence only -- confirmed the pass
        // ran but hid the one thing worth seeing: session 4 found a live case of the thought
        // misattributing NIX'S OWN prior line to the POV character ("The last message from
        // Ramis was Wrong question, old man." -- that line was Nix's, not Ramis's), immediately
        // before an empty-reply turn. Untraceable without the actual text; y/n told us nothing.
        // Not gated separately -- fires once per turn (Fresh Thoughts on), same volume as the
        // line it extends.
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] parsed via labelled-lines'
            + ' (EVENT=' + (ccEa ? 'y' : 'n') + ' RESPONSE=' + (ccEr ? 'y' : 'n') + ' DESIRE=' + (ccId ? 'y' : 'n') + ').'
            + ' EVENT=' + JSON.stringify(ccEa) + ' RESPONSE=' + JSON.stringify(ccEr) + ' DESIRE=' + JSON.stringify(ccId));
        return { eventAnchor: ccEa, emotionalResponse: ccEr, immediateDesire: ccId };
    }

    // JSON fallback. A model that still emits the old shape must keep working — no flag day,
    // and this is also what catches a run where the prompt cache still holds the old form.
    let ccJ = _charchatExtractThoughtJSON(raw);
    if (ccJ) {
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] parsed via JSON-fallback (the model used the old wire format).'
            + ' EVENT=' + JSON.stringify(ccJ.eventAnchor) + ' RESPONSE=' + JSON.stringify(ccJ.emotionalResponse)
            + ' DESIRE=' + JSON.stringify(ccJ.immediateDesire));
        return ccJ;
    }
    return null;
}

function _charchatExtractThoughtJSON(raw: string): any {
    if (!raw) return null;
    let ccJ = String(raw).trim();
    if (!ccJ) return null;

    // 1. Drop markdown fences, opening and closing, wherever they landed. The body primed an
    //    OPEN fence, so the model's own closing fence is usually the only one present.
    ccJ = ccJ.replace(new RegExp(String.fromCharCode(96, 96, 96) + '[a-zA-Z]*', 'g'), '').trim();

    // 2. Trim anything before the first key and after the last value. The strip pass joins
    //    every new section with a newline, so stray blank lines and any prose the model added
    //    after the JSON both arrive attached.
    let ccFirstKey = ccJ.search(/"(eventAnchor|emotionalResponse|immediateDesire)"/);
    if (ccFirstKey > 0 && ccJ.lastIndexOf('{', ccFirstKey) === -1) ccJ = ccJ.substring(ccFirstKey);

    let ccTry = function(s: string, how: string): any {
        try {
            let ccP = JSON.parse(s);
            if (ccP && typeof ccP === 'object') {
                aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] parsed via ' + how + '.');
                return ccP;
            }
        } catch (_e) {}
        return null;
    };

    let ccOut = ccTry(ccJ, 'as-is');
    if (ccOut) return ccOut;

    // 3. Brace repair, WITHOUT extractJSON's fatal not-includes-brace gate. That gate is what
    //    made this unreachable: any nested brace, or the model's own closing brace, made it
    //    false while the OPENING brace was still missing. Test what is actually true -- does
    //    it START with a brace -- not whether one exists somewhere.
    let ccBraced = ccJ;
    if (!ccBraced.startsWith('{')) ccBraced = '{' + ccBraced;
    if (!ccBraced.endsWith('}')) ccBraced = ccBraced.replace(/,\s*$/, '') + '}';
    ccOut = ccTry(ccBraced, 'brace repair');
    if (ccOut) return ccOut;

    // 4. UNQUOTED VALUE repair, for the second observed failure:
    //        "eventAnchor": Nate's defensive question about being fun.,
    //    Conservative on purpose -- only the three known string keys, only when the value is
    //    clearly bare (does not start with a quote/brace/bracket/digit), and the value is
    //    taken to the last comma-or-end on its own line. A general JSON relaxer would be a
    //    far bigger blast radius to fix a case the prompt example should now prevent anyway.
    let ccUq = ccBraced.replace(
        /("(?:eventAnchor|emotionalResponse|immediateDesire)"\s*:\s*)([^"\{\[\d\s][^\n]*?)(,?\s*)$/gm,
        function(_m: string, ccK: string, ccV: string, ccTail: string) {
            let ccClean = String(ccV).trim().replace(/,$/, '').replace(/\\/g, '\\\\').replace(/"/g, '\\"');
            return ccK + '"' + ccClean + '"' + (ccTail.indexOf(',') >= 0 ? ',' : '');
        });
    if (ccUq !== ccBraced) {
        ccOut = ccTry(ccUq, 'unquoted-value repair');
        if (ccOut) return ccOut;
    }

    // 5. Last resort: the shared parser, unchanged. If it can do something we cannot, take it.
    ccOut = extractJSON(raw);
    if (ccOut) { aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] parsed via shared extractJSON fallback.'); return ccOut; }

    api.v1.error('[AE:charchat:thought] ALL parse strategies failed. Raw text follows -- if this '
        + 'looks like valid JSON to a human, the repair chain has a gap worth closing; if it looks '
        + 'like prose, the model ignored the prompt and the fix is upstream, not here:\n'
        + String(raw).substring(0, 300));
    return null;
}

function buildChatThoughtSystemPrompt(char: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): string {
    let ccPov = _aeCharChat.povName || 'the person they are texting';
    return [
        // [v2.70.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-NOSTYLE, per user] cfg.generalStyle REMOVED. It is a
        // story-PROSE style guide (voice/tense/formatting for narrative writing) and this task
        // is the opposite of narrative writing: three short labelled lines in a fixed shape
        // (see CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-SHAPE below). Sitting first in the prompt, ahead of every actual
        // instruction for this task, it cost tokens and competed with the format constraint for
        // no benefit — nothing about a story's prose style should shape a private internal
        // snapshot line. Cut, not conditioned off: no config path resurrects it here.
        char.name + ' is in a live text-message conversation with ' + ccPov + '. Generate a three-part internal snapshot for ' + char.name + ' as they read the newest message and decide how to answer it.',
        'Focus exclusively on ' + char.name + '. Do not generate, reference, or echo the internal states, reactions, or thoughts of anyone else.',
        'This is a text conversation, not a scene. ' + char.name + ' is reacting to what was SAID to them \u2014 the words, the tone, the ask, or the silence. The story context below is background: who they are and what has happened to them. It is not what they are responding to.',
        // [v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-SHAPE] Was "Return ONLY valid JSON with these exact keys".
        // The model does not obey it: handed a chat transcript it writes the next chat message,
        // because the SHAPE of the context outvotes the instruction every time (the same root
        // cause as the v2.41.0 brace bug). JSON also broke on the character's own apostrophes.
        // Three labelled lines instead — nothing downstream ever wanted JSON, only three named
        // strings (see _persistEphemeralSnapshot).
        // [v2.53.1 THOUGHT-VOICE-CASE] Was 'Do not write anything in char's voice' — that
        // directly fought the desired outcome (a natural internal thought in the
        // character's own voice) and, combined with 'each starting with its label in
        // capitals' plus three separate 'Capitalized.' cues below, is what was pushing the
        // model to render the whole line in capitals rather than just the label. The
        // distinction that actually matters — this is a private thought, not a reply that
        // gets sent — is kept; the voice restriction is not.
        'This is NOT a message and will never be sent to ' + ccPov + ' \u2014 it is ' + char.name + '\u2019s own private internal thought as they decide how to answer. Do not continue the conversation. Write RESPONSE the way ' + char.name + ' would actually think it: first person, in their own voice and cadence.',
        'Write EXACTLY three lines and nothing else. Each line starts with its label and a colon (only the label word itself is capitals, e.g. "EVENT:" \u2014 the rest of the line is normal sentence case, never written in all capitals):',
        'EVENT: The specific thing in the most recent message from ' + ccPov + ' that ' + char.name + ' is reacting to \u2014 an actual phrase, question, or refusal, never a mood or abstraction. One sentence, normal sentence case, ending with a period.',
        'RESPONSE: ' + char.name + '\u2019s own unspoken first-person thought \u2014 not a third-person description of an emotion, and not an emotion-label word (\u2018felt\u2019, \u2018angry\u2019, \u2018sad\u2019). Do not use ' + char.name + '\u2019s name. One sentence, normal sentence case, ending with a period.',
        'DESIRE: What ' + char.name + ' intends to do with their reply right now \u2014 a bare verb phrase, under 10 words, no name/subject prefix. Normal sentence case, ending with a period.',
        // [v2.41.0/v2.42.0] A worked example. Retained through the format change because it is
        // the cheapest possible lever on shape adherence, and re-cast into the labelled form.
        // [v2.53.1] RESPONSE line re-cast first-person to match the instruction above.
        'Example of the exact shape required (content is illustrative only \u2014 never reuse these words):',
        'EVENT: She asked if he was still coming on Friday.',
        'RESPONSE: Here we go again \u2014 like I haven\u2019t already blown this once.',
        'DESIRE: Deflect until the plan firms up.',
        'No quotes around the values. No JSON. No code blocks. No speaker name. No fourth line. Never write a line in all capitals \u2014 sentence case only. Every detail must come from the context below, never from the wording of these instructions.'
    ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
}

async function buildChatThoughtBody(char: CharacterRecord): Promise<string> {
    let ccPov = _aeCharChat.povName || 'the person they are texting';
    let ccStory = await _charchatStoryTextAboveMarker(AE_CHATEPH_STORY_TOKENS);
    let ccChat = '';
    try { ccChat = await _charchatCanonicalChatText(); }
    catch (eCe) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] thought body: transcript read failed, story-only context:', eCe); ccChat = ''; }
    if (ccChat.length > AE_CHATEPH_CHAT_CHARS) ccChat = '\u2026' + ccChat.substring(ccChat.length - AE_CHATEPH_CHAT_CHARS);

    let ccEphHist = (char.ephemeralHistory || []).slice(0, 2)
        .map(function(h: any) { return (h && h.compressedLine) || ''; }).filter(Boolean);

    return [
        'Character: ' + char.name,
        char.personalityCore    ? 'Personality: ' + char.personalityCore : '',
        char.voiceAndMannerisms ? 'Voice/manner: ' + char.voiceAndMannerisms : '',
        (char.midRange && char.midRange.currentDesire) ? 'Current goal: ' + char.midRange.currentDesire : '',
        (char.midRange && char.midRange.socialMask)    ? 'Performing: ' + char.midRange.socialMask : '',
        (char.midRange && char.midRange.shiftedView)   ? 'Currently believes: ' + char.midRange.shiftedView : '',
        (char.primaryRelationship && char.primaryRelationship.enabled && char.primaryRelationship.targetName)
            ? 'Primary relationship (' + char.primaryRelationship.targetName + '): ' + (char.primaryRelationship.currentStatus || 'unresolved') : '',
        'Texting with: ' + ccPov,
        ccEphHist.length > 0 ? 'Previous thoughts (do not repeat): ' + ccEphHist.join(' / ') : '',
        ccStory ? '\nStory context \u2014 background only, NOT what they are replying to:\n' + ccStory : '',
        // [v2.42.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-SHAPE] THE FIX, and it is about ORDER, not wording.
        // This body used to END with the transcript, dangling on a speaker cue ('Sara:'),
        // followed by an open json fence. A transcript ending on a speaker's name is the
        // strongest continuation cue there is, so the model did the obvious thing and wrote
        // the next chat message \u2014 confirmed verbatim in the RAW diag ('Sara: really?
        // somehow I\u2019m not convinced / Emotion: smug'). The system prompt's 'return only
        // JSON' never stood a chance: the SHAPE of a context beats an instruction about it.
        // So: the transcript is explicitly TERMINATED with a sentinel (nothing is left
        // dangling to continue), and the request for the three lines is what sits at the
        // true bottom \u2014 the most present position, which is exactly where the thing we
        // actually want asked belongs. The open fence is GONE: under a chat transcript it
        // reads as 'write a code block in the chat', which is very plausibly where the
        // literal fence wrapping a chat reply came from in the first live log.
        ccChat  ? '\nText conversation so far \u2014 THIS is what ' + char.name + ' is reacting to. It is FINISHED; do not add to it:\n'
                  + ccChat + '\n[ END OF CONVERSATION \u2014 nothing follows this line ]' : '',
        '\nThe conversation above is complete. Do NOT write the next message.',
        'Write ' + char.name + '\u2019s private internal snapshot as they read that last message, as exactly three labelled lines (EVENT / RESPONSE / DESIRE) and nothing else:'
    ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
}

async function generateMidRange(
    char: CharacterRecord,
    cfg: GlobalConfig,
    genCount: number = 0 // [v0.9.24 E1] was tokenCount
): Promise<boolean> {
    // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] Two modes. GENESIS (no midRange, or a midRange with no frozen
    // baselineView) establishes a fleshed-out STARTING state from character canon so a
    // character never enters play blank — it writes baselineView (their default belief
    // BEFORE the story touches them) and seeds shiftedView equal to it (no shift yet).
    // ONGOING re-assesses each field against the frozen baseline + the previous values
    // and is explicitly allowed to return a field unchanged — stability is signal, not a
    // failed update. History appends only on genuine change (the change gate below).
    try {
        const HIST_CAP    = 4;
        const isGenesis   = !char.midRange || !char.midRange.baselineView;
        const recentText  = await getRecentStoryText(3000);
        const rel         = char.primaryRelationship;
        const relOn       = !!(rel && rel.enabled && rel.targetName);

        // Ephemeral (moment) feeds UP into this layer.
        const midEphContext: string[] = [];
        if (char.ephemeral?.eventAnchor)       midEphContext.push(`Reacting to: ${char.ephemeral.eventAnchor}`);
        if (char.ephemeral?.emotionalResponse)  midEphContext.push(`Felt: ${char.ephemeral.emotionalResponse}`);
        if (char.ephemeral?.immediateDesire)    midEphContext.push(`Immediate drive: ${char.ephemeral.immediateDesire}`);
        const charState = char.ephemeral ? `Recent moment: "${char.ephemeral.compressedLine}"` : '';

        // Compact change-trail (most recent first) so the model can see what has held.
        const midHist = (char.midRangeHistory || []).slice(0, 2).map(function(h: any) {
            return `  (gen ${h.atGen}) wants ${h.currentDesire || '—'} / believes ${h.shiftedView || '—'}`;
        }).filter(Boolean);

        // Field contracts shared by both modes — wordings match the lorebook templates
        // that embed each value mid-sentence, so the rendered line reads grammatically.
        const RULE_DESIRE = `"currentDesire": ${char.name}'s sustained overarching goal — what they are actively working toward or protecting across scenes, NOT a momentary urge (that is the ephemeral layer). Phrase it to complete "${char.name} currently wants ___": an infinitive or noun phrase, lowercase first word, no closing period. Do not use the word "currently".`;
        const RULE_MASK   = `"socialMask": what ${char.name} outwardly performs to others AND what that performance conceals. Phrase it to complete "Outwardly, ${char.name} is presenting as ___": name the outward projection, then a comma with "while"/"though" and what is really underneath. Lowercase first word, no closing period.`;
        const BOUNDARY    = `This is ${char.name}'s standing psychological state — goals, self-presentation, worldview. It is NOT a summary of any one relationship, nor a faster copy of their current mood; reference a relationship only when it shapes something broader than that relationship itself.`;

        let sys: string;
        if (isGenesis) {
            sys = [
                cfg.generalStyle,
                `Establish the STARTING dynamic state for ${char.name}, who is entering the story. Build it from who they ALREADY are — core personality, background, and how their key relationship began — not from a reaction to the latest beat.`,
                BOUNDARY,
                `Return ONLY valid JSON with exactly these three keys, each genuinely distinct.`,
                RULE_DESIRE,
                RULE_MASK,
                `"baselineView": ${char.name}'s DEFAULT operating belief about their situation, role, or key relationship as they come INTO the story — the assumption they hold before its events change anything. Phrase it to complete "${char.name} believes that ___": a natural clause, no closing period; keep proper nouns capitalized but do not capitalize the first word unless it is a proper noun.`,
                `Do not restate one idea across fields. No markdown fences. No emotion-label words.`
            ].join('\n');
        } else {
            sys = [
                cfg.generalStyle,
                `Re-assess ${char.name}'s dynamic profile. For each field, decide whether the story since the last update genuinely changed it. If a field is still accurate, return the PREVIOUS value EXACTLY — do not rephrase for variety; a stable goal or belief is meaningful signal, not a failure to update.`,
                BOUNDARY,
                `Return ONLY valid JSON with exactly these three keys, each genuinely distinct from the others.`,
                RULE_DESIRE + ` This is the stickiest field — change it only if the story redirected what they are working toward.`,
                RULE_MASK,
                `"shiftedView": ${char.name}'s CURRENT operating belief, measured against the frozen baseline below. If events have genuinely reframed how they read the situation, a person, or themselves, state the new belief; if not, return the previous shifted view unchanged. Phrase it to complete "${char.name} has come to believe that ___": a natural clause, no closing period; keep proper nouns capitalized but do not capitalize the first word unless it is a proper noun.`,
                `No markdown fences. No emotion-label words.`
            ].join('\n');
        }

        const usr = [
            `Character: ${char.name}`,
            char.personalityCore ? `Core personality: ${char.personalityCore}` : '',
            char.background      ? `Background: ${char.background}` : '',
            relOn && rel.archetype     ? `Key relationship (${rel.targetName}): ${rel.archetype}` : '',
            relOn && rel.howItStarted  ? `  how it began: ${rel.howItStarted}` : '',
            relOn && rel.currentStatus ? `  where it stands now: ${rel.currentStatus}` : '',
            relOn && rel.trajectory    ? `  what is pushing it: ${rel.trajectory}` : '',
            midEphContext.length > 0 ? `Immediate state:\n${midEphContext.join('\n')}` : charState,
            !isGenesis && char.midRange?.baselineView ? `Frozen baseline belief (do not restate — measure change against it): ${char.midRange.baselineView}` : '',
            !isGenesis && char.midRange?.currentDesire ? `Previous goal: ${char.midRange.currentDesire}` : '',
            !isGenesis && char.midRange?.socialMask    ? `Previous mask: ${char.midRange.socialMask}` : '',
            !isGenesis && char.midRange?.shiftedView   ? `Previous shifted view: ${char.midRange.shiftedView}` : '',
            !isGenesis && midHist.length > 0 ? `Earlier states (most recent first):\n${midHist.join('\n')}` : '',
            recentText ? `\n${isGenesis ? 'The situation they are entering' : 'Recent Story'}:\n${recentText}` : '',
            '\n```json\n'
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');

        const resp   = await aeDiagGenerate('midRange:' + char.name + (isGenesis ? ':genesis' : ''), [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usr }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 220, temperature: isGenesis ? 0.85 : 0.75 }
        );
        const result = extractJSON(resp.choices[0]?.text || '');
        if (!result) return false;

        const fresh = await loadCharacter(char.id);
        if (!fresh) return false;
        const prev = fresh.midRange;

        if (isGenesis) {
            const baseline = String(result.baselineView || result.shiftedView || prev?.baselineView || '').trim();
            const nd = String(result.currentDesire || prev?.currentDesire || '').trim();
            const nm = String(result.socialMask    || prev?.socialMask    || '').trim();
            fresh.midRange = { baselineView: baseline, currentDesire: nd, socialMask: nm, shiftedView: baseline, updatedAtGen: genCount };
            // Seed the trail with the genesis state so "held since" has an origin point.
            fresh.midRangeHistory = [{ currentDesire: nd, socialMask: nm, shiftedView: baseline, atGen: genCount }, ...(fresh.midRangeHistory || [])].slice(0, HIST_CAP);
        } else {
            const nd = String(result.currentDesire || prev?.currentDesire || '').trim();
            const nm = String(result.socialMask    || prev?.socialMask    || '').trim();
            const nv = String(result.shiftedView   || prev?.shiftedView   || '').trim();
            const changed = !prev || prev.currentDesire !== nd || prev.socialMask !== nm || prev.shiftedView !== nv;
            fresh.midRange = { baselineView: prev?.baselineView || nv, currentDesire: nd, socialMask: nm, shiftedView: nv, updatedAtGen: genCount };
            // Change gate: log a distinct new state only. A field stable for many scenes
            // leaves a short history rather than a duplicate-filled churn log.
            if (changed) fresh.midRangeHistory = [{ currentDesire: nd, socialMask: nm, shiftedView: nv, atGen: genCount }, ...(fresh.midRangeHistory || [])].slice(0, HIST_CAP);
        }
        fresh.lastMidrangeGen = genCount; // [v0.9.24 E1]
        fresh.lastMidrangeTokens = await _aeCurrentTokenCount(); // [v2.54.0] token-mode stamp companion
        await saveCharacter(fresh);
        await refreshSidebar();
        return true;
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:engine] generateMidRange:', e);
        return false;
    }
}

async function generateRelationshipUpdate(
    char: CharacterRecord,
    cfg: GlobalConfig,
    genCount: number // [v0.9.24 E1] was tokenCount
): Promise<boolean> {
    if (!char.primaryRelationship.targetName) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('Requires a Target Name first.', { type: 'warning' });
        return false;
    }
    const rel        = char.primaryRelationship;
    const recentText = await getRecentStoryText(5000);
    const charState  = await buildCharacterStateContext(char);
    const HIST_CAP   = 4;

    // [v2.14.0 GENESIS] Field wordings match the lorebook templates so each renders
    // grammatically: currentStatus is a PASSTHROUGH (self-contained sentence naming both
    // parties), trajectory is fed as a bare predicate after "...bond with X is ___".
    // Examples describe SHAPE ONLY — no copyable story content. Fields are re-assessed for
    // real change and may return the previous value unchanged; history logs only changes.
    const sys = [
        cfg.generalStyle,
        `Re-assess the relationship between ${char.name} and ${rel.targetName}. For each field, decide whether the story since the previous value (shown below) genuinely moved it. If it did not, return the previous value EXACTLY rather than rephrasing. These three answer different questions — do not let them overlap.`,
        ``,
        `"currentStatus": what their interaction LOOKS LIKE right now — a self-contained sentence naming BOTH ${char.name} and ${rel.targetName} and describing only observable surface behaviour (how they speak, move, occupy space when together). No inner states, no "feels". Written as a complete sentence, capitalized, ending with a period.`,
        `  Avoid: naming an emotion instead of a behaviour.`,
        ``,
        `"trajectory": the specific force or recent event pushing this relationship — an action, a revelation, a silence — not a destination it is "heading toward". Phrase it to complete "${char.name}'s bond with ${rel.targetName} is ___": a lowercase gerund or noun phrase, no closing period.`,
        `  Avoid: a prediction of where it ends up; name the pressure acting on it now.`,
        ``,
        `"summary": the core incompatibility — what one needs from the other that they cannot or will not give, the structural knot that keeps this relationship charged. One sentence.`,
        `  Avoid: a vague feelings label.`,
        ``,
        `No markdown fences. Return ONLY valid JSON with exactly these three keys.`
    ].join('\n');

    const relStageName = resolveArchetypeStage(cfg, rel);
    const usr = [
        rel.archetype     ? `Archetype: ${rel.archetype}${relStageName ? ' (current stage: ' + relStageName + ')' : ''}` : '',
        rel.howItStarted  ? `Origin: ${rel.howItStarted}` : '',
        rel.threads.filter(Boolean).length > 0 ? `Active threads: ${rel.threads.filter(Boolean).join('; ')}` : '',
        rel.currentStatus ? `Previous status: ${rel.currentStatus}` : '',
        rel.trajectory    ? `Previous trajectory: ${rel.trajectory}` : '',
        rel.summary       ? `Previous core knot: ${rel.summary}` : '',
        charState         ? `${char.name}'s current state:\n${charState}` : '',
        recentText        ? `\nRecent Story:\n${recentText}` : '',
        '\n```json\n'
    ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');

    try {
        const resp   = await aeDiagGenerate('relationship:' + char.name, [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usr }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 200, temperature: 0.75 }
        );
        const parsed = extractJSON(resp.choices[0]?.text || '');
        if (!parsed) return false;

        const ch = await loadCharacter(char.id);
        if (!ch) return false;
        const pr = ch.primaryRelationship;
        const prevStatus = pr.currentStatus, prevTraj = pr.trajectory, prevSum = pr.summary;

        const nStatus = String(parsed.currentStatus || prevStatus || '').substring(0, 400);
        const nTraj   = String(parsed.trajectory    || prevTraj   || '').substring(0, 400);
        const nSum    = String(parsed.summary       || prevSum    || '').substring(0, 600);
        pr.currentStatus = nStatus;
        pr.trajectory    = nTraj;
        pr.summary       = nSum;

        // Change gate: append a trend snapshot only when something actually moved.
        const changed = nStatus !== prevStatus || nTraj !== prevTraj || nSum !== prevSum;
        if (changed) {
            ch.relationshipHistory = [{ currentStatus: nStatus, trajectory: nTraj, summary: nSum, atGen: genCount }, ...(ch.relationshipHistory || [])].slice(0, HIST_CAP);
        }
        ch.lastArcGen = genCount; // [v0.9.24 E1]
        ch.lastArcTokens = await _aeCurrentTokenCount(); // [v2.54.0] token-mode stamp companion
        await saveCharacter(ch);
        return true;
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:engine] generateRelationshipUpdate:', e);
        return false;
    }
}

async function generateHookUpdates(
    char: CharacterRecord,
    cfg: GlobalConfig,
    genCount: number // [v0.9.24 E1] was tokenCount
): Promise<boolean> {
    const recentText = await getRecentStoryText(4000);
    if (!recentText) return false;
    const charState = await buildCharacterStateContext(char);

    // SCENARIO 1: Update existing hook (the escalator)
    if (char.hooks && char.hooks.length > 0) {
        const hook = char.hooks[0];
        const sys = [
            `Evaluate ${char.name}'s active tension regarding ${hook.targetName}.`,
            `Current hook: "${hook.description}" (Pressure: ${hook.pressureLevel}%)`,
            ``,
            `1. PRESSURE: Adjust "pressureLevel" (0-100) based on what actually happened. Did something force it closer to confrontation, or did it cool? Be specific.`,
            ``,
            `2. REWRITE: Rewrite "description" as a single sentence that reflects the current state. The tone MUST match the pressure tier:`,
            `   0-59 (Latent): Observational. Something noticed, not yet acted on. ("Still hasn't asked why she lied about the docks.")`,
            `   60-84 (Urgent): Active. Demands action, can't be ignored. ("Has to confront him before he tells the captain what he saw.")`,
            `   85-100 (Critical): Explosive. Desperate, do-or-die imperative. ("End this before he destroys everything — one way or another.")`,
            ``,
            `Return ONLY valid JSON: {"pressureLevel": 50, "description": "..."}. No isResolved field needed.`,
            `No markdown fences.`
        ].join('\n');

        const usr = [
            charState ? `${char.name}'s current state:\n${charState}` : '',
            `Story:\n${recentText}`,
            '\n```json\n'
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');

        try {
            const resp   = await aeDiagGenerate('hookUpdate:' + char.name + ':escalate', [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usr }],
                { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 200, temperature: 0.6 }
            );
            const update = extractJSON(resp.choices[0]?.text || '');
            if (!update) return false;

            const f = await loadCharacter(char.id);
            if (!f) return false;

            const targetHook = f.hooks[0];

            // Surface detection (≥85% only) — two consecutive YES clears the hook
            if (targetHook.pressureLevel >= 85 && recentText) {
                const hookWords = [targetHook.targetName, ...targetHook.description.split(' ')]
                    .filter(function(w) { return w && w.length > 3; })
                    .map(function(w) { return w.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z]/g, ''); })
                    .filter(Boolean);
                const recentLower = recentText.toLowerCase();
                const matchCount  = hookWords.filter(function(w) { return recentLower.includes(w); }).length;
                const surfaced    = matchCount >= Math.max(2, Math.floor(hookWords.length * 0.3));
                if (surfaced) {
                    const prevCount = targetHook.surfacedCount || 0;
                    if (prevCount >= 1) {
                        // Second YES — hook surfaced and lingered. Clear it, queue a new spawn.
                        f.hooks = [];
                        f.lastHookGen = genCount; // [v0.9.24 E1]
                        f.lastHookTokens = await _aeCurrentTokenCount(); // [v2.54.0] token-mode stamp companion
                        await saveCharacter(f);
                        api.v1.ui.toast('🔥 ' + char.name + ': hook surfaced — ' + hook.description.substring(0, 50), { type: 'success', autoClose: 4000 });
                        const _hr = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, '[]') as string;
                        const _hi: string[] = JSON.parse(_hr);
                        if (!_hi.includes(char.id + ':hook')) { _hi.push(char.id + ':hook'); await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, JSON.stringify(_hi)); }
                        return true;
                    } else {
                        targetHook.surfacedCount = 1;
                        aeLog('[AE:engine] Hook surfaced (1/2) for ' + char.name + ': ' + hook.description.substring(0, 40));
                    }
                } else {
                    targetHook.surfacedCount = 0;
                }
            }

            targetHook.pressureLevel = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, Number(update.pressureLevel) || targetHook.pressureLevel));
            if (update.description && update.description.trim() !== '') {
                targetHook.description = update.description.substring(0, 300);
            }
            f.lastHookGen = genCount; // [v0.9.24 E1]
                        f.lastHookTokens = await _aeCurrentTokenCount(); // [v2.54.0] token-mode stamp companion
            await saveCharacter(f);
            return true;
        } catch (e) {
            aeLog('[AE:engine] generateHookUpdates escalator error:', e);
            return false;
        }

    // SCENARIO 2: Spawn a new hook
    } else {
        const sys = [
            `Analyze the recent story for a specific, unresolved tension involving ${char.name}.`,
            ``,
            `You are looking for: a direct conflict with another person, a threat that demands response, or an urgent unfulfilled need that recent events created. Passive feelings and general mood do NOT qualify.`,
            ``,
            `If a clear tension exists, set "targetName" to the other person's name and write "description" as a single, punchy sentence naming the core conflict. Use concrete language — what happened, what is owed, what is at stake.`,
            `  Wrong: "feels betrayed by the situation"`,
            `  Right: "Cassius read the letter — he knows, and hasn't said anything yet"`,
            ``,
            `If no qualifying tension exists, set "targetName" to "None".`,
            `Set "pressureLevel" between 20-50 for a new hook (it hasn't had time to escalate).`,
            ``,
            `Return ONLY valid JSON: {"targetName": "Name or None", "description": "...", "pressureLevel": 30}`,
            `No markdown fences.`
        ].join('\n');

        const usr = [
            charState ? `${char.name}'s current state:\n${charState}` : '',
            `Story:\n${recentText}`,
            '\n```json\n'
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');

        try {
            const resp  = await aeDiagGenerate('hookUpdate:' + char.name + ':spawn', [{ role: 'system', content: sys }, { role: 'user', content: usr }],
                { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 200, temperature: 0.65 }
            );
            const spawn = extractJSON(resp.choices[0]?.text || '');
            if (!spawn || !spawn.targetName || spawn.targetName.toLowerCase() === 'none') return false;

            const f = await loadCharacter(char.id);
            if (!f) return false;

            f.hooks = [{
                id:           api.v1.uuid(),
                targetName:   spawn.targetName.substring(0, 50),
                description:  String(spawn.description || '').substring(0, 300),
                pressureLevel: Math.min(100, Math.max(0, Number(spawn.pressureLevel) || 30)),
                developmentNotes: [],
                resolved: false
            }];
            f.lastHookGen = genCount; // [v0.9.24 E1]
                        f.lastHookTokens = await _aeCurrentTokenCount(); // [v2.54.0] token-mode stamp companion
            await saveCharacter(f);
            return true;
        } catch (e) {
            aeLog('[AE:engine] generateHookUpdates spawn error:', e);
            return false;
        }
    }
}

// ── Engine Orchestrator ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// ── AUTO-TRIGGERS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Scans scene-present entities for ActionInstances flagged autoTrigger, evaluates
// their conditions, and fires the ones that pass. Runs post-generation (onGenerationEnd)
// so it never recurses into the active generation. Auto-triggered actions always run
// silently (no nested api.v1.editor.generate) to avoid generation-in-generation.
// ── Tag-Subscription Action Resolver ─────────────────────────────────────────
// [v0.9.86] Returns the merged action list for an entity: its manually-added
// actionInstances PLUS every library template whose tags intersect the entity's
// tagSubscriptions. Templates are returned by reference (not copied) so all
// conditions, effects, branches, and resolution data are fully intact at call time.
// Deduplication is by template id — if a user already imported a template manually
// (templateId back-ref), the live copy from cfg takes precedence over the snapshot.
// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] getTagSubscribedActions always returned [] once actionTemplates/
// actionInstances were removed from the data model (both inputs are permanently empty).
// Simplified to a stub so callers don't need the AERule/ActionInstance machinery.
function getTagSubscribedActions(ent: EntityRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): any[] {
    return [];
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] Auto-trigger scanning always found zero candidates once
// getTagSubscribedActions became a stub (above) — the scan body is gone, callers
// (Schedule tick, travel/teleport, native toolbar) keep calling this harmlessly.
async function runAutoTriggers(): Promise<void> {
    return;
}

// [v2.54.0] Current persistent story token count, NaN-safe with a 0 fallback. Read by the
// generators at persist time to stamp the *Tokens companion fields, and by runDynamicEngine
// in token mode as the progress value.
async function _aeCurrentTokenCount(): Promise<number> {
    try { return Number(await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_TOKEN_COUNT, 0)) || 0; }
    catch (eTc) { return 0; }
}

async function runDynamicEngine(): Promise<void> {
    // [v0.9.24 E1] Generation-based intervals; [v2.54.0] optional token-based cadence.
    // genCount always advances per generation (the load guard below rides it regardless of
    // mode); tokenCount is only consulted when dynamicIntervalMode === 'tokens'.
    let genCount = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_GEN_COUNT, 0) as number;
    genCount = Number(genCount) || 0;
    if (genCount < 1) {
        aeLog('[AE:engine] genCount=0, skipping dynamic engine');
        return;
    }

    ae_lock = true; // [v0.9.34 LOCK1] block travel generates while background gen runs
    aeLog('[AE:TRACE] dynamic engine ACQUIRED ae_lock');
    const index = await loadIndex();
    const cfg   = await loadConfig();
    // [v2.54.0] Cadence unit + the progress value each per-character check compares against.
    const _dynMode: string = ((cfg as any).dynamicIntervalMode === 'tokens') ? 'tokens' : 'gens';
    const _dynTokenCount = (_dynMode === 'tokens') ? await _aeCurrentTokenCount() : 0;
    const _dynProgress = (_dynMode === 'tokens') ? _dynTokenCount : genCount;
    const raw   = await api.v1.tempStorage.get(STORAGE_LORE_IDS) as string | undefined;
    const activeEntryIds = new Set<string>(raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []);

    // ── Retry queue ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    const _retryRaw = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, '[]') as string;
    const _retryQ: string[] = JSON.parse(_retryRaw);
    if (_retryQ.length > 0) {
        await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, '[]');
        for (const entry of _retryQ) {
            if (api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() < 220) break;
            const [_rcId, _rcType] = entry.split(':');
            const _rc = await loadCharacter(_rcId);
            if (!_rc || _rc.isProtagonist) continue;
            if (_rcType === 'eph' && _rc.autoThoughtsEnabled) {
                const _ok = await generateEphemeral(_rc, cfg, genCount).catch(function() { return false; });
                if (_ok) api.v1.ui.toast('💭 ' + _rc.name + ': thoughts updated', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            } else if (_rcType === 'mid' && _rc.autoUpdateEnabled) {
                const _ok2 = await generateMidRange(_rc, cfg, genCount).catch(function() { return false; });
                if (_ok2) api.v1.ui.toast('🌀 ' + _rc.name + ': dynamics updated', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            }
        }
        aeLog('[AE:engine] Retry queue processed.');
    }

    aeLog('[AE:engine] genCount=' + genCount + ', active chars=' + index.filter(function(m: any) { return m.active; }).length);

    // [vNEXT] Dynamic-priority cap. Compute the permitted id set ONCE from the index
    // (no storage reads). When a cap is set, characters outside the top-N by rank are
    // skipped this turn without touching their records — the user's manual Static
    // toggle is untouched; they simply aren't processed while over the cap.
    const _dynCap = Number((cfg as any).maxDynamicCharacters) || 0;
    const _dynAllowed = computeDynamicAllowedSet(index as any, _dynCap);
    if (_dynCap > 0) aeLog('[AE:engine] dynamic cap=' + _dynCap + ', allowed=' + _dynAllowed.size);

    // ── Per-character dispatch ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    for (const meta of index.filter(Boolean)) {
        if (!meta.active) continue;
        if (meta.isProtagonist) continue;
        // [v0.9.34 PERF1] Skip storage read for chars flagged static in the index
        // (propagated there by saveCharacter since v0.9.31). Avoids a DB load per
        // static character per generation end.
        if ((meta as any).isStatic) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Static (index), skipping: ' + meta.name); continue; }
        // [vNEXT] Enforce the dynamic cap from the index before any storage read, so
        // a capped-out character costs nothing this turn.
        if (_dynCap > 0 && !_dynAllowed.has(meta.id)) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Over dynamic cap, skipping: ' + meta.name); continue; }
        const char = await loadCharacter(meta.id);
        if (!char) continue;
        if ((char as any).isStatic) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Static, skipping: ' + char.name); continue; }

        const relevant = charIsRelevant(char, activeEntryIds);

        // NaN-safe — old saves without stamps default to 0 (triggers update immediately).
        // [v2.54.0] In token mode the *Tokens stamps/intervals are read instead of the *Gen
        // ones; _dynProgress (set above) is the matching progress value. Both stamp units
        // are written on every update (see the generators), so a mode switch never compares
        // a stamp against the wrong unit.
        const _tokMode = (_dynMode === 'tokens');
        const lastEph  = Number(_tokMode ? char.lastEphemeralTokens : char.lastEphemeralGen) || 0;
        const lastMid  = Number(_tokMode ? char.lastMidrangeTokens  : char.lastMidrangeGen)  || 0;
        const lastArc  = Number(_tokMode ? char.lastArcTokens       : char.lastArcGen)       || 0;
        const lastHook = Number(_tokMode ? char.lastHookTokens      : char.lastHookGen)      || 0;
        const intEph   = Number(_tokMode ? (cfg as any).thoughtIntervalTokens : cfg.thoughtIntervalGens) || (_tokMode ? 500  : 2);
        const intMid   = Number(_tokMode ? (cfg as any).dynamicIntervalTokens : cfg.dynamicIntervalGens) || (_tokMode ? 1000 : 4);
        const intArc   = Number(_tokMode ? (cfg as any).arcIntervalTokens     : cfg.arcIntervalGens)     || (_tokMode ? 2500 : 10);
        const intHook  = Number(_tokMode ? (cfg as any).hookIntervalTokens    : cfg.hookIntervalGens)    || (_tokMode ? 1500 : 6);
        const _unit    = _tokMode ? 'tok' : 'gen';

        aeLog('[AE:engine] ' + char.name
            + ' rel=' + relevant
            + ' eph=' + char.autoThoughtsEnabled + '(' + (_dynProgress - lastEph) + '/' + intEph + _unit + ')'
            + ' mid=' + char.autoUpdateEnabled + '(' + (_dynProgress - lastMid) + '/' + intMid + _unit + ')'
            + ' budget=' + api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput());


        // [v2.17.0 CHAREDIT-TABS] Toggle semantics split, backing the Dynamic Profile
        // tab's 4-button "Enabled" bar. Previously Midrange/Relationship/Hooks all
        // shared this one autoUpdateEnabled flag (Ephemeral was already independent);
        // Relationship now runs on primaryRelationship.enabled alone, and hooksEnabled
        // (declared on every CharacterRecord since day one, serialized to the bridge,
        // but never actually read by any dispatch condition here) now genuinely gates
        // Hooks. Midrange keeps autoUpdateEnabled as its sole gate.
        if (char.autoThoughtsEnabled && relevant && (_dynProgress - lastEph) >= intEph) {
            if (api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() >= 220) {
                const ephOk = await generateEphemeral(char, cfg, genCount).catch(function(e) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Eph err:', e); return false; });
                if (ephOk) api.v1.ui.toast('💭 ' + char.name + ': thoughts updated', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            } else {
                const _rr = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, '[]') as string;
                const _ri: string[] = JSON.parse(_rr);
                if (!_ri.includes(char.id + ':eph')) { _ri.push(char.id + ':eph'); await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, JSON.stringify(_ri)); }
            }
        }
        if (char.autoUpdateEnabled && relevant && (_dynProgress - lastMid) >= intMid) {
            if (api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() >= 220) {
                const midOk = await generateMidRange(char, cfg, genCount).catch(function(e) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Mid err:', e); return false; });
                if (midOk) api.v1.ui.toast('🌀 ' + char.name + ': dynamics updated', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            } else {
                const _rr2 = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, '[]') as string;
                const _ri2: string[] = JSON.parse(_rr2);
                if (!_ri2.includes(char.id + ':mid')) { _ri2.push(char.id + ':mid'); await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_RETRY_CHARS, JSON.stringify(_ri2)); }
            }
        }
        if (relevant && char.primaryRelationship?.targetName && char.primaryRelationship?.enabled && (_dynProgress - lastArc) >= intArc) {
            if (api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() >= 200) {
                const relOk = await generateRelationshipUpdate(char, cfg, genCount).catch(function(e) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Rel err:', e); return false; });
                if (relOk) api.v1.ui.toast('💞 ' + char.name + ': relationship updated', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            }
        }
        if (char.hooksEnabled && relevant && (_dynProgress - lastHook) >= intHook) {
            if (api.v1.script.getAllowedOutput() >= 200) {
                const hookOk = await generateHookUpdates(char, cfg, genCount).catch(function(e) { aeLog('[AE:engine] Hook err:', e); return false; });
                if (hookOk) api.v1.ui.toast('🎯 ' + char.name + ': hook updated', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
            }
        }
    }

    await recalculateScenePresence();
    queueSceneStateBroadcast();
    aeLog('[AE:TRACE] dynamic engine RELEASING ae_lock');
    ae_lock = false; // [v0.9.34 LOCK1] release after all background generates complete
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 16 — IMAGE PROMPT GENERATION
// Assembles image prompts for auto-snap and Director's Cut, and dispatches
// generation requests to the bridge via EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE.
//
// [CHANGE] Widget system fully removed. All functions that formerly wrote to
// KEY_PENDING and called rebuildWidgets() now call broadcastToTM(EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE, payload)
// directly. No story document writes for image generation.
//
// [CHANGE] getCharacterData() now reads from cachedCharVisuals (CharacterVisualRecord[])
// instead of cachedCharConfigs. Field names updated: c.visuals (was c.characterVisuals),
// c.sceneVisuals, c.uc (was c.characterUC). baseSceneVisuals/baseSceneUC are not tracked
// in CharacterVisualRecord — extractBaseAdditions() returns empty strings naturally.
// Nickname matching added (CharacterVisualRecord includes nicknames[]).
//
// [CHANGE] loadSettings() renamed to loadImageSettings() throughout.
//
// [CHANGE] enhanceDirectorPrompt() and submitDirectorPhoto() extracted as standalone
// pure functions with explicit parameters — no modal state or module-level director vars.
// Called from handleBridgeMessage() (Section 18) when EVT_DIRECTOR_SUBMIT arrives.
//
// NOTE FOR ASSEMBLY: Add to Section 2 Bridge Event constants:
//   const EVT_DIRECTOR_ENHANCE_RESULT = 'DIRECTOR_ENHANCE_RESULT'; // engine→bridge
// ============================================================================

// ── Character Data Lookup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// [v0.9.7.6] Whole-word presence test. Substring matching caused short names
// (e.g. "Nev") to fire inside longer words ("never"). Boundary = anything that
// is not an ASCII letter/digit/underscore, or string start/end. Multi-word names
// match on their outer boundaries; internal spaces are unaffected.
function _nameAppearsAsWord(hayLower: string, needleLower: string): boolean {
    if (!needleLower) return false;
    let esc = needleLower.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
    let re = new RegExp('(?:^|[^a-z0-9_])' + esc + '(?:[^a-z0-9_]|$)');
    return re.test(hayLower);
}

function getCharacterData(promptText: string): { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string; baseSceneVisuals: string; baseSceneUC: string; }[] {
    // Returns an array of matched character visual records for characters mentioned in promptText.
    // [CHANGE] Reads from cachedCharVisuals (CharacterVisualRecord[]) instead of old cachedCharConfigs.
    // [CHANGE] Added nickname matching — CharacterVisualRecord includes nicknames[].
    // [CHANGE] baseSceneVisuals and baseSceneUC always return '' — not tracked in CharacterVisualRecord.
    //          extractBaseAdditions() will return empty strings for these, which is correct behaviour:
    //          the bridge receives an empty extraBaseVisuals/extraBaseUC and generates normally.
    if (!promptText || !cachedCharVisuals.length) return [];
    const lower = promptText.toLowerCase();
    return cachedCharVisuals
        .filter(function(c) {
            if (!c.name || !c.name.trim()) return false;
            if (_nameAppearsAsWord(lower, c.name.trim().toLowerCase())) return true;
            return (c.nicknames || []).some(function(nick) {
                return nick && nick.trim() && _nameAppearsAsWord(lower, nick.trim().toLowerCase());
            });
        })
        .map(function(c) {
            return {
                name:             c.name,
                // Combine core visuals + scene-specific additions for character captions
                visuals:          [c.visuals, c.sceneVisuals].filter(Boolean).join(', '),
                uc:               c.uc || '',
                baseSceneVisuals: '', // not stored in CharacterVisualRecord
                baseSceneUC:      ''  // not stored in CharacterVisualRecord
            };
        });
}

function extractBaseAdditions(matchedChars: { baseSceneVisuals: string; baseSceneUC: string }[]): { extraBaseVisuals: string; extraBaseUC: string } {
    // Extracts base-level prompt additions (added to main fp/fu, not char captions).
    // Preserved verbatim — in the merged system these fields are always '' (see getCharacterData note),
    // so this function will always return { extraBaseVisuals: '', extraBaseUC: '' }.
    return {
        extraBaseVisuals: matchedChars.map(function(c) { return c.baseSceneVisuals; }).filter(Boolean).join(', '),
        extraBaseUC:      matchedChars.map(function(c) { return c.baseSceneUC;      }).filter(Boolean).join(', ')
    };
}

// ── Auto-Snap ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function getCurrentLocationPromptLine(): Promise<string> {
    // [WP-img] Returns "Location: <name> — <resolved description>" for the
    // protagonist's current location, or '' if none. Feeds the image architect so
    // generated scenes reflect where the player is. Uses the (state-resolved)
    // description, NOT locationVisuals — locationVisuals is reserved for renders
    // that are specifically about a location.
    try {
        let locId = await loadPlayerLocation();
        if (!locId) return '';
        let loc = await loadEntity(locId);
        if (!loc) return '';
        let desc = (loc.state && loc.state !== 'default' && loc.stateDescriptions && loc.stateDescriptions[loc.state])
            ? loc.stateDescriptions[loc.state]
            : (loc.description || '');
        desc = (desc || '').trim();
        if (!loc.name && !desc) return '';
        return 'Location: ' + (loc.name || '') + (desc ? ' — ' + desc : '');
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:img] getCurrentLocationPromptLine:', e); return ''; }
}

// [v0.9.8.0] Reusable architect pass over the recent story. Reads the last
// `paragraphs` sections, detects characters (whole-word), runs the GLM architect,
// and returns the raw prompt plus the resolved character records. Shared by
// executeAutoSnap (generate now) and the AUTO_SNAP_PROMPT path (fill the Image
// Studio composer for the user to review/edit before generating).
async function buildArchitectPromptFromStory(paragraphs?: number): Promise<{ prompt: string; matchedChars: { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string; baseSceneVisuals: string; baseSceneUC: string; }[]; } | null> {
    await loadImageSettings();
    const nodeId = await api.v1.document.history.currentNodeId();
    const node   = await api.v1.document.history.nodeState(nodeId);
    if (!node || !node.sections) { api.v1.ui.toast('Error: Could not read document.'); return null; }

    const ctxSize = (typeof paragraphs === 'number' && paragraphs > 0) ? paragraphs : (globalSettings.contextSize || DEFAULT_CTX);
    let asContextText = '';
    const asStartIndex = Math.max(0, node.sections.length - ctxSize);
    for (let i = asStartIndex; i < node.sections.length; i++) {
        if (node.sections[i].section && node.sections[i].section.text) asContextText += node.sections[i].section.text + '\n';
    }

    const asMatched   = getCharacterData(asContextText);
    const asNames     = asMatched.map(function(c) { return c.name; }).join(', ');
    const asLocLine   = await getCurrentLocationPromptLine();
    const asCharCtx   = asNames ? '\n\nCharacters identified in scene: ' + asNames + '. Apply their specific visual tags.' : '';

    try {
        const asResp = await aeDiagGenerate('imgArchitect:manual',
            [
                { role: 'system', content: globalSettings.architectPrompt || DEFAULT_ARCH },
                { role: 'user',   content: 'Current Scene:\n' + asContextText.trim() + asCharCtx + (asLocLine ? '\n\n' + asLocLine : '') + '\n\nGenerate the image prompt:' }
            ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 300 }
        );
        const asPrompt = ((asResp.choices && asResp.choices[0] &&
            (asResp.choices[0].message && asResp.choices[0].message.content || asResp.choices[0].text)) || asResp.text || '').trim();
        if (!asPrompt) return null;
        return { prompt: asPrompt, matchedChars: asMatched };
    } catch (asErr) {
        aeLog('[AE:img] buildArchitectPromptFromStory failed:', asErr);
        return null;
    }
}

async function executeAutoSnap(): Promise<void> {
    // [CHANGE] Widget system removed. Previously wrote to KEY_PENDING + rebuildWidgets().
    //          Now broadcasts EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE directly to bridge.
    // [CHANGE] loadSettings() → loadImageSettings().
    // [CHANGE] Bridge guard added — auto-snap requires the bridge to be connected.
    api.v1.ui.toast('📸 Architect Pass: Analysing scene…');
    await loadImageSettings();

    if (!isBridgeReady) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('⚠️ Bridge not connected — auto-snap requires bridge.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 });
        return;
    }

    const nodeId = await api.v1.document.history.currentNodeId();
    const node   = await api.v1.document.history.nodeState(nodeId);
    if (!node || !node.sections) { api.v1.ui.toast('Error: Could not read document.'); return; }

    const ctxSize = globalSettings.contextSize || DEFAULT_CTX;
    let contextText = '';
    const startIndex = Math.max(0, node.sections.length - ctxSize);
    for (let i = startIndex; i < node.sections.length; i++) {
        if (node.sections[i].section && node.sections[i].section.text) contextText += node.sections[i].section.text + '\n';
    }

    const matchedChars = getCharacterData(contextText);
    const { extraBaseVisuals, extraBaseUC } = extractBaseAdditions(matchedChars);
    const charNames   = matchedChars.map(function(c) { return c.name; }).join(', ');
    const locLine     = await getCurrentLocationPromptLine();
    const charContext = charNames ? '\n\nCharacters identified in scene: ' + charNames + '. Apply their specific visual tags.' : '';

    try {
        const response = await aeDiagGenerate('imgArchitect:autosnap',
            [
                { role: 'system', content: globalSettings.architectPrompt || DEFAULT_ARCH },
                { role: 'user',   content: 'Current Scene:\n' + contextText.trim() + charContext + (locLine ? '\n\n' + locLine : '') + '\n\nGenerate the image prompt:' }
            ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 300 }
        );
        const rawPrompt = ((response.choices && response.choices[0] &&
            (response.choices[0].message && response.choices[0].message.content || response.choices[0].text)) || response.text || '').trim();
        if (!rawPrompt) throw new Error('GLM returned an empty prompt.');

        const compiled = compileActiveImageFields();
        await broadcastToTM(EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE, {
            uuid:             api.v1.uuid(),
            prompt:           rawPrompt,
            characters:       matchedChars.map(function(c) { return { name: c.name, visuals: c.visuals, uc: c.uc }; }),
            extraBaseVisuals: extraBaseVisuals,
            extraBaseUC:      extraBaseUC,
            settings: {
                width:    globalSettings.defaultWidth,
                height:   globalSettings.defaultHeight,
                steps:    Math.min(globalSettings.defaultSteps || 28, 28),
                scale:    globalSettings.defaultScale || 5.0,
                quality:  compiled.quality,
                negative: compiled.negative
            },
            autoDownload: globalSettings.autoDownload
        });
        api.v1.ui.toast('📸 Auto-Snap complete! Image generating in bridge…');
    } catch (err) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('Architect Error: ' + ((err as any).message || String(err)));
    }
}

// ── Director's Cut — Enhance & Submit ─────────────────────────────────────────

async function enhanceDirectorPrompt(
    prompt: string,
    chars: { name: string; visuals: string; uc?: string }[]
): Promise<{ enhancedPrompt: string; charVisuals: { name: string; visuals: string }[] } | null> {
    // [CHANGE] Extracted as pure function with explicit parameters.
    //          Old version mutated module-level state (currentDirectorPrompt, char1Vis_in, etc.)
    //          and called renderModal(). New version returns the enhanced data as a value
    //          so the caller (handleBridgeMessage / EVT_DIRECTOR_SUBMIT) can broadcast it back.
    // [v2.13.0 IMGFIELDS] Generalized from hardcoded char1/char2 (2-slot) to up to 4
    // characters, matching the new click-to-add picker's cap. No more fuzzy
    // getCharacterData(name) lookup here -- the bridge's picker already resolved
    // c.visuals from its own synced character cache before the user could edit it
    // (and per user's call, those edits are one-off, never written back), so
    // c.visuals IS the "known visuals" context; nothing left to re-look-up by name.
    if (!prompt.trim()) return null;

    const capped = (chars || []).slice(0, 4);
    const locLine = await getCurrentLocationPromptLine();

    const charContextLines: string[] = capped.map(function(c, i) {
        const label = 'Character ' + (i + 1) + (c.name && c.name.trim() ? ' (' + c.name.trim() + ')' : ' (unnamed)');
        return c.visuals && c.visuals.trim()
            ? '\n' + label + ': Known visuals are "' + c.visuals.trim() + '". Enhance and adapt for the scene.'
            : '\n' + label + ': No known visuals. Generate appropriate tags.';
    });

    const sysPrompt = 'You are an uncompromising art director. Output ONLY a valid JSON object, no markdown:\n{\n  "enhanced_prompt": "5-10 core tags + 1-2 sentences of observational prose. No quality tags or negative weights.",\n  "char_visuals": ["Tags for character 1 if present, else empty string", "...character 2...", "...character 3...", "...character 4..."]\n}\nThe char_visuals array must have exactly ' + capped.length + ' entries, in the same order as the characters listed below.';

    try {
        const response = await aeDiagGenerate('imgDirectorEnhance',
            [
                { role: 'system', content: sysPrompt },
                { role: 'user',   content: 'Rough Concept: ' + prompt + charContextLines.join('') + (locLine ? '\n' + locLine : '') + '\n\nOutput the JSON:' }
            ],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 350, temperature: 0.7 }
        );
        let rawOutput = ((response.choices && response.choices[0] &&
            (response.choices[0].message && response.choices[0].message.content || response.choices[0].text)) || response.text || '').trim();
        const jsonMatch = rawOutput.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
        if (!jsonMatch) throw new Error('No JSON block found in architect response.');
        const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
        const cv: string[] = Array.isArray(parsed.char_visuals) ? parsed.char_visuals : [];

        const enhancedChars = capped.map(function(c, i) {
            return { name: c.name, visuals: (cv[i] && String(cv[i]).trim()) ? String(cv[i]).trim() : c.visuals };
        });

        return {
            enhancedPrompt: parsed.enhanced_prompt || prompt,
            charVisuals:    enhancedChars
        };
    } catch (err) {
        aeLog('[AE:img] enhanceDirectorPrompt failed:', err);
        return null;
    }
}

async function submitDirectorPhoto(
    prompt: string,
    uc: string,
    chars: { name: string; visuals: string; uc?: string }[]
): Promise<void> {
    // [CHANGE] Extracted as pure function with explicit parameters.
    //          Old version read module-level state (currentDirectorPrompt, char1Name_in, etc.)
    //          and wrote to KEY_PENDING + rebuildWidgets().
    //          New version takes all data from parameters and broadcasts EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE.
    // [CHANGE] Bridge guard added.
    // [v2.13.0 IMGFIELDS] No more fuzzy getCharacterData(name) re-resolution here —
    // the bridge's click-to-add picker already resolved {name, visuals, uc} from its
    // own synced character cache (or left them blank for a one-off unnamed slot) and
    // the user could edit any of it; those edits are one-off, per user's call, never
    // written back to the character record, so what the bridge sends IS final. Cap
    // raised from 2 to 4 (matching the picker's own cap). A blank/unnamed slot is a
    // legitimate one-off figure now — no longer filtered out for lacking a name.
    const rawPrompt = prompt.trim();
    if (!rawPrompt) return;

    if (!isBridgeReady) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('⚠️ Bridge not connected — Director\'s Cut requires bridge.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 });
        return;
    }

    const compiled = compileActiveImageFields();
    const finalUC = uc || compiled.negative;

    const cappedChars: { name: string; visuals: string; uc: string }[] = (chars || []).slice(0, 4).map(function(c) {
        return { name: c.name || '', visuals: c.visuals || '', uc: c.uc || '' };
    }).filter(function(c) { return c.name.trim() || c.visuals.trim() || c.uc.trim(); });

    await broadcastToTM(EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE, {
        uuid:             api.v1.uuid(),
        prompt:           rawPrompt,
        characters:       cappedChars,
        extraBaseVisuals: '',
        extraBaseUC:      '',
        settings: {
            width:    globalSettings.defaultWidth,
            height:   globalSettings.defaultHeight,
            steps:    Math.min(globalSettings.defaultSteps || 28, 28),
            scale:    globalSettings.defaultScale || 5.0,
            quality:  compiled.quality,
            negative: finalUC
        },
        autoDownload: globalSettings.autoDownload
    });
    api.v1.ui.toast('📸 Manual Snap dispatched!');
}


// ---- Snapshot Image (callable: button or future actions) -------------------
// Reads the stored GLM appearance snapshot for charId, resolves the character's
// cached visuals and any location visuals, and broadcasts a tagged
// EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE { entityId, kind:'snapshot' } so the bridge can land the
// result in the snapshot gallery section and the entity's top pane.
async function assembleSnapshotImage(charId: string): Promise<void> {
    try {
        if (!isBridgeReady) {
            api.v1.ui.toast('Bridge not connected - image generation requires bridge.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 });
            return;
        }
        let aChar = await loadCharacter(charId);
        if (!aChar) { aeLog('[AE:img] assembleSnapshotImage: char not found'); return; }
        let aEntId = (aChar as any).entityId || null;
        let aEnt = aEntId ? await loadEntity(aEntId) : null;
        let snapText = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_CHAR_SNAP_PFX + charId, '') as string;
        if (!snapText) {
            api.v1.ui.toast('Generate a text snapshot first.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3000 });
            return;
        }
        let locVisuals = '';
        if (aEnt && aEnt.parentId) {
            let aLoc = await loadEntity(aEnt.parentId);
            if (aLoc && (aLoc as any).visuals) locVisuals = String((aLoc as any).visuals);
        }
        let basePrompt = [snapText, locVisuals].filter(Boolean).join(', ');
        let found = getCharacterData(aChar.name);
        let charVisuals = found.length ? found[0].visuals : '';
        let charUC = found.length ? found[0].uc : '';
        let compiled = compileActiveImageFields();
        await broadcastToTM(EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE, {
