            uuid:             api.v1.uuid(),
            prompt:           basePrompt,
            characters:       charVisuals ? [{ name: aChar.name, visuals: charVisuals, uc: charUC }] : [],
            extraBaseVisuals: '',
            extraBaseUC:      '',
            charId:           aChar.id,
            entityId:         aEntId,
            kind:             'snapshot',
            settings: {
                width:    globalSettings.defaultWidth,
                height:   globalSettings.defaultHeight,
                steps:    Math.min(globalSettings.defaultSteps || 28, 28),
                scale:    globalSettings.defaultScale || 5.0,
                quality:  compiled.quality,
                negative: compiled.negative
            },
            autoDownload: globalSettings.autoDownload
        });
        api.v1.ui.toast('Generating snapshot image for ' + aChar.name + '...', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2500 });
    } catch (snapImgGenErr) {
        api.v1.error('[AE:img] assembleSnapshotImage:', snapImgGenErr);
    }
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] aeGenerateActionImage removed — action-triggered image gen
// (the 'generate_image' effect type) was only reachable through the Actions
// effects pipeline, which is gone. Auto-Snap, Snapshot, and Director's Cut image
// generation are separate paths and are untouched.


// ============================================================================
// SECTION 18 — BRIDGE MESSAGING LAYER
// All communication between the engine (Web Worker) and bridge (Tampermonkey
// page-context script). Engine→Bridge via api.v1.messaging.broadcast().
// Bridge→Engine via the onMessage listener registered in initMessaging().
//
// Broadcast functions queue and debounce to avoid redundant round-trips when
// multiple storage writes fire in rapid succession (e.g., during runDynamicEngine).
//
// buildSceneStatePayload() is the most complex new function. It loads the
// protagonist's location + one-hop entity graph and packages the data the bridge
// needs to render the Scene HUD. Capped to one-hop range for performance (<200ms).
//
// handleBridgeMessage() is the inbound message router. New event handlers:
//   EVT_DIRECTOR_SUBMIT — bridge submitted Director's Cut (enhance or generate)
//   EVT_REQ_SCENE_STATE — bridge requests immediate SCENE_STATE_UPDATE push
//   EVT_REQ_CHAR_DATA   — bridge requests immediate CHARACTER_DATA_SYNC push
// On EVT_HANDSHAKE_ACK: immediately push char data then scene state, then AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC.
// ============================================================================

// ── Broadcast Primitives ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// openEngineModal — wraps api.v1.ui.modal.open to signal the bridge.
// Bridge adds ae-behind-modal class (display:none) on OPENED and removes on CLOSED.
// Use this instead of api.v1.ui.modal.open directly throughout the engine.
async function openEngineModal(opts: any): Promise<any> {
    // [FIX] Was calling openEngineModal(opts) recursively — infinite recursion.
    // Corrected to call api.v1.ui.modal.open(opts) which is the actual NAI API.
    broadcastToTM('AE_MODAL_OPENED', {}).catch(function() {});
    let handle: any;
    try {
        handle = await api.v1.ui.modal.open(opts);
    } catch (modalErr) {
        broadcastToTM('AE_MODAL_CLOSED', {}).catch(function() {});
        throw modalErr;
    }
    if (handle && handle.closed) {
        handle.closed.then(function() {
            broadcastToTM('AE_MODAL_CLOSED', {}).catch(function() {});
        });
    }
    return handle;
}

// ============================================================================
// UNIFIED PICKER  —  openPickerModal<T>()
//
// One modal for every "pick one item from a list" selection. Replaces the
// scattered bespoke pickers (parent, entity, resource, skill, lorebook tag, ...)
// so that search, item rendering, and selected-highlighting are consistent and
// changing them all is a one-place edit. Migrate inline pickers onto this as
// they are touched (look for leftover drawSearchablePicker / openEngineModal
// list pickers).
//
// Items render as full-width buttons: "<icon> <label>  ·  <sublabel>".
// A search box appears automatically for lists > 8 (force with searchable:true).
// The picker closes itself first, THEN runs onSelect — matching the prior
// close-then-act pattern so a reopened parent modal never stacks under it.
//
//   await openPickerModal({
//       title:       'Nest Under Entity',
//       items:       allNodes,
//       getId:       function(m) { return m.id; },
//       getLabel:    function(m) { return m.name; },
//       getIcon:     function(m) { return m.icon || '\ud83d\udccd'; },  // optional
//       getSublabel: function(m) { return m.type; },                       // optional
//       selectedId:  ent.parentId || undefined,                            // optional highlight
//       searchable:  true,                                                 // optional
//       pinnedOptions: [{ id: '__none__', label: 'None', icon: '\u2715',
//                         onSelect: function() { ... } }],                 // optional, unfiltered, on top
//       onSelect:    function(item) { ... }                               // required
//   });
// ============================================================================
interface PickerPinnedOption { id: string; label: string; icon?: string; sublabel?: string; onSelect: () => void | Promise<void>; }
interface PickerConfig {
    title: string;
    items: any[];
    getId: (item: any) => string;
    getLabel: (item: any) => string;
    getIcon?: (item: any) => string | undefined;
    getSublabel?: (item: any) => string | undefined;
    selectedId?: string;
    searchable?: boolean;
    pinnedOptions?: PickerPinnedOption[];
    onSelect: (item: any) => void | Promise<void>;
    size?: string;
    emptyText?: string;
}

async function openPickerModal(cfg: PickerConfig): Promise<void> {
    let handle: any = null;
    let term = '';
    const forceSearch = cfg.searchable === true;
    const allowSearch = cfg.searchable !== false && (forceSearch || cfg.items.length > 8);
    const pickerSize  = cfg.size || 'medium';

    // Close the picker first, then run the caller's handler. This mirrors the
    // prior bespoke order (pm.close() then reopen parent modal) so a modal a
    // handler opens never ends up stacked beneath the (still-open) picker.
    function closingCallback(fn: () => void | Promise<void>) {
        return async function() {
            try { if (handle && !handle.isClosed()) await handle.close(); } catch (e0) {}
            try { await fn(); } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:picker] select handler:', e); }
        };
    }

    function itemButton(label: string, selected: boolean, fn: () => void | Promise<void>): UIPart {
        return {
            type: 'button',
            text: label,
            style: {
                width: '100%', marginBottom: '2px', textAlign: 'left', fontSize: '12px',
                backgroundColor: selected ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.2)' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.04)',
                border: selected ? '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.4)' : '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08)'
            },
            callback: closingCallback(fn)
        };
    }

    function buildContent(): UIPart[] {
        const parts: UIPart[] = [];
        if (allowSearch) {
            parts.push({ type: 'textInput', placeholder: '\ud83d\udd0d Search\u2026', initialValue: term, onChange: async function(v: string) { term = v; await render(); } });
        }
        if (cfg.pinnedOptions && cfg.pinnedOptions.length > 0) {
            for (const po of cfg.pinnedOptions) {
                parts.push(itemButton(aeIconPfx(po.icon) + po.label + (po.sublabel ? '  \u00b7  ' + po.sublabel : ''), false, po.onSelect));
            }
        }
        const needle = term.trim().toLowerCase();
        const filtered = (!allowSearch || needle === '') ? cfg.items : cfg.items.filter(function(it) {
            const lbl = cfg.getLabel(it).toLowerCase();
            const sub = cfg.getSublabel ? (cfg.getSublabel(it) || '').toLowerCase() : '';
            return lbl.indexOf(needle) >= 0 || sub.indexOf(needle) >= 0;
        });
        if ((cfg.pinnedOptions && cfg.pinnedOptions.length > 0) && filtered.length > 0) {
            parts.push({ type: 'text', text: '\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', style: { opacity: '0.2', fontSize: '10px', textAlign: 'center', marginTop: '4px', marginBottom: '4px' } });
        }
        for (const it of filtered) {
            const itemId = cfg.getId(it);
            const icon = cfg.getIcon ? cfg.getIcon(it) : undefined;
            const sub  = cfg.getSublabel ? cfg.getSublabel(it) : undefined;
            const label = aeIconPfx(icon) + cfg.getLabel(it) + (sub ? '  \u00b7  ' + sub : '');
            const isSel = cfg.selectedId !== undefined && itemId === cfg.selectedId;
            parts.push(itemButton(label, isSel, (function(captured) { return function() { return cfg.onSelect(captured); }; })(it)));
        }
        if (filtered.length === 0) {
            parts.push({ type: 'text', text: cfg.emptyText || 'No matches.', style: { opacity: '0.5', fontSize: '11px', padding: '8px', textAlign: 'center' } });
        }
        return parts;
    }

    async function render() {
        const content = buildContent();
        try {
            if (handle && !handle.isClosed()) { await handle.update({ content: content }); return; }
        } catch (e) { /* handle stale — fall through and (re)open */ }
        handle = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-unified-picker', title: cfg.title, size: pickerSize, content: content });
    }

    await render();
}

async function broadcastToTM(event: string, data?: Record<string, any>): Promise<void> {
    // Preserved verbatim from AI engine.
    data = data || {};
    try {
        await api.v1.messaging.broadcast(
            { type: 'MESSAGE', sid: api.v1.script.id, payload: Object.assign({ event: event }, data) },
            'hypebot'
        );
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] broadcastToTM failed:', e);
    }
}

// [FEATURE v2.51.4 ENGINE-THINKING-SIGNAL] Reference count, not a boolean — onGenerationRequested
// and onGenerationEnd fire for EVERY api.v1.generate() call including nested ones (ephemeral
// thoughts, the image architect's own probe), per the existing _diagProbeActive guard comment
// elsewhere in this file ("it now fires every ephemeral/architect call"). If a main story
// generation is still in flight when a nested ephemeral call finishes, that call's onGenerationEnd
// must not turn the indicator off out from under the still-running outer one. Only broadcasts on
// the 0->1 and 1->0 transitions, not every call, so the bridge doesn't get spammed for the length
// of a busy scene.
let _aeThinkingRefs = 0;
async function _aeThinkingPulse(on: boolean): Promise<void> {
    let before = _aeThinkingRefs;
    _aeThinkingRefs = Math.max(0, _aeThinkingRefs + (on ? 1 : -1));
    if ((before === 0) === (_aeThinkingRefs === 0)) return; // no 0<->nonzero transition, nothing to say
    // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG] Gated to an active chat so ordinary story play (where
    // this pulse fires constantly and harmlessly) doesn't get spammed. This is the GHOST GLYPH's
    // real on/off timing, which is a SEPARATE signal from busyStage's send-button badge — if the
    // user is watching the glyph specifically, this is what tells us whether IT clears before the
    // underlying generation slot is actually free, independent of what the badge says.
    if (_aeCharChat.active) _ccRaceDiagMark('ghost signal -> ' + (_aeThinkingRefs > 0 ? 'ON' : 'OFF'));
    try { await broadcastToTM(EVT_ENGINE_THINKING, { on: _aeThinkingRefs > 0 }); }
    catch (eEt) { aeLog('[AE:engine-thinking] pulse broadcast failed:', eEt); }
}

// ── Character Data Broadcast ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function buildCharacterDataPayload(): Promise<CharacterVisualRecord[]> {
    // Reads all active characters and packages their visual + persona fields into
    // CharacterVisualRecord[]. Also updates cachedCharVisuals in-memory so
    // getCharacterData() stays current without a round-trip on the next call.
    try {
        const chars = await loadAllCharacters();
        const result: CharacterVisualRecord[] = chars
            .filter(function(c) { return c.active !== false; })
            .map(function(c): CharacterVisualRecord {
                return {
                    id:                  c.id,
                    name:                c.name,
                    nicknames:           c.nicknames           || [],
                    visuals:             c.visuals             || '',
                    uc:                  c.uc                  || '',
                    sceneVisuals:        c.sceneVisuals        || '',
                    avatarVisuals:       c.avatarVisuals       || '',
                    avatarWidth:         c.avatarWidth         || 832,
                    avatarHeight:        c.avatarHeight        || 1216,
                    avatarSeed:          c.avatarSeed          || 0,
                    species:             c.species             || '',
                    age:                 c.age                 || '',
                    gender:              c.gender              || '',
                    briefDescription:    c.briefDescription    || '',
                    physicalDescription: c.physicalDescription || '',
                    personalityCore:     c.personalityCore     || '',
                    voiceAndMannerisms:  c.voiceAndMannerisms  || '',
                    background:          c.background          || ''
                };
            });
        cachedCharVisuals = result; // keep in-memory cache current
        return result;
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] buildCharacterDataPayload error:', e);
        return [];
    }
}

// [v0.9.36] Single choke-point for ALL editor generations. The trace showed a
// second editor.generate() (from an auto-trigger / chain effect) racing the one in
// commitTravel, which NAI rejects with 'editor is currently blocked'. This guard
// allows exactly one in-flight generation; concurrent or lock-held requests are
// suppressed (the loser of the old race produced no output anyway), and a blocked
// error is swallowed rather than surfaced. ae_genInFlight is cleared in onGenerationEnd.
async function aeEditorGenerate(tag: string): Promise<void> {
    if (ae_genInFlight || ae_lock) {
        aeLog('[AE:gen] suppressed generate (' + tag + ') inFlight=' + ae_genInFlight + ' lock=' + ae_lock);
        return;
    }
    ae_genInFlight = true;
    try {
        await api.v1.editor.generate();
    } catch (gErr) {
        let gMsg = (gErr && (gErr as any).message) ? String((gErr as any).message) : '';
        if (gMsg.indexOf('blocked') >= 0) { aeLog('[AE:gen] editor blocked, suppressed (' + tag + ')'); }
        else { aeLog('[AE:gen] generate error (' + tag + '):', gErr); }
        ae_genInFlight = false;   // kickoff failed — release so we never deadlock
    }
}

async function broadcastCharacterData(): Promise<void> {
    if (!isBridgeReady) return;
    try {
        const payload = await buildCharacterDataPayload();
        await broadcastToTM(EVT_CHAR_DATA_SYNC, { characters: payload });
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] Character data synced:', payload.length, 'characters.');
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] broadcastCharacterData error:', e);
    }
}

function queueCharacterDataBroadcast(): void {
    // [v0.9.36] Serialize + coalesce (same rationale as queueSceneStateBroadcast).
    // commitTravel's scene-presence loop saves several characters in a row, each of
    // which queued a broadcast; this collapses them into one.
    if (!isBridgeReady) return;
    if (ae_cdbInFlight) { ae_cdbPending = true; return; }
    ae_cdbInFlight = true;
    broadcastCharacterData()
        .catch(function(e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] charData broadcast err:', e); })
        .then(function() {
            ae_cdbInFlight = false;
            if (ae_cdbPending) { ae_cdbPending = false; queueCharacterDataBroadcast(); }
        });
}

// ── Scene State Broadcast ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// [v0.9.9.2] Summarise one time condition for a calendar marker. Returns null for
// non-time conditions. Equality placement hints only fire for the '==' operator.
function aeTimeCondSummary(rule: any): { summary: string; dayOfWeek?: number; dayOfCycle?: number; absoluteDay?: number; band?: string } | null {
    let a = (rule && rule.args) ? rule.args : {};
    let t = rule ? rule.type : '';
    if (t === 'time_between') return { summary: 'between ' + (Number(a.startHour) || 0) + ':00 and ' + (Number(a.endHour) || 0) + ':00' };
    if (t === 'time_band_is') return { summary: 'during ' + (a.band || '?'), band: a.band ? String(a.band) : undefined };
    if (t === 'day_of_cycle_is') return { summary: 'cycle day ' + String(a.op || '==') + ' ' + (Number(a.day) || 0), dayOfCycle: (String(a.op || '==') === '==' ? (Number(a.day) || 0) : undefined) };
    if (t === 'day_of_week_is') return { summary: 'on weekday ' + (Number(a.dayOfWeek) || 0), dayOfWeek: (Number(a.dayOfWeek) || 0) };
    if (t === 'date_is') return { summary: 'absolute day ' + String(a.op || '==') + ' ' + (Number(a.day) || 0), absoluteDay: (String(a.op || '==') === '==' ? (Number(a.day) || 0) : undefined) };
    return null;
}

// [v0.9.9.2] Scan library templates (cheap, in-memory) for time conditions so the
// calendar can mark actions tied to times. One entry per template; the first time
// condition found provides the placement hint, the summary lists them all.

// [v1.13.3] Extracted from buildSceneStatePayload so the clock is readable without a
// resolved player location/protagonist. buildSceneStatePayload (and therefore
// getSceneState()) returns null on a fresh scenario before any location is set — that's a
// location precondition, not a clock precondition. openCalendarModal was piggybacking on the
// full scene-state payload just to reach `.clock`, which meant the calendar reported "no clock"
// on every brand-new scenario even though loadClock() itself has always been location-independent
// (it seeds from cfg.timeStartHours).

async function buildSceneStatePayload(): Promise<SceneStatePayload | null> {
    // Assembles the full HUD payload. Reads the current camera location, present
    // characters, party resources, and one-hop entity actions. Capped to one hop for
    // performance. Calls evaluateFieldCondition (Section 19) — forward reference, safe
    // at runtime.
    try {

        // 2. Current location entity — the camera. [v2.31.0 ROOT-LOCATION-HUD, per
        // user: "the HUD still shows No scene state available"] FIXES a gap v2.30.0
        // left: loadPlayerLocation() was fixed to return AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID instead
        // of null when no location exists, but this function still did
        // loadEntity(locId) unconditionally right after — and the root sentinel is
        // DELIBERATELY not a real entity (see its own comment), so that load always
        // returned undefined and this function still bailed to null every time,
        // reproducing the exact same "No scene state available" the v2.30.0 patch was
        // meant to fix. This was the actual gap, not a regression in the sentinel
        // logic itself, which was and is correct — recalculateScenePresence's
        // cRoom===camRoom match already works fine with it.
        // FIX: when locId IS the root sentinel, skip loadEntity entirely and
        // synthesize a minimal stand-in with the fields this function actually reads
        // (name/description/state/parentId) — a location-less world's "scene" is just
        // "wherever everyone's unplaced bodies already are," so this describes that
        // literally rather than pretending a real location exists.
        const locId  = await loadPlayerLocation();
        if (!locId) return null;
        let locEnt: any;
        if (locId === AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID) {
            locEnt = { id: AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID, name: 'Unplaced', description: 'No location has been created yet.', stateDescriptions: {}, state: 'default', parentId: null, type: 'location' };
        } else {
            locEnt = await loadEntity(locId);
            if (!locEnt) return null;
        }
        aeLog('[AE:TRACE] buildSceneState cameraLoc=' + locEnt.name + '(' + locId + ')');

        // 3. Resolve state-dependent description
        const locDesc = (locEnt.stateDescriptions && locEnt.stateDescriptions[locEnt.state])
            || locEnt.description || '';

        // 4. Present characters → SceneCharacterInfo[]. [CE-STRIP vNEXT] No longer
        // force-includes the protagonist via an isProtagonist OR-clause — presence is
        // now purely "co-located with the camera" (recalculateScenePresence), same rule
        // for everyone, protagonist included.
        const allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
        const presentCharacters: SceneCharacterInfo[] = allChars
            .filter(function(c) { return c.scenePresent; })
            .map(function(c): SceneCharacterInfo {
                return {
                    id:              c.id,
                    name:            c.name,
                    entityId:        c.entityId || '',
                    briefDescription: c.briefDescription || '',
                    socialMask:      (c.midRange && c.midRange.socialMask) || '',
                    compressedLine:  (c.ephemeral && c.ephemeral.compressedLine) || '',
                    isProtagonist:   !!c.isProtagonist
                };
            });

        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] Action scope / available actions / available ActionInstances /
        // follow-state annotation removed — Actions system cut. allEntityMeta is still
        // needed below by the (simplified, awareness-only) location graph.
        const allEntityMeta = await loadEntityIndex();

        // 8. Location graph — parent (up), children (down), explicit connections (side)
        // [FIX Bug3] Added currentIcon + parentIcon so bridge can render entity icons on map nodes.
        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] Travel-cost annotations (resourceTag/resourceCost/resourceIcon/
        // timeCost/gated/requiresVehicle) removed — this is now awareness/adjacency only.
        const locationGraph = {
            currentName:    locEnt.name,
            currentType:    'location',
            currentIcon:    (locEnt as any).icon || undefined,
            parentId:    locEnt.parentId || null,
            parentName:  '',
            parentType:  '',
            parentIcon:  undefined as string | undefined,
            children:    [] as { id: string; name: string; type: string; hasChildren: boolean; icon?: string; }[],
            connections: [] as { id: string; name: string; label: string; oneWay: boolean; icon?: string; }[],
            siblings:    [] as { id: string; name: string; type: string; icon?: string; }[]
        };

        if (locEnt.parentId) {
            const parentMeta = allEntityMeta.find(function(m) { return m.id === locEnt.parentId; });
            if (parentMeta) { locationGraph.parentName = parentMeta.name; locationGraph.parentType = parentMeta.type; locationGraph.parentIcon = (parentMeta as any).icon || undefined; }
        }
        for (const childMeta of allEntityMeta) {
            if (childMeta.parentId === locId && childMeta.type === 'location') {
                const lgHasGrandchildren = allEntityMeta.some(function(m) { return m.parentId === childMeta.id && m.type === 'location'; });
                locationGraph.children.push({
                    id: childMeta.id, name: childMeta.name, type: childMeta.type,
                    hasChildren: lgHasGrandchildren, icon: childMeta.icon || undefined
                });
            }
        }
        for (const lgConn of (locEnt.connectedEntityIds || [])) {
            const lgConnMeta = allEntityMeta.find(function(m) { return m.id === lgConn.targetId; });
            if (lgConnMeta) {
                locationGraph.connections.push({
                    id: lgConn.targetId, name: lgConnMeta.name,
                    label: (lgConn as any).label || '', oneWay: (lgConn as any).oneWay || false,
                    icon: lgConnMeta.icon || undefined
                });
            }
        }

        // [v0.9.81] Lateral siblings: location-type entities sharing this location's parentId
        // (null parentId = shared world root, so top-level locations are mutually reachable).
        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] awareness only — no travel cost.
        for (const sibMeta of allEntityMeta) {
            if (sibMeta.type !== 'location' || sibMeta.id === locId) continue;
            if (sibMeta.parentId !== locEnt.parentId) continue;
            locationGraph.siblings.push({
                id: sibMeta.id, name: sibMeta.name, type: sibMeta.type,
                icon: sibMeta.icon || undefined
            });
        }

        // 8b. Present items — item-type entities whose parent is the current location
        const presentItems = allEntityMeta
            .filter(function(m) { return m.type === 'item' && m.parentId === locId; })
            .map(function(m) { return { id: m.id, name: m.name, icon: (m as any).icon || undefined }; });

        const cfg = await loadConfig();

        return {
            locationId:           locId,
            locationName:         locEnt.name,
            locationState:        locEnt.state || 'default',
            presentCharacters:    presentCharacters,
            locationGraph:        locationGraph,
            presentItems:         presentItems,
            // [CE-STRIP v2.1.x] presentMinorNpcs removed — minor-NPC tier retired, everyone is a normal character now.
            // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] npcArchetypes/itemArchetypes removed from scene payload — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.
            defaultBgUrl:         (cfg as any).defaultBackgroundUrl || ''
        };
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] buildSceneStatePayload error:', e);
        return null;
    }
}

// [v0.9.8.6] Stable entry point for renderers (native HUD modals) to read scene
// state, decoupling them from buildSceneStatePayload's name. Pass-through for now;
// a cache with verb-driven invalidation can be layered here once modals consume it.
async function getSceneState(): Promise<SceneStatePayload | null> {
    return await buildSceneStatePayload();
}

async function broadcastSceneState(): Promise<void> {
    if (!isBridgeReady) return;
    try {
        const payload = await buildSceneStatePayload();
        if (payload) await broadcastToTM(EVT_SCENE_STATE, payload);
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] broadcastSceneState error:', e);
    }
}

function queueSceneStateBroadcast(): void {
    // [v0.9.36] Serialize + coalesce. The previous fire-and-forget spawned a fresh
    // broadcastSceneState() on every call; during commitTravel that meant ~5 CONCURRENT
    // payload builds, each calling loadPlayerLocation() at a different instant, so a
    // stale 'old room' payload could land after the fresh one -> visible flicker/flap.
    // Now at most one runs at a time; requests during a run collapse into a single
    // follow-up that reflects the final settled state.
    if (!isBridgeReady) return;
    if (ae_sbcInFlight) { ae_sbcPending = true; return; }
    ae_sbcInFlight = true;
    broadcastSceneState()
        .catch(function(e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] sceneState broadcast err:', e); })
        .then(function() {
            ae_sbcInFlight = false;
            if (ae_sbcPending) { ae_sbcPending = false; queueSceneStateBroadcast(); }
        });
}

// ── Messaging Init ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async function initMessaging(): Promise<void> {
    // [v0.9.29 HSK1] onMessage registration is awaited so the handler is in place
    // before onScriptsLoaded continues (fast ACKs won't be missed).
    // The handshake itself is then retried in a detached loop so story init is not
    // blocked if the bridge proxy installed after our worker was constructed.
    try {
        await api.v1.messaging.onMessage(handleBridgeMessage);
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE:bridge] initMessaging onMessage error:', e);
        return;
    }

    // Fire-and-forget retry loop. Sends the full handshake sequence (IN + OUT + REQ)
    // every 1.5 s until the bridge ACKs (isBridgeReady) or 20 attempts are exhausted.
    // Using an anonymous IIFE avoids the named-async-in-sync-body NAI sandbox constraint.
    (async function() {
        let _hsAttempts = 0;
        while (!isBridgeReady && _hsAttempts < 20) {
            try {
                await api.v1.messaging.broadcast(
                    { type: 'HANDSHAKE', sid: api.v1.script.id, payload: { handshake: 'NAI_SCRIPT:IN' } }
                );
                await api.v1.messaging.broadcast(
                    { type: 'HANDSHAKE', sid: api.v1.script.id, payload: { handshake: 'NAI_SCRIPT:OUT' } }
                );
                await broadcastToTM(EVT_HANDSHAKE_REQ, { scriptId: api.v1.script.id });
            } catch (e2) {
                aeLog('[AE:bridge] handshake attempt', _hsAttempts, 'error:', e2);
            }
            _hsAttempts++;
            if (isBridgeReady) break;
            aeLog('[AE:bridge] Handshake attempt', _hsAttempts, '— waiting for ACK…');
            await api.v1.timers.sleep(1500);
        }
        if (isBridgeReady) {
            aeLog('[AE:bridge] Bridge connected after', _hsAttempts, 'attempt(s).');
        } else {
            aeLog('[AE:bridge] Bridge did not ACK after', _hsAttempts, 'attempts. Running engine-only.');
        }
    })().catch(function(e3) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] initMessaging loop error:', e3); });
}

async function buildEntityDetailPayload(fId: string, char: any, ent: any): Promise<any> {
    const detailPayload: any = {
        id: fId,
        name: char ? char.name : (ent ? ent.name : 'Unknown'),
        type: char ? 'character' : (ent ? ent.type : 'location'),
        compiledThought: char && char.ephemeral ? char.ephemeral.compressedLine : '',
        hooks: char ? (char.hooks || []).filter((h: any) => !h.resolved) : [],
        isEquipped: ent ? !!ent.isEquipped : false,
        equippedToCharId: ent ? ent.equippedToCharId : undefined,
        isProtagonist: !!(char && char.isProtagonist)
    };

    // [v0.9.88] For item entities, resolve the character record ID whose entity body
    // is the item's parent. AE_EQUIP_ITEM requires a character record ID (not an entity ID),
    // so we expose it as ownerCharId. equippedToCharId covers the "currently equipped to"
    // case; ownerCharId covers "which character owns / carries this item" (same as unequip).
    if (ent && ent.type === 'item' && ent.parentId) {
        const _itemAllChars = await loadAllCharacters();
        const _itemOwner = _itemAllChars.find(function(c: any) { return c.entityId === ent.parentId; });
        detailPayload.ownerCharId = _itemOwner ? _itemOwner.id : undefined;
        detailPayload.parentId = ent.parentId; // expose raw entity parentId too for reference
    }

    const targetParentId = char ? char.entityId : (ent ? ent.id : null);
    const entIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
    // [v0.9.32 FOLLOW3] Compute isFollowing: char entity parented under protagonist body.
    if (char && char.entityId && ent) {
        const _fdAllChars = await loadAllCharacters();
        const _fdProtag = _fdAllChars.find(function(c) { return !!c.isProtagonist; });
        detailPayload.isFollowing = !!(ent && _fdProtag && _fdProtag.entityId && ent.parentId === _fdProtag.entityId);
    }
    const children = [];
    if (targetParentId) {
        // [v0.9.9.7] Narrative children carry storyState/isTracked/questTargetName/
        // eventDayTarget so any list that renders a character's or location's
        // narrative children (inventory, quests) can show real state instead of
        // always-locked/never-tracked placeholders. Fetched once, not per-child.
        const _cdTrackedId = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_TRACKED_NARRATIVE, '') as string;
        const childMetas = entIdx.filter(m => m.parentId === targetParentId);
        // [BUGFIX v2.18.1] childOut.id below is cEnt.id -- the child's ENTITY/body id.
        // For a character-type child that's NOT the same id cachedCharVisuals/
        // AE_FETCH_CHAR_PROFILE/AE_OPEN_CHAR_EDIT key on (the character's own storage
        // id, char.id) -- those two ids are deliberately different (see
        // spawnSceneNpc: rec.entityId = body.id, rec.id is separate). A caller that
        // used childOut.id to focus/profile a present character was silently
        // operating on the wrong record. Resolved once, only when there's a
        // character-type child to resolve, reused for all of them below.
        const _hasCharChild = childMetas.some(m => m.type === 'character');
        const _childAllChars = _hasCharChild ? await loadAllCharacters() : [];
        for (const cm of childMetas) {
            const cEnt = await loadEntity(cm.id);
            if (cEnt) {
                const childOut: any = {
                    id: cEnt.id,
                    name: cEnt.name,
                    type: cEnt.type,
                    icon: cEnt.icon || undefined,
                    description: cEnt.description || '',
                    state: cEnt.state || 'default',
                    isEquipped: !!cEnt.isEquipped,
                    equippedToCharId: (cEnt as any).equippedToCharId,
                };
                if (cEnt.type === 'character') {
                    // [BUGFIX v2.18.1] The character's own storage id -- use THIS, not
                    // childOut.id, to focus/profile/edit a present-character child.
                    const _cMatch = _childAllChars.find(function(c: any) { return c.entityId === cEnt.id; });
                    childOut.charId = _cMatch ? _cMatch.id : undefined;
                }
                if (cEnt.type === 'narrative') {
                    childOut.storyState = (cEnt as any).storyState || 'locked';
                    childOut.isTracked = (_cdTrackedId === cEnt.id);
                    childOut.questTarget = (cEnt as any).questTarget || '';
                    childOut.eventDayTarget = (cEnt as any).eventDayTarget;
                    if ((cEnt as any).questTarget) {
                        const _cdQt = entIdx.find(m => m.id === (cEnt as any).questTarget);
                        childOut.questTargetName = _cdQt ? _cdQt.name : '';
                    }
                }
                children.push(childOut);
            }
        }
    }
    detailPayload.children = children;

    // [v0.9.20] Per-character resources (charResourceDefs + charResourceValues)
    if (char) {
        const crdefs: any[] = char.charResourceDefs || [];
        if (crdefs.length > 0) {
            detailPayload.charResources = crdefs.map(function(def: any) {
                const val = (char.charResourceValues || {})[def.id] || 0;
                return { id: def.id, tag: def.tag, name: def.name, type: def.type, value: val,
                    hasBounds: !!def.hasBounds, min: def.min, max: def.max, states: def.states || [] };
            });
        }
    }

    // [v2.15.0 BRIDGE-CONDITIONS] Expose entityId (the bridge needs this as the
    // parent id for character-scoped item creation, see AE_HUD_SPAWN_ENTITY /
    // spawnItem's explicitParentId), plus the Conditions definitions + this
    // character's active state (CE-STRIP v2.6.0 PASS 10 shipped these engine-side
    // with no bridge exposure at the time — this is that catch-up). Definitions
    // are global (cfg.conditions), not character-specific, but only worth the
    // loadConfig() call when there's actually a character to report state for.
    if (char) {
        detailPayload.entityId = char.entityId;
        const bcCfg = await loadConfig();
        detailPayload.conditionDefs = bcCfg.conditions || [];
        detailPayload.activeConditions = char.activeConditions || [];
    }

    // [v2.18.0 BRIDGE-DRAMA] Same catch-up pattern as BRIDGE-CONDITIONS above:
    // ephemeral/midRange/primaryRelationship have lived on CharacterRecord since
    // day one (generateEphemeralUpdate / generateMidRange / relationship
    // generators write them) but were never serialized to the bridge — nothing
    // exposed them for the new flat Drama tab (hooks + these three) to read.
    // Passed through as-is; the bridge renders them read-only, no reshaping needed.
    if (char) {
        detailPayload.ephemeral = char.ephemeral || null;
        detailPayload.midRange = char.midRange || null;
        detailPayload.primaryRelationship = char.primaryRelationship || null;
    }

    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.5] detailPayload.actions/actionInstances removed — dead Actions remnants, always empty.

    if (ent && ent.type === 'narrative') {
        const isTracked = (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_TRACKED_NARRATIVE, '') as string) === ent.id;
        let questTargetName = '';
        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] pathNames (findTravelPath) removed with Travel.
        if (ent.questTarget) {
            const qtTgt = entIdx.find(m => m.id === ent.questTarget);
            if (qtTgt) questTargetName = qtTgt.name;
        }
        detailPayload.narrative = {
            isTracked,
            questTarget: ent.questTarget || '',
            questTargetName,
            pathNames: [] as string[],
            storyState: ent.storyState || 'locked',
            description: ent.description || ''
        };
    }

    return detailPayload;
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] performTravelRequest removed — Travel cut.


// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] toggleCharFollow removed — travel/follow cut.


// [v0.9.9.7] Shared hook resolution. Extracted from AE_RESOLVE_HOOK so the native
// character inspection view can resolve a hook without a message round-trip.
async function resolveCharHook(charId: string, hookId?: string, hookIndex?: number): Promise<void> {
    try {
        if (!charId) return;
        const fresh = await loadCharacter(charId);
        if (!fresh) return;
        if (!fresh.hooks) fresh.hooks = [];

        let idx = -1;
        if (hookId) {
            idx = fresh.hooks.findIndex((h: any) => h.id === hookId);
        } else if (typeof hookIndex === 'number') {
            idx = hookIndex;
        }

        if (idx >= 0 && idx < fresh.hooks.length) {
            const hkFresh = fresh;
            const hkDesc = hkFresh.hooks[idx].description.substring(0, 50);
            hkFresh.hooks.splice(idx, 1);
            await saveCharacter(hkFresh);
            api.v1.ui.toast('✓ Resolved: ' + hkDesc, { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
            await broadcastCharacterData();
            await broadcastSceneState();

            let ent = null;
            if (hkFresh.entityId) ent = await loadEntity(hkFresh.entityId);
            const detailPayload = await buildEntityDetailPayload(charId, hkFresh, ent);
            await broadcastToTM('AE_ENTITY_DETAIL_RESPONSE', detailPayload);
        }
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] resolveCharHook error:', e); }
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] toggleNarrativeTracking removed — quest-tracker cut.


// [v0.9.9.7] Shared equip/unequip. Extracted from AE_EQUIP_ITEM / AE_UNEQUIP_ITEM
// (merged via the equip flag) so native item rows can toggle equip state without a
// message round-trip. Rule preserved exactly: the toggle only ever targets the
// item's own owner/parent character id passed in as charId -- callers resolve which
// character that is (owner for equip, equippedToCharId for unequip); there is no
// character picker.
async function setItemEquip(charId: string, itemId: string, equip: boolean): Promise<void> {
    try {
        if (!charId || !itemId) return;
        const char = await loadCharacter(charId);
        const item = await loadEntity(itemId);
        if (!char || !item) return;
        await aeSetEquip(item, char, equip);
        api.v1.ui.toast(equip ? ('🎒 Equipped: ' + item.name) : ('🎒 Unequipped: ' + item.name), { type: equip ? 'success' : 'info', autoClose: 2000 });
        await broadcastCharacterData();
        await broadcastSceneState();

        let ent = null;
        if (char.entityId) ent = await loadEntity(char.entityId);
        const detailPayload = await buildEntityDetailPayload(charId, char, ent);
        await broadcastToTM('AE_ENTITY_DETAIL_RESPONSE', detailPayload);
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] setItemEquip error:', e); }
}

// ── Inbound Message Router ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// [v0.9.101 ENTITIES] Normalize a kind's entities (memory sections / species / factions)
// into a uniform shape for the HUD. Reads the SAME cfg stores the engine wizard writes.
async function buildWizardEntitiesPayload(kind: string): Promise<any> {
    let ecfg = await loadConfig();
    let items: any[] = [];
    if (kind === 'memory') {
        items = (ecfg.wizardSections || []).map(function(sx: WizardMemorySection) {
            return { id: sx.id, name: sx.label, content: sx.content || '', enabled: sx.enabled !== false, target: sx.target || 'memory', instructions: sx.instructions || '' };
        });
    } else if (kind === 'species') {
        items = (ecfg.species || []).map(function(x: any) { return { id: x.id, name: x.name, content: x.description || '' }; });
    } else if (kind === 'factions') {
        items = (ecfg.factions || []).map(function(x: any) { return { id: x.id, name: x.name, content: x.description || '', relationshipStates: x.relationshipStates || [] }; });
    }
    return { kind: kind, items: items };
}

// [v0.9.97 WIZHUD] Build the wizard snapshot for the HUD: topic registry, chat list
// (lightweight), and the active chat in full (messages + context selection).
async function buildWizardStatePayload(): Promise<any> {
    let wcfg = await loadConfig();
    let topicsSrc = ((wcfg as any).wizardTopics && (wcfg as any).wizardTopics.length) ? (wcfg as any).wizardTopics : DEFAULT_WIZARD_TOPICS;
    let topics = topicsSrc.map(function(t: WizardTopic) { return { id: t.id, label: t.label, commitKind: t.commitKind }; });
    let chats = await loadWizardChats();
    let list = chats.map(function(c: WizardChat) {
        return { id: c.id, topicId: c.topicId, title: c.title, updatedAt: c.updatedAt, msgCount: (c.messages || []).length };
    }).sort(function(a: any, b: any) { return b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt; });
    let activeId = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_WIZ_ACTIVE_CHAT, '') as string;
    let active = null;
    if (activeId) {
        let ac = await loadWizardChat(activeId);
        if (ac) {
            active = {
                id: ac.id, topicId: ac.topicId, title: ac.title,
                messages: ac.messages || [],
                contextChatIds: ac.contextChatIds || [],
                lorebookEntryIds: ac.lorebookEntryIds || [],
                includeStory: !!ac.includeStory
            };
        } else { activeId = ''; }
    }
    return { topics: topics, chats: list, activeChatId: activeId, activeChat: active, project: { name: wcfg.projectName || '', genre: wcfg.projectGenre || '', concept: wcfg.projectConcept || '' } };
}

// [v0.9.97 WIZHUD] List selectable context sources for a chat: other wizard chats,
// lorebook entries, and characters (each flagged whether it already has a lore entry,
// so the picker can show 'will create on add'). Mirrors buildWizardContext's sources.
async function buildWizardSourcesPayload(chatId: string): Promise<any> {
    let chats = await loadWizardChats();
    let otherChats = chats.filter(function(c: WizardChat) { return c.id !== chatId; })
        .map(function(c: WizardChat) { return { id: c.id, title: c.title, topicId: c.topicId }; });
    let lbEntries: any[] = [];
    try {
        let entries = await api.v1.lorebook.entries();
        lbEntries = (entries || []).map(function(e: any) {
            let label = e.displayName || (e.keys && e.keys.length ? e.keys[0] : 'Untitled');
            let preview = (e.text || '').slice(0, 80);
            return { id: e.id, label: label, preview: preview };
        });
    } catch (eWlb) { lbEntries = []; }
    let chars = await loadAllCharacters();
    let charSources = chars.map(function(ch: CharacterRecord) {
        return { id: ch.id, name: ch.name, hasLoreEntry: !!ch.lorebookEntryId, lorebookEntryId: ch.lorebookEntryId || '' };
    });
    let active = chatId ? await loadWizardChat(chatId) : null;
    return {
        chatId: chatId,
        chats: otherChats,
        lorebookEntries: lbEntries,
        characters: charSources,
        selected: active ? {
            contextChatIds: active.contextChatIds || [],
            lorebookEntryIds: active.lorebookEntryIds || [],
            characterIds: active.characterIds || [],
            includeStory: !!active.includeStory
        } : { contextChatIds: [], lorebookEntryIds: [], characterIds: [], includeStory: false }
    };
}

// [v0.9.93 PROFILE] Narrative fields regenerable per-field from the concept dump alone
// (seed-and-go). These map 1:1 to generateCharacterFields' labelled output, so a per-field
// regen runs that generator and applies only the requested key.
var REGENERABLE_CHAR_FIELDS: string[] = [
    'species', 'age', 'gender', 'occupationRole', 'briefDescription',
    'physicalDescription', 'personalityCore', 'voiceAndMannerisms', 'background', 'coreValues'
];

// [v0.9.93 PROFILE] Build the full editable character profile for the HUD. Reads the SAME
// CharacterRecord the native editor reads — single source of truth, no divergence.
async function buildCharProfilePayload(char: CharacterRecord, cfg: GlobalConfig): Promise<any> {
    // Custom static fields: pair each def with its current value.
    let pCustom: any[] = [];
    for (let pCf of (cfg.customStaticFields || [])) {
        pCustom.push({ id: pCf.id, key: 'custom.' + pCf.id, label: pCf.label, instructions: pCf.instructions || '', value: (char.customFieldValues || {})[pCf.id] || '' });
    }

    // Faction relationships paired with display names.
    let pFactionRels: any[] = [];
    let pCfr = char.charFactionRelationships || {};
    for (let pFid of Object.keys(pCfr)) {
        let pFdef = (cfg.factions || []).find(function(x: any) { return x.id === pFid; });
        pFactionRels.push({ id: pFid, name: pFdef ? pFdef.name : pFid, value: pCfr[pFid] });
    }

    let pPr = char.primaryRelationship || ({} as PrimaryRelationship);

    return {
        id: char.id,
        name: char.name,
        isProtagonist: !!char.isProtagonist,
        hasLoreEntry: !!char.lorebookEntryId,
        conceptDump: char.conceptDump || '',
        nicknames: char.nicknames || [],
        species: char.species || '',
        age: char.age || '',
        gender: char.gender || '',
        occupationRole: char.occupationRole || '',
        briefDescription: char.briefDescription || '',
        physicalDescription: char.physicalDescription || '',
        personalityCore: char.personalityCore || '',
        voiceAndMannerisms: char.voiceAndMannerisms || '',
        background: char.background || '',
        coreValues: char.coreValues || [],
        customFields: pCustom,
        sheet: [], // Retired legacy character sheet attribute slots
        primaryRelationship: {
            enabled: pPr.enabled !== false,
            targetName: pPr.targetName || '',
            archetype: pPr.archetype || '',
            stageIndex: pPr.stageIndex || 0,
            howItStarted: pPr.howItStarted || '',
            currentStatus: pPr.currentStatus || '',
            trajectory: pPr.trajectory || '',
            summary: pPr.summary || '',
            threads: pPr.threads || []
        },
        factionRelationships: pFactionRels,
        hooks: (char.hooks || []).filter(function(h: any) { return !h.resolved; }),
        charResources: (char.charResourceDefs || []).map(function(def: any) {
            return { id: def.id, tag: def.tag, name: def.name, type: def.type,
                value: (char.charResourceValues || {})[def.id] || 0,
                hasBounds: !!def.hasBounds, min: def.min, max: def.max, states: def.states || [] };
        }),
        flags: {
            charContextEnabled: !!char.charContextEnabled,
            autoUpdateEnabled: !!char.autoUpdateEnabled,
            autoThoughtsEnabled: !!char.autoThoughtsEnabled,
            hooksEnabled: !!char.hooksEnabled
        },
        regenerableFields: REGENERABLE_CHAR_FIELDS
    };
}

async function handleBridgeMessage(msg: any): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const payload: any = (msg.data && msg.data.payload) ? msg.data.payload : (msg.data || {});
        const event: string = payload.event;

        switch (event) {

            case EVT_HANDSHAKE_ACK:
                // [CHANGE] On ACK: push char data first (bridge needs it for image gen lookups),
                //          then scene state (needs char data for compressedLine/socialMask),
                //          then AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC (bridge needs the field library + defaults
                //          for the Image Studio widget and avatar generation quality).
                isBridgeReady = true;
                aeLog('[AE:bridge] Bridge handshake confirmed.');
                api.v1.ui.toast('🔗 Bridge connected!', { type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 });
                await broadcastCharacterData();
                await broadcastSceneState();
                try {
                    await broadcastToTM('AE_IMG_FIELDS_SYNC', {
                        fields: imagePromptFields,
                        defaults: { width: globalSettings.defaultWidth, height: globalSettings.defaultHeight, scale: globalSettings.defaultScale, steps: globalSettings.defaultSteps }
                    });
                } catch (_) {}
                try { await broadcastJournal(); } catch (_) {}   // [v1.7.0 JOURNAL] populate HUD journal on connect
                // [v2.56.1] Story ID sync — previously only sent from the dead
                // EVT_CHAR_SYNC_PUSH handler, so activeStoryId was always empty
                // and the image gallery could never scope by story.
                try {
                    const hsStoryId = await api.v1.story.id();
                    await broadcastToTM(EVT_STORY_ID_SYNC, { storyId: hsStoryId });
                } catch (_) {}
                // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] broadcastHolding() removed — Holding cut.
                break;

            case EVT_PING:
                await broadcastToTM(EVT_PONG, {});
                aeLog('[AE:bridge] Bridge PING → PONG.');
                break;

            case EVT_CHAR_SYNC_PUSH:
                // Legacy path: bridge pushes character configs (e.g., on reconnect from old bridge version).
                // In the merged system the engine is source of truth, but we accept the push to update
                // the cache in case bridge has newer emotion-portrait data we don't have.
                if (Array.isArray(payload.configs)) {
                    // Merge bridge configs into cachedCharVisuals without overwriting engine fields.
                    // Only update fields that the bridge is authoritative for: none in the merged system.
                    // This case is essentially a no-op for new bridges; kept for backward compatibility.
                    aeLog('[AE:bridge] EVT_CHAR_SYNC_PUSH received (', payload.configs.length, 'entries) — legacy path, no-op in merged system.');
                }
                // Still reply with story ID so bridge can key chat history correctly.
                try {
                    const syncStoryId = await api.v1.story.id();
                    await broadcastToTM(EVT_STORY_ID_SYNC, { storyId: syncStoryId });
                } catch (_) {}
                break;

            // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] EVT_RUN_ACTION_INSTANCE / RUN_QUICK_INSTANCE removed —
            // both always hit "not found" now that actionInstances/actionTemplates are gone.

            // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] AE_SET_SILENT removed — Silent Mode cut with Action Modal.
            case EVT_AUTOSNAP: {
                // [WP-img] Bridge ⚙️ "Auto-Snap" button → story-driven scene image.
                try { await executeAutoSnap(); } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] EVT_AUTOSNAP error:', e); }
                break;
            }
            case EVT_AUTOSNAP_PROMPT: {
                // [v0.9.8.0] Image Studio auto-snap: architect pass over the recent story,
                // result returned to the bridge to fill the composer. payload: { paragraphs }
                try {
                    let asPara = (payload && typeof payload.paragraphs === 'number' && payload.paragraphs > 0) ? Math.floor(payload.paragraphs) : (globalSettings.contextSize || DEFAULT_CTX);
                    if (asPara !== globalSettings.contextSize) { globalSettings.contextSize = asPara; await saveImageSettings(); }
                    let asBuilt = await buildArchitectPromptFromStory(asPara);
                    if (asBuilt) {
                        await broadcastToTM('AUTO_SNAP_PROMPT_RESULT', { prompt: asBuilt.prompt, charNames: asBuilt.matchedChars.map(function(c) { return c.name; }) });
                    } else {
                        await broadcastToTM('AUTO_SNAP_PROMPT_RESULT', { error: 'Could not build a prompt from the recent story.' });
                    }
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] EVT_AUTOSNAP_PROMPT error:', e); await broadcastToTM('AUTO_SNAP_PROMPT_RESULT', { error: 'Auto-snap failed.' }); }
                break;
            }
            case EVT_DIRECTOR_SUBMIT: {
                // [NEW] Bridge submitted a Director's Cut image.
                // payload: { prompt, uc, chars: [{ name, visuals }], enhance?: boolean }
                // If payload.enhance === true: run architect pass and respond with EVT_DIRECTOR_ENHANCE_RESULT.
                // Otherwise: generate and broadcast EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE via submitDirectorPhoto.
                try {
                    if (payload.enhance) {
                        const enhanced = await enhanceDirectorPrompt(payload.prompt || '', payload.chars || []);
                        if (enhanced) {
                            await broadcastToTM('DIRECTOR_ENHANCE_RESULT', {
                                enhancedPrompt: enhanced.enhancedPrompt,
                                charVisuals:    enhanced.charVisuals
                            });
                        } else {
                            await broadcastToTM('DIRECTOR_ENHANCE_RESULT', { error: 'Enhancement failed.' });
                        }
                    } else {
                        await submitDirectorPhoto(payload.prompt || '', payload.uc || '', payload.chars || []);
                    }
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] EVT_DIRECTOR_SUBMIT error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case EVT_REQ_SCENE_STATE:
                // [NEW] Bridge requests an immediate scene state push (e.g., on reconnect or HUD open).
                await broadcastSceneState();
                break;

            case EVT_REQ_CHAR_DATA:
                // [NEW] Bridge requests an immediate character data push (e.g., on reconnect).
                await broadcastCharacterData();
                // [v2.51.8 CHARCHAT-SETTINGS-DETACHED-DOM companion, per user] The bridge also
                // sends this on every Chat launcher open (see _buildCharChatLauncher). Its
                // settings header now renders immediately off whatever _charChatState it has
                // cached (bridge v2.39.0 fix) -- but on a cold session that cache can still be
                // empty/stale, showing Thoughts/Photos as "off" even if enabled in config.
                // Broadcasting charchat state here too means a freshly-opened launcher gets
                // authoritative freshThoughts/images/lastThought within one round-trip, same as
                // the roster does, rather than waiting on some unrelated chat action to trigger
                // AE_CHARCHAT_STATE first. Safe pre-chat: broadcastCharChatState() already
                // handles _aeCharChat.active === false (empty turns/thought, config-only images/
                // freshThoughts).
                await broadcastCharChatState();
                break;

            case 'AE_GEN_SNAPSHOT_TEXT': {
                // [v027] Generate the GLM appearance snapshot for a character and
                // store it under STORAGE_CHAR_SNAP_PFX. Result broadcast to bridge.
                try {
                    let stCharId = payload.charId;
                    let stChar = stCharId ? await loadCharacter(stCharId) : null;
                    if (!stChar) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_GEN_SNAPSHOT_TEXT: char not found'); break; }
                    let stEntId = (stChar as any).entityId || null;
                    let stEnt = stEntId ? await loadEntity(stEntId) : null;
                    if (!stEnt) { api.v1.ui.toast('No scene context for snapshot.', { type: 'warning' }); break; }
                    let stCfg = await loadConfig();
                    let stText = await generateCharacterSnapshot(stChar, stEnt, stCfg);
                    if (stText) {
                        await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_CHAR_SNAP_PFX + stCharId, stText);
                        await broadcastToTM('SNAPSHOT_TEXT_RESULT', { charId: stCharId, text: stText });
                    } else {
                        await broadcastToTM('SNAPSHOT_TEXT_RESULT', { charId: stCharId, error: 'Snapshot generation failed.' });
                    }
                } catch (snapTxtErr) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_GEN_SNAPSHOT_TEXT error:', snapTxtErr); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_GEN_SNAPSHOT_IMAGE': {
                // [v027] Build an image from the stored snapshot text + character
                // visuals (+ optional location visuals) and broadcast EVT_GENERATE_IMAGE.
                try {
                    let siCharId = payload.charId;
                    if (!siCharId) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_GEN_SNAPSHOT_IMAGE: no charId'); break; }
                    await assembleSnapshotImage(siCharId);
                } catch (snapImgErr) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_GEN_SNAPSHOT_IMAGE error:', snapImgErr); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_OPEN_CHAR_EDIT': {
                // [NEW] Bridge requests Smart Modal for character edit.
                // Phase 2: will use openSmartModal(). For now opens NAI sidebar char edit.
                try {
                    const ceCharId = payload.charId;
                    if (ceCharId) {
                        const ceChar = await loadCharacter(ceCharId);
                        if (ceChar) await openCharEditModal(ceCharId);
                    }
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_OPEN_CHAR_EDIT error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_OPEN_ENTITY_EDIT': {
                try {
                    const oeId = payload.entityId;
                    if (oeId) await openEntityEditModal(oeId);
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_OPEN_ENTITY_EDIT error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_OPEN_WIZARD': {
                // [v2.6.0] Bridge header Wizard button (chat-bubble icon) requests the
                // native World-Build Wizard modal, since the bridge has no api.v1.ui access.
                try { await openWizardModal(); }
                catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_OPEN_WIZARD error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] AE_OPEN_CALENDAR removed — Clock/Calendar cut.

            case 'AE_ADJUST_CHAR_RESOURCE': {
                // Bridge steppers in character focus view adjust a per-character resource.
                try {
                    const acCharId = payload.charId;
                    const acTag    = payload.tag;
                    const acDelta  = payload.delta;
                    if (acCharId && acTag && typeof acDelta === 'number') {
                        await adjustCharResource(acCharId, acTag, acDelta);
                        const acFresh = await loadCharacter(acCharId);
                        let acEnt = null;
                        if (acFresh && acFresh.entityId) acEnt = await loadEntity(acFresh.entityId);
                        const acDp = await buildEntityDetailPayload(acCharId, acFresh, acEnt);
                        await broadcastToTM('AE_ENTITY_DETAIL_RESPONSE', acDp);
                        await broadcastSceneState();
                    }
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_ADJUST_CHAR_RESOURCE error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_FETCH_ENTITY_DETAIL': {
                try {
                    const fId = payload.entityId || payload.charId;
                    if (fId) {
                        let char = await loadCharacter(fId);
                        let ent = null;
                        if (char) {
                            if (char.entityId) ent = await loadEntity(char.entityId);
                        } else {
                            ent = await loadEntity(fId);
                            if (ent && ent.type === 'character') {
                                const idx = await loadIndex();
                                const match = idx.find(m => m.entityId === ent.id);
                                if (match) char = await loadCharacter(match.id);
                            }
                        }

                        if (char || ent) {
                            const detailPayload = await buildEntityDetailPayload(fId, char, ent);
                            await broadcastToTM('AE_ENTITY_DETAIL_RESPONSE', detailPayload);
                        } else {
                            await broadcastToTM('AE_ENTITY_DETAIL_RESPONSE', { id: fId, error: 'Not found' });
                        }
                    }
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_FETCH_ENTITY_DETAIL error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_RESOLVE_HOOK': {
                // [v0.9.9.7] Delegated to resolveCharHook() (shared with native char inspect).
                await resolveCharHook(payload.charId, payload.hookId, payload.hookIndex);
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_EQUIP_ITEM': {
                // [v0.9.9.7] Delegated to setItemEquip() (shared with native item rows).
                await setItemEquip(payload.charId, payload.itemId, true);
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_UNEQUIP_ITEM': {
                // [v0.9.9.7] Delegated to setItemEquip() (shared with native item rows).
                await setItemEquip(payload.charId, payload.itemId, false);
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_CHAR_CONDITION_ACTION': {
                // [v2.15.0 BRIDGE-CONDITIONS] Bridge Drama tray add/change conditions.
                // Same activeConditions mutation as buildConditionsRows (the native
                // Character Edit modal's Conditions section, now on the Overview tab,
                // CE-STRIP v2.6.0 PASS 10 / v2.17.0 CHAREDIT-TABS):
                // toggle adds/removes the whole entry (unchecking clears rather than
                // resetting to stage 0); setStage only applies to defs with stages and
                // is clamped to their bounds.
                try {
                    const ccCharId = payload.charId;
                    const ccDefId  = payload.defId;
                    const ccOp     = payload.op;
                    if (ccCharId && ccDefId && ccOp) {
                        const ccChar = await loadCharacter(ccCharId);
                        if (ccChar) {
                            if (!ccChar.activeConditions) ccChar.activeConditions = [];
                            if (ccOp === 'toggle') {
                                const ccExists = ccChar.activeConditions.some((s: any) => s.defId === ccDefId);
                                if (ccExists) {
                                    ccChar.activeConditions = ccChar.activeConditions.filter((s: any) => s.defId !== ccDefId);
                                } else {
                                    ccChar.activeConditions.push({ defId: ccDefId, stageIndex: 0 });
                                }
                            } else if (ccOp === 'setStage' && typeof payload.stageIndex === 'number') {
                                const ccCfg = await loadConfig();
                                const ccDef = (ccCfg.conditions || []).find((d: any) => d.id === ccDefId);
                                const ccState = ccChar.activeConditions.find((s: any) => s.defId === ccDefId);
                                if (ccState && ccDef) {
                                    const ccMaxIdx = Math.max(0, (ccDef.stages || []).length - 1);
                                    ccState.stageIndex = Math.max(0, Math.min(ccMaxIdx, payload.stageIndex));
                                }
                            }
                            await saveCharacter(ccChar);
                            let ccEnt = null;
                            if (ccChar.entityId) ccEnt = await loadEntity(ccChar.entityId);
                            const ccDp = await buildEntityDetailPayload(ccCharId, ccChar, ccEnt);
                            await broadcastToTM('AE_ENTITY_DETAIL_RESPONSE', ccDp);
                        }
                    }
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_CHAR_CONDITION_ACTION error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            // [CE-STRIP vNEXT] AE_PROMOTE_MINOR_NPC handler removed — promoteMinorNpc()
            // no longer exists (minor-NPC tier retired, nothing left to promote to).
            // Bridge still emits this message from its minor-NPC bubble's "Promote to
            // Major" button (Section 6b / _makeEntityBubbleMinor) — now a silent no-op
            // here until the bridge pass removes that button too.

            case 'AE_SET_CHAR_PRESENT': {
                try { if (payload && payload.charId) await aeSetCharScenePresent(payload.charId, !!payload.present); }
                catch (eEx) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_SET_CHAR_PRESENT error:', eEx); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_MOVE_CHAR_TO_SCENE': {
                try { if (payload && payload.charId) await aeMoveCharToScene(payload.charId); }
                catch (eMv) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_MOVE_CHAR_TO_SCENE error:', eMv); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_REFRESH_PRESENCE': {
                try { await aeRefreshPresence(); }
                catch (eRf) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_REFRESH_PRESENCE error:', eRf); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_HUD_GEN_ENTITY': {
                // [v1.15.0] Bridge "Add NPC/Item" popup: generate a scene-fitting draft
                // (name + brief/desc) WITHOUT spawning; echo it back to prefill the popup.
                // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] archetype/template lookup removed — Bestiary/Item
                // Templates cut. Always generates freeform now.
                try {
                    const geKind = (payload && payload.kind === 'item') ? 'item' : 'npc';
                    if (geKind === 'item') {
                        const geRes = await generateSceneItem();
                        await broadcastToTM('AE_HUD_GEN_RESULT', { kind: 'item', ok: !!geRes, name: geRes ? geRes.name : '', brief: geRes ? geRes.desc : '' });
                    } else {
                        const geRes = await generateSceneNpc();
                        await broadcastToTM('AE_HUD_GEN_RESULT', { kind: 'npc', ok: !!geRes, name: geRes ? geRes.name : '', brief: geRes ? geRes.brief : '' });
                    }
                } catch (eGe) {
                    aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_HUD_GEN_ENTITY error:', eGe);
                    try { await broadcastToTM('AE_HUD_GEN_RESULT', { kind: (payload && payload.kind) || 'npc', ok: false, name: '', brief: '' }); } catch (eGe2) {}
                }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_HUD_SPAWN_ENTITY': {
                // [v1.15.0] Bridge "Add NPC/Item" Create: spawn from typed fields at the
                // inspected location. spawn* queues a fresh scene broadcast, so every open
                // HUD refreshes on its own.
                // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] archetype/template lookup removed — Bestiary/Item
                // Templates cut. Always spawns freeform now.
                try {
                    const seKind = (payload && payload.kind === 'item') ? 'item' : 'npc';
                    const seName = (payload && payload.name) ? String(payload.name).trim() : '';
                    const seBrief = (payload && payload.brief) ? String(payload.brief).trim() : '';
                    const seLoc = (payload && payload.locId) ? String(payload.locId) : null;
                    if (seKind === 'item') {
                        if (seName) await spawnItem(seName, seBrief, false, seLoc || '__room__');
                    } else {
                        if (seName) await spawnSceneNpc(seName, seBrief, seLoc, false);
                    }
                } catch (eSe) {
                    aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_HUD_SPAWN_ENTITY error:', eSe);
                }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_HUD_GEN_LOCATION': {
                // [v1.18.0 LOCGEN] Bridge Map-tab "New Location" popup: generate a
                // scene-fitting draft (name + desc + lore) WITHOUT creating anything;
                // echo it back to prefill the popup.
                //
                // >>> PAIRED with AE_HUD_GEN_ENTITY directly above: same round-trip shape,
                // >>> genuinely different payload (relation + suggestion in, 3 fields out,
                // >>> no archetype). Touch one, look at the other.
                try {
                    let glRelIn = (payload && payload.relation === 'child') ? 'child' : 'sibling';
                    let glRefIn = (payload && payload.refLocId) ? String(payload.refLocId) : null;
                    let glSugIn = (payload && payload.suggestion) ? String(payload.suggestion) : '';
                    let glResOut = await generateSceneLocation(glRelIn, glRefIn, glSugIn);
                    await broadcastToTM('AE_HUD_GEN_LOCATION_RESULT', {
                        ok: !!glResOut,
                        relation: glRelIn,
                        name: glResOut ? glResOut.name : '',
                        description: glResOut ? glResOut.description : '',
                        lore: glResOut ? glResOut.lore : ''
                    });
                } catch (eGl) {
                    aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_HUD_GEN_LOCATION error:', eGl);
                    try { await broadcastToTM('AE_HUD_GEN_LOCATION_RESULT', { ok: false, relation: (payload && payload.relation === 'child') ? 'child' : 'sibling', name: '', description: '', lore: '' }); } catch (eGl2) {}
                }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_HUD_SPAWN_LOCATION': {
                // [v1.18.0 LOCGEN] Bridge Map-tab "New Location" Create. spawnLocation
                // queues a fresh scene broadcast, so every open HUD map repaints itself
                // and the new sibling/child node appears without an explicit refresh.
                // >>> PAIRED with AE_HUD_SPAWN_ENTITY directly above.
                try {
                    let slNameIn = (payload && payload.name) ? String(payload.name).trim() : '';
                    let slDescIn = (payload && payload.description) ? String(payload.description).trim() : '';
                    let slLoreIn = (payload && payload.lore) ? String(payload.lore).trim() : '';
                    let slRelIn = (payload && payload.relation === 'child') ? 'child' : 'sibling';
                    let slRefIn = (payload && payload.refLocId) ? String(payload.refLocId) : null;
                    if (slNameIn) await spawnLocation(slNameIn, slDescIn, slLoreIn, slRelIn, slRefIn, false);
                } catch (eSl) {
                    aeLog('[AE:bridge] AE_HUD_SPAWN_LOCATION error:', eSl);
                }
                break;
            }

            // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] AE_TRACK_NARRATIVE removed — quest-tracker cut.

            case 'AE_SET_DEFAULT_BG_URL': {
                // [v0.9.40] Bridge Theme Forge sets module-level scene default background.
                // Saves to GlobalConfig so it persists with the story and flows back
                // through the next buildSceneStatePayload broadcast to all open bridges.
                try {
                    let bgCfg = await loadConfig();
                    (bgCfg as any).defaultBackgroundUrl = (payload.url || '').trim();
                    await saveConfig(bgCfg);
                    await broadcastSceneState();
                    api.v1.ui.toast(
                        (bgCfg as any).defaultBackgroundUrl
                            ? '✓ Scene background URL saved.'
                            : '✓ Scene background cleared.',
                        { type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 }
                    );
                } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] AE_SET_DEFAULT_BG_URL error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case EVT_IMPORT_WORLD: {
                // [v0.9.39] Bridge-delivered adventure module (Try-the-Demo button).
                // Same importPayload() funnel as file import, then seed/refresh exactly
                // like the new-story prompt callbacks.
                try {
                    let iwModule = payload.module || payload.data || null;
                    if (!iwModule) { api.v1.ui.toast('Demo payload was empty.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 2500 }); break; }
                    let iwResult = await importPayload(iwModule);
                    let iwParts: string[] = [];
                    if (iwResult.charCount > 0)   iwParts.push(iwResult.charCount + ' character' + (iwResult.charCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''));
                    if (iwResult.entityCount > 0) iwParts.push(iwResult.entityCount + ' node' + (iwResult.entityCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''));
                    await ensureProtagonistExists();
                    await refreshSidebar();
                    queueSceneStateBroadcast();
                    api.v1.ui.toast('Demo loaded: ' + (iwParts.length ? iwParts.join(', ') : 'world') + '.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
                } catch (e) {
                    api.v1.error('[AE:bridge] AE_IMPORT_WORLD error:', e);
                    api.v1.ui.toast('Demo import failed.', { type: 'error', autoClose: 2500 });
                }
                break;
            }

            case EVT_DEMO_FAILED: {
                // Bridge could not fetch the module (offline / URL down). Seed a fresh
                // story so the player is never stranded on an empty fresh story.
                try {
                    await ensureProtagonistExists();
                    await refreshSidebar();
                    queueSceneStateBroadcast();
                } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE:bridge] AE_DEMO_FAILED fallback error:', e); }
                api.v1.ui.toast('Demo unavailable — started a fresh story instead.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 3500 });
                break;
            }

            // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] AE_TOGGLE_FOLLOW / AE_TRAVEL_REQUEST / AE_VEHICLE_TRAVEL_REQUEST
            // removed — Travel cut. AE_SILENT_TELEPORT (below) is the remaining way to
            // reposition the player — direct, no path-finding, no cost.

            case 'REQUEST_NODE_GRAPH': {
                // Bridge map Inspect button — returns the adjacency graph of any entity.
                // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] awareness/adjacency only — no travel-cost annotations.
                try {
                    const ngId  = payload.entityId;
                    if (!ngId) break;
                    const ngEnt = await loadEntity(ngId);
                    if (!ngEnt) break;
                    const ngAll = await loadEntityIndex();

                    const ngChildren: any[] = [];
                    for (const ngCM of ngAll.filter(function(m) { return m.parentId === ngId && m.type === 'location'; })) {
                        const ngHasGC = ngAll.some(function(m) { return m.parentId === ngCM.id && m.type === 'location'; });
                        ngChildren.push({
                            id: ngCM.id, name: ngCM.name, type: ngCM.type, icon: ngCM.icon || undefined,
                            hasChildren: ngHasGC
                        });
                    }

                    const ngConns: any[] = [];
                    for (const ngC of (ngEnt.connectedEntityIds || [])) {
                        const ngCM2 = ngAll.find(function(m) { return m.id === ngC.targetId; });
                        ngConns.push({
                            id: ngC.targetId,
                            name:   ngCM2 ? ngCM2.name : ngC.targetId,
                            label:  (ngC as any).label   || '',
                            oneWay: (ngC as any).oneWay  || false,
                            icon:   ngCM2 ? ngCM2.icon || undefined : undefined
                        });
                    }

                    // [v0.9.89] Lateral siblings for Orbital HUD
                    const ngSiblings: any[] = [];
                    for (const sibMeta of ngAll) {
                        if (sibMeta.type !== 'location' || sibMeta.id === ngId) continue;
                        if (sibMeta.parentId !== ngEnt.parentId) continue;
                        ngSiblings.push({
                            id: sibMeta.id, name: sibMeta.name, type: sibMeta.type,
                            icon: sibMeta.icon || undefined
                        });
                    }

                    // [FIX Bug3] Added currentIcon + parentIcon to node graph response.
                    const ngGraph: any = {
                        currentName:      ngEnt.name,
                        currentType:      ngEnt.type,
                        currentIcon:      (ngEnt as any).icon      || undefined,
                        parentId: ngEnt.parentId || null, parentName: '', parentType: '', parentIcon: undefined,
                        children: ngChildren, connections: ngConns, siblings: ngSiblings
                    };
                    if (ngEnt.parentId) {
                        const ngPm = ngAll.find(function(m) { return m.id === ngEnt.parentId; });
                        if (ngPm) { ngGraph.parentName = ngPm.name; ngGraph.parentType = ngPm.type; ngGraph.parentIcon = ngPm.icon || undefined; }
                    }
                    await broadcastToTM('AE_NODE_GRAPH_RESPONSE', { entityId: ngId, graph: ngGraph });
                } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE:bridge] REQUEST_NODE_GRAPH error:', e); }
                break;
            }

            case 'AE_SILENT_TELEPORT': {
                // Bridge 📍 Teleport button — same action as the native Map's Teleport
                // button. [CE-STRIP vNEXT] Delegates to commitTravel(id, true) instead of
                // duplicating camera/body-move/presence logic inline — that duplication
                // was also protagonist-gated (silently did nothing without one); one fix
                // in commitTravel now covers both entry points.
                try {
