        { type: 'text', text: 'Select a world pack to import. This will overwrite the current world data.', style: { marginBottom: '14px', fontSize: '12px', lineHeight: '1.5' } }
    ];
    let pklModal: any;

    for (let pklI = 0; pklI < pklPacks.length; pklI++) {
        (function(pklEnt: any, pklDat: any) {
            let pklCC  = Object.keys(pklDat.charRecords || {}).length;
            let pklEC  = Object.keys(pklDat.entRecords  || {}).length;
            let pklLbl = (pklEnt.displayName || '[AE World]') + ' — ' + pklCC + ' chars, ' + pklEC + ' locations';
            pklContent.push({
                type: 'button',
                text: '📥 Import: ' + pklLbl,
                style: { width: '100%', marginBottom: '8px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.15)', border: '1px solid rgba(136,204,255,0.4)', color: '#88ccff', fontWeight: 'bold' },
                disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true,
                callback: async function() {
                    try {
                        await importFromLorebookPack(pklDat);
                        if (pklModal) pklModal.close();
                        api.v1.ui.toast('World imported. Reload the sidebar to activate.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 4000 });
                        await refreshSidebar();
                        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
                    } catch (ePklImp) {
                        api.v1.error('[AE] pickAndImportFromLorebook import failed:', ePklImp);
                        api.v1.ui.toast('Import failed — see engine log.', { type: 'error' });
                    }
                }
            });
        })(pklPacks[pklI].entry, pklPacks[pklI].data);
    }

    pklContent.push({ type: 'button', text: 'Cancel', style: { width: '100%', marginTop: '8px' }, callback: function() { if (pklModal) pklModal.close(); } });
    pklModal = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-pick-import', title: '📥 Import from Lorebook', size: 'small', content: pklContent });
}

// ============================================================================
// ============================================================================
// SECTION 19 — SCENE PRESENCE, LOCATION & EQUIP HELPERS
// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] Was ACTION EXECUTION ENGINE. The AERule/ActionInstance effects
// engine (aeApplyEffects/aeCheckConditions/runActionInstance/runQuickInstance/
// aeRollResolution/aePickTarget/aeBindStakeDef/evaluateFieldCondition/etc — ~40
// functions, the AERule/ActionInstance/AEScope/AEResolution/ActionBranch/
// AEOutcomeBand/AEResOutcome interfaces, and the Travel executors executeTravelTo/
// findTravelPath/findVehicleAncestor/executeVehicleTravel/executeDestinationTravel)
// is fully removed — every remaining external call site was traced and fixed or
// confirmed unreachable (grep "CE-STRIP v2.0.0" across the file for each one).
// What's left is genuinely still needed: scene presence, location resolution,
// item equip, the equip/faction-resource read-write helpers still used from Holding,
// Schedule's NPC positioning, and commitTravel (still used for World-Build Wizard
// intro placement and the native Map's Teleport button — NOT reachable from any
// player-facing travel/action UI any more).
// ============================================================================

function resolveEntityTokens(text: string, ent: EntityRecord, targetEnt?: EntityRecord | null): string {
    // Replaces __self__ and __target__ tokens in action outcome notes with entity names.
    // Preserved verbatim from AE source.
    if (!text || !ent) return text || '';
    let result = text.replace(/__self__/g, ent.name);
    if (targetEnt) result = result.replace(/__target__/g, targetEnt.name);
    return result;
}

function aeSubstituteTokens(text: string, scope: AEScope): string {
    if (!text) return '';
    let out = text;
    const actorName = scope.protag ? scope.protag.name : (scope.sourceEnt ? scope.sourceEnt.name : 'You');
    const targetName = scope.target ? scope.target.name : (scope.sourceEnt ? scope.sourceEnt.name : '');
    out = out.replace(/\{Actor\}/g, actorName);
    out = out.replace(/\{Target\}/g, targetName);
    // also honour the legacy __self__ / __target__ tokens via the existing resolver
    out = resolveEntityTokens(out, scope.sourceEnt, scope.target);
    return out;
}

function composeActionPrompt(base: string, flavor: string): string {
    let b = base.trim();
    let f = flavor.trim();
    if (!f) return b;
    if (!b) { return f.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + f.slice(1); }
    let flavorComplete = /[.!?]$/.test(f);
    if (flavorComplete) { return b + ' ' + f; }
    let stripped = b.replace(/[.!?]\s*$/, '');
    return stripped + ', ' + f + '.';
}

async function aeSetEquip(itemEnt: EntityRecord | null, charRec: CharacterRecord | null, equip: boolean): Promise<void> {
    if (!itemEnt) return;
    let freshItem = await loadEntity(itemEnt.id);
    if (!freshItem) return;
    freshItem.isEquipped = equip;
    freshItem.equippedToCharId = (equip && charRec) ? charRec.id : undefined;
    await saveEntity(freshItem);
    if (charRec) {
        let freshChar = await loadCharacter(charRec.id);
        if (freshChar) {
            if (!freshChar.equippedItemIds) freshChar.equippedItemIds = [];
            if (equip) { if (freshChar.equippedItemIds.indexOf(freshItem.id) < 0) freshChar.equippedItemIds.push(freshItem.id); }
            else { freshChar.equippedItemIds = freshChar.equippedItemIds.filter(function(id) { return id !== freshItem.id; }); }
            await saveCharacter(freshChar);
            // [v0.9.8.5] No lorebook re-sync — equipment now rides an ephemeral temp entry.
        }
    }
}

function aeResolveResourceCompareValue(raw: any, states: string[] | undefined): number {
    let s = String(raw === undefined || raw === null ? '' : raw).trim();
    if (s === '') return NaN;
    let asNum = Number(s);
    if (!isNaN(asNum)) return asNum;
    if (states && states.length) {
        let idx = states.findIndex(function(st) { return String(st).toLowerCase() === s.toLowerCase(); });
        if (idx >= 0) return idx;
    }
    return NaN;
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] aeFactionResDefaultValue / aeSetFactionResource /
// aeSetFactionResourceClamped removed — their only callers were the dead-by-
// starvation Faction Resources display in buildFactionsWorkspace (factionResourceDefs
// no longer exists on GlobalConfig; the block could never render anything real).
// loadFactionResourceValues/saveFactionResourceValues/effectiveFactionResourceSections
// were already missing before this patch (likely lost in the same earlier deletion
// that took aeRenderIndexedStatesEditor's definition) — this finishes that removal
// properly instead of restoring a subsystem whose data model is already gone.

// [FEATURE v2.30.0 ROOT-LOCATION, per user] Sentinel id for "the top of the entity
// graph" — every character's body starts here (parentId: null, see
// createWizardCharacter/spawnSceneNpc) and stays here until something moves it to a
// real location. Ungrouped and grouped-by-nothing used to mean the same thing as
// "unresolvable" (null), which is why a story with characters but NO location
// entities ever computed scene presence at all (loadPlayerLocation had nothing to
// fall back to but null, and recalculateScenePresence bails out entirely on a null
// camera room — see both below). Treating root as a real, comparable id instead of a
// dead end fixes that: a world with no locations now has exactly one implicit place,
// and every unplaced character is correctly co-located there together. Deliberately
// NOT a real entity — nothing should ever loadEntity() this id, which is also why it
// is namespaced away from api.v1.uuid()'s format rather than risking a collision.
var AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID = '__ce_root__';

async function loadPlayerLocation(): Promise<string | null> {
    // [CE-STRIP vNEXT] The "camera" — what the HUD/context treats as the current
    // scene — is now independent of any character. STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC (declared long
    // ago but never actually written until now) is the single source of truth, set by
    // commitTravel/AE_SILENT_TELEPORT (the Map's Teleport action = "select a location").
    // Falls back to a protagonist's physical room if one exists and no camera has been
    // set yet (back-compat for worlds still using a protagonist), then to the first
    // location in the world so a fresh/no-protagonist world isn't permanently stuck on
    // "No scene state available." Each fallback persists into STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC so it
    // stays stable once resolved, rather than re-deriving every call.
    // [v2.30.0 ROOT-LOCATION] FINAL fallback below no longer returns null when there is
    // no location entity anywhere in the world — it returns AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID, also
    // persisted, so this function can no longer return null at all. commitTravel still
    // overwrites STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC unconditionally the moment a real location exists
    // and gets selected, so this is a one-time bootstrap, not a permanent ceiling.
    const camLoc = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, null) as string | null;
    if (camLoc === AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID) return AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID;
    if (camLoc) {
        const camEnt = await loadEntity(camLoc);
        if (camEnt) return camLoc;
        // Stale pointer (location was deleted) — fall through and re-resolve.
    }

    const idx = await loadIndex();
    const pMeta = idx.find(function(m) { return !!m.isProtagonist; });
    if (pMeta) {
        const protag = await loadCharacter(pMeta.id);
        if (protag && protag.entityId) {
            const protagBody = await loadEntity(protag.entityId);
            if (protagBody && protagBody.parentId) {
                await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, protagBody.parentId);
                return protagBody.parentId;
            }
        }
    }

    const allNodes = await loadEntityIndex();
    const firstLoc = allNodes.find(function(n) { return n.type === 'location'; });
    if (firstLoc) {
        await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, firstLoc.id);
        return firstLoc.id;
    }
    await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID);
    return AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID;
}

function getEffectiveRoomId(entityId: string, allNodes: EntityMeta[]): string | null {
    // Walks up the entity tree until a 'location' node is found.
    // Preserved verbatim from AE source, EXCEPT the final line — [v2.30.0 ROOT-LOCATION]
    // walking all the way to the top (parentId chain ends in null) with no 'location'
    // ancestor used to return null here, indistinguishable from "entityId doesn't exist
    // at all." Now returns AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID instead, a real comparable id meaning
    // "this body sits at the top of the graph, ungrouped" — see that constant's own
    // comment for why this matters (recalculateScenePresence's camRoom comparison).
    // A genuinely missing node (!node) still returns null — that is a real error case
    // (a deleted/dangling entityId), not "unplaced," and stays distinguishable from it.
    if (!entityId) return null;
    let currentId: string | null = entityId;
    let sanity = 0;
    while (currentId && sanity < 10) {
        const node = allNodes.find(function(n) { return n.id === currentId; });
        if (!node) return null;
        if (node.type === 'location') return node.id;
        currentId = node.parentId || null;
        sanity++;
    }
    return currentId === null ? AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID : currentId;
}

async function aeGetPlayerRoomId(): Promise<string | null> {
    // [CE-STRIP vNEXT] Delegates to loadPlayerLocation() — the camera already resolves
    // to a location-type node (walking up from a protagonist's body if needed), so a
    // separate walk here is redundant now that "current room" means "camera location"
    // rather than "wherever the protagonist's body specifically sits."
    try {
        return await loadPlayerLocation();
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE] aeGetPlayerRoomId:', e); return null; }
}

async function aeSetCharScenePresent(charId: string, present: boolean): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const c = await loadCharacter(charId);
        if (!c) return;
        c.scenePresent = !!present;
        await saveCharacter(c);
        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE] aeSetCharScenePresent:', e); }
}

async function aeMoveCharToScene(charId: string): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const c = await loadCharacter(charId);
        if (!c || !c.entityId) return;
        const room = await aeGetPlayerRoomId();   // co-locate in the player's actual room
        const body = await loadEntity(c.entityId);
        if (body) { body.parentId = room; await saveEntity(body); }
        await saveCharacter(c);
        await recalculateScenePresence();
        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE] aeMoveCharToScene:', e); }
}

async function aeRefreshPresence(): Promise<void> {
    try {
        await recalculateScenePresence();
        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
    } catch (e) { aeLog('[AE] aeRefreshPresence:', e); }
}

async function recalculateScenePresence(): Promise<void> {
    // [CE-STRIP vNEXT] Updates scenePresent for all active characters based on
    // co-location with the CAMERA location (loadPlayerLocation()) instead of the
    // protagonist's body. Previously this returned immediately if no protagonist
    // existed, so presence was never computed at all without one — root cause of
    // "No scene state available." A protagonist, if one exists, is now just another
    // character for presence purposes: present if co-located, absent otherwise, no
    // longer the anchor everyone else is measured against.
    try {
        // [v2.30.0 ROOT-LOCATION] loadPlayerLocation can no longer return null (its
        // final fallback is now AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID, not null — see both of their own
        // comments), so this guard is believed unreachable now. Left in place as
        // defense-in-depth rather than removed — a null here would mean something
        // deeper broke, and silently proceeding with a null camRoom would make every
        // cRoom === camRoom comparison below false in a way that would look identical
        // to "nobody's present," not "something's wrong."
        const camRoom = await loadPlayerLocation();
        if (!camRoom) return;

        const allChars = await loadAllCharacters();
        const freshEntIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
        for (const c of allChars) {
            if (!c.active) continue;
            if (!c.entityId) {
                // No body in the world graph — cannot be co-located with anyone.
                if (c.scenePresent) { c.scenePresent = false; await saveCharacter(c); }
                continue;
            }
            const cRoom = getEffectiveRoomId(c.entityId, freshEntIdx);
            const shouldBePresent = (cRoom !== null) && (cRoom === camRoom);
            if (c.scenePresent !== shouldBePresent) {
                c.scenePresent = shouldBePresent;
                await saveCharacter(c);
            }
        }
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE] recalculateScenePresence error:', e);
    }
}


async function commitTravel(targetId: string, isSilent: boolean): Promise<void> {
    try {
        const ctTarget = await loadEntity(targetId);
        if (!ctTarget) return;
        aeLog('[AE:TRACE] commitTravel ENTER target=' + ctTarget.name + '(' + targetId + ') isSilent=' + isSilent + ' ae_lock=' + ae_lock);
        const ctIsSpatial = ctTarget.type === 'location';
        await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_LAST_TRAVEL, Date.now());

        // [CE-STRIP vNEXT] Camera: set unconditionally, no protagonist required — this
        // IS the "select a location from the Map" action. Previously the whole function
        // bailed out here if no protagonist existed.
        if (ctIsSpatial) { await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, targetId); }

        const ctChars  = await loadAllCharacters();
        const ctProtag = ctChars.find(function(c) { return c.isProtagonist; });
        if (ctProtag && ctProtag.entityId) {
            // Move body
            if (ctIsSpatial) {
                const ctBody = await loadEntity(ctProtag.entityId);
                if (ctBody && ctBody.id !== targetId) { aeLog('[AE:TRACE] commitTravel body move ' + ctBody.parentId + ' -> ' + targetId); ctBody.parentId = targetId; await saveEntity(ctBody); }
                else { aeLog('[AE:TRACE] commitTravel body NOT moved (ctBody=' + (ctBody ? ctBody.id : 'null') + ' target=' + targetId + ')'); }
            }

            // Legacy per-character camera field — kept in sync for any code still
            // reading it, but STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC above is the actual source of truth now.
            const ctFreshProtag = await loadCharacter(ctProtag.id);
            if (ctFreshProtag) { ctFreshProtag.currentLocationId = targetId; await saveCharacter(ctFreshProtag); }
        }

        // Pre-generation auto-triggers
        if (ctIsSpatial) { await runAutoTriggers(); }

        // [v0.9.9.2] Position offstage NPCs per their hourly schedule for the current
        // hour before computing who is scene-present at the destination.
        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] resolveSchedules() call removed — Schedule cut.

        // Scene presence — camera-anchored (recalculateScenePresence), no longer a
        // protagonist-room loop duplicated inline here.
        if (ctIsSpatial) { await recalculateScenePresence(); }

        // History
        const ctHist = await loadEntityHistory();
        ctHist.unshift(targetId);
        await saveEntityHistory(ctHist);

        // Quest arrival check
        if (ctIsSpatial) {
            try {
                const ctNarrId = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_TRACKED_NARRATIVE, '') as string;
                if (ctNarrId) {
                    const ctNarr = await loadEntity(ctNarrId);
                    if (ctNarr && ctNarr.questTarget) {
                        const ctQIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
                        const ctQTgt = ctQIdx.find(function(m) { return m.id === ctNarr.questTarget; });
                        if (ctQTgt) {
                            const ctNavId = ctQTgt.type === 'location' ? ctQTgt.id : (ctQTgt.parentId || '');
                            if (ctNavId && targetId === ctNavId) {
                                await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_TRACKED_NARRATIVE, '');
                                api.v1.ui.toast('🎯 Reached: ' + ctQTgt.name + ' — quest tracking cleared.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 3000 });
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            } catch (_) {}
        }

        // [v0.9.25 E4] Encounter roll removed from commitTravel

        await refreshSidebar();
        queueSceneStateBroadcast();

        if (!isSilent) {
            // [v0.9.37 DEFER1] Do NOT generate inline. move_player_to_self can sit in the
            // MIDDLE of an effects list (e.g. before inject_narrative); generating here
            // blocks the editor so any later inject_narrative/inject_message throws
            // 'editor is currently blocked' and the narrative never lands. Set a pending
            // flag; the top-level handler generates ONCE after every effect completes.
            aeLog('[AE:TRACE] commitTravel DEFER generate (target=' + targetId + ')');
            ae_pendingGenerate = true;
        } else {
            aeLog('[AE:TRACE] commitTravel SILENT SKIP generate (target=' + targetId + ')');
        }
    } catch (e) {
        aeLog('[AE] commitTravel error:', e);
    }
}

// SECTION 20 — SIDEBAR
// refreshSidebar, buildSidebar, buildCharCard, killCharacter,
// openCharEditModal and all character sub-section builders.
//
// [CHANGE] renderSceneHUD() -> queueSceneStateBroadcast() throughout.
// [CHANGE] typeof renderSceneHUD guards removed — call directly.
// [CHANGE] refreshSidebar no longer calls renderSceneHUD:
//          queueSceneStateBroadcast() is called instead.
// ============================================================================

async function refreshSidebar(): Promise<void> {
    // [CHANGE] renderSceneHUD() removed — bridge owns the HUD now.
    //          queueSceneStateBroadcast() fires so bridge HUD stays in sync.
    await api.v1.ui.update([{ type: 'sidebarPanel', id: 'ce-panel', name: 'Adventure', iconId: 'compass', content: await buildSidebar() } as any]);
    queueSceneStateBroadcast();
    try { await refreshWorkspace(); } catch (e) {}
}

async function buildSidebar(): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    // [v1.10.0] Landing hub. The old four-tab bar is replaced by a launcher of
    // full-screen workspaces (see openWorkspace / WORKSPACE_DEFS). Roster, Drama,
    // Locations & Entities and Action Studio render inside the shared full-screen
    // workspace modal; Wizard, World & Engine Settings and Bestiary keep their
    // existing modals for now (Phase B folds them into the shell).
    const parts: UIPart[] = [];
    let cfg: GlobalConfig;
    try { cfg = await loadConfig(); } catch (e) { cfg = {} as GlobalConfig; }

    // 1. PROJECT SETUP \u2014 hoisted from the Wizard Setup tab.
    parts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Name your project and jot the initial concept. These seed world generation and every Wizard chat.', style: { fontSize: '12px', opacity: '0.7', lineHeight: '1.5', marginBottom: '8px' } });
    parts.push({ type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '8px', marginBottom: '8px' }, content: [
        { type: 'textInput', label: 'Project Name', initialValue: (cfg as any).projectName || '', placeholder: 'Untitled Project', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0 }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c as any).projectName = v; }); } },
        { type: 'textInput', label: 'Genre', initialValue: (cfg as any).projectGenre || '', placeholder: 'e.g. dark fantasy, cyberpunk noir\u2026', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0 }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c as any).projectGenre = v; }); } }
    ] });
    parts.push({ type: 'multilineTextInput', label: 'Initial Concept', initialValue: (cfg as any).projectConcept || '', placeholder: 'The working idea your world starts from\u2026', style: { minHeight: '70px', marginBottom: '12px' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c as any).projectConcept = v; }); } } as UIPart);

    // 2. LAUNCHER \u2014 two-column grid of the seven workspaces.
    parts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'OPEN A WORKSPACE', style: { fontSize: '10px', fontWeight: 'bold', opacity: '0.5', letterSpacing: '0.5px', marginBottom: '6px' } });
    parts.push({ type: 'row', wrap: true, style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '12px' }, content: WORKSPACE_DEFS.map(function(d) {
        return { type: 'button', text: d.label, iconId: d.iconId as any, style: { flex: '1 1 46%', margin: 0, fontSize: '12px', textAlign: 'left', padding: '8px', border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)' }, callback: async function() { await launchWorkspace(d.id); } } as UIPart;
    }) });

    // 2b. WORLD MAINTENANCE — whole-world actions that belong to the project, not to any
    // one workspace. [v1.22.3] "Sync All Lorebooks" moved here from the Wizard's Memory
    // tab, where it sat below an unrelated commit button and was routinely missed.
    // [v1.24.0 CLEANUP] Adventure Module Management (lorebook pack + JSON import/export)
    // moved here from World Settings — same "whole-world action" rationale as Sync All.
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: 'World maintenance', iconId: 'book-open', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ae_landing_maint', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: 'Create any missing lorebook entries and refresh existing ones for every character, location, species, and faction. Safe to run at any time; it also repairs entries deleted in NovelAI.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.7', lineHeight: '1.5', marginBottom: '6px' } },
        { type: 'button', text: '\ud83d\udcda Sync All Lorebooks', iconId: 'book-open' as any, style: { width: '100%', fontSize: '11px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.10)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
            api.v1.ui.toast('Syncing all lorebooks\u2026', { id: 'ae-lore-all', autoClose: false, type: 'info' });
            try {
                let t = await syncAllLorebooks();
                let totCreated = t.characters.created + t.locations.created + t.species.created + t.factions.created;
                let totSynced  = t.characters.synced  + t.locations.synced  + t.species.synced  + t.factions.synced;
                api.v1.ui.toast(totCreated + ' created, ' + totSynced + ' synced (chars ' + t.characters.created + '+' + t.characters.synced + ', locs ' + t.locations.created + '+' + t.locations.synced + ', species ' + t.species.created + '+' + t.species.synced + ', factions ' + t.factions.created + '+' + t.factions.synced + ').', { id: 'ae-lore-all', type: 'success', autoClose: 4000 });
            } catch (eSyncAll) {
                api.v1.error('[AE:lore] Sync All button:', eSyncAll);
                api.v1.ui.toast('Sync All failed.', { id: 'ae-lore-all', type: 'error', autoClose: 2500 });
            }
        } },
        { type: 'box', style: { borderTop: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1)', marginTop: '12px', paddingTop: '12px' }, content: [
            { type: 'text', text: '📦 Adventure Module Management', style: { fontSize: '12px', fontWeight: 'bold', marginBottom: '6px', color: '#7ec8a0' } },
            { type: 'text', text: 'Pack the full world into a hidden lorebook entry that travels with your .scenario export. Use the picker to import any AE pack found in the lorebook.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.7', marginBottom: '8px' } },
            { type: 'row', style: { gap: '8px', marginBottom: '10px' }, content: [
                { type: 'button', text: '📦 Pack to Lorebook', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, backgroundColor: 'rgba(126,200,160,0.12)', border: '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.35)', color: '#7ec8a0' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                    await packConfigToLorebook();
                } },
                { type: 'button', text: '📥 Import from Lorebook', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.12)', border: '1px solid rgba(136,204,255,0.35)', color: '#88ccff' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                    await pickAndImportFromLorebook();
                } }
            ] },
            { type: 'text', text: 'Export to a plain JSON file for backups or external tools. Import a previously exported JSON file.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.7', marginBottom: '8px' } },
            { type: 'row', style: { gap: '8px' }, content: [
                { type: 'button', text: '⬇ Export JSON', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, backgroundColor: 'rgba(232,168,124,0.12)', border: '1px solid rgba(232,168,124,0.35)', color: '#e8a87c' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                    await exportAll();
                } },
                { type: 'button', text: '⬆ Import JSON', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, backgroundColor: 'rgba(232,168,124,0.12)', border: '1px solid rgba(232,168,124,0.35)', color: '#e8a87c' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                    await importData();
                } }
            ] }
        ] } as UIPart
    ] } as UIPart);

    // 3. DISPLAY MODE \u2014 HUD routing (was top chrome; demoted to a collapsed strip).
    var _hudMode = (cfg as any).interfaceMode || 'native';
    function hudModeBtn(mode: string, label: string, icon: any): UIPart {
        var on = _hudMode === mode;
        return { type: 'button', text: label, iconId: icon, style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', fontWeight: on ? 'bold' : 'normal', backgroundColor: on ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.18)' : 'transparent', color: on ? '#7ec8a0' : undefined, border: on ? '1px solid #7ec8a0' : '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)', opacity: on ? '1' : '0.7' }, callback: async function() { await setInterfaceMode(mode); } } as UIPart;
    }
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: 'Display mode', iconId: 'monitor', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ae_display_mode', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: 'Where the game interface renders. Native uses NovelAI\u2019s own UI; Bridge uses the HUD userscript; Both runs them together.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.7', marginBottom: '6px' } },
        { type: 'row', style: { gap: '6px' }, content: [ hudModeBtn('native', 'Native', 'cloud-off'), hudModeBtn('bridge', 'Bridge', 'cloud'), hudModeBtn('both', 'Both', 'layers') ] }
    ] } as UIPart);

    // 4. HOW THIS PANEL WORKS \u2014 default collapsed.
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: 'How this panel works', iconId: 'help-circle', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ae_landing_intro', content: [
        { type: 'text', markdown: true, text: 'This landing is your project home. Fill in the setup fields above, then open a **workspace** for focused work. Every workspace has a **Navigate** header to jump anywhere in two taps, plus its own **Help** button explaining its controls.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.85', lineHeight: '1.5' } }
    ] } as UIPart);

    return parts.filter(Boolean) as UIPart[];
}

async function buildRosterWorkspace(): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    const parts: UIPart[] = [];
    try {
const index = await loadIndex(); 
            const cfg = await loadConfig();
            const entIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
            const searchQuery = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault('temp:ae_char_search', '') as string;
            
            // 1. TOP ROW: ADD & IMPORT & HELP
            parts.push({ type: 'row', style: { gap: '8px', marginBottom: '8px' }, content: [
                { type: 'button', text: 'Character', iconId: 'plus', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0 }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
                    try {
                        let addCfg = await loadConfig();
                        let addEnt = makeDefaultEntity('New Character');
                        addEnt.type = 'character';
                        addEnt.parentId = null;
                        await saveEntity(addEnt);
                        let addChar = makeDefaultCharacter('New Character', addCfg);
                        addChar.entityId = addEnt.id;
                        await saveCharacter(addChar);
                        try { await createLinkedLorebookEntry(addChar.id); } catch (lbErr) {}
                        if (typeof openCharEditModal === 'function') await openCharEditModal(addChar.id);
                        await refreshSidebar();
                    } catch (e) {
                        api.v1.error('[AE] Create character failed:', e);
                        api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to create character.', { type: 'error' });
                    }
                } },
                { type: 'button', text: 'Import', iconId: 'download', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.15)', border: '1px solid rgba(136,204,255,0.3)', color: '#88ccff' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() { 
                    await pickAndImportFromLorebook(); 
                } },
            ] });

            // 2. SEARCH BAR
            parts.push({
                type: 'textInput', 
                placeholder: 'Search characters\u2026', 
                initialValue: searchQuery,
                style: { marginBottom: '12px' }, 
                onChange: async function(v: string) {
                    await api.v1.tempStorage.set('temp:ae_char_search', v);
                    await refreshSidebar();
                }
            });
            
            if (index.length === 0) {
                parts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'No characters yet.', style: { fontSize: '12px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } });
            }
            
            // 3. FILTER & SORT LOGIC
            let displayIndex = index.filter(Boolean);
            
            if (searchQuery) {
                const sq = searchQuery.toLowerCase();
                displayIndex = displayIndex.filter(m => (m.name || '').toLowerCase().includes(sq));
            }

            displayIndex.sort(function(a, b) {
                // Tier 0 Sort: Protagonist always at the absolute top
                if (a.isProtagonist !== b.isProtagonist) return a.isProtagonist ? -1 : 1;

                // Tier 1 Sort: Pinned
                const aPinned = !!(a as any).pinned;
                const bPinned = !!(b as any).pinned;
                if (aPinned !== bPinned) return aPinned ? -1 : 1;
                
                // Tier 2 Sort: Scene Present
                const aScene = !!a.scenePresent;
                const bScene = !!b.scenePresent;
                if (aScene !== bScene) return aScene ? -1 : 1;
                
                // Tier 3 Sort: Fallback to underlying array order (retains natural order)
                return 0; 
            });

            // 4. RENDER CARDS
            for (let i = 0; i < displayIndex.length; i++) {
                const meta = displayIndex[i];
                if (meta) {
                    // Extract global position so Up/Down manual-sort buttons know true array bounds
                    const globalPos = index.findIndex(m => m.id === meta.id);
                    parts.push(buildCharCard(meta, cfg, entIdx, globalPos, index.length)); 
                }
            }

    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] Roster render error:', e);
        parts.push({ type: 'box', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,107,107,0.1)', padding: '12px', borderRadius: '6px', margin: '8px 0' }, content: [
            { type: 'text', text: 'Roster failed to load', style: { fontWeight: 'bold', fontSize: '12px', color: '#ff6b6b', marginBottom: '4px' } },
            { type: 'text', text: String(e), style: { fontSize: '10px', fontFamily: 'monospace', opacity: '0.8', whiteSpace: 'pre-wrap', color: '#ff6b6b' } }
        ] });
    }
    return parts.filter(Boolean) as UIPart[];
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 20b \u2014 WORKSPACE SHELL  [v1.10.0]
// One full-screen modal hosts the "workspace" surfaces, swapped in place via
// handle.update() (api.v1.ui.modal.open). buildWorkspaceNav() gives every
// workspace a persistent collapsible cross-nav so any destination is two taps
// away. refreshSidebar() (Section 20) also calls refreshWorkspace() so the open
// workspace re-renders on any state change \u2014 existing refreshSidebar() call
// sites therefore update both surfaces with no change. Roster / Drama /
// Locations & Entities / Action Studio render through the shell; Wizard /
// Settings / Bestiary still open their own modals (Phase B folds them in).
// ============================================================================

var WORKSPACE_DEFS: { id: string; label: string; iconId: string; shell: boolean }[] = [
    { id: 'wizard',   label: 'World-Build Wizard',       iconId: 'message-square', shell: false },
    { id: 'settings', label: 'Settings',                 iconId: 'settings',       shell: true  },
    { id: 'roster',   label: 'Character Roster',         iconId: 'users',          shell: true  },
    { id: 'drama',    label: 'Drama Manager',            iconId: 'frown',            shell: true  },
    { id: 'entities', label: 'Locations & Entities',     iconId: 'globe',          shell: true  }
    // [CE-STRIP v2.11.0] 'factions' entry removed — folded into the Settings workspace
    // as a section (buildSettingsWorkspace calls buildFactionsWorkspace internally now).
    // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] 'bestiary' (NPC & Item Templates) entry removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] 'actions' (Action Studio) entry removed — no longer navigable.
    // The modal code itself (SECTION "3") is still physically present pending a full
    // deletion pass; this just takes it out of reach so it's no longer "visually there."
];

var _aeWorkspaceHandle: any = null;
var _aeWorkspaceId: string = '';

function _aeWorkspaceOpen(): boolean {
    return !!(_aeWorkspaceHandle && (typeof _aeWorkspaceHandle.isClosed !== 'function' || !_aeWorkspaceHandle.isClosed()));
}

async function _aeCloseWorkspace(): Promise<void> {
    try { if (_aeWorkspaceOpen() && typeof _aeWorkspaceHandle.close === 'function') { await _aeWorkspaceHandle.close(); } } catch (e) {}
    _aeWorkspaceHandle = null;
    _aeWorkspaceId = '';
}

async function workspaceBody(id: string): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    if (id === 'settings') return await buildSettingsWorkspace();
    if (id === 'roster')   return await buildRosterWorkspace();
    if (id === 'drama')    return await buildDramaTabContent();
    if (id === 'entities') return await buildEntitiesTab();
    // [CE-STRIP v2.11.0] 'factions' routing removed — buildFactionsWorkspace is now
    // called internally by buildSettingsWorkspace instead of being its own shell page.
    // [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] 'actions' routing removed — Action Studio cut.
    return [{ type: 'text', text: 'This workspace has no shell renderer.' } as UIPart];
}

function buildWorkspaceNav(currentId: string): UIPart {
    var cells: UIPart[] = WORKSPACE_DEFS.map(function(d) {
        var on = d.id === currentId;
        return { type: 'button', text: d.label, iconId: d.iconId as any, disabled: on, style: { flex: '1 1 46%', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', padding: '6px 8px', textAlign: 'left', fontWeight: on ? 'bold' : 'normal', backgroundColor: on ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.15)' : 'transparent', border: on ? '1px solid #7ec8a0' : '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)', borderRadius: '4px', opacity: on ? '1' : '0.8' }, callback: async function() { await launchWorkspace(d.id); } } as UIPart;
    });
    cells.push({ type: 'button', text: 'Help', iconId: 'help-circle', style: { flex: '1 1 46%', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', padding: '6px 8px', textAlign: 'left', backgroundColor: 'transparent', border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)', borderRadius: '4px', opacity: '0.8' }, callback: async function() { await openWorkspaceHelp(currentId); } } as UIPart);
    return { type: 'row', wrap: true, style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '12px', paddingBottom: '10px', borderBottom: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1)' }, content: cells } as UIPart;
}

async function renderWorkspace(id: string): Promise<void> {
    _aeWorkspaceId = id;
    var def = WORKSPACE_DEFS.filter(function(d) { return d.id === id; })[0];
    var title = def ? def.label : 'Workspace';
    var body: UIPart[];
    try { body = await workspaceBody(id); }
    catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] workspace render error:', e);
        body = [{ type: 'box', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,107,107,0.1)', padding: '12px', borderRadius: '6px' }, content: [
            { type: 'text', text: 'Workspace failed to load', style: { fontWeight: 'bold', color: '#ff6b6b', marginBottom: '4px' } },
            { type: 'text', text: String(e), style: { fontSize: '10px', fontFamily: 'monospace', opacity: '0.8', whiteSpace: 'pre-wrap', color: '#ff6b6b' } }
        ] } as UIPart];
    }
    var content: UIPart[] = [buildWorkspaceNav(id)].concat(body);
    if (_aeWorkspaceOpen() && typeof _aeWorkspaceHandle.update === 'function') {
        try { await _aeWorkspaceHandle.update({ title: title, content: content }); return; }
        catch (e) { _aeWorkspaceHandle = null; }
    }
    try {
        _aeWorkspaceHandle = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-workspace', title: title, size: 'full', content: content });
        if (_aeWorkspaceHandle && _aeWorkspaceHandle.closed && typeof _aeWorkspaceHandle.closed.then === 'function') {
            _aeWorkspaceHandle.closed.then(function() { _aeWorkspaceHandle = null; _aeWorkspaceId = ''; });
        }
    } catch (openErr) {
        _aeWorkspaceHandle = null; _aeWorkspaceId = '';
        api.v1.error('[AE] workspace modal open failed:', openErr);
        try { api.v1.ui.toast('Could not open ' + title + '. ' + String(openErr), { type: 'error' }); } catch (e2) {}
    }
}

async function openWorkspace(id: string): Promise<void> {
    await renderWorkspace(id);
}

async function refreshWorkspace(): Promise<void> {
    if (_aeWorkspaceOpen() && _aeWorkspaceId) { await renderWorkspace(_aeWorkspaceId); }
}

async function launchWorkspace(id: string): Promise<void> {
    var def = WORKSPACE_DEFS.filter(function(d) { return d.id === id; })[0];
    if (def && def.shell) { await openWorkspace(id); return; }
    await _aeCloseWorkspace();
    try {
        if (id === 'wizard')   { await openWizardModal(); return; }
        // [CE-STRIP v2.11.0] 'settings' special-case removed — settings is shell:true
        // now (see WORKSPACE_DEFS) and routes through the generic path above via
        // buildSettingsWorkspace, which merges the old World Settings + System
        // Settings + Factions Manager into one workspace.
        // [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] 'bestiary' route dispatch removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] launchWorkspace legacy:', e); }
}

function _wsHelpLine(label: string, body: string): UIPart[] {
    return [
        { type: 'text', text: label, style: { fontWeight: 'bold', fontSize: '12px' } } as UIPart,
        { type: 'text', text: body, style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.8', marginBottom: '8px' } } as UIPart
    ];
}

    entities: { title: 'Locations & Entities Help', parts: ([
        { type: 'text', text: 'The world graph: locations, items, and narrative objects, nested tree-style \u2014 a region holds settlements, a settlement holds buildings, a building holds rooms. Travel and adjacency are computed from this structure automatically.', style: { fontSize: '12px', lineHeight: '1.5', marginBottom: '8px' } } as UIPart
    ] as UIPart[])
        .concat(_wsHelpLine('Create Entity', 'Adds a blank entity anywhere in the tree \u2014 for items, narrative objects, or a location you want full manual control over.'))
        .concat(_wsHelpLine('Map', 'A room-centered visual view for authoring locations. + New Location spawns a sibling (same tier, no extra adjacency) or a child (nested inside), generated from context or written by hand.'))

    // [BUGFIX v2.26.0] The 'actions' entry (Action Studio Help — Stakes/Action Library/
    // Four Fs/2d6+Modifier) is REMOVED. It was already flagged as unreachable (no button
    // routes to an 'actions' workspace since [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] — see the note near the

    wizard: { title: 'World-Build Wizard Help', parts: [

Set your project name and concept on the landing page, then work through Topics here \u2014 each chat feeds a matching generator: Species, Factions, Locations, Characters, Drama, Intro.', style: { fontSize: '12px', lineHeight: '1.5', marginBottom: '8px' } } as UIPart,
        { type: 'text', text: 'Every batch is a draft \u2014 review, edit, or discard each result in the gallery before anything commits to your world or lorebook.', style: { fontSize: '12px', lineHeight: '1.5' } } as UIPart
    ] },
    settings: { title: 'Settings Help', parts: ([

    ] as UIPart[]) },

    // described a Holding Document that CE-STRIP v2.0.x removed long before this pass.)

// [BUGFIX v2.26.0] openWorkspaceHelp was CALLED by every workspace's Help button
// (buildWorkspaceShell's action row, `callback: async function() { await
// openWorkspaceHelp(currentId); }`) but never DEFINED anywhere in the file — flagged
// as a real bug in an earlier pass (see the note above this pass's own additions) but
// left alone pending "what should the Help button chain actually do now" — answered:
// nothing special: a straight WORKSPACE_HELP[id] lookup into a modal is all any current
// workspace needs, now that 'actions' (the one entry that wanted special-case behavior,
// openActionStudioHelp — also never defined, also never called from anywhere but this
// same dead comment) is gone. Tapping Help previously threw a ReferenceError, surfaced
// by NAI as "Error in user script ... in UI callback ... unwrapResult".
async function openWorkspaceHelp(id: string): Promise<void> {
    const entry = WORKSPACE_HELP[id];
    if (!entry) { api.v1.ui.toast('No help available for this section.', { type: 'info', autoClose: 2500 }); return; }
    await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-workspace-help', title: entry.title, size: 'medium', content: entry.parts });
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.0.0] Action Studio (buildActionTab, buildStakeTrackItems,
// stakeTrackDisplayLabel, buildCharResWidget, the whole UIRuleManifest condition/
// effect catalog and its arg-rendering helpers, openActionBuilderModal, and the
// Action Studio Help modal) fully removed. The workspace nav entry was already
// removed earlier in this pass, so this was unreachable; now it's gone outright.
// Every external caller (Entry Action / Travel Action buttons, EVT_RUN_ACTION_
// INSTANCE, RUN_QUICK_INSTANCE, the native Available-Actions/Quick-Action-Composer
// buttons) was traced and fixed or confirmed already-unreachable earlier in this pass.

async function moveChar(id: string, direction: number) {
    const idx = await loadIndex();
    const pos = idx.findIndex(m => m.id === id);
    if (pos < 0) return;
    const newPos = pos + direction;
    if (newPos < 0 || newPos >= idx.length) return;
    
    const temp = idx[pos];
    idx[pos] = idx[newPos];
    idx[newPos] = temp;
    
    await saveIndex(idx);
    await refreshSidebar();
}

// ── Characters Tab: Card & Kill ──────────────────────────────────────────────

function buildCharCard(meta: any, cfg: GlobalConfig, entIdx: any[], idxPos: number, idxLength: number): UIPart {
    const isScene = meta.scenePresent;
    const isPinned = !!meta.pinned;
    const isStatic = !!meta.isStatic;
    const isProtag = !!meta.isProtagonist;
    let avatarCol: UIPart | null = null;
    
    // Left edge highlight for scene presence (Major/Minor logic removed)
    const cardBorderLeft = isScene ? '4px solid #7ec8a0' : '4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.05)';

    // ── RIGHT COLUMN ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Row 1: Name + Dynamic/Static + Pin
    const hasCritHookMeta = !!meta.hasCriticalHook;
    const protagPrefix = isProtag ? '👑 ' : '';
    const nameRow: UIPart = { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '4px', marginBottom: '2px' }, content: [
        { type: 'text', text: protagPrefix + (hasCritHookMeta ? '🔥 ' : '') + (meta.name || 'Unnamed'), style: { fontSize: '13px', fontWeight: 'bold', flex: '1 1 0', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis', color: isProtag ? '#ffb432' : undefined } },
        { type: 'button', text: isStatic ? '▫️Static' : '⚡️Dynamic', style: { margin: 0, padding: '2px 4px', fontSize: '9px', backgroundColor: isStatic ? 'rgba(255,255,255,0.05)' : 'rgba(136,204,255,0.15)', color: isStatic ? '#888' : '#88ccff', border: 'none' }, callback: async function() {
            const f = await loadCharacter(meta.id); if (!f) return;
            f.isStatic = !f.isStatic;
            // Master gate: flip all dynamic flags in sync with card toggle
            const nowDynamic = !f.isStatic;
            f.charContextEnabled = nowDynamic;
            f.autoUpdateEnabled = nowDynamic;
            f.autoThoughtsEnabled = nowDynamic;
            f.hooksEnabled = nowDynamic;
            await saveCharacter(f);
            
            // Sync to index for immediate UI refresh
            const idx = await loadIndex();
            const mMatch = idx.find(function(m) { return m.id === meta.id; });
            if (mMatch) { (mMatch as any).isStatic = f.isStatic; await saveIndex(idx); }
            await refreshSidebar();
        } },
        // [vNEXT] Dynamic-priority rank. Hidden for the protagonist (never dynamic).
        // Shows the current rank or a neutral marker when unranked; tapping opens the
        // swap-enforced rank picker.
        (isProtag) ? { type: 'text', text: '', style: { width: '0px' } } : { type: 'button', text: (typeof meta.dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && meta.dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? ('#' + meta.dynamicPriorityRank) : '#–', style: { margin: 0, padding: '2px 4px', fontSize: '9px', backgroundColor: (typeof meta.dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && meta.dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? 'rgba(232,168,124,0.15)' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.05)', color: (typeof meta.dynamicPriorityRank === 'number' && meta.dynamicPriorityRank > 0) ? '#e8a87c' : '#888', border: 'none' }, callback: async function() {
            await openDynamicRankPicker(meta.id, async function() { await refreshSidebar(); });
        } },
        { type: 'button', text: (isPinned || isProtag) ? '📌' : '📌', style: { margin: 0, padding: '2px 4px', fontSize: '10px', backgroundColor: 'transparent', opacity: (isPinned || isProtag) ? '1' : '0.3', border: 'none' }, disabled: isProtag, callback: async function() {
            const f = await loadCharacter(meta.id); if (!f) return;
            f.pinned = !f.pinned; await saveCharacter(f);
            
            const idx = await loadIndex();
            const mMatch = idx.find(function(m) { return m.id === meta.id; });
            if (mMatch) { (mMatch as any).pinned = f.pinned; await saveIndex(idx); }
            await refreshSidebar();
        } }
    ] };

    // Row 2: Species, Age, Gender, Role
    let detailsArr: string[] = [];
    if (meta.species) detailsArr.push(meta.species);
    if (meta.age) detailsArr.push(meta.age);
    if (meta.gender) detailsArr.push(meta.gender);
    if (meta.occupationRole) detailsArr.push(meta.occupationRole);
    const detailsStr = detailsArr.length > 0 ? detailsArr.join(' · ') : 'No details';
    
    const descRow: UIPart = { type: 'text', text: detailsStr, style: { fontSize: '10px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '4px', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis' } };

    // Row 3: Equipped items (if any)
    const equipped = (meta.equippedItemNames || []).filter(Boolean);
    const equipRow: UIPart | null = equipped.length > 0 ? {
        type: 'text', text: `🎒 ${equipped.join(', ')}`, style: { fontSize: '10px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '4px', fontStyle: 'italic', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis' }
    } : null;

    // Row 4: Action Row (Scene Toggle + Location + Kill + Sort + Edit)
    const locName = meta.currentLocationId
        ? ((entIdx || []).find(function(l: any) { return l.id === meta.currentLocationId; })?.name || 'Unknown')
        : 'No Location';

    const sceneBtn: UIPart = {
        type: 'button',
        text: isScene ? '🟢 Scene' : '⚫ Away',
        style: { margin: 0, padding: '4px 6px', fontSize: '10px', backgroundColor: isScene ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.15)' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.05)', color: isScene ? '#7ec8a0' : '#888', border: 'none' },
        callback: async function() {
            const f = await loadCharacter(meta.id); if (!f) return;
            f.scenePresent = !f.scenePresent; await saveCharacter(f); await refreshSidebar();
        }
    };

    const locationBtn: UIPart = { type: 'button', text: `📍 ${locName}`, style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, padding: '4px 6px', fontSize: '10px', textAlign: 'left', opacity: '0.8', borderRadius: '4px', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis' }, callback: async function() {
        const freshEntIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
        // [BUGFIX v2.26.0] Same fix as the character edit modal's Set Location picker —
        // was `n.type !== 'character'`, which let items and narrative threads show up
        // as location options. Only type==='location' entities are valid spatial parents.
        const spatialNodes = freshEntIdx.filter(function(n: any) { return n.type === 'location'; });
        const pm = await openEngineModal({ title: 'Set Location', size: 'medium', content: [
            { type: 'button', text: '✕ No Location', style: { width: '100%', marginBottom: '8px' }, callback: async function() {
                const f = await loadCharacter(meta.id); if (!f) { pm.close(); return; }
                if (f.entityId) { let locEnt = await loadEntity(f.entityId); if (locEnt) { locEnt.parentId = null; await saveEntity(locEnt); } }
                await saveCharacter(f); await recalculateScenePresence(); pm.close(); await refreshSidebar();
            } },
            ...spatialNodes.filter(Boolean).map(function(l: any) {
                return { type: 'button', text: `📍 ${l.name}`, style: { width: '100%', marginBottom: '2px', textAlign: 'left', backgroundColor: meta.currentLocationId === l.id ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.2)' : undefined }, callback: async function() {
                    const f = await loadCharacter(meta.id); if (!f) { pm.close(); return; }
                    if (f.entityId) { let locEnt2 = await loadEntity(f.entityId); if (locEnt2) { locEnt2.parentId = l.id; await saveEntity(locEnt2); } }
                    await saveCharacter(f); await recalculateScenePresence(); pm.close(); await refreshSidebar();
                } } as UIPart;
            })
        ]});
    } };

    const actionRowContent: UIPart[] = [ sceneBtn, locationBtn ];
    if (!isProtag) {
        actionRowContent.push({
            type: 'button', text: '💀', style: { margin: 0, padding: '4px 6px', fontSize: '11px', backgroundColor: 'transparent', color: '#ff6b6b', border: '1px solid rgba(255,107,107,0.2)' },
            callback: async function() { await killCharacter(meta.id); await refreshSidebar(); }
        } as UIPart);
    }
    actionRowContent.push(
        { type: 'button', iconId: 'arrow-up', style: { margin: 0, padding: '4px', backgroundColor: 'transparent', opacity: idxPos > 0 ? '1' : '0.2' }, disabled: idxPos === 0, callback: async function() { await moveChar(meta.id, -1); } },
        { type: 'button', iconId: 'arrow-down', style: { margin: 0, padding: '4px', backgroundColor: 'transparent', opacity: idxPos < idxLength - 1 ? '1' : '0.2' }, disabled: idxPos >= idxLength - 1, callback: async function() { await moveChar(meta.id, 1); } },
        { type: 'button', iconId: 'edit-2', style: { margin: 0, padding: '4px', backgroundColor: 'transparent' }, callback: async function() { await openCharEditModal(meta.id); } }
    );

    const actionRow: UIPart = { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '4px', marginTop: '4px' }, content: actionRowContent };

    const rightCol: UIPart = { type: 'column', style: { flex: '1 1 0', gap: '0', overflow: 'hidden' }, content: [
        nameRow, descRow,
        ...(equipRow ? [equipRow] : []),
        actionRow
    ] };

    return {
        type: 'box',
        style: { marginBottom: '8px', padding: '8px', borderRadius: '8px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.02)', border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.05)', borderLeft: cardBorderLeft, opacity: meta.active ? '1' : '0.45' },
        content: [{ type: 'row', style: { gap: '10px' }, content: avatarCol ? [avatarCol, rightCol] : [rightCol] }]
    };
}

// ============================================================================
// [vNEXT] FREEFORM SCENE NPC SPAWNING
// [CE-STRIP vNEXT] Minor-NPC tier retired — the "+ Add Character Here" flow now
// creates an ordinary CharacterRecord with normal defaults (dynamic, full
// lorebook/dossier fallback via buildSceneLorebookActivation's standard
// per-character path, same as a character made any other way). No more
// starved-record/promote lifecycle — there is nothing to promote FROM anymore.
// ============================================================================

// [vNEXT] Expand {a|b|c} alternation groups in a template string, picking one
// option per group via the seeded RNG. Runs ONCE at spawn time and the result is
// baked into the record — deliberately kept out of the live |macro| evaluator,
// which re-evaluates every generation and would re-roll the choice each turn.
function aeExpandAlternations(txt: string): string {
    let outAlt = String(txt || '');
    let guardAlt = 0;
    while (guardAlt < 24) {
        let mAlt = outAlt.match(/\{([^{}|]*(?:\|[^{}|]*)+)\}/);
        if (!mAlt) break;
        let optsAlt = mAlt[1].split('|');
        let idxAlt = 0;
        try { idxAlt = api.v1.random.roll('1d' + optsAlt.length).total - 1; } catch (eAlt) { idxAlt = 0; }
        if (idxAlt < 0 || idxAlt >= optsAlt.length) idxAlt = 0;
        let posAlt = (typeof mAlt.index === 'number') ? mAlt.index : 0;
        outAlt = outAlt.substring(0, posAlt) + optsAlt[idxAlt].trim() + outAlt.substring(posAlt + mAlt[0].length);
        guardAlt++;
    }
    return outAlt;
}

async function spawnSceneNpc(name: string, brief: string, locId: string | null, quiet?: boolean): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {
    try {
        const cfg = await loadConfig();
        if (!locId) { locId = await aeGetPlayerRoomId(); }   // [vNEXT] default to the player's actual room
        let baseName = aeExpandAlternations(name || 'Someone').trim();
        if (!baseName) baseName = 'Someone';
        let finalBrief = aeExpandAlternations(brief || '').trim();

        // Numbered suffix on collision with any active character (Skeleton, Skeleton 2...).
        const nsIdx = await loadIndex();
        let finalName = baseName;
        let nsN = 2;
        while (nsIdx.some(function(m) { return !!m && m.active && (m.name || '') === finalName; }) && nsN < 50) {
            finalName = baseName + ' ' + nsN;
            nsN++;
        }

        const body = makeDefaultEntity(finalName);
        body.type = 'character';
        body.parentId = locId ? locId : null;
        body.description = finalBrief;
        await saveEntity(body);

        const rec = makeDefaultCharacter(finalName, cfg);
        rec.entityId = body.id;
        rec.briefDescription = finalBrief;
        rec.active = true;
        rec.scenePresent = true;
        await saveCharacter(rec);

        await recalculateScenePresence();
        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
        if (!quiet) api.v1.ui.toast('Character "' + finalName + '" added.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 });
        return { id: rec.id, name: finalName };
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] spawnSceneNpc:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to add character.', { type: 'error' });
        return null;
    }
}

// [vNEXT] Scene-grounded NPC generator. Uses generateWithStory in 'background' mode
// (same path as generateCharacterSnapshot) so the model sees the live story +
// location and invents someone who fits the moment. Returns a {name, brief} for
// review/spawn; does NOT create anything itself.
async function generateSceneNpc(): Promise<{ name: string; brief: string } | null> {
    try {
        let gsLocName = 'the current location';
        let gsLocDesc = '';
        let gsLocId = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, null) as string | null;
        if (gsLocId) {
            let gsLocEnt = await loadEntity(gsLocId);
            if (gsLocEnt) { gsLocName = gsLocEnt.name || gsLocName; gsLocDesc = gsLocEnt.description || ''; }
        }
        let gsCfg = await loadConfig();
        let gsSys: string[] = [
            gsCfg.generalStyle || '',
            '',
            'You invent ONE background character who would plausibly be present in the current scene.',
            'Base them on what is actually happening in the story right now and on the location.',
            'Output EXACTLY two lines and nothing else, no preamble:',
            'NAME: <a short name or label, e.g. Gate Guard, Mara, Hooded Vendor>',
            'DOING: <one present-tense sentence describing what they are doing here and why>'
        ];
        let gsUsr: string[] = [
            'Location: ' + gsLocName,
            gsLocDesc ? 'Setting: ' + gsLocDesc.slice(0, 160) : '',
            'Invent a fitting NPC now. Two lines only.'
        ].filter(Boolean);

        let gsResp = await api.v1.generateWithStory(
            [{ role: 'system', content: gsSys.join('\n') }, { role: 'user', content: gsUsr.join('\n') }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 120 },
            { suppressScriptHooks: 'self', contextLimitReduction: 450 },
            undefined,
            'background'
        );
        let gsTxt = (gsResp && gsResp.choices && gsResp.choices.length > 0) ? String(gsResp.choices[0].text || '') : '';
        if (!gsTxt.trim()) return null;

        let gsName = '';
        let gsBrief = '';
        gsTxt.split('\n').forEach(function(ln) {
            let t = ln.trim();
            let up = t.toUpperCase();
            if (up.indexOf('NAME:') === 0) gsName = t.substring(5).trim();
            else if (up.indexOf('DOING:') === 0) gsBrief = t.substring(6).trim();
        });
        if (!gsName || !gsBrief) {
            let gsLines = gsTxt.split('\n').map(function(s) { return s.trim(); }).filter(Boolean);
            if (!gsName && gsLines.length > 0) gsName = gsLines[0].replace(/^NAME:\s*/i, '');
            if (!gsBrief && gsLines.length > 1) gsBrief = gsLines.slice(1).join(' ').replace(/^DOING:\s*/i, '');
        }
        gsName = gsName.replace(/^["'\s\-]+/, '').replace(/["'\s]+$/, '').slice(0, 60);
        gsBrief = gsBrief.replace(/^["'\s\-]+/, '').slice(0, 240);
        if (!gsName) return null;
        return { name: gsName, brief: gsBrief };
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] generateSceneNpc:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Scene generation failed.', { type: 'error' });
        return null;
    }
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] NPC Archetype editor (aeMutateArchetype/buildArchetypeEditContent/refreshArchetypeEditModal/openArchetypeEditModal), Bestiary modal (buildBestiaryContent/refreshBestiaryModal/openBestiaryModal), and Item Template mutator (aeMutateItemArchetype) all removed — Bestiary + Item Templates cut.

// Spawn one item from a template. Target defaults to the player's inventory (parented
// to the protagonist's body); a template may instead choose the current room, where the
// loose item becomes pick-up-able via the library Pick Up action. {a|b|c} alternations
// in name/description are rolled once, here, and baked into the copy.
async function spawnItem(name: string, desc: string, quiet?: boolean, explicitParentId?: string | null): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {
    try {
        // Target resolution: an explicit entity id (e.g. the inspected room) wins; the
        // sentinels '__room__' / '__inventory__' force a mode; default is inventory (the
        // protagonist's body).
        // [FIX v1.19.0] spTarget used to be declared inside the `else` below, so the
        // explicit-parent-id branch (the exact path AE_HUD_SPAWN_ENTITY takes) skipped
        // its declaration entirely -- saveEntity succeeded but the toast line threw a
        // ReferenceError, surfacing as "Failed to add item." on a spawn that actually
        // worked. Hoisted to outer scope; explicit parent counts as 'room' (a place in
        // the world, not the protagonist's body).
        let target: string | null = null;
        let spTarget: string;
        if (explicitParentId && explicitParentId !== '__inventory__' && explicitParentId !== '__room__') {
            target = explicitParentId;
            spTarget = 'room';
        } else {
            if (explicitParentId === '__room__') spTarget = 'room';
            else spTarget = 'inventory';
            if (spTarget === 'room') {
                target = await aeGetPlayerRoomId();
            } else {
                const spChars = await loadAllCharacters();
                const spProtag = spChars.find(function(c) { return c.isProtagonist; });
                target = (spProtag && spProtag.entityId) ? spProtag.entityId : await aeGetPlayerRoomId();
            }
        }

        let baseName = aeExpandAlternations(name || 'Item').trim();
        if (!baseName) baseName = 'Item';
        let finalDesc = aeExpandAlternations(desc || '').trim();

        // Numbered suffix on collision with any item already in the same container.
        const siIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
        let finalName = baseName;
        let siN = 2;
        while (siIdx.some(function(m) { return !!m && m.type === 'item' && m.parentId === target && (m.name || '') === finalName; }) && siN < 50) {
            finalName = baseName + ' ' + siN;
            siN++;
        }

        const body = makeDefaultEntity(finalName);
        body.type = 'item';
        body.parentId = target ? target : null;
        body.description = finalDesc;
        await saveEntity(body);

        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
        if (!quiet) api.v1.ui.toast('Item \u201c' + finalName + '\u201d added' + (spTarget === 'room' ? ' to the room.' : ' to inventory.'), { type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 });
        return { id: body.id, name: finalName };
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] spawnItem:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to add item.', { type: 'error' });
        return null;
    }
}

// Scene-grounded item generator. Same background path as generateSceneNpc: reads the
// live location, invents an item that fits the moment, returns {name, desc} for
// review/spawn. Creates nothing itself.
async function generateSceneItem(): Promise<{ name: string; desc: string } | null> {
    try {
        let giLocName = 'the current location';
        let giLocDesc = '';
        let giLocId = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, null) as string | null;
        if (giLocId) {
            let giLocEnt = await loadEntity(giLocId);
            if (giLocEnt) { giLocName = giLocEnt.name || giLocName; giLocDesc = giLocEnt.description || ''; }
        }
        let giCfg = await loadConfig();
        let giSys: string[] = [
            giCfg.generalStyle || '',
            '',
            'You invent ONE item that would plausibly be found in the current scene.',
            'Base it on what is actually happening in the story right now and on the location.',
            'Output EXACTLY two lines and nothing else, no preamble:',
            'NAME: <a short item name, e.g. Rusted Key, Silk Scarf, Hunting Knife>',
            'DESC: <one sentence describing what it is and its notable qualities>'
        ];
        let giUsr: string[] = [
            'Location: ' + giLocName,
            giLocDesc ? 'Setting: ' + giLocDesc.slice(0, 160) : '',
            'Invent a fitting item now. Two lines only.'
        ].filter(Boolean);

        let giResp = await api.v1.generateWithStory(
            [{ role: 'system', content: giSys.join('\n') }, { role: 'user', content: giUsr.join('\n') }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 120 },
            { suppressScriptHooks: 'self', contextLimitReduction: 450 },
            undefined,
            'background'
        );
        let giTxt = (giResp && giResp.choices && giResp.choices.length > 0) ? String(giResp.choices[0].text || '') : '';
        if (!giTxt.trim()) return null;

        let giName = '';
        let giDesc = '';
        giTxt.split('\n').forEach(function(ln) {
            let t = ln.trim();
            let up = t.toUpperCase();
            if (up.indexOf('NAME:') === 0) giName = t.substring(5).trim();
            else if (up.indexOf('DESC:') === 0) giDesc = t.substring(5).trim();
        });
        if (!giName || !giDesc) {
            let giLines = giTxt.split('\n').map(function(s2) { return s2.trim(); }).filter(Boolean);
            if (!giName && giLines.length > 0) giName = giLines[0].replace(/^NAME:\s*/i, '');
            if (!giDesc && giLines.length > 1) giDesc = giLines.slice(1).join(' ').replace(/^DESC:\s*/i, '');
        }
        giName = giName.replace(/^["'\s\-]+/, '').replace(/["'\s]+$/, '').slice(0, 60);
        giDesc = giDesc.replace(/^["'\s\-]+/, '').slice(0, 240);
        if (!giName) return null;
        return { name: giName, desc: giDesc };
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] generateSceneItem:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Scene generation failed.', { type: 'error' });
        return null;
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// [v1.18.0 LOCGEN] IN-PLAY LOCATION AUTHORING
// The spatial sibling of spawnItem / generateSceneItem, directly above. Same two-step
// shape: generate a draft from live story context, hand it back for review, then create.
//
// RELATION is the whole model. There is no third case:
//   'sibling'  same tier, adjacent      -> parentId = reference location's parentId
//   'child'    contained within here    -> parentId = reference location's id
// Sibling adjacency is implicit (shared parentId); no connectedEntityIds edge is written.
//
// >>> PAIRED CODE, three ways. Change one, check the others:
// >>>   generateLocationBatch()  — the wizard's chat-context twin of the prompt below.
// >>>   createLocationEntity()   — the shared entity+lorebook birth helper both use.
// >>>   spawnItem/spawnNpc       — the popup/round-trip pattern this mirrors, and the
// >>>                              AE_HUD_GEN_ENTITY / AE_HUD_SPAWN_ENTITY message pair
// >>>                              that AE_HUD_GEN_LOCATION / AE_HUD_SPAWN_LOCATION copy.
// ============================================================================

// Scene-grounded location generator. Background generation (NOT executeYoinkGeneration —
// that hijacks the foreground editor call, which is right for an interactive wizard chat
// and wrong for a HUD button). Reads the reference location and an optional author
// suggestion; returns {name, description, lore} for review. Creates nothing itself.
async function generateSceneLocation(relation: string, refLocId?: string | null, suggestion?: string): Promise<{ name: string; description: string; lore: string } | null> {
    try {
        let glRel = (relation === 'child') ? 'child' : 'sibling';
        let glRefId = refLocId ? refLocId : (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, null) as string | null);
        let glRefEnt = glRefId ? await loadEntity(glRefId) : null;
        let glRefName = (glRefEnt && glRefEnt.name) ? glRefEnt.name : 'the current location';
        let glRefDesc = (glRefEnt && glRefEnt.description) ? glRefEnt.description : '';
        let glParentId = (glRel === 'child') ? (glRefId || null) : (glRefEnt ? (glRefEnt.parentId || null) : null);
        let glParentEnt = (glRel === 'sibling' && glParentId) ? await loadEntity(glParentId) : null;

        let glIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
        let glExisting = glIdx
            .filter(function(m) { return m.type === 'location' && m.parentId === (glParentId || null); })
            .map(function(m) { return m.name; })
            .filter(Boolean);

        let glCfg = await loadConfig();
        let glScope = (glRel === 'child')
            ? 'a NEW place contained INSIDE \u201c' + glRefName + '\u201d \u2014 one scale-step smaller than it (a room within a house, a district within a city, a glade within a forest).'
            : 'a NEW place standing ALONGSIDE \u201c' + glRefName + '\u201d at the SAME scale' + (glParentEnt ? ', both contained within \u201c' + glParentEnt.name + '\u201d' : ', both at the top level of the world') + '.';

        let glSys: string[] = [
            glCfg.generalStyle || '',
            '',
            'You invent exactly ONE new location for a story world: ' + glScope,
            'Ground it in what is actually happening in the story right now, and in the reference location below.',
            'CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT use any markdown. DO NOT bold headers or names. Output plain text only.',
            'FORMAT: output exactly these three labelled blocks and nothing else, no preamble:',
            'NAME: <short place name>',
            'DESC: <1-2 sentences capturing ONLY what a camera physically senses on arrival (light, texture, scale). No history.>',
            'LORE: <a full, comprehensive entry (roughly 5-8 sentences) covering history, significance, culture, and tensions. Never restate the DESC line.>'
        ];
        if (glExisting.length) {
            glSys.push('');
            glSys.push('These places ALREADY EXIST at that same level \u2014 do NOT recreate or duplicate them:\n' + glExisting.map(function(gn) { return '- ' + gn; }).join('\n'));
        }

        let glUsr: string[] = [
            'Reference location: ' + glRefName,
            glRefDesc ? 'Setting: ' + glRefDesc.slice(0, 240) : '',
            (glParentEnt && (glParentEnt.description || '').trim()) ? 'Containing place \u2014 ' + glParentEnt.name + ': ' + glParentEnt.description.trim().slice(0, 240) : '',
            (suggestion || '').trim() ? 'The author asks specifically for: ' + String(suggestion).trim().slice(0, 400) : '',
            'Invent the location now. Three labelled blocks only.'
        ].filter(Boolean);

        let glResp = await api.v1.generateWithStory(
            [{ role: 'system', content: glSys.join('\n') }, { role: 'user', content: glUsr.join('\n\n') }],
            { model: 'glm-4-6', max_tokens: 420 },
            { suppressScriptHooks: 'self', contextLimitReduction: 700 },
            undefined,
            'background'
        );
        let glTxt = (glResp && glResp.choices && glResp.choices.length > 0) ? String(glResp.choices[0].text || '') : '';
        if (!glTxt.trim()) return null;

        // Same three regexes generateLocationBatch uses per chunk (see PAIRED PROMPT note there).
        let glNmM = glTxt.match(/NAME:\s*(.+)/i);
        let glDeM = glTxt.match(/DESC:\s*([\s\S]+?)(?:\nLORE:|$)/i);
        let glLoM = glTxt.match(/LORE:\s*([\s\S]+)/i);
        if (!glNmM) return null;
        let glName = glNmM[1].trim().replace(/^["'\s\-]+/, '').replace(/["'\s]+$/, '').slice(0, 60);
        let glDesc = glDeM ? glDeM[1].replace(/\nLORE:[\s\S]*$/i, '').replace(/\nNAME:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim() : '';
        let glLore = glLoM ? glLoM[1].replace(/\n-{3,}[\s\S]*$/, '').replace(/\nNAME:[\s\S]*$/i, '').trim() : '';
        if (!glName) return null;
        return { name: glName, description: glDesc, lore: glLore };
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] generateSceneLocation:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Location generation failed.', { type: 'error' });
        return null;
    }
}

// Create one location relative to a reference location (default: the player's current
// one). Depth decides the tier travel template, exactly as the wizard batch path does.
// {a|b|c} alternations in the name/description are rolled once, here.
async function spawnLocation(name: string, description: string, lore: string, relation: string, refLocId?: string | null, quiet?: boolean): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {
    try {
        let slRel = (relation === 'child') ? 'child' : 'sibling';
        let slRefId = refLocId ? refLocId : (await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC, null) as string | null);

        // [v2.51.0 ROOT-ADDLOC, per user] At the world root there is no reference ENTITY to
        // anchor to: AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID is a sentinel (deliberately never a real entity, so
        // loadEntity() on it always returns undefined), and a location-less world has no
        // STORAGE_PLAYER_LOC target either. Both bailed here before — the sentinel hit the
        // "Reference location not found." dead end, a null ref hit "No current location..." —
        // which is exactly why a new location could not be created at root from either the
        // native map or the bridge round-trip (both funnel through this one function). At the
        // root, 'sibling' and 'child' collapse to the same result: a new TOP-LEVEL location
        // (parentId null). Handle that directly instead of loading the sentinel.
        let slIsRoot = (!slRefId) || (slRefId === AE_ROOT_LOCATION_ID);
        let slRefEnt: any = null;
        let slParentId: string | null;
        if (slIsRoot) {
            slParentId = null;
        } else {
            slRefEnt = await loadEntity(slRefId);
            if (!slRefEnt) {
                if (!quiet) api.v1.ui.toast('Reference location not found.', { type: 'error' });
                return null;
            }
            slParentId = (slRel === 'child') ? slRefId : (slRefEnt.parentId || null);
        }

        let slBase = aeExpandAlternations(name || '').trim();
        if (!slBase) slBase = 'New Location';

        // Numbered suffix on collision with any location already sharing the new parent.
        let slIdx = await loadEntityIndex();
        let slFinal = slBase;
        let slN = 2;
        while (slIdx.some(function(m) { return !!m && m.type === 'location' && m.parentId === (slParentId || null) && (m.name || '') === slFinal; }) && slN < 50) {
            slFinal = slBase + ' ' + slN;
            slN++;
        }

        let slDesc = aeExpandAlternations(description || '').trim();
        let slId = await createLocationEntity(slFinal, slDesc, (lore || '').trim(), slParentId);

        queueSceneStateBroadcast();
        if (!quiet) {
            let slWhere = slIsRoot
                ? 'at the top level of the world'
                : ((slRel === 'child')
                    ? 'inside ' + (slRefEnt.name || 'here')
                    : 'beside ' + (slRefEnt.name || 'here'));
            api.v1.ui.toast('Location \u201c' + slFinal + '\u201d created ' + slWhere + '.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 2600 });
        }
        return { id: slId, name: slFinal };
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] spawnLocation:', e);
        api.v1.ui.toast('Failed to create location.', { type: 'error' });
        return null;
    }
}

// [CE-STRIP v2.1.6] Item Template editor (buildItemArchetypeEditContent/refreshItemArchetypeEditModal/openItemArchetypeEditModal/buildItemTemplateRows) removed — Item Templates cut along with Bestiary.


// ── [vNEXT] Add-NPC modal (freeform, scene-grounded) ──────────────────────────
// [v1.19.0 LOCGEN] >>> PAIRED with openAddLocationModal() (near killCharacter, below):
// >>> same three-part shape (module state / build.../ open...), spatial instead of
// >>> populational. Its bridge-side twin is _showAddLocationPopup (Map tab).
var _aeAddNpcHandle: any = null;
var _aeAddNpcName = '';
var _aeAddNpcBrief = '';
var _aeAddNpcLoc: string | null = null;
var _aeAddNpcRefresh: (() => Promise<void>) | null = null;

async function buildAddNpcContent(): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    let amParts: UIPart[] = [];
    amParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'A quick drop-in character at the current location. Name plus what they are doing here — an ordinary character like any other, just fast to create.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.7', marginBottom: '8px' } } as UIPart);
    amParts.push({ type: 'textInput', label: 'Name', placeholder: 'e.g. Skeleton, Gate Guard, Fruit Vendor', initialValue: _aeAddNpcName, onChange: function(v: string) { _aeAddNpcName = v; } } as UIPart);
    amParts.push({ type: 'multilineTextInput', label: 'What are they doing here?', placeholder: 'e.g. A brittle skeleton lurching forward to attack.', initialValue: _aeAddNpcBrief, style: { minHeight: '50px' }, onChange: function(v: string) { _aeAddNpcBrief = v; } } as UIPart);
    amParts.push({ type: 'button', text: '✨ Generate from Scene', style: { width: '100%', marginTop: '6px', fontSize: '11px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.12)', color: '#88ccff' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
        api.v1.ui.toast('Generating an NPC that fits the scene…', { id: 'ae-gen-am', autoClose: false });
