// flat rather than nested inside another collapsible, since they're already
// self-contained (avoids double-nesting). Factions' own builder doesn't wrap
// itself in a collapsible (it was a whole standalone workspace page before),
// so it gets one wrapper section here to sit as a peer of World/System's
// sections instead of dumping its full contents inline uncollapsed.
async function buildSettingsWorkspace(): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    const parts: UIPart[] = [];
    parts.push.apply(parts, await buildWorldbuildingContent());
    const cfgForCount = await loadConfig();
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: `⚔️ Factions (${(cfgForCount.factions || []).length})`, initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:settings_factions', content: await buildFactionsWorkspace() } as UIPart);
    parts.push.apply(parts, await buildConfigContent());
    return parts.filter(Boolean) as UIPart[];
}

async function openWorldbuildingModal(): Promise<void> {
    if (worldbuildingModalHandle && !worldbuildingModalHandle.isClosed()) worldbuildingModalHandle.close();
    worldbuildingModalHandle = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-worldbuilding', size: 'full', fillWidth: true, title: '🌍 Adventure Engine: Worldbuilding', content: await buildWorldbuildingContent() });
}

async function buildWorldbuildingContent(): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    const cfg = await loadConfig(); const parts: UIPart[] = [];

    // 2. Species
    // [v1.24.0 CLEANUP] The old "Introduction & Setup" walkthrough (bridge-toast /
    // widget onboarding copy) was removed here — stale relative to the current
    // landing-page + workspace shell, and workspace Help buttons now cover onboarding.
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: `🧬 Species (${(cfg.species || []).length})`, initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:wb_cfg_species', content: await (async function() {
        const spParts: UIPart[] = [];
        spParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Define species available as a selector on character cards. Create lorebook entries to give the AI detailed knowledge of each.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        for (let si = 0; si < (cfg.species || []).length; si++) {
            const sp = cfg.species[si];
            // [v1.10.2 FACFIX] per-iteration bindings — var here leaked one shared
            // capture to every row's callbacks (all edits/deletes hit the last row).
            const captSi = si; const captSpId = sp.id;
            
            const tmpSpKey = `wb_tmp_sp_${captSpId}`;
            const spLbContent: UIPart[] = [];
            if (sp.lorebookEntryId) {
                spLbContent.push(
                    { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '6px' }, content: [{ type: 'text', text: '✓ Lorebook entry linked', style: { fontSize: '12px', color: '#7ec8a0', flex: '1 1 0' } }] },
                    { type: 'text', text: `Keys: ${getSpeciesLorebookKeys(sp).join(', ')}`, style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.55', marginBottom: '8px' } },
                    { type: 'row', style: { gap: '6px' }, content: [
                        { type: 'button', text: 'Sync Static Core', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(126,200,160,0.1)', border: '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.3)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() { await syncSpeciesLorebookEntry(captSpId); } },
                        { type: 'button', text: 'Unlink', style: { margin: 0, fontSize: '11px' }, callback: async function() { await unlinkSpeciesLorebookEntry(captSpId); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
                    ] }
                );
            } else {
                spLbContent.push(
                    { type: 'text', text: "No lorebook entry. Create one to inject this species' profile.", style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } },
                    { type: 'button', text: '+ Create & Link Lorebook Entry', style: { width: '100%', marginBottom: '8px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(136,204,255,0.1)', border: '1px solid rgba(136,204,255,0.3)' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() { await createLinkedSpeciesLorebookEntry(captSpId); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
                );
            }
            spLbContent.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Trigger Keys:', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginTop: '12px', marginBottom: '4px' } });
            (sp.lorebookTags || []).forEach(function(tg, ti) {
                spLbContent.push({ type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '4px', marginBottom: '2px' }, content: [
                    { type: 'text', text: '• ' + tg, style: { flex: '1 1 0', fontSize: '12px' } },
                    { type: 'button', iconId: 'x', style: { margin: 0, fontSize: '10px' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.species[captSi].lorebookTags!.splice(ti, 1); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
                ] } as UIPart);
            });
            spLbContent.push({ type: 'row', style: { gap: '4px' }, content: [
                { type: 'textInput', placeholder: 'Add key...', storageKey: `temp:${tmpSpKey}`, style: { flex: '1 1 0' } },
                { type: 'button', iconId: 'plus', style: { margin: 0 }, callback: async function() { const v = await api.v1.tempStorage.get(tmpSpKey) as string || ''; if (!v.trim()) return; await mutConfig(function(c) { if (!c.species[captSi].lorebookTags) c.species[captSi].lorebookTags = []; if (!c.species[captSi].lorebookTags!.includes(v.trim())) c.species[captSi].lorebookTags!.push(v.trim()); }); await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(tmpSpKey); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
            ] });

            spParts.push({ type: 'box', style: { padding: '8px', marginBottom: '6px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', borderRadius: '4px', borderLeft: '3px solid #88ccff' }, content: [
                { type: 'textInput', label: 'Name', initialValue: sp.name || '', onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.species[captSi].name = v; }); } },
                { type: 'multilineTextInput', label: 'Description', initialValue: sp.description || '', style: { minHeight: '40px', marginTop: '4px' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.species[captSi].description = v; }); } },
                { type: 'box', style: { borderTop: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1)', marginTop: '12px', paddingTop: '12px' }, content: spLbContent },
                { type: 'button', text: 'Delete Species', style: { marginTop: '8px', fontSize: '11px', color: '#ff6b6b', backgroundColor: 'transparent', border: '1px solid rgba(255,107,107,0.3)' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { const di = (c.species || []).findIndex(function(x: any) { return x.id === captSpId; }); if (di >= 0) c.species.splice(di, 1); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
            ] } as UIPart);
        }
        if ((cfg.species || []).length === 0) spParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'No species defined.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.5', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        spParts.push({ type: 'button', text: '+ Add Species', style: { marginTop: '4px', width: '100%', fontSize: '11px' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { if (!c.species) c.species = []; c.species.push({ id: 'sp_' + Date.now(), name: 'New Species', description: '', lorebookTags: [] }); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } });
        return spParts;
    })() });

    // 4. Relationship Archetypes
    function buildPresetSection(title: string, items: string[], skBase: string, addKey: string, onAdd: (v: string, c: GlobalConfig) => void, onRemove: (i: number, c: GlobalConfig) => void): UIPart {
        const rows: UIPart[] = items.filter(Boolean).map(function(item, i) {
            const pi = i;
            return { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '4px', marginBottom: '2px' }, content: [
                { type: 'text', text: item, style: { flex: '1 1 0', fontSize: '12px' } },
                { type: 'button', iconId: 'x', style: { margin: 0 }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c){ onRemove(pi, c); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
            ] } as UIPart;
        });
        rows.push({ type: 'row', style: { gap: '4px', marginTop: '4px' }, content: [
            { type: 'textInput', placeholder: `Add ${title}…`, storageKey: `temp:${addKey}`, style: { flex: '1 1 0' } },
            { type: 'button', iconId: 'plus', style: { margin: 0 }, callback: async function() { const v = await api.v1.tempStorage.get(addKey) as string || ''; if (!v.trim()) return; await mutConfig(function(c){ onAdd(v.trim(), c); }); await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(addKey); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
        ] } as UIPart);
        return { type: 'collapsibleSection', title, initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: `temp:${skBase}`, content: rows };
    }
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: `💞 Relationship Archetypes (${(cfg.relationshipArchetypes || []).length})`, initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:wb_cfg_rels', content: (function() {
        const archParts: UIPart[] = [];
        archParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Each archetype has an ordered stage progression (cold → warm). Actions can set the archetype and advance/retreat the stage. Leave stages blank for a label-only archetype.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        (cfg.relationshipArchetypes || []).forEach(function(def, i) {
            const ai = i;
            archParts.push({ type: 'box', style: { padding: '8px', marginBottom: '6px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', borderRadius: '4px', borderLeft: '3px solid #d88fc0' }, content: [
                { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '4px' }, content: [
                    { type: 'textInput', initialValue: def.name, placeholder: 'Archetype name', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '12px', fontWeight: 'bold' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.relationshipArchetypes[ai].name = v; }); } },
                    { type: 'button', iconId: 'trash-2', style: { margin: 0, padding: '2px', color: '#ff6b6b' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.relationshipArchetypes.splice(ai, 1); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
                ] },
                { type: 'text', text: 'Stages (cold \u2192 warm):', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '2px' } },
                aeRenderIndexedStatesEditor(def.stages || [], async function(next) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.relationshipArchetypes[ai].stages = next; }); }, refreshWorldbuildingModal, 'Stage')
            ] } as UIPart);
        });
        if (!(cfg.relationshipArchetypes || []).length) archParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'No archetypes defined.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.5', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        archParts.push({ type: 'button', text: '+ Add Archetype', style: { width: '100%', fontSize: '11px', margin: 0 }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.relationshipArchetypes.push({ id: 'arch_' + Date.now(), name: 'New Archetype', stages: ['Stage 1', 'Stage 2', 'Stage 3'] }); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } });
        return archParts;
    })() });

    // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] Same shape/pattern as Relationship Archetypes above,
    // deliberately — but a character can have MULTIPLE active at once (checked, not
    // picked), and this feeds the per-character dynamic lorebook entry, not the AN.
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: `🩺 Conditions (${(cfg.conditions || []).length})`, initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:wb_cfg_conditions', content: (function() {
        const condParts: UIPart[] = [];
        condParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Each condition has an optional ordered severity progression (mild → severe) — leave stages blank for a pure on/off condition. Multiple conditions can be active on a character at once. Injects into a temporary per-character lorebook entry while active, not the Author\u2019s Note.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        (cfg.conditions || []).forEach(function(def, i) {
            const ci = i;
            condParts.push({ type: 'box', style: { padding: '8px', marginBottom: '6px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', borderRadius: '4px', borderLeft: '3px solid #e8a87c' }, content: [
                { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '4px' }, content: [
                    { type: 'textInput', initialValue: def.name, placeholder: 'Condition name', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '12px', fontWeight: 'bold' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.conditions[ci].name = v; }); } },
                    { type: 'button', iconId: 'trash-2', style: { margin: 0, padding: '2px', color: '#ff6b6b' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.conditions.splice(ci, 1); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } }
                ] },
                { type: 'text', text: 'Stages (mild \u2192 severe, blank = on/off only):', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '2px' } },
                aeRenderIndexedStatesEditor(def.stages || [], async function(next) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.conditions[ci].stages = next; }); }, refreshWorldbuildingModal, 'Stage')
            ] } as UIPart);
        });
        if (!(cfg.conditions || []).length) condParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'No conditions defined.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.5', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        condParts.push({ type: 'button', text: '+ Add Condition', style: { width: '100%', fontSize: '11px', margin: 0, marginBottom: '10px' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.conditions.push({ id: 'cond_' + Date.now(), name: 'New Condition', stages: [] }); }); await refreshWorldbuildingModal(); } });

        // ── Narrative template + live preview (sample data) ──────────────────
        condParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Lorebook line template — how each active condition renders.', style: { fontSize: '11px', color: '#88ccff', marginTop: '4px' } });
        condParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Macros: |name|  |conditionName|  |stageText|', style: { fontSize: '10px', opacity: '0.5', marginBottom: '4px', fontFamily: 'monospace' } });
        condParts.push({ type: 'multilineTextInput', initialValue: cfg.lorebookTemplates.condition, placeholder: 'Enter template...', style: { fontFamily: 'monospace', fontSize: '11px', minHeight: '40px' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { c.lorebookTemplates.condition = v; }); } });
        const _condSample = (cfg.conditions || [])[0] || { name: 'Intoxicated', stages: ['Tipsy, giggly, and warm to the touch.'] };
        const _condPreview = parseMacroTemplate(cfg.lorebookTemplates.condition, { name: 'Aria', conditionName: _condSample.name, stageText: (_condSample.stages || [])[0] || '' });
        condParts.push({ type: 'text', text: `Preview: ${_condPreview || '(Blank)'}`, style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: 0.7, fontStyle: 'italic' } });

        return condParts;
    })() });

    // [v2.9.0 LOREFOLD] Lorebook Additions — which dynamic character fields fold into
    // that character's own lorebook entry each generation, and how each renders.
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: '📖 Lorebook Additions', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:wb_cfg_lorefold', content: (function() {
        const lfParts: UIPart[] = [];
        lfParts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'These fold into each scene-present character\u2019s lorebook entry (not the Author\u2019s Note \u2014 thoughts and the NPC web live there). Uncheck any that feel excessive; the underlying character data is kept, just not injected. Each enabled line renders as prose via its template.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } });
        const lf: any = cfg.lorebookFields || {};
        const lt: any = cfg.lorebookTemplates || {};
        const LF_FIELDS = [
            { key: 'equipment',     label: 'Equipment',               macros: '|name|  |equipList|',                                              sample: { name: 'Aria', equipList: 'a worn leather coat, an iron dagger' } },
            { key: 'relationship',  label: 'Relationship',            macros: '|name|  |relArchetype|  |primaryRelationship.targetName|',           sample: { name: 'Aria', relArchetype: 'Estranged Sister', primaryRelationship: { targetName: 'the protagonist' } } },
            { key: 'relTrajectory', label: 'Relationship trajectory', macros: '|name|  |primaryRelationship.targetName|  |primaryRelationship.trajectory|', sample: { name: 'Aria', primaryRelationship: { targetName: 'the protagonist', trajectory: 'thawing under an apology neither has spoken aloud' } } },
            { key: 'relStatus',     label: 'Relationship status',     macros: '|primaryRelationship.currentStatus|',                                sample: { name: 'Aria', primaryRelationship: { targetName: 'the protagonist', currentStatus: 'Aria and the protagonist trade clipped, formal sentences and never sit at the same table.' } } },
            { key: 'goal',          label: 'Goal / desire',           macros: '|name|  |midRange.currentDesire|',                                   sample: { name: 'Aria', midRange: { currentDesire: 'to clear her name before the tribunal convenes' } } },
            { key: 'socialMask',    label: 'Social mask',             macros: '|name|  |midRange.socialMask|',                                      sample: { name: 'Aria', midRange: { socialMask: 'someone unbothered, while privately mapping every exit' } } },
            { key: 'shiftedView',   label: 'Shifted view',            macros: '|name|  |midRange.shiftedView|',                                     sample: { name: 'Aria', midRange: { shiftedView: 'the people who raised her have been lying to her since the start' } } },
            { key: 'journal',       label: 'Journal',                 macros: '|name|  |journalBody|',                                              sample: { name: 'Aria', journalBody: 'Found the letter behind the loose brick. Said nothing to the others yet.' } }
        ];
        LF_FIELDS.forEach(function(fd) {
            const fk = fd.key;
            lfParts.push({ type: 'box', style: { padding: '8px', marginBottom: '6px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', borderRadius: '4px', borderLeft: '3px solid #7ec8a0' }, content: [
                { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '8px', marginBottom: '4px' }, content: [
                    { type: 'checkboxInput', initialValue: lf[fk] !== false, onChange: async function(v: boolean) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c.lorebookFields as any)[fk] = !!v; }); } },
                    { type: 'text', text: fd.label, style: { fontSize: '12px', fontWeight: 'bold', flex: '1 1 0' } }
                ] },
                { type: 'text', text: 'Macros: ' + fd.macros, style: { fontSize: '10px', opacity: '0.5', marginBottom: '4px', fontFamily: 'monospace' } },
                { type: 'multilineTextInput', initialValue: (lt[fk] || ''), placeholder: 'Enter template...', style: { fontFamily: 'monospace', fontSize: '11px', minHeight: '36px' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c.lorebookTemplates as any)[fk] = v; }); } },
                { type: 'text', text: 'Preview: ' + (parseMacroTemplate(lt[fk] || '', fd.sample) || '(Blank)'), style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: 0.7, fontStyle: 'italic', marginTop: '2px' } }
            ] } as UIPart);
        });
        // Conditions toggle (its template + defs live in the Conditions section above).
        lfParts.push({ type: 'box', style: { padding: '8px', marginBottom: '6px', backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', borderRadius: '4px', borderLeft: '3px solid #e8a87c' }, content: [
            { type: 'row', alignment: 'center', style: { gap: '8px', marginBottom: '2px' }, content: [
                { type: 'checkboxInput', initialValue: lf.conditions !== false, onChange: async function(v: boolean) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c.lorebookFields as any).conditions = !!v; }); } },
                { type: 'text', text: 'Conditions', style: { fontSize: '12px', fontWeight: 'bold', flex: '1 1 0' } }
            ] },
            { type: 'text', text: 'Template + definitions live in the \ud83e\ude7a Conditions section above.', style: { fontSize: '10px', opacity: '0.5' } }
        ] } as UIPart);
        return lfParts;
    })() });
    
        return parts.filter(Boolean) as UIPart[];
}

// ============================================================================
// [v2.20.0 GENDIAG] GENERATION DIAGNOSTICS — shared UI builder
// ============================================================================
// Shared content builder for the Generation Diagnostics viewer, backed by the
// [DIAG GEN] ring buffer (_diagGenLog / diagGenRecord / aeDiagGenerate) near the
// top of the file. Two entry points render this SAME content so they never drift:
//   1. Config modal → "🩺 Generation Diagnostics" collapsible section (always available).
//   2. A quick-access button next to "Generate All From Concept Dump" in the character
//      editor's Static tab (openGenDiagModal), for review right where a failure was hit.
// refreshFn lets each host redraw itself (config modal body vs. this standalone modal)
// after Refresh/Copy/Clear/Probe without the two entry points needing to know about
// each other.
async function buildGenDiagContent(refreshFn: () => Promise<void>): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    const parts: UIPart[] = [];
    const errCount = _diagGenLog.filter(function(e) { return e.outcome === 'error'; }).length;
    parts.push({ type: 'text', text: 'Every engine generation call is logged here as it happens \u2014 both the budget-free path (World-Build Wizard, "Generate All From Concept Dump") and the budgeted per-field / dynamic-engine path (ephemeral, mid-range, relationship, hooks, image prompts). A thrown error (including a transient AbortError from NAI\u2019s own pipeline) now gets one automatic retry before the call is reported failed. Session-only \u2014 resets on script reload. Also mirrored to the console (visible in eruda) as it happens, and to /gendiag (or /gendiagclear) in any wizard chat.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px', lineHeight: '1.5' } });
    parts.push({ type: 'text', text: _diagGenLog.length + ' call' + (_diagGenLog.length === 1 ? '' : 's') + ' logged this session \u00b7 ' + errCount + ' error' + (errCount === 1 ? '' : 's'), style: { fontSize: '11px', fontWeight: 'bold', marginBottom: '6px', color: (errCount > 0 ? '#ff8080' : '#7fd88f') } } as UIPart);

    parts.push({ type: 'row', style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '6px' }, content: [
        { type: 'button', text: 'Refresh', iconId: 'refresh-cw', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px' }, callback: async function() { await refreshFn(); } },
        { type: 'button', text: 'Copy Log', iconId: 'copy', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px' }, callback: async function() {
            try {
                const granted = await api.v1.permissions.request('clipboardWrite', 'Copy the generation diagnostics log to your clipboard.');
                if (!granted) { api.v1.ui.toast('Clipboard permission denied.', { type: 'warning' }); return; }
                await api.v1.clipboard.writeText(dumpGenDiagLog());
                api.v1.ui.toast('Copied to clipboard.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 });
            } catch (eGdCopy) { api.v1.ui.toast('Copy failed: ' + String((eGdCopy && (eGdCopy as any).message) || eGdCopy), { type: 'error' }); }
        } },
        { type: 'button', text: 'Clear', iconId: 'trash-2', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', color: '#ff6b6b' }, callback: async function() { clearGenDiagLog(); await refreshFn(); } }
    ] } as UIPart);

    parts.push({ type: 'button', text: 'Run A/B Probe (yoink vs direct)', iconId: 'activity', style: { width: '100%', margin: '0 0 8px', fontSize: '11px' }, disabledWhileCallbackRunning: true, callback: async function() {
        api.v1.ui.toast('Running probe\u2026', { id: 'ce-genprobe', autoClose: false, type: 'info' });
        await runBudgetProbe();
        api.v1.ui.toast('Probe complete \u2014 see log below.', { id: 'ce-genprobe', type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 });
        await refreshFn();
    } } as UIPart);

    parts.push({ type: 'box', style: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', border: '1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.1)', padding: '8px', borderRadius: '6px', maxHeight: '320px', overflowY: 'auto' }, content: [
        { type: 'text', text: dumpGenDiagLog(), style: { fontSize: '10px', fontFamily: 'monospace', whiteSpace: 'pre-wrap', opacity: '0.85' } }
    ] } as UIPart);

    return parts;
}

// Quick-access standalone modal — same content as the Config modal section, opened
// from a button next to "Generate All From Concept Dump" (buildCharStaticTab) so a
// failure can be reviewed without leaving the character editor.
var genDiagModalHandle: any = null;
async function openGenDiagModal(): Promise<void> {
    if (genDiagModalHandle && !genDiagModalHandle.isClosed()) { genDiagModalHandle.close(); }
    async function refreshGenDiagModal(): Promise<void> {
        if (genDiagModalHandle && !genDiagModalHandle.isClosed()) {
            await genDiagModalHandle.update({ content: await buildGenDiagContent(refreshGenDiagModal) });
        }
    }
    genDiagModalHandle = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ce-gendiag', title: '\ud83e\ude7a Generation Diagnostics', size: 'large', content: await buildGenDiagContent(refreshGenDiagModal) });
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 24 — CONFIG MODAL (Engine Tweaks Only)
// ============================================================================

async function mutConfig(fn: (c: GlobalConfig) => void): Promise<void> {
    const c = await loadConfig(); fn(c); await saveConfig(c);
}
async function refreshConfigModal(): Promise<void> {
    if (configModalHandle && !configModalHandle.isClosed()) {
        await configModalHandle.update({ content: await buildConfigContent() });
        return;
    }
    // [CE-STRIP v2.11.0] System Settings now renders inside the merged Settings
    // workspace (buildSettingsWorkspace) instead of its own standalone modal —
    // refresh whichever workspace is currently open instead.
    await refreshWorkspace();
}
async function openConfigModal(): Promise<void> {
    if (configModalHandle && !configModalHandle.isClosed()) configModalHandle.close();
    configModalHandle = await openEngineModal({ id: 'ae-config', size: 'full', fillWidth: true, title: '⚙ Adventure Engine Settings', content: await buildConfigContent() });
}

async function buildConfigContent(): Promise<UIPart[]> {
    const cfg = await loadConfig(); const parts: UIPart[] = [];

    // 1. Engine Timing
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: '⚡️Dynamic Updates Timing', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ce_cfg_timing', content: (function() {
        // [v2.54.0] Cadence unit toggle, per user. Default 'gens' (unchanged behaviour for
        // every existing save — the migration only ever fills a missing field, never flips
        // an explicit one). Switching modes does not reset any progress: both stamp units
        // are written on every dynamic update regardless of which mode is active (see
        // runDynamicEngine / _persistEphemeralSnapshot / generateMidRange / generateRelation-
        // shipUpdate / generateHookUpdates), so toggling back and forth never causes a
        // spurious immediate fire the way discarding one unit's progress would.
        let _cadMode = ((cfg as any).dynamicIntervalMode === 'tokens') ? 'tokens' : 'gens';
        let _isTok = _cadMode === 'tokens';
        let _content: UIPart[] = [
            { type: 'text', text: 'How much story progress must pass before each update type fires \u2014 measured in generations (replies) or in context tokens.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } },
            { type: 'row', style: { gap: '6px', marginBottom: '10px' }, content: [
                { type: 'button', text: 'Per Generation', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', backgroundColor: !_isTok ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.2)' : undefined, color: !_isTok ? '#7ec8a0' : undefined, border: !_isTok ? '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.4)' : undefined }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).dynamicIntervalMode = 'gens'; }); await refreshConfigModal(); } } as UIPart,
                { type: 'button', text: 'Per Tokens', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, fontSize: '11px', backgroundColor: _isTok ? 'rgba(126,200,160,0.2)' : undefined, color: _isTok ? '#7ec8a0' : undefined, border: _isTok ? '1px solid rgba(126,200,160,0.4)' : undefined }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).dynamicIntervalMode = 'tokens'; }); await refreshConfigModal(); } } as UIPart
            ] }
        ];
        if (!_isTok) {
            _content.push(
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Ephemeral (Thoughts) Interval', min: 1, max: 20, step: 1, initialValue: cfg.thoughtIntervalGens, suffix: ' gens', defaultValue: 2, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.thoughtIntervalGens = v; }); } },
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Mid-Range State Interval', min: 1, max: 40, step: 1, initialValue: cfg.dynamicIntervalGens, suffix: ' gens', defaultValue: 4, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.dynamicIntervalGens = v; }); } },
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Primary Relationship Interval', min: 2, max: 50, step: 1, initialValue: cfg.arcIntervalGens, suffix: ' gens', defaultValue: 10, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.arcIntervalGens = v; }); } },
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Hook Scan Interval', min: 1, max: 30, step: 1, initialValue: cfg.hookIntervalGens, suffix: ' gens', defaultValue: 6, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.hookIntervalGens = v; }); } }
            );
        } else {
            _content.push(
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Ephemeral (Thoughts) Interval', min: 100, max: 5000, step: 50, initialValue: Number((cfg as any).thoughtIntervalTokens) || 500, suffix: ' tok', defaultValue: 500, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).thoughtIntervalTokens = v; }); } },
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Mid-Range State Interval', min: 100, max: 10000, step: 50, initialValue: Number((cfg as any).dynamicIntervalTokens) || 1000, suffix: ' tok', defaultValue: 1000, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).dynamicIntervalTokens = v; }); } },
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Primary Relationship Interval', min: 250, max: 20000, step: 50, initialValue: Number((cfg as any).arcIntervalTokens) || 2500, suffix: ' tok', defaultValue: 2500, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).arcIntervalTokens = v; }); } },
                { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Hook Scan Interval', min: 100, max: 15000, step: 50, initialValue: Number((cfg as any).hookIntervalTokens) || 1500, suffix: ' tok', defaultValue: 1500, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).hookIntervalTokens = v; }); } }
            );
        }
        _content.push(
            { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Max Ephemeral Injections per Generation', min: 1, max: 6, step: 1, initialValue: cfg.ephemeralCap, defaultValue: 3, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.ephemeralCap = v; }); } } as UIPart,
            { type: 'text', text: 'Dynamic character cap: how many present characters stay dynamic at once (0 = no cap). Ranked characters fill slots first by rank, then unranked present characters. Set each character\u2019s rank from their card or edit panel.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginTop: '10px', marginBottom: '4px' } } as UIPart,
            { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Max Dynamic Characters', min: 0, max: 20, step: 1, initialValue: Number(cfg.maxDynamicCharacters) || 0, suffix: ' (0 = off)', defaultValue: 0, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ (c as any).maxDynamicCharacters = Math.max(0, Math.floor(v || 0)); }); } } as UIPart
        );
        return _content;
    })() });


    // 2. Generation Style
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: '✍ Generation Style', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ce_cfg_style', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: 'Injected at the top of every generation prompt. Controls overall tone and register. Tip: add "Ignore any game mechanic formatting in the story text — numbered choice lists, > action markers, ## headers, {} or () notes, LOREBOOK: references. Treat only prose narrative as story content."', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px' } },
        { type: 'multilineTextInput', initialValue: cfg.generalStyle, style: { minHeight: '100px' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.generalStyle = v; }); } },
        { type: 'button', text: 'Reset to Default', style: { fontSize: '11px', marginTop: '6px' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.generalStyle = DEFAULT_GENERAL_STYLE; }); await refreshConfigModal(); } }
    ] });

    // 3. Static Field Settings
    const fieldSections: UIPart[] = [
        { type: 'checkboxInput', label: 'Show field headers in context injection', initialValue: cfg.showFieldHeaders, onChange: async function(v: boolean) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.showFieldHeaders = v; }); } } as UIPart
    ];
    STATIC_FIELD_DEFS.forEach(function(def) {
        fieldSections.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: cfg.fieldLabels[def.key] || def.label, initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: `temp:ce_cfg_f_${def.key}`, content: [
            { type: 'textInput', label: 'Display Label', initialValue: cfg.fieldLabels[def.key] || def.label, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.fieldLabels[def.key] = v; }); } },
            { type: 'multilineTextInput', label: 'Generation Instructions', initialValue: cfg.fieldInstructions[def.key] || def.defaultInstructions, style: { minHeight: '80px' }, onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.fieldInstructions[def.key] = v; }); } },
            { type: 'sliderInput', label: 'Generation Token Budget', min: 50, max: 500, step: 25, initialValue: cfg.fieldBudgets[def.key] || def.defaultBudget, suffix: ' tokens', defaultValue: def.defaultBudget, onChange: async function(v: number) { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.fieldBudgets[def.key] = v; }); } },
            { type: 'button', text: 'Reset Instructions', style: { fontSize: '11px', marginTop: '4px' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c){ c.fieldInstructions[def.key] = def.defaultInstructions; c.fieldBudgets[def.key] = def.defaultBudget; }); await refreshConfigModal(); } }
        ] } as UIPart);
    });
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: '📝 Static Field Settings', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ce_cfg_fields', content: fieldSections });

    // 4. Scene Default Background
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: '🖼 Scene Default Background', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ce_cfg_bg', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: 'URL of the fallback image shown in the scene banner when a location has no portrait. Stored in the module config — anyone who loads this story sees the same background. Leave blank for the CSS gradient fallback.', style: { fontSize: '11px', opacity: '0.6', marginBottom: '8px', lineHeight: '1.5' } },
        { type: 'textInput', label: 'Image URL', initialValue: (cfg as any).defaultBackgroundUrl || '', onChange: async function(v: string) { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c as any).defaultBackgroundUrl = v.trim(); }); } },
        { type: 'button', text: '✕ Clear', style: { fontSize: '11px', marginTop: '4px', color: 'var(--ae-color-danger)' }, callback: async function() { await mutConfig(function(c) { (c as any).defaultBackgroundUrl = ''; }); await refreshConfigModal(); api.v1.ui.toast('Scene background cleared.', { type: 'success', autoClose: 2000 }); } }
    ] } as any);

    // 5. Generation Diagnostics [v2.20.0 GENDIAG]
    parts.push({ type: 'collapsibleSection', title: '\ud83e\ude7a Generation Diagnostics', initialCollapsed: true, storageKey: 'temp:ce_cfg_gendiag', iconId: 'activity', content: await buildGenDiagContent(refreshConfigModal) });

    parts.push({ type: 'text', text: ' ', style: { marginBottom: '48px' } });
    return parts.filter(Boolean) as UIPart[];
}

async function confirmDeleteChar(id: string, name: string): Promise<void> {
    const m = await openEngineModal({ title: 'Confirm Delete', size: 'small', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: `Delete "${name}"? This cannot be undone.`, style: { marginBottom: '16px' } },
        { type: 'row', style: { gap: '8px' }, content: [
            { type: 'button', text: 'Delete', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0, color: '#ff6b6b' }, callback: async function() { await deleteCharacter(id); m.close(); await refreshSidebar(); try { await broadcastCharacterData(); await broadcastSceneState(); } catch (_) {} } },
            { type: 'button', text: 'Cancel', style: { flex: '1 1 0', margin: 0 }, callback: function() { m.close(); } }
        ] }
    ] });
}



// ============================================================================
// SMART IMPORTER
// ============================================================================
// importPayload(data) — accepts a parsed JSON object and imports whatever
// recognizable blocks it finds. Returns a summary of what was imported.
// All import entry-points (module button, config modal, new-story prompt)
// funnel through this single function so behaviour is always identical.
//
// Recognised top-level blocks (any combination, all optional):
//   config          — GlobalConfig object  → [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] merged field-by-
//                                            field over the CURRENT config, not the
//                                            engine defaults. Every id-keyed array
//                                            (actionTemplates, factions, species,
//                                            resources, characterResourceDefs,
//                                            relationshipArchetypes, factionResourceDefs,
//                                            stakeDefs, npcArchetypes, itemArchetypes,
//                                            customStaticFields, sheetCategories,
//                                            wizardSections) is upserted by `id` —
//                                            matched items are updated in place, new
//                                            ones appended, anything not mentioned in
//                                            the import is left alone. tierTravelDefaults
//                                            upserts by `templateId`. fieldLabels /
//                                            fieldInstructions / fieldBudgets do a
//                                            shallow key merge. Everything else
//                                            (scalars, contextTemplates, holdingDef,
//                                            time settings) only overwrites when the
//                                            key is actually present in the import, so
//                                            a hand-trimmed partial config (e.g. just
//                                            2 new action templates) merges safely
//                                            instead of resetting the rest to defaults.
//                                            totalGenerations is never imported — it's
//                                            live per-story runtime state.
//   characters      — CharacterRecord[]    → merged into character index
//   character       — CharacterRecord      → single character (not bundle)
//   entities        — EntityRecord[]       → merged into entity index
//   locations       — EntityRecord[]       → backwards-compat alias for entities
//   entity          — EntityRecord         → single entity node
//   npcWeb          — NpcWebEntry[]        → [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] upserted by `id`,
//                                            same as config arrays (was: full replace)
//   partyState      — PartyState           → [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] resourceValues is
//                                            a shallow key merge (was: full replace)
//
// Special typed wrappers (detected first, return early):
//   { type: 'character_bundle', character, entity? }
//
// Bare root-level records (no top-level array key):
//   { id, name, ...CharacterRecord }  — no `type` field  → single character
//   { id, name, type, ... }           — has `type` field → single entity
// ============================================================================

// ImportResult interface defined in Section 1 (types block).

// [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] Generic upsert-by-key merge for GlobalConfig's id-keyed
// arrays — the config-level analogue of what saveCharacter/saveEntity already do
// per-record. Matched items (by keyFn) are replaced in place with the incoming
// version; unmatched incoming items are appended; existing items absent from the
// incoming array are left untouched. Returns the merged array plus tallies so
// callers can report what actually changed.
function mergeArrayById(existing: any[] | undefined, incoming: any, keyFn: (item: any) => string | undefined): { merged: any[]; counts: ImportMergeCounts } {
    const base: any[] = Array.isArray(existing) ? existing.slice() : [];
    const counts: ImportMergeCounts = { added: 0, updated: 0 };
    if (!Array.isArray(incoming)) return { merged: base, counts: counts };
    for (const item of incoming.filter(Boolean)) {
        const key = keyFn(item);
        if (key === undefined || key === null || key === '') continue;
        const idx = base.findIndex(function(x) { return keyFn(x) === key; });
        if (idx >= 0) { base[idx] = item; counts.updated++; }
        else { base.push(item); counts.added++; }
    }
    return { merged: base, counts: counts };
}

// [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] Shallow key-merge for Record<string, any> config fields
// (fieldLabels, fieldInstructions, fieldBudgets, partyState.resourceValues) —
// incoming keys overwrite matching existing keys; every other key is preserved.
function mergeRecordShallow(existing: any, incoming: any): any {
    const base: any = (existing && typeof existing === 'object') ? { ...existing } : {};
    if (incoming && typeof incoming === 'object' && !Array.isArray(incoming)) {
        for (const k of Object.keys(incoming)) base[k] = incoming[k];
    }
    return base;
}

// [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] Builds the merged config that replaces `existing`, plus a
// per-category count summary. Deliberately starts from `existing` (the CURRENT
// story's config, already migrated) rather than a fresh default config — a partial
// import that only sets a few fields must never reset the rest to engine defaults.
function mergeConfigForImport(existing: GlobalConfig, incoming: any): { cfg: GlobalConfig; counts: { [category: string]: ImportMergeCounts } } {
    const merged: any = { ...existing };
    const counts: { [category: string]: ImportMergeCounts } = {};
    const byId = function(x: any) { return x && x.id; };

    const idArrayFields: string[] = [
        'sheetCategories', 'customStaticFields', 'relationshipArchetypes', 'resources',
        'characterResourceDefs', 'species', 'factions', 'factionResourceDefs',
        'stakeDefs', 'actionTemplates', 'wizardSections', 'conditions'
        // [v2.7.0 CONDITIONS] 'conditions' added — same id-keyed upsert as relationshipArchetypes.
        // NOTE: resources/characterResourceDefs/factionResourceDefs/stakeDefs/actionTemplates
        // are all confirmed-dead fields from earlier CE-STRIP passes, still listed here —
        // that's user's own flagged separate to-do ("make sure import/export reflect current
        // state"), deliberately not touched in this pass.
    ];
    for (const field of idArrayFields) {
        if (incoming[field] === undefined) continue;
        const r = mergeArrayById((existing as any)[field], incoming[field], byId);
        merged[field] = r.merged;
        counts[field] = r.counts;
    }

    // tierTravelDefaults has no `id` — keyed by templateId (a small fixed set of
    // system travel templates: sys_default_travel, sys_travel_tier1, sys_travel_tier2).
    if (incoming.tierTravelDefaults !== undefined) {
        const r = mergeArrayById((existing as any).tierTravelDefaults, incoming.tierTravelDefaults, function(x: any) { return x && x.templateId; });
        merged.tierTravelDefaults = r.merged;
        counts['tierTravelDefaults'] = r.counts;
    }

    // Record<string,*> fields — shallow key merge rather than whole-object replace.
    if (incoming.fieldLabels !== undefined)       merged.fieldLabels       = mergeRecordShallow(existing.fieldLabels, incoming.fieldLabels);
    if (incoming.fieldInstructions !== undefined) merged.fieldInstructions = mergeRecordShallow(existing.fieldInstructions, incoming.fieldInstructions);
    if (incoming.fieldBudgets !== undefined)      merged.fieldBudgets      = mergeRecordShallow(existing.fieldBudgets, incoming.fieldBudgets);

    // factionResourceSections is an ordered string[], not id-keyed — union, existing
    // order first, then append any section names the import introduces.
    if (Array.isArray(incoming.factionResourceSections)) {
        const cur: string[] = Array.isArray(existing.factionResourceSections) ? existing.factionResourceSections.slice() : [];
        for (const sec of incoming.factionResourceSections) { if (cur.indexOf(sec) < 0) cur.push(sec); }
        merged.factionResourceSections = cur;
    }

    // Everything else: scalar/object fields only overwrite when the import actually
    // sets them, so an import that never mentions (say) projectGenre can't blank it.
    const passthroughFields: string[] = [
        'contextTemplates', 'lorebookTemplates', 'lorebookFields', 'interfaceMode', 'thoughtIntervalGens', 'dynamicIntervalGens',
        'arcIntervalGens', 'hookIntervalGens', 'ephemeralCap', 'maxDynamicCharacters',
        'autoUpdateDefault', 'autoThoughtsDefault', 'showFieldHeaders', 'generalStyle',
        'defaultBackgroundUrl',
        'projectName', 'projectGenre', 'projectConcept'
    ];
    for (const field of passthroughFields) {
        if (incoming[field] !== undefined) merged[field] = incoming[field];
    }

    // totalGenerations is live per-story runtime state, never imported — always keep
    // whatever the destination story already has, regardless of what the import carries.
    merged.totalGenerations = existing.totalGenerations;

    return { cfg: merged as GlobalConfig, counts: counts };
}

// [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] Totals added/updated across every category in a
// configCategoryCounts map — used for the compact one-line import summary toast.
function sumMergeCounts(map: { [k: string]: ImportMergeCounts } | undefined): ImportMergeCounts {
    const out: ImportMergeCounts = { added: 0, updated: 0 };
    if (!map) return out;
    for (const k of Object.keys(map)) { out.added += map[k].added; out.updated += map[k].updated; }
    return out;
}

// ── Import Payload (full module importer) ────────────────────────────────────
async function importPayload(data: any): Promise<ImportResult> {
    const result: ImportResult = {
        charCount: 0, entityCount: 0,
        configImported: false, npcWebImported: false, partyStateImported: false
    };

    // 1. Config block — [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] merged field-by-field over the
    //    CURRENT config (id-keyed arrays upserted, everything else only touched
    //    when the import actually sets it) rather than replaced wholesale — see
    //    the Smart Importer comment block above for the full field-by-field rules.
    if (data.config && typeof data.config === 'object' && !Array.isArray(data.config)) {
        const priorCfg = await loadConfig();
        const mergeRes = mergeConfigForImport(priorCfg, data.config);
        const newCfg = migrateConfig(mergeRes.cfg);
        await saveConfig(newCfg);
        result.configImported = true;
        result.configCategoryCounts = mergeRes.counts;
    }

    const activeCfg = await loadConfig();

    // 2. Character bundle — typed single-export format (char + optional entity)
    //    Detected first and returns early so its keys don't fall through to the
    //    generic `character` / `entity` checks below.
    if (data.type === 'character_bundle' && data.character) {
        await saveCharacter(migrateCharacter(data.character, activeCfg));
        result.charCount = 1;
        if (data.entity && typeof data.entity === 'object') {
            await saveEntity(migrateEntity(data.entity));
            result.entityCount = 1;
        }
        return result;
    }

    // 3. Characters — array, singular block, or bare root record
    if (data.characters && Array.isArray(data.characters)) {
        for (const c of data.characters.filter(Boolean)) {
            await saveCharacter(migrateCharacter(c, activeCfg));
            result.charCount++;
        }
    } else if (data.character && typeof data.character === 'object' && !Array.isArray(data.character)) {
        await saveCharacter(migrateCharacter(data.character, activeCfg));
        result.charCount = 1;
    } else if (data.id && data.name && !data.type && !data.entities && !data.locations && !data.characters) {
        // Bare root-level CharacterRecord — no `type` field distinguishes it from entity
        await saveCharacter(migrateCharacter(data, activeCfg));
        result.charCount = 1;
    }

    // 4. Entities — array (with backwards-compat alias), singular block, or bare root record
    const rawNodes: any[] = Array.isArray(data.entities) ? data.entities
                          : Array.isArray(data.locations) ? data.locations   // pre-v0.9 exports
                          : [];
    if (rawNodes.length > 0) {
        for (const n of rawNodes.filter(Boolean)) {
            await saveEntity(migrateEntity(n));
            result.entityCount++;
        }
    } else if (data.entity && typeof data.entity === 'object' && !Array.isArray(data.entity)) {
        await saveEntity(migrateEntity(data.entity));
        result.entityCount = 1;
    } else if (data.id && data.name && data.type && !data.characters && !data.entities && !data.locations) {
        // Bare root-level EntityRecord — presence of `type` (entity kind) is the signal
        await saveEntity(migrateEntity(data));
        result.entityCount = 1;
    }

    // 5. NPC Web — [v1.20.0 IMPORT-MERGE] upserted by `id`, same as the config
    //    arrays (was: full replace, which silently dropped any web link the
    //    import didn't happen to include).
    if (data.npcWeb && Array.isArray(data.npcWeb)) {
        if (typeof loadNpcWeb === 'function' && typeof saveNpcWeb === 'function') {
            const priorWeb = await loadNpcWeb();
            const webRes = mergeArrayById(priorWeb, data.npcWeb, function(x: any) { return x && x.id; });
            await saveNpcWeb(webRes.merged);
            result.npcWebCounts = webRes.counts;
        }
        result.npcWebImported = true;
    }

    return result;
}

// ── Image / Architect Settings helpers ───────────────────────────────────────
// [NEW] Merged from auto-illustrator-sb engine. These functions were previously
//       in the AI engine's standalone modal. Now part of the AE config modal.

async function injectNote(type: string, isAdding: boolean): Promise<void> {
    // Inserts or removes the image System Note from Memory or Author's Note.
    try {
        const note = (globalSettings.systemNote || DEFAULT_NOTE).trim();
        if (!note) { api.v1.ui.toast('No System Note defined in Image Settings.'); return; }
        if (type === 'memory') {
            const text = (await api.v1.memory.get()) || '';
            if (isAdding && !text.includes(note)) {
                await api.v1.memory.set(text ? text + '\n\n' + note : note);
                api.v1.ui.toast('✅ Image note inserted into Memory.');
            } else if (!isAdding && text.includes(note)) {
                await api.v1.memory.set(text.replace('\n\n' + note, '').replace('\n' + note, '').replace(note, '').trim());
                api.v1.ui.toast('🗑 Image note removed from Memory.');
            } else {
                api.v1.ui.toast(isAdding ? 'Note already in Memory.' : 'Note not found in Memory.');
            }
        } else if (type === 'an') {
            const text = (await api.v1.an.get()) || '';
            if (isAdding && !text.includes(note)) {
                await api.v1.an.set(text ? text + '\n\n' + note : note);
                api.v1.ui.toast('✅ Image note inserted into Author\'s Note.');
            } else if (!isAdding && text.includes(note)) {
                await api.v1.an.set(text.replace('\n\n' + note, '').replace('\n' + note, '').replace(note, '').trim());
                api.v1.ui.toast('🗑 Image note removed from Author\'s Note.');
            } else {
                api.v1.ui.toast(isAdding ? 'Note already in Author\'s Note.' : 'Note not found.');
            }
        }
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('Injection failed — ensure storyEdit permission is granted.');
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// SECTION 26 — HOOK REGISTRATIONS
// All api.v1.hooks.register() calls.
//
//    ADVENTURE ENGINE PIPELINE:
//    onBeforeContextBuild  → world-state AN injection (AE context builder S13)
//    onLorebookEntrySelected → lore ID tracking
//    onContextBuilt        → imperative block + background drama + probability
//    onGenerationEnd       → token count + runDynamicEngine (S15)
//    onHistoryNavigated    → retry/undo skip flag
//
// ============================================================================

const AE_AN_SENTINEL = '[ Scene State ]';
// [v0.9.7.3 AN-FIX] Sentinel bracketing the wizard-owned region of the persisted AN.
// Final layout of the AN slot at generation time:
//   [user manual content, if any]
//   \n\n[ World Style ]          <- AE_WIZ_AN_SENTINEL (persisted by commitWizardMemory)
//   [wizard style/tone AN sections]
//   \n\n[ Scene State ]          <- AE_AN_SENTINEL (ephemeral, appended per-generation)
//   [quest line + world state + drama]
// Style first so the model frames itself before reading the immediate scene.
// Scene last so it carries the highest recency weight inside the AN slot.
const AE_WIZ_AN_SENTINEL = '[ World Style ]';

// [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] Hard switch: block any user/story-initiated generation while the
// wizard mode is on. Our own yoink call sets _aeYoink.active BEFORE it calls
// api.v1.editor.generate() (see executeYoinkGenerationAttempt), so by the time this
// hook fires for that call, _aeYoink.active is already true and it passes through.
api.v1.hooks.register('onGenerationRequested', async function(params: any) {
    // [FEATURE v2.51.4 ENGINE-THINKING-SIGNAL] Deliberately BEFORE the probe guard below --
    // "any of our thinking" per the user's own framing covers ephemeral/architect probes too,
    // not just user-visible story generation. If that turns out too noisy live (rapid flicker
    // across a busy scene's nested calls), move this after the guard instead.
    try { await _aeThinkingPulse(true); } catch (eEtS) { aeLog('[AE:engine-thinking] start pulse failed:', eEtS); }
    if (_diagProbeActive) return; // [DIAG] api.v1.generate() probe call — not a story generation

    // [v2.37.0 CHARCHAT-DOM-INJECT] v2.36.0's commit-on-Send block is REMOVED from this hook.
    // It appended the draft here on the reading that onGenerationRequested fires "before
    // context is built" and therefore in time. Live test falsified that: the model replied
    // "..." to what was effectively an empty prompt. Rather than chase whether the hook fired
    // or whether NAI snapshots the document before it, the design stopped needing to know --
    // the bridge now writes the turn into NAI's editor at pointerdown on NAI's Send, so the
    // turn is ordinary typed document text before NAI's own click handler even runs and this
    // hook has no job in the chat send path at all. See the CE-INDEX CHARCHAT-DOM-INJECT
    // entry and op 'povclaim' in handleCharChatAction.
    //
    // NOTHING replaces it here deliberately: the whole lesson of v2.36.0 is that this hook is
    // the wrong place to put the user's turn into the document. Do not add it back.

    if (_aeWizardMode && !_aeYoink.active) {
        api.v1.ui.toast('Wizard mode is on \u2014 close it to generate story text.', { type: 'warning', autoClose: 4000 });
        return { stopGeneration: true };
    }
});

api.v1.hooks.register('onBeforeContextBuild', async function(params: any) {

    // [v2.34.2 CHARCHAT-OVERRIDE-LEAK] Nested-generate guard. One of OUR OWN
    // api.v1.generate() calls is in flight (budget probe, generateEphemeral, the image
    // architect — aeDiagGenerate sets this for all of them now). api.v1.generate() fires
    // the full hook lifecycle, so without this the call inherits whatever override the
    // branches below apply and stops being the isolated prompt its caller built. That is
    // exactly what leaked buildCharChatInstruction's bracket block into the document and
    // broke the ephemeral/architect JSON parses. The old log line here fired on every such
    // call once the guard actually covered them all; the question it was asking
    // ("does api.v1.generate build story context?" — yes, it does) is answered and settled
    // in the CE-INDEX, so it is gone rather than left to spam the console every turn.
    if (_diagProbeActive) return;

    // [vNEXT] Yoink hijack active — leave the context alone; our prompt is swapped
    // in at onContextBuilt. Returning nothing means no world-state AN injection.
    if (_aeYoink.active) return;

    // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] In-document wizard chat turn. The story prefix (system prompt,
    // memory, lorebook) is left completely untouched — prefix caching must survive the
    // chat — and the Author's Note is deactivated (a tail-of-context change only), per
    // the design: story style directives must not steer chat replies. No world-state
    // injection, no scene lorebook activation. Applied on the dryRun pass too so the
    // cost estimate matches the real turn.
    // [v2.51.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-WRAP-T1] Wrap any still-unwrapped Director turn so the first
    // message is presented correctly instead of reaching the model raw for one turn.
    // [v2.58.0 WIZLIVE-CTX-STRIP + WIZLIVE-INSTRUCTION] Two structural fixes ported from
    // CharChat's hardened pipeline: (1) Director turns now have their "Director:" header
    // stripped in the model's view — the model sees plain instruction-brace text, removing
    // the prefix it was imitating (mirrors CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE v2.42.0). (2) A true-bottom
    // instruction (buildWizardChatInstruction) is appended to storyText, immediately before
    // the generation point — mirrors buildCharChatInstruction. These are the two structural
    // decisions CharChat received across v2.24.0-v2.51.0 that WIZLIVE had never gotten.
    if (_aeLiveWiz.active) {
        let wlResult: any = { authorsNote: { active: false, content: '' } };
        // [v2.58.0 WIZLIVE-INSTRUCTION + WIZLIVE-CTX-STRIP] Two changes to storyText:
        // (1) _wizliveCanonicalStoryOverride now strips the "Director:" header from the
        //     model's view (document keeps it), so the model sees plain instruction-brace
        //     text with no prefix to imitate — mirrors CHARCHAT-CTX-BRACE v2.42.0.
        // (2) buildWizardChatInstruction is appended at the TRUE BOTTOM of storyText,
        //     immediately before the generation point — mirrors buildCharChatInstruction.
        //     This is the structural fix for both "model writes as the Director" and
        //     "user input not recognised as instruction": the only reliable position for
        //     a steering directive is the last thing the model reads before generating.
        //     The protocol text (setup paragraph) is still valuable as user-facing
        //     framing, but the model's actual constraint now comes from here.
        try {
            let wlStory = await _wizliveCanonicalStoryOverride(params);
            let wlBase = (wlStory !== null) ? wlStory
                       : ((params && params.storyText && params.storyText.content) ? params.storyText.content : '');
            if (wlBase) {
                wlBase = wlBase.replace(/\s+$/, '');
                wlBase += '\n\n' + buildWizardChatInstruction();
                // [BUGFIX v2.70.0 WIZLIVE-USERTOKEN, mirrors CHARCHAT-USERTOKEN v2.69.0] Same root
                // cause, same fix. buildWizardChatInstruction's last line is a CLOSED bracket block
                // ('[ Write in pure prose... ]'), so the model reads a complete, well-formed thing at
                // the generation point and the likely next token is the chat-template end-of-turn
                // control token \u2014 a zero-length completion with a perfectly normal lifecycle
                // around it, indistinguishable from CharChat's bug until the user's own NAI
                // token-probability capture proved it there. End on a dangling speaker cue instead.
                // UNLIKE CharChat, no companion strip is needed: _wizliveNormalizeDocument already
                // never stamps a 'Wizard:' header on Wizard's replies (only Director turns get
                // header/brace treatment \u2014 see its own comment, "Wizard replies pass through
                // untouched"), and onResponse's WIZLIVE branch does no cleanup at all (early
                // return, raw passthrough). storyText is INPUT context only, never written to the
                // document \u2014 the model's own completion continues PAST 'Wizard:' rather than
                // regenerating it, so the written document stays exactly the header-less prose it
                // always was. Nothing downstream needed to change.
                if (!CE_DIAG.wlNoPrefill) {
                    wlBase += '\n\nWizard:';
                } else {
                    aeLog('[AE:diag] wlNoPrefill ON \u2014 context ends on a closed block (pre-v2.70.0 shape).');
                }
                wlResult.storyText = { active: true, content: wlBase };
            }
        } catch (eWl) { api.v1.error('[AE:wizlive] context override failed \u2014 story text left raw this turn:', eWl); }
        return wlResult;
    }

    // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] In-story character chat turn. The OPPOSITE trade to WIZLIVE
    // above: we take the whole prompt (system prompt, lorebook activation, Author's
    // Note, story text) rather than protecting the prefix cache. Story generation
    // cannot run while a chat is open, so every generation reaching this line IS a
    // chat turn — which is what makes applying the override unconditionally safe.
    // Applied on the dryRun pass too, deliberately: that makes NAI's own context
    // viewer the debugging window for the whole subsystem. See the CHARCHAT section.
    if (_aeCharChat.active) {
        // [DIAG CE-DIAG-FLAGS] ccRawContext — leave NAI's context completely untouched for
        // this chat turn. DIAGNOSTIC ONLY, not a real mode: no system prompt swap, no
        // lorebook suppression, no chat-region rebuild, no instruction tail. Isolates
        // whether the override itself (rather than anything downstream of it) is
        // implicated in the empty-reply bug.
        if (CE_DIAG.ccRawContext) { aeLog('[AE:diag] ccRawContext ON \u2014 context override skipped for this chat turn.'); return undefined; }
        return await runCharChatContextOverride(params);
    }

    // ── AE path: Authors Note world-state injection ──────────────────────────
    if (params.dryRun) return;
    diagBudgetSnapshot('STORY', (params && params.continuityId) || null);   // [DIAG] pre-read for a real story turn
    await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_LORE_IDS, '[]').catch(function() {});

    // [v0.9.8.5] Scene lorebook activation (static identity + ephemeral equipment).
    // Returned regardless of whether an AN block is produced below.
    let aeResult: any = {};
    try {
        const sceneAct = await buildSceneLorebookActivation(params.lorebookEntries);
        if (sceneAct.lorebookEntries.length > 0) aeResult.lorebookEntries = sceneAct.lorebookEntries;
        if (sceneAct.temporaryLorebookEntries.length > 0) aeResult.temporaryLorebookEntries = sceneAct.temporaryLorebookEntries;
    } catch (eAct) { api.v1.error('[AE] scene activation:', eAct); }

    try {
        // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] Quest line (buildTrackedNarrativeBlock) and Drama
        // block (buildDramaContextBlock) are gone — both functions deleted, both had
        // exactly one caller (here). buildWorldStateBlock alone is now the whole AN
        // body: per-character thoughts + critical hooks. anProtag is still resolved
        // and passed for signature back-compat only — see buildWorldStateBlock's own
        // header comment (pending protagonist pass).
        const anIdx = await loadIndex();
        const anProtagMeta = anIdx.find(function(m: any) { return m.isProtagonist; });
        const anProtag = anProtagMeta ? await loadCharacter(anProtagMeta.id) : null;
        const worldStateBlock = await buildWorldStateBlock(anProtag);

        const fullBlock = worldStateBlock.trim();
        if (fullBlock) {
            // [v0.9.38 AN-IDEMPOTENT] Strip any prior AE region before re-appending.
            // We always append our block LAST, sentinel-prefixed, so the sentinel (or
            // the '\n\n'+sentinel join that precedes it) marks the trailing AE region.
            // Removing it first means N hook passes / N engine copies converge on one copy.
            let existingANraw = (params.authorsNote && params.authorsNote.content) ? params.authorsNote.content : '';
            let anMarker = '\n\n' + AE_AN_SENTINEL;
            let anMarkerIdx = existingANraw.lastIndexOf(anMarker);
            let baseAN = (anMarkerIdx >= 0)
                ? existingANraw.substring(0, anMarkerIdx)
                : ((existingANraw.indexOf(AE_AN_SENTINEL) === 0) ? '' : existingANraw);
            baseAN = baseAN.replace(/\s+$/, '');
            let wrappedBlock = AE_AN_SENTINEL + '\n' + fullBlock;
            aeResult.authorsNote = {
                active: true,
                content: baseAN ? (baseAN + '\n\n' + wrappedBlock) : wrappedBlock
            };
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] Author\'s Note injection error:', e); }

    return Object.keys(aeResult).length > 0 ? aeResult : undefined;
});

api.v1.hooks.register('onLorebookEntrySelected', async function(params: any) {
    try {
        if (!params.entryId) return;
        const raw = await api.v1.tempStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_LORE_IDS, '[]') as string;
        const ids: string[] = JSON.parse(raw);
        if (!ids.includes(params.entryId)) {
            ids.push(params.entryId);
            await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_LORE_IDS, JSON.stringify(ids));
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] LoreTracking error:', e); }
});


api.v1.hooks.register('onResponse', async function(params: any) {
    // [DIAG] Probe guard — do not swallow; api.v1.generate returns text via its own value.
    if (_diagProbeActive) return;   // [v2.34.2] nested api.v1.generate() in flight — see onBeforeContextBuild's guard. Log removed: it now fires every ephemeral/architect call.
    // [vNEXT] While a yoink generation is active, buffer the streamed chunks and
    // swallow them so nothing reaches the user's document. Inert otherwise.
    // [v1.22.1] Discriminate by continuityId when we have one: a response belonging to a
    // DIFFERENT generation is either a story turn or a late chunk from a generation the
    // watchdog abandoned, and must not be swallowed into the wizard's buffer.
    //
    // FAIL-SAFE: if we never captured a continuityId (older NAI build, or the field is
    // absent), fall back to the pre-v1.22.1 behavior of swallowing while active. Leaking
    // wizard prose into the user's story is far worse than over-swallowing.
    // [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] Swallow ALL output unconditionally while wizard mode is on, and
    // for any zombie continuityId left over from a watchdog-abandoned generation, even
    // after mode has since exited. This replaces the old "leave someone else's text
    // untouched" branch — that was the exact gap that let mismatched/abandoned yoink
    // output leak into the story. Only genuinely-ours chunks get buffered; everything
    // else is swallowed silently rather than passed through.
    const ygZombie = !!(params && params.continuityId && _wizZombieIds[params.continuityId]);
    if (_aeYoink.active || _aeWizardMode || ygZombie) {
        let ygMine = _aeYoink.active && (!_aeYoink.continuityId || !params || !params.continuityId || params.continuityId === _aeYoink.continuityId);
        if (ygMine && params && params.text && Array.isArray(params.text)) {
            _aeYoink.responseText += params.text.join('');
        }
        return { text: [''] };
    }

    // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] Clean the reply before it lands in the document. Native
    // generation exposes no per-turn stop sequences, so this regex net plus native
    // turn alternation is the whole anti-impersonation defence.
    //
    // STREAMING: this hook can fire many times per generation with partial chunks, and
    // a cleanup pass cannot run correctly across a chunk boundary (an impersonation
    // marker can straddle two chunks). Returning { seenData: false } while !final tells
    // NAI to re-deliver the same data plus anything new on the next call, so the final
    // call carries the entire reply and we clean it once, whole. On the final call
    // seenData has no effect, per the hook contract.
    // [UNVERIFIED — watch this in P1 testing] whether deferring seenData this way also
    // defers the streamed text appearing in the editor. If replies visibly land all at
    // once instead of streaming in, this is why, and the fix is to clean only on final
    // without the seenData deferral (accepting cross-chunk misses).
    if (_aeCharChat.active) {
        // [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-POST] v2.43.0's thoughtPass passthrough is DELETED with the
        // flag it read. It existed because the thought used to arrive through THIS hook (it was
        // NAI's own generation) and was being cleaned as if it were a reply — header stamped,
        // truncated at the first POV name, emotion-parsed. That cannot happen now: the thought
        // is a direct api.v1.generate() call, aeDiagGenerate sets _diagProbeActive around it,
        // and this hook's own probe guard returns before reaching here. Everything below is a
        // reply again, unconditionally, exactly as in v2.37.1.

        // [DIAG v2.63.0 CHARCHAT-RESP-DIAG] Record every call before any early-out. The three
        // early-outs below (!final, text-not-an-array, whitespace-only) were ALL silent, which
        // is why an empty-looking turn has been indistinguishable from a lost one for five
        // versions. See the _ccRespCid declaration for what the counts mean.
        let ccRespCid = String((params && params.continuityId) || '');
        if (ccRespCid !== _ccRespCid) { _ccRespCid = ccRespCid; _ccRespCalls = 0; _ccRespBest = ''; _ccRespFinalSeen = false; }
        _ccRespCalls++;
        let ccRespChunk = (params && Array.isArray(params.text)) ? params.text.join('') : '';
        if (ccRespChunk.length > _ccRespBest.length) _ccRespBest = ccRespChunk;
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:resp] call#' + _ccRespCalls
            + ' final=' + !!(params && params.final)
            + ' isArray=' + (!!(params && Array.isArray(params.text)))
            + ' len=' + ccRespChunk.length + ' bestLen=' + _ccRespBest.length
            + ' head=' + JSON.stringify(ccRespChunk.substring(0, 60)));

        if (!params || !params.final) {
            // [DIAG CE-DIAG-FLAGS] ccNoSeenDefer — do not request re-delivery. The chunk is
            // left untouched (no seenData override) instead of asking NAI to replay it plus
            // future chunks on the next call. _ccRespBest above still tracks the longest chunk
            // seen for the diag summary either way; this only changes what NAI does with the
            // data, not what we observe. Accepts cross-chunk impersonation-marker misses in
            // exchange for testing whether the deferral itself is implicated in the empty-reply
            // failure (CE-INDEX flags this contract behaviour as unverified — see the deferral's
            // own header comment above).
            if (CE_DIAG.ccNoSeenDefer) return;
            return { seenData: false };
        }
        _ccRespFinalSeen = true;

        // [BUGFIX v2.63.0 CHARCHAT-RESP-RESCUE] The deferral safety net. seenData:false asks NAI
        // to re-deliver everything on the next call, and script-types confirms that is the
        // contract ("the same data in addition to any new data" / "if this is the final call,
        // this has no effect"). What the contract does NOT promise is that the FINAL call
        // re-delivers a buffer that was deferred by the call before it. If it does not, the
        // reply is destroyed here, silently, with a perfectly normal generation lifecycle
        // around it — which is exactly the reported symptom. So: if the final call arrives
        // without usable text but we watched real text go past on an earlier call, use what we
        // saw instead of dropping the turn. When the contract behaves as documented this branch
        // never fires and behaviour is byte-identical to v2.62.0.
        let ccFinalRaw = (params.text && Array.isArray(params.text)) ? params.text.join('') : '';
        let ccRawReply = ccFinalRaw;
        if (!ccRawReply.trim() && _ccRespBest.trim()) {
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:resp] RESCUE — the final call carried '
                + (params.text && Array.isArray(params.text) ? 'an empty text array' : 'no text array')
                + ' but ' + _ccRespBest.length + ' chars were delivered earlier this turn and deferred with '
                + 'seenData:false. Using the deferred text. THIS CONFIRMS THE DEFERRAL LOSES REPLIES — '
                + 'see CHARCHAT-RESP-DIAG; the deferral itself should now be removed rather than patched.');
            ccRawReply = _ccRespBest;
        }
        if (!ccRawReply.trim()) {
            aeLog('[AE:charchat:resp] final call and nothing was ever delivered this turn ('
                + _ccRespCalls + ' call(s), longest 0 chars). The model genuinely returned no text — '
                + 'this is NOT a document or cleanup problem, do not look downstream.');
            return;
        }
        let ccEmo = charChatParseEmotion(ccRawReply);
        if (ccEmo) { _aeCharChat.lastEmotion = ccEmo; }
        // [v2.27.0] No thought to parse out of the reply anymore — CHARCHAT-THINK is
        // gone. Whatever this contact is thinking now lives in char.ephemeral (updated
        // after each reply by onGenerationEnd's CHARCHAT branch when
        // charChatFreshThoughts is on — [v2.29.0] moved from a pre-reply call),
        // which is already persisted and already has readers, unlike the old log-only
        // stopgap this replaced.
        // [DIAG v2.67.0 CHARCHAT-OVERRUN-DIAG] Every diagnostic upstream of this point
        // truncates (60 chars in the per-call resp log above, 80 in empty-reply-diag's
        // document dump) -- so nothing in this file has ever shown the FULL raw text the
        // model actually returned. Session 4 theory: charChatCleanReply's anti-impersonation
        // guard only truncates when it finds the POV NAME as a literal header/trailing token;
        // a run-on completion that drifts into a new, UNLABELLED sentence after the Emotion
        // line sails through uncaught and gets written verbatim, leaving a dangling fragment
        // that may be poisoning the NEXT turn's context. This channel is OFF by default
        // (/log charchat:rawfull on) -- it's verbose and single-purpose, meant to be off again
        // once CHARCHAT-OVERRUN-DIAG is closed one way or the other.
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:rawfull] RAW (pre-clean), ' + ccRawReply.length + ' chars: ' + JSON.stringify(ccRawReply));
        let ccCleaned = charChatCleanReply(ccRawReply, _aeCharChat.contactName, _aeCharChat.povName);
        aeLog('[AE:charchat:rawfull] CLEANED, ' + ccCleaned.length + ' chars: ' + JSON.stringify(ccCleaned)
            + (ccCleaned.length === ccRawReply.length ? ' -- cleaning removed NOTHING, raw text was written as-is.' : ''));
        return { text: [ccCleaned] };
    }

    // [v2.54.0] STORY-TURN TOKEN ACCUMULATION (token-mode dynamic cadence). Only reached for
    // genuine story turns — every probe / yoink / wizard-mode / charchat path above has
    // already returned. onResponse streams, so sum tokenIds across the chunks of the SAME
    // generation; a change of continuityId self-resets the running total, so no separate
    // start-of-turn reset is needed. Banked once per turn by onGenerationEnd's story path.
    // Returns nothing, so native streaming behaviour is untouched. Wrapped so a counting
    // failure can never disturb a story turn.
    try {
        if (params && params.continuityId && params.continuityId !== _aeStoryTurnCid) {
            _aeStoryTurnCid = params.continuityId;
            _aeStoryTurnTokens = 0;
        }
        if (params && Array.isArray(params.tokenIds)) {
            for (let _ti = 0; _ti < params.tokenIds.length; _ti++) {
                let _row = params.tokenIds[_ti];
                if (_row && typeof _row.length === 'number') _aeStoryTurnTokens += _row.length;
            }
        } else if (params && Array.isArray(params.text)) {
            // Fallback estimate (~4 chars/token, same ratio wizCountTokens uses) if this
            // build does not populate tokenIds.
            _aeStoryTurnTokens += Math.ceil(params.text.join('').length / 4);
        }
    } catch (eTokAcc) { aeLog('[AE] story-turn token accumulation failed (non-fatal):', eTokAcc); }
});

api.v1.hooks.register('onContextBuilt', async function(params: any) {

    // [DIAG] Probe guard — leave a probe generation's context untouched (no hijack, no injection).
    if (_diagProbeActive) return;   // [v2.34.2] nested api.v1.generate() in flight — see onBeforeContextBuild's guard. Log removed: it now fires every ephemeral/architect call.

    // [vNEXT] Yoink hijack — replace the whole context with our prompt. Runs before
