    // the dryRun gate and all normal injection so the wizard gen is fully isolated.
    // [v1.22.2 DRYRUN-FIX] onContextBuilt fires TWICE: once with dryRun:true (a costing/
    // preview pass) and once for the real generation, and the two carry DIFFERENT
    // continuityIds. v1.22.1 latched the dryRun's id, so the real generation's
    // onGenerationEnd never matched, was discarded as "foreign", and the yoink promise
    // stranded until the 90s watchdog fired. Every wizard call therefore took 90s and
    // returned nothing. Capture ONLY on the real pass; still hijack the context on both
    // so the dryRun cost estimate reflects our prompt rather than the user's story.
    if (_aeYoink.active) {
        if (!params.dryRun && !_aeYoink.continuityId && params && params.continuityId) {
            _aeYoink.continuityId = params.continuityId;
        }
        return { messages: _aeYoink.messages };
    }

    // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] Chat turns get no imperative/GM/knowledge injection — they are
    // conversation, not story. The pending-inject queue stays queued for the next
    // real story turn instead of being consumed here.
    if (_aeLiveWiz.active) return;

    // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] Same reasoning as WIZLIVE above: a text message is not a story
    // turn, so no imperative/GM/knowledge injection. The pending-inject queue stays
    // queued for the next real story turn rather than being consumed by a chat reply.
    if (_aeCharChat.active) return;

    // ── AE normal path: gate on dryRun ────────────────────────────────────────
    if (params.dryRun) return;
    const { messages } = params;
    if (!messages || messages.length === 0) return;

    // ── IMPERATIVE BLOCK ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    try {
        const impIdx = await loadIndex();
        const impLines: string[] = [];
        // [v0.9.25 E4] pendingEncounter injection removed — STORAGE_PENDING_ENCOUNTER is now a tombstone
        const pendingKnowledge = await api.v1.tempStorage.get(STORAGE_PENDING_KNOWLEDGE) as any;
        if (pendingKnowledge && pendingKnowledge.charName && pendingKnowledge.snapshot) {
            await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(STORAGE_PENDING_KNOWLEDGE);
            impLines.push('[' + pendingKnowledge.charName + '\'s established knowledge of ' + pendingKnowledge.entityName + ': ' + pendingKnowledge.snapshot + ']');
        }
        const pendingInject = await api.v1.tempStorage.get(STORAGE_PENDING_INJECT) as string | undefined;
        if (pendingInject && pendingInject.trim()) {
            await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(STORAGE_PENDING_INJECT);
            impLines.push('[GM: ' + pendingInject.trim() + ']');
        }
        // [CE-STRIP v2.6.0 CTX-CUT] Critical (85%+) hook loop REMOVED from here — it
        // now builds inside buildWorldStateBlock and rides the AN block instead of
        // this splice, merged with that character's thoughts line. See CE-INDEX
        // PASS 9 / Section 13 header for the full rationale.
        const impWeb = await loadNpcWeb();
        const impAllChars = await loadAllCharacters();
        const impPresentIds = new Set(impIdx.filter(function(m) { return m.scenePresent; }).map(function(m) { return m.id; }));
        const impHighLinks = impWeb.filter(function(link) { return link.priority >= 70 && impPresentIds.has(link.charA_id) && impPresentIds.has(link.charB_id) && link.dynamic.trim() !== ''; }).sort(function(a, b) { return b.priority - a.priority; });
        for (const link of impHighLinks) {
            const cA = impAllChars.find(function(c) { return c.id === link.charA_id; });
            const cB = impAllChars.find(function(c) { return c.id === link.charB_id; });
            if (cA && cB) impLines.push('[' + cA.name + ' and ' + cB.name + ': ' + link.dynamic.trim() + ']');
        }
        if (impLines.length > 0) {
            const impBlock = impLines.join('\n');
            const lastUserForImp = messages.map(function(m: any) { return m.role; }).lastIndexOf('user');
            const insertAtImp = lastUserForImp >= 0 ? lastUserForImp : messages.length;
            messages.splice(insertAtImp, 0, { role: 'user', content: impBlock });
        }
    } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] Imperative injection error:', e); }

    // ── BACKGROUND DRAMA ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // [WP-0] Background drama moved to the Author's Note block in

    // ── EQUIPMENT DESCRIPTIONS ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // [WP-0] Removed. Full equipped-item descriptions now live on each character's
    // lorebook entry (buildLorebookContent "Wearing/Carrying" section), activating
    // with the character's keys instead of loading on every generation.

    // [RETIRED vNEXT] Legacy freeform #tag 1d6 probability engine removed — all skill
    // checks now run through the Quick Action composer (2d6 fail-forward + Stakes).
    return { messages };
});

// ── onGenerationEnd ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
api.v1.hooks.register('onGenerationEnd', async function(params: any) {
    try {
        // [FEATURE v2.51.4 ENGINE-THINKING-SIGNAL] First line, deliberately outside every branch
        // below (yoink/orphaned/probe/normal) so it fires exactly once per onGenerationEnd call
        // no matter which of those paths the rest of this handler takes -- pairs 1:1 with the
        // start pulse in onGenerationRequested regardless of how this particular generation ends.
        try { await _aeThinkingPulse(false); } catch (eEtE) { aeLog('[AE:engine-thinking] end pulse failed:', eEtE); }
        // [DIAG] Probe guard — a probe api.v1.generate() must never advance story state.
        if (_diagProbeActive) return;   // [v2.34.2] nested api.v1.generate() in flight — see onBeforeContextBuild's guard. Log removed: it now fires every ephemeral/architect call.
        // [vNEXT] Yoink generation finished — hand the captured text back to the
        // awaiting caller and STOP. Must return before the gen counter, auto-triggers,
        // and dynamic engine so a wizard gen never advances story state.
        // [v1.22.1] Only if this end event belongs to OUR generation. A late end event
        // from a watchdog-abandoned generation must not resolve the newer call that has
        // since taken the slot. Same fail-safe as onResponse: with no continuityId
        // captured, behave exactly as before.
        if (_aeYoink.active) {
            let ygEndMine = !_aeYoink.continuityId || !params || !params.continuityId || params.continuityId === _aeYoink.continuityId;
            if (ygEndMine) {
                ae_genInFlight = false;
                if (_aeYoink.resolve) _aeYoink.resolve(_aeYoink.responseText);
                _aeYoink.active = false;
                _aeYoink.resolve = null;
                _aeYoink.reject = null;
                _aeYoink.continuityId = null;
                diagBudgetClose((params && params.continuityId) || null);   // [DIAG] close the YOINK reading
                if (_wizModeExitPending) { await aeCompleteWizardModeExit(); } // [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] resolve a deferred exit now that the yoink is done
                return;
            }
            // An orphaned generation ended. Do not touch _aeYoink; also do not run the
            // story-advancing logic below on its behalf.
            if (params && params.continuityId && _wizZombieIds[params.continuityId]) {
                delete _wizZombieIds[params.continuityId]; // [v1.2.0 WIZMODE] zombie finally resolved — stop tracking it
                aeLog('[AE:wizard] zombie generation ' + params.continuityId + ' ended \u2014 cleaned up.');
            } else {
                aeLog('[AE:wizard] ignoring generation-end from an abandoned/foreign generation.');
            }
            return;
        }

        // [v2.4.0 WIZLIVE] A finished chat turn is not a story turn: release the gen
        // guard, retint the freshly grown chat region, and skip the counter, auto-
        // triggers, and dynamic engine entirely.
        if (_aeLiveWiz.active) {
            ae_genInFlight = false;
            diagBudgetClose((params && params.continuityId) || null);
            // [FEATURE v2.29.0 WIZLIVE-BRACE-WRAP] Runs BEFORE the decoration refresh so
            // the tint is computed against the paragraphs that actually survive the pass —
            // same ordering CharChat's normalize-then-decorate already uses.
            try { await _wizliveNormalizeDocument(); } catch (eWn) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] brace-wrap pass failed (chat still usable, formatting may be off):', eWn); }
            try { await refreshLiveWizDecorations(); } catch (eLd) { aeLog('[AE:wizlive] decoration refresh failed:', eLd); }
            return;
        }

        // [v2.21.0 CHARCHAT] A finished text message is not a story turn either: release
        // the gen guard, retint the freshly grown chat region, and skip the gen counter,
        // auto-triggers, and the dynamic engine entirely. Letting the dynamic engine run
        // here would let chat turns silently drive ephemeral/mid-range updates — exactly
        // the context poisoning this design promises not to do.
        if (_aeCharChat.active) {
            ae_genInFlight = false;
            diagBudgetClose((params && params.continuityId) || null);
            // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG] t0 for this turn's timeline — see the mark
            // helper's own header comment near _ccThoughtSignal for what this whole trace is for.
            _ccRaceDiagMark('main reply landed (outer onGenerationEnd start)');
            // [DIAG v2.63.0 CHARCHAT-RESP-DIAG] The one line that settles "did NAI produce
            // anything at all this turn". Compared by continuityId so a silent turn reports
            // zero rather than reprinting the previous turn's numbers.
            try {
                let ccEndCid = String((params && params.continuityId) || '');
                if (ccEndCid && ccEndCid !== _ccRespCid) {
                    aeLog('[AE:charchat:resp] turn summary \u2014 onResponse NEVER FIRED for this '
                        + 'generation (cid ' + ccEndCid + '). NAI ran a full generation lifecycle and '
                        + 'delivered no response callback at all. Nothing downstream of here can be at '
                        + 'fault; look at the prompt or the generation slot.');
                } else {
                    aeLog('[AE:charchat:resp] turn summary \u2014 calls=' + _ccRespCalls
                        + ' finalSeen=' + _ccRespFinalSeen + ' longestChunk=' + _ccRespBest.length + ' chars.');
                }
            } catch (_eRs) {}

            // [v2.24.0, per user: "maybe we handle it after generation?"] This is that
            // "after". The scaffold-append that used to live here is gone; instead the
            // whole chat region is re-canonicalised now that both this turn's POV message
            // and the reply are in the document — headers applied, stray blank paragraphs
            // dropped, unclosed braces closed. Runs BEFORE the decoration refresh so the
            // tint is computed against the paragraphs that actually survive the pass.
            try { await _charchatNormalizeDocument(); } catch (eNz) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] turn normalisation failed (chat still usable, formatting may be off):', eNz); }
            // [DIAG CE-DIAG-FLAGS] ccSkipDecorations — skips BOTH the bubble-styling pass and
            // the widget clear/rebuild nested inside it (refreshCharChatImageWidgets is only
            // ever called from here). Use ccSkipWidgets instead if only the widget half needs
            // isolating.
            if (CE_DIAG.ccSkipDecorations) {
                aeLog('[AE:diag] ccSkipDecorations ON \u2014 decoration + widget refresh skipped this turn.');
            } else {
                try { await refreshCharChatDecorations(); } catch (eCd) { aeLog('[AE:charchat] decoration refresh failed:', eCd); }
            }
            _ccRaceDiagMark('normalisation + decoration refresh settled');
            let ccCfgEnd = await loadConfig();
            // [v2.45.0 CHARCHAT-THOUGHT-POST] THE THOUGHT FIRES HERE — after the reply, on the
            // transport that never stopped working. This is where v2.29.0 put it, and after
            // v2.38.0-v2.44.1 spent four versions failing to do better, it is where it belongs.
            // Read the CE-INDEX v2.45.0 block before moving it again.
            //   ORDER: after normalisation (the transcript this reads must be settled and
            //   un-merged first) and BEFORE the image pass, so the two nested api.v1.generate()
            //   calls are serialised and _diagProbeActive's scope stays unambiguous — the same
            //   reason the image pass's own comment gives for sitting last.
            //   ONE TURN STALE, BY DESIGN: this describes the reply that just landed and informs
            //   the NEXT one, via buildCharChatInstruction's ccEph read. That is the accepted
            //   trade, not a bug. [CORRECTED v2.62.0] It is NO LONGER "pinned last in context by
            //   v2.43.0 CHARCHAT-TAIL-ORDER" — the Emotion directive is the unconditional final
            //   block now (CHARCHAT-TAIL-TERMINAL); the thought sits directly above it.
            //   AWAITED, not fire-and-forget: the reply is already in the document and
            //   normalised before this starts, so nothing this call does can corrupt it. What it
            //   can still race is the user's NEXT Send — nothing gates NAI's Send button — and
            //   that race is handled by op 'arm' cancelling this call at pointerdown (grep
            //   CHARCHAT-ARM-CANCEL), not by assuming it cannot happen.
            //   Fresh Thoughts OFF is the control: it restores the plain v2.37.1 path exactly.
            if (ccCfgEnd && ccCfgEnd.charChatFreshThoughts && !CE_DIAG.ccNoThought) {
                await _charchatSetBusy('thinking');
                // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG, per user's "the thinking indicator might be
                // ending too quickly" hypothesis] This mark is the moment the UI is FIRST told
                // "busy — thinking". Compare its gap from the previous mark (should be near-zero:
                // nothing async happens between normalisation settling and this call) against the
                // gap the NEXT mark shows once the thought call actually returns.
                _ccRaceDiagMark('busyStage=thinking set, calling generateChatEphemeral');
                try {
                    let ccEndThChar = await loadCharacter(_aeCharChat.contactId);
                    if (ccEndThChar) {
                        let ccEndThGen = 0;
                        try { ccEndThGen = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_GEN_COUNT, 0) as number; }
                        catch (_eEndThG) { ccEndThGen = 0; }
                        await generateChatEphemeral(ccEndThChar, ccCfgEnd, ccEndThGen);
                    } else {
                        aeLog('[AE:charchat:thought] contact could not be loaded \u2014 no thought this turn, previous one stands.');
                    }
                } catch (eEndTh) {
                    // Non-fatal by design, same rule the image pass follows: a failed thought must
                    // never take down a chat whose reply has already landed correctly.
                    api.v1.error('[AE:charchat:thought] post-reply thought failed (chat unaffected, previous thought stands):', eEndTh);
                }
                // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG] The gap this mark reports is the thought
                // call's real wall-clock duration — compare against the [AE:gendiag] ms field
                // for the same call to confirm they agree (they should; this is measuring the
                // same await from the outside).
                _ccRaceDiagMark('generateChatEphemeral settled (cancelled, errored, or completed)');
            } else {
                // [DIAG CE-DIAG-FLAGS] This branch is now reached two ways: Fresh Thoughts is
                // genuinely OFF, or ccNoThought forced a skip of the thought call above while
                // Fresh Thoughts is ON. Either way ccNoPrime independently controls whether THIS
                // call fires, so the two flags combine cleanly: ccNoThought+ccNoPrime together
                // isolate a turn with neither nested generate() running at all.
                // [BUGFIX v2.64.0 CHARCHAT-SLOT-PRIME] Fresh Thoughts OFF was the turn that always
                // needed a second Send. With no thought firing between turns, NAI's generation slot
                // was never exercised after this turn's document edits, and the next native Send
                // came back empty. Prime it with a 1-token throwaway on the same cancellable
                // background transport the thought uses. This runs ONLY in the OFF branch: when
                // thoughts are on, generateChatEphemeral above has already exercised the slot, and
                // priming again would just be a second redundant call. See _charchatPrimeSlot.
                if (CE_DIAG.ccNoPrime) {
                    aeLog('[AE:diag] ccNoPrime ON \u2014 slot-prime call skipped this turn.');
                } else {
                    _ccRaceDiagMark('Fresh Thoughts off \u2014 priming the generation slot instead');
                    try { await _charchatPrimeSlot(); }
                    catch (eEndPr) { aeLog('[AE:charchat:prime] prime pass threw (chat unaffected):', eEndPr); }
                }
                _ccRaceDiagMark('slot prime settled (cancelled, errored, or completed)');
            }
            // [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-IMG] Attachment pass. AFTER normalisation (the text it
            // rewrites must be settled and un-merged first) and after the ephemeral
            // refresh (both make nested api.v1.generate() calls; serialising them keeps
            // the _diagProbeActive guard's scope unambiguous). Non-fatal by design — a
            // failed photo must never take down a working chat.
            if (ccCfgEnd && ccCfgEnd.charChatImages && !CE_DIAG.ccNoImages) {
                // [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] Staged, so the panel says "sending photo…"
                // instead of sitting silent through an architect call plus a bridge image
                // generation.
                // [BUGFIX v2.61.0 CHARCHAT-IMG-BUSY-FLASH] The busyStage='image' flip moved
                // INTO _charchatProcessImages itself (only once it confirms a real job exists)
                // — this call site used to flip it unconditionally just because the FEATURE
                // was on, flashing "sending photo…" on every turn even when nothing was
                // actually being sent.
                _ccRaceDiagMark('calling _charchatProcessImages (may or may not find a job)');
                try {
                    let ccImgN = await _charchatProcessImages();
                    if (ccImgN > 0) {
                        try { await refreshCharChatDecorations(); } catch (_eccId) {}
                    }
                } catch (eccImg) { api.v1.error('[AE:charchat] image attachment pass failed (chat unaffected):', eccImg); }
                _ccRaceDiagMark('_charchatProcessImages settled');
            }
            // [v2.38.0 CHARCHAT-THINK-FIRST] Idle again — set before the final broadcast so
            // that repaint carries it. _charchatSetBusy broadcasts too, but its
            // no-op-on-same-value guard makes the redundant push free.
            await _charchatSetBusy('');
            // [DIAG v2.59.0 CHARCHAT-RACE-DIAG] The gap from the last checkpoint to HERE, plus
            // the arm handler's own mark (see op 'arm' below), is the number that answers the
            // user's question directly: how long after the UI says "safe to send" does the
            // NEXT Send's arm signal actually arrive, and was anything still in flight when it did.
            _ccRaceDiagMark('busyStage cleared \u2014 turn fully idle, next Send is expected to be safe now');
            // [v2.32.0 CHARCHAT-HUD] Push the grown transcript to the bridge panel. Last,
            // so the payload includes this turn's reply, its normalisation, and any
            // image tag the pass above just resolved.
            try { await broadcastCharChatState(); } catch (_eccBc) {}
            return;
        }

        ae_genInFlight = false; // [v0.9.36] generation finished — release the generate guard
        diagBudgetClose((params && params.continuityId) || null);   // [DIAG] close the STORY reading (no-op if none pending)
        // Skip on inline generations (tooltips, etc.) and retry/undo flagged turns
        if (params?.inlineGeneration) return;
        const skipFlag = await api.v1.tempStorage.get(STORAGE_ENGINE_SKIP) as string | undefined;
        if (skipFlag === 'true') {
            await api.v1.tempStorage.remove(STORAGE_ENGINE_SKIP);
            aeLog('[AE] Engine skipped this turn (retry/undo).');
            return;
        }

        // [v0.9.24 E1] Increment persistent generation count (replaces token estimation).
        try {
            let _genC = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_GEN_COUNT, 0) as number;
            _genC = (Number(_genC) || 0) + 1;
            await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_GEN_COUNT, _genC);
            aeLog('[AE] Generation count: ' + _genC);
        } catch (e) { api.v1.error('[AE] Gen count increment error:', e); }

        // [v2.54.0] Bank this story turn's tokens onto the persistent counter (token-mode
        // dynamic cadence). _aeStoryTurnTokens was summed across this generation's onResponse
        // chunks; reset both accumulators after banking so the next turn starts clean. The
        // counter advances every turn regardless of mode — cheap, and it means switching to
        // token mode mid-story has a meaningful counter already in place.
        try {
            let _tokAdd = Number(_aeStoryTurnTokens) || 0;
            if (_tokAdd > 0) {
                let _tokC = await api.v1.storyStorage.getOrDefault(STORAGE_TOKEN_COUNT, 0) as number;
                _tokC = (Number(_tokC) || 0) + _tokAdd;
                await api.v1.storyStorage.set(STORAGE_TOKEN_COUNT, _tokC);
                aeLog('[AE] Token count: ' + _tokC + ' (+' + _tokAdd + ')');
            }
        } catch (eTokBank) { api.v1.error('[AE] Token count bank error:', eTokBank); }
        _aeStoryTurnTokens = 0;
        _aeStoryTurnCid = null;

        // [CE-STRIP v2.0.x] Time auto-advance removed — Clock/Calendar cut.

        aeLog('[AE] Generation complete — running auto-triggers...');
        await runAutoTriggers();
        aeLog('[AE] Generation complete — engaging dynamic engine...');
        await runDynamicEngine();
    } catch (e) {
        api.v1.error('[AE] onGenerationEnd error:', e);
        // [vNEXT] Failsafe — never leave the yoink lock stuck on error.
        if (_aeYoink.active) {
            if (_aeYoink.reject) _aeYoink.reject(e);
            _aeYoink.active = false;
            _aeYoink.resolve = null;
            _aeYoink.reject = null;
            _aeYoink.continuityId = null;
        }
    }
});

// Skip engine on retry and undo — token stamps shouldn't update when story reverts
api.v1.hooks.register('onHistoryNavigated', async function(params: any) {
    ae_genInFlight = false; // [v0.9.36] safety release if a generation was aborted/navigated
    if (params?.cause === 'retry' || params?.cause === 'undo') {
        await api.v1.tempStorage.set(STORAGE_ENGINE_SKIP, 'true');
    }
});


// ============================================================================
// SECTION 27 — STARTUP
//
// [CHANGE] onScriptsLoaded startup sequence:
//   1. Register sidebarPanel only (no ae-scene-hud scriptPanel — bridge owns HUD)
//   2. Request permissions
